PT P
PN CN107812187-A
TI Assisted skin absorption method using carbon dioxide gas bubbles and ultrasonic waves comprises preparing carbon dioxide topical preparation comprising first and second formulation, mixing, and generating multiple carbon dioxide bubbles.
AU YE Z
AE YE Z (YEZZ-Individual)
GA 2018239526
AB    NOVELTY - An assisted skin absorption method using carbon dioxide gas bubbles and ultrasonic waves comprises preparing carbon dioxide topical preparation comprising first and second formulation, mixing, generating multiple carbon dioxide bubbles, and emitting ultrasonic wave via ultrasonic device to oscillate carbon dioxide bubbles.
   USE - Assisted skin absorption method using carbon dioxide gas bubbles and ultrasonic waves.
   ADVANTAGE - The product increases kinetic energy of skin care products and skin cells, increases skin temperature and enlarges pores, and contributes to beauty and body care ingredients absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first formulation comprises 1.5-10 wt.% citric acid and 3-15 wt.% sodium citrate; and second formulation comprises 3-12 wt.% sodium bicarbonate, 3-5 wt.% thickener and 83-94 wt.% solvent. The first formulation further comprises 1-2 wt.% polysorbate, 2-5 wt.% glycerol, 68-92.5 wt.% distilled water and active ingredient at pH value of 4-5.5. The thickener comprises sodium acrylate/acryloyl dimethyl taurate copolymer, isohexadecane and polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monooleate. The solvent is propylene glycol or glycerol. The viscosity range of first formulation is 3000 mPa.s, and second formulation is 4600 mPa.s.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.)

PT P
PN CN113655183-A; CN113655183-B
TI Method for detecting carbon dioxide content in cardiac surgery, involves calculating carbon dioxide diffusion data according to first, second and third concentration data and first and second distance data respectively.
AU PAN F
   ZHAO Q
   QIAN Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN WEST CHINA HOSPITAL (USCU-C)
GA 2021D6236L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining distance between a first sensor and a second sensor as first distance data. The distance between the second sensor and the third sensor is obtained as second distance data. The carbon dioxide diffusion data is calculated according to the first concentration data, the second concentration data and the first-distance data. An outer carbon dioxide diffluent data is obtained according to second and third concentration data, the third and second-distribution data, and the second-and third-distortion data, where the ratio of the outer diffusion data to the inner diffusion data are used as a control parameter to control the flow of carbon dioxide thorax perfusion.
   USE - Method for detecting carbon dioxide content in a cardiac surgery, cardiovascular anesthesia and cardiopulmonary circulation technology of a patient during endocardial surgery.
   ADVANTAGE - The device for detecting carbon dioxide content ensures the carbon dioxide concentration of each important area in the operation, effectively improving the safety of the surgical environment. The control unit takes the ratio of the outer and inner carbon dioxide diffusion data as the control parameter to control carbon dioxide thorax perfusion flow. The carbon dioxide over-flow is controlled based on doctor experience, and thus carbon dioxide cannot ensure in the operating room is in the range of safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for detecting carbon dioxide content in a cardiac surgery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of a method for detecting carbon dioxide content in a cardiac surgery (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN113617187-A; CN113617187-B
TI Recycling carbon dioxide, comprises heating gas separating tower comprising trapping agents and impurity removing agent, introducing regeneration gas into gas separation tower, and cooling mixed gas to condense and recycle carbon dioxide.
AU GUO X
   LIU X
   HOU Y
   WANG L
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0050A
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling carbon dioxide comprises heating gas separation tower to 40-80degrees Celsius, entering the waste gas containing carbon dioxide into the gas separating tower and sequentially connecting with the impurity removing agent. The first trapping agent and the second trapping agent are used for contacting carbon dioxide, where the exhaust gas introduction s stopped. The mixed gas is cooled to below -10degrees Celsius for condensing and recycling carbon dioxide of the regenerated gas, where the pressure of the first and second trapping agents for carbon dioxide adsorption is 0.02-0.05 MPa. The regeneration gas is introduced into the air separation tower, where the regeneration gas is sequentially contacted with the second and third trapping agents to obtain mixed gas, which is cooled to condense and recover carbon dioxide from the recycled carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for recycling carbon dioxide used for oil extraction, food industry, petroleum exploitation, and replacement of stratum crude oil in production well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method selectively absorbs the carbon dioxide in the waste gas, so as to improve the recycling amount of carbon dioxide and recycling purity. The carbon dioxide after recycling can be directly used for oil extraction and food industry. The method avoids the crushing of the first trapping agent and the second trapping agent. The service life of the trapping agent is improved. After multiple adsorption-desorption circulation, maintaining high carbon dioxide recovery purity is maintained. The flow path of the regeneration gas is opposite to the flow of the exhaust gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN111407280-A; CN111407280-B
TI End-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring device for            non-invasive ventilator, has connecting hose, which is            connected to breathing valve, where oxygen tube and            carbon dioxide exhalation tube of non-invasive            ventilator are formed.
AU LI W
   JI X
   MA D
   HAN Y
   MA Z
   MENG X
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2020679906
AB    NOVELTY - The end-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring device                has a connecting hose, which is connected to a                breathing valve, where an oxygen tube and a carbon                dioxide exhalation tube of a non-invasive                ventilator are formed. A gas outlet of the carbon                dioxide exhalation tube is connected to a gas                chamber and a monitoring terminal, where the gas                chamber is provided with a carbon dioxide detection                device. A moisture sensor is provided for detecting                the carbon dioxide concentration and a humidity                data in the gas respectively. The monitoring                terminal performs the detection of the carbon                dioxide concentration based on the detected                humidity data. An output of a waveform of the                end-tidal carbon dioxide partial pressure is                corrected with time. An infrared light source is                provided as a nickel-chromium wire, which emits the                infrared light.
   USE - End-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring device for                a non-invasive ventilator.
   ADVANTAGE - The connecting hose is connected to a                breathing valve, where an oxygen tube and a carbon                dioxide exhalation tube of a non-invasive                ventilator are formed, and hence enables                eliminating the error caused by the steam                condensing in a monitoring process and possibly                eliminates the influence of moisture on an                experiment result, and also improves the accuracy                of an end-tidal carbon dioxide concentration                detection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                end-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring method for a                non-invasive ventilator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an                end-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring device.

PT P
PN CN110735622-A
TI Coalbed methane production involves drilling            horizontal well from ground having horizontal section            located in coal seam to be fractured, and then            delivering carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide booster            pump.
AU LIANG W
   ZHU D
   WU P
   NIU D
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202011266W
AB    NOVELTY - Coalbed methane production involves drilling                horizontal well from ground having horizontal                section located in coal seam to be fractured, and                then delivering carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide                booster pump. The thickener is injected into closed                sand mixing device with fine sand to form carbon                dioxide fracturing fluid with a low sand ratio. The                carbon dioxide fracturing fluid is transferred into                the main carbon dioxide fracturing vehicle. The                staged initial fracturing of the coal seam is to be                performed after completing the fracturing. The oil                pipe is used to transport the drilling rig. The                clear water and sand are mixed in the water-mixing                sand device, and then transported to the main water                press. The water injection valve is opened, where                the water-based fracturing fluid is pushed into the                horizontal well through the casing to perform                secondary fracturing of the coal seam.
   USE - Method for producing coalbed methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the coalbed methane in a                simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                complex shape and reasonable fracture width.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for mining coalbed methane, which comprises carbon                dioxide tanker truck (1), a carbon dioxide booster                pump truck (2), a tackifier pumping device (3), a                closed sand mixing device (4) and carbon dioxide                main pressure truck (5).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural                diagram of the device for extracting coalbed                methane by supercritical carbon dioxide and water                composite fracturing.
   Carbon dioxide tanker truck (1)
   Carbon dioxide booster pump truck (2)
   Tackifier pumping device (3)
   Closed sand mixing device (4)
   Carbon dioxide main pressure truck (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The initial fracturing                  uses carbon dioxide and fine sand as proppant and                  fracturing fluid. The secondary fracturing uses                  water-based fracturing fluid, where the particle                  size of water-based fracturing to carry sand to                  be larger than carbon dioxide fracturing sand                  particle size.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Liquid carbon dioxide separation oil-containing sediment device comprises separation tower, liquid carbon dioxide, storage tank, carbon dioxide (CO2) pipeline, oil-water pipeline, oil-sludge inlet, sediment outlet, oil-water outlet.
AU YU G
   LIU D
   ZHANG J
   LIANG Y
AE DONGYING JINDAO ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
   SHENGLI OILFIELD JINDAO IND CO LTD AGRIC (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201879568A
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid carbon dioxide separation oil-containing sediment device comprises a separation tower (1), a liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank (2), a carbon dioxide pipeline, an oil-water pipeline, an oil-sludge inlet, a sediment outlet (6), an oil-water outlet (7), a carbon dioxide inlet (8), a carbon dioxide inlet and outlet, a filtered membrane, sediment vent, CO2 pump, oil pump, air pump, globe valve, ball valve, umbrella oil hood, serpentine hose and safety valve, where the liquid carbon dioxide CO2 has ultra-low viscosity, super fluidity and permeability, where physical properties of high density and superabsorbent force are mixed with oil-containing sediment in a supercritical pressure separation tower to replace the oil and water adhering to the surface of the sediment particles, where the separation tower and the liquid CO2 storage tank are steel cylindrical sealed pressure vessels.
   USE - Liquid carbon dioxide separation oil-containing sediment device.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid carbon dioxide separation oil-containing sediment device enables to the absence of new harmful substances in the whole process of processing, not containing new pollution and secondary treatment and has special beneficial effects and functions in the dual sense of environmental protection and resource utilization, good application value.

TI Method for producing liquid carbon dioxide, involves depressurizing liquid carbon dioxide through pressure regulator mounted in flow path downstream, and storing liquid carbon dioxide at storage temperature in product stage.
AU STALLMANN O
   GISART F
   BRITZ C K
   CARUSO A
   WEITZEL M J
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
GA 2018358235
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a carbon dioxide gas stream (36) at a first end (24) of a flow path defined by conduits (12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 22). The gas stream is condensed into liquid carbon dioxide through a refrigeration unit (30). The liquid carbon dioxide is depressurized through a pressure regulator (32) mounted in the flow path downstream so as to cool the liquid carbon dioxide to a storage temperature that is below an ambient temperature of a product stage (28) fluidly connected to a second end (26) of conduit. The liquid carbon dioxide is stored at storage temperature in product stage.
   USE - Method for producing liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The level of processing responsiveness that a user senses as sufficiently immediate or enables the processor to keep up with an external process. By utilizing a refrigeration unit to facilitate condensation of carbon dioxide at low temperatures, some embodiments provide for a reduction in the operating pressures along the flow path, which in turn may provide for a reduction in size and/or elimination of the compressor. The method is provided for a substantial increase in overall efficiency in liquefying carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for producing liquid carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the system for producing liquid carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN BR102018005495-A2
TI Waste treatment process generated in creation of pigs and products, comprises e.g. waste sludge preparation by e.g. directing generated sludge in bays to large sedimentation tanks and centrifugation by pumping sludge into centrifuge.
AU DA SILVA M N
AE DA SILVA M N (DSIL-Individual)
   DA SILCA G D T (DSIL-Individual)
GA 201987779R
AB    NOVELTY - Waste treatment process generated in creation of pigs and products thus obtained, comprises e.g.: (a) waste sludge preparation by directing generated sludge in bays to large sedimentation tanks with average solids content of 3%, and after targeting these sedimentation tanks, adding 0.1% of flocculant polymer which will thicken sludge so that it has a pasty, pumpable sludge consistency with a viscosity of up to 600 cps; (b) centrifugation by pumping thickened sludge into a centrifuge that will work at 3000 rpm, and separating solid from liquid contained in thickened sludge; (c) centrifuge disposal fluid treatment by e.g. (c1) with separation of liquid phase through centrifuge it will be recovered by adding a total of 5% calcium hydroxide on liquid volume; and (d) obtaining net product by suspension obtained product with 10% double calcium and potassium carbonate and 90% nutrient rich organics and secreting product in storage tanks and centrifuged again removing solid with its compounds.
   USE - The process is useful for waste treatment generated in pig breeding and products thus obtained.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: recovers 100% of the sludge residue from waste turning it into noble raw material for applications in consumer products, liquid and solid agricultural fertilizer, as main components; is transformation of waste currently sent to landfill into noble products; is sustainable; is longer life of landfills; is no soil contamination; reduces carbon dioxide and methane emissions from waste in the atmosphere; and provides products that can act in the agricultural line.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waste treatment process generated in the creation of pigs and products thus obtained, comprises: (a) waste sludge preparation by directing the generated sludge in the bays to large sedimentation tanks with an average solids content of 3%, and after targeting these sedimentation tanks, adding 0.1% of flocculant polymer which will thicken the sludge so that it has a pasty, pumpable sludge consistency with a viscosity of up to 600 cps; (b) centrifugation by pumping the thickened sludge into a centrifuge that will work at 3000 rpm, and separating the solid from the liquid contained in the thickened sludge; (c) centrifuge disposal fluid treatment by (c1) with the separation of the liquid phase through the centrifuge it will be recovered by adding a total of 5% calcium hydroxide on the liquid volume, (c2) adding carbon dioxide (carbonation) using liquid carbon dioxide that will be injected directly into the reaction tank (carbonator) through a low pressure, high flow blower using the pressure of the liquid carbon dioxide tank itself, (c3) injecting the liquid carbon dioxide into the tank will occur the precipitation of calcium hydroxide into calcium carbonate, however as much of the sludge volume is organic, carbon dioxide will first acidify the water to carbon dioxide hydrogen carbonate and then capture free calcium: the end of the reaction occurs when pH reaches 7.5-8.5, (c4) organic particles do not react with calcium, but they become inoculated by increasing the particle nucleus with the insertion of carbon dioxide, in this phase the particle increases more than 20 times its size and the calcium carbonate generated makes it heavy making sedimentation very fast, and (c5) the liquid phase of the manure has a soluble potassium concentration that needs to be precipitated, when injecting the carbon dioxide the precipitation reaction generates the potassium carbonate, so the previous process liquid becomes a double potassium calcium carbonate with up to 10% of concentration being its organic remnant of its own origin; (d) obtaining net product by suspension the obtained product with 10% double calcium and potassium carbonate and 90% nutrient rich organics, thus making it a suspended fertilizer and secreting the obtained product in storage tanks and centrifuged again removing the solid with its stabilized compounds; (e) obtaining solid product by transferring the obtained mixture in the 40% solids mixer to a continuous type indirect drying rotary dryer where no direct heat is applied to the product but a heat exchange through the dryer wall; (f) mixing product by the products before drying have a phosphorus concentration of 8%, so at this stage the centrifuged liquid and sludge products are mixed in a low speed horizontal mixer and the mixture of these compounds is fed to the dryer; and (g) drying of solid product by heating the dryer by steam boiler generated water at a temperature of 150-180 degrees C, because it is a material with low specific weight, when drying the material in the rotary dryer, the material enters 30% solids and 70% water, upon leaving the drying system, reaches 70% solids and 30% humidity. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for product obtained through the process of waste treatment generated in the rearing of pigs, comprising final product in semi-granulated dry powder, where the final dried product is agricultural fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The centrifuge is of the decanter type.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113621562-A
TI Culturing method for C2C12 myoblasts to differentiate into mature muscle cells comprises e.g. taking C2C12 myoblasts and inoculating in culture dish containing growth medium, placing in carbon dioxide incubator, washing cells with phosphate-buffered saline, and adding growth medium.
AU LI J
   MA Y
AE GENHOUSE MEDICINE SUZHOU CO LTD (GENH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0026X
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing method for C2C12 myoblasts to differentiate into mature muscle cells comprises (1) taking C2C12 myoblasts and inoculating them in a culture dish containing growth medium, and placing them in a carbon dioxide incubator, where the culture conditions are 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide, (2) when the cell confluence reaches about 80%, discarding the old medium and washing the cells once with phosphate-buffered saline, then adding trypsin cell digestion solution to cover the cells and digest for a few minutes at 37C, during this period, observing the cell morphology with an inverted microscope, when the cells are round, it indicates that the cell digestion has been completed, at this time, adding 5 ml growth medium to terminate the trypsin digestion, using pipette to gently pipette many times to break up the cell clumps, transferring the cell suspension to a 15 ml centrifuge tube, and (3) when the cells have grown to 80% confluence, withdrawing the growth medium.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing for C2C12 myoblasts to differentiate into mature muscle cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses C2C12 mouse myoblasts as the object, through cell proliferation culture, differentiation culture and other steps, and improves the efficiency of C2C12 myoblasts to differentiate into mature muscle cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing method for C2C12 myoblasts to differentiate into mature muscle cells comprises (1) taking C2C12 myoblasts and inoculating them in a culture dish containing growth medium, and placing them in a carbon dioxide incubator, where the culture conditions are 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide, (2) when the cell confluence reaches about 80%, discarding the old medium and washing the cells once with phosphate-buffered saline, then adding trypsin cell digestion solution to cover the cells and digest for a few minutes at 37degrees Celsius, during this period, observing the cell morphology with an inverted microscope, when the cells are round, it indicates that the cell digestion has been completed, at this time, adding 5 ml growth medium to terminate the trypsin digestion, using pipette to gently pipette many times to break up the cell clumps, transferring the cell suspension to a 15 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 800 revolutions per minute for 3 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding specific amount of fresh growth medium, uniformly blowing using a straw, spreading it in a 12-well plate at a density of 10,000 cells/cm2 and placing it in a carbon dioxide cell culture box, where the culture condition is 37degrees Celsius with 5% carbon dioxide, and observing the cell morphology with inverted microscope, and (3) when the cells has grown to 80% confluence, withdrawing the growth medium, adding differentiation medium to induce myotropic differentiation, replacing the differentiation medium every 48 hours, observing the cell morphology with an inverted microscope, where the growth medium is high glucose Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM), 10% fetal bovine serum, and 1% double antibody, the differentiation medium is high-sugar DMEM medium, 2% horse serum, 1% double antibody, and 100 nM insulin, the preparation method of primary myoblast differentiation medium is: adding 5 ml penicillin-streptomycin solution (100X) (Biyuntian, catalog number C0222) to 500 ml of DMEM medium (Hyclone, catalog number SH30243.01), adding 10 ml horse serum (Shenggong, product number E500007-0400), uniformly mixing, storing at 4degrees Celsius, each time the differentiation medium is changed, adding insulin with final concentration of 100 nM to the above differentiation medium, uniformly mixing, and then changing the medium.

PN CN108659241-A; CN108659241-B
TI Star polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness, and carbon dioxide responsivenes for preparing self-healing hydrogel, is prepared by dissolving dried pentaerythritol in first solvent and adding acid-binding agent.
AU YUAN W
   WANG C
   WEI J
   LI S
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201883627M
AB    NOVELTY - Star polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness, and carbon dioxide responsivenes is represented by (I).
   USE - Star polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness, and carbon dioxide responsivenes for preparing self-healing hydrogel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The star polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness, and carbon dioxide responsivenes has good biocompatibility and high self-healing efficiency, solid-state aid, medicine slow release, reactor field of cell culture and has latent application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Star polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness, and carbon dioxide responsivenes is represented by formula (I).. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a method for preparing preparing star-shaped polymer having temperature responsiveness, pH responsiveness and carbon dioxide responsiveness, which involves dissolving dried pentaerythritol in first solvent , adding acid-binding agent triethylamine to a dry single-necked flask, and dissolving reagent 2-bromoisobutyryl bromide in first solvent, adding to a constant pressure dropping funnel, and dropped into a flask in an ice bath, after the completion of the dropwise addition;
   (2) removing ice bath apparatus, and vigorously stirring reaction at room temperature for 24-48 hours;
   (3) mixing obtained reaction product with a large amount of anhydrous diethyl ether and washing three times with a saturated sodium hydrogen carbonate solution to obtain an organic layer and drying over anhydrous magnesium sulfate, after removing water;
   (4) filtering and evaporating filtrate to dryness, crystallizing product from ethyl ether, and recrystallizing product to give a pale-yellow solid sulfur-bromine;
   (5) drying pale yellow solid product in a vacuum oven at 50 degrees C for 12-36 hours;
   (6) dissolving final product, dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate (DMAEMA), and second catalyst in the third solvent in molar ratio of 1:80-100:4-6;
   (7) adding to reaction bottle, followed by freezing, vacuuming, argon filling - freezing for three times until reaction is carried out under magnetic stirring at 40-90 degrees C for 3-7 hours;
   (8) stopping reaction, carrying out dialysis for 2-5 days, and lyophilizing to constant weight to obtain a white solid star-shaped poly(dimethyl) aminoethyl-Br;
   (9) dissolving final product in fourth solvent in molar ratio of 1:4-12, adding to a dry reaction flask, sealing and reacting at 40-90 degrees C for 36-60 hours, stopping after lysis for 2-5 days and lyophilizing to obtain star-shaped poly(dimethyl) aminoethyl-N3;
   (10) dissolving propargyl alcohol, and second catalyst in the third solvent in a molar ratio of 1:4-12:4-6 and adding to the reaction in the bottle, after three rounds of system freezing-vacuum-argon-freezing;
   (11) carrying out reaction under magnetic stirring at 40-90 degrees C for 3-7 hours, stopping reaction, dialyzing for 3 days and lyophilizing to constant weight to obtain white solid star-shaped poly(dimethyl) aminoethyl-OH;
   (12) adding dry fifth solvent, dry acid binding agent diisopropylamine, sixth reagent to single-necked flask in a volume ratio of 20:1:1;
   (13) dissolving final product into the dry first solvent, transferring to a constant pressure dropping funnel, dropping into the flask dropwise, and reacting at room temperature for 12-48 hours, after the reaction is finished; and
   (14) adding deionized water to dissolve for 2-5 days and freeze-drying to constant weight to give star-shaped poly(dimethyl) aminoethyl-phosphate; and
   (15) a method for preparing preparing pH-responsive, carbon dioxide-responsive self-healing hydrogel, which involves formulating star polymer poly(dimethyl) aminoethyl-phosphate into an aqueous solution of 0.10 gram per milliliter, and adding solution of 4.00 mole per liter slowly dropwise, and stirring aqueous solution of r ferric nitrate nonahydrate vigorously, and transferring solution to gel within 3 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first solvent is selected from tetrahydrofuran, acetone, dichloromethane or chloroform. The second catalyst is cuprous chloride/bipyridine, cuprous bromide/bipyridine, cuprous chloride/pentamethyldivinyltriene One or more of an amine, cuprous bromide/pentamethyldiethylenetriamine, cuprous chloride/hexamethyltriethylenetetramine or cuprous bromide/hexamethyltriethylenetetramine. The third solvent is tetrahydrofuran, toluene, anisole, N,N-diethylformamide or N,N-dimethylacetamide. The fourth solvent is toluene, anisole, N,N-dimethylformamide or N,N-dimethylacetamide. The fifth solvent is one of tetrahydrofuran, acetone, dichloromethane or chloroform. The sixth reagent is phosphorus oxychloride.

AB    (CN211695087-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of heating, claims a steam hot water heating pump device, comprising a carbon dioxide heat pump system and steam hot water combined supply system coupled by air cooler heat exchange; the carbon dioxide heat pump system comprises an expander; the heat discharging side outlet of the air cooler is connected with the inlet of the expander; the outlet of the expander is connected with the heat discharging side inlet of the air cooler; the steam hot water supply system comprises a water supply pump, a flash tank and a steam compressor, the water supply pump is connected with the inlet of the flash tank through the heat absorbing side of the air cooler; the flash evaporation tank comprises a hot water outlet and a steam outlet connected with the inlet of the water vapour compressor; the expander is connected with the water vapour compressor through a transmission shaft in a transmission manner. GA 2020811189
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, involves adding graphen oxide (GO) to dimethylformamide (DMF) and sonicating for 6 hours to obtain GO mixture, weighing zirconium salt, 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-dicarboxylic acid and formic acid and adding to the GO mixture and stirring to obtain a mixture of GO and UiO-bpy. The molar ratio of zirconium salt, 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-dicarboxylic acid, formic acid and DMF is 0.106:0.104:0.018:0.145. The reaction solution is reacted at 100-160 degrees C for 12-36 hours. The collected solid phase is washed with a mixed solvent of DMF and tetrahydrofuran (THF) and dried to obtain a solid sample. The solid copper salt is dissolved in THF to obtain a copper salt solution, the obtained solid sample is added to the copper salt solution, stirred well, centrifuged, washed with THF.
   USE - Method for preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, which ensures ultra-high selectivity, increases methanol yield, has strong thermal stability, which avoids high reaction temperature in the CO2 conversion process leading to the deactivation of the catalyst by carbon deposition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, involves adding graphen oxide (GO) to dimethylformamide (DMF) and sonicating for 6 hours to obtain GO mixture, weighing zirconium salt, 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-dicarboxylic acid and formic acid and adding to the GO mixture and stirring to obtain a mixture of GO and UiO-bpy. The molar ratio of zirconium salt, 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-dicarboxylic acid, formic acid and DMF is 0.106:0.104:0.018:0.145. The reaction solution is reacted at 100-160 degrees C for 12-36 hours. The collected solid phase is washed with a mixed solvent of DMF and tetrahydrofuran (THF) and dried to obtain a solid sample. The solid copper salt is dissolved in THF to obtain a copper salt solution, the obtained solid sample is added to the copper salt solution, stirred well, centrifuged, washed with THF, and placed the resulting precipitate in THF and stored in an inert atmosphere, where copper salt solids account for 25-75 wt.% of the solid sample. The zinc salt solid is dissolved in hexane to obtain a zinc salt solution. The zinc salt solution is added to the precipitate, THF is added, stirred well, centrifuged, washed with THF, and dried obtained solid sample stored in an inert atmosphere, where the zinc salt solids account for 25-60 wt.% of the solid sample. The solid sample is placed in a reducing atmosphere, and reduced in situ at 200-260 degrees C to obtain a catalyst for carbon dioxide to methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zirconium salt is one of zirconium nitrate, zirconium chloride and zirconium sulfate. The solid copper salt is one of copper nitrate, copper chloride dihydrate and copper sulfate. The zinc salt is one of zinc nitrate hexahydrate, diethyl zinc and zinc sulfate. The reducing atmosphere is H2 or a mixed gas of H2 and CO2.

PN FR3118851-A1; WO2022157096-A1
TI Method for raising animals such as poultry or            pigs, involves injecting water for watering, carrying            out injections simultaneously in water, one of carbon            dioxide and one of oxygen, and injecting mixture of            oxygen and carbon dioxide into water.
AU IBARRA D
   CAMPO P
   COUSIN F
   LE DRU P
AE AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202293764J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying water to the                animals for their watering. The water for watering                is injected before water does not reach the                animals, oxygen and carbon dioxide in one of the                following ways such as two injections are carried                out simultaneously in the water, one of carbon                dioxide and one of oxygen, and a mixture of oxygen                and carbon dioxide is injected into the water. The                injection of oxygen desorbs the carbon dioxide when                carbon dioxide is injected then oxygen. An                injection of carbon dioxide intervening after an                oxygen injection, when it goes beyond carbon                dioxide, desorbs oxygen. The carbonates are                transformed into bicarbonates, soluble and not                encrusting.
   USE - Method for raising animals such as poultry,                pigs and rabbits by doping oxygen in poultry                drinking water with limescale control.
   ADVANTAGE - The solution solves the phenomenon of the                formation of limescale deposits. The formation of                biofilm is limited in the water supply network of                the breeding. The method consists in enriching the                water with carbon dioxide by injecting a mixture                oxygen/ carbon dioxide instead of just injecting                oxygen. The carbonates are transformed into                bicarbonates, soluble and not encrusting. The                injection of a 1% carbon dioxide in oxygen allows                the equilibrium to maintain approximately 17 mg/l                of carbon dioxide dissolved and avoids the                formation of scale deposits. The pH is measured to                optimize the injection to obtain a level of carbon                dioxide dissolved in accordance with the intended                purpose to avoid the formation of scale deposits,                for precise control of the injection of carbon                dioxide when the injection of carbon dioxide is                carried out separately from the injection of                oxygen. The reduction of the phenomenon of                limestone formation, and a great simplicity of                implementation are made. The supply of bicarbonates                is advantageous to facilitate the digestion of the                animals. The co-injection of oxygen and carbon                dioxide is made for higher levels of carbon dioxide                dissolved, to benefit from the combined effects of                water rich in oxygen and carbon dioxide for animal                feed. The gas is simply injected to achieve an                optimum result. The existence of a synergy at the                level of the yield of the transfers by combining                oxygen and carbon dioxide, bringing more than a                simple addition of the effects of one and the other                of the gases. The sufficient contact time achieves                dissolution. The injection in a retention tank                often present in the type of breeding, or even in                the technical room for water treatment present on                such facilities is carried out.

TI Method of electrochemically utilizing carbon dioxide, involves reacting carbon dioxide to first product gas at cathode, and letting out liquid electrolyte and first product from carbon dioxide electrolysis cell through first output opening.
AU BALDAUF M
   FERNANDEZ S E M
   HANEBUTH M
   KRAUSE R
   LANDES H
   RELLER C

   FERNANDEZ SANCHIS E M
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2019135805
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a carbon dioxide electrolysis cell (3) for reducing carbon dioxide to a first product gas. A first cathode compartment has a first input port for supplying the liquid electrolyte (KL) comprising bicarbonate. A second cathode compartment (23) has a first outlet opening for discharging the liquid electrolyte comprising a first product and hydrogencarbon. The liquid electrolyte is passed into the first cathode compartment through the first input port. The liquid electrolyte comprises salt and bicarbonate. The bicarbonate is converted to carbon dioxide in the first cathode compartment. The carbon dioxide is reacted to first product gas at a cathode. The carbon dioxide and the first product gas flows through the cathode in direct current. The liquid electrolyte and the first product are let out of the carbon dioxide electrolysis cell through the first output opening.
   USE - Method of electrochemically utilizing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method of operating a carbon dioxide electrolyzer and a carbon dioxide electrolyzer provides high efficiency electrolysis. The catholyte is advantageously chosen so that its buffer capacity is sufficient to trap all protons before they reach the cathode. The very large portion of the volume of the porous cathode is used for the reaction, which is advantageous in increasing the efficiency of the electrolysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for the electrochemical use of carbon dioxide.

TI Calculating the amount of brine pre-extraction and            re-injection that maximizes carbon dioxide            sequestration efficiency involves calculating the brine            pre-extraction volume of the bottom brine layer, then            the brine extraction is continuing for a period of            time.

   CHEN S
   WANG W
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2022B9066T
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating the amount of brine pre-extraction                and re-injection that maximizes carbon dioxide                (CO2) sequestration efficiency involves calculating                the brine pre-extraction volume of the bottom brine                layer, then the brine extraction is continuing for                a period of time, but the brine extraction volume                during this period is not included in the brine                pre-extraction volume. The allowable value of the                collapse pressure of the bottom brine layer is                determined. The pore pressure drop of the bottom                brine layer caused by the pre-extraction of brine                is determined. The brine reinjection volume of the                top saline aquifer is calculated, then determined                the allowable value of the rupture pressure of the                top saline aquifer. The pore pressure increase of                the top saline layer caused by the brine                reinjection is determined.
   USE - Method for calculating the amount of brine                pre-extraction and re-injection that maximizes                carbon dioxide sequestration efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maximizes carbon dioxide (CO2)                sealing efficiency of salt water pre-extraction                amount and back-injection amount calculation                method, and improves sealing ability and sealing                safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calculating the amount of brine pre-extraction                and re-injection that maximizes carbon dioxide                (CO2) sequestration efficiency involves calculating                the brine pre-extraction volume of the bottom brine                layer, then the brine extraction is continue for a                period of time, but the brine extraction volume                during this period is not included in the brine                pre-extraction volume. The allowable value of the                collapse pressure of the bottom brine layer is                determined. The pore pressure drop of the bottom                brine layer caused by the pre-extraction of brine                is determined. The brine reinjection volume of the                top saline aquifer is calculated, then determined                the allowable value of the rupture pressure of the                top saline aquifer. The pore pressure increase of                the top saline layer caused by the brine                reinjection is determined, and when CO2 injection                and brine extraction occur simultaneously in the                bottom brine layer, established the relationship                between CO2 injection rate and brine extraction                rate, and when the CO2 injection of the bottom                brine layer and the brine reinjection of the top                brine layer occur simultaneously, established the                relationship between the CO2 injection rate and the                brine reinjection rate.

TI Air conditioner for detecting fault of carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor, has controller that judges whether first and second CO2 sensors include fault based on difference between first CO2 concentration and second CO2 concentration and preset threshold.
AU GAO X
AE HISENSE SHANDONG AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (HISG-C)
GA 2022323037
AB    NOVELTY - The conditioner has an outdoor heat exchanger and an indoor heat exchanger (103). One exchanger is a condenser to work. Other exchanger is an evaporator to work. A four-way valve (104) is provided for controlling a cooling medium flow in a cooling medium loop (101), so that the heat exchangers are switched between a condenser and an evaporator. An indoor environment temperature sensor (105) detects indoor environment temperature. An indoor coil temperature sensor (106) detects the indoor coil temperature. A first CO2 sensor (107) is provided for obtaining a first CO2 concentration and sending the first CO2 concentration to a controller (109). A second CO2 sensor (108) obtains a second CO2 concentration and sends the second CO2 concentration to the controller. A controller judges whether the first and second CO2 sensors include a fault based on a difference between the first CO2 concentration and the second CO2 concentration and a preset threshold.
   USE - Air conditioner for detecting fault of carbon dioxide sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide sensor fault detection method is provided to quickly detect whether the carbon dioxide sensors of the fresh air-conditioner includes fault, so as to improve the accuracy of the new air conditioner to carbon dioxide concentration detection, thus, improving the user experience of the user. The CO2 sensor is set on the remote controller, so that the CO2 sensors are set at the air inlet of the air conditioning unit, thus, reducing the size of the charging pile, and hence reducing the cost of the charge pile. The controller judges whether the first and second CO2 concentrations are greater than or equal to the preset threshold value if the difference between the first CO2 concentration and the second CO 2 concentration is equal to or greater than the predetermined value, thus, preventing the operation of the operation from being stopped.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for detecting fault of CO sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an air conditioner. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113577126-A
TI Preparing ointment containing Acronychia pedunculata essential oil, comprises e.g. crushing dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices, sieving, adding into areaction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extractor, adding molten rosin, ethanol and raw material powder, and mixing.
AU LIU H
AE HAINAN CHANGYONGHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D12934
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ointment containing Acronychia pedunculata essential oil, comprises e.g. (i) crushing the dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices, and sieving to obtain raw material powder, (ii) adding the raw material powder into a reaction kettle of a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extractor, first adding molten rosin, ethanol and raw material powder, mixing them with static extraction, and performing dynamic extraction of carbon dioxide with ethanol as the entrainer to obtain an extract, (iii) dispersing the extract with warm water, and extracting with ethyl acetate reflux to obtain an extract, (iv) adding the extract to warm water and uniformly stirring, sequentially adding ethanol, potassium sorbate and polydimethylsilane to it, and stirring to obtain an ointment, where the dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices are obtained from the harvested fragrant root slices and dried, and the stems are subjected to electrical stimulation before being harvested.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ointment containing Acronychia pedunculata essential oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ointment: can form a film in three minutes; can prevent the active substances of the ointment from evaporating; improves the utilization rate of the active ingredients; utilizes rosin in the extraction process that can significantly increase the extraction rate of effective substances; and reduces the raw material purchase costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ointment containing Acronychia pedunculata essential oil, comprises (i) crushing the dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices, and sieving to obtain raw material powder, (ii) adding the raw material powder into a reaction kettle of a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extractor, first adding molten rosin, ethanol and raw material powder, mixing them with static extraction, and performing dynamic extraction of carbon dioxide with ethanol as the entrainer to obtain an extract, (iii) dispersing the extract with warm water, and extracting with ethyl acetate reflux to obtain an extract, (iv) adding the extract to warm water and uniformly stirring, sequentially adding ethanol, potassium sorbate and polydimethylsilane to it, and stirring to obtain an ointment, where the dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices are obtained from the harvested Acronychia pedunculata fragrant root slices and dried, and the stems of the Acronychia pedunculata fragrant are subjected to electrical stimulation before being harvested.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for ointment containing Acronychia pedunculata essential oil, prepared by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the amount of dried Acronychia pedunculata fragrant slices is 1 kg. In the step (ii), the amount of rosin is 5-10 g. In the step (iv), the amount of ethanol is 10-15 g. In the step (iv), the dosage of potassium sorbate is 5-10 g. In the step (iv), the amount of polydimethylsilane is 40-60 g. In the step (ii), the amount of rosin is 8 g, and the amount of ethanol in the step (iv) is 11 g. In the step (iv), the amount of potassium sorbate is 7 g. In the step (iv), the amount of polydimethylsilane is 50 g.Preferred Method: The static extraction conditions are 25 MPa, 40degrees Celsius for 40 minutes. The dynamic extraction conditions are: carbon dioxide flow rate of 4 l/minute, ethanol flow rate 5 ml/minute, and extraction at 40degrees Celsius for 30 minutes. The number of extractions with ethyl acetate reflux is 5 times.

PT P
PN CN111847486-A; CN111847486-B
TI Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by pre-calcining metal lithium slag, calcining, dissolving in aqueous solution to obtain suspension, filtering, recycling, removing soluble impurities, passing carbon dioxide, filtering and heating.
AU WU Z
   HAO J
   GUO X
   TANG K
   ZHONG B
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020A9485K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by metal lithium slag, involves (a) pre-calcining the metal lithium slag in air, or oxygen atmosphere in a horizontal tube furnace, (b) placing the pre-calcined metal slag in a horizontal tube furnace, passing carbon dioxide gas, and calcining, (c) dissolving the calcined lithium metal residue in an aqueous solution to obtain a suspension, filtering the suspension, washing the resulting filter residue by deionized water, recycling the filtrate, dissolving the calcined lithium metal residue, filtering, washing, and removing soluble impurities such as sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate, (d) re-dispersing the filter residue obtained in step (c) in water solution, passing in excess carbon dioxide, and filtering to remove other insoluble substances, and (e) heating the filtrate obtained in the previous step at 75-95 degrees C, and decomposing in a stirring tank to obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by metal lithium slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly prepares the lithium slag through calcination and other processes to obtain battery-grade lithium carbonate, avoids the direct digestion reaction between metal lithium and water, and has a short process and high safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by metal lithium slag, involves (a) pre-calcining the metal lithium slag in air, or oxygen atmosphere in a horizontal tube furnace at calcination temperature of 250-300 degrees C for 2-5 hours, (b) placing the pre-calcined metal slag in a horizontal tube furnace, passing carbon dioxide gas, and calcining at 500-800 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (c) dissolving the calcined lithium metal residue in an aqueous solution to obtain a suspension, filtering the suspension, washing the resulting filter residue by deionized water, recycling the filtrate, dissolving the calcined lithium metal residue, filtering, washing, and removing soluble impurities such as sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate, (d) re-dispersing the filter residue obtained in step (c) in water solution, passing in excess carbon dioxide, and filtering to remove other insoluble substances, and (e) heating the filtrate obtained in the previous step at 75-95 degrees C, and decomposing in a stirring tank to obtain lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the pre-calcined metal slag is mixed with inorganic carbon and calcined in a tube furnace.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

PN CN110628616-A
TI Reactor system comprises centrifuge tube rack,            centrifuge tube and oscillating carbon dioxide            incubator, centrifuge tube is inserted into centrifuge            tube rack, centrifuge tube rack is mounted on tray in            oscillating carbon dioxide incubator.
AU NIE J
   BAI Z
   SUN Y
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 202004602W
AB    NOVELTY - Reactor system comprises a centrifuge tube                rack (2), a centrifuge tube and an oscillating                carbon dioxide incubator, and the centrifuge tube                is inserted into the centrifuge tube rack, and the                centrifuge tube rack is mounted on a tray in the                oscillating carbon dioxide incubator (3). The                centrifuge tube (1) includes a tube body and a                cover. A carrier is fixed to the lower portion of                the inner cavity of the tube body. The cover is                provided with a ventilation hole and a filter film                is built in, and the filter film encapsulates the                ventilation hole.
   USE - Reactor system for the high-throughput                development of process conditions for the                production of cell-derived porcine pseudorabies                vaccine, where the seed cells of the cell-derived                porcine pseudorabies vaccine are adherent cells                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for using reactor system, which comprises:
   (A) inoculating seed cells are separately in                centrifuge tubes with cell culture medium for cell                culture, at different stages of preset growth time,                then discard the cell culture medium in the                centrifuge tube, add virus maintenance medium, and                inoculate the virus, and determine the optimal time                for inoculation according to the virus titer;
   (B) seeding seed cells individually into                centrifuge tubes with cell culture medium,                performing cell culture, and at the optimal time                for inoculation, discarding the cell culture medium                in the centrifuge tube and adding virus maintenance                medium;
   (C) carrying out virus inoculation at                different doses, determining the optimal dose                according to virus titer;
   (D) seeding the seed cells individually seeded                in a centrifuge tube encapsulated with a cell                culture medium, and performing cell culture at the                optimal time for inoculation, and discarding the                cell culture medium in the centrifuge tube and                adding virus maintenance medium;
   (E) performing the inoculation according to                the optimal inoculation dose, and harvesting the                cell maintenance medium in the centrifuge tube by a                full harvest process, a half harvest process, and a                full harvest process; and
   (F) calculating the vaccine harvest dose based                on the volume of the cell maintenance medium and                the virus titer, and determining the vaccine                harvest dose.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                reactor system.
   Centrifuge tube (1)
   Centrifuge tube rack (2)
   Oscillating carbon dioxide incubator                (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The seed cells include                  Vero cells, CHO cells, and pig testis ( ST)                  cells, PK-15 (pig kidney) cells.

AB    NOVELTY - A gel based test paper comprises high-range gel based test paper and low-range gel based test paper, where the high-range gel based test paper and low-range gel based test paper mainly contain sodium hydroxide (base) and acid-base indicator, the high-range gel based test paper is utilized for detecting compost sample with carbon dioxide respiration amount of 1-50 mg within 30 minutes per unit time, the high-range gel based test paper comprises 1-2% agarose gel as detection medium and 100 g gel comprises 5-30 g sodium hydroxide (base), 0.1-1 g alizarin yellow R (indicator), 0.1-1 g thymol blue (indicator) and 2-6 g glycerol.

PT P
PN CN108239704-A; CN108239704-B
TI Producing alumina by secondary aluminum ash recycling by introducing aluminum ash slag and sodium carbonate into high-temperature calciner, insulating calciner with oxygen, removing aluminum melt and collecting carbon dioxide gas.
AU DU J
   MA Y
   WEN Y
   ZHANG M
   HE K
AE MEP SOUTH CHINA ENVIRONMENTAL SCI INST (HNES-C)
   SOUTH CHINA INST ENVIRONMENTAL SCI (HNES-C)
GA 2018548088
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing alumina by secondary aluminum ash recycling involves (a) simultaneously introducing aluminum ash slag and sodium carbonate into a high-temperature calciner through a feed port, insulating the high-temperature calciner with oxygen to form an aluminum solution, carbon dioxide gas, secondary aluminum ash and sodium oxide, removing aluminum melt and preparing into an aluminum ingot, collecting carbon dioxide gas into carbon dioxide collector through a gas recovery pipe and transferring the cooled secondary aluminum ash and sodium oxide to a wet mill diluter via a transmission track, and (b) adding water, starting wet grinding diluter to wet the internal secondary aluminum ash and sodium oxide to form a slurry, adding water to carry out dilution treatment until the concentration of sodium hydroxide is 30-40%, transferring suspension to a reactor through a transfer track, reacting and filtering red precipitated mud to obtain a crude liquid.
   USE - Method is useful for producing alumina by secondary aluminum ash recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mass ratio of aluminum ash to sodium carbonate in the step (a) is 2:1. In the step (b), the insulating step is carried out at 520-550 degrees C for 45 minutes. The slurry in the step (b) has a particle size of 120-150 mu m. In the step (b), the reacting step is carried out at a temperature of 200-250 degrees C and a gas pressure of 30-40 atmospheres for 0.5-1 hour. The method involves (c) introducing crude liquid into low-temperature agitator through a conveying track, starting the low-temperature agitator at a temperature of 80-90 degrees C and a speed of 80-120 rpm 5-10 minutes, pouring out the semen from low-temperature agitator, transferring the semen to the high-temperature calciner through a conveying track, hydrolyzing sodium aluminate after being slowly cooled to obtain white aluminum hydroxide solid, controlling the operation of the high-temperature baking furnace at a temperature of 1000-1100 degrees C for 1-2 hours and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the calciner, and (d) connecting top end of the high-temperature calciner to a wet mill diluter through the condensation tube.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC E34-C01; M25-A02; M25-E02; M25-G01; M25-G04; M25-H; M25-J

TI Carbon dioxide-based biodegradable multi-copolymer            post-treatment process, comprises e.g. stirring carbon            dioxide-based multi-component copolymer and their            impurities, dissolving with e.g. acetone, stirring with            alcohol, alkane and/or ether, extruding, and performing            devolatilization.

   LI H
AE SHANDONG LIANXIN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022947492
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based biodegradable                multi-copolymer post-treatment process, comprises                (1) adding glue solution in a common dissolving                tank, stirring at a rotating speed of 60-100                revolutions per minute, slowly adding an organic                solvent to dissolve, stirring and dissolving at                certain temperature for 1-10 minutes to obtain                mixed solution, (2) transferring the mixed solution                into a precipitation device, stirring at a rotating                speed of 60-100 revolutions per minute, adding                precipitation agent, and again continue to stir for                3-10 minutes and allowing to stand for 5-30 minutes                to obtain supernatant, and (3) pumping out the                supernatant into the recovery system with a                pipeline to obtain lower solid phase, extruding the                lower solid phase into a devolatilizer for                devolatilization by screw extrusion, performing                devolatilization at certain temperature and under                vacuum for 0.1-2 hours, and discharging to obtain                final product.
   USE - The method is useful for purification of                carbon dioxide-based biodegradable binary and above                copolymers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes 1,2-dichloroethane                organic solvent and absolute ethanol as the                synergistic effect of the precipitation agent,                which improves the removal rate of impurities e.g.                catalysts and by-products, improves the quality of                polymer products, and ensures transparent resin                polymers; and utilizes glue solution and organic                solvent in a weight ratio of 1:0.4-1 and glue                solution and precipitation agent in weight ratio of                1:0.4-1.5, which improves the impurity removal rate                and avoids loss of small molecular polymer in the                product, improves the product yield, and increases                the weight proportion of propylene carbonate in the                polymer.

The comonomer in the                  carbon dioxide-based multi-component copolymer                  includes carbon dioxide, epoxy compounds, acid                  anhydride compounds and/or ester compounds. The                  epoxy compounds are alkylene oxides including                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide,                  cyclohexane oxide, epichlorohydrin, and/or                  pentoxide oxide. The dipole moment of the organic                  solvent is 3-5D, the organic solvent includes                  methylene chloride, 1,2-dichloroethane,                  1,2-dichloropropane, 1,3-dichloropropene , ethyl                  acetate, methyl acetate, acetone and/or                  cyclohexanone. The precipitation agent includes                  alcohols, alkanes and/or ethers. The weight ratio                  of glue solution and organic solvent is 1:0.2-2                  and the weight ratio of glue solution and                  precipitation agent is 1:0.2-3.

PT P
PN CN113750769-A
TI Purification method of sintering flue gas by using LF refining waste residue, involves cooling discharged portion of LF refining slag hot water, transporting to soaking water tank for soaking, and putting water tank solution into flat flow sedimentation tank.
AU HAN P
   NIU S
   YU F
   GU F
   YAN W
AE TIANJIN XINTIANGANG STEEL GROUP CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202202801V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling a discharged portion of a LF refining wastewater hot water to less than 300degreesC and transporting to the soaking water tank for soaking. The water tank solution is placed into the flat flow sedimentation tank when the pH value of the soaking pool solution is greater than 12.5. The precipitated clear lime water is transported to the smoke discharging pipeline by the water pump. The calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) reacts with carbon dioxide (CO2) in the flue gas to generate calcium carbonate (CaCO3) precipitate and calcium bicarbonate (Ca(HCO3) 2) solution. The mixed solution and the precipitate are returned to a soaking pool, and continued to react with the mixed carbonic acid (H2CO3) and Ca(HCO3)2 to the water tank to generate the CaCO3 precipitate. The LF refining waste residue and generated CaCO3 are taken out, the water is drained and sent to a steel slag micro powder workshop to grind into a steel slag micro powder.
   USE - Purification method of sintering flue gas by using LF refining waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the emission of greenhouse gas, and the carbon dioxide emissions can be reduced by about 2 million tons throughout the year based on the annual output of 1 billion tons of LF refined steel. The CO2 in the flue gas is captured and fixed in the stable CaCO3 form by soaking the LF refining waste residue, thus realizing the capture and fixation of the carbon dioxide in the sintering smoke gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the process of using LF refining waste residue to purify sintering flue gas and solidify carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AE HUALU ENG & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 202204907H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas comprises (i) sending                the electric energy generated by power generation                system to the electrolytic water hydrogen                production device to obtain hydrogen and oxygen,                (ii) dividing generated hydrogen into three                strands, (iii) sending a strand to the hydrogen                storage device for storage, and sending another                strand to reverse conversion device, (iv) sending a                remaining strand to an outlet of the brine                electrolysis device, (v) carrying out inverse                conversion reaction on hydrogen from the water                electrolysis hydrogen production device and carbon                dioxide gas from purification device in the inverse                conversion device to obtain water and carbon                monoxide, and (vi) mixing hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and water from the inverse                conversion device with the hydrogen from the water                electrolysis hydrogen production device and                purified gas from the purification device to obtain                synthesis gas, and sending the synthesis gas to                downstream synthesis device as raw material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis                gas for realizing zero carbon emission by utilizing                electric energy combined inverse conversion                reaction, and also used in preparation of methanol,                ammonia, dimethyl ether, olefin, oil and other                chemical products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of large carbon                dioxide emission in the traditional coal gas                production process, improves utilization rate of                the carbon element in the raw material, and                realizes zero-carbon discharge production of coal                chemical industry.

PT P
PN WO2021191213-A1; AT17374-U1
TI Production of ceramic goods, e.g. masonry bricks, involves molding unfired blank(s) for ceramic goods from starting materials.
AU KURKA A
   SARVAS O
AE WIENERBERGER AG (WIEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B3672D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves molding unfired blank for the ceramic goods (11, 12, 13) from starting materials (2) in a moulding step (1). The unfired blank used as an unfired brick is pressed under a pressure of 40 MPa in a pressing step (3). The starting materials of the unfired blank are grounded. The aggregates (5) are added to the starting materials of the unfired blank before the molding step. The aggregates comprise biogenic materials, such as organic carbon, glass and / or melt treatment agents.
   USE - Method for producing ceramic goods such as brick, for e.g. masonry brick (all claimed) and load-bearing brick for construction of buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The ratio of the compressive strength of the ceramic goods with respect to the carbon dioxide release during the manufacture of ceramic goods is greatly improved. Because the unfired blank is pressed with a pressure of greater than or equal to 40 MPa in a pressing step following the molding step, the overall compressive force of the blank is increased. This can be used to shape the blank into an unfired ceramic product. The ceramic goods can be fired, either at low firing temperatures, in order to reduce the environmental pollution caused by carbon dioxide, or at high temperatures, as a result of which a particularly high-compressive strength is achieved. The environmental impact of carbon dioxide during the manufacturing process can be greatly reduced. The energy saved in the subsequent manufacturing process ultimately results in a positive energy balance and thus less carbon dioxide is released.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ceramic goods.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flow diagram illustrating the process for producing ceramic goods.
   Step for performing molding step (1)
   Starting material (2)
   Step for performing pressing step (3)
   Aggregate (5)
   Ceramic goods (11, 12, 13)
