PT P
PN CN113443645-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises e.g.            preparing calcium hydroxide, carbonizing by adding            dextrorotatory-sodium gluconate to calcium hydroxide            solution and continuously introducing carbon dioxide            gas, filtering nano calcium carbonate suspension,            washing, grinding and sieving.
AU WANG X
   CHEN X
   WANG B
   YANG B
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 2021B6094D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                preparing calcium hydroxide solution including                taking a quantitative amount of calcium oxide                powder, mixing with deionized water, completely                stirring for 3 hours and aging at constant                temperature of 60degrees Celsius for 12 hours, (ii)                carbonizing including adding a preset amount of                dextrorotatory (D)-sodium gluconate to the obtained                calcium hydroxide solution, continuously                introducing carbon dioxide gas in an intermittent                manner under stirring conditions, stopping the                introduction of carbon dioxide gas until the                calcium ion in calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide                have completely reacted to obtain a nano calcium                carbonate suspension, and (iii) finishing product                including filtering nano calcium carbonate                suspension, washing and drying at a constant                temperature of 60degrees Celsius, grinding and                sieving with 100 meshes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the sugars as crystal                form control agents and lime emulsion carbonization                method to prepare spherical nano-calcium carbonate                with uniform morphology; modifies the prepared                product; optimizes the modification process                conditions; reduces the oil absorption of nano                calcium carbonate through modification; and                improves the lipophilic and hydrophobic properties                of the particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                modifying the nano calcium carbonate prepared by                above mentioned method, comprising (1) taking a                quantitative amount of calcium carbonate powder and                mixing with deionized water to form 8 wt.% calcium                carbonate suspension, (2) adding a quantitative                modifier to the calcium carbonate suspension                obtained in the step (1), and stirring and                modifying under the heating condition of water                bath, and (3) filtering the modified calcium                carbonate suspension obtained in the step (2),                drying at 70degrees Celsius and sieving with 100                meshes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  complete reaction between calcium ion and carbon                  dioxide is determined by the pH value of the                  calcium hydroxide solution reaching the end                  point. The pH endpoint value is obtained by                  creating a state in which calcium ion and carbon                  dioxide completely react with each other through                  a simulation test, and performing pH value                  measurement under this state. The simulation test                  is carried out by (a) establishing a control                  group including preparing calcium hydroxide                  solution with the same amount and concentration                  as step (i), and adding a moderate amount of                  sodium D-gluconate in step (ii) to the calcium                  hydroxide solution to obtain a control mixed                  solution, (b) simulating endpoint conditions                  including adding calcium carbonate powder that is                  consistent with the theoretical output of calcium                  carbonate when calcium ion and carbon dioxide                  completely react in step (ii) to the control                  mixed solution, and (c) measuring the pH final                  point value including stirring the control mixed                  solution containing calcium carbonate obtained in                  the step (b) at the same stirring rate as in step                  (ii), stopping stirring, statically measuring the                  pH value of the solution for multiple times, and                  taking the average value to obtain the pH final                  point value. In the step (ii), the introduction                  of the carbon dioxide gas adopts a staged                  intermittent introduction method, which is                  specifically includes (A) continuously                  introducing carbon dioxide gas into the calcium                  hydroxide solution under stirring conditions,                  always keeping the input of carbon dioxide before                  the end of the reaction, and input different                  amounts of carbon dioxide in an intermittent                  manner at different stages, and simultaneously                  carrying out the pH value dynamic monitoring                  during the process of carbon dioxide gas                  introduction, (B) changing the carbon dioxide gas                  to intermittent pulse input, when the pH value                  reaching the preset warning value before the pH                  end value, continuously stirring for a period of                  time during the carbon dioxide gas stop phase to                  make the calcium ion and carbon dioxide                  completely reacting, stopping stirring and                  performing pH value detection on the calcium                  hydroxide solution, and (C) repeating the step                  (B) until the pH value of the detected calcium                  hydroxide solution is equal to the pH end value.                  The setting of the preset pH value warning value                  includes taking the control mixture containing                  calcium carbonate obtained in the step (b), using                  the stirring rate consistent with step (ii) for                  stirring, carrying out the pH value dynamic                  monitoring of the control mixture for many times                  under stirring conditions, taking the maximum                  value +1 in the dynamic monitoring process to be                  the preset pH value warning value. The method                  further comprises combining the filtrate and                  washing water filtered obtained in the step (iii)                  to perform post-processing on the combined liquid                  including firstly introducing carbon dioxide gas                  into the combined solution, adding quantitative                  sodium hydroxide, filtering the combined liquid,                  filtering the filter residue, washing, drying at                  a constant temperature of 60degrees Celsius,                  recycling and reusing. In the step (ii), the                  carbonization is carried out at 50degrees                  Celsius. The stirring is carried out at 800                  revolutions/minute. The modification is carried                  out at 70degrees Celsius for 50 minutes. In the                  step (2), stirring and modifying is carried out                  by (a) performing pulsed gas stirring on the                  calcium carbonate suspension so that the calcium                  carbonate particles are coating with modifier                  molecules, (b) continuously performing the pulsed                  gas stirring for 50 minutes and stopping                  stirring, and (3) introducing an inert gas into                  the bottom of the calcium carbonate suspension to                  perform bubbling stirring of the calcium                  carbonate suspension until the calcium carbonate                  particles are completely covered by the modifier                  molecules. Preferred Components: In the step (i),                  the mass fraction of calcium(hydroxide)0 in the                  calcium hydroxide solution is 7 wt.%. In the step                  (ii), the mass fraction of the sodium D-gluconate                  in the calcium hydroxide solution is 1.5 wt.%.                  The flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 60                  ml/minute. The modifier comprises sodium dodecyl                  sulfonate. The mass fraction of the modifier in                  the calcium carbonate suspension is 3.5                  wt.%.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

TI Coupling device for automatic coupling of compressed gas container e.g. carbon dioxide gas cylinder, has drive device that guides pressurized gas container in preset orientation along longitudinal axis, so that releasable hermetic connection of pressurized gas container is enabled for adapter.
AU MURR M
   SOLDNER P
AE SIELAFF GMBH & CO AUTOMATENBAU (SIEL-Non-standard)
   SIELAFF GMBH & CO AUTOMATENBAU (SIEL-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6304E
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a connection adapter (3), and a receiving device (4) which is configured to receive a pressurized gas container (2) and to hold in a predetermined alignment with the connection adapter. A drive device (5) is configured to guide the pressurized gas container held in the predetermined orientation in the direction of the longitudinal axis (X) to the connection adapter, so that a releasable hermetic connection of the pressurized gas container is enabled for the connection adapter.
   USE - Coupling device for automatic coupling of compressed gas container e.g. carbon dioxide gas cylinder, for use in gas filling machine used for filling carbon dioxide gas cylinders used in soda makers. Can also be used for fluids e.g. carbon dioxide, flammable gases, industrial gases, compressed air or comparable fluids.
   ADVANTAGE - The entire jacket surface of the pressurized gas container is scanned in simple manner for identification of feature and visually checked for damage. The deviations due to production or thermal expansion are compensated and the unimpeded movement of the connection adapter in the longitudinal direction of the compressed gas container and precise measurement of the weight of the compressed gas container including the filled fluid is made possible. Both the connection adapter and the pressurized gas container or the receiving device are moved away from one another and thus decoupled particularly quickly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a machine for automatically filling pressurized gas container; and
   (2) a method for automatically filling pressurized gas container.

PN WO2021175605-A1
TI Composition used for treating and preventing a complication associated with surgery and/or in or prior to surgery that is expected to result in high blood loss, comprises oxygen and carbon dioxide.
AU STUDER M
   OKE S
   BOYLE R
   SCHIMMER B B
   SCHLAEPFER M
AE AVIATION WORKS LTD (AVIA-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZUERICH (UYZU-C)
GA 2021A37478
AB    NOVELTY - Composition for treating and preventing at least one complication associated with surgery and/or in, or prior to, surgery, where the person undergoing surgery is desirable to have a low blood pressure during the surgery, preferably the blood pressure is less than 90 mm mercury systolic, and the surgery is expected to result in high blood loss, and the surgery requires relaxed blood vessels, comprises 91-99 vol.%, preferably 93-97 vol.% oxygen and 1-9 vol.%, preferably 3-7 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The composition is useful: for treating and preventing at least one complication associated with surgery and/or in, or prior to, surgery, where the person undergoing surgery is desirable to have a low blood pressure during the surgery, preferably the blood pressure is less than 90 mm mercury systolic, and the surgery is expected to result in high blood loss, and the surgery requires relaxed blood vessels; treating and preventing at least one complication associated with apnoea expected during anaesthesia including oxygen deficiency e.g. limited blood flow or oxygen to the heart or brain and cerebral hypoxia, high blood pressure, diabetes, transient ischemic attack (TIA), stroke, heart attack and death and/or increasing apnoea tolerance (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: reduces perioperative risk; enables the patients to be pre-oxygenated with 100% oxygen to maximize their oxygen reserve and to allow longer period of apnoea to be tolerated without severe consequences (increasing apnoea tolerance), thus lowering the risk of hypoxia and brain damage; accelerates respiratory and heart rate; increases cardiac output; decreases ventilation-perfusion (V/Q) mismatch and serum lactate levels due to addition of carbon dioxide to the hypoxic gas mixture; comprises carbon dioxide, which maintains cognitive function in individuals performing complex tasks in hypoxic environments; and prevents irreparable damage of the brain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 95 vol.% oxygen, 5 vol.% carbon dioxide and 0-8 vol.% carrier gas comprising nitrogen, helium, xenon, argon and/or krypton. Preferred Method: The surgery is ophthalmic surgery, neurosurgery, vascular surgery (preferred), otorhinolaryngology surgery on the middle ear or bariatric surgery. The surgery involves arterial catheterisation, preferably cardiac angiogram, cardiac stent placement, rotablation and/or intra-arterial balloon tamponading. The composition is administered to the subject before and/or during surgery, preferably before both surgery and anaesthesia. The treatment is for people who are obese, preferably having a body mass index (BMI) of greater than 35 kg/m3, low functional residual lung capacity (FRC), preferably an FRC of less than 30 ml/kg when measured in the supine position. The composition is administered under normobaric conditions. The composition is administered under general anaesthesia, or before general anaesthesia, preferably before general anaesthesia. The composition is administered to the subject as a gas by inhalation.
   ACTIVITY - CNS-Gen; Cerebroprotective; Vasotropic; Hypotensive; Antidiabetic; Cardiant.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None Given.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details of biological studies that does not support the claims.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).)

TI Air purifier used in air conditioner used in indoor environment in building, has body, air inlet and air outlet openings, carbon dioxide absorbing unit, base dosing unit, acid dosing unit, electrodialysis unit, and water electrolysis unit.
AU SARICIFTCI N S
   YESILCUBUK A
   ER D
   YAVUZ P
   YALCIN T
AE ARCELIK AS (ARCK-C)
GA 202174391T
AB    NOVELTY - Air purifier (1) comprises a body (2); an air inlet opening (3) provided on the body; a fan (4) which enables the air to be directed through the air inlet opening into the body; an air outlet opening which enables the air purified in the body to be released out of the body; a carbon dioxide (CO2) absorbing unit (6) into which the air is sucked; a base dosing unit (8) and an acid dosing unit (7) which supply respectively a base solution and an acid solution into the CO2 absorbing unit; a CO2 tube (10) enabling the CO2 absorbed in the CO2 absorbing unit to be discharged from the environment where the air is purified; an electrodialysis unit (9) where the salt created in the CO2 absorbing unit is resolved back to acid and base; a condenser (12) which condenses the humidity in the environment; and a water electrolysis unit (11) where the water received from the condenser is electrolyzed, comprising an anode receptacle (13) and a cathode receptacle (14).
   USE - The air purifier is useful in air conditioner (claimed) used in an indoor environment in a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen gas released in the body is enabled to be combusted producing water and rendered harmless.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an air conditioner comprising the air purifier.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air purifier.
   Air purifier (1)

   Base dosing unit (8)
   Electrodialysis unit (9)
   Carbon dioxide tube (10)
   Water electrolysis unit (11)
   Condenser (12)

   Cathode receptacle (14)

GA 202174291F
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a second gas-liquid separator (10) whose bottom outlet pipeline is connected with the inlet of a heat-conducting oil pump (8). The outlet of the heat conducting oil pump is divided into two paths. One path is connected with the inlet of a second adjusting valve (17). The outlet pipeline of the second adjusting valve is connected with the cold side inlet of a starting heater (21). The other path is connected with the hot side inlet pipeline of a heat conducting oil cooler (9). The heat conducting oil outlet pipeline is connected with the outlet pipeline of the second adjusting valve after cooling by a heat conducting oil cooler. The hot side outlet of the starting heater is connected with the heat conducting oil inlet below a column pipe section housing of a single-pipe reactor (3). The heat-conducting oil outlet pipeline above the column pipe section housing of the single-pipe reactor is connected with the inlet of the second gas-liquid separator.
   USE - Single pipe test device for directly preparing Petrol fraction hydrocarbyl by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.

TI Method for producing product gas stream containing carbon monoxide, involves subjecting raw gas stream to vacuum pressure swing adsorption, which results in adsorption at adsorption pressure level and desorption at desorption pressure level.
AU LEITMAYR W
   PESCHEI A
   HENTSCHEL B
   FITZTHUM M
   PESCHEL A
AE LINDE GMBH (LIND-Non-standard)
GA 202138482Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves subjecting an insert (3) to a conversion (E) at a conversion pressure level so as to obtain raw gas stream (4) containing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The obtained raw gas stream is subjected to vacuum pressure swing adsorption (A), which results in adsorption at adsorption pressure level and desorption at sub-atmospheric desorption pressure level. Some of device portions operated in vacuum pressure swing adsorption at desorption pressure level are surrounded with shielding gas (1).
   USE - Method for producing product gas stream containing carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The product gas stream containing carbon monoxide is produced with high purity requirements without excessive additional structural effort even when using vacuum pressure swing adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for producing product gas stream (6) containing carbon monoxide, has a conversion unit that is set up to subject the insert to conversion at conversion pressure level while obtaining raw gas stream containing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. A vacuum pressure swing adsorption unit which is set up to transfer the raw gas stream to vacuum pressure swing adsorption while obtaining adsorbate stream (7) that is depleted in carbon monoxide and enriched in carbon dioxide and precursor stream (5) enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide. A casing device is set up to surround device portions operated in vacuum pressure swing adsorption at desorption pressure level with shielding gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show schematic view of the arrangement for producing product gas stream containing carbon monoxide at different levels.
   Shielding gas (1)
   Insert (3)
   Raw gas stream (4)
   Precursor stream (5)
   Product gas stream (6)
   Adsorbate stream (7)
   Vacuum pressure swing adsorption (A)
   Conversion (E)

PT P
PN CN112138720-A; CN112138720-B
TI Single atom catalyst system useful for preparing a            cobalt by electrochemical reduction of carbon            dioxide.
AU LIU L
   CHEN Z
   ZHANG X
   MU K
   JIAO M
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 202103066P
AB    NOVELTY - Single atom catalyst system expressed as                M-N4/CNH2 and M is a transition metal. The catalyst                system uses a single atom of transition metal M as                active component and each transition metal atom is                coordinated with four nitrogen and carbon as a                carrier. The transition metal atoms are uniformly                dispersed on the surface of the carbon support,                where the carbon carrier is modified to include an                amino group, is claimed.
   USE - The single atom catalyst system is useful for                preparing a cobalt by electrochemical reduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; avoids the traditional                synthesis method of complex acid washing step; and                does not reduces the selectivity of cobalt .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the single atom catalyst system                comprising (i) preparing transition metal precursor                solution and dimethyl imidazole solution separately                and then obtain transition metal atom-doped ZIF-8                precursor by precipitation method, (ii) pyrolyzing                the resultant precursor in an inert atmosphere at a                temperature of 850-1050 degrees C to obtain a                nitrogen-doped carbon support supported transition                metal single-atom catalyst system M-N4/C,(iii)                grinding and blending the carbonamide and resultant                M-N4/C in a mass ratio of 100-10:1 and performing                pyrolysis treatment at 350-550 degrees C in                ammonia, (iv) immersing the product obtained by                pyrolysis in the ethanol/ammonia mixed solution                with a volume ratio of 1-5:1,(v) transferring the                product obtained by impregnation to concentrated                ammonia water for hydrothermal reaction at 120-180                degrees C, where M represents transition metals                from nickel, iron and zinc; and
   (2) preparing a cobalt by electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide comprising (i)                supporting single atom catalyst system on carbon                cloth or carbon paper to obtain a gas diffusion                electrode and (ii) carrying out electrochemically                reducing reaction of carbon dioxide to cobalt in                the reaction electrolyzer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The based on total                  weight of single-atom catalyst system, the                  content of transition metal is 1-10 wt.%,                  preferably 5-10 wt.%, preferably, the transition                  metal M is nickel, iron and zinc, preferably, the                  content of the amino group is 1-5 wt.%, more                  preferably 3 wt.%. The transition metal precursor                  is nickel acetylacetonate, iron acetylacetonate                  and zinc acetate dehydrate, preferably, the                  solvent in the solution of transition metal                  precursor and dimethylimidazole is methanol, the                  molar ratio of metal precursor and                  dimethylimidazole is 1:4-10, preferably 1:8. The                  loading amount of single-atom catalyst system is                  1-5 mg cm2, preferably 1-4 mg cm2. Preferred                  Method: In the step (ii), the inert atmosphere is                  an argon atmosphere. The pyrolysis temperature is                  900-1050 degrees C, preferably 1000 degrees C.                  The flow rate of argon is 10-50 sccm, preferably                  20-30 sccm, the pyrolysis time is 1-5 hours,                  preferably 2-4 hours, the heating rate is 1-5                  degrees C min1, preferably 2-4 degrees C min1.In                  the step (iii), the mass ratio of carbonamide to                  the M-N4/C is 60-40:1, more preferably 50:1, the                  flow rate of ammonia gas is 5-30 sccm, the                  pyrolysis temperature is 400-500 degrees C. In                  the step (iv), the volume ratio of                  ethanol/ammonia is 2-3:1 and the immersion time                  is 12-48 hours, carrying out at room temperature                  and normal pressure, preferably, the electrolytic                  cell is a closed double-chamber H-shaped                  electrolytic cell or a flowing electrolytic cell,                  preferably, the electrolyte in the electrolytic                  cell is potassium bicarbonate or potassium                  hydroxide solution, the concentration is 0.1-2M,                  preferably 0.5-1.5Mm, preferably, the circulating                  flow rate of the electrolyte is 10-30 ml min1,                  preferably, the reference electrode is an                  silver/silver-chloride electrode and the counter                  electrode is a platinum sheet or foamed nickel                  electrode. The voltage applied to the                  electrolytic cell is a constant voltage and the                  electrolytic potential is (-0.3)to(-1.0v),                  preferably, the diaphragm between the double                  chambers of H-type electrolytic cell is a Nafion                  117 proton exchange membrane and the diaphragm of                  the flow electrolytic cell is an anion exchange                  membrane. In the step (v), the temperature of the                  hydrothermal reaction in ammonia is 140-160                  degrees C, preferably 150 degrees C and the                  reaction time is 6-24 hours.

TI Carbon dioxide trans-critical cold and hot comprehensive utilization system, has variable frequency compressor and intermediate temperature variable frequency compressor arranged in parallel, and gas cooler connected with heat exchanger.
AU DU L
   YIN X
   LIN L
   ZHOU D
   WU Z
AE PANASONIC REFRIGERATOR SYSTEM DALIAN CO (PANA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B54078
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a low temperature evaporator (1) connected with a low temperature regenerator (15) and connected with an air suction pipeline of a low temperature variable frequency compressor (1). A parallel variable frequency compressor (16) and an intermediate temperature variable frequency compressor (3) are arranged in parallel. A gas cooler (7) is connected with a gas heat exchanger (10) through a middle temperature system exhaust main pipeline (B). An inlet and an outlet of a middle temperature evaporator (12) are connected with a medium temperature regenerator (13). The medium temperature heat regenerator is connected with an air suction pipe of the intermediate temperature variable frequency compressor through a pipeline. The gas cooler is additionally provided with a cooling device (D).
   USE - Carbon dioxide trans-critical cold and hot comprehensive utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts low temperature, medium temperature, parallel compression three evaporation sections, so that load is comprehensively considered and reasonably configured the low temperature compressor and the middle temperature compressor. The system reduces compression ratio of a single machine head, power consumption of compression, and improves reliability and efficiency of carbon dioxide as refrigerant in middle and low temperature coexistence condition to improve feasibility and stability of hot season system operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide trans-critical cold and hot comprehensive utilization system.

AU LEE M
   BURTON E A
AE CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2018709068
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonating a concrete mix comprises combining                carbonated mix water with concrete materials to                produce a carbonated concrete mix.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonating the                concrete mix.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is performed in-situ or through a                separate, stand-alone process; produces concrete                with reduced permeability and a reduced degradation                rate, thus increasing the service life of the                concrete and structures made with the concrete;                reduces the amount of cement required to produce                concrete and the corresponding reduction in the                cost associated with cement in the concrete mix,                and thus the total cost of production of concrete                is reduced by adding carbon dioxide to the                concrete, and allows a cubic yard of concrete or                cubic meter of concrete to be produced at a lower                total cost; reduces the amount of cement needed                during concrete production and decreases energy                consumption for the production of concrete; allows                the addition of carbon dioxide to the concrete mix                and thus increases the ratio of water to cement                used to produce the concrete; improves the                consistency and workability of the freshly mixed                concrete; produces concrete with increased break                strength and increased break strength consistency                compared to concrete produced using known methods;                and allows concrete producers and users to                formulate more precise concrete mix designs for the                desired structural properties of the concrete                treated with carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   an apparatus for producing a carbonated                concrete mix comprising a system (2) for                carbonating water to be used in a concrete mix,                operably connected to a mixer (8) for mixing the                concrete mix; and
   a concrete composition comprising cement,                aggregate and carbonated water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flowchart diagrammatic                view of the system for applying carbon dioxide in a                concrete production process.
   2System
   4Storage container
   6Piping
   8Mixer
   10Tank isolation valve
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbonated mix water:                  is produced by injecting carbon dioxide into                  water to be used in the production of the                  concrete mix; comprises water that is naturally                  carbonated; and comprises effluent water from a                  direct hydrocarbon fired heater. The method                  further comprises: converting liquid carbon                  dioxide to solid and gaseous carbon dioxide and                  mixing the gaseous and solid carbon dioxide with                  the concrete mix; and adding a mixture to the                  concrete mix. The carbonated water provides                  carbon dioxide to the concrete mix at 1-27 pounds                  of carbon dioxide per cubic yard of concrete mix.                  The amount of carbon dioxide provided to the                  concrete mix by the carbonated mix water and by                  the solid and gaseous carbon dioxide is 1-27                  pounds of carbon dioxide per cubic yard of                  concrete mix. Preferred Composition: The                  composition further comprises other cementitious                  material besides cement, and comprises fly ash                  and/or blast furnace slag. Preferred Components:                  The concrete mix comprises hydraulic cement and                  aggregates, and further comprises other                  cementitious material besides cement. The other                  cementitious material comprises fly ash and/or                  blast furnace slag.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system for                  carbonating water comprises a system for                  injecting carbon dioxide into the water. The                  apparatus further comprises: a system for                  delivering solid and gaseous carbon dioxide to                  the concrete mix, where the system comprises a                  storage container (4) for liquid carbon dioxide                  operably connected to an injection assembly to                  convert the liquid carbon dioxide to solid and                  gaseous carbon dioxide and to direct the mixture                  of solid and gaseous carbon dioxide into the                  mixer; and a controller operably connected to the                  system for contacting the concrete with solid and                  gaseous carbon dioxide, where the controller                  comprises a system to monitor the amount of                  carbon dioxide delivered to the concrete mix and                  a system to stop delivery of carbon dioxide to                  the mixer when a predetermined amount of carbon                  dioxide has been delivered. The predetermined                  amount of carbon dioxide is 1-27 pounds of carbon                  dioxide per cubic yard of concrete mix in the                  mixer. The system to monitor the amount of carbon                  dioxide delivered to the concrete mix comprises a                  system to monitor weight of the storage                  container, a system to monitor mass of carbon                  dioxide delivered, a system to monitor time,                  and/or a manual application system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Extracting hydrocarbons from subterranean            formation, comprises injecting surfactant composition            having substituted triamine compounds into subterranean            formation, and collecting hydrocarbons displaced by            injected surfactant composition.
AU CUI L
   BOURREL M
   DUBOS F
   KLIMENKO A
   BREARD M
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTALENERGIES SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 201863775F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting hydrocarbons from a subterranean                formation, comprises: injecting a surfactant                composition into the subterranean formation; and                collecting hydrocarbons displaced by the injected                surfactant composition, where the surfactant                composition comprises at least one surfactant                compound of substituted triamine compounds (I) and                the total number of carbon atoms in (I) is                10-21.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                hydrocarbons from a subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the surfactant that                exhibits good chemical stability, good thermal                stability up to a temperature of at least 90                degrees C, 100 degrees C or 110 degrees C, low                adsorption on minerals, preferably carbonate                minerals present in the subterranean formation,                high solubility in carbon dioxide at high                temperature of more than 100 degrees C, high                solubility in water at high temperature of more                than 100 degrees C in pH of 3-7, and at a high                salinity of e.g. more than 200000 ppm and                satisfactory partitioning coefficient between water                and carbon dioxide, and is effectively generate                carbon dioxide/water emulsions as compared to                conventional surfactants at high temperature and                high salinity, thus achieving a larger and/or                quicker increase in apparent viscosity; and                delivers the surfactant deeper in the reservoir to                more efficiently generate an emulsion or foam,                while maintaining good injectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting hydrocarbons from a subterranean                formation, comprises: injecting a surfactant                composition into the subterranean formation; and                collecting hydrocarbons displaced by the injected                surfactant composition, where the surfactant                composition comprises at least one surfactant                compound of substituted triamine compounds of                formula (I) and the total number of carbon atoms in                (I) is 10-21.
   x = 0-3;
   R1-R4, R = H or alkyl; and
   A = alkylene group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                composition comprising liquid or supercritical                carbon dioxide and the surfactant compound
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous solution                  comprises inorganic salts comprising sodium                  chloride, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate and/or                  sodium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises: at                  least one step of injecting liquid or                  supercritical carbon dioxide into the                  subterranean formation; successive or alternating                  steps of injecting at least one aqueous solution                  and injecting liquid or supercritical carbon                  dioxide into the subterranean formation; steps of                  simultaneously injecting at least one aqueous                  solutions and injecting liquid or supercritical                  carbon dioxide into the subterranean formation;                  steps of injecting different aqueous solutions                  having different salinities; steps of                  simultaneously, successively or alternatively                  injecting liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide                  comprising (I), and brine, into the subterranean                  formation, where the salinity of the brine varies                  over time; steps of simultaneously, successively                  or alternatively injecting liquid or                  supercritical carbon dioxide and brine comprising                  (I), into the subterranean formation, where the                  salinity of the brine varies over time; and steps                  of simultaneously, successively or alternatively                  injecting liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide,                  brine, and an aqueous solution comprising (I),                  into the subterranean formation, where the                  salinity of the brine varies over time. The                  injecting step(s) are carried out via at least                  one injection well and the step(s) of collecting                  hydrocarbons are carried out via at least one                  production well. Preferred Composition: The                  composition is in the form of a liquid or                  supercritical carbon dioxide/water emulsion. The                  surfactant composition comprises a single                  surfactant compound of (I) or many surfactant                  compounds of (I). The surfactant composition                  comprises at least one additional surfactant                  which is not (I), preferably comprising cationic                  and/or non-ionic surfactants. Preferred                  Components: The total number of carbon atoms in                  surfactant compound of (I) is 12-20, preferably                  17-19. The single surfactant compound of (I)                  comprises                  N1-dodecyl-N3,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine,                  N1-dodecyl-N1,N3,N3-trimethylpropane-1,3-diamine,                  N1-(2,2-diethyloctyl)-N3,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine,                  N1-octyl-N3,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine,                  N1-decyl-N3,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine or                  N1-tetradecyl-N3,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine.                  The surfactant compound of (I) comprises                  500-50000 (preferably 1000-20000) ppm (w/v). The                  surfactant composition is an aqueous solution.                  The aqueous solution is a buffered aqueous                  solution. The surfactant composition comprises                  liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide. The                  surfactant of (I) is present in at least one of                  the aqueous solutions and/or is present in the                  liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide.
   Preferred Definitions:
   x = 0;
   A = 3C alkylene;
   R1 = methyl or H;
   R = H;
   R2 = H or methyl; and
   R3, R4 = methyl or H.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (D) has a data storage part (23) for storing electrical conductivity data, which is provided with a electrical conductivity value of alkaline developing solution with which melt photoresist concentration and absorption carbon dioxide levels of the developing solution are repeatedly used. A control part (21) i.e. computer, is equipped with a controlling valve (41) provided in a pipe line (81), which liquid-feeds replenishment liquid e.g. image development undiluted solution, liquid and pure water, replenished to the developing solution with a control part (31) emitting a control signal. A warning part (WD) emits a warning while deviating from management range to which electrical conductivity of the developing solution, the melt photoresist concentration and the absorption carbon dioxide levels are set.
   USE - Developing solution control apparatus for an image development process installation for controlling concentration of alkaline developing solution utilized for developing a photoresist film on a semiconductor and image development process of a circuit board in a liquid crystal panel.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus maintains a component, which has activity to image development effect in the developing solution, uniformly even when the developing solution becomes a type of the melt photoresist concentration and the absorption carbon dioxide levels so as to realize development, which maintains desired image development performance, desired line width and remaining-film thickness, thus monitoring density of the developing solution in a convenient manner.

GA 201807695T
AB    NOVELTY - A gas hydrate decomposition and reaction liquid recycling device comprises three-phase separator (14), gas hydrate decomposing unit (18), carbon dioxide collecting tank (21), absorption liquid regeneration unit (27), chemical absorbing tower (24), absorption liquid storage tank (30), slurry pump (16), back pressure valve (23), first corrosion resistant pump, second corrosion resistant pump, and third corrosion resistant pump. The input end of the three-phase separator is connected with a hydrate slurry inlet.
   USE - Gas hydrate decomposition and reaction liquid recycling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device solves hydrate decomposition heat supply problem, quickly discharges decomposed gas, reduces gas loss and possible occurrence of hydrate secondary generation, has good high temperature heat convection transfer, and improves decomposing efficiency of hydrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas hydrate decomposition and reaction liquid recycling device comprises three-phase separator (14), gas hydrate decomposing unit (18), carbon dioxide collecting tank (21), absorption liquid regeneration unit (27), chemical absorbing tower (24), absorption liquid storage tank (30), slurry pump (16), back pressure valve (23), first corrosion resistant pump, second corrosion resistant pump, and third corrosion resistant pump. The input end of the three-phase separator is connected with a hydrate slurry inlet. The first output port of the hydrate decomposition liquid is connected with gas hydrate decomposing unit output port. The chemical absorbing column is provided with a hydrogen output for outputting high purity hydrogen.

PN EP3366645-A1; WO2018158026-A1; AU2018229396-A1; CA3052094-A1; IN201947038208-A; CN110325476-A; US2020002273-A1; EP3589583-A1; BR112019017712-A2; EP3589583-B1; RU2019129728-A; IN377339-B; RU2764453-C2; US11236043-B2
TI Making ammonia and urea in ammonia-urea integrated plant comprises reforming hydrocarbon source forming make-up gas containing hydrogen and nitrogen and converted into ammonia after purification followed by treating in urea recovery section.
AU NETTUNO F
   RUGNONE L
   OSTUNI R
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE CO LTD (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 201867427T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia and urea in ammonia-urea integrated plant comprises: reforming hydrocarbon source obtaining make-up gas containing hydrogen and nitrogen, where make-up gas after purification is converted into ammonia, at least part of synthesized ammonia provides ammonia feed of urea synthesis process, urea synthesis process also receiving carbon dioxide feed, urea synthesis process comprises reaction of ammonia and carbon dioxide in urea synthesis section to form urea aqueous solution, and subsequent treatment of solution in urea recovery section, purification of make-up gas comprises removal of carbon dioxide by means of first step of carbon dioxide removal and second step of carbon dioxide removal, which are carried out in series or in parallel, one of first and second carbon dioxide removal steps comprises washing carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas with carbamate solution taken from urea recovery section.
   USE - The process is useful for producing ammonia and urea in ammonia-urea integrated plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The ammonia section operates during startup or temporary shutdown of the urea section, e.g. for maintenance. The capacity of a ammoniaurea plant is increased the amount of ammonia makeup gas may exceed the capacity of the available carbon dioxide removal section, usually carbon dioxide absorption section. The process provides additional amount of syngas that can be treated for carbon dioxide removal by washing with some carbamate solution withdrawn from the urea recovery section. The carbamate washing stage is also less expensive than a conventional absorption section since it does not need a regeneration section. The preliminary compression of the carbon dioxide-containing gas pressurized carbon dioxide removal is the increase of the carbon dioxide partial pressure in the CDR section and, consequently, a more efficient separation of carbon dioxide and a reduced size of the related equipment. The arrangement of two ammonia synthesis loops is that the capacity can be increased while the size of the ammonia reactors can be contained to within limits of fabrication. The process allows the achievement of a large capacity, even beyond 3'000 MTD, in a cost-effective manner and overcomes the major bottlenecks of the main syngas compressor, the CDR section and the synthesis loop.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing ammonia and urea in ammonia-urea integrated plant comprises: reforming hydrocarbon source obtaining make-up gas containing hydrogen and nitrogen, where make-up gas after purification is converted into ammonia, at least part of synthesized ammonia provides ammonia feed of urea synthesis process, urea synthesis process also receiving carbon dioxide feed, urea synthesis process comprises reaction of ammonia and carbon dioxide in urea synthesis section to form urea aqueous solution, and subsequent treatment of solution in urea recovery section, purification of make-up gas comprises removal of carbon dioxide by means of first step of carbon dioxide removal and second step of carbon dioxide removal, which are carried out in series or in parallel, one of first and second carbon dioxide removal steps comprises washing carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas with carbamate solution taken from urea recovery section, and carbon dioxide feed of urea synthesis process comprises at least part of carbon dioxide separated from carbon dioxide removal steps. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) ammonia-urea integrated plant comprising ammonia section and urea section, where the ammonia section comprises reforming section for conversion of a hydrocarbon source into a raw make-up gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, purification section of the raw make-up gas including carbon dioxide-removal section, high-pressure ammonia synthesis loop for conversion of the purified make-up gas into ammonia, a urea section comprising a urea synthesis section where carbon dioxide and ammonia react to form an aqueous solution of urea, and a urea recovery section where the aqueous solution of urea is concentrated and a carbamate solution is obtained, where the carbon dioxide-removal section comprises a first carbon dioxide removal unit and a second carbon dioxide removal unit arranged in series or in parallel, the plant comprises a line feeding at least part of the carbamate solution to one of the carbon dioxide removal units, and the carbamate solution acts as a washing medium to remove carbon dioxide from the make-up gas; and
   (2) revamping the ammonia section of an ammonia-urea integrated plant, comprising reforming section fed with a hydrocarbon source, a purification section comprising carbon dioxide absorption section, where installation of a second carbon dioxide-removal section in addition to the existing carbon dioxide removal section, and operating by washing with a carbamate solution, provision of a flow line for feeding the second carbon dioxide-removal section with a stream of carbamate solution taken from the urea section, and preferably of a further line to feed the second carbon dioxide-removal section also with gaseous or liquid ammonia.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of the integrated ammonia-urea plant.
   First synthesis loop (13)
   Second synthesis loop (14)
   Make-up gas (44)
   Aqueous solution of ammonia (53)
   Carbamate solution (61)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide removal units are arranged in series, the first unit of the series operating with the carbamate solution as a washing medium, and the plant further comprising a bypass line for a portion of the make-up gas to bypass the first unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the purification step of carbon dioxide is performed with carbamate solution and it is also added with liquid or gaseous ammonia. The other of the carbon dioxide removal steps comprises absorption of carbon dioxide into an absorbing medium. The carbon dioxide removal steps are carried out in series. The portion of the carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas admitted to the carbon dioxide removal steps bypasses the first carbon dioxide removal step of the series, and is sent directly to the subsequent carbon dioxide removal step. The washing with carbamate solution is first of the series. The carbon dioxide removal steps are carried out in parallel, where the portion of carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas admitted to the absorption step being greater than the portion admitted to washing with carbamate solution. The portion of carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas admitted to the adsorption step is at least 70% of the total amount of gas. The process comprises compression of the carbon dioxide-containing make-up gas prior to the carbon dioxide removal steps, where the make-up gas being compressed to an intermediate pressure lower than the ammonia synthesis pressure, where the carbon dioxide removal steps are carried out under the intermediate pressure. The carbon dioxide removal by washing with the carbamate solution (61) further includes the washing with an aqueous solution of ammonia (53). The aqueous solution of ammonia is obtained from treatment of a purge gas drawn from an ammonia synthesis section of the integrated ammonia-urea plant. The conversion of the purified make-up gas into ammonia is carried out in at least a first synthesis loop (13) and a second synthesis loop (14) where the first synthesis loop produces a first amount of ammonia and a first stream of unreacted make-up gas (44) , and the unreacted make-up gas is converted in the second synthesis loop obtaining a second amount of ammonia and a second stream of unreacted make-up gas, and at least a portion of the second stream of unreacted make-up gas is recycled to the first synthesis loop.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E35; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Preparation of chemotherapeutic drug            nanoparticle-iodine oil superstable homogeneous embolic            agent involves reacting drug solution prepared using            drug and low-boiling point solvent, collecting drug            nanoparticles, mixing with iodine containing iodine oil            injection, and ultrasonically dispersing.
AU CHEN B
   CHENG H
   CHU C
   HE P
   ZHANG Y
   LIU G
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202247049D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of chemotherapeutic drug                nanoparticle-iodine oil superstable homogeneous                embolic agent involves: (1) dissolving drug in                low-boiling point solvent to obtain 0.5-3 mg/mL                drug solution; (2) taking carbon dioxide as gas                source to pressurize a reaction kettle to 8-12 MPa,                stabilizing temperature and pressure at                45-50&#8451; and 10-12 MPa, adding the drug                solution to the reaction kettle at 0.8-2.5                mL/minute, reacting for 0.4-1.5 hours, then                removing the low-boiling point solvent by carbon                dioxide decompression, then decompressing to remove                carbon dioxide, and collecting drug nanoparticles;                and (3) mixing the drug nanoparticles with iodine                containing 37-39% iodine oil injection at a ratio                of (1-10) mg:10 ml, and ultrasonically dispersing                in water bath.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                chemotherapeutic drug nanoparticle-iodine oil                superstable homogeneous embolic agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is pure physical method process                without introducing other cosolvent, emulsion or                other reagent, which may cause in-vivo toxicity.                The pure drug nanoparticles have controllable shape                and unchanged properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the drug solution in step (1) is preferably 1-2                  mg/mL.Preferred Components: The chemotherapeutic                  drug is alkane drug, anti-metabolism drug,                  anti-tumor antibiotics, anti-tumor animal and                  plant ingredient drug, anti-tumor hormone drug,                  platinum drug or dacarbazine. The alkane drug                  comprises nimustine, cyclophosphamide and                  glyciphosphoramide. The anti-metabolism drug                  comprises 5-fluorouracil, gemcitabine and                  methotrexate. The anti-tumor antibiotics comprise                  doxorubicin, epirubicin and pirarubicin. The                  anti-tumor animal and plant ingredient drug                  include irinotecan, camptothecin, paclitaxel and                  etoposide. The anti-tumor hormone drug comprises                  anastrozole and tamoxifen. The platinum drug                  comprises lobaplatin, oxaliplatin and cisplatin.                  The chemotherapeutic agent is doxorubicin,                  doxorubicin hydrochloride, pirarubicin,                  5-fluorouracil, loplatin, oxaliplatin, cisplatin,                  camptothecin or paclitaxel, preferably                  doxorubicin hydrochloride or oxaliplatin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The low-boiling point                  solvent is methanol, ethanol or                  dichloromethane.

AU LIU X

   WANG H
   ZHOU Y
   YAO Y
   YAN Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2022159094
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrocatalytic material boron copper oxide for carbon dioxide electroreduction reaction, involves (1) mixing 500 mg copper nitrate, 1.5 ml 30% aqueous ammonia, 400 mg sodium hydroxide and 40 mL ultrapure water to obtain a solid, and then centrifugal drying to obtain precursor (A), (2) mixing 200 mg precursor (A), 64 mg boric acid, and 100 ml anhydrous ethanol to obtain a solid substance, and obtaining the precursor (B) by centrifugal drying, (3) heating precursor (B) in a muffle furnace, heating to 500degrees Celsius at room temperature, keeping at this temperature for 120 minutes, and finally cooling to room temperature naturally to obtain precursor (C) and (4) electrochemically treating the precursor (C).
   USE - Preparation of electrocatalytic material boron-copper oxide for carbon dioxide electroreduction to produce ethylene (all claimed)
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has a certain catalytic activity, the time required for material synthesis is shortened, the energy consumption in the preparation process is reduced, and the obtained material has a uniform microscopic morphology and concentrated size distribution.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (4), the ethanol solution of the precursor (C) is ultrasonically vibrated for 1 hour, the obtained liquid is coated on the glassy carbon electrode using a pipette gun, and then the glassy carbon electrode is placed in the electrochemical cell, and then electrochemically reduced at -2.0 V for 15 minutes to obtain the catalyst boron-copper oxide (B-Cu2O).Preferred Components: The reference electrode is silver/silver chloride, the counter electrode is a glass slide electrode, and the electrolyte is a 0.5 M potassium bicarbonate solution.

PN CN113403085-A; CN113403085-B
TI Preparation of soil loosening agent by capturing carbon dioxide products from nanofiltration seawater includes mixing nano magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture, polyacrylamide, naphthalene sulfonate, organic matter and water.
AU WANG R
   WANG Z
   CAO S
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0994L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of soil loosening agent by capturing carbon dioxide products from nanofiltration seawater comprises crushing magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, wet grinding recovered products magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate by ball material, drying wet ground magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate to obtain nano mixture of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate and mixing nano magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture, polyacrylamide (PAM), naphthalene sulfonate, organic matter and water for 5-10 hours at 1000-2000 revolutions per minute (rpm). The soil loosening agent comprises 10.5-18.5 pts. wt. nano magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture, 0.05-0.5 pts. wt. PAM, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. naphthalene sulfonate, 5.5-10.5 pts. wt. organic matter and 70-83.85 pts. wt. water.
   USE - The method is for preparation of soil loosening agent by capturing carbon dioxide products from nanofiltration seawater.
   ADVANTAGE - The soil loosening agent is rich in calcium, magnesium and organic matter, improves soil structure, increases soil aeration and soil vitality, supplements organic matter and has long shelf life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The organic matter is kitchen waste and/or agricultural straw.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The recovered products of carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltration seawater e.g. magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate are obtained by adding sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate to adjust pH of wastewater to 7-9, adding 0.05-2% basic polyaluminium chloride, 0.5-100 ppm anionic polyacrylamide (APAM) and 0.05-0.1% sodium methylene naphthalene sulfonate (NNO) until large flocculent calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate are formed in product wastewater containing carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltration seawater, cooling product wastewater to 5-10 degrees C, accelerating sedimentation of calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, discharging settled calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate wastewater into concentration tank, pressing calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate to obtain massive calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, cutting block calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, crushing block calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate and transporting magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate to thermal infrared drying heating chamber through material transmission system for thermal infrared drying. Preferred Components: The ball material is alumina and/or zirconia.

PN CN112479230-A; CN112479230-B
TI Preparing ammonium bicarbonate by carbon fixation of high alkaline alumina red mud comprises e.g. drying red mud, grinding, sieving, adding ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, introducing excessive carbon dioxide into saturated aqueous ammonia.
AU LIU J
   HAI R
   ZHANG L
   HUI C
   LI J
   CHENG X
   LI Z
AE UNIV ZHONGYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UZYT-C)
GA 2021307493
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonium bicarbonate by carbon fixation of high alkaline alumina red mud comprises (i) drying red mud, grinding and sieving by a ball mill, (ii) grinding the red mud, calcium oxide and water, stirring uniformly, adding ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, strong stirring and allowing to stand, circulating for 3-5 times, (iii) strong stirring and allowing to stand, collecting ammonia gas through the one-way exhaust pipeline, then collecting phosphorus-containing red mud residue, and (iv) introducing the ammonia gas collected in the step (iii) into the reaction kettle container filled with distilled water to obtain saturated aqueous ammonia, introducing excessive carbon dioxide into the saturated aqueous ammonia to obtain ammonium bicarbonate.
   USE - The ammonium bicarbonate is useful in the field of building material and soil improvement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the fixation of the flue gas carbon dioxide, and prepares nitrogen fertilizer for agricultural production through the solid carbon process, alkaline reduction of red mud, improves reaction activity, and is rich in large amount of phosphate ion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the red mud is alumina residue, the components include quartz, goethite, feldspar, boehmite, calcite, rutile, calcium silicate and calcium aluminosilicate, and the pH value is 11-13 and both the internal and external radioactivity index are greater than 1.5. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of ground red mud, calcium oxide, water and ammonium dihydrogen phosphate is 50:5-10:15-25:18-25. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the grinding is carried out for 15-25 minutes, and then the vacuum is 4-6 KPa, the residue on the 80 mu m square hole sieve is not greater than 5%, and the standby sieve analysis time is 120 seconds after passing through the 150 mu m square hole sieve. In the step (ii), ground red mud, calcium oxide and water are stirred vigorously for 20 minutes, allowing to stand for 1.5-2 hours, repeatedly 2-3 times, then adding ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, stirring vigorously for 30-40 minutes, and then , allowing to stand for 1.5-2 hours, circulating 3-5 times. In the step (iv), ammonium bicarbonate crystallizes out in excess at room temperature, and ammonium bicarbonate in the unsaturated solution is precipitated by cooling in an ice bath.

PN CN112390293-A; CN112390293-B
TI Preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional manganese oxide and two-dimensional nickel-manganese layered double hydroxide nanocomposite materials for the high-efficiency catalytic carbon dioxide reduction, involves adding divalent manganese salt.
AU ZHUANG Z
   ZHOU L
   ZHUANG G
   YU Y
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021210074
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional manganese oxide (Mn3O4) and two-dimensional nickel-manganese layered double hydroxide (Ni-MnLDH) nanocomposite materials involves adding the divalent manganese salt and the divalent nickel salt to the alcohol, mixing and dissolving them thoroughly, and making a uniformly dispersed reaction precursor liquid. The reaction precursor liquid is transferred to a stainless steel autoclave lined with polytetrafluoroethylene for constant temperature reaction. The reaction mixture is cooled, centrifuged, washed, and dried until the water is completely volatilized to obtain a solid powder two-dimensional (2D) manganese oxide/two-dimensional nickel-manganese layered double hydroxide (Mn3O4/2DNi-MnLDH) nanocomposite material.
   USE - Method for preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional manganese oxide and two-dimensional nickel-manganese layered double hydroxide nanocomposite materials used in the high-efficiency catalytic carbon dioxide reduction (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano composite material can efficiently and selectively photocatalyze the reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under the condition of a photosensitizer, and preparation process is simple, the cycle is short, the cost is low, large-scale industrial production is possible, and it has good economic and environmental benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of ultra-thin two-dimensional Mn3O4 is between 3.75-4 nanometer. The thickness of the two-dimensional layered double hydroxide (LDH) nanomaterial is less than 5 nanometer. Preferred Components: The manganese salt is manganese formate (Mn(HCOO)2). The divalent nickel salt is nickel chloride hexahydrate (NiCl2.6H2O). The alcohol is methanol.

PN CN112362649-A
TI Detecting rhGH neutralizing antibody useful for population of rhGH medicine subjects, comprises e.g. mixing inactivated sample with rhGH working solution, and incubating in carbon dioxide incubator to obtain neutralized sample.
AU LIU L
   YU H
   WANG Y
   WANG F
   WANG L
AE CHANGCHUN GENESCIENCE PHARM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021186436
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting rhGH neutralizing antibody comprises (1) inactivating the sample at 50-60 degrees C for 30-35 minutes, where the sample comprises serum samples to be tested, negative control samples and positive control samples, (2) mixing the inactivated sample with the rhGH working solution, and incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator to obtain neutralized sample, (3) mixing the cell suspension with the neutralized sample, and performing cell culture at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, (4) mixing the cell chromogenic solution with the cultured cells, and allowing to stand in the dark at room temperature, and (5) using the fluorescent cell activity detection system to detect the number of living cells, obtaining the inhibition rate of the serum sample to be tested, and determining whether there is rhGH neutralizing antibody in the serum sample to be tested.
   USE - The method is useful for population of rhGH medicine subjects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has highly sensitivity, and ; strong antiserum interference ability, and high medicine tolerance level; and detects low concentrations of rhGH neutralizing antibodies in human serum.

PN CN112316722-A
TI Preparation of antifreeze and quality-preserving automobile urea liquid added with multiple active agents includes reacting high-purity ammonia and carbon dioxide, collecting gas and performing flash evaporation on liquid urea melt.
AU LI F
   SUN C
   SANG S
AE HENAN HONGKANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202117905W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of antifreeze and quality-preserving automobile urea liquid added with multiple active agents comprises reacting high-purity ammonia and carbon dioxide through first reactor and second reactor under high temperature and high pressure, collecting gas of second reactor, compressing, sending through second reactor again to collect liquid urea melt fluid of first reactor and second reactor, performing flash evaporation on liquid urea melt to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide under the condition of reduced pressure, adding high-purity water to obtain saturated urea solution, collecting flash treated gas, introducing to first reactor, filtering urea aqueous solution by membrane with pore size of 0.2-0.5 mu m, placing saturated urea solution in multistage liquid film crystallizer to crystallize urea crystal, dissolving urea crystal in deionized water and adding soot catalytic material and active agent.
   USE - The method is for preparation of antifreeze and quality-preserving automobile urea liquid added with multiple active agents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves existing technical problems of low production efficiency, poor dispersion and unsuitability for extreme low temperatures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The soot catalytic material is prepared by taking lanthanum (III) nitrate hexahydrate, potassium nitrate, cobalt (II) nitrate hexahydrate, nickel (II) nitrate hexahydrate, ammonium metavanadate and citric acid to prepare 0.9-1.0% solution with deionized water, adding 0.45-0.5 wt.% sodium metaaluminate, adding nano tungsten trioxide-titanium dioxide mixture powder, mixing, performing ultrasonic vibration for 20-45 minutes, roasting in electric furnace at 550-560 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, cooling to room temperature, heating material at 650-680 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, cooling to room temperature and adding vanadium pentoxide. Preferred Components: The active agent comprises 1-2 pts. wt. sodium percarbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate, 1-2 pts. wt. ammonium alcohol ether carboxylate, 1-2 pts. wt. silicon antifreeze, 3-4 pts. wt. butanol and 0.5-1 pt. wt. dispersant.

PN CN112281086-A; CN112281086-B
TI Preparation of three-dimensional braided fiber reinforced magnesium composite involves placing carbon fiber preform in packaging mold, degumming, activating and pressing molten liquid metal into mold by air pressure to infiltrate preform.
AU WANG Z
   CAI C
   XU Z
   YU H
   YANG W
   XIONG B
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 202113364B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of heat-resistant three-dimensional braided fiber reinforced magnesium composite involves preparing carbon fiber preform, placing carbon fiber preform in packaging mold, degumming at pressure and temperature that make the carbon dioxide liquid pass into packaging mold, so that the liquid carbon dioxide is completely immersed in the carbon fiber preform for 20-24 hours, placing packaging mold in closed environment at 950-980 degrees C and activating for 90-120 minutes, placing packaging mold in vacuum pressure infiltration device and pressing molten liquid metal into the packaging mold by air pressure to infiltrate the carbon fiber preform.
   USE - Preparation of heat-resistant three-dimensional braided fiber reinforced magnesium composite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide to oxidize the surface of the carbon fiber in the gas phase, which chemically reacts with the unsaturated carbon atoms on the surface of the carbon fiber, so that the surface of the carbon fiber preform increases the active oxygen-containing functional groups, and increases the surface roughness of the carbon fiber to improve the interface bonding strength of the carbon fiber composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The material for activation is sol solution. The sol solution is one or more of silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide and zirconium dioxide. The carbon fiber preform is made of graphite fiber M40 woven in three dimensions and five directions.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The molten liquid metal for infiltration is one or more of AZ91D magnesium alloy, AZ31D magnesium alloy and AZ61D magnesium alloy.

AU CAI J

   LI Q
   LIU Z
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2020A1149S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan membrane material involves using chitosan solution with pH value of 6-8, preparing dispersion, adding chitosan to dispersion, controlling temperature at freezing point to 35 degrees C, and introducing carbon dioxide into solution, stirring at the same time to dissolve the chitosan. The chitosan is completely dissolved, then stopped the introduction of carbon dioxide. The transparent chitosan solution with pH value of 6-8 is obtained. The chitosan aqueous solution obtained is formed into film by casting method and then immersed in regeneration bath for regeneration. The washing, plasticization and drying are performed to obtain chitosan film material.
   USE - Method for preparing chitosan membrane material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare chitosan membrane material that is highly efficient, energy-saving, and environmentally friendly, has strong acid and strong alkaline residues, good mechanical properties, improves production efficiency and reduces production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing blended chitosan film, which involves introducing functional organic or inorganic additives, low-dimensional nanomaterials into the surface or inside of the membrane material or introducing natural or synthetic polymers for blending to prepare the membrane material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersion is selected from one or mixture of two of urea aqueous solution and thiourea aqueous solution. The alcohol is selected from methanol and ethanol. The regeneration bath is selected from one or more mixed aqueous solutions of alcohols, salts, amides, dimethyl sulfoxide, ethyl acetate, and acetone. The salt is selected from ammonium salt, sodium salt, potassium salt, magnesium salt, calcium salt, aluminum salt. The functional organic or inorganic additives are selected from one or more of plasticizers, reinforcing agents, refractory additives, dyes, optical stabilizers, antibacterial and antibacterial agents, conductive materials, and surfactants. The low-dimensional nanomaterial is selected from graphene and derivatives, carbon nanotubes and derivatives, metal or metal oxide nanoparticles, organic framework compounds or layered nanomaterials. The natural polymer or synthetic polymer is selected from polymer nanofibers, animal proteins, plant proteins, collagen, alginate, cellulose and derivatives, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol or conductive polymer. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the urea aqueous solution is 6-60 wt.%. The concentration of the thiourea aqueous solution is 3-14 wt.%. The concentration of the alcohol is 10-100 wt.%. The concentration of the salt solution is 5-40 wt.%. The mass ratio of chitosan to dispersion is 1:7-1:1000.
