PT P
PN CN111495378-A
TI Methanation catalyst useful for catalytic            preparing methane, and prepared by calcining            nickel-based hydrotalcite precursor supported by metal            oxide in ammonia atmosphere, and reducing with            hydrogen.
AU WANG G
   LU B
   ZHOU K
   TANG D
   QIU Y
   WAN Y
AE CNBM BENGBU DESIGN & RES INST GLASS IND (CHNB-C)
GA 202078320B
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation catalyst is claimed. The                methanation catalyst is prepared by calcining                nickel-based hydrotalcite precursor supported by a                metal oxide in ammonia atmosphere, and then                reducing with hydrogen.
   USE - The methanation catalyst is useful for                catalytic preparing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalytic activity,                strong carbon deposition resistance, and high                methane selectivity. The method: has simple                process; and is suitable for industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                catalytic preparing methane, comprising (i) adding                methanation catalyst into a reactor, then passing                the reaction raw material gas into the reactor,                where the composition of the feed gas in the carbon                monoxide methanation reaction is: the molar ratio                of carbon monoxide, hydrogen and nitrogen is                1:3-4:1-5, the composition of the feed gas in the                carbon dioxide methanation reaction is: the molar                ratio between carbon dioxide, hydrogen and nitrogen                is 1:4-6:1-5, and the mass space velocity of the                catalyst in the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                methanation reaction is 3000-12000 ml/(g.h), and                the methane product can be obtained by continuous                reaction at 200-600 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcination                  temperature under ammonia gas is 100-600 degrees                  C. The method comprises (i) dispersing soluble                  nickel salt and metal oxide in deionized water to                  obtain mixed solution A, and controlling the                  concentration of soluble nickel salt in solution                  A to 10-50 mmol/l and the concentration of metal                  oxide to be 1-100 g/l, (ii) dissolving the                  precipitant in deionized water to obtain solution                  B with concentration of 100-500 mmol/l, (iii)                  mixing the solution A and solution B, placing the                  mixed solution in a hydrothermal kettle, and                  reacting at 60-150 degrees C for 6-24 hours to                  obtain crude product, and (iv) filtering the                  crude product obtained in step (iii), washing the                  filter cake, drying, calcining at 100-600 degrees                  C for 1-6 hours in ammonia gas, then carrying out                  hydrogen reduction treatment, and cooling to room                  temperature in inert gas atmosphere to obtain                  methanation catalyst. Preferred Components: The                  methanation catalyst 40-90 wt.% metal oxide and                  1-40 wt.% nickel. The metal oxide is titanium                  dioxide, zirconium dioxide, aluminum oxide,                  cerium(IV) oxide, manganese(IV) oxide and/or                  vanadium(V) oxide. The soluble nickel salt is                  nickel(II) nitrate, nickel(II) acetate,                  nickel(II) chloride and/or nickel(II) sulfate.                  The metal oxide is titanium dioxide, zirconium                  dioxide, aluminum oxide, cerium(IV) oxide,                  manganese(IV) oxide and/or vanadium(V) oxide. In                  step (ii), the precipitating agent is urea,                  hexamethylenetetramine, sodium carbonate, sodium                  hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, ammonia, potassium                  hydroxide, potassium carbonate and/or potassium                  bicarbonate.

TI Monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped carbon            electrocatalyst is used for electrocatalyzing reduction            of carbon dioxide in bioelectrochemical system,            comprises iron which is encapsulated in the pores of            metal organic framework ZIF-8.
AU HOU Y
   ZENG Q
   LI Z
   YANG B
   LEI L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020743710
AB    NOVELTY - Monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped carbon                electrocatalyst comprises iron which is                encapsulated in the pores of metal organic                framework ZIF-8 with microporous and/or mesoporous                structure in the form of monoatomic iron-nitrogen                coordination. The catalyst includes micropores with                pore diameter of less than 3 nanometer, mesopores                with diameter of 3-5 nanometer and 29-32                nanometer.
   USE - Monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped carbon                electrocatalyst is used for electrocatalyzing                reduction of carbon dioxide in bioelectrochemical                system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped carbon                electrocatalyst has good repeatability, realizes                the high selectivity, high activity and low                overpotential of carbon monoxide from                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped                carbon electrocatalyst, which involves adding iron                phthalocyanine to the methanol solution of                2-methylimidazole, and dissolving to obtain mixed                solution, adding methanol solution of zinc nitrate                to the mixed solution, mixing, centrifuging,                washing and drying to obtain ZIF-8 packaged iron                phthalocyanine composite material, calcining                ZIF-8/iron phthalocyanine composite material and                acid washing under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain                monoatomic iron-nitrogen co-doped carbon                electrocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The BET specific                  surface area of the catalyst is 500-1100 m2g-1,                  pore volume is 0.36-0.97 cm3g-1. The molar ratio                  of 2-methylimidazole to zinc nitrate is                  7.5-8.5:1. The molar concentration of                  2-methylimidazole is 0.75-0.85mol/L. The molar                  concentration of zinc nitrate is 0.09-0.11 mol/L.                  The mass concentration of the iron phthalocyanine                  in the mixed solution after adding the iron                  phthalocyanine is 0.2-1.2 g/L.

PT P
PN CN111439764-A
TI Preparing nano-lithium carbonate by adding lithium chloride to absolute ethanol, pouring carbon dioxide gas into ethanol aqueous solution, adding saturated alcohol solution to lithium alcohol solution, and placing into distillation kettle.
AU GUI A
AE SUZHOU BOLI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202071734X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nano-lithium carbonate, involves (a) adding lithium chloride to absolute ethanol to completely dissolve to form a lithium alcohol solution, (b) pouring carbon dioxide gas into the low-temperature ethanol aqueous solution until a saturated alcoholic aqueous solution of carbon dioxide is formed, (c) adding the saturated alcohol aqueous solution to the lithium alcohol solution at a constant temperature, and stirring evenly until precipitation no longer occurs, and continuously dripping for 5-10 minutes to obtain a suspension solution, and (d) placing the suspension into a vacuum distillation kettle, performing a gradient heating process to obtain a concentrated solution, filtering and drying to obtain nano-lithium carbonate crystals.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nano-lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the dependence of the dispersant, utilizes the low solubility of lithium carbonate in water and ethanol, uses the difference in the concentration of lithium ions, realizes the crystal precipitation, and cooperates with the dripping reaction to control the crystal size, and realizes the formation of nano-scale lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of lithium chloride in absolute ethanol in step (a) is 20-40 g/l, and the stirring speed is 1000-2000 rpm, and the temperature is not greater than 30 degrees C. The low temperature in step (b) -30 degrees C to 40 degrees C, and the ethanol aqueous solution comprises 90-95 vol.% ethanol. The constant temperature in step (c) is -30 degrees C to 40 degrees C, and the dripping speed is 5-10 ml/minute. The gradient heating in step (d) comprises adopting two gradient heating, where the temperature of the first gradient is 20-40 degrees C, the pressure is 90% of atmospheric pressure, and the time is 1-3 hours, and the temperature of the second gradient is 75-85 degrees C, the pressure is 85-90% of atmospheric pressure, and the time is 2-4 hours, and a bowl of concentrated liquid just covers the lithium carbonate crystals. The drying temperature in step (d) is 100-120 degrees C.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

TI Preserving cheeses with bloomy rind, comprises e.g. placing cheese in packaging at least in oxygen and carbon dioxide permeable, or even at least partially micro-perforated, and setting atmosphere up in packaging.
AU IBARRA D
   TUMA Y
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020496304
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving cheeses with a bloomy rind, comprises: placing the cheese in a packaging at least in oxygen and carbon dioxide permeable, or even at least partially micro-perforated; setting an atmosphere up in the packaging which contains oxygen at a content greater than 2%, preferably 2-10%, and carbon dioxide at a content od 10-30%; and closing the packaging and storing in the cold, i.e. at a temperature of 0-10 degrees C, preferably 0-4 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving cheeses with a bloomy rind.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: extends the shelf life, and maintains satisfactory visual appearance; limits the oxidation reactions; slows down microbial development; limits the development of Penicillium on the sides of the slices (we maintain Penicilliumbut we slow down its metabolism and therefore its development); limits moisture losses, and therefore of weight losses; limits the proteolytic activity on the surface of the proteolytic activity corresponds to the hydrolysis activity of proteins by proteases, enzymes produced by microorganisms including molds such as Penicillium); and preserves aromas and texture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The atmosphere is set up: by injecting an nitrogen-carbon dioxide-oxygen mixture falling within the specifications; or by injecting an nitrogen-carbon dioxide mixture and making settings for the atmosphere packaging machine that is used, to obtain the required final atmosphere, where the machine settings in question includes line speed and/or gas pressure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of polyether block amide/MIL-101 mixed matrix membrane for carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation, involves ultrasonically stirring MIL-101 in polyether block amide solution, pouring flat, covering with filter paper, and drying.
AU SONG C
   FAN Z
   LI R
   QIU Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202015489X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide)/MIL-101 mixed matrix membrane, involves adding half the total amount of MIL-101 to 8 wt.% Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) solution, refluxing and stirring at 125 degrees C for 6 hours, transferring the suspension to an ultrasonic oscillator, ultrasonically treating for 1 hour to disperse MIL-101 in the membrane liquid, adding the remaining half of MIL-101, continuously refluxing and stirring for 12 hours, ultrasonically treating for 1 hour, pouring the suspension flat into a Petri dish, drying at room temperature for 48 hours, covering the upper portion with a filter paper to control solvent evaporation speed, volatilizing the solvent slowly, and vacuum-drying at 50 degrees C for 24 hours to ensure removal of residual solvent.
   USE - Preparation of Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide)/MIL-101 mixed matrix membrane used for carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides matrix membrane having uniform morphology, and excellent carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation performance at both room temperature and low temperature.

TI Preparation of carrier-free methanol carbon dioxide hydrogenation hollow ball production catalyst by stirring sucrose and water, reacting, centrifugally separating, washing by water and ethanol, drying. dispersing, roasting, and reducing.

AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2019A6744W

TI New imidazolium-based nickel ionic liquid used in            preparation of metal-nitrogen-doped carbon catalytic            material used as catalyst for electrocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU ZHANG J
   CHENG X
   TAN D
   TAN X
   SHI J
   YANG G
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 201974054W
AB    NOVELTY - An imidazolium-based nickel ionic liquid (I)                is new.
   USE - New imidazolium-based nickel ionic liquid is                used in preparation of metal-nitrogen-doped carbon                catalytic material (all claimed) used as catalyst                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-nitrogen-doped carbon catalytic                material obtained using the imidazolium-based                nickel ionic liquid has excellent catalytic                activity and selectivity for electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide, and carbon-monoxide                Faraday efficiency of 94% at an overpotential of                0.75 V with respect to standard hydrogen electrode.                Since the structure and properties of the                imidazolium-based nickel ionic liquid can be                designed and regulated by changing the anion and                cation, the nickel ionic liquid can be prepared by                simple, convenient and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An imidazolium-based nickel ionic liquid of                formula (I) is new.
   n = 0-7;and
   X = Cl, Br or I.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of imidazolium-based nickel                ionic liquid (I); and
   (2) preparation of metal-nitrogen-doped carbon                catalytic material, which involves calcining the                ionic liquid (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The ionic liquid (I)                  is prepared by mixing an imidazolium halide                  compound of formula (II) with a nickel halide of                  formula: NiX2, and reacting. Preferred Process:                  The preparation of metal-nitrogen-doped carbon                  catalytic material further involves grinding the                  calcined product, sequentially reacting with                  hydrochloric acid and nitric acid at 60-100                  degrees C for 5-15 hours, collecting the solid                  material by centrifugation, and drying. Preferred                  Condition: The reaction process is carried out at                  30-90 degrees C for 12-48 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of imidazolium                  halide compound and nickel halide is                  0.5-3:1.

PN CN109100279-A; CN109100279-B
TI Carbon dioxide water-rock reaction rock            three-dimensional pore structure measuring method,            involves determining pore structure evolution            characteristic of rock column sample by pore change            characteristic of rock sample.
AU WU S
   ZHAI X
   BAI L
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019030288
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a                post-experiment porosity of a first rock sample. A                high-resolution scanning is performed on                micro-drill samples according to a scanning                parameter and a scanning position. A                post-experiment porosity of a second rock sample is                determined by utilizing a volume ratio of the                micro-drill sample. A pore change characteristic of                the first rock sample is determined according to                the pre-experiment porosity and a post-experiment                porosity of the first rock sample. A pore change                characteristic of the second rock sample is                determined according to a pre-experiment porosity                and the post-experiment porosity of the second rock                sample. A pore structure evolution characteristic                of a rock column sample is determined by the pore                change characteristic of the first rock sample                according to the pore change characteristics of the                second rock sample.
   USE - Carbon dioxide water-rock reaction rock                three-dimensional pore structure measuring                method.

PT P
PN MX2016017353-A1
TI Extracting coconut copra products involve drying of coconut endosperm or copra, reducing size of dried coconut endosperm to particles, positioning of particles in one sample holder, positioning sample holder,and pumping of carbon dioxide.
AU ROMERO DE LA VEGA G
AE UNIV COMUNIDAD DEL GOLFO CENT AC (UYCO-Non-standard)
   ROMERO D L V G (ROME-Individual)
   INNOVACIONES TECNOLOGICAS KOKOC SAPI (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A55215
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting coconut copra products involve drying of coconut endosperm (pulp of coconut) or copra, reducing the size of the dried coconut endosperm to particles, positioning of the particles in one sample holder, positioning the sample holder, bracket pumping of carbon dioxide from one container by medium of one pump, heating, pressurization of the cell with the carbon dioxide values of 2500-3000 pound per square inch (psi), raising the temperature to 35-45 degrees C, opening valve of an outlet for collection of product, increasing of the pressure of the cell to 3500-4500 psi of pressure and the temperature to 50-70 degrees C, opening of valve of heating output for collection of product, and uncovering of cell extraction for removing the raw material that is subjected to the extraction of copra.
   USE - Method for extracting coconut copra products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to obtain coconut copra products in the form of pulp which is highly refined, deodorized and defatted, harmless, inert, has white color and texture that contains approximately 20% protein, 45% carbohydrate, 11% fiber and the rest minerals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The coconut copra products are dried in natural form (solar drying) or by forced convection at 80 degrees C for preventing lipid oxidation, and the time of drying is subjected to relative humidity of 8-15%. The sample holder is one of stainless steel tube with hollow caps. The cell of extraction are hermetically closed so that it can withstand high pressures, so it is necessary to adjust the conditions of process for the extraction. The carbon dioxide is passed by one of cooling system to maintain between 2-(-)2 degrees C. The cells are filled with supercritical carbon dioxide. The outlet is heated to maintain the coconut fat in the liquid state at its melting point 23-26 degrees C so extraction is performed at 30-60 degrees C.

TI Producing sodium hydrosulfide comprises reacting the aqueous solution of ruthenium sulfide and carbon dioxide, collecting the cesium carbonate, and reacting the unreacted carbon dioxide and the aqueous cesium sulfide solution.
AU WANG Y
AE CHONGQING KINGLONG FINE STRONTIUM CHEM (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING KINGLONG FINE STRONTIUM CHEM (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201891927M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium hydrosulfide comprises (i) passing an aqueous solution of ruthenium sulfide into the first reaction column through a carbon dioxide recovery column, introducing carbon dioxide into the first reaction column, and reacting the aqueous solution of ruthenium sulfide and carbon dioxide; (ii) collecting the cesium carbonate, introducing the generated hydrogen sulfide gas and the unreacted carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide recovery tower, and reacting the unreacted carbon dioxide and the aqueous cesium sulfide solution, and passing hydrogen sulfide gas into second reaction tower; (iii) passing the sodium hydroxide lye through the hydrogen sulfide recovery tower into the second reaction tower, and reacting the hydrogen sulfide gas with the sodium hydroxide lye; and (iv) collecting the reaction product sodium hydrosulfide, and introducing the unreacted hydrogen sulfide gas into the hydrogen sulfide recovery column to be reacted with the sodium hydroxide alkali solution.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium hydrosulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good production technique, and simple preparation technique.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sodium hydrosulfide comprises (i) passing an aqueous solution of ruthenium sulfide into the first reaction column through a carbon dioxide recovery column, introducing carbon dioxide into the first reaction column, and reacting the aqueous solution of ruthenium sulfide and carbon dioxide in the first reaction column; (ii) collecting the cesium carbonate, introducing the generated hydrogen sulfide gas and the unreacted carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide recovery tower from the first reaction tower, and reacting the unreacted carbon dioxide and the aqueous cesium sulfide solution in the carbon dioxide recovery tower, and passing hydrogen sulfide gas into second reaction tower through the carbon dioxide recovery tower; (iii) passing the sodium hydroxide lye through the hydrogen sulfide recovery tower into the second reaction tower, and reacting the hydrogen sulfide gas with the sodium hydroxide lye in the second reaction column; and (iv) collecting the reaction product sodium hydrosulfide, and introducing the unreacted hydrogen sulfide gas into the hydrogen sulfide recovery column to be reacted with the sodium hydroxide alkali solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the sodium hydroxide lye is 40-45%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The number of carbon dioxide recovery columns is at least two. The pressure in the first reaction column is 2-3.5 MPa. The pressure in the second reaction column is 2.0-3.5 MPa.

PN CN111777503-A
TI Synthesizing heptafluoroisobutyronitrile comprises e.g. reacting hexafluoropropylene and carbon dioxide under catalysis of fluoride salt to obtain heptafluoroisobutyrate, acidifying, and performing esterification reaction.
AU ZHOU J
   YU Q
   LIAO M
   ZHOU Y
   YU D
AE FUJIAN ZHANGPING JIUDING FLOURINE CHEM (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A31641
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing heptafluoroisobutyronitrile comprises (1) reacting hexafluoropropylene and carbon dioxide under the catalysis of fluoride salt to obtain heptafluoroisobutyrate, (2) acidifying the obtained heptafluoroisobutyrate, and performing an esterification reaction with alcohol to obtain heptafluoroisobutyrate, and (3) reacting the obtained heptafluoroisobutyrate with ammonia and dehydrated to obtain heptafluoroisobutyronitrile.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing heptafluoroisobutyronitrile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts raw materials, which are easily available; has mild reaction conditions; has high reaction conversion rate and yield; has easy separation and purification; is simple, safe, economical, and has short synthesis route.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (3), reacting the obtained heptafluoroisobutyrate with ammonia, and dehydrating, heating and gasifying heptafluoroisobutyrate, mixing with ammonia gas to form a mixed gas, passing the mixed gas into a catalyst tower for catalytic dehydration reaction with a dehydration catalyst to obtain heptafluoroisobutyronitrile. In the step (3), the molar ratio of heptafluoroisobutyrate to ammonia in the mixed gas is 1: 1-10. In the step (3), during the catalytic dehydration reaction, the temperature of the catalyst tower is 200-800 degrees C. In the step (3), reacting and dehydrating the obtained heptafluoroisobutyrate with ammonia specifically comprises first reacting the heptafluoroisobutyrate with ammonia to obtain heptafluoroisobutyramide, dehydrating the heptafluoroisobutyramide by a dehydrating agent to obtain heptafluoroisobutyronitrile. In the step (3), in the reaction step of heptafluoroisobutyrate and ammonia, the molar ratio of ammonia to heptafluoroisobutyrate is 1.0-3.0:1, the reaction temperature is 0-80 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.1 -1 MPa, the reaction time is 1-20 hours, the ammonia is ammonia water, ammonia alcohol solution and/or ammonia gas. The reaction solvent is alcohol solvents, ether solvents, nitrile solvents, amide solvents or sulfone solvents. The molar ratio of the reaction solvent to heptafluoroisobutyrate is 1-10: 1. In the step (3), dehydrating heptafluoroisobutyramide with a dehydrating agent, the dehydrating agent is acid chloride, acid anhydride, phosphorus pentoxide or phosphorus oxychloride, the molar ratio of heptafluoroisobutyramide to dehydrating agent is 0.5-1:1, the reaction temperature is -40-180 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-20 hours. In the step (3), the dehydration catalyst is oxides or salts of silicon, aluminum, manganese, boron, vanadium, barium, zirconium, cerium, or thorium. In the step (1), the fluoride salt is lithium fluoride, sodium fluoride, potassium fluoride, cesium fluoride and/or zinc fluoride. The molar ratio of hexafluoropropylene to carbon dioxide is 0.5-1.5):1. The reaction solvent is ether solvents, nitrile solvents, amide solvents, and/or sulfone solvents. The molar ratio of the reaction solvent to the fluoride salt is 1-25:1. The reaction temperature is -50-100 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.1-2 MPa, and the reaction time is 1-20 hours. In the step (2), the acid in the acidification reaction process is anhydrous hydrogen fluoride, hydrofluoric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid or hydrochloric acid, during the acidification reaction, the molar ratio of acid to heptafluoroisobutyrate is 1-5:1, the temperature of the acidification reaction is 0-80 degrees C, the pressure of the acidification reaction is 0.1-5 MPa, the acidification reaction time is 1-20 hours, and the alcohol in the esterification reaction is methanol, ethanol or propanol, the molar ratio of heptafluoroisobutyric acid to alcohol during the esterification reaction is 1-10:1, the temperature of the esterification reaction is 0-150 degrees C, the pressure of the esterification reaction is 0.1-5 MPa, the time of the esterification reaction is 1-20 hours.

PT P
PN CN110917310-A
TI Extracting Alpinia galangal flavonoid compound by pretreating Alpinia galangal, performing microwave extraction, enzymatically hydrolyzing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, concentrating and freeze-drying concentrate.
AU CHEN M
AE FANGCHENGGANG LVHUAYUAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (FANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020260220
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting Alpinia galanga flavonoid compound involves subjecting A.galanga to pretreatment, performing microwave extraction, enzymatically hydrolyzing the extract, then performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, concentrating the extract under vacuum and vacuum freeze-drying the concentrate to obtain the A.galanga flavonoid compound.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting A.galanga flavonoid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves dissolution rate and extraction rate of flavonoids, reduces waste of resources, does not leave organic solvent, which ensures that the product is 100% natural and obtains higher purity flavonoids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) removing impurities in fresh A.galanga, cleaning the A.galanga, adding the A.galanga and purified water into a grinding machine and cleaning to obtain the A.galanga pulp having particle size 80-100 mesh, (b) subjecting the A.galanga pulp obtained in step (a) to microwave extraction 3 times at frequency of 0.1-0.2 KHz, power of 400-450 W and 20-25 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 25-30 minutes to obtain an extraction solution, (c) adding cellulase, protease and lignocellulosic enzyme to the extract obtained in step (b) under agitation for enzymatically hydrolyzing at 41-45 degrees C for 30-40 minutes to obtain an hydrolyzate solution, (d) adding 45-55 %mass ethanol solution having concentration of 95-98 %mass to the hydrolyzate solution obtained in step (c), evenly stirring the mixture, then adding the mixture into an extractor and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 44-47 degrees C and 30-40 MPa for 145-160 minutes under carbon dioxide flow rate of 19-22 l/hour to obtain an extract, (e) adding extract obtained in step (d) into a desiccator, vacuum concentrating under vacuum degree of 0.065-0.075 MPa and pressure of 0.01-0.03 MPa at 16-20 degrees C and vacuum freeze-drying to obtain the compound. In step (a), the mass ratio of the A.galanga to purified water is 1:1.5-2. In step (c), the mass ratio of the cellulase, protease, lignocellulosic enzyme and extract solution is 0.4:0.6-0.8:0.8-0.3:0.5:1. The step (e) involves feeding the concentrate into the freeze-dryer, then vacuuming under vacuum degree of 3-5 Pa and pressure is 55-65 Pa, cooling to -32 degrees C to -26 degrees C for 3-5 hours, thawing at a rate of 1-2 degrees C/minute to 2-6 degrees C, incubating for 100-120 minutes, heating at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minute to 50-55 degrees C, incubating for 60-80 minutes, cooling at a rate of 4-5 degrees C/minute to 30-35 degrees C, incubating for 50-60 minutes, cooling at a rate of 3-4 degrees C/minute to 20-25 degrees C, incubating for 150-160 minutes and continuing drying until the moisture content is less than 10%.

PN CN110483581-A; CN110483581-B
TI New palladium complex containing bisphosphine carborane ligand catalyst for catalyze reaction of halogenated hydrocarbons with carbon dioxide to synthesize carboxylic acids.
AU YAO Z
   CHEN J
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2019A04049
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium complex containing bisphosphine carborane ligand (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst for catalyze reaction of halogenated hydrocarbons (chlorobenzene, 1-bromonaphthalene, 4-nitrochlorobenzene, p-dimethylaminobromobenzene, p-nitrochlorobenzene, p-dibromobenzene, trimethyliodobenzene, 3-iodobenzenamine or 1-bromo-2-methoxybenzene) with carbon dioxide to synthesize carboxylic acids (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has stable physical and chemical property, and produces high yield carboxylic acids. The method: has simple process, excellent selectivity and high yield product; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Palladium complex containing bisphosphine carborane ligand of formula (I) is new.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) adding n-butyllithium solution into meta-carborane solution, and reacting at room temperature for 30-60 minutes, (ii) add diphenylphosphonium chloride and reacting at room temperature for 3-6 hours, and (iii) adding palladium(II) chloride and reacting at room temperature for 3-5 hours, and post-treating to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (i), the n-butyllithium solution is solution of n-butyllithium in n-hexane solution, and the meta-carborane solution is solution of meta-carborane in tetrahydrofuran. The eluent is a mixed solvent of dichloromethane and ethyl acetate during the column chromatography separation, and the volume ratio between dichloromethane and ethyl acetate is 3-6:1. The molar ratio of the n-butyllithium, meta-carborane, diphenylphosphonium chloride and palladium(II) chloride is 2.1-2.5:1:2:1. The molar ratio between palladium complex and halogenated hydrocarbon is 0.01-0.03:1. Preferred Method: In step (i), (a) the n-butyllithium solution is added into the meta-carborane solution at -80 to -75 degrees C, and then continue stirred for 25-35 minutes, and (b) the reaction solution is heating at room temperature and continue the reaction for 30-60 minutes. The post-processing process in step (iii) comprises after the reaction, allowing to stand the solution, filtering, drying the solvent under reduced pressure to obtain crude product, and separating the crude product by column chromatography.

TI Improving composition and color stability of healthcare wine comprises adding vitamin C to wine, stirring, dissolving, introducing an inert gas or carbon dioxide gas, removing oxygen, and sealing.
AU CHEN Z
   XU L
   WANG P
   SHAN Y
   HU L
AE JINPAI BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (JINP-Non-standard)
GA 201909302L
AB    NOVELTY - Improving composition and color stability of healthcare wine comprises adding vitamin C to wine, stirring, and dissolving; and introducing an inert gas or carbon dioxide gas, removing oxygen, and sealing.
   USE - Method for improving composition and color stability of healthcare wine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can remove the oxygen in the wine without introducing impurities, avoids reacting oxygen with ingredients in the wine during storage to produce suspended solids or sediments, and improves color stability and composition of the wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The wine has oxygen content of below 0.1-0.3 mg/L after introducing inert gas or carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of vitamin C is 10-500 mg/L. Preferred Method: The method before introducing inert gas or carbon dioxide is filtering using 0.1-0.5 mu m film. The flow rate of introducing inert gas or carbon dioxide is 0.01-1 L/minute for 5-30 minutes. The first step further comprises simultaneously adding 1-8 wt.% alcohol-soluble beta -glucan extract with vitamin C. The alcohol-soluble beta -glucan extract is prepared by extracting highland barley with acid; adding 0.05-0.2 wt.% alpha -amylase and 0.2-0.6 wt.% glucoamylase, enzymatically hydrolyzing at 40-60 degrees C, enzymolyzing, centrifuging, concentrating, precipitating alcohol, filtering, and drying. The acid extraction step is carried out by adding 15-25 times water to highland barley, mixing, adding citric acid to adjust pH to 4-6, and extracting for 2-3 times at 40-60 degrees C each for 1-2 hours.

PN WO2018054445-A1; DE112016007162-A5; CN109790016-A; IN201917012305-A; US2019308153-A1
TI Producing carbon-containing products comprising e.g. gasoline, diesel, paraffin and methane, comprises obtaining the carbon-containing products predominantly from the steam and from carbon dioxide.
AU SMOLKA P P
   SMOLKA P
AE SMOLKA P P (SMOL-Individual)
   SMOLKA P (SMOL-Individual)
   SMOLKA P P (SMOL-Individual)
   SMOLKA P P (SMOL-Individual)
GA 2018253680
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon-containing products comprising gasoline, diesel, paraffin and methane from the steam upstream or downstream of a steam turbine or from geothermally derived steam, comprises obtaining the carbon-containing products predominantly from the steam and from carbon dioxide at temperatures above 300 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon-containing products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out in cost efficient manner and produces high-quality carbon-containing products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fuel production plant for the production of carbon-containing products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is contained in the air and/or used in the exhaust of fossil fuel-powered device. The vapor is split into hydrogen and oxygen, where the cleavage takes place in the presence of metals or metal alloys. The cleavage takes place in the presence of metals comprising zirconium, palladium, rhodium and platinum, in the presence of zirconium alloy and/or in the presence of material from nuclear fuel rods. The products used in the step of cleavage of the vapor into hydrogen and oxygen is present after the reaction in oxidized form, and is again reduced in the step of cleavage of steam into hydrogen and oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The plant is arranged to first crack the vapor into hydrogen and oxygen. The plant comprises a heat pump and/or a heat exchanger, which act as an auxiliary device for cooling and/or heating of the steam, and/or which serve to heat the carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111632490-A
TI Treatment method to reduce emission of carbon dioxide involves putting different reaction material into different reaction container, collecting waste gas containing carbon dioxide, and guiding waste gas containing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHAO Y
   YANG X
   HUANG M
   ZHANG G
   JIA K
AE YUNNAN ENERGY INST CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2020883545
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment method to reduce emission of carbon dioxide involves preparing reaction material, preparing reflecting material for processing waste gas with carbon dioxide, putting different reaction material into different reaction container, collecting waste gas containing carbon dioxide by collecting device, and guiding waste gas containing carbon dioxide to desulfurization and depinning device by guide structure; guiding waste gas to interior of desulfurization and denitration device, processing nitrogen oxide and sulfur oxide in waste gas by processing agent in desulfurization and denitration device, removing nitrogen-containing substance in waste gas, introducing desulfurization and denitration treated gas into oxidation device, and first oxidation treating; and second oxidation treating, cooling gas, introducing waste gas into reaction tank, using sodium hydroxide solution in reaction tank to remove carbon dioxide, and reacting.
   USE - The method is used for treating to reduce emission of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses oxidation treatments to convert carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide, reduces waste gas pollution, and removes carbon dioxide using sodium hydroxide and sodium dioxide so that whole treatment is more complete. The product can be used for various industrial production, and reduces discharge of carbon dioxide and reduces processing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treatment method to reduce emission of carbon dioxide involves:
   (A) preparing reaction material, preparing reflecting material for processing waste gas with carbon dioxide, preparing reaction container, putting different reaction material into different reaction container, collecting waste gas containing carbon dioxide using collecting device, and guiding waste gas containing carbon dioxide to desulfurization and depinning device using guide structure;
   (B) guiding waste gas to interior of desulfurization and denitration device, processing nitrogen oxide and sulfur oxide in waste gas by processing agent in desulfurization and denitration device, removing nitrogen-containing substance in waste gas, introducing desulfurization and denitration treated gas into oxidation device, and first oxidation treating to gas so that carbon monoxide in gas is oxidized to carbon dioxide;
   (C) second oxidation treating to first oxidation treated gas so that remaining carbon monoxide in gas is completely converted into carbon dioxide, introducing gas into cooling device, cooling gas using cooling device so that gas temperature is reduced to normal temperature state, introducing waste gas into reaction tank, using sodium hydroxide solution in reaction tank to remove carbon dioxide, and reacting sodium hydroxide with carbon dioxide to generate sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, water and reaction gas reaching emission standard;
   (D) discharging generated discharged gas, introducing remaining liquid into processing device, processing liquid, adding sodium hydroxide into processing device, reacting sodium hydroxide with sodium bicarbonate in liquid to generate sodium carbonate, water and small amount of carbon dioxide, introducing carbon dioxide into post-processing device for processing, and introducing mixed liquid of sodium carbonate and water into sedimentation tank; and
   (E) precipitating mixed liquid to obtain water and sodium carbonate crystal, discharging water to corresponding device, recovering precipitated sodium carbonate crystal, and ending whole reaction process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction material comprises desulfurization and denitration material, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, copper manganese oxide catalyst and palladium-activated carbon catalyst. Preferred Method: The reduction treatment is carried out for 3-5 minutes.

TI Carbon dioxide organic amine adsorbent comprises at least one nano-material carrier and organic amines supported on carrier material.
AU LIU Q
   QIAO X
   QI L

   FEI Z
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   NANJING ZIHUAN ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202179422J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide organic amine adsorbent comprises at least one nano-material carriers and organic amines supported on the carrier materials. The carbon dioxide molecules have been pre-anchored on the organic amine.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide organic amine adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts a pre-anchor strategy to improve the amine efficiency of organic amines, combines the easily agglomerated organic amine molecules with carbon dioxide molecules in advance and combines them with the carrier to ensure that each amine atom has the possibility of binding with carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide adsorbent: has extremely high amine efficiency and excellent cycle stability, can adsorb carbon dioxide molecules efficiently and quickly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide organic amine adsorbent, comprising (i) taking a silane coupling agent with an amine group or a long alkyl chain as an organic amine source, mixing with surfactant and ethanol, fully stirring to dissolve to prepare a precursor liquid and (ii) introducing the precursor liquid and carrier material into a stirred reactor, passing carbon dioxide gas, fully reacting, carrying out suction filtration, washing and drying to obtain carbon dioxide organic amine adsorbent prepared by pre-anchoring method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier includes three-dimensional, two-dimensional or one-dimensional nanomaterials, preferably silicon dioxide, porous organometallic framework, graphene oxide and/or carbon nanorods. The mass ratio of the organic amine to the carrier is 1:1-9. In step (i), the silane coupling agents with amine groups or long alkyl chains are 3-(2-aminoethylamino)propyltrimethoxysilane, 3-(2-aminoethylamino)propyltriethoxysilane, 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, 3-(2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethylamino)propyl-trimethoxysilane, N-butylamine, N-n-butylethylenediamine and/or N-(2-ethylamino)-1,3-propanediamine; preferably is 3-(2-aminoethylamino)propyltrimethoxysilane or 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane. The washing solution is methanol, ethanol and/or acetone. Preferred Method: In step (i), the mass ratio of the organic amine to the surfactant is 1:2-4, the mixing temperature of the precursor liquid is 25-90 degrees C and the stirring time is 0.2-12 hours, preferably 3-9 hours. In step (ii): the precursor liquid and the carrier material are introduced into stirred reactor, the reactor is evacuated and 0.1-60 bar carbon dioxide is introduced, preferably 3-10 bar; after stirring at room temperature to 80 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours, preferably 2-5 hours, adjusting the temperature in the reactor and heating to 50-90 degrees C, preferably 55-60 degrees C and continuously stirring for 3-12 hours, preferably 3-6 hours; the specific process of suction filtration is the circulating water vacuum pump suction until the vacuum no longer changes; the specific process of washing is rinsing with the washing solution 2-3 times; the specific process of drying is 3-24 h at 40-90 degrees C, preferably drying at 40-90 degrees C for 12 hours and more preferably drying at 55-60 degrees C for 12 hours.

The connection portion of water storage well and carbon dioxide storage well are provided with water turbine (5). A water inlet and water outlet of water storage well are connected with water source. The carbon dioxide storage well is provided with heat exchanger (11) connected with heat storage system, carbon dioxide air bag (14), floating plate (15) and baffle (16). The carbon dioxide air bag is provided with carbon dioxide conveying channel (10), and temperature and pressure monitoring sensor. The baffle is set in middle of carbon dioxide storage well. The floating plate is set below the baffle. The water storage well is provided with liquid level meter (8). The top cover of carbon dioxide storage well is sealed.
   USE - Double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide air bag compression energy storage system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is working substance can effectively solve the problem of low compressed air storage density, at the same time, it can greatly reduce the volume of the storage tank, so that the system is more compact. The heat storage system makes the carbon dioxide expand to force the water liquid well side to return to the water storage well side through the heat exchanger, and pushes the water turbine to rotate quickly to do work power generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation method of double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide air bag compression energy storage system.

PN WO2022129863-A1; GB2603615-A
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from gas mixture            containing hydrogen, involves contacting gas mixture            with shaped sorbent comprising layers of            photopolymerized resin containing particles of            molecular sieve carbon dioxide sorbent material.
AU BOOTLAND A
   DAVIS D
   EDGAR J
   HIGGINS J
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
GA 202283850D
AB    NOVELTY - Process for removal of carbon dioxide from a                gas mixture containing hydrogen, involves                contacting the gas mixture with a shaped sorbent                comprising layers of photopolymerized resin                containing particles of a molecular sieve carbon                dioxide sorbent material.
   USE - Process for removal of carbon dioxide from gas                mixture containing hydrogen, preferably for                absorption of carbon dioxide from gas                mixtures.
   ADVANTAGE - The process ensures high efficacy for carbon                dioxide absorption from gas mixtures containing                hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The adsorption of carbon                  dioxide from the hydrogen-containing gas mixture                  is performed in a pressure-swing adsorption (PSA)                  process, a vacuum swing adsorption (VSA) process,                  and/or a temperature swing adsorption (TSA)                  process. Preferred Conditions: The adsorption of                  carbon dioxide from the gas mixture is performed                  at a temperature of 10-200 degrees C and at a                  pressure of 1-100 bar abs. Preferred Components:                  The molecular sieve carbon dioxide sorbent                  material comprises a zeolite material, preferably                  a zeolite material chosen from zeolite 13X and                  zeolite 5A. The shaped sorbent comprises 1-70                  vol.% molecular sieve carbon dioxide sorbent                  material. The shaped sorbent comprises 5-5000                  layers. The gas mixture containing hydrogen is                  derived from an electrolysis process or from a                  process that converts a hydrocarbon or                  carbonaceous fossil fuel, biomass or municipal                  waste to synthesis gas containing hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide. The hydrogen-containing gas                  mixture is a synthesis gas stream comprising                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide having a carbon                  dioxide content of 1-50 vol.%, preferably 10-30                  vol.%, also potentially containing water vapor,                  carbon monoxide and methane each in the range of                  0-1 vol.%, preferably 0-0.5 vol.%, with the                  remainder of gas mixture comprising hydrogen.                  Preferred Properties: The maximum particle size                  (Dv100) of the molecular sieve carbon dioxide                  sorbent material in the shaped sorbent is less                  than the layer thickness, preferably no less than                  a fifth of layer thickness. The shaped sorbent is                  foamed. Each layer of layers in the shaped                  sorbent has a thickness of 10-300 mu m,                  preferably 20-100 mu m. The shaped sorbent is a                  particulate material with a cross-sectional                  length width or height, of 0.3-100 mm, preferably                  0.3-50 mm. The shaped sorbent is a flow-through                  monolith with a cross-sectional length width or                  height, of 10-200 cm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The photopolymerized                  resin is derived from a photopolymer comprising a                  mixture of multifunctional monomers and oligomers                  functionalized by an acrylate, and preferably                  photoinitiator. The photopolymer is a                  urethane-based photopolymer.

PN JP2021116294-A
TI Effective usage system of recovery carbon dioxide, has structure which is adjusted so that hydrogen and carbon dioxide is supplied to methanation reaction device, when filling rate of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is more than predetermined value.
AU ORITA H
   INAGAKI R
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 202191898U
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a carbon dioxide separation recovery installation (6,7) which separates and recovers a carbon dioxide (203) from the gas containing a carbon dioxide. The methanation installation has a methanation reaction device (20) which generates methane (208) using the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen (207) is provided. A hydrogen storage unit (15) stores the hydrogen generated with the water electrolysis facility (12). A carbon-dioxide-storage unit stores the carbon dioxide recovered by the carbon dioxide separation recovery installation. The filling rate of the hydrogen in the hydrogen storage unit and the filling rate of the carbon dioxide in the carbondioxide-storage unit are settled in a predetermined range. The structure is adjusted so that the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide is supplied to the methanation reaction device, when the filling rate of the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide is more than predetermined value.
   USE - Effective usage system of recovery carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The generated electric power by the renewable energy fluctuates when a recovery carbon dioxide is used effectively. The flow volume of the generated water matter is fluctuated in connection with the system. Each flow volume of the hydrogen is supplied to a methanation installation with respect to the flow volume of the generation carbon dioxide fluctuated by the load fluctuation of the installation which emits a carbon dioxide, and the flow volume of the recovery carbon dioxide fluctuated in connection with the system. A carbon dioxide is stably made regularity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The water electrolysis facility electrolyzes water (209) using the electric power obtained with the renewable energy power generation equipment (11), and generates hydrogen. The hydrogen storage unit contains a hydrogen storage tank or the canister which contained the hydrogen storage alloy. The carbon-dioxide-storage unit contains a carbon-dioxide storage tank (9). The carbon-dioxide flow regulating valve and a carbon-dioxide flowmeter (18a) are installed between the carbon-dioxide-storage unit and the methanation reaction device. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) The utilization method of a recovery carbon dioxide involves separating and recovering a carbon dioxide from the gas containing a carbon dioxide. The electric power is produced by a renewable energy. The water is electrolyzed using the electric power obtained at the renewable energy electric power generation process and the hydrogen is generated. The methane is generated using the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen. The hydrogen obtained at the water electrolysis process is stored. The carbon dioxide obtained by the carbon dioxide separation collection process is stored. The filling rate of the hydrogen in the hydrogen storage process and the filling rate of the carbon dioxide in the carbon-dioxide-storage process is settled in a predetermined range. The hydrogen and the carbon dioxide are supplied to the methanation process, when the filling rate of the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide is more than predetermined value;
   (2) a control method of the effective usage system of the recovery carbon dioxide involves installing the carbon-dioxide filling amount measurement unit for the carbon-dioxide-storage unit. The second threshold value of the first threshold value of the carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide and the third threshold value of the carbon dioxide are set to order expensive about the filling rate of the carbon dioxide calculated from the filling amount of the carbon dioxide measured by the carbon-dioxide filling amount measurement unit. The filling of the carbon dioxide to the carbon-dioxide storage tank is stopped when the filling rate of the carbon dioxide is more than the first threshold value of the carbon dioxide. The stop of the filling is maintained until the filling rate of the carbon dioxide falls to the second threshold value of the carbon dioxide. The filling of the carbon dioxide is restarted when the filling rate of the carbon dioxide becomes second threshold value or less of the carbon dioxide. The release of the carbon dioxide from the carbondioxide storage tank is stopped when the filling rate of the carbon dioxide becomes third threshold value or less of the carbon dioxide. The stop of the release is maintained until the filling rate of the carbon dioxide increases to the second threshold value of the carbon dioxide. The release of the carbon dioxide is restarted when the filling rate of the carbon dioxide becomes second threshold value or less of the carbon dioxide; and
   (3) a display apparatus used for the effective usage system of the recovery carbon dioxide, comprises filling rate of each of the carbon-dioxide-storage unit and the hydrogen storage unit or time-dependent change of filling amount is displayed with a graph. The display apparatus displays the threshold value of the carbon-dioxide-storage unit and the hydrogen storage unit on the graph. The filling of each of the carbondioxide-storage unit and the hydrogen storage unit and the state of release are displayed on the same screen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram which shows the recovery carbondioxide effective usage system.
   Effective usage system (1)
   Carbon dioxide separation recovery installation (6,7)
   Carbon-dioxide storage tank (9)
   Renewable energy power generation equipment (11)
   Water electrolysis facility (12)
   Hydrogen storage unit (15)
   Carbon-dioxide flowmeter (18a)
   Methanation reaction device (20)
   Carbon dioxide (203)
   Hydrogen (207)
   Methane (208)
   Water electrolysis facility electrolyzes water (209)

TI Carbon dioxide absorbent used in apparatus for            separating and recovering carbon dioxide comprises            heterocyclic amine and the heterocyclic amine having            specified pKa of any one amino group included in            heterocyclic ring and organic solvent.
AU SUZUKI A

   MURAI S

GA 201925844B
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises:                heterocyclic amine (I), and the heterocyclic amine                having a pKa of any one amino group included in a                heterocyclic ring (1) of 7-8.6; and an organic                solvent having a boiling point of 150 degrees C or                more at 1 atm, where a total mass of the                heterocyclic amine and the organic solvent is                70-100 mass% of the carbon dioxide absorbent.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is useful in                apparatus for separating and recovering carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus exhibits excellent operability,                absorption characteristics, and desorption                characteristics of carbon dioxide and is                cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises:                heterocyclic amine of formula (I), and the                heterocyclic amine having a pKa of any one amino                group included in a heterocyclic ring (1) of 7-8.6;                and an organic solvent having a boiling point of                150 degrees C or more at 1 atm, where a total mass                of the heterocyclic amine and the organic solvent                is 70-100 mass% of the carbon dioxide                absorbent.
   R1-R4 = H, oxygen double-bonded with carbon of                the heterocyclic ring, CH3, OH, or an alkyl chain                optionally including a functional group; and
   X1 = H, CH3, CO, C(O)OC2H5, C(O)OC3H7,                C(O)OCH(CH3)2, C(O)OC(CH3) 3, C(O)OCH3, C(O)CH3, or                C(O)C2H5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                apparatus of separating and recovering carbon                dioxide, using the carbon dioxide absorbent, the                carbon dioxide absorbent being a carbon dioxide                unabsorbed carbon dioxide absorbent, comprising                absorption tower configured to separate and recover                carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide                by contacting the gas containing carbon dioxide                with the carbon dioxide unabsorbed carbon dioxide                absorbent to allow carbon dioxide to be absorbed in                the carbon dioxide unabsorbed carbon dioxide                absorbent and a carbon dioxide absorbed carbon                dioxide absorbent is obtained and regeneration                tower configured to regenerate the carbon dioxide                absorbed carbon dioxide absorbent by desorbing                carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide absorbed                carbon dioxide absorbent which has absorbed carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  an alcohol having 6-12 carbon atoms. The organic                  solvent has an amide bond. The organic solvent is                  at least one of dimethylacetamide, N-methyl                  pyrrolidone or dimethylformamide. The ratio of                  the heterocyclic amine to the organic solvent is                  1.5-9. When carbon dioxide is dissolved at a                  saturation concentration in the carbon dioxide                  absorbent, the carbon dioxide absorbent is in a                  single phase state. The viscosity of the carbon                  dioxide absorbent is 0-200 cps at 25 degrees C.                  The absorbent is a carbon dioxide unabsorbed                  carbon dioxide absorbent, the carbon dioxide                  unabsorbed carbon dioxide absorbent is used in an                  apparatus of separating and recovering carbon                  dioxide, the apparatus including: an absorption                  tower configured to separate and recover carbon                  dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide by                  contacting the gas containing carbon dioxide with                  the carbon dioxide unabsorbed carbon dioxide                  absorbent to allow carbon dioxide to be absorbed                  in the carbon dioxide unabsorbed carbon dioxide                  absorbent, and a carbon dioxide absorbed carbon                  dioxide absorbent is obtained; and a regeneration                  tower configured to regenerate the carbon dioxide                  absorbed carbon dioxide absorbent by desorbing                  carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide absorbed                  carbon dioxide absorbent which has absorbed                  carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide unabsorbed                  carbon dioxide absorbent is in a single phase                  state, and the carbon dioxide absorbed carbon                  dioxide absorbent is in a single phase                  state.
   EXAMPLE - Bubbling of carbon dioxide-containing gas was                performed on carbon dioxide absorbent in which 80                wt.% of 1-ethoxy-carbonylpiperazine and 20 wt.% of                N-methyl pyrrolidone were mixed with each other,                and absorption equilibrium amounts of carbon                dioxide at 40-70?C were measured.

PT P
PN CN114522518-A
TI Low-cost carbon emission reduction system useful            for carbon-containing recycling gas power plant and in            fossil fuel application field, comprises e.g.            low-pressure indirect cooling heat exchanger whose            carbon dioxide outlet connected with high-pressure            inlet compressor.
AU YANG J
   FENG Y
   QIN J
   WANG J
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN GRADUATE (HAIT-C)
GA 202275905S
AB    NOVELTY - Low-cost carbon emission reduction system                comprises a low-pressure indirect cooling heat                exchanger (8) whose carbon dioxide outlet is                connected with a high-pressure inlet compressor. A                carbon dioxide outlet of a high pressure compressor                is connected to a carbon dioxide inlet of a carbon                dioxide inlet exchanger. The carbon dioxide outlet                of the high pressure indirect cooling exchanger                (10) is connected between a pump and a carbon                dioxide inlet end of a pump (11). A carbon dioxide                outlet end of the pump is communicated with the                carbon dioxide inlet end of a hydrogenation                reaction heat recovery exchanger (12). The carbon                dioxide outlets of the hydrogenation reactor (13)                are connected to the carbon dioxide inlet end of                carbon dioxide pre-heater (3). The carbon dioxide                outlets of a gas turbine are connected with carbon                dioxide reflux inlets of the gas turbine. A flue                gas outlet of an electrolytic cell is connected in                communication with an oxygen inlet.
   USE - The low-cost carbon emission reduction system                is useful for carbon-containing recycling gas power                plant, and also useful in fossil fuel application                field e.g. thermal power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas-fired power plant reduces carbon                dioxide emission cost, reduces generation of                nitrogen oxide, saves cost of nitric oxide emission                reduction, improves carbon dioxide concentration,                reduces separation difficulty, condenses steam by                cooling the flue gas, and separates and purifies                carbon dioxide. The method does not need chemical                absorption, physical adsorption, air separator and                other complex device for separating and purifying                carbon dioxide, and saves device cost and operation                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                realizing low-cost carbon emission reduction based                on the low-cost carbon emission reduction system of                a gas-fired power plant.

PN CN112875803-A
TI Circulating carbon dioxide regeneration ion            exchange system comprises a carbon dioxide dissolving            tank, a cation exchanger tank and an anion exchanger            tank.
AU LI J
   BU Q
AE SHANDONG JINZE WATER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WATER RESOURCES RES INST SHANGDONG PROVI (SDWR-C)
GA 202164336G
AB    NOVELTY - Circulating carbon dioxide regeneration ion                exchange system comprises a carbon dioxide                dissolving tank (1), a cation exchanger tank (2)                and an anion exchanger tank (3). The carbon dioxide                dissolving tank is provided with a carbon dioxide                gas inlet. The water outlet at the bottom of the                carbon dioxide dissolving tank is orderly connected                with the cation exchanger tank, anion exchanger                tank in series through the connecting pipeline. The                connecting pipeline is provided with an inner                circulating pump. The water outlet of the anion                exchanger tank is connected with the water inlet of                the top of the carbon dioxide dissolving tank                through the inner circulating pipeline.
   USE - Circulating carbon dioxide regeneration ion                exchange system.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulating carbon dioxide regeneration                ion exchange system makes it possible to use                low-purity (3050%) carbon dioxide waste gas from                industrial emissions to desalinate water bodies,                and at the same time achieve 40% carbon emission                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cyclic                carbon dioxide regeneration ion exchange process                that realizes water purification and carbon                emission reduction at the same time, which                involves:
   (A) calculating required carbon dioxide gas                pressure according to Henry's law according to the                pKa value of the cation exchange resin and the                actual measured zeta potential;
   (B) comparing with the experimental results to                determine the best operating pressure;
   (C) calculating the total makeup water volume                required in the continuous stirring system                according to the regeneration performance test of                the cation exchanger;
   (D) switching the system to the internal                circulation regeneration mode;
   (E) adjusting circulating liquid flow rate to                2-15 anion exchanger bed/hour through the internal                circulation pump;
   (F) pressurizing the closed system to 2-15 kg                through the carbon dioxide cylinder connected to                the carbon dioxide dissolving tank, where system                circulates for 15-120 minutes to achieve the effect                of simultaneous regeneration of anion and cation                exchangers;
   (G) slowly releasing and collecting excess                carbon dioxide gas and release the pressure of the                closed system to normal pressure; and
   (H) connecting the anion exchanger tank and                the cation exchanger tank in sequence for the next                cycle of desalination treatment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                circulating carbon dioxide regeneration ion                exchange system.
   Carbon dioxide dissolving tank (1)
   Cation exchanger tank (2)
   Anion exchanger tank (3)
   High pressure resistant circulating pump                (4)
   Valve (5)
DC A91 (Ion-exchange resins, polyelectrolytes.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN US2022177389-A1
TI Heat treating hydrocarbons, comprises providing            hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with oxidant source and            carbon dioxide gas to boiler, separating water from            carbon dioxide in heated outlet gas and recycling            portion of separated back to boiler.
AU FERNANDES D P
   BUSS R
   CHEN D H
AE UNIV LAMAR (UYLA-Non-standard)
GA 202277869S
AB    NOVELTY - Heat treating hydrocarbons, comprises:                providing a hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with an                oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas to a boiler,                where the hydrocarbon fuel source is burned in the                boiler to produce a heated outlet gas comprising                carbon dioxide and water; separating the water from                the carbon dioxide in the heated outlet gas; and                recycling at least a portion of the separated                carbon dioxide back to the boiler.
   USE - The method is useful for heat treating                hydrocarbons (claimed) to form ethylene and/or                propylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: minimizes heat loss and achieve                essentially zero carbon dioxide emission to the                air; utilizes integrating the furnace exhaust heat                for preheating, steam generation, or power                generation (running a turbine) that reduces energy                consumption of the cracking system, at the minimum                natural gas consumption, the cracking system will                have the lowest carbon footprint; utilizes same                circulating carbon dioxide concept can be extended                to steam boiler to generate steam with carbon                dioxide production for enhanced oil recovery or                other carbon dioxide utilization purposes (e.g.                urea, baking soda, or polycarbonate); and utilizes                facility-wide carbon dioxide pipeline network to                deliver carbon dioxide as a working gas for major                combustion units e.g. furnaces, boilers, and                heaters without greenhouse and nitric oxide                emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: heating the recycled portion of the                  separated carbon dioxide stream with heat from                  the heated outlet gas exiting the boiler; heating                  at least a portion of the oxidant source with                  heat from the heated outlet gas exiting the                  boiler; providing the water separated from the                  water/carbon dioxide stream back to the boiler,                  and heating the water with heat generated in the                  boiler to produce high pressure steam;                  superheating the high pressure steam with heat                  from the heated outlet gas exiting the boiler;                  heating the mol sieve beds with at least some                  recycled carbon dioxide; superheating and cooling                  the heated outlet gas before separation; and                  pressurizing the recycle carbon dioxide before                  providing to the boiler. The water is separated                  from the carbon dioxide in at least one mol sieve                  bed. The mol sieve beds adsorb water at                  125degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon fuel                  source includes natural gas. The mol sieve beds                  include two mol sieve beds.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

   USE - The method is useful for electrosynthesizing                organic matter by catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide by microorganism cathode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has faster rate of reducing carbon                dioxide to synthesize organic substances; and                important application value for carbon dioxide                fixed conversion and synthesis of organic                chemicals.

   Anolyte (2)
   Anode titanium plate (3)
   Proton exchange membrane (4)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode electrode is                  made of titanium plate, and cathode foamed copper                  is made of foamed copper. The anolyte in the                  microbial electrosynthesis system is 6.5 g/l                  monopotassium phosphate, and the pH of the                  electrolyte in the anode chamber is adjusted to                  6.8 by 0.95 g/L sodium hydroxide. The anolyte in                  the microbial electrosynthesis system is 2.15 g/l                  dipotassium phosphate, 1.75 g/l monopotassium                  phosphate, 2.50 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 1.3 g/l                  ammonium chloride, 0.15 g/l potassium chloride,                  1.10 g/l magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.70 g/l                  sodium chloride, 0.25 g/l calcium chloride                  dihydrate, 0.60 g/l L-cysteine, 12 ml/l trace                  element solution, 20 ml/l vitamin solution, and                  catholyte pH is adjusted to 6.7. Preferred                  Method: In the microbial electrosynthesis system,                  the cathodic polarization power supply is                  controlled at -0.8 V to -1.2 V (vs silver/silver                  chloride).

AU LIU H
   HUANG Y
   LIN Q
   CHA Z
   LUO W
AE RUBBER RES INST CHINESE ACADEMIC TROPICA (CTAG-C)
GA 202008416M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide-based biodegradable polymer coated fertilizer comprises preparing coating material by uniformly mixing carbon dioxide-based polyol and catalyst, and heating at 70-150 degrees C; preheating fertilizer granules to 65-85 degrees C, then spraying the heated carbon dioxide-based polyol and the curing agent to the fertilizer particle surface to obtain the carbon dioxide-based biodegradable polymer coated fertilizer.
   USE - The method is for preparing carbon dioxide-based biodegradable polymer coated fertilizer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The use of carbon dioxide-based polyurethane as coating material to prepare coated controlled-release fertilizers can make up for the shortcomings of petroleum-based polyurethanes and bio-based polyurethane coating materials; and gives good coated release-controlled fertilizer release performance and excellent biodegradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide-based degradable polymer-coated fertilizer prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The diameter of the fertilizer granule is 3-5 mm. The fertilizer granule is large-particle urea, granular potassium fertilizer, diammonium phosphate, or granular compound fertilizer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide based polyol is prepared by using carbon dioxide and epoxy compound as raw materials through ring-opening copolymerization reaction, where molecular weight is 2000-3000. The curing agent is toluene diisocyanate, polymethylene phenyl diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, trimethyl hexamethylene diisocyanate, xylylene diisocyanate, dicyclohexyl methane diisocyanate, cyclohexane dimethylene diisocyanate, and/or methylcyclohexyl diisocyanate. The catalyst is stannous octoate, dibutyltin diacetate, stannous octoate, zinc naphthenate, zinc isooctanoate, bismuth isooctanoate, bismuth carboxylate, and/or zirconium carboxylate. The isocyanate ion/hydroxyl (-NCO/-OH) ratio of the curing agent and the carbon dioxide-based polyol is set to 0.75-2.00. The amount of coating material sprayed on the surface of the fertilizer particles is 4-10 mass% of the total mass of the fertilizer. The catalyst dosage is 0.1-1.0% of the mass of the coating material.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202057777W
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor (20) has a refrigerant heat exchanger (31) which is connected to the carbon dioxide compressor and heat-exchanging the carbon dioxide compressed in the carbon dioxide compressor with a refrigerant to cool and liquefy the carbon dioxide. A first pump (21) is connected to the refrigerant heat exchanger to pump liquid carbon dioxide from the refrigerant heat exchanger to pressurize the carbon dioxide to a supercritical pressure. A waste heat exchanger (41) heats the pressurized carbon dioxide from the first pump using waste heat. A turbine (42) is driven by high temperature and high pressure carbon dioxide heated in the waste heat exchanger. A cooling heat exchanger (43) cools and liquefies carbon dioxide from the turbine. A discharge flow path discharges high-pressure, liquid-state carbon dioxide from the cooling heat exchanger to a carbon dioxide demand destination.
   USE - Carbon dioxide compressor for compressing gaseous carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Controls the discharge pressure of the turbine so that the pure water efficiency becomes maximum. Increases the compression day of the final compressor, and provides optimum point of the pure efficiency. Easier to transport liquid carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide demand destination by liquefying the carbon dioxide from the turbine in the cooling heat exchanger.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide pressurization system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic block diagram of the carbon dioxide pressurization system for capturing and storing carbon dioxide.

AU KIM B
