GA 202150523V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reaction solution production tank (220) that produces a direct reaction solution by suspending the calcium-based metal oxide mixture in water without an extraction process. A trapped material storage tank (240) stores a reaction solution containing carbon dioxide traps in the reaction tank. A dehydrator (250) dehydrates the reaction liquid discharged from the trapped material storage tank to discharge moisture of the carbon dioxide trapped material containing calcium carbonate in a cake form. A dryer (260) dries the carbon dioxide trapped material containing calcium carbonate discharged from the dehydrator. The reaction tank is equipped with a carbon dioxide concentration meter at the final stage of the exhaust gas, injects the reaction solution using the measured carbon dioxide residual concentration, and discharges the carbon dioxide trapped material to automatically perform continuous operation.
   USE - System for continuous separation of carbon dioxide generated in hydrogen production process or fossil fuel combustion process.

The reaction solution is automatically introduced and the reaction is completed, and the carbon dioxide trapped material is automatically discharged, based on the residual carbon dioxide concentration after treatment, thus stably performing continuous operation.

PN KR2020043021-A
TI Carbon dioxide capture and distribution module system for use in greenhouse, has modular housing which includes base frame and mounting frame, and cylinder which stores carbon dioxide separated from greenhouse gas and discharged by membrane.
AU NAGYUDONG
AE GIVA INT CO LTD (GIVA-Non-standard)
GA 202036043F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first compressor (20) to compress and supply greenhouse gas. A dryer (30) dries the greenhouse gas to separate moisture. A pair of membranes (70) separates carbon dioxide from the greenhouse gas, and discharges the carbon dioxide for storage in a cylinder (80). A second compressor compresses and discharges carbon dioxide in the cylinder. A flow control unit (121) regulates and discharges the carbon dioxide from the second compressor and cylinder at constant pressure. A pressure switch (80) between membrane and tank controls the pneumatic valve (60) between dryer and filter according to the cylinder internal pressure. The system is contained in a module housing including a base frame and a mounting frame.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide from livestock houses, pig houses, and houses and distributing carbon dioxide to vinyl greenhouse for fertilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is modular and can be installed in a facility or apparatus in which greenhouse gas is generated so as to easily collect carbon dioxide in greenhouse gas emitted from the facility or apparatus with improved versatility and compatibility. Enables to to supply carbon dioxide collected in a required area simply and quickly as carbon dioxide is contained in a cylinder or tank. The valve can be protected so that the carbon dioxide collected can be moved using a general truck.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide capture and distribution module system.
   First compressor (20)
   Dryer (30)
   Pneumatic valve (60)
   Membranes (70)
   Cylinder (80)
   Pressure switch (80)
   Flow control unit (121)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110624370-A; CN210934379-U
TI Low-temperature methanol washing process carbon dioxide recovery system comprises a absorption tower, a carbon dioxide stripper, a first vacuum tank, a heat regenerating tower and a methanol/water separation tower.
AU WEN Y
   LIU R
   FAN X
   XU Q
   WANG X
AE GANSU HUATING COAL & ELECTRIC POWER CO (GANS-Non-standard)
   CHINA HUANENG COAL & ELECTRIC POWER CO (CHHN-C)
GA 202003780P
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature methanol washing process carbon dioxide recovery system comprises a absorption tower (1), a carbon dioxide stripper (2), a first vacuum tank, a heat regenerating tower (4) and methanol/water separation tower (5). The absorption tower is divided into four sections, the lowest section is a desulfurization section and the upper three sections are decarbonization sections. The inlet of the desulfurization section of the absorption tower is connected to the gas outlet of the first gas-liquid separation tank (6). The outlet of the desulfurization section of the absorption tower is connected to the carbon dioxide stripper. The outlet of the upper section of the carbon dioxide stripper is connected to the inlet of the first vacuum tank. The air outlet end of the first vacuum tank is connected to the top exhaust end of the carbon dioxide stripper. The liquid outlet end of the first vacuum tank is connected to the inlet of the heat regenerating tower.
   USE - Used as low-temperature methanol washing process carbon dioxide recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces hydrogen sulfide concentration tower, does not generates tail gas and desorbs all of the carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide product gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide recovery during low temperature methanol washing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the low temperature methanol washing process carbon dioxide recycling system.
   Absorption tower (1)
   Carbon dioxide stripper (2)
   Heat regenerating tower (4)
   Methanol/water separation tower (5)
   Gas-liquid separation tank (6)

PN KR2018138230-A
TI Method and device useful for reducing pH of waste concrete/recycled aggregate, comprises charging waste concrete/recycled aggregate and water in a reaction chamber, and reacting with carbon dioxide in a supercritical fluid state.
AU CHUNG C W
   LEE M
AE UNIV PUKYONG NAT (UYPN-C)
GA 201903311V
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing pH of waste concrete/recycled aggregate, comprises (a) charging waste concrete/recycled aggregate and water in a reaction chamber, and (b) reacting with carbon dioxide in a supercritical fluid state.
   USE - The method and device are useful for reducing pH of waste concrete/recycled aggregate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method and device can maintain pH of waste concrete and recycled aggregate to less than or equal to 9 without decreasing pH value even after lapse of reaction time, and can permanently crystallize carbon dioxide in the form of calcium carbonate, and is environmentally friendly due to utilization of carbon dioxide as reaction material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pH reduction device comprising a reaction chamber enclosing waste concrete/recycled aggregate and water inside and sealed to cause reaction with carbon dioxide in supercritical fluid state, a temperature controller for controlling a temperature inside the reaction chamber, a carbon dioxide storage tank for storing carbon dioxide supplied into the reaction chamber, and a pressure regulator for controlling the pressure of the carbon dioxide stored in the carbon dioxide storage tank and supplying the carbon dioxide into the reaction chamber, where the temperature controller and the pressure controller adjust the temperature and the pressure inside the reaction chamber so that the carbon dioxide supplied from the carbon dioxide storage tank in the reaction chamber becomes carbon dioxide in the supercritical state.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The temperature controller maintains temperature inside the reaction chamber at 50-80 degrees C. The pressure regulator maintains pressure inside the reaction chamber at 100-200 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the waste concrete/recycled aggregate and water are stirred and injected into the reaction chamber at predetermined ratio.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J02 (Mixing and including dispersing (B01F).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PT P
PN US2019202762-A1; US10696614-B2
TI Converting carbon dioxide to methanol involves irradiating carbon dioxide, water, and copper oxide having facet to form methanol, where water is present as liquid, irradiating involves exposure to ultraviolet to visible light.
AU WU Y
   RAJH T
   MCNULTY I
   LIU Y
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
GA 201959246B
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide to methanol involves irradiating carbon dioxide, water, and copper oxide having (i i 0) facet to form methanol, where i is 1-12.
   USE - Method for converting carbon dioxide to methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to convert carbon dioxide to methanol that has low cost and high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, which involves irradiating carbon dioxide, water, and Molybdenum disulfide which is adsorbed onto copper oxide to form carbon monoxide, where copper oxide has a (i i 0) facet, and i is 1-12.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The irradiating involves exposure to ultraviolet to visible light. The carbon dioxide is continuously flowed through a suspension of the copper oxide in water during the irradiating. The copper oxide having a (i i 0) facet is prepared by admixing copper acetate, sodium hydroxide, glucose, and a surfactant and heating the admixture to 60 degrees C for 30-90 minutes to form copper oxide having a (i i 0) facet. Preferred Condition: The wavelengths of exposure to light is 200-650 nanometer. The quantum efficiency is 50%. Preferred Components: The water is present as a liquid. The water is present as water vapor. The (i i 0) facet is (110) facet. The copper oxide having (110) facet is octahedral, truncated cubic, or a mixture. The surfactant comprises sodium dodecyl sulfate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110817797-A; CN213112522-U
TI Reformed and separated integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system comprises reforming separation device, three-phase heat exchange device, traps, water-cooled heat exchangers, and carbon dioxide liquefaction devices.
AU YUE X
   HAN D
   YUE Y
   LI J
   ZHAO J
   JIAO W
   CHEN F
AE ZHONGKE LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN (ZHON-Non-standard)
   CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECH (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 202018156H
AB    NOVELTY - Reformed and separated integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system, comprises a reforming separation device, a three-phase heat exchange device, traps, water-cooled heat exchangers, and carbon dioxide liquefaction devices. The reforming and separating device comprising an upper reaction chamber and a lower reaction chamber. The upper reaction chamber is with the lower reaction chamber, the upper reaction chamber is filled with a first catalytic filler, and the lower reaction chamber is filled with a second catalytic filler . The upper reaction chamber is provided with a first inlet for inputting methanol water vapor and a first outlet for outputting carbon dioxide mixed residual gas, inserting a hydrogen absorption tube into upper reaction chamber. The hydrogen absorption tube performs hydrogen absorption separation on mixed gas in upper reaction chamber. The output adsorbed hydrogen from hydrogen absorption tube.
   USE - The system and method is useful for reformed and separated integrated high-pressure hydrogen production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system and method circulates gas and purifies, has theoretical yield value more 100%, and hydrogen yield is greater than or equal to 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for high-pressure hydrogen production method, comprises using reforming and separation integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system, comprising (i) liquid pump sends methanol water into methanol water vapor pipe, the pump pressure is 18-50 MPa, heating methanol water to vaporize into methanol water vapor, introducing methanol water vapor in upper reaction chamber of reforming separation device, and methanol water vapor undergoes a reforming reaction in upper reaction chamber to generate hydrogen, the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and hydrogen absorption tube separates generated hydrogen gas, a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide into hydrogen, the gas phase components of mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide are 65-75% of hydrogen, and 20-20 26%, 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, (ii) hydrogen absorption tube separates mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and collecting separated pure hydrogen from output of hydrogen absorption tube, the remaining carbon dioxide mixed waste gas is output from carbon dioxide mixed waste gas outlet pipe, the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is controlled by liquid pump pressure, water-cooled heat exchanger controls temperature of carbon dioxide mixed residual gas, the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is introduced to a carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation, the gas phase group of carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is divided into 25-45% of hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0-3% water, 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, the pressure controlled by liquid pump is 18-50 Mpa, and temperature controlled by water-cooled heat exchanger is 18-30.8 degrees C (iii) separating carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into a liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in a carbon dioxide separator, and liquid carbon dioxide is output and collected, the composition of hydrogen mixed residual gas is 65-75% of hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide and 3-9% carbon monoxide, (iv) introducing hydrogen mixed gas into lower reaction chamber of reforming and separating device, and reformed mixed gas is prepared with water, water distribution according to carbon monoxide content, water distribution ratio (carbon monoxide: water) is 1: 1-20, reforming fed hydrogen mixed gas into water in lower reaction chamber to reform a mixed gas, the gas phase component of reformed mixed gas is hydrogen 62-77%, 22-27% carbon dioxide, 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide in reformed mixture, carbon monoxide ratio, closing to proportion of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and (V) reformed mixed gas is introduced upper reaction chamber and is mixed with mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, the hydrogen absorption tube performs hydrogen separation operation on reformed mixed gas and mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen absorption tube is a niobium tube, The first catalytic filler is a copper-based filler or a zirconium-based filler. The second catalytic filler is a copper-based filler or a zirconium-based filler. Preferred Method: The hydrogen absorption tube is a palladium film tube or a palladium alloy film tube, the first catalytic filler is a copper-based filler or a zirconium-based filler, the second catalytic filler is a copper-based filler or a zirconium-based filler, and the operating temperature of the heating chamber is 250-550 degrees C. The pure hydrogen outlet pipe is connected to a hydrogen storage tank, the pure hydrogen is introduced into hydrogen storage tank under pump pressure of a liquid pump, the hydrogen storage tank is connected to a hydrogenation machine, and hydrogen storage tank is connected to a hydrogenation machine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The output pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are all heat-exchanged and cooled after being passed through three-phase heat exchange device, and methanol water is heat-exchanged and vaporized into methanol water vapor through three-phase heat exchange device. The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.

PN CN111389048-A
TI Recovering carbon dioxide in production of supercritical extraction of vegetable oil comprises mixing extractant carbon dioxide with vegetable oil in extraction kettle in supercritical state and separating in separation kettle.
AU YANG Z
   ZHANG R
   XU K
   LUO C
   CHEN W
AE HUALU ENG & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2020668125
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbon dioxide in production of supercritical extraction of vegetable oil comprises mixing extractant carbon dioxide with vegetable oil in the extraction kettle in a supercritical state, separating in the separation kettle, making the produced vegetable oil continue to undergo three-stage flash separation and recovering by controlling the temperature and pressure and returning the separated carbon dioxide to the extraction kettle for recycling by stepwise pressurization.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering carbon dioxide in production of supercritical extraction of vegetable oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the recovery rate of the extractant carbon dioxide, reduces carbon dioxide emissions, saves resources and reduces production costs, since the amount of carbon dioxide escaped during the production process is reduced, the escape of vegetable oil products with carbon dioxide is also reduced and the yield of vegetable oil products is increased and ensures escape of product vegetable oil in the air is reduced and a clean production environment in the workshop.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) making the carbon dioxide-containing finished vegetable oil from the separation kettle enter first-stage flash evaporator to control the pressure and temperature of the first-stage flash evaporator, making carbon dioxide gas escape from the top of the first-stage flash evaporator after gas-liquid separation and pressurizing the released carbon dioxide by the first-stage pressurization unit and returning to the extraction system as an extractant, where the pressure of the first-stage flash evaporator is controlled to be 3-14 MPaG, preferably 5-12 MPaG and the temperature is 25-75 degrees C, preferably 35-65 degrees C; (ii) making the vegetable oil coming out of the bottom of the primary flash evaporator enter the secondary flash evaporator, controlling the pressure and temperature of the secondary flash evaporator, so that carbon dioxide gas escapes from the top of the secondary flash evaporator after gas-liquid separation, pressurizing the released carbon dioxide by the secondary pressurization unit and returning to the inlet of the primary pressurization unit. Where the pressure of the secondary flash evaporator is controlled to be 1-8 MpaG, preferably 2-6 MPaG and the temperature is 25-75 degrees C, preferably 30-60 degrees C; (iii) placing the vegetable oil coming out of the bottom of the second-stage flash evaporator to the third-stage flash evaporator to control the pressure and temperature of the third-stage flash evaporator, separating the carbon dioxide gas from the top of the three-stage flash evaporator after gas-liquid separation and pressurizing the escaped carbon dioxide by the three-stage pressurization unit and returning to the inlet of the second-stage pressurization unit, where the product vegetable oil coming out of the bottom of the three-stage flash evaporator has a low carbon dioxide content because the pressure and temperature are close to the ambient temperature, recycling large amount of carbon dioxide, and reducing the product oil brought out by carbon dioxide and improving yield of finished vegetable oil, where the pressure of the third-stage flash evaporator is controlled to be 0.1-3 MPaG, preferably 0.1-2 MPaG and the temperature is 25-60 degrees C, preferably 30-50 degrees C. The three-stage flash evaporator adopts a kettle-type flash evaporator and the top of the flash evaporator is equipped with sufficient gas-liquid separation space, with a defoaming device.

AB    NOVELTY - Equipment comprises first piping for receiving                a wet carbon dioxide recycle stream from a recovery                well. The wet carbon dioxide recycle stream                comprises water, carbon dioxide, natural gas, and                natural gas liquids. The dehydrator receives the                wet carbon dioxide stream from the first piping and                dehydrates the wet carbon dioxide recycle stream                using ethylene glycol to produce a dry carbon                dioxide recycle stream. The second piping receives                the dry carbon dioxide recycle stream from the                dehydrator. The natural gas liquids recovery column                receives the dry carbon dioxide recycle stream from                the second piping and separates the dry carbon                dioxide recycle stream into a carbon dioxide                reinjection stream and a natural gas liquids                stream. The third piping receives the carbon                dioxide reinjection stream from the natural gas                liquids recovery column and is configured to                transfer the carbon dioxide reinjection stream to a                reinjection well.

The second piping is configured to                receive the dry carbon dioxide recycle stream from                the dehydrator. The natural gas liquids recovery                column is configured to receive the dry carbon                dioxide recycle stream from the second piping and                configured to separate the dry carbon dioxide                recycle stream into a carbon dioxide reinjection                stream and a natural gas liquids stream.    (3) separating the dry carbon dioxide recycle                stream into a carbon dioxide reinjection stream and                a natural gas liquids stream; and
   (4) transferring the carbon dioxide                reinjection stream to a reinjection well.

TI Natural gas pre-processing system, has moisture-content removal portion that removes moisture content which distributes moisture-content adsorption agent to filled layer, and is contained in natural gas in natural gas.
AU KAKUTANI Y
   FURUBAYASHI Y
   UCHIDA H
AE JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
GA 2020C8808H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon-dioxide removal portion that removes the carbon dioxide which makes absorption liquid and the natural gas contact, and is contained in the natural gas. The moisture-content removal portion removes the moisture content which distributes moisture-content adsorption agent to the filled layer, and is contained in the natural gas in the natural gas, after contacting with the absorption liquid in carbon-dioxide removal portion. The carbon-dioxide adsorption agent for removing by absorption the carbon dioxide which did not remove and cut in carbon-dioxide removal portion and is contained in the filled layer. The exit side carbon-dioxide measurement portion measures the carbon dioxide levels contained in the natural gas in the exit side of the moisture-content removal portion.
   USE - Natural gas pre-processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The outflow of the carbon dioxide to the downstream side of moisture-content removal portion can be suppressed, even when it is a case where the natural gas containing the carbon dioxide beyond the management value flows out.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The moisture-content adsorption agent and carbon-dioxide adsorption agent are molecular sieves, and the carbon-dioxide adsorption agent has a mean pore diameter of a molecular sieve larger than moisture-content adsorption agent. The molecular sieve has moisture-content adsorption agent. The molecular sieve has carbon-dioxide adsorption agent with zeolite selected from a 4A-type, a 5A-type, 10 X types, and 13 X type. In The filled layer is separated into the layer from which the moisture-content adsorption agent and the carbon-dioxide adsorption agent differ. The entrance-side carbon-dioxide measurement portion measures the carbon dioxide levels contained in natural gas in the entrance side of moisture-content removal portion. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a pre-processing method of natural gas. The carbon dioxide which makes absorption liquid and the natural gas contacting is removed and is contained in the natural gas. The moisture content which distributes moisture-content adsorption agent to the included filled layer is removed, and is contained in the natural gas in the natural gas after making it contacting with absorption liquid. The carbon dioxide which did not remove and cut at process of carrying out a carbon-dioxide removal is removed by the distribution of the natural gas to filled layer in the process of removing moisture content. The carbon dioxide levels contained in the natural gas is measured after making it contacting with absorption liquid before implementation of the process of removing moisture content which is included after implementation of the process of removing carbon dioxide.

PN CN114910437-A
TI Measuring method for eliminating interference of            carbon dioxide in gas sample to carbon dioxide            concentration measurement, involves using first            standard curve of pre-established carbon dioxide level            and carbon dioxide concentration, and using second            standard curve and third standard curve.
AU LI S
   JI Y
   ZHU G
   MA Y
AE SHENZHEN XIANYA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A9104B
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises using a first standard                curve of a pre-established carbon dioxide level and                carbon dioxide concentration according to the                measured carbon dioxide (CO2) level of the gas                sample to obtain the CO2 concentration of the air                sample, using a second standard curve and a third                standard curve to obtain a carbon dioxide (CO)                correcting level of a carbon dioxide test level of                gas sample, adding the CO test level and the CO                conditioning level obtained in the step to obtain                CO level of the carbon dioxide sample, and using a                third-standard curve of the CO level and CO                concentration to obtain an CO concentration of gas                samples according to obtained CO level of air                sample.
   USE - Measuring method for eliminating the                interference of CO2 in gas sample to CO                concentration measurement including an automobile                exhaust, incomplete combustion of a mineral fuel,                harmfulness of air pollution, and the diagnosis of                disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively eliminates the                influence of carbon dioxide with high concentration                in the gas sample to trace carbon dioxide                concentration measurement, and overcomes the                difficulty of using infrared spectrum method to                measure trace carbon monoxide in breath to remove                carbon dioxide in the breath, eliminates the                interference of carbon-dioxide (CO2) in gas                sample-to-CO concentration measurement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                measuring system for eliminating the interference                of CO2 in gas sample to CO concentration                measurement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the measuring method for eliminating the                interference of CO2 in gas sample to CO                concentration measurement (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN KR2033745-B1; WO2020262795-A1; NO202200044-A; US2022266193-A1
TI Device for selectively collecting and separating            carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from e.g. power plant,            has heat exchanger for desorbing carbon dioxide of            adsorbent, where heat exchanger is fixed between            reaction tower and desorption tower.
AU PARK Y K
   SEO H M
   KIM K
   KIM D
   KIM K W
   KIM D J
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)

GA 201987980U
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a reaction tower (110)                arranged with a carbon dioxide adsorption unit                (111) that absorbs carbon dioxide from exhaust gas.                A desorption tower (120) is connected to the                reaction tower. An adsorbent heating unit (121)                heats adsorbent e.g. carbon dioxide adsorbent and                heated adsorbent, circulated through the reaction                tower. A heat exchanger (130) desorbs carbon                dioxide of the adsorbent. The heat exchanger is                fixed between the reaction tower and the desorption                tower in a parallel manner. A heat exchange part                (132) performs direct heat exchange or indirect                heat exchange between the carbon dioxide adsorbent                and the heated adsorbent.
   USE - Device for selectively collecting and                separating carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas                discharged from a power plant, a large combustion                furnace or a steel mill.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat exchanger is fixed between the                reaction tower and the desorption tower in a                parallel manner so as to reduce energy required for                collecting carbon dioxide, so that sensible heat                can be exchanged between the adsorbent in which                carbon dioxide is adsorbed and the heated                adsorbent, thus improving heat exchange efficiency.                The device reduces length of the reaction tower or                the desorption tower so as to reduce an                installation space of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbon dioxide collecting method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partially enlarged side                sectional view of a carbon dioxide collecting                device. '(Drawing includes non-English language                text)'

Preferred Components: The photo-culturing is carried out in a photo-bioreactor.

AU YU T
   XU L
   LI Q
   TAN Y
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 2022246835
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining reservoir                permeability distribution distributed along a                horizontal well through well logging data. An                injection pressure is obtained according to a                supercritical carbon dioxide flow rate of each                section. The carbon dioxide is set as a calculating                unit. A carbon dioxide inflow and a pore volume                multiple occupied by carbon dioxide in a period of                time are calculated. A permeability of a horizontal                section is obtained after the carbon dioxide                injected for five hours through a pattern plate. A                cycle time is determined according to carbon                dioxide injection time. A horizontal well different                permeability reservoir section carbon dioxide                injecting amount and the permeability after                injection are obtained.
   USE - Method for evaluating horizontal well carbon                dioxide injection section.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing quantitative                characterization of non-homogeneous reservoir                horizontal well carbon dioxide injection section,                and evaluating the horizontal well CO2 injection                effect, realizing efficient CO2 displacement and                geological sequeller, and reducing cost of                horizontal well injection profile test, simplifying                operation, and realizing evaluation of different                reservoir type horizontal well CO2 injection                profile.

PN CN111234799-A
TI Carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid comprises            thickener, polyelectrolyte composite nanomaterial,            crosslinking agent, foaming agent, saline water and            liquid carbon dioxide.
AU LI C
   ZHANG K
   TANG M
   XUE X
   LIU J
   ZHANG X
   WANG J
   YIN G
   LV C
   LI X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202051865M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid comprises                0.3-0.5 wt.% thickener, 0.25-0.4 wt.%                polyelectrolyte composite nanomaterial, 0.1-0.5                wt.% crosslinking agent, 0.03-0.05 wt.% foaming                agent, 10-30 wt.% saline water and remaining of                liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - As carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The fracturing fluid has high sand-carrying                performance, improves the stability of foam,                increases the life by more than 1 time, reduces the                liquid filtration rate, improves the fracturing                fluid jointing ability, improves the fracturing                fluid suspended sand and sand carrying performance,                and enhances the effect of fracturing                stimulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the fracturing fluid, involving (a)                slowly adding thickener to saline water under                stirring condition, uniformly stirring for 15-30                minutes, and completely dissolving the thickener,                (b) adding polyelectrolyte composite nanomaterial                to dissolved thickener, uniformly stirring, and                injecting to carbon dioxide storage tank, (c)                adding foaming agent to carbon dioxide storage                tank, and injecting liquid carbon dioxide using                carbon dioxide filling machine to obtain carbon                dioxide foam base in overnight, and (d) adding                crosslinking agent to obtained carbon dioxide foam                base in construction sand ratio to obtain the                fracturing fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particle diameter of                  silicon dioxide nanoparticles are 100-180 nm.                  Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent                  comprises zirconium oxychloride, titanium                  tetrachloride, potassium chromate sulfate,                  potassium dichromate and/or titanium                  tetrachloride. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of sodium chloride in saline water                  is 1-4%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The initiator is prepared                  by mixing tert-butyl hydroperoxide and sodium                  metabisulfite in a weight ratio of 1:(1.5-3).                  Preferred Component: The foaming agent is                  non-ionic alcohol foaming agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyelectrolyte                  composite nanomaterial comprises 25-35 %mass                  polyethyleneimine, 20-30 %mass sodium polystyrene                  sulfonate, 15-20 %mass silicon dioxide                  nanoparticles, 0.1-0.2 %mass initiator and                  remaining of deionized water. The thickener                  comprises hydroxypropyl guar gum, hydroxymethyl                  guar gum and/or carboxymethyl hydroxypropyl guar                  gum. Preferred Method: The polyelectrolyte                  composite nanomaterial is prepared by (i) adding                  polyethyleneimine and deionized water to reaction                  kettle to obtain polyethyleneimine aqueous                  solution, (ii) adding sodium polystyrene                  sulfonate and silicon dioxide nanoparticles to                  obtained polyethyleneimine aqueous solution, and                  uniformly stirring for 3-5 minutes to obtain                  mixed solution, (iii) adding initiator to                  obtained mixed solution, uniformly stirring for                  10-15 minutes, heating at 40-60 degrees C under                  nitrogen protection atmosphere, and leaving still                  for 5-8 hours to obtain colloidal product, and                  (iv) granulating, drying and crushing obtained                  colloidal product to obtain polyelectrolyte                  composite nanomaterial.

TI Vehicle-mounted carbon dioxide phase change cycle heating system for use in vehicle-mounted heater, has refrigerant that absorbs latent heat to complete heating of refrigerant, and carbon dioxide vapor that is converted into liquid carbon dioxide to flow out.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI YIBO ENTERPRISE MANAGEMENT CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C59356
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an evaporator (62) that is respectively connected to a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, to receive the liquid carbon dioxide provided by the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The liquid carbon dioxide absorbs the sensible heat of the air outside the vehicle flowing through surface to vaporize and heat up, and convert into gaseous carbon dioxide. A booster pump (69) is connected to the evaporator to alternately receive, pressurize and raise the gaseous carbon dioxide that is discharged from the evaporator. A condenser (72) is connected to the booster pump, to receive the carbon dioxide vapor discharged from the booster pump, and transfer the latent heat through the tube wall surface of the condenser. The refrigerant flows through the surface of the condenser, and absorbs latent heat to complete the heating of the refrigerant. The carbon dioxide vapor in the condenser, is liquefied to release latent heat, and is converted into liquid carbon dioxide to flow out.
   USE - Vehicle-mounted carbon dioxide phase change cycle heating system for use in vehicle-mounted heater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fresh air heat exchanger and filter are utilized to neutralize and purify the loaded refrigerant after cooling by the evaporator, and control the output of suitable fresh air temperature and fresh air rate to improve the comfort of the car. The service life and work efficiency of a single charge of the battery pack is improved. Vehicle-mounted refrigeration system realize a variety of temperature zones to control heat according to the size of the heating demand. The heat exchange efficiency and carbon dioxide recovery efficiency are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an on-board carbon dioxide phase change cycle heating method of the heating system.

TI Producing ammonia from methane-hydrate at location of methane-hydrate extraction involves providing at location of methane-hydrate extraction carbon dioxide to reacting with methane-hydrate to produce methane and carbon-dioxide-hydrate.
AU ZHAO J
AE ZHAO J (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 201921779C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia from methane-hydrate at location of methane-hydrate extraction involves providing at location of methane-hydrate extraction carbon dioxide to reacting with methane-hydrate to produce methane and carbon-dioxide-hydrate via first reaction, where first reaction is reacting carbon dioxide and methane-hydrate to form carbon-dioxide-hydrate and methane, providing methane produced from first reaction to react with water to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen via second reaction. The second reaction is reacting methane with water to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The produced carbon dioxide is provided to first reaction and reaching steady state first reaction and second reaction which are occurred simultaneously and feeding off of each other and nitrogen from air separation process is provided to react with hydrogen produced from second reaction to synthesize ammonia via third reaction by reacting nitrogen and hydrogen to form ammonia.
   USE - Method for producing ammonia from methane-hydrate at location of methane-hydrate extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first reaction takes place in molten methane-hydrate. Preferred Process: The method involves including second reaction by reacting methane and water to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen which is combination of methane steam reforming reaction by reacting methane and water to form carbon monoxide and three moles of hydrogen and water-gas shift reaction by reacting cabon monoxide and hydrogen to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The method involves processing third reaction by reacting nitrogen and hydrogen to form ammonia by widely using haber-bosch synthesis process. The processes of second reaction and third reaction take place on same ocean platform. The processes of second reaction and third reaction take place on separate ocean platforms which are with ten miles from one another. The processes of second reaction and third reaction take place on separate ocean platforms which are linked together via pipes from one another to transport both methane and carbon dioxide among platforms.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022032281-A1; US2022040668-A1
TI Reducing carbon dioxide emissions, comprises            contacting flue gas from source with carbon dioxide            adsorbing material to reversibly adsorb carbon dioxide            from the gas, and reducing the concentration of carbon            dioxide in the gas.
AU WESTON S C
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202221732A
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide emissions involves (a)                contacting a flue gas from a source with a carbon                dioxide adsorbing material to reversibly adsorbs                carbon dioxide from the flue gas thereby generating                an adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide,                (b) reducing the concentration of carbon dioxide                from the flue gas to less than 15000 ppmv, and (c)                stripping a major portion of the carbon dioxide                from the adsorption material enriched for carbon                dioxide using a regeneration process. The carbon                dioxide adsorbing material comprises a tetraamine                functionalized metal organic framework.
   USE - Reducing carbon dioxide emissions from fossil                fuel combustion sources e.g. natural gas combined                cycle power plants and thermoelectric power                plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high selectivity for carbon                dioxide over nitrogen dioxide, and is cost                effective, and has high performance under humid                conditions. (I): utilizes the tetraamine ligands,                which are specific to carbon dioxide and maximize                carbon dioxide capture; and utilizes the metal                framework, which has low cost, effective                performance under humidity conditions, and high                selectivities for the capture of carbon dioxide.                The method utilizes (I), which can be prepared in a                simple and cost effective manner, and can be used                for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas streams                of fossil fuel-fired power plants in which the                emissions are negative, essentially removing carbon                dioxide (CO2) from the combustion air back to                combustion air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                carbon dioxide direct air capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tetraamine                  functionalized metal organic framework comprises                  divalent cations and polytopic organic linkers.                  The divalent cation is chosen form one or more of                  magnesium, calcium, manganese, chromium, iron,                  cobalt, nickel, copper and zinc, preferably                  magnesium. The source is a natural gas combined                  cycle (NGCC) power plant. Preferred Composition:                  The carbon dioxide in the flue gas is reduced to                  a concentration of preferably less than 4000                  ppmv.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polytopic organic                  linkers comprise                  4,4'-dioxidobiphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylate                  (dobpdc4-),                  4,4''-dioxido-(1,1':4',1''-terphenyl)-3,3''-dicarboxylate                  (dotpdc4-), 2,5-dioxidobenzene-1,4-dicarboxylate                  (dobdc4-) or                  3,3'-dioxide-biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate                  (para-carboxylate-dobpdc4-). PT P
PN CN114230781-A
TI Preparing low molecular weight polycarbonate polyether polyol with autocatalytic effect, comprises e.g. adding double metal cyanide catalyst, amine initiator and epoxy compound into reaction kettle, then filling with carbon dioxide, reacting, then cooling, and eliminating residual carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG L
   DONG J
   LIU B
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 2022492540
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low molecular weight polycarbonate polyether polyol with autocatalytic effect, comprises adding double metal cyanide catalyst (DMC), amine initiator and epoxy compound into reaction kettle, then filling with carbon dioxide, reacting at 30-150degrees Celsius for 1-20 hours; then cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, and eliminating residual carbon dioxide to obtain final product, where the addition amount of the epoxy compound is 103-105 times the quality of the DMC; the molar ratio of epoxy compound to amine initiator is 5-50; the pressure range of carbon dioxide is 0.1-8 MPa; the amine initiator is an aliphatic amine initiator (I) or an aromatic amine initiators (II)-(VIII). The terminal epoxy compound is compound (IX) and the intramolecular epoxy compound is compound (X).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low molecular weight polycarbonate polyether polyol with autocatalytic effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The low molecular weight polycarbonate polyether polyol: has autocatalytic effect, adjustable functionality between 220, number average molecular weight precisely adjustable between 1000-100000, and the carbonate unit content upto 40-70%. The method has high selectivity, has content of by-product PC as low as 2-3 wt.%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low molecular weight polycarbonate polyether polyol with autocatalytic effect, comprises adding double metal cyanide catalyst (DMC), amine initiator and epoxy compound into reaction kettle, then filling with carbon dioxide, reacting at 30-150degrees Celsius for 1-20 hours; then cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, and eliminating residual carbon dioxide to obtain final product, where the addition amount of the epoxy compound is 103-105 times the quality of the DMC; the molar ratio of epoxy compound to amine initiator is 5-50; the pressure range of carbon dioxide is 0.1-8 MPa; the amine initiator is an aliphatic amine initiator of formula (I) or an aromatic amine initiators of formulae (II)-(VIII). The terminal epoxy compound is compound of formula (IX) and the intramolecular epoxy compound is compound of formula (X).
   z = number of primary (secondary) amine groups in the amine compound i.e. 1-20 (when z is 1, the aliphatic amine initiator is a monofunctional aliphatic primary or secondary amine compound; when z is 2, the aliphatic amine initiator is a bifunctional aliphatic primary or secondary amine compound;
   and when z is 3-20, the aliphatic amine initiator is a multifunctional aliphatic primary or secondary amine compound);
   n = 1-10;
   R = H, 2-30C saturated or unsaturated alkyl or its branched isomers, cycloalkyl, haloalkyl, alkoxy, ester or aryl; R1 and R2 = 2-30C saturated or unsaturated (halo)alkyl groups and their branched isomers, cycloalkyl groups, alkoxy groups, ester groups or aryl groups;
   R1-R5 (in monofunctional aromatic primary or secondary amine compound) = hydrogen atom, halogen atom, aniline, o-toluidine, m-toluidine, p-methylaniline, 2,3-dimethylaniline, 2,4-dimethylaniline, 2,5-dimethylaniline, 2,6-dimethylaniline, o-ethylaniline, p-butylaniline, o-chloroaniline, o-bromoaniline, p-nitroaniline, o-nitroaniline, 2-aminoanthracene, 3-aminoperylene, N-ethylaniline, N-butylaniline, allylaniline or N-phenyl-4-methoxybenzylamine;
   R1-R5 (in the described bifunctional aromatic primary or secondary amine compound) = Hydrogen atom, halogen atom, p-xylylenediamine, m-xylylenediamine, diethyltoluenediamine, dimethylthiotoluenediamine, 4,4'-Methylenebis(2-ethyl)aniline (MOEA), 4,4'-methylenebis(2,6-diethylaniline) (MDEA), 4,4'-diaminodicyclohexylmethane (PACM), 4,4'-Methylenebis(6-methyl-2-ethylaniline) (MMEA), 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dichlorodiphenylmethane (MOCA), 1,1-bis(4-aminophenyl)cyclohexane, 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane (MDA) ), 3,6-thioanthracene diamine, 4-(4-amino-3,5-dimethylbenzyl)-2,6-dimethylaniline, 9,9-bis(4-aminophenyl)fluorene , 4,4'-methylenebis(3-chloro-2,6-diethylaniline), 4,4'-methylenebis-(2,6-diisopropylaniline) (MDPA), 4,4'-Methylenebis(2-nitroaniline), 2,2-bis(4-aminophenyl)propane, cardanol diamine or, 2,3-diaminonaphthalene 2,6-di aminoanthracene, N,N'-diphenylethylenediamine, N-phenylethylenediamine or N-phenylaminoacetic acid ethyl ether;
   R1-R5 (in multifunctional aromatic primary or secondary amine compound) = hydrogen atoms, halogen atom, 4,4',4"-triaminotriphenylmethane, tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)methane, polyamine modified curing agent, 3,4,9,10-perylenetetramine or 2,3,6,7-tetraaminoanthracene, 3,3'-diaminobenzidine or tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The terminal epoxy compound is monofunctional ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, 4-vinyl epoxycyclohexane, glycidyl ether, glycidyl ester, styrene oxide, alkyl-substituted styrene oxide; bifunctional bisphenol A epoxy resin, phenol type phenolic resin, bisphenol S type epoxy resin, 1,4-butanediol diglycidyl ether, polypropylene glycol diglycidyl ether or polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether; multifunctional glycerol triglycidyl ether, trimethylolpropane triglycidyl ether, pentaerythritol tetraglycidyl ether. The intramolecular epoxy compound is epoxycyclohexane, 4,4-dimethyl-3,5,8-trioxabicyclo(5,1,0)octane, 1,2-epoxycyclopentane, 3,4-epoxytetrahydrofuran, 4-vinyl epoxycyclohexane, bis(7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)3-heptylmethyl) adipate and intramolecular epoxy compounds obtained by oxidation of intramolecular unsaturated double bonds.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R, R1 (in monofunctional aliphatic primary or secondary amine compound) = Hydrogen atom, propylamine, hexylamine, dodecylamine, hexadecylamine, octadecylamine, isopropylamine, tert-butylamine, 3-butenamine, isobutylamine, lysine, cysteine, glutamic acid, benzylamine, phenethylamine, 2-Chlorophenethylamine, (4-benzyl-1,4-oxa-2-yl)methanamine, 2-aminomethyl-4-(4-fluorobenzyl)morpholine, diethylamine, diethylamine Butylamine, dibenzylamine, diallylamine, diisobutylamine, N-ethylbenzylamine, N-benzylglycine ethyl ester or N-ethyl-n-butylamine;

PN CN112304842-A; CN112304842-B
TI Performing shale oil carbon dioxide/nitrogen            alternately displacing injection quantity simulation            analysis involves obtaining carbon dioxide/nitrogen            alternately displacing injection amount adjusting            expression of shale oil.
AU CAO Q
   QI M
   HUANG Y
   ZHANG Y
   PENG X
   DU H
   LIU H
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   SICHUAN KEYUAN ENG TEST CENT (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202113770C
AB    NOVELTY - Performing shale oil carbon dioxide/nitrogen                alternately displacing injection quantity                simulation analysis involves taking a shale core                after pre-processing hole permeability test.                Initial porosity of the shale core is measured. The                shale core is vacuumized. Saturated stratum water                is pressurized to set pressure. Manganese water is                utilized to drive ground layer water. Total oil                displacement efficiency of carbon dioxide/nitrogen                alternately displacing shale oil is calculated                based on curve area difference of a nuclear                magnetic resonance spectrum. Porosity change rate                of the shale core is calculated. Injection amount                adjusting expression of the shale oil is                obtained.
   USE - Method for performing shale oil carbon                dioxide/nitrogen alternately displacing injection                quantity simulation analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adjusting reference of                carbon dioxide/nitrogen injection amount in shale                oil carbon dioxide/nitrogen alternately displacing                mining process so as to improve carbon                dioxide/nitrogen oil displacement total efficiency                in actual production process, thus improving                extraction rate of shale oil and reducing use                amount of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a shale                oil carbon dioxide/nitrogen alternate flooding                injection quantity simulation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The total oil                  displacement rate is equal to the curve area                  difference between the nuclear magnetic resonance                  spectrum N obtained at the Nth time and the                  nuclear magnetic resonance T2 spectrum A in the                  initial state and the curve of the nuclear                  magnetic resonance T2 spectrum A in the initial                  state. The expression of the displacement                  efficiency change of multiple displacements of                  shale oil is obtained by obtaining the nuclear                  magnetic resonance T2 spectrum area difference                  measured after any two adjacent displacements,                  obtaining the oil displacement efficiency of any                  single shale oil displacement according to the                  NMR T2 spectral area difference measured after                  any two adjacent displacements. A polynomial                  equation is used to fit the relationship between                  displacement efficiency and the number of times                  of CO2/N2 alternate displacement of shale oil to                  obtain the displacement efficiency of multiple                  displacements of shale oil. The porosity change                  rate is equal to the ratio of the difference                  between the initial porosity p0 and the porosity                  p to the initial porosity p0, and the specific                  expression is P change=(p0-p)/ p0x 100%. The                  adjustment expression of the injection amount of                  the CO2/N2 alternate displacement shale oil is                  obtained by substituting the porosity change rate                  into the oil displacement efficiency change                  expression of the multiple displacement shale oil                  to obtain the relationship between the porosity                  change rate and the number of alternate CO2/N2                  injections, establishing a calculation formula                  for each time the CO2/N2 alternate injection                  amount and the porosity of the shale core during                  this alternate CO2/N2 injection, and substituting                  the calculation formulation into the relational                  formulation to obtain an adjustment expression                  for the injection volume of CO2/N2 alternate                  displacement of shale oil. The first injection                  quantity of carbon dioxide is 0.3 times the                  initial shale core pore volume. The initial                  injection volume of nitrogen is 0.2 times the                  initial pore volume of the shale core, the                  injection pressure of carbon dioxide and nitrogen                  is 20-30 megapascals (MPa). The pretreatment of                  the shale core includes washing and drying of the                  shale core. The manganese water is prepared by                  using formation water as a solvent.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-E07; S03-F06B
IP G01N-015/08; G01N-024/08

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polymerization                  reaction is selected from living polymerization                  reactions such as reversible addition                  fragmentation chain-transfer (RAFT), atom                  transfer radical polymerization (ATRP) or                  nitroxide mediated radical polymerization (NMRP),                  preferably the reaction is a RAFT polymerization. The initiator is selected from the                  group consisting of a peroxy initiator or an azo                  initiator. The peroxygen initiator is selected                  from benzoyl peroxide, ammonium persulfate,                  potassium persulfate or the like, and the azo                  initiator is preferably azobisisobutyronitrile or                  4,4'-azobis (4- Cyanovaleric acid),                  azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride,                  azodiisopropylimidazoline and                  azodiisopropylimidazoline hydrochloride. PN CN114135353-A
TI Method for starting supercritical carbon dioxide device with fixed quality control, involves recycling redundant carbon dioxide working substance in experiment device when compressor inlet pressure is higher than set point.
AU ZHUO W
   HUANG Y
   LIU G
   WANG J
   ZANG J
AE CHINA NUCLEAR POWER DESIGN INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2022387151
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using an injection unit to inject low-density supercritical carbon dioxide into an experimental device. A densitometer is used to monitor density of a monitoring point in the experimental device to ensure density of the experiment device is equivalent to density of an injected carbon dioxide working substance. The experimental device is started. An injection unit is started when compressor inlet pressure is lower than a set point. Carbon dioxide working materials are continuously supplemented to the experimental equipment. A recovery unit is turned on when compressor pressure is higher than the set point, and recycled the redundant carbon dioxide work materials in the experiment equipment.
   USE - Method for starting supercritical carbon dioxide device e.g. Brayton cycle device, with fixed quality control.
   ADVANTAGE - The starting method effectively reduces the filling amount of the carbon dioxide working substance in the supercritical carbon dioxide experiment device, and solves the problem that the working substance is wasted when the conventional experiment device is started.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide device starting system with fixed quality control.

TI Fixation of carbon dioxide used for treating            combustion exhaust gas, involves contacting mixture            containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride with            gas containing carbon dioxide, and contacting liquid            mixture and gas by feeding gas.
AU KENJI S
   SORIMACHI K
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
   KENJI S (KENJ-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
   SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
GA 202117188S
AB    NOVELTY - Fixation of carbon dioxide involves contacting                a mixture containing sodium hydroxide and calcium                chloride with the gas containing carbon dioxide,                and contacting the liquid mixture and the gas by                feeding the gas into the mixture. The concentration                of the sodium hydroxide in the mixture is 0.01-0.2                N. The concentration of calcium chloride in the                mixture is 0.05-0.5 mol/L. In the contact process,                the feeding is performed by a motor-driven pump.                The motor is driven by using the electric power                generated by solar power generation.
   USE - Fixation of carbon dioxide used for treating                combustion exhaust gas containing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables fixation of carbon dioxide                having reduced concentration, suppressed generation                of harmful gas, and suppressed heat generation of                sodium hydroxide even when a high concentration of                0.2 N or more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of fixed carbon dioxide, which                involves fixing the carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide fixation device, which                consists of contact unit for contacting a mixture                containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride                with a gas containing carbon dioxide, including a                container which accommodates the liquid mixture and                a gas feeding unit which feeding the gas into the                liquid mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves performing solar power generation and                  driving the motor by using the electric power                  generated by the power generation step, supplying                  the power generated by the photovoltaic power                  generation to the storage battery and performing                  motor driven by using the power of the storage                  battery, and switching the electric power in the                  contact process generated by the photovoltaic                  power generation, the electric power of the                  storage battery and the combination of the                  electric power generated by the photovoltaic                  power generation and the electric power of the                  storage battery. In the contact step, the gas is                  bubbling and feeding into the liquid mixture. The                  method further involves cooling the liquid                  mixture after the contact step. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the sodium                  hydroxide in the mixture is preferably 0.05 N or                  more. Preferred Condition: The liquid mixture is                  performed at 70 degrees C or more.

PN EP3673972-A1; WO2020136211-A1; CA3123713-A1; EP3902621-A1
TI Method of capturing carbon dioxide involves            contacting gas comprising carbon dioxide with liquid            alkaline solution, reacting carbon dioxide with alkali            metal hydroxide, and electrochemically converting            bicarbonate into organic compound.
AU BOUWMAN L
   PANT D
   BULUT M
   VANBROEKHOVEN K
AE VITO NV (VLAA-C)
   VITO NV (VLAA-C)
GA 202057944X
AB    NOVELTY - Method of capturing carbon dioxide involves                contacting a gas comprising carbon dioxide with a                liquid alkaline solution comprising an alkali metal                hydroxide, reacting carbon dioxide with the alkali                metal hydroxide to form a bicarbonate, obtaining a                bicarbonate solution, and electrochemically                converting bicarbonate into an organic                compound.
   USE - Method of capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently captures and converts                carbon dioxide with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) device (10), which consists of unit (11)                comprising a inlet (I1) for a gas comprising carbon                dioxide, inlet (I2) for a liquid alkaline solution                comprising an alkali metal hydroxide and outlet                (O1), unit (12) comprising an electrochemical cell                coupled to the outlet (O1). The unit (11) comprises                a contact system (115) configured to bring the gas                in contact with the liquid alkaline solution, in                which the carbon dioxide reacts with the                alkali-metal hydroxide to form bicarbonate. The                contact system is coupled to the outlet (O1). The                electrochemical cell (123) is configured to                electrochemically convert the bicarbonate into an                organic compound; and
   (2) ventilation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device for capturing and converting carbon                dioxide.
   Device (10)
   Units (11,12)
   Contact system (115)
   Electrochemical cell (123)
   Ion exchange membrane (126)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrochemically                  conversion of bicarbonate is assisted by a                  catalyst chosen from ruthenium, rhodium,                  palladium, silver, osmium, iridium, platinum,                  copper and gold. The liquid alkaline solution is                  an aqueous solution of alkali metal hydroxide.                  The electrochemical cell comprises an electrode                  comprising a metal chosen from ruthenium,                  rhodium, palladium, silver, osmium, iridium,                  platinum, copper and gold. Preferred Property:                  The liquid alkaline solution has a pH of 8-14                  when brought in contact with the gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic compound is                  an alcohol, a hydrocarbon, syngas or an organic                  acid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2019010791-A1; US10358901-B2
TI Carbon dioxide to hydrocarbon conversion device for converting carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons, has injector system which injects feed stream with carbon dioxide into reservoir up to operating pressure.
AU BAXTER L
   DAVIS N A
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   DAVIS N A (DAVI-Individual)
   SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 201903482Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a partially produced hydrocarbon subterranean reservoir with residual hydrocarbons. A carbon dioxide source is positioned on a surface proximate to the reservoir. A surface mounted high-pressure fluid injector system is connecting the carbon dioxide source and the reservoir. A backflow preventer is arranged in-line between the surface mounted high-pressure fluid injector system and the reservoir. The injector system injects a feed stream (520) with the carbon dioxide into the reservoir up to an operating pressure. The backflow preventer is maintaining the operating pressure in the reservoir and sequestering the feed stream in the reservoir. The sequestered carbon dioxide reacts with water and a portion of the residual hydrocarbons over a time period of the 5 years to produce additional hydrocarbons. The water is present in the reservoir and provided by the feed stream such that the additional hydrocarbons are recovered after the time period.
   USE - Carbon dioxide to hydrocarbon conversion device for converting carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The feed stream is prevented from flowing out of reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for converting carbon dioxide into hydrocarbons.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the device for converting carbon dioxide into hydrocarbon.
   During injection phase (500)
   During removal phase (501)
   Oil reservoir (504)
   Pipeline (510)
   Feed stream (520)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The feed stream comprises oxygen. The residual hydrocarbons comprise alkanes, cycloalkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatics, asphaltenes, and other organic compounds.

TI Carbon-dioxide removal device for submarine, has device body including dry regeneration absorption rotors intended to be rotated inside device body, and air outlet intended to remove air containing carbon-dioxide.
AU JIN S A
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 2018558852
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a device body (100) including dry regeneration absorption rotors (110) intended to be rotated inside the device body. An air outlet (101) for removing air containing carbon-dioxide is equipped at a rear end of the device body. A fan (120) is equipped at a front end of the device body to deliver the outside air to the dry regeneration absorption rotors. A carbon-dioxide storage unit (130) stores the carbon-dioxide removed from the outside air by the dry regeneration absorption rotors. The device body includes a rotary shaft (111) for rotating the dry regeneration absorption rotors.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide removal device for a submarine.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry regeneration absorption rotors are intended to be rotated inside the device body, and the air outlet is intended to remove the air containing carbon-dioxide, thus allowing the carbon-dioxide to be continuously removed by using the dry regeneration absorption rotors in a simple and economic manner while reducing the manufacturing cost and the running cost of the carbon-dioxide removal device.

   USE - The catalyst is useful for gas phase carbon dioxide catalyzed hydrogenation to produce carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high efficiency carbon monoxide generation rate and high carbon monoxide selectivity, and a stable catalytic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Transition metal phosphide catalyst, is new. The transition metal phosphide catalyst particles having a particle size of 8-16 nm, and the chemical formula is Ni12-Cux-P5/TiO2,
   x = less than 5-7.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the transition metal phosphide catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of the transition metal phosphide catalyst, comprises (i) adding the mixed metal salt of nickel nitrate and copper nitrate to deionized water, ultrasonically stirring makes it completly dissolved, adding ammonium phosphate, where the molar ratio of mixed metal salt and ammonium phosphate is 12:5, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hours, raising the temperature at 110-125 degrees C and stirring the reaction for 2-3 hours to obtain the precursor, and (ii) soaking titanium dioxide into the precursor obtained in the step (i), stirring at 60-70 degrees C for 30-45 minutes, drying at 100-120 degrees C, placing in a tube furnace, annealing at 300-350 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 8-10 hours, raising the temperature at 650-750 degrees C, and calcining for 6-8 hours under mixed reducing gas atmosphere to obtain catalyst Ni12-Cux-P5/TiO2. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of nickel nitrate and copper nitrate is 12:x, where the 5 less than or equal to x less than or equal to 7. Preferred Method: The temperature rising rate from room temperature to annealing temperature during the annealing treatment stage is 4-6 degrees C/minute. In the calcining stage, the heating rate from the annealing temperature to the calcining temperature is 0.8-1 degrees C/minute. In the annealing stage, the nitrogen gas flow rate is 0.5-0.6 l/hour. The mixed reducing gas in the calcining stage comprises hydrogen and helium according to the ratio of 10%:90%, and the gas flow rate is 0.25-0.4 l/hour.

PN CN112125927-A
TI Preparation of silane-coupling-agent-containing elastomer, involves reacting carbon dioxide and monomer containing epoxy group, and heating and reacting obtained monomer containing cyclic carbonate group with amino silane coupling agent.
AU WANG G
   HE J
   YU R
   WANG Z
   LIAO J
AE UNIV HAINAN (UYNN-C)
GA 2021013244
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of silane-coupling-agent-containing elastomer, involves reacting carbon dioxide and a monomer containing an epoxy group in the presence of catalyst (A), to obtain a monomer containing a cyclic carbonate group, mixing the monomer with a silane coupling agent containing an amino group, and heating and reacting in the presence of catalyst (B).
   USE - Preparation of silane-coupling-agent-containing elastomer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides elastomer, which can be hydrolyzed and combined with an inorganic substance, and has excellent bonding strength, high crosslinking density, and adhesive force.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compounds: The monomer containing epoxy group is epoxychloropropane or glycidyl ether monomer. Catalyst (A) is tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium iodide, potassium bromide, calcium chloride, and/or zinc bromide. The silane coupling agent containing amino group is 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, gamma -aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane, gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, gamma -divinyltriaminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, N-( gamma -dimethylaminopropyl)- gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, hindered triamino silane coupling agent, gamma -piperazinylpropylmethyldimethoxysilane, N-cyclohexyl- gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, and/or N,N-dimethylaminopropylaminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane. The reaction solvent in final step is absolute ethanol, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, or dimethylformamide. Catalyst (B) is triethylenediamine, zinc acetate, sodium acetate, and/pr zinc acetate-tributyl phosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of monomer containing epoxy group and catalyst (A) is (10-50):1. The molar ratio of monomer containing cyclic carbonate group and silane coupling agent containing amino group is 1:(1-1.05). Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide and a monomer containing an epoxy group are reacted at 0.5-2 MPa and 120-140 degrees C for 24-48 hours, while stirring at 200-400 rpm. The final reaction is carried out at 70-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours.

PN CN111978156-A
TI Preparing cannabidiol involves using supercritical            fluid chromatography to separate the sample solution            containing cannabidiol to obtain high-purity            cannabidiol, and adding supercritical carbon dioxide            with modifier.
AU YU C
   GAO W
   LIU X
   CHANG T
   ZHAO L
AE YUNNAN HEMPMON PHARM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9208Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cannabidiol involves using                supercritical fluid chromatography to separate the                sample solution containing cannabidiol to obtain                high-purity cannabidiol. The mobile phase in the                supercritical fluid chromatography is supercritical                carbon dioxide added with modifier.
   USE - Method for preparing cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cannabidiol that                is suitable for separation and purification of                various samples containing cannabidiol, and                suitable for industrial scale-up production, has                high purity of cannabidiol as 97.59-99.89%, large                sample amount, short separation period, significant                separation cycle, improves overall yield, reduces                the content of its main impurity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass percentage of                  the modifier in the mobile phase is 5-95 wt.%.                  The mass percentage of the water and/or alcohol                  compound in the mobile phase is 0.5-15 wt.%. The                  flow rate of the mobile phase when the sample is                  separated is 1-40 mL/minute. The mobile phase in                  the supercritical fluid chromatography is                  supercritical carbon dioxide added with modifier                  of 5-95%. The back pressure when the sample is                  separated is 10-25 megapascal. The flow rate of                  the mobile phase when the sample is separated is                  1-40mL/min. Preferred Process: The method                  involves preparing sample solution containing                  cannabidiol, directly dissolving the sample. The                  sample containing cannabidiol is transferred once                  to obtain sample transfer solution. The sample                  containing cannabidiol is extracted to obtain                  sample extract. Preferred Components: The                  modifier includes any one or combination of two                  of alkanes, halogenated alkanes or ester                  compounds. The modifier includes water and/or                  alcohol compounds, preferably alcohol compounds                  include methanol and/or ethanol. The stationary                  phase used in supercritical fluid chromatography                  comprises pure silica gel and/or group-bonded                  silica gel. The group-bonded silica gel includes                  linole ether-based bonded silica gel or                  sugar-based bonded silica gel. The method                  involves filtering microporous the sample                  solution to get filtrate, using supercritical                  fluid chromatography. The sample solution                  containing cannabidiol is pretreated. The sample                  solution is eluted with extraction column to                  obtain decolorizing solution.
