PN CN111392706-A; CN111392706-B
TI Recycling waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material comprises adding phosphate ester into waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, filtering, heating and calcining.
AU LI L
   LI Y
   LU C
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGDONG GUANGHUA SCI-TECH CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   ZHUHAI ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY MATERIALS CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2020676087
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material comprises adding phosphate ester into waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, filtering to obtain solid intermediate A, heating the solid intermediate A to obtain solid intermediate B, adding benzene ring-containing polymer into the solid intermediate B and calcining to obtain recycled lithium iron phosphate.
   USE - The method is useful in recycling waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; realizes low environmental pressure, controllable quality and easy industrialization; ensures good atomic economy and good performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The benzene ring-containing polymer is at least one of acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene terpolymer, polystyrene and lignin. The added amount of benzene ring-containing polymer is 0.1-3.5% the mass of solid intermediate B. The phosphate ester is at least one of phosphodiester and phosphotriester, preferably diethyl phosphate and tributyl phosphate. The added amount of the phosphate ester is 0.5-5 times the mass of waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material. Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 30-70 degrees C under 7.2-70 MPa. The process of heating is microwave heating which is carried out at 500-650 degrees C for 5-30 minutes. The process comprises adding benzene ring-containing polymer is added to the solid intermediate B and calcining under oxygen-free and non-reducing conditions to obtain recycled lithium iron phosphate.

PN CN111214846-A; CN111214846-B
TI Preparing Haematococcus pluvialis extract by mixing Haematococcus pluvialis powder with subcritical extraction solvent, extracting, and subjecting subcritical extract of Haematococcus pluvialis to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU YU S
   DU W
   ZUO S
   LV P
AE YUNNAN GREEN BIOLOGICAL IND PARK CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2020505300
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Haematococcus pluvialis extract involves: (1) mixing H.pluvialis powder with a subcritical extraction solvent, continuously carrying out subcritical extraction to obtain a subcritical extract of H.pluvialis and collecting the subcritical extract; and (2) subjecting the subcritical extract to continuous supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment to remove residual solvent in the extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing H.pluvialis extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and large-scale industrial preparation of the H.pluvialis extract using inexpensive device, with high yield, astaxanthin content of greater than 8 wt.% and residual butane content of less than 1 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation method further involves carrying out thin-film evaporation treatment after the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment. The process of continuous subcritical extraction involves using a subcritical fluid extraction device to carry out extraction at an extraction pressure of 8-12 MPa, preferably 10 MPa and an extraction temperature of 30-60 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C, continuously extracting at a separation temperature of 25-40 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C for 3-6 times, preferably 4 times, where each extraction time is 1-2 hours, preferably 1.5 hours, and collecting the extract obtained from each extraction. The process of continuous supercritical carbon dioxide extraction treatment involves: (1) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the H.pluvialis extract in a supercritical extraction equipment under conditions of an extraction temperature of 60-80 degrees C and a pressure of 15-25 MPa for 20-60 minutes, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.4-0.8 cm3/second; (2) then separating the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide through a separation kettle (I), and again separating the subcritical extraction solvent remaining in the extract of H.pluvialis through a separation kettle (II) to obtain supercritical extract of H.pluvialis, where the separation condition of the separation kettle (I) has a temperature of 50-60 degrees C and a pressure of 7-8 MPa, and the separation condition of the separation kettle (II) has a temperature of 30-50 degrees C and a pressure of 3-4 MPa; and (3) continuously processing the supercritical extract of H.pluvialis 2-5 times according to the steps (1) and(2), recovering and purifying the carbon dioxide and subcritical extraction solvent separated each time. Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the conditions of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction are carbon dioxide flow rate of 0.6 cm3/second, extraction pressure of 20 MPa, extraction temperature of 70 degrees C and extraction time of 30 minutes. In step (2), the separation condition of the separation kettle (I) has a temperature of 55 degrees C and a pressure of 7.5 MPa, and the separation condition of the separation kettle (II) has a temperature of 40 degrees C and a pressure of 3.5 MPa. In step (3), the repeated processing is 3 times. The thin film evaporation treatment is carried out at 70-90 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C for 2-5 seconds, preferably 3 seconds. Preferred Components: The subcritical extraction solvent is butane, where the mass-to-volume ratio of the H.pluvialis powder to the subcritical extraction solvent is 1 kg:(4-8) l.

PN PL431941-A1; PL241122-B1
TI Cutting and packing cheese i.e. Swiss-Dutch            cheeses by cutting cheese into slices and packing            cheese slices in unit package in protective atmosphere            i.e. mixture comprising carbon dioxide, nitrogen and            oxygen, and ripening cheeses.
AU GROCHOWINA A
   MERTA M
AE HOCHLAND POLSKA SP ZOO (HOCH-Non-standard)
GA 2020812024
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cutting and packing cheese i.e.                Swiss-Dutch cheeses involves cutting cheese into                slices and packing cheese slices in unit package in                a protective atmosphere i.e. mixture comprising                55-65% carbon dioxide, 35-45% nitrogen and 1% or                less oxygen content, and ripening cheeses in the                protective atmosphere, where the cheese is packed                into package formed in both the top and bottom of                soft foil.
   USE - The method is useful for cutting and packing                cheese i.e. Swiss-Dutch cheeses.

The                symmetrical metal complexes may also form light                emitting fluorescent molecules, e.g.                palladium-based complexes exhibit                photoactivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A symmetrical pincer metal complex of formula                (I) is new.
   M=metal;
   T, T'=N or C;
   Z=CH, C, or N;
   n=0-3;
   L=neutral or charged ligand; and
   R=alkyl, Nx, or CH2TMS.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                symmetrical bimetallic complex of formula                (II)..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is                  given.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R=Me, Et, propyl, butyl, pentyl, or                hexyl;
   M=Ni, Pt, or Pd;
   Z=N in formula (II);
   R=Bu in formula (II);
   MLn=Rh(COD)X in formula (II); and
   X=I, Br, or Cl in formula (II).
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 31 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed,                  e.g.                  2-(1,3-bis(N-butylimidazol-2-ylidene)phenylene)(chloro)nickel(II)                  (2);                  1,3-bis(1-butylimidazolene-3-yl)benzenepalladium(II)acetate;                  2-(1,3-bis(N-butyl-imidazol-2-ylidene)phenylene)phenylacetyleneylplatinum(II);                  2-(1,3-bis(N-butyl-imidazol-2-ylidene)phenylene)(trifluoroacetato)platinum(II);                  and                  1,3-bis(1-butylimidazolene-3-yl)benzenepalladium(II)phenoxide.
   EXAMPLE - Under an inert atmosphere                1,3-bis(1-benzylimidizol-3-yl)benzene dichloride                (0.307 g) and tetrakis(dimethylamino)zirconium(IV)                (Zr(NMe2)4) (0.301 g) were combined in                dichloromethane (DCM) (10 ml) and stirred for 1                hour. Nickel dichloride (NiCl2) (glyme) (0.168 g)                was added and stirred for 3 hours. Water (0.6 ml)                was added, producing a precipitate that was removed                and washed with DCM (3x 10 ml). The filtrate was                concentrated, and the resulting orange-yellow solid                was dried under reduced pressure. Isopropyl alcohol                (2 ml) was added, and resulting suspension was                vigorously stirred for 2 minutes. Filtration                afforded a glittering orange-yellow                microcrystalline solid (0.182 g) of                2-(1,3-bis(N-butylimidazol-2-ylidene)phenylene)(chloro)-nickel(II)                (2). X-ray quality crystals were obtained by a                vapor diffusion of diethyl ether with DCM solution                compound (2).

TI Nonalcoholic beer-taste beverage comprises collagen peptide with predetermined mean molecular weights, carbon dioxide gas, iso (alpha) acid, sour agent, and soybean protein degradation product.
AU MASEDA H
   KATO Y
   MIZUKUCHI I
   KURAKANE S
AE SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
   SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
GA 201907140C
AB    NOVELTY - A nonalcoholic beer-taste beverage comprises (A) collagen peptide with mean molecular weights of 500-8000, (B) carbon dioxide gas, (C) iso ( alpha ) acid, (D) sour agent, and (E) soybean protein degradation product in which beverage has pH 3-4, 280-1800 mg collagen peptide/100 mL beverage, 0.5x 10-3-5x 10-3 mass% iso ( alpha ) acid, and protein content of 1-70 mg/100 mL.
   USE - A nonalcoholic beer-taste beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - Beverage has good first taste and balance of acidity and bitterness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: Beer-taste beverage contains 30-193 mg hydroxyproline per 100 ml beverage. Hydroxyproline contains material derived from collagen peptide. Mass ratio of components (C) to (D) is 0.003-0.05. Beverage has calories of less than or equal to 11 kcal/100 ml. Beverage contains less than or equal to 0.5 g carbohydrates per 100 ml. Raw material does not contain malt.

PN CN108395929-A; CN108395929-B
TI Extraction method of rice bran oil used for preparing cosmetic composition, involves carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology on rice bran without using entrainer.
AU MEI J
   ZHANG X
   QIAN J
AE SHENZHEN LANTING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GONJ-C)
GA 2018652671
AB    NOVELTY - The extraction method of rice bran oil involves using rice bran as a raw material, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology without using entrainer.
   USE - Extraction method of rice bran oil for preparing rice bran cosmetic composition (all claimed) used for providing repairing, whitening and moisturizing effects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of rice bran oil which is suitable for sensitive skin and damaged skin keratin barrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a rice bran cosmetic composition, which comprises 5-20 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.% rice bran oil based on total weight of the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The extraction pressure of 35 MPa, the extracting temperature is 40 degrees C, and the extracting time is 2.5 hours. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20 l/hour. The composition comprises 0.1 wt.% ceramide, 1 wt.% ammonium acryloyldimethyltaurate/vinylpyrrolidone copolymer as an emulsifier, 0.2 wt.% methylparaben, 0.1 wt.% propylparaben, and 0.5 wt.% 1,2-hexanediol.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition in the form of an emulsion, cream, facial mask, eye cream, lip balm, lipstick, lip gloss or emollient oil.

PT P
PN CN107890033-A
TI Preparing rabbit meat sausage by microbial fermentation, involves tumbling and marinating rabbit meat, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, mincing meat, mixing with microorganisms, maturing and drying product.
AU HOU D
AE WUHU SHENGHONG RABBIT IND BREEDING CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018289356
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a rabbit meat sausage by microbial fermentation, involves initially tumbling and marinating rabbit meat using a vacuum tumbler, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to remove maggots, mincing the meat, placing in a cans, mixing with microorganisms, maturing and drying to obtain product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a rabbit meat sausage by microbial fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares rabbit meat sausage with reduced rabbit meat fishy smell, improved taste and bright color, where the rabbit meat sausage is harmless to human body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) vacuum tumbling and marinating by preparing a marinating liquid comprising 3 %mass salt and 2 %mass glucose, cooling to less than 4 degrees C, using a saline injection machine to evenly inject the marinating liquid into rabbit meat pieces, then placing the injected rabbit meat into a vacuum tumbler, tumbling for 30-50 minutes at rolling speed of 10-20 revolutions/minute (rpm) under pressure of 0.02 MPa, maintaining under static condition for 10-20 minutes, continuing to knead, performing intermittent rolling and tumbling for 3-4 hours, and maintaining the temperature after tumbling to be not greater than 8 degrees C, (b) performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction by placing the rabbit meat in a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction kettle after tumbling, and extracting at 38-43 degrees C under pressure of 20-30 MPa for 1-2 hours, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-18 kg/hour, (c) mixing the processed rabbit meat and pig fat in a mass ratio of 4-6:1, mincing, adding seasoning and mixed starter, mixing uniformly, obtaining stuffing, filling the stuffing into a casing and sealing the casing, (d) placing in a fermentation tank at 28-35 degrees C, fermenting under conditions of relative humidity of 90-95% for 18-26 hours, and then aging at 10-15 degrees C under humidity of 80-85% for 8-15 hours, and (e) drying the aged sausages at 45-55 degrees C, naturally cooling to room temperature, vacuum packing, sterilizing, and refrigerating product. The method involves using 100 parts rabbit meat and seasoning comprising 28-33 parts edible salt, 28-32 parts white granulated sugar, 3-6 parts glucose, 12-17 parts white wine, 2-5 parts white pepper powder, 3-6 parts monosodium glutamate and 1-3 parts allspice powder. Preferred Components: The mixed starter comprises Lactobacillus plantarum, Staphylococcus xylosus and Debaryomyces hansenii in a volume ratio of 1:2:1. The inoculation amount of the mixed starter is 1x 107 colony forming unit (cfu)/g.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D03-H01B2; D03-H01T2A; D03-H02B2B; D05-A04D
IP A23L-013/40; A23L-013/60; A23L-013/70; A23L-005/20

PT P
PN CN107866625-A
TI Welding cast iron comprises feeding cast iron to low-temperature furnace, heating, cooling, welding with Z308 nickel cast iron welding rod, drying, and gas welding under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU LIU C
AE CHONGQING XIANGYANG APP CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201829117G
AB    NOVELTY - Welding cast iron comprises feeding cast iron to low-temperature furnace, heating to 700-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours, cooling to 300 degrees C for 5-8 hours, welding with Z308 nickel cast iron welding rod, drying at 150-350 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and gas welding under carbon dioxide atmosphere at 50-90 A, 15 V and 8-10 L/minute gas flow. Welding material is H08Mo2SiA.
   USE - Method for welding cast iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced crack generation.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)

PT P
PN US2018002265-A1; WO2018004992-A1; US9981896-B2
TI Producing dimethyl ether, comprises introducing at least one feed stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide into a reformer to generate synthesis gas, and converting synthesis gas to dimethyl ether in one step.
AU WRIGHT H A
   ROBERTSON M K
   JIANG W
AE RES USA LLC (REUS-Non-standard)
   RES USA LLC (REUS-Non-standard)
GA 2018009900
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dimethyl ether, comprises: introducing at least one feed stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide into a reformer to generate synthesis gas; and converting synthesis gas to dimethyl ether in one step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dimethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the reformer that uses no process water and requires no oxygen (claimed); has low natural gas consumption per liter of dimethyl ether produced; does not water consumption, and oxygen consumption; and has low greenhouse gas emissions per liter of dimethyl ether produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reformer: comprises a nickel catalyst; is a pressurized fluidized bed dry reforming reactor; and comprises a hydrogen membrane, which removes hydrogen contained in the synthesis gas and shifts reforming reactions toward completion, comprises palladium alloy membranes, or palladium alloys supported on ceramic or metal substrates, is placed vertically in the reformer as hydrogen membrane tubes hanging from the top of the reformer, and coated with an erosion resistant layer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method: the feed stream enters the bottom of the reformer via a manifold or distributor, and fluidizes the catalyst in the reformer; the reformed gas exits the top of the reformer and is separated from spent catalyst, which is routed to a regenerator in which the catalyst is regenerated, where the regenerated catalyst is returned to the reformer, and the reformer comprises a cyclone for solid gas separation; a bi-functional catalyst is used to convert synthesis gas to dimethyl ether in one step, where the bi-functional catalyst is capable of methanol synthesis and dehydration, and comprise nickel/cerium-zirconium dioxide/aluminum trioxide, copper oxide-zinc oxide-aluminum trioxide (CZA) over clinoptilolite, CZA over gamma -alumina, CZA over zeolites, where the zeolites include ferrierite, zirconium dioxide, ZSM-5, NaY and HY; and a multi-tubular fixed bed reactor is used for converting synthesis gas to dimethyl ether, and is placed inside a shell, where water is circulated. The method comprises: recovering and separating dimethyl ether as product; and collecting and separating carbon dioxide for recycle to the reformer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-H01E; E11-D02; E11-F05; E11-W; H04-E05; N01-A; N01-B; N01-C03; N02; N03; N06-A; N06-C01; N07-B02; N07-D06

In the                  method, the separator is a cation exchange                  membrane. In the method, the strain is                  domesticated mixed culture system, which mainly                  includeing Acetobacterium, Candida sp.S, and                  Geobacter. In the method, the microbial                  electrosynthesis system catalyzes the reduction                  of carbon dioxide to synthesize organic matter,                  and the organic matter is mainly formic acid and                  acetic acid. In the                  microbial electrosynthesis system, the anolyte is                  2.18 g/l dipotassium phosphate, 1.70 g/l                  monopotassium phosphate, 2.50 g/l sodium                  bicarbonate, 1.25 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.10 g/l                  potassium chloride, 1 g/l magnesium chloride                  hexahydrate, 0.80 g/l sodium chloride, 0.30 g/l                  calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.50 g/l L-cysteine,                  10 ml/l trace element solution, and 10 ml/l                  vitamin solution; adjusting pH of the cathode                  liquid to 6. In the method, the activity of                  methanogen in sludge is inhibited by adding                  methane inhibitor (sodium 2-bromoethylsulfonate)                  to the cathode reactor. In the microbial                  electrosynthesis system, the cathodic                  polarization potential is set to be controlled                  from -0.6V to -1.0V (vs silver/silver chloride).                  In the method, the microbial electrosynthesis                  system is two-chamber reactor in which the anode                  and the cathode are not in the same reaction                  chamber.

PN US2018230371-A1; US10131833-B2
TI Hydraulic fracturing fluid useful for treating subterranean formations, comprises fluid comprising carbon dioxide, and self-suspending proppants including proppant particles coated with carbon dioxide-philic coating suspended in the fluid.
AU LIANG F
   AL-MUNTASHERI G A
   LI L
   REDDY B R
   JOHN G
   ADIYALA V
AE ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   UNIV NEW YORK CITY RES FOUND (UNYC-C)
GA 201863429R
AB    NOVELTY - Hydraulic fracturing fluid comprises a fluid comprising carbon dioxide, and self-suspending proppants suspended in the fluid, where the self-suspending proppants comprising proppant particles coated with a carbon dioxide-philic coating in which the carbon dioxide-philic coating is lightly crosslinked and has a physical structure that constrains carbon dioxide molecules.
   USE - Hydraulic fracturing fluid is useful for treating subterranean formations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluid further comprises nitrogen. The physical structure of the carbon dioxide-philic coating comprises constrained carbon dioxide molecules that volumetrically expand the self-suspending proppants to a swollen state. The physical structure of the carbon dioxide-philic coating is volumetrically expanded by at least 100% from a non-swollen state to the swollen state. The self-suspending proppants in a swollen state have a density of ~ 200% of the density of the fluid. The proppant particles have rough surfaces with an arithmetic average roughness of greater than or equal to 1 nm. The carbon dioxide-philic coating comprises a carbon dioxide-philic material comprising polysaccharide acetates, polyethylene glycols, partially fluorinated oxygen-containing polymers, oxygenated polymers, crosslinked oxygen-containing polystyrenes and/or polyvinyl acetates. The carbon dioxide-philic coating does not dissociate from the proppant particles when the self-suspending proppants are added to a water-based fluid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide circulation system which includes a carbon dioxide reservoir (7), a carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator (1), and a pressure relief pipeline that connecting the carbon dioxide reservoir and the carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator. A pressure relief component is provided on the pressure relief pipeline. The carbon dioxide reservoir and the carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator are connected when the pressure relief component is opened. The carbon dioxide reservoir and the carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator are cut off and connected when the pressure relief component is closed. The amount of carbon dioxide is charged into the carbon dioxide circulation system. The internal pressure value of the carbon dioxide circulation system is less than a safe pressure value when the pressure relief component is in an open state and reaches a set maximum temperature.

GA 2019A7409H
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capturing method involves                feeding a flue gas comprising carbon dioxide to                rotary packed bed absorber rotating circularly,                providing a solvent through an inner radius of the                rotary packed bed absorber moves towards an outer                radius of the rotary packed bed absorber, reacting                the solvent with the flue gas in a counter-current                flow, passing carbon dioxide rich solvent from the                rotary packed bed absorber to a rotary packed bed                oxygen eliminator, or a static packed bed oxygen                eliminator, and eliminating dissolved oxygen from                the solvent.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue                gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables capturing carbon dioxide                with lower energy requirement and in cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide capturing system,                comprising rotary packed bed absorber configured to                rotate circularly, and rotary packed bed oxygen                eliminator, or a static packed bed oxygen                eliminator, where rotary packed bed rotates                circularly a solvent provided through an inner                radius of the rotary packed bed absorber moves                towards an outer radius of the rotary packed bed                absorber, and the solvent reacts with flue gas in a                counter-current flow to capture carbon dioxide, the                rotary packed bed oxygen eliminator, or a static                packed bed oxygen eliminator for eliminating oxygen                from carbon dioxide rich solvent, the rotary packed                bed oxygen eliminator, or the static packed bed                oxygen eliminator, and positioned to eliminate                oxygen from carbon dioxide rich solvent leaving the                rotary packed bed absorber
   (2) a vacuum solvent reclamation system,                comprising reboiler configured to increase the                temperature of carbon dioxide capture solvents such                that the heat stable salts, degradation products                and other contaminants accumulate in the reboiler,                the reboiler configured to permit batch or                semi-batch removal of heat stable salts,                degradation products and other contaminants from                the carbon dioxide capture solvents, and a                condenser for decreasing the temperature of cleaned                carbon dioxide capture solvents emitted from the                reboiler.

PT P
PN CN108034487-A; CN108034487-B
TI Enzymatic hydrolysis and electric field combined treatment of tuna scrap comprises e.g. washing tuna waste, crushing, adding water, mixing, adding pepsin and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis and centrifuging.
AU LU Z
   WEI C
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201839843R
AB    NOVELTY - Enzymatic hydrolysis and electric field combined treatment of tuna scrap comprises e.g. (i) washing tuna waste, crushing, adding water, mixing, adding brine into the slurry and mixing slurry with salt water; (ii) adding pepsin and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis; (iii) placing first enzymatic hydrolysate and introducing carbon dioxide gas; (iv) adding the neutral protease for carrying out secondary enzymatic hydrolysis; (v) adding hydrochloric acid into secondary enzymolysis solution; and (vi) placing material liquid in a centrifugal device and centrifuging.
   USE - The method is useful for enzymatic hydrolysis and electric field combined treatment of tuna scrap.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extraction rate of fish oil and improves the content of eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) in fish oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enzymatic hydrolysis and electric field combined treatment of tuna scrap comprises (i) washing tuna waste with clean water, crushing, adding water equivalent to 0.5-2 times of the tunafish's waste to obtain slurry, mixing slurry uniformly, preparing 0.3-0.5 g/ml saline using sodium chloride, heating brine to 25-30 degrees C, adding brine into the slurry, where the mass ratio of the brine and the slurry is 1:5-8, stirring slurry continuously during adding brine, mixing slurry with salt water and allowing to stand; (ii) adding pepsin into the slurry and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 30-35 degrees C at pH is 2-3 for 9-10 hours, where amount of pepsin added is 2-2.5 wt.% mass of the slurry to obtain an enzymatic hydrolysate; (iii) placing first enzymatic hydrolysate in a container, introducing carbon dioxide gas is continuously into the primary enzymatic hydrolysate for 1-2 hours, where carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 4-5 m/second, placing vessel in a high-voltage pulsed electric field for processing by feeding carbon dioxide gas continuously at flow rate of 1-2 m/second and treating at high-voltage pulsed electric field by introducing carbon dioxide gas continuously by controlling flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is consistent with the flow rate during the high-voltage pulse electric field treatment process, where electric field intensity of the high-voltage pulsed electric field is 55-60 kV/cm at 100-150Hz, pulse width is 20-25 mu s for 5-10 seconds; (iv) allowing material solution to stand for 30-60 minutes, adding the neutral protease for carrying out secondary enzymatic hydrolysis at 40-45 degrees C with pH value 8-9 for 3-4 hours to obtain a secondary enzymolysis solution, where amount of neutral protease added is 2-3 wt.% of the material; (v) adding 2-3 % hydrochloric acid into the secondary enzymolysis solution, adjusting pH of the secondary enzymolysis solution to 4-5, adding anhydrous ethanol continuously, where amount of the secondary enzymolysis solution is 65-70% of the volume of the secondary enzymatic hydrolysate and allowing to stand for 20-23 hours; and (vi) placing material liquid in a centrifugal device and centrifuging at 8000-9000 revolutions/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises introducing nitrogen gas continuously into the slurry during a single enzymatic hydrolysis, where flow rate of nitrogen is 1.5-2.5 m/s and carrying out ultrasonic treatment at 800- 950 kHz. The step (iv) comprises introducing nitrogen gas continuously into the slurry during a single enzymatic hydrolysis, where flow rate of nitrogen is 3.5-5.5 m/s and carrying out ultrasonic treatment at 650-700kHz. The step (iii) comprises introducing carbon dioxide gas into the primary enzymolysis solution for 1 hours at flow rate of 4 m/second, during the high-voltage pulsed electric field processing introducing carbon dioxide gas continuously at flow rate of 1m/second. Preferred Components: The neutral protease in step (iv) is trypsin, papain and subtilisin at mass ratio 4-5:1-2:0.5-1, preferably 4:1:0.5.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02C; D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/00; C11B-001/02; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/10; C11B-003/16

PN CN107792872-A; CN107792872-B
TI Preparing fusiform nano-calcium carbonate            comprises taking high activated calcium oxide and            carbon dioxide as raw material, carbonizing reaction            process, adding e.g. crystalline control agent A,            pulping, aging, filtering, washing and drying.
AU LI Y
   SONG H
   SONG J
   SONG M
   ZHOU X
AE MAANSHAN BOGUANG NANOMETER NEW MATERIAL (MAAN-Non-standard)
   SHIJIAZHUANG XIANGBORUI ENVIRONMENTAL (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018223528
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fusiform nano-calcium carbonate                comprises taking high activated calcium oxide and                carbon dioxide as raw material, using two stage                carbonization tower as reaction device, performing                carbonization reaction, carbonizing reaction                process including taking the activated high lime                and digestion water for digestion and sieving to                obtain lime milk, adding crystalline control agent                A and crystal control agent B to obtain an initial                slurry, pumping the initial slurry into the                first-stage carbonization tower, pulping to obtain                a grade of carbide material, aging, filtering,                washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fusiform                nano-calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The fusiform nano-calcium carbonate has                excellent dispersibility, uniform size, regular                appearance and high purity. The method improves the                utilization rate of the reaction apparatus and is                suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fusiform nano-calcium carbonate                comprises taking 360-420 ml high activated calcium                oxide and carbon dioxide with purity not less than                32% as raw material, using two stage carbonization                tower as reaction device, performing carbonization                reaction, where the two-stage carbonization tower                is composed of a primary carbide tower and a second                stage carbonization tower in series in a reaction                volume ratio of 1:4, where the reaction volume                ratio of the quantity of carbon dioxide in each                carbonization tower to the carbonizing tower is 3-5                l/minute:0.8l, (B) carbonizing reaction process                comprising (a) taking the activated high lime and                digestion water in a mass ratio of 1:5 digestion                and sieving to obtain lime milk, (b) under the                condition at 20-25 degrees C, adjusting the                concentration of the lime milk to 6-8 baume                degrees, adding crystalline control agent A and                crystal control agent B to obtain an initial                slurry, where the addition amount of the crystal                form control agent A is 0.8-1.2 wt.% of the dry                matrix calcium hydroxide contained in the milk of                lime, where the added amount of crystalline form                control agent B is contained in the milk of lime                dry matrix calcium hydroxide is 0.8%, (c) pumping                the initial slurry into the first-stage                carbonization tower, introducing carbon dioxide,                maintaining the temperature in the tower to pH                value of 9, maintaining the temperature in the                tower carbonized at 35 degrees C to pH of 9, where                the first-stage carbonization tower controlling the                rotating speed at 200 plus minus 50                revolutions/minute, using the same technique                repeated pump into the initial slurry,                carbonization, pulping, storing in a primary                storage tank to obtain a grade of carbide material,                (d) taking the first stage carbonization material,                allowing to stand for aging 72 hours, taking the                first-stage carbonization material pump into the                secondary carbonizing tower, then inletting carbon                dioxide, maintaining temperature in the tower at 35                degrees C for carbonization reaction, when the pH                of the slurry reaches is 6.8-7, stopping                carbonizing to obtain final reaction slurry, (e)                taking the obtained final reaction slurry,                filtering, washing and drying to obtain the                length-diameter ratio of 200 nm: 40 nm of fusiform                nano-calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (b)                  crystal control agent A is industrial sugar and                  crystal control agent B is a polyphosphate. In                  the step (a) specific process of the mixed                  digestion of the high-activity quicklime and the                  digestive water comprises first adding digestive                  water at 58-60 degrees C to 2/3 of the volume of                  the digester, then the high-activity lime is                  placed on the feeding platform of the digester,                  turning on the digester, adding high-activity                  quicklime and remaining digestive water and                  digestion can be done. In the step (a) sieving                  device is a vibrating sieve of 240 meshes.

PN CN109513313-A
TI Method for catalytic regeneration of carbon dioxide trapping solvent, involves adding solid catalyst to regeneration tower and/or reboiler to reducing activation energy of carbon dioxide regeneration reaction by catalytic action of catalyst.
AU MAO S
   GUO B
   YE N
   ZHAO Y
   CHEN X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   NCIG RES INST (NCIG-Non-standard)
GA 201929565U
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide trapping solvent catalytic regeneration method involves adding a solid catalyst to the regeneration tower and/or the reboiler to reducing the activation energy of the carbon dioxide regeneration reaction by the catalytic action of the catalyst, increasing the regeneration reaction rate of the carbon dioxide-rich solvent, and carrying out the regeneration reaction at a low temperature.
   USE - Method for catalytic regeneration of carbon dioxide trapping solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables catalytic regeneration of carbon dioxide trapping solvent with increased regeneration rate, high regeneration efficiency, in rapid manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid catalyst used as a solid acid. The carbon dioxide capture solvent used as a solvent. The catalyst comprises molecular sieves, oxides and metal salts, solid phosphoric acid, solid heteropolyacids, solid superacids and acidic resins. The solvent comprises an alkyl alcohol amine, an aqueous solution containing an alkyl alcohol amine, a nonaqueous solution and a phase change absorption liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl alcohol amine comprises monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, diisopropanolamine and N-methyl monoethanolamine.

PN WO2020124169-A1; AU2019404617-A1; CA3126729-A1; IN202137032395-A; BR112021012302-A2; KR2021117273-A; EP3897962-A1; CN113557084-A; SG11202107769-A1; US2022080378-A1; VN82669-A; JP2022516042-W; EP3897962-A4
TI Conversion of carbon dioxide used for producing            one or more small organic compounds e.g. methanol,            involves exposing carbon dioxide and/or related form to            beta-particles activated high band-gap semiconductor,            and converting carbon dioxide.
AU JONES B
   KELLY J F
AE PHOSENERGY LTD (PHOS-Non-standard)
   PHOSENERGY LTD (PHOS-Non-standard)
   PHOSENERGY LTD (PHOS-Non-standard)
   PHOSENERGY LTD (PHOS-Non-standard)
   ENTX LTD (ENTX-Non-standard)
GA 2020570737
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon dioxide and/or related                form to one or more small organic compounds                involves exposing carbon dioxide and/or related                form to ( beta )-particles activated high band-gap                semiconductor, and converting carbon dioxide and/or                related form to one or more small organic                compounds.
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide used for                producing one or more small organic compounds e.g.                carbon monoxide, formaldehyde, methane, methanol,                formic acid, ethanol, acetaldehyde and acetic acid                (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) production of one or more small organic                compounds, which involves converting carbon dioxide                and/or related form to one or more small organic                compounds;
   (2) one or more small organic compounds;
   (3) system, which comprises a source of carbon                dioxide and/or related form, a reaction container                comprising a high band-gap semiconductor coupled                closely with a ( beta )-particles emitting                radionuclide for exposure to carbon dioxide and/or                related form, and unit for extracting the one or                more small organic compounds produced by exposure                of carbon dioxide and/or related form to the high                band-gap semiconductor and radionuclide;
   (4) activation of high band-gap semiconductor,                which involves exposing the high band-gap                semiconductor to a ( beta )-particles emitting                radionuclide and activating the                semiconductor;
   (5) high band-gap semiconductor;
   (6) radiocatalytic material, which comprises a                high band-gap semiconductor coupled with a ( beta                )-particles emitting radionuclide;
   (7) use of radiocatalytic material, for                producing one or more small organic compounds from                carbon dioxide and/or related form;
   (8) identification of high band-gap                semiconductor, which involves exposing carbon                dioxide and/or related form to ( beta )-particles                emitting radionuclide coupled closely with a                candidate high band-gap semiconductor, determining                the ability of the candidate high band-gap                semiconductor to convert carbon dioxide and/or                related form to one or more small organic                compounds, and identifying the candidate high                band-gap semiconductor as a high band- gap                semiconductor for converting carbon dioxide and/or                related form to one or more small organic compounds                by ( beta )-particles activation of the high                band-gap semiconductor; and
   (9) semiconductor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The unit for                  extracting one or more small organic molecules                  comprises a distillation unit and/or condensing                  unit, or a differential adsorption unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The semiconductor                  comprises one or more of a titanate, zirconate,                  molybdate, vanadate, technetate, pertechnetate,                  tungstate, niobate, tantalate, chromate, doped                  tin oxides, doped zinc oxide, hafnate, germanium                  oxide, simple oxide, an oxide of manganese,                  cobalt and iron, sulfide, chalcogenide and carbon                  allotrope. The ( beta )-particles activated high                  band-gap semiconductor comprises ( beta                  )-particles activation through continuous                  excitation by ( beta )-particles, and ( beta                  )-particles activation through emission from a                  radionuclide located at a distance to the                  semiconductor. The radionuclide comprises one or                  more of isotropic carbon-14, isotropic                  strontium-90, isotropic technetium-99, isotropic                  hydrogen-3, isotropic nickel-63, isotropic                  cesium-137, isotropic promethium-147, isotropic                  samarium-151, isotropic tin-121m, isotropic                  europium-155, isotropic zirconium-93, isotropic                  lead-210 and isotropic tin-126. The ( beta                  )-particles activated semiconductor and                  radionuclide are coupled to form a radioactive                  catalyst. The radioactive catalyst comprises                  radionuclide in contact with the high band-gap                  semiconductor, radionuclide proximal to the high                  band-gap semiconductor, radionuclide physically                  admixed with the high band-gap semiconductor,                  radionuclide chemically incorporated into the                  high band-gap semiconductor, radionuclide loaded                  into the high band-gap semiconductor, and/or high                  band-gap semiconductor is located externally to                  the radionuclide. The radioactive catalyst and                  radiocatalytic material is in the form of                  particles, granule, bead, powder, pellet or frit.                  The reaction container comprises a radioactive                  catalyst comprising a high band-gap semiconductor                  and ( beta )-particles emitting radionuclide for                  exposure carbon dioxide and/or related form. The                  source of carbon dioxide comprises one or more of                  waste carbon dioxide, atmospheric carbon dioxide,                  liquid carbon dioxide, sequestered carbon                  dioxide, carbon dioxide complexed with another                  agent, bicarbonate, carbonate, carbonate ore, or                  chemical compound which provides carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Properties: The semiconductor has a                  band-gap of at least 2.6 eV, and conduction band                  edge energy of less than -0.15 V, preferably -0.8                  V with respect to the standard hydrogen                  electrode. The radioactive catalyst is porous.                  The radiocatalytic material is porous. Preferred                  Process: The conversion method and production                  method of one or more small organic compounds                  preferably involves exposing a high band-gap                  semiconductor undergoing electronic excitation by                  energetic ( beta )-particles to carbon dioxide                  and/or related form, and exposing carbon dioxide                  and/or related form to ( beta )-particles                  emitting radionuclide coupled with a high                  band-gap semiconductor and high band-gap                  semiconductor activated by ( beta )-particles                  from a radionuclide.

PN EP3501632-A1; JP2019108290-A; JP6786054-B2; EP3501632-B1
TI Apparatus for producing methane from carbon            dioxide-containing gas, comprises reactor provided with            carbon dioxide storage-reduction catalyst, reactor            provided with methanation catalyst, units for supplying            purge gas and reducing gas.
AU ITOH Y
   SAKAI M
   IMAGAWA H
   SAYAMA S
   BABA N
   ITO Y
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2019525858
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus comprises reactor (A) provided                with a carbon dioxide storage-reduction catalyst                having a carbon dioxide storage capacity and a                methane generation ability, at least one reactor                (B) provided with a methanation catalyst, at least                one unit (1) for supplying purge gas, and at least                one unit (2) for supplying reducing gas. In the                apparatus, two or more of the reactor (A) are                arranged in parallel, the purge gas supplying unit                and reducing gas supplying unit are arranged                upstream of the reactor (A) on gas flow path, the                reactor (B) is arranged downstream of the reactor                (A) on the gas flow path, and a gas outlet of one                of the reactor (A) is connected to a gas inlet of                one of the reactor (A) via a purge gas                recirculation line (5) which supplies a purge gas                emitted from the gas outlet of the reactor (A) to                its gas inlet.
   USE - Apparatus is used for producing methane from                carbon dioxide-containing gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus enables continuous production of                highly-pure and highly-concentrated methane with                suppressed emission of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of methane form a carbon                dioxide-containing gas using the apparatus, which                involves allowing the carbon dioxide                storage-reduction catalyst to store carbon dioxide                by supplying a carbon dioxide-containing gas to the                reactor (A), reducing the carbon dioxide by                supplying a reducing gas to the reactor (A) to                obtain a reaction-produced gas, supplying the                reaction-produced gas to the reactor (B), supplying                the purge gas to one reactor after storing carbon                dioxide and supplying the emitted purge gas from                one reactor to another reactor after reduction                reaction, and reducing residual carbon dioxide in                the reaction-produced gas. At least one of the                reactors (A) stores carbon dioxide while the                remaining of the reactor (A) reduces carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus.
   Unit for supplying purge gas (1)
   Unit for supplying reducing gas (2)
   Gas supply line (3)
   Gas transfer line (4)
   Gas recirculation line (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The apparatus                  comprises at least one storage container that                  stores a reaction-produced gas emitted from gas                  outlets of the reactor (A) provided between the                  reactor (B) and each of the reactors (A).

PN CN107986314-A
TI Preparing spherical precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. adding quicklime into warm water, stirring, charging carbon dioxide, adding crystal into reaction vessel, adding calcium hydroxide slurry and crystal agent and refilling.
AU ZHENG Y
   WANG Z
   CHEN G
AE SHANGHAI CALCIUM CARBONATE FACTORY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018395674
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing spherical precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) adding quicklime into warm water, stirring to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, then sieving and cooling, adding into reaction vessel containing dispersion slurry, and (ii) opening dispersion slurry, charging carbon dioxide, when the carbonization rate of slurry in reaction vessel is 5%, then stop charging carbon dioxide, adding crystal into reaction vessel, recharging carbon dioxide, when the carbonization rate of slurry in reaction vessel is 15%, stop charging carbon dioxide, adding water as calcium hydroxide slurry into reaction vessel, adding calcium hydroxide slurry, then adding calcium hydroxide slurry and crystal agent into reaction vessel, adjusting dispersion line speed, refilling carbon dioxide to obtain aqueous slurry of spherical precipitated calcium carbonate; or adding crystals into reaction vessel, opening dispersion slurry and charging carbon dioxide to obtain precipitated calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing spherical precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing spherical precipitated calcium carbonate comprises (i) adding quicklime into warm water and stirring to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, then sieving and cooling, and adding into reaction vessel containing dispersion slurry, and (ii) opening dispersion slurry, charging carbon dioxide, when the carbonization rate of slurry in reaction vessel is 5%, then stop charging carbon dioxide, adding crystal into reaction vessel, recharging carbon dioxide, when the carbonization rate of slurry in reaction vessel is 15%, stop charging carbon dioxide, adding water as calcium hydroxide slurry into reaction vessel, refilling with carbon dioxide until the reaction vessel slurry carbonization rate is 100%, adding 50-200% calcium hydroxide slurry, then adding 50-60% calcium hydroxide slurry and crystal agent into reaction vessel, adjusting dispersion line speed and refilling carbon dioxide until the reaction vessel slurry carbonization rate is 100% to obtain aqueous slurry of spherical precipitated calcium carbonate; or adding crystals into reaction vessel, opening dispersion slurry, charging carbon dioxide, when the carbonization rate of slurry in reaction vessel is 15%, stop charging carbon dioxide, then adding water as calcium hydroxide slurry into reaction vessel, refilling with carbon dioxide until the reaction vessel slurry carbonization rate is 100% to obtain aqueous slurry of spherical precipitated calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), concentration of calcium hydroxide slurry is 95-105 g/l. In step (ii), linear velocity of dispersion is 12-15 m/second, and adjusted line speed is 2-3 m/second; or linear velocity of dispersion is 12-15 m/second. In step (ii), volume fraction of first charge of carbon dioxide and fourth charge of carbon dioxide is 30%, addition rate is 0.01 liter per minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide; second charge of carbon dioxide is 30%, and addition rate is 0.001 liter/minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide; third charge of carbon dioxide is 80%, and addition rate is 0.0045 liter per minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide; or first time the volume of carbon dioxide is 30%, and addition rate is 0.001 liter/minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide; second charge of carbon dioxide is 80%, and addition rate is 0.0045 liter/minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide. In step (ii), addition amount of first addition crystal is 0.75 gram per gram of dry calcium hydroxide, addition amount of second addition crystal is 0.5 g per gram of dry calcium hydroxide; or addition amount of crystal is 0.75 grams per gram of dry calcium hydroxide. The crystal agent is 2-4 wt.% aqueous solution of sodium hexametaphosphate.

TI Producing synthesis gas by providing stream            comprises hydrogen, providing stream comprising carbon            dioxide or nitrogen, combining streams and cleaning            combined streams.
AU TJAERNEHOV E A
   HAN P A
   KNUDSEN L B
AE TOPSOE AS (TOPS-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4446P
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing synthesis gas comprises:                (a) providing a stream (4) comprises hydrogen; (b)                providing a stream (8) comprising carbon dioxide or                a stream (12) comprising nitrogen; (c) combining                the streams obtained in steps (a) and (b) into                streams and (d) cleaning (E) the combined                streams.
   USE - The process is used for producing synthesis                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides for the reduction of the                cleaning units and other equipment such as                compressing units in a plant by improving/reducing                capital expenditure (CAPEX), provides savings in                CAPEX (total number of compressors is reduced) due                to combined compression/combined cleaning and less                strict requirement for nitrogen purity, increases                overall allowable content of oxygen (impurity),                allows to control the overall oxygen content to the                synthesis by cleaning of both streams, and removes                the requirement of a heater through the interstaged                position of the cleaning step.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a system for producing synthesis gas                comprising (a) a unit (A) for providing a stream                (4) comprising hydrogen, (b) a carbon capture unit                (B) for providing a stream comprising carbon                dioxide or an air separation unit (C) for providing                a stream comprising nitrogen, and (c) a cleaning                unit (E) for removal of impurities from combined                streams; and
   a plant comprising the system for operating                the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The unit (A) is an                  electrolyzer. The unit (A) is a unit for                  performing chloralkali process. The compression                  unit (D) is upstream to the cleaning unit (E).                  The cleaning unit (E) is integrated with a                  compression unit (G). The cleaning unit (E) is                  arranged downstream to the last compression stage                  of compression unit (G). The compression unit (G)                  comprises multiple compression stages. A hydrogen                  scrubber (A') is integrated with or downstream to                  electrolyzer (A).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The combined streams are                  subjected to a compression step (G). The streams                  obtained in steps (a) and (b) are independently                  subjected to a compression (D) step, before being                  combined in step (c). The stream (4) is prepared                  by electrolysis of water. The stream (8) is                  prepared by carbon capture of a flue gas or                  synthesis gas. The stream (12) is prepared using                  air separation.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the general                preparation of synthesis gas that does not support                the claims.

PN JP2022108782-A
TI Producing weakly acidic hypochlorous acid aqueous            solution by mixing water with carbon dioxide, supplying            carbonated water and sodium hypochlorite aqueous            solution to mixer, driving stirring blade rotatably            arranged in mixer, and mixing carbonated water and            sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution.
AU MIZUNO S
   KOGA A
AE KANKYO EISEI CO LTD (KANK-Non-standard)
GA 2022958114
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a weakly acidic                hypochlorous acid aqueous solution, which is a                weakly acidic aqueous solution containing                hypochlorous acid as a main component, involves (a)                mixing water with carbon dioxide to obtain                carbonated water, and (b) supplying the carbonated                water and sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution to a                mixer, driving a first stirring blade rotatably                arranged in the mixer and a second stirring blade                rotatably arranged in the mixer in a direction                opposite to the first stirring blade, while                generating flows in different directions around one                stirring blade and around the second stirring                blade, and mixing the carbonated water and the                sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution supplied to                the mixer, to obtain a weakly acidic aqueous                hypochlorous acid solution.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a weakly                acidic hypochlorous acid aqueous solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and sufficiently mixes                carbonated water and sodium hypochlorite,                stabilizes the pH of the hypochlorous acid aqueous                solution in a weakly acidic region, and ensures                prolonged storage without generating harmful                substances, and reduces consumption and emission of                carbon dioxide gas, and reduces cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The first stirring                  blade and second stirring blade are arranged on a                  concentric axis, In step (b), the mixer comprises                  a mixing tank, where the carbonated water and                  sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution are supplied                  into the mixing tank having a cylindrical shape,                  and by generating the flows in different                  directions in the around the first stirring blade                  and the around the second stirring blade, a                  turbulent flow or a vortex is generated in the                  mixer, and the dissolved concentration of carbon                  dioxide gas in the mixture of carbonated water                  and sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution is                  adjusted to 200 parts per million or more. The                  first stirring blade is arranged in the mixing                  tank in the longitudinal direction along the                  central axis of the mixing tank. The second                  stirring blade is arranged in the mixing tank in                  the longitudinal direction along the inner                  circumference of the mixing tank. The first                  stirring blade and second stirring blade have a                  spiral shape. The first stirring blade has a                  different helical shape than the second stirring                  blade. In step (a), the dissolved concentration                  of carbon dioxide in carbonated water is adjusted                  to 100-1200 parts per million, and the obtained                  carbonated water has a pH of 7 or less. The                  weakly acidic hypochlorous acid aqueous solution                  has a pH of 4-6.5. The effective chlorine                  concentration of the weakly acidic hypochlorous                  acid aqueous solution is 80 parts per million or                  more. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  comprises liquefied carbon dioxide, carbon                  dioxide generated from sodium hydrogen carbonate,                  and a mixed gas of liquefied carbon dioxide and                  carbon dioxide generated from sodium hydrogen                  carbonate in step (a). Preferred Method: The                  method involves adding an acid to the weakly                  acidic hypochlorous acid aqueous solution                  obtained in the step (b), where the acid                  comprises malic acid, citric acid, hydrochloric                  acid, acetic acid and/or oxalic acid, and adding                  a surfactant to the weakly acidic hypochlorous                  acid aqueous solution obtained in the step (b),                  where the surfactant comprises benzalkonium                  chloride or an alkylamine oxide, or a combination                  of benzalkonium chloride and an alkylamine oxide,                  and the surfactant is contained in an amount of                  0.01-1 %mass with respect to the total weight of                  the weakly acidic hypochlorous acid aqueous                  solution.

PN US2022220252-A1; WO2022153149-A1
TI Continuous production of polycarbonate polyol            involves contacting mixture containing peroxides and            carbon dioxide, with a solid catalyst and chain            transfer agent having sites for initiating            copolymerization, polymerizing until a desired            molecular weight polyol is formed, terminating            polymerization.
AU XU W
   ALI O
   YAMI H M
   ALROAITHI M S
AE SAUDI ARAMCO TECHNOLOGIES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARAMCO TECHNOLOGIES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022920178
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production of polycarbonate polyol,                involves: (i) providing a solid catalyst into a                continuous stirred tank reactor (CSTR); (ii)                contacting a reaction mixture containing epoxide(s)                and carbon dioxide, with a solid catalyst and a                chain transfer agent containing sites capable of                initiating copolymerization of epoxides and carbon                dioxide in CSTR; (iii) enabling polymerization                reaction to proceed until a desired molecular                weight polyol is formed; and (iv) terminating the                polymerization reaction.
   USE - The method is used for continuous production                of polycarbonate polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The continuous manufacture of polycarbonate                polyol enables to prevent issues associated with                catalyst deactivation during the batch production                process. The process enables to achieve high                moisture tolerant level, reduced induction time                and/or decreased viscosity of prepared                polycarbonate polyol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. prepared polyol.
   2. system for preparing the polyol, which                comprises: 1) a CSTR in which polymerization                reaction of the polyol, takes place; 2) solid                catalyst input subsystem fluidly connected to the                CSTR, solid catalyst are fed into the CSTR, through                the subsystem; and 3) a reagent input subsystem                fluidly connected to the CSTR.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The moisture tolerance                  level of the polymerization reaction performed in                  polycarbonate polyol continuous production                  process, is 100- 500 ppm. The moisture level of                  the reaction mixture is below the moisture                  tolerance level of the polymerization                  reaction.The number average molecular weight of the                  polycarbonate polyol prepared, is 750-1500                  g/mol.The solid catalyst comprises a metal                  coordination compound containing a permanent                  ligand set and at least one ligand containing a                  polymerization initiator. The polyol formed has                  polydispersity index 1.2- 1.45 and viscosity 100-                  10,000 centipoise, at 75degreesC.Preferred Process: The process does not                  involve drying either the reaction mixture or the                  chain transfer agent before the polymerization                  reaction. There is no observable induction time                  for the solid catalyst used in the process,                  during the reactor startup, and the solid                  catalyst is optionally not activated before the                  feeding step (i).During the continuous production process,                  one or more epoxides and the carbon dioxide are                  added separately, but continuously. The method                  further involves continuously providing chain                  transfer agent to CSTR, followed by continuously                  supplying a solvent to CSTR, separating the solid                  catalyst after the terminating step, and removing                  the solid catalyst using an ionic exchange                  resin.The method also involves discharging the                  polycarbonate polyol from the CSTR after the                  polymerization reaction, and separating the                  polycarbonate polyol.Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of                  carbon dioxide during the process, is 20-100                  g/hour. The production rate of the polycarbonate                  polyol is 200- 1000 g/hour.The residence time of the reaction mixture                  in the reactor, is 12- 24 hours.The prepared polyol is discharged from the                  system, at an interval of one second to one hour,                  and the solid catalyst is injected into the CSTR                  at an interval of one second to one hour.Preferred Components: The system used for                  preparing the polyol, comprises a quenching tank                  fluidly connected to CSTR, in which the                  termination of polymerization reaction of the                  polyol occurs. The system further comprises a                  solid catalyst separation unit fluidly connected                  to the CSTR and a polyol separation unit fluidly                  connected to the CSTR.Preferred Compounds: A solvent comprising                  methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, acetone, methyl                  tethyl ketone, propylene oxide, tetrahydrofuran,                  monoglyme, triglyme, propionitrile,                  1-nitropropane, cyclohexanone, dibasic ester                  (DBE), tetrahydrofuran (THF), ether,                  dimethylformamide (DMF) and/or dimethyl sulfoxide                  (DMSO), is used in CSTR, during the                  process.The solid catalyst preferably comprises                  cobalt-based salen catalyst, double metal cyanide                  (DMC) and/or a zinc-based catalyst. The chain                  transfer agent comprises propylene glycol,                  dipropylene glycol and/or polypropylene                  glycol.

GA 202283145R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxide-derived densely arranged                copper array material comprises using the copper                material as the anode and the copper rod as the                cathode, performing constant-current electrolysis                to obtain a copper material with a black copper                oxide array on the surface, using the copper                material whose surface is a black copper oxide                array as the working electrode, introducing carbon                dioxide into the three-electrode system for                electrolysis to obtain oxide-derived densely                arranged copper array material, where current of                the constant current electrolysis is 0.22-0.28                mA/cm^2.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                oxide-derived densely arranged copper array                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has high catalytic activity and                selectivity of carbon dioxide reduction producing                ethylene, and lower free energy barrier with                respect to other products, thus facilitating the                production; reduces the resistor dioxide reduction                reaction; prevents the migration of the structure                in the reaction process; can effectively disperse                the current density of the electrode surface; and                reduces the dissolution/re-deposition process of                the catalyst surface so that the nano structure                remains stable without agglomeration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reference                  electrode in the three-electrode system includes                  a silver/silver chloride electrode. The counter                  electrode includes a platinum electrode. The                  electrolyte used in the electrolysis includes                  potassium chloride solution. The potassium                  chloride solution includes saturated carbon                  dioxide.

PN CN114160140-A
TI Preparation method of catalyst for catalytic            conversion of carbon dioxide, involves mixing modified            basalt fiber with copper salt solution and nickel salt            solution, adding reducing agent, and reacting,            calcining, mixing catalyst with glacial acetic acid,            gamma-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane.
AU HOU M
   ZHAO S
   LV L
   ZHU J
   DENG J
   LUO L
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2022422541
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of catalyst, which                involves: i. adding basalt fiber to the modifier                for modification, and then drying and roasting to                obtain the modified basalt fiber; ii. mixing the                modified basalt fiber in step 1 with copper salt                solution and nickel salt solution, adding a                reducing agent, and reacting to obtain a supported                catalyst; iii. under reducing gas conditions,                calcining the supported catalyst obtained by the                step 2 reaction to obtain a shaped catalyst; and                iv. mixing catalyst obtained from step 3 with                glacial acetic acid,                gamma-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, and                deionized water, and grinding into a slurry, and                then immersing cordierite honeycomb ceramic matrix                in the slurry; finally, drying cordierite honeycomb                ceramic substrate coated with slurry and                calcining.
   USE - Preparation method of catalyst for catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carrier basalt fiber BF is modified, acid-base                site balance of the basalt fiber is adjusted, so                that the copper-nickel catalytic efficiency is                promoted.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an x-ray powder diffraction                patterns of the catalysts finally prepared in                catalyst for catalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step 1, the                  modifier is sodium hydroxide,                  gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane or hydrochloric                  acid. Copper salt solution is a copper nitrate                  solution, the nickel salt solution is a nickel                  nitrate solution. Preferred Composition:                  Concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is                  0.1-0.5 mol per liter, the concentration of the                  hydrochloric acid is 0.1-0.5 mol per liter, and                  addition amount of the                  gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 2 wt.% of the                  quality of the basalt fiber. In step 2, reducing                  agent is sodium borohydride, molar ratio of the                  total molar amount of the copper salt solution                  and the nickel salt solution to the sodium                  borohydride is (2-5):1. In step 2, the total                  loading of copper ions in the copper salt                  solution and nickel ions in the nickel salt is                  10-25 wt.% in terms of the percentage content of                  the total weight of the raw materials. Molar                  ratio of copper ions in the copper salt solution                  to nickel ions in the nickel salt solution is                  1:1. In step 4, the added amount of the glacial                  acetic acid is 3 wt.% of the mass of the shaped                  catalyst. Preferred Method: In step 1, the drying                  temperature is 80 &#176;C, and the                  calcination temperature is 500 &#176;C. In                  step 2, the reaction time is 2-5 hours. In step                  3, the reducing gas includes hydrogen and                  nitrogen, the volume ratio of the hydrogen and                  the nitrogen is 1:9, the roasting temperature is                  400-500 &#176;C, and the roasting is                  performed for 2-5 hours. In step 4, the roasting                  temperature is controlled at 400-450 &#176;C,                  and the roasting time is 3-4 hours.

PN CN113976174-A
