AU SHEN K
   LI Y
   ZHANG J
   CHEN J
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2022237370
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic framework                encapsulation organic-inorganic perovskite                composite photocatalytic material comprises (i)                dissolving PbX2 powder in dimethylformamide                ultrasonically dispersing and dissolving to obtain                PbX2 perovskite precursor solution, (ii) adding the                PbX2 perovskite precursor solution into the                MIL-101(chromium) powder, ultrasonically                dispersing, and stirring at room temperature to                obtain PbX2/MIL-101(chromium) suspension,                centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain                PbX2/MIL-101(chromium) material, (iii) dissolving                methyl amine halide powder in ethanol solution,                dispersing and dissolving by ultrasonic to obtain                MAX perovskite precursor solution, dispersing                PbX2/MIL-101(chromium) in toluene solution, and                (iv) adding MAX perovskite precursor solution into                the PbX2/MIL-101(chromium) toluene solution,                stirring at room temperature, centrifuging,                washing, and drying to obtain MAPbX3/MIL101                (chromium) composite.
   USE - The metal-organic framework encapsulation                organic-inorganic perovskite composite                photocatalytic material useful for reducing carbon                dioxide to produce carbon monoxide and                methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has greatly improved stability,                enriched pore structure due to the introduction of                metal-organic framework, exhibits high catalytic                performance in photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide to produce carbon monoxide and methane, and                has good application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  concentration of the PbX2 perovskite precursor                  solution is 0.055-0.14 mol/l. In the step (iii),                  the concentration of the MAX perovskite precursor                  solution is 0.055-0.14 mol/l; the volume of the                  toluene solution in which the                  PbX2/MIL-101(chromium) material is dispersed is 1                  ml.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the time                  of room temperature stirring is 6-72 hours, the                  centrifugation is carried out at 8000-10000                  revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes, and the                  drying is carried out at 50-70degrees Celsius for                  6-24 hours; the washing condition is to use                  dimethylformamide solution to wash once; the                  preparation method of MIL-101 (chromium) powder                  comprises (a) adding chromium(III) nitrate                  nonahydrate, terephthalic acid, hydrofluoric acid                  and deionized water into the inner lining of the                  reaction kettle, ultrasonically dispersing,                  placing in an oven at 200-220degrees Celsius, and                  keeping at constant temperature for 7-8 hours,                  and slowly cooling to room temperature for 12-15                  hours, (b) filtering solution using a funnel to                  obtain green powder, washing with deionized water                  and ethanol for many times, dispersing in 95%                  ethanol solution, adding into a reactor and                  placing in a 70-80degrees Celsius in an oven for                  24-30 hours, and (c) filtering while hot to                  obtain MIL-101(chromium) green powder, and vacuum                  drying the green powder at 150-200degrees Celsius                  for 10-12 hours, and activating. In the step                  (iv), the stirring is carried out at 400-900                  revolutions/minute for 5-15 minutes; the                  centrifugation is carried out at 5000-10000                  evolutions/minute for 3-10 minutes; and the                  washing condition is to use cyclohexane solution                  to wash once, the drying time is 6-24 hours, and                  the drying temperature is 70degrees                  Celsius.

PT P
PN CN113318698-A
TI Alkali metal-loaded ceramic catalytic filler used            in absorption tower for chemically absorbing carbon            dioxide by organic amines, comprises porous ceramic            filler and active component attached to the porous            ceramic filler.
AU CAI M
   CHENG G
   YANG S
   ZHAO H
   YANG C
   LI Y
   GUO Z
   FU K
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021A4137H
AB    NOVELTY - Alkali metal-loaded ceramic catalytic filler                comprises porous ceramic filler and active                component attached to the porous ceramic filler,                the active component including alkali metal ions                and magnesium oxide.
   USE - The ceramic catalytic filler used in                absorption tower for chemically absorbing carbon                dioxide by organic amines (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic catalytic filler: can accelerates                rate at which the tertiary amine absorbs carbon                dioxide, and can avoids problems of insufficient                space in absorption tower, excessive internal                parts, and gas-liquid flow; and is simple to                prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the alkali metal-loaded ceramic catalytic                filler comprising (i) pre-treating the porous                ceramic filler; (ii) adding magnesium salt to                deionized water to prepare 5-20 wt.% magnesium salt                solution; adding alkali metal hydroxide to                deionized water to prepare an 5-20 wt.% alkali                solution; (iii) immersing the porous ceramic filler                in magnesium salt solution, and then performing                ultrasonic impregnation, drying, calcining and                cooling; and (iv) immersing the porous ceramic                filler treated in step (iii) in an alkaline                solution, performing ultrasonic impregnation,                drying, calcining and cooling to obtain alkali                metal-loaded ceramic catalytic filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous ceramic                  filler is honeycomb porous ceramic filler or foam                  porous ceramic filler. The porous ceramic filler                  is Pall ring, Raschig ring, ceramic ball or                  corrugated plate. The magnesium salt is magnesium                  nitrate hexahydrate or magnesium acetate                  tetrahydrate. The alkali metal hydroxide is                  sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide.                  Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises removing                  the impurities on the surface of the porous                  ceramic filler, cleaning the porous ceramic                  filler with 30 wt.% oxalic acid solution,                  condensing, refluxing and boiling for 1 hour,                  cooling, washing it with deionized water until                  the pH of the washing solution was 7, and placing                  in an oven and dried at a temperature of 110                  degrees C for 12 hours. The step (iii) comprises                  immersing the porous ceramic filler in the                  magnesium salt solution, ultrasonically immersing                  and drying for 2-4 times, where, ultrasonic                  immersing time is 1-3 hours, the drying                  temperature is 110 degrees C, and drying time is                  12 hours, heating to 500-700 degrees C at heating                  rate of 5 degrees C/minute under inert gas                  atmosphere, and calcining at constant temperature                  for 2 hours, and naturally cooling it to room                  temperature under an inert gas atmosphere. The                  step (iv) comprises immersing the calcined porous                  ceramic filler in alkaline solution,                  ultrasonically immersing and drying for 2-4                  times, where, ultrasonic immersion time is 1-3                  hours, drying temperature is 110 degrees C, and                  drying time is 12 hours, heating to 500-700                  degrees C at heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute                  under inert gas atmosphere, calcining at constant                  temperature for 2 hours, naturally cooling to                  room temperature under an inert gas atmosphere to                  obtain an alkali metal-loaded ceramic catalytic                  filler.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-B04D2; E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; E33-A03; E34-B01; J04-E04; J04-X; N01-A01; N01-B; N06-C07; N06-F; N07-L01C; N07-L02C; N07-L02D
IP B01D-053/62; B01D-053/78; B01D-053/86; B01J-019/30; B01J-023/04

PN CN112981946-A
TI Preparing silk large biological fiber containing cocoa active ingredient comprises carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process of cocoa powder in an extraction cylinder with cosolvent, soaking silk fiber and dispersing.
AU HUANG X
   ZHEN L
   CHI S
   LIU Y
AE BESTEE MATERIALS QINGDAO CO LTD (BEST-Non-standard)
   ZHONGKE TEXTILE RES INST QINGDAO CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
   QINGDAO BAICAO NEW MATERIAL STOCK CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021721520
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process of cocoa powder in an extraction cylinder with cosolvent at 35-80 degrees C for 3-6 hours under extraction pressure of 15-40 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate as 3-5 l/hour, and obtaining an active ingredient, (b) dissolving the extracted active ingredient in water of 50-80 degrees C, where mass ratio of active ingredient and water is 1:10-15, and obtaining active ingredient aqueous solution, (c) soaking silk fiber in a surfactant solution for 30-180 minutes, completely washing with deionized water, and naturally drying, (d) grafting and modifying the pre-treated silk fiber using acrylonitrile, methyl methacrylate or methacrylamide, and (e) adding the aqueous solution containing active ingredient, the grafted-modified silk fiber, a coupling agent, surfactant, antioxidant into a high speed shearing disperser, cutting and dispersing, and heating, taking out the silk fiber, cleaning and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silk large biological fiber containing cocoa active ingredient.
   ADVANTAGE - The silk fiber has antibacterial and antiviral functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the silk large biological fiber containing cocoa active ingredient prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (c), the surfactant is quaternary ammonium salt surfactant, and the concentration is 15-25%. In the step (e), the coupling agent is silane coupling agent. Preferred Method: The step (d) silk fiber graft modification comprises dissolving acrylonitrile, methyl methacrylate or methacrylamide in aqueous ethanol solution with 50% concentration, soaking the pre-processed silk fiber in the solution, heating in a water bath at 60-90 degrees C, maintaining heat and reacting for 0.5-2 hours, taking out the silk fiber, washing with distilled water and drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the cosolvent is ethanol of 50-80% concentration, the adding amount of ethanol is 2-6 ml/g. in the step (c), the surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate, the antioxidant includes pentaerythritol ester, dibutyl hydroxy toluene and/or tertiary butyl hydroquinone; and the mass ratio of the aqueous solution containing the active component, the modified silk fiber, the coupling agent, the surfactant and the antioxidant is 150-200:80-110:3-5:3-5:3-5.

PN CN112431576-A
TI Method for evaluating impact of carbon dioxide            flooding asphaltene precipitation on recovery, involves            outputting recovery factor curve considering asphaltene            precipitation and without considering asphaltene            precipitation.
AU SHANGGUAN Y
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Y
   XIONG W
   YANG J
   ZHENG Z
   WANG J
   LI W
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202123457W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using the Eclipse software                to establish a one-dimensional numerical model of                the physical properties of the target reservoir and                the properties of formation fluids. The different                pressure differences are input in the                one-dimensional numerical model. The asphaltene                deposition amount obtained from the experiment and                the asphaltene deposition amount obtained from the                one-dimensional numerical model are fitted. The                numerical model of carbon dioxide flooding                asphaltene precipitation is used to calculate the                numerical model with and without asphaltene                precipitation. The recovery factor curve                considering asphaltene precipitation and the                recovery factor curve without considering                asphaltene precipitation are respectively output.                The correlation is found between the two curves.                The asphaltene precipitation has no effect on the                recovery factor when the correlation between the                two curves is not less than 0.9. The asphaltene                precipitation has an effect on the recovery                factor.
   USE - Method for evaluating impact of carbon dioxide                flooding asphaltene precipitation on                recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize high repeatability and                strong operability, and is convenient for oilfield                development and technical personnel to master and                apply.

PT P
PN CN112110452-A; CN112110452-B
TI Synthesizing lithium halide borate by gas-solid reaction, by mixing lithium borohydride, boron oxide, lithium halide and lithium hydroxide by milling, introducing with carbon dioxide into reactor, reacting, collecting gas and calcining.
AU LIANG C
   WANG J
   ZENG C
   ZHANG W
   GAN Y
   HUANG H
   ZHANG J
   XIA Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2020C9893A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing lithium halide borate by gas-solid reaction, involves (a) putting a certain proportion of lithium borohydride, boron oxide, lithium halide, and lithium hydroxide into a sealed ball mill tank in an inert atmosphere, and uniformly mixing them by mechanical ball milling, (b) transferring the uniformly mixed materials in step (a) to a sealed reactor under an inert atmosphere, and introducing excess carbon dioxide into the reactor, (c) heating the gas-solid mixture in the reactor to a certain temperature and keeping it for a period of time, (d) after reacting, lowering the temperature to room temperature, and collecting the gas in the reactor to realize gas-solid separation, and (e) calcining the solid powder obtained in step (d) in an air or oxygen atmosphere to obtain a lithium halide borate powder.
   USE - Method for synthesizing lithium halide borate by gas-solid reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of fewer existing synthetic methods for lithium halide borate, high energy consumption in the process, and complex synthetic routes. The method effectively fixes and utilizes the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide, and provides high-efficiency, low-cost, environmentally-friendly, and easy-to-industrialized method for synthesizing lithium halide borate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The conditions of mechanical ball milling and mixing in step (a) include the ball-to-material mass ratio of (10-80):1, the ball milling speed of 100-500 rpm, and the ball milling time of 2-100 hours. The heating temperature in step (c) is 350-800 degrees C, and the holding time is 1-100 hours. The calcination conditions in step (e) are under normal pressure air or oxygen atmosphere, the heating temperature of 400-500 degrees C, and the holding time of 0.1-10 hours. Preferred Components: In step (a), boron oxide, lithium halide and lithium hydroxide are all anhydrous reagents. The inert atmosphere is any one or more of argon or helium. The composition of the lithium halide borate in step (e) is lithium boron oxide metal compound of formula: Li4+xB7O12+x/2Y. The chemical equation for the synthesis reaction of the lithium halide borate is: 4 moles lithium borohydride (4LiBH4), 12 moles diboron trioxide (12B2O3), 4 moles lithium metal compound (4LiY), (8+4x) moles lithium hydroxide ((8+4x)LiOH), and (2-x) moles carbon dioxide ((2-x)CO2) to produce 4 moles lithium boron oxide metal compound (4Li4+xB7O12+x/2Y), (12+2x) moles hydrogen and (2-x) moles carbon. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the molar ratio of lithium borohydride, boron oxide, lithium halide and lithium hydroxide is 1:3:1:(2-4). In step (b), the molar ratio of excess carbon dioxide to lithium hydroxide is 1:(2-4), and the carbon dioxide pressure is 10-20 bar.Y = Cl, Br, and/or I;andx = 0-2.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   HAN H

GA 2019A0403K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide (I) involves epoxidizing (2,22)-diene-24S-ethyl-5 alpha -cholestol-6-one compound (II) under the action of oxidant, catalyst (A) and solvent (P) to obtain intermediate (III), subjecting intermediate (III) to esterification reaction under the action of catalyst (B), carbon dioxide and solvent (Q) to obtain intermediate (IV), carrying out oxidation reaction under the action of m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid and water to obtain intermediate (V) and hydrolyzing intermediate (V) under the action of sodium hydroxide.
   USE - Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces compound (I) using catalyst having high selectivity, high efficiency and is economical. The process is simple, easy and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide of formula (I) involves epoxidizing (2,22) -diene-24S-ethyl-5 alpha -cholestol-6-one compound of formula (II) under the action of oxidant, catalyst (A) and solvent (P) to obtain intermediate of formula (III), subjecting intermediate (III) to esterification reaction under the action of catalyst (B), carbon dioxide and solvent (Q) to obtain intermediate of formula (IV), carrying out oxidation reaction under the action of m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid and water to obtain intermediate of formula (V) and hydrolyzing intermediate (V) under the action of sodium hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant comprises hydrogen peroxide and sodium hypochlorite, preferably hydrogen peroxide. The catalyst (B) comprises binary catalyst comprising tetrabutylammonium bromide and graphene oxide prepared by improved hummers. The solvent (P) comprises one of water, tetrahydrofuran, ethyl acetate, dioxane and dichloromethane, preferably water. The catalyst (A) is chiral metal salen manganese complex of formula (1).R = H, phenyl or ethyl;R1 = H or t-butyl;andR2 = H, methyl or methoxy.Preferred Composition: The ratio of the amount of compound (I) to chiral catalyst is 1:0.1-4%. The mass ratio of compound (II) to oxidant and solvent (P) is 1:0.09-0.3:2-10. The mass ratio of intermediate (III) to catalyst (B) is 1:0.08-0.2. The mass ratio of intermediate (III) to solvent (Q) is 1:3-10. Preferred Conditions: The epoxidation reaction is carried out at 0-30 degrees C. The esterification reaction is carried out at 25-50 degrees C.

An oxygen extraction device is connected with the gasification furnace, and extracts the oxygen in air and conveys the extracted oxygen to the gasification furnace such that carbon dioxide, water vapor, coal ash and the oxygen in the gasification furnace are subjected to gasification reaction to generate molten slag and carbon monoxide.

PN US2019284049-A1
TI Producing a gas e.g. chlorine dioxide at a controlled rate comprises directing air through a layered bed to produce gas from a precursor, and the layered bed comprises alternating layers of layer of dry particles comprising the precursor.
AU ERNST W
   ISAAC T
AE ICA TRINOVA LLC (ICAT-Non-standard)
GA 201980181Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a gas at a controlled rate, comprises: directing air through a layered bed to produce gas from precursor, where the layered bed comprises alternating layers of a layer of dry particles comprising the precursor and a layer of dry particles comprising a proton-generating species, total number of layers in layered bed is 3 or more; gas is produced at a rate that is controlled by controlling the presence or absence of air flowing though layered bed, the amount of time the air flows through layered bed, total number of layers in layered bed, the average thickness of each of layers of dry particles comprising the precursor in the layered bed, the average thickness of each of the layers of dry particles comprising the proton-generating species in layered bed, dry precursor particles comprise chlorine dioxide precursor and/or gas comprises chlorine dioxide the dry precursor particles comprise a carbon dioxide precursor and gas comprises carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a gas (claimed) comprising chlorine dioxide and/or carbon dioxide at.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed at a temperature of -25 degrees C to 50 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The layer in the bed first contacted with the air is the layer of dry particles comprising the proton-generating species. Preferred Components: 75% or more of the precursor is converted to the gas. The dry particles comprising the precursor further comprise a porous carrier consisting of zeolite crystals, silica, pumice, diatomaceous earth, bentonite, and clay, and the precursor is impregnated in the porous carrier. The dry particles comprising the precursor include 1-90 wt.% of the dry particles of the precursor. The dry precursor particles comprise a carbon dioxide precursor and the carbon dioxide precursor comprises a carbon-containing compound consisting of carbonates, bicarbonates and/or sesquicarbonates. The carbon-containing compound is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium sesquicarbonate. The dry precursor particles comprise a chlorine dioxide precursor and the chlorine dioxide precursor comprises a chlorine dioxide-producing compound consisting of a metal chlorite, a metal chlorate, chloric acid, and/or hypochlorous acid. The metal chlorite comprises sodium chlorite, barium chlorite, calcium chlorite, lithium chlorite, potassium chlorite, and/or magnesium chlorite or where the metal chlorate comprises sodium chlorate, lithium chlorate, potassium chlorate, magnesium chlorate, and/or barium chlorate. The dry particles comprising the proton-generating species further comprise a porous carrier consisting of zeolite crystals, silica, pumice, diatomaceous earth, bentonite, or clay, where the proton-generating species is impregnated in the porous carrier. The dry particles comprising the proton-generating species include 1-90 wt.% of the dry particles of the proton-generating species. The proton-generating species comprises an organic acid, an inorganic acid, and/or metal salt. The proton-generating species comprises an organic acid and/or an inorganic acid consisting of acetic acid, citric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, propionic acid, and/or sulfuric acid. The proton-generating species comprises a metal salt consisting of e.g. ferric chloride, ferric sulfate, sodium acetate, sodium citrate, sodium sulfate, sodium bisulfate, hydrogen phosphate, and/or disodium hydrogen phosphate. The average thickness of each of the layers of dry particles comprising the precursor in the layered bed is 1-50 cm where the average thickness of each of the layers of dry particles comprising the proton-generating species in the layered bed is 1-50 cm and/or the average thickness of each of the layers in the layered bed is 1-50 cm. The total number of layers in the layered bed is 3 layers to 48 layers. The layered bed comprises the alternating layers and further comprises at least one layer comprising a mixture of dry particles comprising a precursor and dry particles comprising a proton-generating species. 50% or more of the precursor is converted to the gas in 7 days or less. 50% or more of the precursor is converted to the gas in 2 months or more.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Synthesis of alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid, involves reacting alkene and carbon dioxide with transition metal complex, forming metal lactone intermediate, and allowing Lewis acid and base to act on intermediate.

   MIYAMA T
   DASANAYAKE A R
AE SEKISUI CHEM IND CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 201980612C
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid involves reacting an alkene and carbon dioxide with a transition metal complex in presence of Lewis acid and base, forming a metal lactone compound (I) as an intermediate, and allowing Lewis acid and base to act on the intermediate (I). The acid deviation constant of the conjugate acid of the base is higher than the acid deviation constant of the ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid.
   USE - Synthesis of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid with excellent production efficiency. Even if the base becomes conjugate acid it is not consumed with the reaction and can be easily regenerated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid involves reacting alkene and a carbon dioxide with a transition metal complex in presence of Lewis acid and base, forming a lactone compound of formula (I) as an intermediate in which the acid deviation constant of the conjugate acid and base is higher than the acid deviation constant of the ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid.
   M = transition metal;and
   L = monodentate ligand or bi-dentate ligand.

TI Catalyst useful for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide comprises carbon material coated with surface organic polymer layer distributed with a non-noble metal.
AU ZHU M
   HAN Y
   XU J
   CHEN J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201969900N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carbon material having outer surface coated with organic polymer layer, where the surface organic polymer layer is distributed with a non-noble metal, the mass ratio of the surface organic polymer layer to the carbon nano material is 1:0.5-1:4 and the weight of non-noble metal in the catalyst is 1-10 wt.%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly and simple; The catalyst: has high catalytic activity, selectivity and stability, and provides a new idea for the electrocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, and has a good industrialization prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon monoxide catalyst comprising (a) dispersing carbon material, organic polymer monomer, polymerization catalyst and 1-10 mmol non-noble metal in aqueous alcohol or water, where the concentration of organic polymer monomer in alcohol or water solvent is 1-15 mmol, (b) heating mixed liquid obtained in step (a) at 120-200 degrees C for 5-10 hours and polymerizing to obtain suspension, and (c) subjecting the suspension to centrifugation, washing, and drying to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The reaction temperature in the method is a normal temperature, reaction pressure is normal pressure, the electrolyte used for the reaction is a 0.5 mol/L aqueous solution of Sodium bicarbonate, and the reaction voltage is -0.4 V to -0.7 V. Preferred Components: The organic polymer layer is a non-noble metal complex of a polymerized phthalocyanine. The carbon material is carbon nanotubes, graphene and/or carbon black. The carbon material is carbon nanotubes of any diameter. The non-noble metal is iron, cobalt or nickel, preferably cobalt. The non-noble metal in step (a) is a chloride salt, and the solvent in step (a) is ethanol or water. Preferred Composition: The thickness of organic polymer layer coated on outer surface of material is 2-10 nm.

PN WO2019087336-A1
TI Environmental index unit for use with livestock            house system e.g. poultry farming system, has control            unit which controls air conditioning, so that carbon            dioxide and ammonia concentration are less than or            equal to threshold value.
AU HIGUCHI K
   KITAYAMA H
   ITO K
   OKADA T
AE MAEKAWA MFG CO LTD (MAEK-C)
GA 2019405274
AB    NOVELTY - The unit has an environmental index sensor                (80) which is provided with an ammonia                concentration sensor that detects ammonia                concentration in a livestock house (10). The                temperature and humidity are detected by the                environmental index sensor. The discomfort index of                the livestock is calculated based on the wind                speed. The temperature, humidity, and wind speed in                the livestock house are controlled, so that the                calculated discomfort index approaches a desired                value. A control unit (90) controls the air                conditioning in the livestock house, when the                carbon dioxide concentration and ammonia                concentration in the livestock house exceeds a                predetermined threshold value, so that carbon                dioxide and ammonia concentration are &#8804;                to the threshold value.

PT P
PN CN109574703-A
TI Preparation of foam ceramic used for wastewater            treatment, involves mixing diatomaceous earth, kaolin,            quartz, straw biochar and alpha-aluminum oxide fine            powder, grinding, water ball milling, drying, adding            gelatin solution and sealing.
AU JIANG J
AE JIANG J (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201933497K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of foam ceramic comprises mixing                diatomaceous earth, kaolin, quartz, straw biochar                and alpha -aluminum oxide fine powder, grinding to                fineness less than 0.1 mm, water ball milling to                particle size of less than or equal to 2 mu m,                drying mixed powder, adding gelatin solution to                prepare slurry with total solid content of 40-45%,                ball milling for 5-10 minutes, magnetically                stirring slurry, vacuum degassing, injecting into                mold, sealing, solidifying in oven at 85 degrees C                for 1 hour, oven-drying at 50 degrees C, demolding,                placing in autoclave of supercritical carbon                dioxide foaming device, filling with low-pressure                carbon dioxide to replace air in kettle, filling                with high-pressure carbon dioxide at 50-80 degrees                C with pressure of 10-20 MPa for 20 minutes and                rapid pressure relief within 2 seconds, baking in                muffle furnace at 1300-1350 degrees C for 4 hours                and cooling.
   USE - The method is used for preparing foam ceramic                for wastewater treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has very good treatment effect on                chemical oxygen demand and ammonia nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises uniformly tiling on the ground                  electrode in low-temperature plasma processing                  apparatus, processing modified foam ceramic with                  power supply frequency of 10-15 kHz, operating                  voltage of 20 kV, and discharge power of 70-80 W                  for 10-15 minutes, taking 200-250 pts. wt.                  aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and 600-650 pts. wt.                  distilled water, stirring, dissolving, placing in                  water bath, heating under reflux at 90 degrees C,                  slowly adding 35-40 pts. wt. aluminum powder to                  react, adjusting pH value of control solution to                  3.0-4.0, removing the underlying gray precipitate                  after centrifugation to obtain colorless                  transparent sol, adding 20-25 pts. wt.                  polyethylene glycol, 3-6 pts. wt. lanthanum                  nitrate and 4-8 pts. wt. barium nitrate, stirring                  to dissolve to obtain pale yellow transparent                  sol, soaking modified foam ceramic in light                  yellow transparent sol, vacuum soaking for 0.5-1                  hour, taking out, drying, placing in muffle                  furnace and baking at 500-800 degrees C for 5                  hours. The swelling step is done at 65 degrees C                  with pressure of 15 MPa for 15 minutes. Preferred                  Composition: The content of gelatin in the mixed                  powder is 20-30 wt.%.

PT P
PN CN109207715-A; CN109207715-B; WO2020077851-A1
TI Recovering iron by roasting modified copper slag comprises e.g. heating desiliconization modifier and the copper slag to sintering temperature, maintaining roasting temperature and inert gas protection and separating by magnetic field.
AU JIANG P
   LIU J
   LIAO C
   QIU T
   CENG Y
   ZENG Y
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
   UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 201908052K
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering iron by roasting modified copper slag comprises (i) heating the desiliconization modifier and the copper slag to a sintering temperature under the protection of an inert gas, freeing ferrous olivine in the slag ferrous oxide free; (ii) maintaining roasting temperature and inert gas protection, communicating the roasting system with the outside atmosphere, simultaneously introducing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas into the sintering system in the step (i) maintaining the volume percentage of carbon monoxide in the total gas components of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas is 0-5%, oxidizing the free ferrous oxide to triiron tetroxide, and growing magnetic triiron tetroxide crystal; and (iii) cooling after the grinding and classification process to obtain modified slag with different granularities, magnetic separating under magnetic field to obtain iron concentrate and magnetic separation tailings.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering iron by roasting modified copper slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can utilize the industrial waste gas carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to efficiently recover iron in the copper residue; an iron concentrate suitable for iron making raw materials and a magnetic separation tail which can be used as a raw material for building materials e.g. cement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcination temperature is 800-1200 degrees C. In the step (ii), roasting at constant temperature, the total flow rate of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas is 150-250 ml/minute, raising the temperature at constant roasting temperature and roasting is finished during cooling, the intake flow of the inert gas is 250 ml/minute. The gas flow rates of the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide are 0-10 ml/minute and 190-200 ml/minute respectively. In the step (iii), grinding by ball mill or vibrating mill, classifying by vibrating sieve and separating by magnetic separation pipe. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the inert gas is nitrogen, argon and/or carbon dioxide. The desiliconization modifier include hydrated lime. The mass ratio of desiliconization modifier and copper is 1:10-3:10. In the step (iii) the particle size of modified slag is 38-100 mu m and the strength of the magnetic field is 80-130 mT.

   IMPERIAL OIL RESOURCES LTD (IMOI-C)
GA 2018726780
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an oil sand slurry stream (202) to a primary separation cell (PSC) (204) to form a bitumen froth (206), a middlings stream (208), and a coarse sand tailings stream (CST) (210). The middlings stream is introduced to a flotation process to form the recycle froth (214) and the flotation tailings stream (216). The carbon dioxide (218,220,224) is introduced into the PSC, beneath a froth layer, the middlings stream and the flotation process for increasing bitumen recovery. The carbon dioxide in the CST and the flotation tailings stream are sequestered to increase the fines capture in the CST.
   USE - Method for oil sand slurry treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The heavy oils can be used with the several reservoirs to improve gravity drainage of liquids. The thickener operation when treating flotation tailing with lower pH can be improved. The solids and bitumen separation can be facilitated in the cases where fines can be highly concentrated in the middlings layer.

PN CN107873833-A
TI Storing rice involves naturally drying the rice which is uniformly paved on bamboo mat for placing rice paper, carding the rice for one time every day, insolating per day, and primary screening for mechanically removing crushed grains.
AU LUO Q
AE ZUNYI HANYA GRAIN & OIL BUYING & SELLING CO LTD (ZUNY-Non-standard)
GA 201829502X
AB    NOVELTY - Storing rice involves naturally drying the rice which is paved uniformly on bamboo mat for placing rice paper, carding the rice for one time every day for 2-4 hours, insolating per day for 4-6 hours under sun for 2-4 days, primary screening for mechanically removing crushed grains, leaves and grass impurities. The rice is selected in accordance with a predetermined standard quality of rice and processed. The processed rice is put in sealed container, carbon dioxide is injected into container, and concentration of the carbon dioxide in the container is 90-95%. The rice is irradiated for 10-12 hours, sterilized by using ultraviolet deinsectization. The processed rice is filtered to remove rice with mildew. The rice is placed in drying room for drying till the rice water content is less than 8%, and placed in ventilate jute. The air is drawn out from each bag, and then filled in a proportion of 8-12 kilograms of carbon dioxide per 10,000 kilograms of rice and finally the bags are sealed.
   USE - Method for storing rice.

AU LING Z
   ZHANG L
AE CHENGDU DAOFEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN JIXING ANIMAL PHARM CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201812849D
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises active ingredient of the medicine composition including 1-1000 pts. wt. Macleaya cordata total alkaloid extract and 1-2000 pts. wt. quinolone compound.
   USE - The composition is useful in preparing medicine for livestock, poultry, pets and preferably piglets, treating gastrointestinal bacterial infections and controlling the infection of the disease (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has good curative effect, stable quality and safe. The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composition comprising (i) dissolving the antioxidant, metal ion chelating agent, bacteriostatic agent (analgesic) agent and freezing agent with carbon dioxide or nitrogen saturated with water for injection. then adding quinolones drug and stirring to make it dissolve; (ii) using of acid extraction alkali precipitation collecting alcohol reflux extracting to obtain Macleaya cordata total alkali, where the extraction rate is not less than 60% and dissolving the Macleaya cordata total alkaloid extract and polysorbate 80 in in carbon dioxide or Nitrogen-saturated water for injection; (iii) mixing the solution, injecting carbon dioxide or Nitrogen-saturated water for injection, diluting with hydrochloric acid to adjust pH to 4.2-5, filtering, filling, sealing, sterilizing for 30 minutes with 100 degrees C flowing steam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of Macleaya cordata total alkaloid extract and quinolone compound is 1:200-100:1. The quinolone compound is derived from ciprofloxacin, sparfloxacin, levofloxacin, ofloxacin, fleroxacin, enoxacin, ibafloxacin , sarafloxacin, marbofloxacin, orbifloxacin, danafloxacin or ester is the salt of the carbostyril medicine or the quinolones drug. The mass percentage of the quinolone compound includes composition of not greater than 1%. The drug dosage form can be liquid preparation, derived from injection, syrup and oral solution, where the solid preparation is derived from tablet, dispersible tablet, slow release tablet, capsule, granule, powder and semi-solid preparation can be ointment, paste, or gel. The compound macleaya cordata injection comprises macleaya cordata, quinolones drug, antioxidant, metal ion chelating agent, bacteriostatic agent and analgesic, ice point regulator, pH regulator, solubilizer and water for injection, where the dosage of macleaya cordata is 0.5%; the quinolones drug is derived from the group consisting of ciprofloxacin, and its dosage is 1%. The medicine is useful for treating animal diarrhea.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Antibacterial.

PN US10549232-B1
TI Carbon dioxide capturing involves introducing mixture of reaction materials into carbonation reactor, where reaction materials comprise water and magnetite and iron, or magnetite and carbon.
AU MENDOZA E Y M
   DROZD V
   DURYGIN A
   SAXENA S K
AE UNIV FLORIDA (UYFL-C)
   MENDOZA E Y M (MEND-Individual)
   DROZD V (DROZ-Individual)
   DURYGIN A (DURY-Individual)
   SAXENA S K (SAXE-Individual)
GA 202012302W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capturing involves introducing a mixture of reaction materials into a carbonation reactor, where the reaction materials comprise water and magnetite and iron, or magnetite and carbon. A stream is passed through gas comprising carbon dioxide into carbonation reactor. A revolution speed is applied for rotating carbonation reactor. The carbonation reactor grinds the reaction materials while carbon dioxide reacts with the reaction materials to form siderite. The siderite is decomposed at room temperature to produce iron (II,III) oxide and iron, or iron (II,III) oxide and carbon for subsequent cycles.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method captures the carbon dioxide in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved capture properties with subsequent capture while pure carbon dioxide is stored.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the scheme of carbon dioxide capture via siderite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction materials comprise magnetite, hematite, wustite or goethite. The reducing agent is selected from metallic iron and carbon. The grinding mechanism comprises grinding balls.

PN CN114487286-A
TI Carbon dioxide concentration online monitoring system for e.g. coal-fired power plant, has display system connected with carbon dioxide quality control system and displaying calculation result of control system.
AU HOU J
AE SHANGHAI BOCHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202270417J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon-dioxide-continuous emission monitoring systems (CEMS) system placed in the flue and used to collect sample gas in the flue. A carbon-dioxide quality control system connects with the carbon-dioxide-CEMS system and collect the carbon-dioxide concentration data output by the carbon-dioxide CEMS system in real time. The carbon-dioxide concentration is calculated. The difference between the carbon-dioxide concentration data calculated by the carbon-dioxide-CEMS system and the carbon-dioxide concentration data calculated by the carbon-dioxide quality control system is compared with the threshold value. The invalid data is maintained during calibration, at the same time, when carbon-dioxide-CEMS fails and supplemented with carbon-dioxide concentration data calculated by carbon-dioxide quality control system. A display system connects with the carbon-dioxide quality control system and displays the calculation result of the carbon-dioxide quality control system.
   USE - Online monitoring system for measuring carbon emission of source of fossil fuel combustion. Uses include but are not limited to coal-fired power plant, cement plant, steel and iron plant, petroleum and petrochemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 concentration online monitoring system comprises a CO2-CEMS system that is set in a flue and used for collecting the sample gas in the flue, where CO2 quality control system is connected with the CO2 CEMS system, and thus enables to solve the technical problem of unstable current data.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) a carbon-dioxide concentration online monitoring method; (2) a carbon-dioxide-CEMS system data automatic quality control method; and (3) an automatic complementing method of carbon-dioxide-CEMS system data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon-dioxide concentration online monitoring method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113389533-A; CN113389533-B
TI Integrated method of carbon dioxide capture,            reservoir reconstruction, and extraction of crude oil            comprises introducing imidazole and metal-organic            framework material into alcohol solvent, and performing            fracturing modification operation.
AU LIU H
   YAO D
   GUO P
   WANG J
   WANG Z
   DU J
   DAI P
   LI R
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2021A8527U
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated method of carbon dioxide capture,                reservoir reconstruction, and extraction of crude                oil comprises introducing imidazole or its                derivative and metal-organic framework material                into alcohol solvent, collecting carbon dioxide in                the tail gas at the tail gas port of the thermal                power plant or chemical plant using the mixed base                liquid, adding cross-linking agent and thickening                agent into the base liquid capturing carbon                dioxide, and preparing into the alcohol-based                fracturing fluid, injecting the alcohol-based                fracturing fluid into the reservoir wellhead,                performing fracturing modification operation to the                reservoir, releasing the carbon dioxide gas for                improving the crude oil property and even oil                displacement, reducing the well bottom pressure,                discharging the rest injection liquid in the                reservoir, and exploiting crude oil and collecting                the part of carbon dioxide collected with the crude                oil to obtain the alcohol-based fracturing fluid                again.
   USE - The method is useful for integrating carbon                dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a reliable principle, simple                operation, capable of effectively capturing carbon                dioxide, reservoir reconstruction, carbon dioxide                oil gas, and carbon dioxide coupling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated method of carbon dioxide capture,                reservoir reconstruction and extraction of crude                oil comprises (i) introducing imidazole or its                derivative and metal organic framework material                into alcohol solvent to obtain mixed base liquid;                (ii) collecting carbon dioxide in the tail gas at                the tail gas port of the thermal power plant or                chemical plant using the mixed base liquid; when                the concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas                discharged from the top of the absorption liquid is                less than or equal to 5 %, the absorption amount of                the base liquid to the carbon dioxide is 2.7-3                mol/g less than 5 %, stopping absorbing; (iii)                adding cross-linking agent and thickening agent                into the base liquid capturing carbon dioxide, and                preparing into alcohol-based fracturing fluid; (iv)                injecting the alcohol-based fracturing fluid into                the reservoir wellhead, performing fracturing                modification operation to the reservoir; (v)                injecting acid gel breaking agent into the                wellhead, making the acidic gel breaking agent and                the alcohol fracturing contact damage carbon                dioxide and imidazole molecule in the seam net; the                acting force between the alcohol molecule and the                metal organic framework material, releasing the                carbon dioxide gas for improving the crude oil                property and even oil displacement; (vi) reducing                the well bottom pressure, discharging the rest                injection liquid in the reservoir, and exploiting                crude oil; (vii) collecting the part of carbon                dioxide collected with the crude oil to obtain the                alcohol-based fracturing fluid again; and repeating                the steps (iv)-(vi).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  imidazole derivative is 2-ethyl-4-methyl                  imidazole, 2-phenyl imidazole, and/or 2-methyl                  imidazole. In the step (i) the metal-organic                  framework material is hydrophobic material whose                  framework opening diameter is less than or equal                  to 4.5 nm, In the step (i), the metal-organic                  framework material is ZIF-8 or ZIF-67. In the                  mixed base liquid of step (i), the mass fraction                  of each component is as follows: 60-80% alcohol                  solvent, 5-40% imidazole or imidazole                  derivatives; and metal-organic framework material                  0-20%. In the step (i), the alcohol solvent is                  glycol or propanetriol. In the step (iii), the                  cross-linking agent is organic boron. In the step                  (iii), the thickening agent is plant glue. In the                  step (v), the acid gel breaker is ammonium                  persulfate, and the injection amount is 5-30 % of                  the mass of the alcohol base fracturing fluid                  invading the reservoir.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q35 (Refuse collection, conveyors (B65F, G)); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PT P
PN CN109893964-A
TI Method for absorbing and storing carbon dioxide, involves mixing monoethanolamine with tetrabutylammonium bromide and heating, followed by collected pressure parameters in reactor and equilibrium tank at equilibrium of absorption.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG L
   LUO G
   JU J
   FENG R
   HUA Y
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 2019563692
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorbing and storing method involves mixing monoethanolamine with tetrabutylammonium bromide, heating to obtain a light yellow transparent solution, and cooling to room temperature to obtain an eutectic solvent, adding N-methyldiethanolamine solvent and deionized water to the solvent, mixing to obtain an absorbent, placing the absorbent in a reaction vessel, injecting carbon dioxide gas into an equilibrium reactor, collecting a pressure parameters, evacuating the reaction kettle, injecting the carbon dioxide gas in the balance tank, collecting the pressure parameters, closing the carbon dioxide gas communication line, injecting the carbon dioxide gas into the balance tank, collecting the pressure parameters, opening the carbon dioxide gas communication line. The carbon dioxide gas is injected into the reaction kettle, collected the pressure parameters and collected the pressure parameters in the reactor and the equilibrium tank at the equilibrium of absorption.
   USE - Method for absorbing and storing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables absorbing and storing carbon dioxide with inhibited the alcohol amine solution degradation and improved the carbon dioxide of solution absorption property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide absorbing and storing method involves mixing monoethanolamine with tetrabutylammonium bromide, heating to obtain a light yellow transparent solution, and cooling to room temperature to obtain a low eutectic solvent, adding N-methyldiethanolamine solvent and deionized water to the eutectic solvent, mixing uniformly to obtain an absorbent, placing the absorbent in a reaction vessel, injecting carbon dioxide gas into an equilibrium reactor, controlling temperature of the reaction kettle, collecting a pressure parameters in the equilibrium kettle when the temperature in the reactor is stable and a pressure in the equilibrium tank is stable, evacuating the reaction kettle, injecting the carbon dioxide gas in the balance tank into the reaction kettle, after the pressure in the reaction kettle is stabilized, collecting the pressure parameters in the reaction kettle and the equilibrium kettle after the absorption equilibrium is reached, closing the carbon dioxide gas communication line between the reaction vessel and the equilibrium reactor, continuing to inject the carbon dioxide gas into the balance tank, when the pressure in the balance tank is stable, collecting the pressure parameters in the balance tank, opening the carbon dioxide gas communication line between the reaction tank and the balance tank. The carbon dioxide gas in the balance tank is continuously injected into the reaction kettle, and the pressure in the reaction kettle is stabilized, after the absorption equilibrium is reached, collected the pressure parameters in the reaction kettle and the equilibrium kettle, used a stepwise pressure absorption method until the pressure in the reactor reaches 1.3-1.8 MPa, and collected the pressure parameters in the reactor and the equilibrium tank at the equilibrium of absorption.

PN WO2018200526-A1
TI Recapturing carbon dioxide, comprises pressuring stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component to produce pressurized stream, and indirectly exchanging thermal energy between streams.
AU KEISER D R
   LOPEZ A S
AE OXY USA INC (OCCI-C)
GA 201885624B
AB    NOVELTY - Recapturing (M1) carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) providing a stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component; (b) pressuring the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component to produce a pressurized stream; and (c) indirectly exchanging thermal energy between the pressurized stream and a thermal-energy accepting stream including a boilable component.
   USE - The method is useful for recapturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides carbon dioxide with improved energy recapture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recapturing (M2) carbon dioxide from a stream including carbon dioxide, comprising (i) providing a first stream containing carbon dioxide, (ii) adsorbing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide in the first stream in an adsorbing means, (iii) desorbing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide in the adsorbing means to obtain a desorbed carbon dioxide stream, and (iv) compressing the desorbed carbon dioxide stream immediately following the step of desorbing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the step of indirectly exchanging thermal energy results in condensing at least a portion of the condensable component and boiling at least a portion of the boilable component; the step of pressuring occurs before any step of providing the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component to a heat exchanger; the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component is provided as an outlet stream of a carbon dioxide adsorbing and desorbing process, where the step of pressuring is immediately sequential following the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component exiting the carbon dioxide adsorbing and desorbing process; and the step of indirectly exchanging thermal energy between the pressurized stream and the thermal-energy accepting stream includes the condensable component in the pressurized stream having a condensation temperature at least 5 degrees F greater than the boiling temperature of the boilable component in the thermal-energy accepting stream. In the method (M2): the first stream containing carbon dioxide further includes a condensable component, where the step of compressing produces a pressurized stream, the method further comprising (v) indirectly exchanging thermal energy between the pressurized stream and a thermal-energy accepting stream having a boilable component in it, where the condensable component in the pressurized stream has a condensation temperature at least 5 degrees F greater than the boiling temperature of the boilable component in the thermal-energy accepting stream; the thermal-energy accepting stream includes water, so that the step of indirectly exchanging thermal energy results in vaporization of a substantial portion of the water in the thermal-energy accepting stream upon thermal energy exchange; the condensing of the water vapor subsequently results in a separate condensate stream from a knock out drum, the method further comprising the step of returning the condensate stream to the thermal-energy accepting stream; the step of desorbing occurs in a solid sorbent, where the vaporization of a substantial portion of the water in the thermal-energy accepting stream produces a steam stream, where the steam stream is routed back to the solid sorbent; the step of desorbing occurs in a regenerator, where the thermal-energy accepting stream is routed from the bottom of the regenerator, to a heat exchanger where the step of indirectly exchanging thermal energy is performed, and back to a side inlet of the regenerator; the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component is provided as an outlet stream of a fuel cell, where the step of pressuring is immediately sequential following the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component exiting the fuel cell; and the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component is provided as an outlet stream of an oxy-fuel combustion process, where the step of pressuring is immediately sequential following the stream containing carbon dioxide and at least one other condensable component exiting the oxy-fuel combustion process. Preferred Components: The other condensable component is water vapor. The stream containing carbon dioxide includes 50 vol.% or 10-90 vol.% carbon dioxide. The adsorbing means is: a solid sorbent; and an amine solution within an amine system. The thermal-energy accepting stream: is water; and includes amine, so that the step of indirectly exchanging thermal energy results in vaporization of a substantial portion of the amine in the thermal-energy accepting stream upon thermal energy exchange.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108952639-A; CN108952639-B
TI Method for combined gas displacement and vibration            field exploitation of natural gas hydrate reservoirs,            involves deploying carbon dioxide medium injection            well, extracting part of free gas followed by injecting            medium into reservoir.
AU LIU H
   ZHAN S
   GUO P
   ZHAO J
   CHEN S
   WANG C
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2018A0416S
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas hydrate reservoirs combined gas                displacement and vibration field exploitation                method involves deploying a carbon dioxide medium                injection well and a natural gas production well in                a natural gas hydrate reservoir, and extracting                part of the free gas from the natural gas                production well. The vibration generator is                inserted into the hydrate reservoir from the carbon                dioxide medium injection well, followed by                injecting medium into reservoir to realize the                replacement of gas by carbon dioxide in the                hydrate, continuously or intermittently opening                vibration generator, utilizing vibration wave to                weaken cementation between the crystals of the                hydrate and between the crystal and solid deposit,                during displacement mining, promoting the diffusion                of carbon dioxide in the sediment layer, and                improving the hydration of carbon dioxide                displacement mining,, forming cracks in the                reservoir, providing a seepage channel for further                replacement of carbon dioxide for natural gas                hydrate.
   USE - Method for combined gas displacement and                vibration field exploitation of natural gas hydrate                reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the efficiency                and rate of replacing the natural gas hydrate by                the carbon dioxide medium, and maintaining the                stability of the reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A natural gas hydrate reservoirs combined gas                displacement and vibration field exploitation                method involves deploying a carbon dioxide medium                injection well and a natural gas production well in                a natural gas hydrate reservoir, and extracting                part of the free gas from the natural gas                production well to reduce the pressure of the                hydrate reservoir to 3-5 MPa. The vibration                generator is inserted into the hydrate reservoir                from the carbon dioxide medium injection well,                followed by injecting medium into the reservoir to                realize the replacement of natural gas by the                carbon dioxide in the hydrate, where the injection                pressure of the carbon dioxide medium is 4-8 MPa,                continuously or intermittently opening vibration                generator, utilizing vibration wave to weaken the                cementation between the crystals of the hydrate and                between the crystal and the solid deposit, during                the displacement mining, promoting the diffusion of                carbon dioxide in the sediment layer, and improving                the hydration of carbon dioxide displacement                mining, when the carbon dioxide concentration in                the free gas of the reservoir is constant or                changes very slowly, working load of the vibration                generator is further increased, and the generated                carbon dioxide-gas hydrate mixed reservoir and the                unreplaced hydration are generated by the vibration                wave energy, forming cracks in the reservoir,                providing a seepage channel for further replacement                of carbon dioxide for natural gas hydrate, when the                carbon dioxide concentration in the free gas of the                reservoir is less than 30 mol%, natural gas                production pressure greater than the phase                equilibrium pressure of the carbon dioxide hydrate                at the hydrate reservoir temperature, which is                smaller than the hydrate reservoir.

PN WO2022111026-A1; CN114558549-A
TI Use of carboxylate compounds as absorbents for            capturing carbon dioxide and in preparation of            absorbents for capturing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG Z
   HAN B
AE BEIJING YUTAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING YUTAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202276445Q
AB    NOVELTY - Use of carboxylate compounds as absorbents for                capturing carbon dioxide and in the preparation of                absorbents for capturing carbon dioxide. In the                carboxylate compound, the carboxylate anion is a                carboxylate having carbon chain with a carbon                number of greater than or equal to 3, and the                cation is a substituted quaternary ammonium ion                and/or a quaternary phosphorus ion.
   USE - The carboxylate compounds are useful as                absorbents for capturing carbon dioxide and in                preparation of absorbents for capturing carbon                dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carboxylate compounds have advantage of                cheap and easily available raw materials, simple                synthesis, high collection amount, stable water                collection, low temperature required for                desorption, and high speed, low energy consumption;                good application prospect; can be regenerated and                achieved under reduced pressure at room                temperature; have absorption time from many minutes                to many hours depending on the conditions; and are                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                capturing carbon dioxide with high efficiency and                energy saving using carboxylate compounds to                stabilize water, comprising placing aqueous                solution of the carboxylate compound in carbon                dioxide atmosphere to absorb carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ion includes                  lithium, sodium, potassium and/or cesium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylate having                  greater than or equal to 3C carbon chain is a                  branched carboxylate. The number of carbon atoms                  in the carboxylic acid having greater than or                  equal to 3C carbon chain is 4-20, preferably                  4-10. The carboxylate having greater than or                  equal to 3C carbon chain includes                  2,2-dimethylbutyrate, 2,2-dimethylhexanoate,                  2-ethylhexanoate, 2-ethylheptanoate,                  2-propylpentanoate, 2-propylhexanoate,                  cyclohexylformate, and/or n-octanoate. The                  substituted quaternary ammonium ions contain                  quaternary ammonium-based compound of formula                  (I). The quaternary phosphorus ions contain                  quaternary phosphorus compound of formula (II).                  The substituted quaternary ammonium ion includes                  triethylbutylammonium, tetrabutylammonium and/or                  tributylhexylammonium. The substituted quaternary                  phosphorus ion includes tetrabutylphosphine,                  tributylhexylphosphine, tributyloctylphosphine,                  tributyldecylphosphine, tributyldodecylphosphine,                  tributyltetradecylphosphine, and/or                  tributylhexadecylphosphine. In the carboxylate                  compound, the carboxylate anion is branched chain                  carboxylate with total number of carbon atoms of                  greater than or equal to 6, and the cation is                  substituted quaternary ammonium ion, quaternary                  phosphorus ion, pyridine ion, pyrrole ion,                  piperidine ion, imidazolium ion and/or metal ion.                  The number of carbon atoms in the branched-chain                  carboxylate having total number of carbon atoms                  of greater than or equal to 6 is 7-20, preferably                  8-16. The branched-chain carboxylate having total                  number of carbon atoms of greater than or equal                  to 6 includes 2,2-dimethylhexanoate,                  2-ethylhexanoate, 2-ethylheptanoate,                  2-propylvalerate, 2-propylhexanoate,                  2-propylnonanoate, 2-butyloctanoate and/or                  2-hexyldecanoate. The substituted quaternary                  ammonium ion includes tetramethylammonium,                  tetraethylammonium and/or tributylmethylammonium.                  The substituted quaternary phosphorus ion                  includes tetramethylphosphine,                  tetraethylphosphine and/or                  tributylmethylphosphine. The pyridinium ion                  contains N-substituted pyridinium cation of                  formula (III). The pyrrolidine ion contains                  N-substituted pyrrole cation of formula (IV). The                  piperidinium ion contains N-substituted                  piperidinium cation of formula (V). The                  imidazolium ion contains disubstituted and/or                  trisubstituted imidazolium cation of formula                  (VI). The molar ratio of the carboxylate compound                  to water is 1:0.5-300, preferably 1:0.5-100. The                  carbon dioxide has partial pressure of 0.01-2MPa,                  preferably 0.00004-0.5 MPa. The carbon dioxide                  absorption amount per mole of the carboxylate                  compound is 0.1-1.2 moles, preferably 0.1-1.1                  moles.n = 2-4;m = 4-16;R1 and R2 = greater than or equal to 1C                  straight-chain aliphatic chains; andR3 = H or CH3.Preferred Method: The method is carried out                  at 25-80 degrees C, preferably 5-80 degrees C.                  The method further comprises regenerating the                  carboxylate compound after the carbon dioxide                  absorption is completed, where the regeneration                  process is carried out at 10-120 degrees C for 1                  minute to 10 hour under reduced pressure, and                  pressure of 0.01-1 atmosphere.
   EXAMPLE - 0.86 g Tributylhexylphosphine 2-ethylhexanoate                and 0.72 g water in glass vials were added, carbon                dioxide balloons were turned on, the solution was                placed in carbon dioxide at atmospheric pressure,                stirred at 25 degrees C for 12 hours, and the                system was divided into an organic phase and a                water phase. After the balloon was removed, the gas                carbon dioxide on the upper portion of the solution                in the bottle was replaced with air, titrated with                a sodium hydroxide solution (0.2 wt.%), and the                amount of carbon dioxide absorbing carbon dioxide                was 0.98 mmol.

PT P
PN CN113617235-A
TI Preparation of saturated carbon dioxide solution, involves heating water and using negative pressure to remove portion of air, heating carbon dioxide flowing out of storage tank, combining water and carbon dioxide gas in mixing tank, and passing mixed liquid into temperature riser, and emitting gas.
AU WANG J
   TONG J
   QIAN C
AE JIANGSU ASIA ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0213X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of saturated carbon dioxide solution, involves (S1) heating the water and using negative pressure to remove portion of the air, and then reducing the water to 5-10degrees Celsius, (S2) heating the carbon dioxide flowing out of the high-pressure gas storage tank to 5-10degrees Celsius through the evaporator, (S3) combining 5-10degrees Celsius water and 5-10degrees Celsius carbon dioxide gas in a gas-liquid mixing tank, and (S4) passing the mixed liquid into the temperature riser to be heated to the required temperature, and emitting the gas during the temperature rising process through the gas-liquid separation valve.
   USE - Preparation of saturated carbon dioxide solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the carbon dioxide in the water is in a saturated state to make the solubility of carbon dioxide gas in water higher than that at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (S1), the water temperature is lowered to room temperature by exchanging heat with the air by the first heat exchanger, and then the room temperature water is lowered to 5-10degrees Celsius through the refrigerator. The refrigerator is the second heat exchanger, where one end of the second heat exchanger is connected to the outlet of the high-pressure gas storage tank, in the case of controlling the flow rate, the carbon dioxide gas flows out through the high-pressure gas storage tank to cool the water, and the carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the second heat exchanger is combined with the carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the evaporator and then passed into the gas-liquid mixing tank. In the step (S1), the first heat exchanger and the carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the high-pressure gas storage tank carry out heat exchange to reduce the temperature to 5-10degrees Celsius, and the carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the first heat exchanger is combined with the carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the evaporator and then passed into the gas-liquid mixing tank. After step (S3), the conductivity of the mixed liquid in the gas-liquid mixing tank process is also measured by a conductivity measuring device to judge whether the carbon dioxide gas in the mixed liquid is saturated. The gas flowing out of the gas-liquid mixing tank is collected through the discharge tank. The gas-liquid mixing tanks are arranged in parallel. The gas emitted from the gas-liquid separation valve is mixed with carbon dioxide gas flowing out of the dehumidification tank and the high-pressure gas storage tank. Portion of the high-pressure gas storage tank flows out of carbon dioxide into the heat preservation tank around the gas-liquid mixing tank, so that the gas-liquid mixing tank can be maintained at 5-10degrees Celsius. Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature of water in step (S1) is 60-70degrees Celsius.

PN CN111701611-A; CN111701611-B
TI Divalent copper carbon dioxide reduction catalyst used in reducing and preparing ethylene by carbon dioxide one-step method comprises lanthanum copper oxide perovskite type oxide as main body having surface loaded with lanthanum carbonate.
AU ZHOU W
   SUN Z
   WU X
   XIE F
   GUO Y
   SHAO Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2020968998
AB    NOVELTY - A divalent copper carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises lanthanum copper oxide (La2CuO4) perovskite type oxide as main body. The surface is loaded with lanthanum carbonate.
   USE - Divalent copper carbon dioxide reduction catalyst used in reducing and preparing ethylene by carbon dioxide one-step method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits carbonate synergistic effect; improves efficiency of carbonate participation reaction; and prepares ethylene by one-step method of Faraday efficiency close to 60% at normal temperature and normal pressure. The method is simple, and has low raw material cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a preparation method of the divalent copper carbon dioxide reduction catalyst which comprises preparing La2CuO4 perovskite type oxide; dispersing perovskite type oxide in potassium bicarbonate (KHCO3) solution, and introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) to react, and making surface generate lanthanum carbonate; and
   (2) use of divalent copper carbon dioxide reduction catalyst in reducing and preparing ethylene by carbon dioxide one-step method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lanthanum carbonate is in the shape of crystal branch. Preferred Method: The perovskitetype oxide is obtained by sol-gel method, solid phase reaction method, co-precipitation method, and/or hydrothermal method. The sol-gel method comprises dissolving lanthanum nitrate according to stoichiometric ratio; dissolving copper acetate in the deionized water and uniformly stirring; adding EDTA and citric acid monohydrate as metal ion complexing agent and ammonia water as pH regulator of mixed solution; heating and stirring mixed solution to gel shape; drying for 3-10 hours at 180-250 degrees C to obtain precursor powder; calcining obtained precursor powder at 600-1000 degrees C (preferably 900 degrees C), for 4-6 hours, preferably 5 hours; and cooling, grinding to obtain La2CuO4 perovskite type oxide. The concentration of KHCO3 solution is 0.5-5 M. The reaction time of CO2 is 0.5-5 hours. In the application of the catalyst, the three-electrode system is used. The reduction catalyst is the cathode, the silver-silver chloride is the reference electrode, and the platinum sheet is the counter electrode. About 0.1M KHCO3 solution is used in the electrolytic cell, and carbon dioxide gas is introduced to react.

methanol flash section under reduced pressure flash and gas stripping, combining the flash vapors flashed out of the carbon dioxide rich methanol flash section and the flash vapors flashed out of the hydrogen sulfide rich methanol flash section into flash vapors and output, leaving the carbon dioxide-rich methanol from the bottom of the carbon dioxide-rich methanol flash section enters the
