sulfide-rich methanol from the bottom of the hydrogen sulfide-rich methanol flashing section enters the stripping and re-absorbing section at the lower part of the re-absorption tower, the gas stripping and reabsorption section at the lower part of the reabsorption column has nitrogen gas flow through, and desorbing the carbon dioxide in the hydrogen sulfide-rich methanol through the effects of reduced pressure flashing and nitrogen stripping, again absorbing the sulfide again by the carbon dioxide-rich methanol solution flowing from the low-pressure flash section to the re-absorption section, and enriching at the bottom of the column, desorbing the carbon dioxide in the methanol-rich solution at the bottom of the tower to the maximum extent under the action of low-pressure flash evaporation and nitrogen gas stripping, and desorbing the remaining carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the methanol-rich solution, after merging the top of the re-absorption section, sulfur and methanol.

TI Carbon dioxide recovery system that separates            carbon dioxide from gas by electrochemical reaction,            has electrochemical cell with counter electrode and            working electrode containing carbon dioxide adsorbent            held in base material by binder.
AU IIJIMA G

   LIJIMA G
   IIJIMA T

   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 2022531994
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide recovery system that                separates carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide-containing gas by an electrochemical                reaction, comprises electrochemical cell (101)                comprising a working electrode (102) and a counter                electrode (103). Working electrode comprises an                electrode base material (102a), a carbon dioxide                adsorbent (102b), and a binder (102c). Application                of a voltage between the working electrode and the                counter electrode causes electrons to be supplied                from the counter electrode to the working                electrode, and enables the carbon dioxide adsorbent                to bind carbon dioxide to it as electrons are                supplied. The binder has electrical conductivity,                and the carbon dioxide adsorbent is held in the                electrode base material by the binder.
   USE - A carbon dioxide recovery system that                separates carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide-containing gas by an electrochemical                reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent is held in the                electrode base material by using a binder. As a                result, the carbon dioxide adsorbent is less likely                to become detached from the electrode base                material, and a decrease in the amount of carbon                dioxide adsorbed by the electrochemical cell over                time can be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for working                electrode used in the carbon dioxide recovery                system, comprising electrode base material, carbon                dioxide adsorbent, and binder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                an electrochemical cell.
   Electrochemical cell (101)
   Working electrode (102)
   Electrode base material (102a)
   Carbon dioxide adsorbent (102b)
   Binder (102c)
   Counter electrode (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Binder is a gel-state                  material or a solid-state material. Binder is an                  ionic liquid gel obtainable by gelation of an                  ionic liquid. Carbon dioxide-containing gas                  contains a set of types of gases, and the binder                  has carbon dioxide-selective permeability, which                  enables carbon dioxide to be selectively                  permeated among the set of types of gases                  contained in the carbon dioxide-containing gas.                  The binder and the carbon dioxide adsorbent form                  a mixture, and the mixture is held in the                  electrode base material.Preferred Properties: Binder has carbon                  dioxide permeability. The binder has                  hydrophobicity.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Ion conductive                  component having ionic conductivity is provided                  between the working electrode and the counter                  electrode, and the ion conductive component is a                  gel-state material or solid-state material.

PN WO2021153650-A1; JP2021574092-X; CN114901383-A
TI Composition used for separating and purifying            carbon dioxide from combustion exhaust gas generated in            thermal power plant, steel plant, cement and factory,            and refining and separating carbon dioxide from steam            reforming gas, comprises amine compound.
AU RYOTARO F
   KOTARO S
   MANABU Y
   HIROYUKI K
   KISO H
   YANASE M
   SAKODA K
   FUJII R
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202190334T
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises amine compound (I) or                (II).
   USE - Composition used for separating and purifying                carbon dioxide from combustion exhaust gas                generated in thermal power plant, steel plant,                cement and factory, and refining and separating                carbon dioxide from steam reforming gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has excellent diffusion                efficiency and emission efficiency than a                conventionally known material, high selectivity and                carbon dioxide absorption rate, can efficiently                absorb and separate a large amount of carbon                dioxide emitted in a large-scale thermal power                generation, and suppressed risk of accumulation of                solid material and blockage of pipes in the carbon                dioxide separation equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition comprises amine compound of                formula (I) or (II).
   R1-R3 = H, or 1-4C alkyl;
   R10-R14 = H, 1-4C alkyl, hydroxy,                hydroxymethyl, 2-hydroxyethyl, or 1-4C                alkoxy;
   a,b = 0 or 1;
   a+b = 1;and
   R15 = H, 1-4C alkyl, methoxymethyl,                methoxyethoxymethyl, or 2-hydroxyethyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of carbon dioxide, which involves                contacting a gas containing carbon dioxide with the                composition to absorb carbon dioxide in the mixed                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition                  comprises a mixture of the compound (I) and                  compound (II). The compound (I) is preferably                  chosen from 15 compounds of formula e.g.                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-piperazine,                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-4-methylpiperazine,                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-4-ethylpiperazine,                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-4-propylpiperazine, and                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-4-butylpiperazine. The                  amine compound (II) comprises 1,4-diazabicyclo                  (2.2.2)octane-2-methanol. Preferred Composition:                  The mixing ratio of the amine compound (I) to the                  amine compound (II) is 0.1-99.9. The composition                  further comprises 20-95 wt.%, preferably 30-95                  wt.% water.
   EXAMPLE - 70 g Pure water to 30 g                1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-piperazine was added, mixed                and stirred to prepare 100 g carbon dioxide                absorption liquid in 200 mL gas absorption bottle,                measured in emission efficiency of carbon dioxide                gas, and precipitated due to the influence of                nitrogen oxides to obtain composition. The amount                of carbon dioxide absorbed (L) for 1 hour was 2.45                L in terms of standard conditions. The amount of                carbon dioxide absorbed (L) per 1 kg carbon dioxide                absorbing solution per hour was 24.5 L in terms of                standard conditions. The obtained composition had                emission efficiency of carbon dioxide gas was                0.51.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility, involves performing methane generation process by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility or carbon dioxide separated and recovered from exhaust gas to generate methane, and performing methane consumption process by using methane generated in methane generation process as alternative fuel for portion or all of fossil fuel to cement production facility, and continuously repeating methane generation process and methane consumption process.
   USE - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon dioxide originating from energy and enhances the effect of reducing greenhouse gases by continuously repeating the production of methane from carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas of cement manufacturing equipment and using it as fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide utilization system in cement manufacturing exhaust gas, comprising a methanation device for generating methane by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility or carbon dioxide separated and recovered from the exhaust gas, and a methane supply device for supplying methane generated by methanation device to cement production facility as alternative fuel for portion or all of fossil fuel, where the methanation device is connected to cement production facility to which methane is supplied by methane supply device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The methane supply device is connected to fuel supply line for cement firing kiln or calcination furnace in cement production facility. In methanation apparatus, the hydrogen is directly added to exhaust gas to produce methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methane consumption step process involves using methane as alternative fuel for at least either cement firing kiln or calcination furnace in cement production facility. The carbon dioxide is obtained by separating and recovering exhaust gas from cement production facility by contacting exhaust gas with carbon dioxide absorbent. The method involves directly adding hydrogen to exhaust gas to produce methane.

AU KOO C W
   LEE J Y
AE UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
GA 202189607Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a collection unit (110) for collecting cognitive ability task results performed on occupants in indoor environmental conditions to change carbon dioxide concentrations of different levels. A calculation unit (120) calculates an evaluation index for evaluating an effect of a change in the carbon dioxide concentration on the occupant's cognitive ability in the indoor environmental condition based on the cognitive ability task result and the EEG data. The calculation unit calculates a theta wavelength value measured from a frontal lobe of an occupant as the evaluation index.
   USE - Carbon dioxide concentration based indoor living environment evaluation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus performs the cognitive ability task results performed on the occupants under indoor environmental conditions for each carbon dioxide concentration and measures occupant neurological response during the task execution process so as to comprehensively analyze effects of changes in carbon dioxide concentrations on occupants' cognitive status, so that effect of changes of the indoor carbon dioxide can be examined based on occupants brain function and cognitive ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide concentration based indoor living environment evaluation device operating method.

GA 202170240D

The first amount of carbon dioxide reacts with the portion of water while in the open mine space to reduce the first amount of carbon dioxide to a second amount of carbon dioxide. GA 202164780K
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) comprises a deionized water inlet (100) into which deionized water is introduced, and a carbon dioxide inlet (200) into which a gas containing carbon dioxide is introduced. A deionized water flow passage is formed, through which the deionized water introduced from the deionized water inlet is passed.    CARBONREUSE FINLAND OY (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONREUSE FINLAND OY (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONRELLSE FINLAND OY (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202049216X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing gas                containing carbon dioxide. The pressurized gas is                supplied to the absorption. The carbon dioxide                contained in the pressurized gas is absorbed into                water in an absorption tank (0). The water produced                in the absorption is circulated into which water                carbon dioxide is absorbed from the absorption tank                to desorption. The carbon dioxide absorbed in water                is desorbed from water. The carbon dioxide desorbed                from water is recovered. The carbon dioxide                absorbed in water is desorbed from water by unit of                a first desorption tank (2) and a second desorption                tank (3). A lower pressure is applied in the second                desorption tank than in the first desorption                tank.
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide from                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy is saved and process control is                facilitated since the under-pressure pump is                designed for a much lower output. The fill block                column is not required to enhance the recovery                process. The carbon recovery rate of the process                can be improved than using an auxiliary adsorption                column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for recovering carbon dioxide from gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for recovering carbon dioxide from                gas.

   Desorption tank (1)
   First desorption tank (2)
   Second desorption tank (3)
   Pumping tank (4)

AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019830662
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a separator device which separates carbon dioxide from the mixed gas using a separator. A separator unit (120) includes a separator for collecting carbon dioxide. A supply line (110) into which gas is injected, a residual gas line (140) through which the residual gas of the separator is discharged, and a permeate gas line (130) through which the permeate gas of the separator is discharged are connected. A separator cooling device (150) cools the separator which is a polymer separator, a microporous separator, and a zeolite separator.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation system for separating carbon dioxide from mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The ratio of carbon dioxide contained in the permeate gas that has passed through the separation membrane is increased, by cooling the separation membrane to perform carbon dioxide separation, thus the separation efficiency is improved. Since the fixed membrane is cooled, cooling is easier than cooling the moving gas, and cooling is performed using a relatively inexpensive cooling device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of separating carbon dioxide from mixed gas using separator; and

PN CN113087953-A
TI Preparing breathable film for medical protective clothing, involves adding dried poly(lactic acid) pellets through main feed hopper of twin-screw extruder, adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, mixing.
AU LIU Z
   ZHANG G
AE HANGZHOU BENSONG NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOG (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202180139J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing breathable film for medical protective clothing involves providing poly(lactic acid) (PLA) resin raw material and supercritical gas mixed extrusion casting followed by adding dry PLA pellets through main feed hopper of twin-screw extruder, adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to twin-screw extrusion through metering pump in machine, where volume and dosage of carbon dioxide (scCO2) fluid is 5-10% of volume of PLA pellets, pressure of scCO2 fluid is 20-35 Megapascal, temperature of screw from barrel to die of extruder is 160-240 degrees C, mixing scCO2 fluid and PLA melt uniformly through shearing section, entering casting orifice through melt metering section; setting gas protection cover at die, introducing constant temperature and constant pressure nitrogen, where temperature is 40-70 degrees C, pressure is 5-10 atmospheres, forming membrane material holes in gas shield under constant temperature and pressure conditions.
   USE - Method for preparing breathable film for medical protective clothing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide high pore forming efficiency, more uniform pore diameter distribution, more regular pore shape etc., where more uniform pore size is conducive to control of transmittance of film, and more regular pore shape provides higher film strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing breathable film for medical protective clothing involves providing poly(lactic acid) (PLA) resin raw material and supercritical gas mixed extrusion casting followed by adding dry PLA pellets through main feed hopper of twin-screw extruder, adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to twin-screw extrusion through metering pump in machine, where volume and dosage of carbon dioxide (scCO2) fluid is 5-10% of volume of PLA pellets, pressure of scCO2 fluid is 20-35 Megapascal, temperature of screw from barrel to die of extruder is 160-240 degrees C, mixing scCO2 fluid and PLA melt uniformly through shearing section, entering casting orifice through melt metering section; setting gas protection cover at die, introducing constant temperature and constant pressure nitrogen, where temperature is 40-70 degrees C, pressure is 5-10 atmospheres, forming membrane material holes in gas shield under constant temperature and pressure conditions; entering film material in air knife for cooling immediately after passing through protective cover, and temperature of air cooling is 20-40 degrees C; providing film which enters setting roller station after passing through air knife, where roller temperature is 20-25 degrees C, and matching roller speed with extrusion rate to ensure film thickness of 40-60 mu m, cutting and winding, which becomes transparent and breathable membrane with certain pore size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The length of gas shield along length of membrane is 10-15 cm. The screw speed of twin-screw extruder is 150-200 rotation/minute. The average diameter of micropores of membrane is 50-80 mu m.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC A05-E02B; A11-A03; A11-A04; A11-A05C; A11-B07A; A11-B07D; A12-C02; A12-S06A; A12-S06B; A12-V00V; A12-W11A

PN WO2020183717-A1; CA3132259-A1; CN113574049-A; BR112021016382-A2; EP3938344-A1; IN202147045125-A; US2022185772-A1; JP2022522448-W
TI Producing urea by synthesizing urea from ammonia            and carbon dioxide to generate a urea synthesis            solution, heating generated urea synthesis solution,            decomposing ammonium carbamate and separating gaseous            mixture, and recycling liquid.
AU KEIGO S
   SASAKI K
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 202090879V
AB    NOVELTY - Urea is produced by synthesizing urea from                ammonia and carbon dioxide to generate a urea                synthesis solution; heating generated urea                synthesis solution, decomposing ammonium carbamate                and separating a gaseous mixture containing ammonia                and carbon dioxide from urea synthesis solution to                obtain a urea synthesis solution which is higher in                urea concentration than generated urea synthesis                solution; with use of a submerged condenser                including a shell and tube heat exchange structure                including a U-tube, absorbing and condensing                greater than or equal to 1 portion of gaseous                mixture obtained in the decomposition step in                absorption medium on a shell side, and generating                steam on a tube side with use of heat generated                during the condensation; recycling greater than or                equal to 1 portion of liquid that is obtained from                shell side in condensation step, to synthesis step;                and supplying water to the tube side of submerged                condenser at a mass flow rate that is greater than                or equal to 3 times of generation rate of steam                generated in the submerged condenser.
   USE - Production of urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing temperature                distribution in a submerged condenser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus                for urea production comprising a synthesis reactor                (A) which synthesizes urea from ammonia and carbon                dioxide to generate a urea synthesis solution; a                decomposer (B) which, by heating the urea synthesis                solution generated by the synthesis reactor,                decomposes ammonium carbamate and separating a                gaseous mixture containing ammonia and carbon                dioxide from urea synthesis solution, to obtain a                urea synthesis solution which is higher in urea                concentration than the urea synthesis solution                obtained by synthesis reactor; submerged condenser                (C) including a shell and tube heat exchange                structure containing a U-tube, and absorbs and                condenses greater than or equal to 1 portion of                gaseous mixture obtained by decomposer in an                absorption medium on a shell side, and generates                steam on tube side with use of heat generated                during the condensation; recycling unit for                recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of                liquid that is obtained from shell side of                submerged condenser, to the synthesis reactor; and                water supply unit for supplying water to tube side                of submerged condenser at a mass flow rate that is                greater than or equal to 3 times of a generation                rate of steam generated in the submerged                condenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic process flow                diagram of the apparatus for urea                production.
   Synthesis reactor (A)
   Decomposer (B)
   Submerged condenser (C)
   Ejector (D)
   Heat exchanger (E)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Flow speed of water                  supplied to the tube side of submerged condenser                  is greater than or equal to 0.3 m/s at inlet of                  U-tube. The mass flow rate of water supplied to                  tube side of submerged condenser is greater than                  or equal to 8 times of generation rate of the                  steam generated in the submerged condenser.                  Preferred Process: Water supply step includes                  pressurizing the water to be supplied to the tube                  side of the submerged condenser with use of a                  pump. Water supply step includes a gas liquid                  separation step of performing gas liquid                  separation of a fluid, where the fluid is                  obtained from tube side of submerged condenser,                  to obtain steam and water. Water supply step                  includes a step of supplying the water obtained                  in gas liquid separation step to the tube side of                  the submerged condenser. The synthesis step and                  condensation step are performed in a single                  pressure vessel. Preferred Devices: The submerged                  condenser is a vertical submerged condenser. The                  vertical submerged condenser has a tube sheet                  provided below the U-tube. The water supply unit                  includes a pump for pressurizing the water to be                  supplied to tube side of the submerged condenser;                  and gas liquid separator for performing gas                  liquid separation of a fluid, where the fluid is                  obtained from tube side of submerged condenser,                  to obtain steam and water. The water supply unit                  includes a unit for supplying the water obtained                  from gas liquid separator to tube side of the                  submerged condenser. The submerged condenser is a                  vertical submerged condenser. The vertical                  submerged condenser has a tubesheet provided                  below the U-tube. The apparatus comprises a                  single pressure vessel in which the synthesis                  reactor and submerged condenser are                  integrated.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. PN CN111514845-A; CN111514845-B
TI Preparation of adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves mixing clay with saturated sodium chloride solution and lithium chloride solution, heating, filtering, washing, unstripping impurities, acid-treating, and freeze-drying.
AU SONG Y
   LING Z
   SHI C
   ZHANG Y
   CHAI F
   ZHAO J
   YANG L
   LI Y
   ZENG Z
   CHEN S
   FU Y
   DENG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202078761B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of adsorbent involves mixing clay with saturated sodium chloride solution, heating, cooling, filtering, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol, mixing with lithium chloride solution, heating at 105-120 degrees C for 6-24 hours, cooling, filtering, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol to obtain sodium and lithium ions-intercalated clay, mixing with deionized water, ultrasonically treating in ice bath to exfoliate two-dimensional clay nanosheets to obtain mixture of two-dimensional clay nanosheets dispersion, unstripping clay particles and impurities, centrifuging to remove unstripped clay particles and impurities to obtain stable dispersion, freezing, freeze-drying to obtain two-dimensional clay nanosheets, acid-treating surface of clay nanosheets, mixing with 0.1-1 mol/L acid solution in mass-volume ratio of 4 g/2 L, stirring vigorously for 6-12 hours, taking out supernatant, freezing turbid liquid of lower layer, and freeze-drying.
   USE - Preparation of adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method simple, safe, has mild reaction conditions, and high production efficiency, and provides adsorbent with open pores, large specific surface area, and high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity increased by 118%. The method utilizes raw materials which are safe, stable, abundantly available, and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of adsorbent involves mixing clay with saturated sodium chloride solution, placing in hydrothermal reactor, heating at 105-120 degrees C, preferably 110 degrees C for 6-24 hours, cooling, filtering reaction product, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol, mixing with 1-3 mol/L lithium chloride solution, placing in hydrothermal reactor, heating at 105-120 degrees C, preferably 110 degrees C for 6-24 hours, cooling, filtering, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol to obtain sodium and lithium ions-intercalated clay, mixing with deionized water, ultrasonically treating in ice bath to exfoliate two-dimensional clay nanosheets to obtain mixture of two-dimensional clay nanosheets dispersion, unstripping clay particles and impurities, centrifuging to remove unstripped clay particles and impurities to obtain stable dispersion, freezing, freeze-drying to obtain two-dimensional clay nanosheets, acid-treating surface of clay nanosheets, mixing with 0.1-1 mol/L acid solution in mass-volume ratio of 4 g/2 L, stirring vigorously for 6-12 hours, taking out supernatant, freezing turbid liquid of lower layer, and freeze-drying. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the carbon dioxide adsorbent, which involves putting high-efficiency adsorbent into fixed bed reactor, vacuum degassing at 100 degrees C for 12 hours, and performing carbon dioxide adsorption at adsorption temperature of 25 degrees C and pressure of 0-50 bar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass-volume ratio of clay and saturated sodium chloride solution is 3 g/1 L. The volume ratio of deionized water to saturated sodium chloride solution is 4:5. Preferred Condition: The ultrasonic treatment is performed in a sophisticated ultrasonic instrument at an amplitude of 40% for 0.5-1.5 hours. Preferred Component: The clay is one or more of vermiculite, kaolin, montmorillonite, and illite. The acid solution is one or more of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid, and hydrofluoric acid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Low-temperature flue gas waste heat comprehensive utilization system, comprises first heater, cooling mixed filling machine, direct contact type flue gas heat exchanger, blowing device and blowing device exhaust pipe.
AU HAN S
   WANG X

AE TONGFANG ENERGY SAVING EQUIP CO LTD (TONG-C)

PN CN109850898-A
TI Salt mud recycling method of chlor-alkali industry comprises e.g. preparing waste sludge into slurry, filtering by magnetic separation, drying, calcining dried salt slurry in rotary kiln and adding with water to prepare slurry.
AU HU F
   YANG X
   LIU T
   XING J
   KONG Z
   FENG L
   ZHU F
   WANG F
   GAO J
   PAN Y
   WANG G
   LIU Y
   HU H
   GUO Z
AE HU F (HUFF-Individual)
   YANG X (YANG-Individual)
   LIU T (LIUT-Individual)
   XING J (XING-Individual)
   KONG Z (KONG-Individual)
   FENG L (FENG-Individual)
GA 201953355G
AB    NOVELTY - Salt mud recycling method of chlor-alkali industry comprises e.g. preparing waste sludge into a slurry, filtering by magnetic separation and drying, calcining dried salt slurry in a rotary kiln, adding with water to prepare a slurry, pouring salt slurry into enamel reactor, stirring, slowly adding concentrated nitric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid mixture to the reaction kettle, heating mixture, recycling produced carbon dioxide gas, filtering and washing the above solution to obtain white precipitate of barium sulfate and a filtered supernatant, drying barium sulfate and crushing by air flow to obtain ultrafine barium sulfate, transferring solution filtered into reactor, adding concentrated aqueous ammonia, stirring and reacting until pH of the solution rises, discarding precipitate by filtration, returning filtered supernatant to the reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting, filtering, drying and jet milling to obtain ultrafine calcium carbonate and concentrating filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for salt mud recycling method of chlor-alkali industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves recovery rate and environmental protection degree of salt and alkali resource utilization method in chlor-alkali industry by using mixed acid to dissolve and adding carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Salt mud recycling method of chlor-alkali industry comprises (i) preparing waste sludge into a slurry, filtering by magnetic separation and drying; (ii) calcining dried salt slurry in a rotary kiln, adding with water to prepare a slurry of 3:1-4:1; (iii) pouring salt slurry into enamel reactor, stirring, slowly adding concentrated nitric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid mixture to the reaction kettle, and heating mixture to 50 degrees C for 2 hours, recycling produced carbon dioxide gas, where ratio of mixed solution of concentrated nitric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid is 2-3:1 and concentrated nitric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid mixed solution is added in an amount of 0.4-0.6 times the mass of the salt slurry; (iv) filtering and washing the above solution to obtain white precipitate of barium sulfate and a filtered supernatant; (v) drying barium sulfate and crushing by air flow to obtain ultrafine barium sulfate; (vi) transferring solution filtered in step (iv) into reactor, adding concentrated aqueous ammonia, stirring and reacting until pH of the solution rises to 9-10 and discarding precipitate by filtration; (vii) returning filtered supernatant to the reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting for 1-2 hours, filtering, drying and jet milling to obtain ultrafine calcium carbonate; and (viii) concentrating filtrate in step (vii) and drying to obtain nitrogen fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) preparing slurry by adding 5-10 times of mass of water to the sludge and pulping for 20 hours in a beater at 80-100 revolutions/minute. In the step (i) using filter screen with 300-400 mesh. In the step (ii) temperature of rotary kiln is 800-900 degrees C and calcination time is 40-60 minutes. In the step (iii) stirring rate of enamel reaction kettle is 2200-2500 revolutions/minute. In the step (vii) using carbon dioxide derived from carbon dioxide recovered in the step (iii). Preferred Components: The concentration of concentrated nitric acid is 90% and concentration of concentrated sulfuric acid is 95%.

PN CN109574057-A; CN109574057-B
TI Preparing calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. calcining limestone, adding to mixture of acetone and water, soaking, passing through lime kiln flue gas, adding into supercritical reaction device, passing carbon dioxide, drying and pulverizing.
AU GU D
AE JIANGYOU WANSHAN MINING CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201933513R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate, comprises (i) calcining limestone, adding calcined product to mixture of acetone and water, adding into supercritical device, soaking in a supercritical acetone-water system to obtain a milk of lime, (ii) passing through a lime kiln flue gas with a flow rate of 0.5-1.5 m3/min and washing with 30-50% carbon dioxide for 6-12 hours to obtain a crude calcium carbonate slurry, where in the process of carbonization at the same time, introducing nitrogen gas at 70-80 degrees C into the lime milk at a rate of 200-300 ml/min, and (iii) adding 50-100 pts. wt. crude calcium carbonate slurry into the supercritical reaction device, adding 3-10 pts. wt. crystal form controlling agent and 5-8 pts. wt. ethanol, and introducing carbon dioxide into the device after sealing at 5-8 MPa and temperature of 60-80 degrees C conditions for 5-8 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding into the vertical filter press pressure filtration, drying and pulverizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high dispersibility and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate, comprises (i) calcining limestone at 1000-1280 degrees C, adding 50-80 pts. wt. calcined product to 200-300 pts. wt. mixture of acetone and water, and adding into the supercritical device, soaking in a supercritical acetone-water system at 360-400 degrees C and a pressure of 15-25 MPa for 30-90 minutes to obtain a milk of lime, where the volume ratio of acetone to water in the supercritical acetone-water system is 3:1, (ii) passing through a lime kiln flue gas with a flow rate of 0.5-1.5 m3/min and washing with 30-50% carbon dioxide for 6-12 hours to obtain a crude calcium carbonate slurry, where in the process of carbonization at the same time, introducing nitrogen gas at 70-80 degrees C into the lime milk at a rate of 200-300 ml/min, and (iii) adding 50-100 pts. wt. crude calcium carbonate slurry into the supercritical reaction device, adding 3-10 pts. wt. crystal form controlling agent and 5-8 pts. wt. ethanol, and introducing carbon dioxide into the device after sealing at 5-8 MPa and temperature of 60-80 degrees C conditions for 5-8 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding into the vertical filter press pressure filtration, drying and pulverizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form controlling agent comprises 3-5 pts. wt., preferably 4 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid, 1.5-2.5 pts. wt. , preferably 2 pts. wt. triethanolamine, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. , preferably 0.2 pts. wt. potassium hydrogen phthalate, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. , preferably 0.4 pts. wt. glycolic acid, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. , preferably 0.2 pts. wt. 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole lactic acid, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. , preferably 0.4 pts. wt. sodium salicylate and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. , preferably 0.2 pts. wt. polydimethyldiallyl ammonium chloride. The weight ratio of the octaaminophenyl silsesquioxane to calcium carbonate is 1-3:100. The mass ratio of the isooctyl bis-caphoacetate di-n-octyltin to calcium carbonate is 0.5-1.5:100. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), subjecting the obtained calcium carbonate to the following treatment process placing the calcium carbonate in a low-temperature plasma treatment machine for 10-15 minutes, adding into a mixer, and adding octaaminophenyl cage, stirring silsesquioxane and isooctyl bis-decyl acetate uniformly, placing in a 60Co gamma radiation field for irradiation, where the absorption dose rate of 130-150 Gy/min is selected at the placement position, and absorbed dose is 100-200kGy. The atmosphere of the low-temperature plasma processor is argon or nitrogen. The low-temperature plasma processor has a frequency of 40-70 kHz, a power of 35-80 W, and a pressure of 30-80 Pa. The method further comprises modification process of the calcium carbonate after the irradiation treatment adding irradiated calcium carbonate and butyl acrylate in the supercritical reaction device allyl thiourea, diallyldimethylammonium chloride, ammonium perchlorate, water and stirring uniformly, and sealing system and passing, reacting the carbon dioxide at 25-35 MPa and at a temperature of 60-75 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and releasing pressure, and precipitating with ethanol, drying and removing the homopolymer produced by the reaction by washing with methyl ethyl ketone. In the step (i), the heating process used for calcination is heating to 600-800 degrees C at a temperature of 5-10 degrees C/min for 30 minutes, and continuing to increase the temperature to 800-1000 degrees C at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/min for 1-2 hours, heating to 1100-1280 degrees C at 1-2 degrees C/min for 1-3 hours.

PN CN108394878-A; CN108394878-B
TI Purifying helium gas using hydrogen-containing            helium tail gas by compressing helium tail gas into            methanation unit through compression unit, and            supplementing carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide            under action of methanation catalyst.
AU ZHANG W
   LIU T
   LI G
AE XIAN BAOAILUO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (XIAN-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU ZEJIA HUAFEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201865624K
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of helium gas using                hydrogen-containing helium tail gas involves: (1)                compressing the hydrogen-containing helium tail gas                into a methanation unit through a compression unit,                supplementing carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                to the content of hydrogen in the helium tail gas                under the action of a methanation catalyst and                reacting hydrogen with carbon monoxide and/or                carbon dioxide to generate methane; (2) separating                the condensed water by cooling, introducing the                methanation reaction gas after the methanation unit                into a gas separation unit (A) according to the                content of helium in the methanation reaction gas                and adopting a gas separation technology (A) to                separate helium and hydrogen from the methanation                reaction gas with other components; and (3)                introducing the separated gas after the gas                separation unit (A) into a catalytic oxidation                dehydrogenation unit, and supplementing the excess                oxygen according to the amount of hydrogen                contained in the separated gas.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying helium gas                using hydrogen-containing helium tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of simply and                economically purifying the helium gas using                hydrogen-containing helium tail gas with high                helium recovery rate and reduced carbon discharge                in an environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of helium gas using                hydrogen-containing helium tail gas involves: (1)                compressing the hydrogen-containing helium tail gas                into a methanation unit through a compression unit,                supplementing carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                to the content of hydrogen in the                hydrogen-containing helium tail gas under the                action of a methanation catalyst, and reacting                hydrogen with carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                to generate methane; (2) separating the condensed                water by cooling, introducing the methanation                reaction gas after the methanation unit into a gas                separation unit (A) according to the content of                helium in the methanation reaction gas, and                adopting a gas separation technology (A) to                separate helium and hydrogen from the methanation                reaction gas with other components; (3) introducing                the separated gas after the gas separation unit (A)                into a catalytic oxidation dehydrogenation unit,                supplementing the excess oxygen according to the                amount of hydrogen contained in the separated gas                under the action of catalytic dehydrogenation                catalyst, and reacting hydrogen and oxygen in the                separated gas to generate water; and (4) cooling                the mixture obtained in step (3) to enter into a                gas separation unit (B), separating the helium gas                from other trace components by adopting a gas                separation technology (B) to obtain high-purity                helium, returning the desorbed gas and mixing with                the hydrogen-containing helium tail gas for                recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The gas separation unit                  (A) includes a pressure swing adsorption unit (A)                  and a membrane separation unit. The gas                  separation unit (B) includes a pressure swing                  adsorption unit (B). The pressure swing                  adsorption units (A) and (B) are composed of                  adsorption towers equipped with an adsorbent. The                  membrane separation unit is composed of a single                  membrane or membranes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The gas separation                  technology (A) includes a pressure swing                  adsorption technique (A) and a membrane                  separation technique. The gas separation                  technology (B) includes a pressure swing                  adsorption technique (B).

PT P
PN CN108219955-A; CN108219955-B
TI Preparing fructus cannabis oil useful in            cosmetics, comprises e.g. pre-treating hemp seed by            drying, pulverizing and sieving hemp seed to obtain            hemp seed powder, extracting in presence of            carbon-dioxide, decolorizing and deacidification.
AU WEI P
   YOU X
   ZHANG Y
   SUN J
   HUANG C
   LI M
   WANG Y
   ZHOU K
   XIE X
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG NATIONALITY AUTONOMOUS RE (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ZHUANG NATIONALITY AUTONOMOUS RE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201853345A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fructus cannabis oil, comprises (i)                pre-treating hemp seed by drying, pulverizing and                sieving the hemp seed to obtain hemp seed powder;                (ii) extracting in presence of carbon-dioxide,                turning on a heating switch to adjust the set                temperature value, where the extraction pressure is                25-45 MPa and increasing the pressure to a                specified value, opening a collection bottle valve,                adjusting the carbon-dioxide flow rate to 4-8                l/minute/hour; (iii) decolorizing extracted sesame                oil with activated clay, stirring, filtering after                decolorization and collecting the filtrate; (iv)                adding alkaline clay to the decolorized mixture to                carry out deacidification, stirring, filtering and                collecting the filtrate, i.e. primary fructus                cannabis oil; and (v) heating to boil, heating                under high temperature steam not in contact with                the primary fructus cannabis oil, but directly                heating the distillation tower and condensing the                vapor produced while boiling primary fructus                cannabis oil using a condenser.
   USE - The fructus cannabis oil is useful in                cosmetics (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product with less                impurities, light color, high oil yield, natural                taste and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fructus cannabis oil, comprises (i)                pre-treating hemp seed by drying, pulverizing and                sieving the hemp seed to obtain hemp seed powder;                (ii) loading the hemp seed powder into an                extraction kettle, turning on a supercritical                cooling device, once the temperature drops below 5                degrees C, extracting in presence of carbon-dioxide                to 30-50 degrees C, turning on a heating switch to                adjust the set temperature value, where the                extraction pressure is 25-45 MPa and increasing the                pressure to a specified value, opening a collection                bottle valve, adjusting the carbon-dioxide flow                rate to 4-8 l/minute/hour and extraction time is                1-4 hours to obtain hot fructus cannabis oil; (iii)                decolorizing the extracted sesame oil with                activated clay, continuously stirring, filtering                after decolorization and collecting the filtrate;                (iv) adding alkaline clay to the decolorized                mixture to carry out deacidification, stirring                constantly, filtering after the end of                deacidification process and collecting the                filtrate, i.e. primary fructus cannabis oil; and                (v) placing the primary fructus cannabis oil after                deacidification into a distillation tower, heating                to boil, heating under high temperature steam not                in contact with the primary fructus cannabis oil,                but directly heating the distillation tower and                condensing the vapor produced while boiling primary                fructus cannabis oil using a condenser to obtain                purified product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hemp                  kernel is dried to a moisture content of 5-20%,                  and the drying temperature is 50-70 degrees C,                  sieved mesh has a mesh size of 10-60 mesh. In the                  step (ii), the extraction conditions are                  extraction temperature of 32-40 degrees C, an                  extraction pressure of 35-40 MPa, and                  carbon-dioxide flow rate is 6-8 LPM/hour and                  extraction time is 2-4 hours. In the step (iii),                  the decolorization time is 10-50 minutes, the                  decolorization temperature is 25-55 degrees C,                  and the activated clay added is 1-5% to the                  weight of the sesame oil. In the step (iv), the                  deacidification time is 20-200 minutes, the                  deacidification temperature is 25-60 degrees C,                  and the amount of alkaline clay added is 1-5% of                  the weight of the sesame oil. In the step (v),                  the temperature in the distillation column is                  100-120 degrees C and the pressure is 1-5 Pa. The                  process parameters includes in the pretreatment                  step of the hemp seed, the particle size of the                  hemp seed powder is 20 mesh, the water content of                  the hemp seed powder rate is 10%, in the                  supercritical extraction step under extraction                  pressure of 36.6 MPa, extraction temperature of                  39.32 degrees C, carbon-dioxide flow rate of 7.02                  LPM/hour, extraction time of 3.5 hour, in the                  decolorization step the decolorization time is 25                  minutes and decolorization temperature is 40                  degrees C, amount of activated clay used is 2                  wt.% of the hemp seed oil, in the deacidification                  step the deacidification time is 50 minutes and                  deacidification temperature is 45 degrees C, the                  amount of alkaline calcium-based clay is 2.8 wt.%                  of the hemp seed oil and in the purification                  step, the temperature in the distillation column                  is 105 degrees C and the pressure is 4 Pa. In the                  step (ii), the activated clay is subjected to                  treatment by taking citric acid and oxalic acid,                  uniformly mixing, adding to the activated clay,                  and mixing uniformly, placing in the environment                  of 50-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, rinsing under                  pH of 5-6, drying, pulverizing, where the weight                  ratio of citric acid, oxalic acid and activated                  clay is 1:8-10:13-18.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

   USE - The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst is                useful: for reducing carbon dioxide; and a carbon                dioxide reduction device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst:                exhibits high efficiency in water; utilizes the                porphyrin complex which is insoluble in water as it                contains only a small number of hydrophilic groups                in its structure and induces or promotes a carbon                dioxide reduction reaction by lowering the                activation energy for the reduction of carbon                dioxide; can be produced inexpensively and has an                equivalent or superior performance, as compared to                conventional electrodes using a noble metal.

   Either R = H, 1-12C hydrocarbyl, hydroxy,                amino, carboxy, sulfo, mercapto or formyl, where                the total number of hydroxy, amino, sulfo and                mercapto moieties that are contained in one                molecule of the porphyrin complex is less than or                equal to 10; or
   adjacent R's = optionally bound with each                other via a 1-12C hydrocarbon chain to form a                cyclic structure;
   M = a (2+n)-valent metal ion;
   n = greater than or equal to 0; and
   X = an n-valent anion.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) reducing carbon dioxide, comprising                bringing a carbon dioxide reduction electrode (202)                comprising the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst                into contact with an electrolyte solution (205),                and introducing carbon dioxide to the electrolyte                solution and reducing the thus introduced carbon                dioxide by the electrode; and

   EXAMPLE - In a 200-ml flask,                5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphyrin iron(III)                chloride was dissolved in chloroform and Ketjen                Black (RTM: Electro-conductive carbon black) was                added to it. Subsequently, the solvent was removed                using an evaporator, and the complex was allowed to                adsorb to Ketjen Black (RTM: Electro-conductive                carbon black).This Ketjen Black (RTM:                Electro-conductive carbon black) was added to a 2.5                wt.% Nafion (RTM: Perfluorinated resin) solution                and dispersed by ultrasonication, and the resulting                dispersion was spray-coated on a carbon paper to                give the carbon dioxide reduction electrode.

PT P
PN WO2021075382-A1; JP2021064549-A
TI Fuel cell system has fuel discharge piping that discharges fuel waste liquid containing carbon dioxide generated by fuel electrode, and includes carbon-dioxide removal portion to remove carbon dioxide.
AU TAKUYA T
   YUSUKE U
   TOSHIYUKI S
   MOTOO N
   ATSUSHI K
   AKIHIRO T
   TSUJIGUCHI T
   UEDA Y
   SAITO T
   NAKAI M
   KUBO A
   TAKAZATO A
AE JTEKT CORP (JTEK-C)
   UNIV KANAZAWA NAT CORP (UYZA-C)
   JTEKT CORP (JTEK-C)
   UNIV KANAZAWA (UYZA-C)
GA 202141706B
AB    NOVELTY - The fuel cell system (1) has a direct liquid-type fuel cell (7) having a fuel electrode (10) for generating a carbon dioxide when oxidizing a fuel, and an air electrode (20) to restore oxygen. A fuel discharge piping (61) discharges fuel waste liquid containing the carbon dioxide generated by the fuel electrode. The fuel discharge piping includes a carbon-dioxide removal portion (70) to remove the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Fuel cell system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is removed by carbon-dioxide removal portion provided in fuel discharge piping. Therefore, the carbon dioxide which arose by oxidation of fuel in fuel electrode is recovered, and the carbon dioxide emitted by the exterior of fuel cell system is suppressed. Since the amine absorber exhibits high absorption efficiency even if carbon dioxide is at low concentration, the carbon dioxide is removed efficiently. Since the carbon-dioxide removal portion holds a porous structure, the system is made into a comparatively simple structure. Since the carbon-dioxide removal portion includes moisture-content removal portion, the fall of removal efficiency of carbon dioxide with the moisture content is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the fuel cell system. (Drawing shows a non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN113187459-A; CN113187459-B
TI Carbon dioxide acid fracturing method for            reforming carbonate reservoirs in ultra-deep wells by            preparing ground equipment and construction materials,            injecting liquid carbon dioxide into wellbore, and            increasing injection displacement.
AU LUO P
   YANG F
   MENG L
   ZHANG J
   JIA W
   GUO X
   FU M
   SONG B
   YU Y
AE XINJIANG HENGZHOU JINGYE PETROLEUM TECHN (XINJ-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG HENGZHOU JINGYE PETROLEUM TECHN (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 202194655Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide acid fracturing method for                reforming carbonate reservoirs in ultra-deep wells                includes preparing ground equipment and                construction materials, running fracturing string                and lowering the downhole fracturing tool to                predetermined position in wellbore, installing                fracturing wellheads, connecting ground equipment                and conducting tests and rectifications until                construction requirements are met; injecting liquid                carbon dioxide into wellbore with small                displacement, gradually increasing injection                displacement to produce sufficient supercritical                carbon dioxide, using the super-penetrating power                of supercritical carbon dioxide to reduce the                fracture pressure of the reservoir rock, and                connecting more natural fractures in the reservoir;                and injecting high-viscosity fracturing fluid, i.e.                liquid carbon dioxide and high-viscosity acid                liquid into the well to form artificial main                cracks, and continuously injecting mixture of                liquid carbon dioxide and high-viscosity                acid.
   USE - The method is for carbon dioxide acid                fracturing for reforming carbonate reservoirs in                ultra-deep wells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively improve the                complexity of reservoir fractures, can increase the                volume of the cracks to mining requirements, can                further increase the effective range of artificial                cracks, and can effectively improve the extraction                efficiency of carbonate reservoirs oil and gas                resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide acid fracturing method for                reforming carbonate reservoirs in ultra-deep wells                comprises:
   (A) preparing ground equipment and                construction materials in accordance with                construction design, running fracturing string and                lowering the downhole fracturing tool to                predetermined position in wellbore, installing                fracturing wellheads, connecting ground equipment                and conducting tests and rectifications until                construction requirements are met;
   (B) injecting liquid carbon dioxide into                wellbore in advance with small displacement,                gradually increasing injection displacement to                produce sufficient supercritical carbon dioxide,                using the super-penetrating power of supercritical                carbon dioxide to reduce the fracture pressure of                the reservoir rock, and connecting more natural                fractures in the reservoir which is conducive to                the formation of more and more complicated                secondary cracks;
   (C) injecting high-viscosity fracturing fluid,                i.e. liquid carbon dioxide and high-viscosity acid                liquid into the well to form artificial main                cracks, and continuously injecting mixture of                liquid carbon dioxide and high-viscosity acid to                increase the length of main crack and form complex                cracks at the ends of the cracks;
   (D) pumping mixture of liquid carbon dioxide                and medium viscous acid into the well, continuously                injecting medium-viscosity fracturing fluid, i.e.                liquid carbon dioxide and medium mucous acid liquid                into the barrel, mixing thoroughly in the wellbore                to form complex fracture network around the main                fracture in the middle of the fracture;
   (E) continuously injecting low viscosity                fracturing fluid, i.e. liquid carbon dioxide and                low viscosity acid liquid into the barrel, mixing                thoroughly in the wellbore to create a complex                secondary fracture network around the main fracture                near the well; and
   (F) shutting the well after the slippery water                is in place, waiting for acid solution to fully                react with the reservoir, controlling the pressure,                releasing the spray, and switching to oil test and                gas test operation.
   The high viscous acid liquid, medium mucous                acid liquid and low viscous acid liquid are                composed of 20% hydrochloric acid, carbon dioxide                thickening drag reducing agent and atomizing                stabilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  high-viscosity acid solution, medium-viscosity                  acid solution, low-viscosity acid solution and                  total added amount of liquid carbon dioxide is                  0-0.5:0-0.5:0.1-0.5:1. The high-viscosity                  fracturing fluid comprises liquid carbon dioxide                  and high-viscosity acid fluid. The high-viscosity                  acid fluid comprises hydrochloric acid, 2-2.5                  vol.% liquid carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide                  thickening and drag reducing agent, and 1 vol.%                  liquid carbon dioxide atomization stabilizer. The                  medium viscous fracturing fluid comprises liquid                  carbon dioxide and medium viscous acid fluid                  comprises hydrochloric acid, 1-1.5 vol.% liquid                  carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide thickening and                  drag reducing agent, and 1 vol.% liquid carbon                  dioxide atomization stabilizer. The low-viscosity                  fracturing fluid comprises liquid carbon dioxide                  and low-viscosity acid fluid. The low-viscosity                  acid fluid comprises hydrochloric acid, carbon                  dioxide thickening and drag reducing agent, 0.6-1                  vol.% liquid carbon dioxide, and 1 vol.% liquid                  carbon dioxide atomization stabilizer. The                  concentration of hydrochloric acid is 20%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  thickening and drag reducing agent is siloxane                  polymer. The atomization stabilizer is composite                  mixture of surfactant and polymer.

PN JP2020033284-A; JP7014690-B2
TI Methane production system has current measurement device that is installed between power generation device and water electric field device, and methanation reactor which is connected to carbon dioxide supply device.
AU INAGAKI R
   MUKAIDE M
   SATO K
   KANNO S
   SASAKI T
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2020193013
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a water electric field device that performs a water electric field based on the electric power supplied from a renewable energy power generation device. A carbon dioxide supply device supplies carbon dioxide. A methanation reactor produces methane from hydrogen generated by the water electric field device and carbon dioxide supplied from carbon dioxide supply device. A current measurement device is installed between the power generation device and the water electric field device. The carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide supply device is supplied as a purge gas to methanation reactor according to the state of electricity supplied from the power generation device. The methanation reactor is connected to the carbon dioxide supply device, and the purge gas is supplied to the carbon dioxide supply device. The methanation reactor performs catalytic process using a solid catalyst and a biomethanation process using methane-producing bacteria.
   USE - Methane production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane production system is excellent in controllability corresponding to fluctuating gas supply.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen flow detection device is provided between water electrolysis device and methanation reactor. The hydrogen flow detection device compares the hydrogen flow value at the time of steady operation with the current hydrogen flow value, and controls the carbon dioxide supply amount according to the measured hydrogen flow value. The methane concentration measuring device detects a methane concentration of methane discharged from the methanation reactor. The methane concentration measurement device measures the methane concentration at the time of the carbon dioxide purge operation, and supplies the discharged methane to the carbon dioxide supply device in the previous period when the methane concentration falls below a predetermined threshold value. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing methane, involves performing a water electric field based on the electric power supplied from the power generation device, supplying carbon dioxide, and producing methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The current is supplied according to the state of electricity supplied from the power generation device. The carbon dioxide is supplied as a purge gas to a methanation reaction step, the methanation reaction step and the carbon dioxide supply step are connected, and the purge gas is supplied to the carbon dioxide supply step. The methanation step is a catalytic method using a solid catalyst, and a bio-methanation method using a methane producer. The hydrogen flow rate detection step is provided between the water electrolysis step and the methanation reaction step. The hydrogen flow value during steady operation and the current hydrogen flow value are compared, and the carbon dioxide supply amount is controlled according to the measured hydrogen flow value in the hydrogen flow detection step. A methane concentration of methane discharged from the methanation reaction step is detected. The methane concentration during the carbon dioxide purge operation is measured. The discharged methane is supplied to the supply process when the methane concentration falls below a predetermined threshold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the methane production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

AB    NOVELTY - A self-immolative carbon-dioxide production body (1a) comprises support body (2), and self-immolative carbon-dioxide production layer (3) which is formed on support body and generates a carbon dioxide by the photocatalytic action of photocatalyst (51) including an organic substance binder, and photocatalyst dispersed to organic substance binder (52). The surface areas of radiation range of the actinic light of photocatalyst of self-immolative carbon-dioxide production layer constitute greater than or equal to 150% of the projection areas of radiation range. Carbon dioxide is emitted at normal temperature and normal pressure and greater than or equal to 1 nL/cm2.s per unit area of projection area to which actinic light is irradiated when the actinic light with respect to photocatalyst is irradiated by intensity of 0.8 mJ/cm2.s.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is a metal oxide semiconductor containing greater than or equal to 1 selected from titanium oxide (TiO2), zinc oxide (ZnO), strontium titanium oxide (SrTiO3), tin oxide (SnO2) and tungsten oxide (WO3) which may have a co-catalyst. The co-catalyst is a substance containing greater than or equal to 1 of platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), copper(II), iron(III), gold (Au), silver (Ag), ruthenium (Ru) and nickel (Ni). The system is provided with light source which can excite the photocatalyst. The system has retention space in which the carbon dioxide generated from the self-immolative carbon-dioxide production body is made to retain. The carbon dioxide levels discharged from the retention space are higher than carbon dioxide levels in air of greater than or equal to 600 ppm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The self-immolative carbon dioxide generation layer contains a low-molecular-weight organic compound having a polar group and/or bond having an affinity with photocatalyst. The side of the carbon dioxide collection well is provided with a carbon dioxide delivery pipe (6).    (A) dividing VD refining process through                mixing period, high vacuum decarburization period,                bottom blowing mixing period and molten steel                purifying period, blowing VD refining steel ladle                by carbon dioxide gas or carbon dioxide-argon mixed                gas through bottom nozzle at different stages,                selecting strong denitrification mode and weak                denitrification mode for smelting of molten steel                with different nitrogen contents, and ensuring VD                station steel liquid nitrogen at hydrogen contents                of less than 30 ppm and 1.5 ppm;
   (B) establishing bottom blowing flow real-time                monitoring curve, judging blocking condition of                bottom blowing device, selecting strong                denitrification mode or weak denitrification mode                in VD refining process according to different                nitrogen content of molten steel in VD process,                using small flow of bottom blowing carbon                dioxide-argon mixed gas during mixing of molten                steel components, and bottom blowing pure carbon                dioxide gas to decarburize; and
   (C) judging bottom blowing blocking condition,                ensuring normal stable operation of bottom blowing                device, using bottom blowing carbon dioxide-argon                mixed gas, promoting denitrification, continuously                reacting hydrogen and stirring. The strong                denitrification mode is for stationed molten steel                having nitrogen content of 60-90 ppm. The weak                denitrification mode is for stationed molten steel                having nitrogen content of 30-60 ppm.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining lake bottom elevation data and multiple drive data of lake water surface area (S1). Multiple groups of climate model data are obtained (S2). Climate model information is input into a flux block formula to calculate water-gas interface carbon dioxide flux. Carbon dioxide exchange amount between the water-water surface area and carbon dioxide of a water and gas interface is calculated (S3) according to the lake surface area and the water surface area to obtain an amount of carbon dioxide exchanged between the lake and an air-water interface.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing simulation and engraving of lake physical distribution type, comprehensively considering a lake water balance item through coupling of a climate mode, and quantitatively evaluating the exchange amount of lake water-gas interface carbon dioxide.    (1) A system for evaluating lake water-gas interface carbon dioxide exchange amount; and
   (2) A computer readable storage medium for storing a set of instructions for evaluating lake water-gas interface carbon dioxide exchange amount in land water ring and natural carbon water circulation by a computer device.

AB    NOVELTY - Green environmental protection type carbon                dioxide waste gas recycling equipment includes a                freezing box (1) with an air inlet pipe on the side                wall and a breeding shed (2). A dry ice compression                device (3) is fixedly installed on the inner side                wall of the freezer. The inner bottom surface of                the freezer is fixedly connected with an inclined                guide plate (4). The inner bottom surface of the                freezer is fixedly connected with a baffle plate                (5). The two discharge ports (6) are opened inside                the baffle plate. The two discharge ports are                connected with a dry ice ejection mechanism. The                two first connecting plates fixedly connected to                the inner wall of the top of the breeding shed                inside. A protective plate is fixedly connected to                the bottom of the two first connecting plates. A                tapered guide block is fixedly connected to the top                of the protective plate.
   USE - Green environmental protection type carbon                dioxide waste gas recycling equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The green environmental protection type carbon                dioxide waste gas recycling equipment compresses                and collects excessive industrial carbon dioxide                every time by using a dry ice compression device to                prevent the carbon dioxide continuously discharged                into the breeding greenhouse from being lost due to                incomplete use, and when carbon dioxide needs to be                added, use the push plate and push rod to cooperate                with the telescopic cylinder to move. The                compressed carbon dioxide is ejected into the                breeding shed, so that the carbon dioxide impacts                the cross rotating plate, prompting the protective                net cover to fall and docking with the receiving                net plate to protect the solid carbon dioxide. The                cross rotating plate moves the solid carbon dioxide                to rotate and continuously releases carbon dioxide                gas, and increases the concentration of carbon                dioxide gas in the cultivation greenhouse, which is                used for photosynthesis of plants.

PN CN107775862-A; CN107775862-B
TI Method for preparing green polyester foaming plate, involves subjecting polyester particles into extrusion process, followed by placed in high-pressure vessel, filling with carbon dioxide, and performing first cycle of saturation.
AU ZHAO L
   XIA T
   LIU T
   XI Z
   JIANG R
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201821007X
AB    NOVELTY - A green polyester foaming plate preparing method involves subjecting the polyester particles into extrusion process to prepare a polyester sheet. The polyester sheet is placed in a high-pressure vessel, filled with carbon dioxide, and performed first cycle of the polyester saturated with carbon dioxide, released the pressure and started the carbon dioxide saturation process for second cycle. The polyester sheet saturated with carbon dioxide is heated and foamed to obtain polyester foamed material, which is cooled, washed, dried, and subjected to isothermal annealing treatment.
   USE - Method for preparing green polyester foaming plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the green polyester foaming plate with high-efficiency, effectively shortening the time of saturated polyester solid-state foaming, increasing carbon dioxide concentration in the polyester matrix and improving foaming rate of polyester foaming material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A green polyester foaming plate preparing method involves subjecting the polyester particles into extrusion process and/or calendering to prepare a polyester sheet. The polyester sheet is placed in a high-pressure vessel, filled with carbon dioxide to saturation pressure, and performed first cycle of the polyester saturated with carbon dioxide, maintained the saturation temperature during the saturation of the entire polyester carbon dioxide, after completing the first saturation cycle and released the pressure in the high-pressure vessel to atmospheric pressure, recharged carbon dioxide to the saturation pressure and started the carbon dioxide saturation process of the polyester for second cycle and repeated the step including carbon dioxide saturation of the polyester-pressure relief until the carbon dioxide concentration in the polyester matrix reaches a certain value. The polyester sheet saturated with carbon dioxide is heated to the foaming temperature and foamed for a certain period of time to obtain polyester foamed material. The polyester foamed material is cooled in a water bath at room temperature to prevent the cells from growing, washed the polyester foamed material and performed drying treatment. The polyester foam material is subjected to isothermal annealing treatment with the protection of atmospheric nitrogen gas during the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyester particles comprise commercial recycled material, polyethylene terephthalate, polybutylene terephthalate and polytrimethylene terephthalate.

   ADVANTAGE - The carbonic anhydrase support increases conversion rate of carbon dioxide, prevents release and loss of carbonic anhydrase during operation, maintains activity, reduces initial facility investment and is economical. The system improves production rate of carbonate and limestone from carbon dioxide by using carbonic anhydrase supporting body, and reduces sulfurous acid gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing carbonic anhydrase support, comprising (a) culturing the whole cells to express and accumulate carbonic anhydrase in the cell gap, and (b) supporting the whole cells by mixing a prepolymer of hydrophilic polyurethane foam with the cultured whole cells;
   (2) capturing and converting carbon dioxide, comprising reacting by supplying carbon dioxide to the carbonic anhydrase support; and
   (3) carbon dioxide fixing and gypsum production system comprising a carbon dioxide absorption tank for generating carbonate by supplying carbon dioxide and hydroxide salt to the carbon dioxide anhydrase support, a calcium extraction tank (100) for extracting calcium ions by supplying hydrochloric acid to industrial by-products, a limestone generation tank for generating limestone by reacting the generated carbonate with the extracted calcium ions, and a gypsum generating tank for generating gypsum by supplying exhaust gas containing sulfurous acid gas to the generated limestone.

The limestone generating tank further includes a pH measuring unit and adjusting the pH in the limestone generating tank to 8-11.
