PN EP3275981-A1; WO2018019507-A1; EP3275981-B1; ES2714924-T3; US2019270949-A1; US10494587-B2
TI Fractionating suspension from microalgal biomass or milk, comprises contacting suspension with solvent consisting in supercritical carbon dioxide, continuously recovering supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase and continuously separating.
AU BADENS E
   CRAMPON C
AE UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE CENT MARSEILLE (ECOL-Non-standard)
   UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE CENT MARSEILLE (ECOL-Non-standard)
   UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE CENT MARSEILLE (ECOL-Non-standard)
   UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   ECOLE CENT MARSEILLE (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 201808706R
AB    NOVELTY - Fractionation of suspension from microalgal biomass or milk, comprises: (a) contacting the suspension with solvent consisting in supercritical carbon dioxide in contactor, where the suspension and supercritical carbon dioxide being introduced in counter current mode; (b) continuously recovering a supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase exiting from the top of the contactor, after its contacting with suspension, where the supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase contains lipids extracted from suspension; and (c) continuously separating the neutral lipids and/or the compounds of interest.
   USE - The process is useful for fractionation of a suspension from microalgal biomass containing 0.5-200 gl-1 of microalgae or milk that is from animal or vegetal origin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: enables to fractionate large quantities of microalgal suspension; avoids disrupt the microalgal cells before fractionating and recovering the neutral lipids and the microalgal compounds of interest; and makes use of only one particular solvent, supercritical carbon dioxide, except water coming from the wet microalgae biomass itself, for extracting lipids and compounds having a high added value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fractionation of a suspension from a microalgal biomass or milk, comprises: (a) contacting the suspension with a solvent consisting in supercritical carbon dioxide in a contactor, where the suspension and the supercritical carbon dioxide being introduced in a counter current mode; (b) continuously recovering a supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase exiting from the top of the contactor, after its contacting with the suspension, where the supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase contains lipids extracted from the suspension, and a raffinate exiting from the bottom of the contactor; and (c) continuously separating the neutral lipids and/or the compounds of interest contained in the supercritical carbon dioxide rich phase, and the raffinate and no step of disrupting the cells of the algal biomass is performed before step (a) is carried out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Process: The packing of the fractionation column is random packings or structured packings. The fractionation is carried out at 8-30 MPa and 35-70 degrees C. The process is carried out with the massic ratio (flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide)/ (flow rate of suspension) of 2/1-250/1. The separated supercritical carbon dioxide is recirculated in the fractionation column. Preferred Components: The contactor is a fractionation column, preferably packed fractionation column, or a settler-mixer. The flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide comprised of 3-20 (or 50 or 600) kg h-1 and the flow rate of suspension comprised of 0.08-1.5 (or 5 or 50) kg h-1. The contacting step (a) is performed in presence of a polar modifier and/or an esterification agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The neutral lipids are triglycerides, diglycerides, monoglycerides, and/or free fatty acids preferably omega-3 fatty acids. The compounds are antioxidants, carbohydrates or pigments.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN114868561-A
TI Energy-saving type passion fruit breeding device            used as vegetable, comprises carbon dioxide detector            for detecting carbon dioxide concentration in culturing            chamber, and illumination intensity detector for            detecting illumination intensity in culture            chamber.
AU LI Z
AE LI Z (LIZZ-Individual)
GA 2022A59613
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide (CO2) detector                for detecting the CO2 concentration in the                culturing chamber. An illumination intensity                detector for detecting the illumination intensity                in the culture chamber. A control processor data is                connected to the CO2 detector and the illumination                intensity detector, calculating the CO2 consumption                rate according to the CO2 concentration detected by                the CO2 detector. The control processor is capable                of comparing the CO2 consumption rate with a CO2                consumption rate preset value and determining a CO2                threshold interval corresponding to the CO2                consumption rate, so that the control processor                compares the illumination intensity with the                illumination intensity preset value based on the                determined CO2 threshold interval, and determines                the illumination intensity threshold interval. The                processor sends the shading instruction and/or CO2                supplement instruction according to the determined                illumination intensity threshold interval.
   USE - Energy-saving passion fruit seedling device                used as vegetable, feed, medicine, oil, and                preparing beverage, and can also be used in                greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the different periods of passion fruit                seedling for illumination are different, by carbon                dioxide (CO2) consumption rate can reasonably judge                the growth period of the passion fruit, so based on                different time to give passion fruit reasonable                illumination and according to passion fruit whether                to control the administration of CO2 fertilizer                according to the photosynthesis, can make passion                fruit have the most suitable growth environment                during the seedling period. By CO2 concentration                detection, supplementing the CO 2 fertilizer can be                more reasonable, avoiding CO2 generator                continuously working caused by the reduction of CO2                concentration caused by exhaust ventilation and so                on, saves the working energy consumption of the CO2                generator, at the same time, reduces CO2 emission                caused by ventilation of the existing device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                energy-saving type passion fruit breeding                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                current passion fruit seedling device.

TI Simplified synthesis system for producing methanol            and hydrocarbon, consists of gas-tight oven, catalytic            pressurized reaction chamber and separation units,            where reaction zones of carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide            generation, and methanol/hydrocarbon production takes            place at similar temperature.
AU KOHNE S
   KONRAD M
AE KS IPR UG (KSIP-Non-standard)
GA 202286075L
AB    NOVELTY - A simplified synthesis system comprises a                gas-tight oven for carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide                generation (9) from calcium carbonate and hydrogen,                a downstream catalytic pressurized reaction chamber                (25) for the synthesis of methanol from carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen, each                provided with a temperature monitor, a pressure                monitor and a controller, a separation/removal unit                for water, methanol and/or hydrocarbons, and a                removal unit for calcium oxide. The methanol and/or                hydrocarbon production takes place directly from                calcium carbonate and hydrogen and the reaction                zones of the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide                generation, and the methanol and/or hydrocarbon                production takes place at a similar temperature of                250-900 degrees C, which mutually support each                other in a reaction and control/regulation of the                system, via temperature and pressure, calcium                carbonate supply and hydrogen supply.
   USE - Simplified synthesis system used for producing                methanol and hydrocarbon from calcium carbonate and                hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system is economical and requires reduced                amount of external energy supply for the operation                such that methanol synthesis operation is also                possible during the phases in which electrical                energy is only available at high cost, since                methanol synthesis operation requires almost                reduced additional energy supply and energy largely                obtained from the conversion of hydrogen into                methanol. The system provides continuous supply of                the alkali metal carbonates and/or alkaline earth                metal carbonates and/or its salts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis                of direct production of methanol and/or                hydrocarbons from calcium carbonate and hydrogen,                which involves using the synthesis system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show a cross-sectional view of                the methanol synthesis system and possible variant                of the system.
   Air supply (1)
   Exhaust pipe (2)
   Carbon capture process (3)
   Natural source (5)
   Gas tight oven (9)
   Reaction chamber (25)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The system consists of                  a downstream reaction zone after the oven for                  carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide generation, in                  which the reduced starting material after the                  carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide release, such as                  calcium oxide is converted into hydroxide                  carbonates using water and the released heat is                  available to other processes by heat transfer                  system such as dryer for calcium carbonate, a                  delivery unit for increasing pressure of the gas                  flow arranged between the reaction stage of                  carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide generation and                  methanol production, and several hydrogen feeds.                  The system is connected to the separation unit                  for recovery of the methanol and/or the                  hydrocarbons, and/or the system works according                  to the distillation principle. The carbon                  monoxide/carbon dioxide is generated from another                  alkali and/or alkaline earth metal and/or other                  salts and hydrogen. The reaction to form methanol                  and/or hydrocarbons takes place in a multi-stage                  furnace/reactor. Water is separated between the                  two reaction zones of the carbon monoxide/carbon                  dioxide generation and methanol production. A                  residual gas from the separation unit of the                  reaction zone of the carbon monoxide/carbon                  dioxide generation is fed back. Electrolysis or                  reforming process generates hydrogen, and the                  electrolysis enables pressure charging of the                  system. Preferred Process: The carbonate such as                  calcium carbonate is made available from calcium                  hydroxide and carbon dioxide capturing process                  (3), which is generated in a conversion process                  by absorbing carbon dioxide. The alkali and/or                  alkaline earth carbonate such as potassium                  carbonate and/or sodium carbonate, is made                  available from the carbon dioxide capturing                  process, which is produced in a conversion                  process by absorbing carbon dioxide from                  hydroxide, passing the carbonate to the admixed                  calcium hydroxide in the water-dissolved phase                  and then precipitating as calcium carbonate and                  skimming and finally feeding to the carbon                  monoxide/carbon dioxide generation reaction zone.                  The carbonate as a carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide                  source, and the catalyst(s) for the methanol                  and/or hydrocarbon production, are mixed in the                  reaction zone, such that the reaction takes place                  in a reactor and removing from the reactor                  together after the reaction.

TI Doped cerium oxide particle for generating hydrogen gas, for converting carbon dioxide to methane, and for removing nitrous oxide from a feedstock, comprises cerium oxide and dopant, where the doped cerium oxide particle has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific surface area of specified range.
AU SMITH C
   ZULKIFLI N
   LE-ROUX P
   DING J
AE LYNAS RARE EARTHS LTD (LYNA-Non-standard)
   LYNAS RARE EARTHS LTD (LYNA-Non-standard)
GA 2022729841
AB    NOVELTY - Doped cerium oxide particle comprises 90-99.9 wt.% cerium oxide (CeO2) and 10 wt.% dopant, where the doped cerium oxide particle has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of more than 150 m2/g after calcination at 500degrees Celsius for 8 hours. The doped cerium oxide particle has an oxygen storage capacity (OSC) of more than 900 millimol oxygen (O2)/g after calcination at 500degrees Celsius for 8 hours.
   USE - Doped cerium oxide particle used for generating hydrogen gas, for converting carbon dioxide to methane, and for removing nitrous oxide from a feedstock (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The doped cerium oxide particles show improved properties in oxygen mobility, particularly as compared to ceria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. a method of generating hydrogen gas (H2), which involves contacting the doped cerium oxide with a precursor feedstock;
   2. a method of converting carbon dioxide (CO2) to methane (CH4), which involves contacting the doped cerium oxide of with carbon dioxide;
   3. a method of removing nitrous oxide (N2O) from a feedstock, which involves contacting the doped cerium oxide with a feedstock including nitric acid (HNO3) and/or adipic acid ((CH2)4(COOH)2); and
   4. a method for generating a doped cerium oxide particle, which involves adding dopant to a cerium trinitrate (Ce(NO3)4) solution, the Ce(NO3)4 solution having a rare earth oxide concentration of between 10 grams and 50 grams CeO2 per liter solvent, adding the at least one dopant, heating the Ce(NO3)4 solution with dopant to cause hydrolysis, cooling a resulting mixture, neutralizing the resulting mixture to a pH between 8 and 9, filtrating the neutralized mixture to generate a filtrate and a permeate, calcinating the filtrate, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dopant comprises silicon dioxide (SiO2) powder, silicon dioxide (SiO2) gel, tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS), white carbon black, aluminum oxide (Al2O3) powder, lanthanum oxide (La2O3) or a zeolite.

TI Method for estimating emission ratio coefficient of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, involves determining area to be analyzed and time to be analyzed, and obtaining meteorological reanalysis data and gas data of to-be-analyzed area at to-be-analyzed moment.
AU WEI C
   JIN J
   GU Q
   HUANG Y
   WANG M
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 202268718Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the area to be analyzed and the time to be analyzed. The meteorological reanalysis data and gas data of the to-be-analyzed area are obtained at the to-be-analyzed moment. The carbon monoxide (CO) assimilation grid flux data set, carbon dioxide (CO2) assimilation grid flux data set, and CO prediction area are obtained at the time to be analyzed by a preset acquisition method Flux and CO2 predicted regional fluxes based on the meteorological reanalysis data and gas data at the time to be analyzed in the to-be-analyzed area. The CO and CO2 regional emission ratio coefficients are obtained based on the CO predicted regional flux and the CO2 predicted regional flux.
   USE - Method for estimating emission ratio coefficient of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide by using terminal (claimed) used in energy source combustion and conversion. Can also be used in atmospheric chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - The emission ratio coefficient of CO and CO2 estimating method obtains the proportional coefficients of CO2 and CO with spatiotemporal characteristics in different countries, provinces, cities, and regions around the world and the obtained proportional coefficients in different regions are comparable. The initial boundary field data and CO2 prediction gas initial-boundary field data of the CO prediction gas are obtained at the next moment through the atmospheric chemical model which improves the accuracy while obtaining the flux data.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for estimating emission ratio coefficient of CO and CO;
   (2) a storage medium stored with a computer program for estimating the emission ratio coefficient of CO and CO.

A first sealing component (8) and a second sealing component (9) are filled between the inner wall of the box and the outer wall of the connecting rod. The first sealing component and the second sealing component are arranged at intervals. A balance cavity (10) is formed between the two. The balance cavity is connected to the liquid inlet of a pump (3) via a pipeline (11). The pipeline is provided with a converter for medium and lubricating oil. The outer wall of the connecting rod participating in the formation of the balance cavity is provided with a balance surface (20) for the hydraulic balance of the plunger.
   USE - Reciprocating plunger pump for densely conveying liquid carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The power end structure is simplified under the premise of not gasifying the liquid carbon dioxide when using, and it is convenient to disassemble and maintain. The hydraulic balance cavity is set on the power end of the reciprocating pump and the hydraulic balance cavities are input the lubricating oil converted by the medium and the lubrication oil converter, so that the liquid CO2 will not be gasified because of heat production when conveying, but also conveyed under the condition of 100 percent of the pressurizing parameter value. The liquidCO2 is conveyed in the dense state. The balance surface of the connecting rod is provided with a balance surface for hydraulic balance of the plunger.

GA 202120147E
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a flow rate control unit (106) for introducing a first gas into a flow path from the outside. A supply control unit (102) adjusts a supply amount of carbon dioxide supplied to the flow path. An injection port is connected to a flow path portion (104), and spaced apart from the object to provide second gas mixed with the first gas and carbon dioxide supplied to the flow path portion to the outside. A distance sensing unit obtains a distance indication value related to a distance between the injection hole and the object. A control unit (103) controls the flow rate adjusting unit to adjust the amount of the first gas introduced into the flow path unit, and controls the supply adjusting unit to adjust the supply amount. The control unit controls the supply control unit to supply carbon dioxide to the flow path, when the distance indication value obtained through the distance detection unit is greater than a first reference value and less than a second reference value.

   ADVANTAGE - The injection port is fixed at the adjusted position, so that the sleep induction device provides a constant sleep induction environment composition. The                double facade arrangement has a store for                electrical energy such as battery in order to                enable the use of electrical energy for the drive                or control of such elements also at night and when                there is little solar radiation.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets by supercritical stripping, comprises taking compressed carbon dioxide to reaches supercritical state in high-pressure airtight reactor, embedding carbon dioxide molecules in two-dimensional carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride material in the form of liquid molecules for supercritical stripping, stripping carbon nitride or phosphide carbon nitride material into sheets, and performing ultrasonic-assisted peeling, and peeling off the sheet-form carbon nitride or carbon phosphide nitride into ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or carbon phosphide nano-sheets.
   USE - The ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets are useful: for preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional doped carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets; and as photocatalyst in photocatalytic water splitting reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets with high-efficiency photocatalytic water splitting; adopts supercritical stripping and re-doping method to modify existing carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride; exposes more active sites and provides more channels for electron transport after stripping and doping to ensures photocatalytic activity of carbon nitride; utilizes compressed carbon dioxide gas, which is environmentally friendly, requires simple exhaust operation, and does not requires additional treatment; can complete stripping in a small reactor and perform batch supercritical stripping in medium-sized reactor; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing ultra-thin two-dimensional doped carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets by supercritical peeling, comprising doping ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets with single atom; and
   (2) photocatalytic water splitting reaction, comprising mixing photocatalyst with distilled water, triethanolamine and chloroplatinic acid in photocatalytic hydrogen production device, dispersing ultrasonically, then exhausting air with nitrogen, closing the device, and carrying out photocatalytic water splitting reaction under visible light irradiation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonic-assisted peeling is carried out in at least one mixed solvents of water, absolute ethanol and isopropanol, and ultrasonicating for 10-60 minutes. The doping method comprises dissolving ultra-thin two-dimensional carbon nitride or carbon phosphide carbon nitride nano-sheets and cobalt salt in acetonitrile solvent, and ultrasonicating for 2-10 minutes, dispersing the sample completely in acetonitrile, then adding triethylamine dropwise under uniform stirring for 0.5-2 hours, and transferring to microwave tube, performing microwave reaction for 1-4 hours, then collecting product by centrifugation, washing with chloroform, anhydrous methanol, and acetonitrile, and drying ultra-thin two-dimensional doped carbon nitride or phosphating carbon nitride nano-sheets.

PN KR2020032827-A; KR2177108-B1
TI Smart mask for e.g. controlling transmission of fine dust into respiratory tract of user, has exhalation discharge module installed on face cover to discharge carbon dioxide in user`s exhalation from face cover to outer side of face cover.
AU JAEJEONG G
   OH S H
AE PI-IND CO LTD (PIIN-Non-standard)
   LSTAR CO LTD (LSTA-Non-standard)
   TKLABS CO LTD (TKLA-Non-standard)
GA 202026428D
AB    NOVELTY - The mask (10) has a face cover (11) for covering and sealing a face portion of a user. An exhalation discharge module (12) is installed on the face cover to discharge carbon dioxide in user`s exhalation from the face cover to an outer side of the face cover. A fixing cap is detachably coupled to the exhalation discharge module and protruded through a fastening hole of the face cover to fix the exhalation discharge module to the face cover. An inner cover portion is coupled to an outer cover portion. A discharge fan is installed between the outer cover portion and the inner cover portion. A fan driving unit drives the discharge fan.
   USE - Smart mask for controlling transmission of harmful substances e.g. fine dust and yellow sand, into respiratory tract of a user, and viruses from people with severe acute respiratory diseases. Uses include but are not limited to industrial sites, leisure activities e.g. sports and mountain climbing activities, public institutions, shopping malls, buses and underground railways.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhalation discharge module is installed on the face cover to discharge carbon dioxide in user`s exhalation from the face cover to the outer side of the face cover so as to effectively prevent adverse effects caused by resorption of carbon dioxide, while enhancing cooling performance of the mask and comfort to the user. The exhalation discharge module is detachably installed using the fixing cap on the face cover, so that the face cover can be easily replaced to improve hygiene effects for the user. The exhalation discharge module is arranged within the face cover, so that the exhalation discharge module is not exposed to an external environment, thus improving aesthetic effects of the mask.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a smart mask.
   Smart mask (10)
   Face cover (11)
   Exhalation discharge module (12)

PN CN110756228-A
TI Multi-structure titanium dioxide/carbon metal            organic framework fiber film photo-catalytic material            useful as photocatalyst for catalytic reduction of            carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU LI J
   ZHOU A
   ZHAO C
   ZHOU J
   XIE Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2020138942
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-structure titanium dioxide/carbon metal                organic framework fiber film photo-catalytic                material is claimed. The metal organic framework is                grown in situ on the fibers of the titanium dioxide                nano-fiber membrane.
   USE - The photo-catalytic material is useful as                photocatalyst for catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photo-catalytic material: maintains                precursor titanium dioxide/polyacrylonitrile                self-supporting fiber membrane property and                original framework structure property; has large                specific surface area, and excellent photocatalytic                performance; is conducive to the capture of                reaction substrates and product diffusion; exhibits                synergistic effect; and improves the utilization of                sunlight (metal organic framework can capture                visible light).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the photo-catalytic material                comprising (i) dissolving tetravalent titanium salt                and polyacrylonitrile in N, N-dimethylformamide,                transferring to a syringe with a stainless steel                needle, electro-spinning on an electro-spinning                device using a tin foil as a collector to prepare                fiber membrane, peeling the obtained fiber membrane                from the tin foil, vacuum drying at room                temperature to obtain titanium                dioxide/polyacrylonitrile (TiO2/PAN) fiber                membrane, (ii) heating the obtained TiO2/PAN fiber                membrane at 260-800 degrees C at a heat rate of                1-15 degrees C/minute, and calcining for 0.5-6                hours to obtain titanium dioxide/carbon, (iii)                dissolving the metal zinc salt and                2-methylimidazole in water, stirring, placing it                into the titanium dioxide/carbon fiber membrane,                reacting under the reaction condition of 20-60 for                1-24 hours, taking out, washing, drying, growing                the zinc-metal organic framework on titanium                dioxide/carbon; and
   (2) use of photo-catalytic material as                photocatalyst for catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide comprising taking the                photo-catalytic material using micro-water Jinxing                surface wetting, and using a 300W xenon lamp as                light source for photocatalytic experiment, using                carbon dioxide as reaction atmosphere, and first                performing equilibrium under dark conditions for                one hour and performing light reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  concentration of PAN in the system is 1-20 wt.%,                  preferably 10 wt.%. The tetravalent titanium salt                  solution is preferably tetrabutyl titanate, the                  addition amount of tetrabutyl titanate in 20 ml                  of dimethylformamide is 1-5 ml, preferably 3 ml.                  The solution flow rate during spinning in step                  (i) is 0.1-1 ml/hour, and preferably 0.5 ml/hour.                  In step (iii), the mass ratio between zinc metal                  organic framework and titanium dioxide/carbon                  fiber membrane is 10:1-1:10, preferably 1:1. In                  step (iii), the concentration of zinc nitrate in                  the aqueous solution is 0.1-1 mmol/l, and the                  molar ratio to 2-methylimidazole is 5:1-1:1,                  preferably 3:1. Preferred Method: In step (ii),                  the calcination temperature is 500 degrees C, the                  calcination time is 2 hours, and the heating rate                  is 5 degrees C/minute.

PN US2020039912-A1; US10926247-B2
TI Catalyst system used to form alpha, beta unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid includes e.g. transition metal precursor having transition metal and first ligand, optionally second ligand, olefin, carbon dioxide and diluent.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 202010103A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst system comprises: (a) a transition metal precursor comprising a Group 8-11 transition metal and at least one first ligand; (b) optionally, at least one second ligand; (c) an olefin; (d) carbon dioxide; (e) a diluent; and (f) an oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin comprising a sulfonated polyaromatic resin, a phosphonated polyaromatic resin, a sulfinated polyaromatic resin, a thiosulfonated, or a thiosulfinated polyaromatic resin, and further comprises associated metal cations.
   USE - The catalyst systems are useful for forming an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salts, where the carboxylic acid comprises acrylic acid, methacrylic acid, 2-ethylacrylic acid, cinnamic acid or their salts (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst systems utilize the sulfonated or the phosphonated polyaromatic resin that results in high yields of the desired alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salts, comprising (a) contacting in any order (1) a transition metal precursor comprising a Group 8-11 transition metal and at least one first ligand, (2) optionally, at least one second ligand, (3) an olefin, (4) carbon dioxide, (5) a diluent and (6) an oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin comprising a sulfonated polyaromatic resin, a phosphonated polyaromatic resin, a sulfinated polyaromatic resin, a thiosulfonated, or a thiosulfinated polyaromatic resin, further comprising associated metal cations, to provide a reaction mixture, and (b) applying reaction conditions to the reaction mixture suitable to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salts; and
   (2) a catalyst system comprising a transition metal precursor comprising a Group 8-11 transition metal and the first ligand, optionally, the second ligand, the olefin, carbon dioxide, the diluent, and an oxoacid anion-substituted styrene-based polymer or copolymer comprising a sulfonated, a phosphonated, a sulfinated, a thiosulfonated or a thiosulfinated styrene polymer or styrene-divinyl arene copolymer, further comprising associated metal cations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diluent comprises an ether diluent, a carbonyl-containing diluent, an aromatic hydrocarbon diluent, a halogenated aromatic hydrocarbon diluent or an alcohol diluent. The olefin comprises ethylene, propylene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptane, octene or styrene. The reaction mixture comprises a metalalactone compound or an adduct of a metalalactone compound and the oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin, and the reaction conditions to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salts induces a metalalactone elimination reaction to produce the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salts. Preferred Process: The contacting step and/or the applying step is/are conducted at a pressure of 5 psig (34 KPa) to 10000 psig (68,948 KPa) and at 0-250 degrees C. The process further comprises regenerating the oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin and associated cations by contacting a by-product acid form of the oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin with a metal-containing base or a metal-containing salt. The olefin is ethylene and the step of contacting a transition metal precursor compound with the olefin and carbon dioxide is conducted using a constant addition of the olefin and carbon dioxide in an ethylene:carbon dioxide molar ratio of 3:1-1:3, to provide the reaction mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The catalyst system further comprises a metalalactone compound or an adduct of a metalalactone compound and the oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin. The oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin has (a) an average particle size of 0.1-1 mm and/or (b) an average pore diameter of 50-250 nm. The oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin is formed into a bead, supported onto an inert inorganic support, supported onto an inert organic support, or used in the absence of a support and the oxoacid anion-substituted polyaromatic resin is arranged as a fixed bed, a bubbling bed, a moving bed, or a stirred bed. The metal of the transition metal precursor compound is nickel, and the first ligand and the second ligand comprise diphosphine ligand, a diamine ligand, a diene ligand, a diether ligand, or dithioether ligand and the associated metal cations comprises a Group 1, 2, 12, or 13 metal.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110615895-A; CN110615895-B
TI New covalent triazine polymer useful as a catalyst in chemical conversion of carbon dioxide for preparing cyclic carbonate.
AU DENG W
   LI Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202001794C
AB    NOVELTY - Covalent triazine polymer (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as a catalyst in chemical conversion of carbon dioxide for preparing cyclic carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has high catalytic activity. The method: has easy separation and no metal loading; and does not require co-catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Covalent triazine polymer of formula (I) is new.
   R1-R12 = C or N;
   R13 = alkyl, phenyl and benzyl (which are optionally substituted), H, halo includes fluoro, bromo or chloro, methyl, ethyl, 2-chloroethyl, n-butyl, phenyl, benzyl or trifluoromethyl; and
   T = 1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl moeity of formula (A).
   Provided that: R1 and R2 are different, R3 and R4 are different, R5 and R6 are different, R8 and R9 are different and R11 and R12 are different. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) catalyst, comprising (I) as active ingredient; and
   (3) preparing cyclic carbonate, comprising either reacting (I) with an alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to obtain cyclic carbonate, or carrying out cyclic addition reaction of (I) under the catalysis of the catalyst to obtain cyclic carbonate, where the alkylene oxide is substituted oxirane compound of formula (III) and cyclic carbonate is 1,3-dioxolan-2-one compound of formula (IV), the alkylene oxide is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromhydrin, 1,2-epoxybutane, 1,2-epoxyhexane, phenylethylene oxide, glycidol, cyclohexene oxide, 1,2-epoxydodecane, 1,2-epoxy-5-hexene, glycidyl propargyl ether, 1,2,7,8-diepoxyoctane, isobutylene oxide, 1,2-epoxy-2-methylpropane, trans-2 3-epoxybutane, trans-stilbene oxide, allyl glycidyl ether, glycidyl phenyl ether, 2-fluoro-ethylene oxide (sic), 2,2-difluoroethylene oxide, or 1,1,1-trifluoro-2,3-epoxypropane, and the conditions for the cycloaddition reaction are: carbon dioxide pressure is 0.1-1 MPa, and the temperature is 25-130 degrees C, the mass ratio between (I) and alkylene oxide is 1:10-50.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises carrying out carrying out cyano-cyclization reaction of 2,5-dicyanopyridine as raw material to obtain trimer of triazine ring compound of formula (II) and then continuing cyano-cyclization reaction of (II) to obtain final product.R12-R62 = C or N.Provided that: R12-R22 are different, R32-R42 are different, and R52-R62 are different. Preferred Method: The method comprises heating 2,5-dicyanopyridine and catalyst under vacuum to a temperature of not less than 400 degrees C, preferably the 2,5-dicyanopyridine and the catalyst are evacuated under an inert atmosphere, where the reaction temperature is 350-650 degrees C, preferably 395-405 degrees C, the reaction time is 40-80 hours, the catalyst is zinc chloride, the mass ratio between catalyst and 2,5-dicyanopyridine is 5-12:1, preferably 9.5-10.5:1, or, washing and drying the reacted materials, preferably, the washing process is: first washing with water, then washing with an acid solution, then washing with water, and finally washing with tetrahydrofuran. The method comprises heating 2,5-dicyanopyridine and zinc chloride to a temperature of not less than 400 degrees C under a vacuum condition for a reaction to obtain (I), where the mass ratio between zinc chloride and 2,5-dicyanopyridine is 5-12:1, preferably 9.5-10.5:1, and (II) is 6,6',6''-(1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl)trinicotinonitrile, 6,6'-(6-(6-cyanopyridin-3-yl)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diyl)dinicotinonitrile, 5,5'-(6-(5-cyanopyridin-2-yl)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diyl)dipicolinonitrile and/or 5,5',5''-(1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl)tripicolinonitrile. Preferred Components: (I) has pores with a diameter of less than 2 nm. PN EP3502317-A1; JP2019112717-A; CA3028495-A1; US2019194816-A1; FR3075832-A1; JP6803903-B2; CA3028495-C; US11053599-B2; EP3502317-B1
TI Operating in stand-by mode or in stand-by mode of            unit comprises e.g. when it is desired to increase            temperature of N reactors or its fraction or when            available level of electricity is insufficient to            implement high temperature electrolysis.
AU BRUNOT A
   LACROIX V
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE & AUX ENE (COMS-C)
GA 201952573M
AB    NOVELTY - Operating in a stand-by mode or in a stand-by                mode of a unit, referred to as power-to-gas,                comprising a number N of reactors stacked with                elementary electrolysis cells of the solid oxide                type (SOEC) whose cathodes are made of methanation                reaction catalyst material (s), comprises, when it                is desired to increase the temperature of the N                reactors or its fraction, or when the available                level of electricity is insufficient to implement                high temperature electrolysis ( EHT) or a                co-electrolysis of water and carbon dioxide within                all N reactors as it is supplied electrically and                if necessary thermally a number P of reactors and,                is fed and distributed to the cathodes P reactors                or water vapor, a mixture of water vapor and carbon                dioxide, so as to achieve at each cathode P                reactors is a EHT of the water vapor, a high                temperature co-electrolysis of water vapor and                carbon dioxide, recovering at least a portion of                the gases resulting from the electrolysis                (hydrogen, water vapor) or co-electrolysis (water                vapor, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane)                with which is supplied and distributed to each                cathode of a number X of non-electrically powered                reactors, the number X being less than or equal to                NP, so as to achieve at each cathode X reactors                methanation by heterogeneous catalysis.
   USE - The method is useful for operating in a                stand-by mode or in a stand-by mode of a unit e.g.                as power-to-gas for producing methane from a                so-called intermittent energy source                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing methane from a so-called intermittent                energy source, implementing the method of stand-by                operation of the power-to-gas unit, the second is                implemented when the intermittent source is no                longer capable of producing the level of                electricity sufficient for all the N                reactors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is                  emitted by a production source, a production                  site, preferably from the sites of energy                  production (nuclear power plants for the fatal                  heat, the thermal power plants of biogas or                  gasification of biomass for fatal heat and/or                  carbon dioxide), industrial production sites                  (cement plants, iron and steel industry for fatal                  heat and/or carbon dioxide), tertiary buildings                  including hospitals, closed transport networks or                  disposal sites including waste heat treatment                  units. The method, where, in stand-by mode or                  start-up mode, the heat requirements of the unit,                  of which the reactors (SOEC), are provided by the                  fatal heat of a production source, a production                  site, comprising energy production sites (nuclear                  power plants for waste heat, thermal methanation                  or biomass gasification plants for fatal heat                  and/or carbon dioxide), industrial production                  sites (cement plants, iron and steel industry for                  fatal heat and/or carbon dioxide), tertiary                  buildings including hospitals, closed transport                  networks, and disposal sites such as waste heat                  treatment units. The method comprises the gases                  produced by methanation in the SOEC reactors are                  introduced into the N dedicated methanation                  reactors of the power unit to-gas. The second                  step is carried out at temperatures of 600-1000                  degrees C. The second step is carried out at                  temperatures of 400-800 degrees C, and at least                  100 degrees C lower than the temperature of first                  step. The first and second steps are carried out                  at pressures of 0-100 (preferably 4-80) bar. The                  N reactors are all stacking reactors of                  elementary electrolysis cells SOEC type each                  formed of a cathode, an anode and an electrolyte                  interposed between the cathode and the anode, and                  many electrical and fluidic interconnectors each                  arranged between two adjacent elementary cells                  with one of its faces in electrical contact with                  the anode of one of the two elementary cells and                  the other of its faces in electrical contact with                  the cathode of the other of the two elementary                  cells. The first step is carried out by supplying                  and distributing to each cathode P reactors or                  water vapor, a mixture of water vapor and carbon                  dioxide or supplying and distributing water vapor                  at each cathode of one of the two adjacent                  elementary cells of the P reactors and feeding                  and distributing carbon dioxide to the cathode of                  the other of the two elementary cells of the P                  cells reactors, so as to achieve at each cathode                  P reactors is an electrolysis at high                  temperatures of the water vapor, a high                  temperature co-electrolysis of water vapor and                  carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109536991-A
TI Preparation of loose porous cuprous oxide material            used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,            involves using cuprous iodide powder as raw material            and preparing complex solution in potassium iodide or            sodium iodide solution.
AU DU X
   ZOU C
   MAO J
   ZHENG W
   DONG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019309923
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of loose porous cuprous oxide                material involves synthesizing loose cuprous oxide                material by basic hydrolysis process using a                complex solution of cuprous iodide, washing                analytical pure cuprous iodide powder with dilute                acid aqueous solution, ultrasonically oscillating                for removing acid solution, washing with nitrogen                deoxygenated ethanol, and drying with nitrogen,                taking potassium iodide or sodium iodide solution,                adding to cuprous iodide powder, placing in an oven                for 4 plus minus 2 hours, complexing to obtain                cuprous iodide ion complexing solution, taking                strong alkali solution in a container according to                strong base and complex ion mass ratio of                (1:1)-(1:6), adding cuprous iodide ion complexing                solution to obtain large amount of cuprous orange                yellow precipitate, taking out the orange-yellow                precipitate of cuprous oxide, centrifuging and                washing with ultrapure water and acetone, and                drying the centrifuged solid by nitrogen.
   USE - Preparation of loose porous cuprous oxide                material used for electrocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of loose porous                cuprous oxide material which has high specific                surface area, high catalytic activity, reduces the                activation barrier of intermediate product coupling                reaction, promotes reactivity to multi-carbon                products, realizes electrocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide to ethylene, increases total Faraday                efficiency of multi-carbon products such as ethanol                and n-propanol to above 80%, by simple, safe and                quick process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of loose porous cuprous oxide material                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,                which involves using working electrode comprising                catalyst ink, weighing 10 mg loose porous cuprous                oxide powder, adding 1 mL methanol solution and 40                mu L 5% Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene                based fluoropolymer-copolymer) aqueous solution,                ultrasonically oscillating for 30 minutes to obtain                uniform catalyst ink, dripping glassy carbon                electrode with a diameter of 3 mm to 5 mu L                obtained catalyst ink and naturally drying to                obtain working electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  dilute acid aqueous solution is 0.1 mol/L. The                  purity of analytical pure cuprous iodide powder                  is greater than 99.5%. The concentration of                  potassium iodide or sodium iodide solution is 2.5                  and 8.0 mol/L. The strong alkali solution                  comprises 0.1-3.0 mol/L sodium hydroxide or                  potassium hydroxide. Preferred Conditions: The                  centrifuging speed after ultrapure water or                  acetone washing is 6000 rpm, and the centrifuging                  time is 3 minutes. The centrifuging speed after                  acetone washing is 12000 rpm and the centrifuging                  time is 5 minutes.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN CN108997539-A
TI Multi-scale halloysite aerogel material is obtained by placing multi-scale halloysite hydrogel in carbon dioxide supercritical high-pressure extraction device containing carbon dioxide as medium and supercritically drying.
AU LI H
   SONG L
   LIU H
   ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018A3573A
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-scale halloysite aerogel material is obtained by adding 0.8-18 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.07-1.8 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.45 pt. wt. initiator and 0.05-9 pts. wt. tetrabromobisphenol A to mixture containing 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring, adding obtained solution to halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuumizing, reducing pressure, vacuumizing, washing, dispersing resultant product in 100 pts. wt. water, increasing temperature of water bath to 52-78 degrees C, initiating polymerization for 48 hours or more, washing obtained product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, adding 0.07-18 pts. wt. tris(2,3-dibromo)propyl borate and 0.01-1.6 pts. wt. initiator, stirring, increasing temperature to 64-98 degrees C, polymerizing for 36 hours or more, obtaining multi-scale halloysite hydrogel, placing in carbon dioxide supercritical high-pressure extraction device containing carbon dioxide as medium and supercritically drying.
   USE - Multi-scale halloysite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The multi-scale halloysite aerogel material has excellent synergistic flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The multi-scale halloysite aerogel material has average specific surface area of 601-607 m2/g, and nanoscale and microscale pores. The nanoscale pores have average size of 10-12 nm. The microscale pores have average size of 20.2-21.7 mu m. The halloysite dispersion is obtained by adding 0.8-18 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to mixture containing 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and ultrasonically dispersing. Polydivinylsiloxane has number average molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, and is chosen from amino-terminated polydimethylene vinyl siloxane or amino double-terminated polydimethyldivinyl siloxane. The ratio of number of moles of vinyl group with respect to number of moles of amino group of monodimethyldivinylsiloxane is 0.2-4.6%. The supercritically drying process is carried out at 5-350 degrees C for 1 hour or more in the pressure of 0.5-30 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.

AU NAKAGAWA T
AE HIRAMA RIKA KENKYUSHO KK (HIRA-Non-standard)
   HIRAMA LAB CO LTD (HIRA-Non-standard)
   HIRAMA RIKA KENKYUSHO KK (HIRA-Non-standard)

PN CN114998075-A
TI Method for calculating road traffic transportation            carbon emission rate by using computing system,            involves determining traffic carbon dioxide emission            measurement model as optimal traffic carbon dioxide            emission measurement model when loss is minimum.
AU WANG C
AE UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
GA 2022B9574C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining carbon dioxide                past discharge data, greenhouse gas discharge data                and carbon dioxide discharge data. The greenhouse                air discharge data is pre-processed to form a data                set. The data set is divided into a test set and a                verification set. A traffic carbon dioxide emission                measurement model is established. The test set is                used to train the traffic carbon dioxide emission                measurement model. The verification set is utilized                to verify the traffic carbon dioxide emission                measurement model to calculate a corresponding                loss. The traffic carbon dioxide emission                measurement model is determined as an optimal                traffic carbon dioxide emission measurement model                when the loss is minimum. The transportation carbon                emission rate is calculated by adopting the optimal                traffic carbon dioxide emission measurement model                to obtain a calculation result.
   USE - Method for calculating a road traffic                transportation carbon emission rate by using a                computing system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the traffic                carbon dioxide emission measurement model as the                optimal traffic carbon emission measurement model                when the loss is minimum, thus accurately                calculating the carbon emission amount of the road                traffic, and hence providing basis for subsequent                carbon emission control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                computing system; (2) a computer-readable storage                medium comprising a set of instruction for                calculating road traffic transportation carbon                emission rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for calculating a road traffic                transportation carbon emission rate by using a                computing system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

GA 202274826N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing multi-branch horizontal well construction process. Multi-branch horizontal well drilling process is used for forming a main horizontal well along the coal bed bottom plate. Multiple branch wells are formed on the main horizontal well. The branch wells are extended to the coal layer. The coal bed gas is collected to the stratum pressure to reduce the waste pressure for replacing the production pipe column into the gas injection pipe column. The traversing packer is set in the main horizontal well. A permanent packer is set in the straight well. High pressure carbon dioxide is injected into the cavity through the gas injection pipe column. The carbon dioxide storage is finished when the carbon dioxide injection amount reaches the design value or the carbon dioxide pressure in the cavity reaches the set value to stop injecting gas.

PN WO2020249770-A1; GB2584704-A; EP3983111-A1
TI Method for treatment of input gas e.g. biogas, involves circulating carbon dioxide absorption solution through liquid side of gas-liquid contact stage to absorb carbon dioxide from intermediate gas to leave output gas flowing out.
AU MCADAM E
AE UNIV CRANFIELD (CRNA-C)
   UNIV CRANFIELD (CRNA-C)
GA 2020C71033
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a first gas-liquid contact stage. The first gas-liquid contact stage comprises gas-liquid hollow fiber membrane contactors permeable to carbon dioxide. The input gas is flowed through the gas side of the first gas-liquid contact stage. A second gas-liquid contact stage is provided. The second gas-liquid contact stage comprises gas-liquid hollow fiber membrane contactors permeable to carbon dioxide. The second carbon dioxide absorption solution is circulated through the liquid side of the second gas-liquid contact stage, to absorb carbon dioxide from the intermediate gas to leave an output gas flowing out of the second gas-liquid contact stage. The process is controlled so that more precipitation of ammonium bicarbonate occurs in the first carbon dioxide absorption solution, per unit volume of first carbon dioxide absorption solution, than in the second carbon dioxide absorption solution, per unit volume of second carbon dioxide absorption solution.
   USE - Method for treatment of input gas such as biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The recirculation of a chilled absorbent within hollow fiber membrane contactors (HFMCs) can reduce or prevent lumen-side blockage and enable consistent and reproducible shell-side nucleation and growth of ammonium bicarbonate through chemical carbon dioxide absorption from the gas phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas treatment system for treatment of input gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the method for treatment of input gas.

PT P
PN WO2020053619-A1
TI Biomass activated carbon as adsorbent for separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from gas having carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and inert component, have porous structure formed from fruit stones and impregnated with copper chloride.
AU PEVIDA GARCIA C
   RUBIERA GONZALEZ F
   ALVAREZ GUTIERREZ N
   REYES RODRIGUEZ J
AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 202021993A
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass activated carbon have a porous structure prepared from fruit stones and impregnated with copper chloride.
   USE - Biomass activated carbon as adsorbent for separation of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from a gas containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and/or inert components such as nitrogen and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The biomass activated carbon can be easily regenerated, and unlike other physical adsorbents such as zeolites or Metal Organic Framework adsorbents, it is hydrophobic and show high stability in humid conditions. It efficiently recovers both carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide from gas, especially from blast furnace gas. It is cheap, easily accessible and widely available.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing biomass activated carbon, which involves supplying particles of fruit stones, activating the particles to obtain activated particles of fruit stones; and mixing the activated particles of fruit stones with dissolved copper chloride, and drying and heat treating the mixture to obtain the desired product;
   (2) a method for separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from a gas containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and/or inert components such as nitrogen and hydrogen, which involves subjecting the gas to flow through a carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer comprising the biomass activated carbon; and
   (3) an apparatus for separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from gas containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and/or inert components such as nitrogen and hydrogen, which comprises one or more adsorption unit, where carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer/s comprising the biomass activated carbon is/are arranged in a single layered bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fruit stones comprises olive stones. Preferred Conditions: The particles of fruit stones have a size of 1-3 millimeters. The activation step is carried out in a single activation procedure and under carbon dioxide flow. The gas is first subjected to flow through carbon dioxide adsorbent layer and then subjected to flow through carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer. The carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer and the carbon dioxide adsorbent layer are arranged in a single layered bed. The height of the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer and the height of the carbon dioxide adsorbent layer in the single layered bad are substantially equal. The gas is subjected to flow through the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer, or the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide adsorbent layers, under a Vacuum Swing Separation method. The Vacuum Swing Separation method is a four-step Vacuum Swing Separation method, which involves performing pressurization with feed, adsorption, rinse and evacuation, or a five-step Vacuum Swing Separation method, which involves performing pressurization with feed, adsorption, rinse, evacuation and purge. The step of rinse is carried out with a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. The duration of step of rinse is less than the duration of step of evacuation. The gas is a blast furnace gas. The carbon dioxide adsorbent layer is arranged before the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide adsorbent layer according to the direction of the gas flow.Preferred process: The gas is further subjected to flow through a carbon dioxide adsorbent layer comprising a biomass activated carbon having a porous structure prepared from fruit stones. The Vacuum Swing Separation method is a three-step Vacuum Swing Separation method, which involves performing pressurization with feed, adsorption and evacuation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN KR2019067398-A
TI Method for estimating surface carbon dioxide fluxes with aircraft-measurement-based assimilation, involves pre-processing an aerial view data about the atmosphere and producing input data used in the data assimilation.
AU KIM H M
   KIM H J
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 2019525614
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-processing an aerial view data about the atmosphere and producing input data used in the data assimilation. The carbon dioxide model value is provided by using the observation operator about input data. The reversely is generated, which estimates the surface of the earth carbon dioxide flux using the atmosphere inverse modeling among the atmosphere from carbon dioxide concentration observation data. The aerial view data and the surface of the earth carbon dioxide flux includes the step of doing with the data assimilation by reflecting the observed value to the carbon dioxide analysis method.
   USE - Method for estimating surface carbon dioxide fluxes with aircraft-measurement-based assimilation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for estimating surface carbon dioxide fluxes reduces the uncertainty generated due to the error according to the transportation model and observation shortage by using the observation operator for aerial view data and generated input data as the data assimilation box and it improves the accuracy of the surface of the earth carbon flux estimation result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a computer-readable recording medium for performing a method for estimating surface carbon dioxide fluxes with aircraft-measurement-based assimilation; and
   (2) an apparatus for estimating the surface of the earth carbon dioxide flux.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method of the surface of the earth carbon dioxide flux accompanying the aerial view data assimilation. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN WO2018056787-A1; KR2018034281-A; AU2017330155-A1; CN109890969-A; US2019256882-A1; DE112017004802-T5; AU2017330155-B2
TI Converting carbon dioxide to useful substance e.g.            acetic acid, by producing metal-carbonate product and            sulfuric acid, culturing sulfated microorganism in            presence of metal-carbonate product and sulfur, and            recovering useful substance.
AU PARK W C
   CHO K K
   KIM T
   LA Y H
   RYU J
   LEE S M
   LA YEON H
   PARK J
   ZHAO G
   JIN T
   LUO L
   LEE S
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 201825649Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for converting carbon dioxide to useful                substance involves (a) performing carbon dioxide                mineralization process for producing                metal-carbonate product and sulfuric acid, (b)                culturing sulfated microorganism in presence of the                metal-carbonate product and sulfur to produce a                mixture of useful substance produced by the                sulfated microorganism and the metal sulfate, and                (c) recovering the useful substance and the metal                sulfate produced in step (b), and recycling the                recovered metal sulfate to the carbon dioxide                mineralization step of step (a).
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon                dioxide to useful substance chosen from                polyhydroxyalkanoate, ethanol, acetic acid, lactic                acid, glycerol, 3-hydroxypropionic acid,                isobutanol, isobutyric acid, succinic acid, butyric                acid, n-butanol, 1,3-propanediol, 2,3-butanediol,                1,4-butanediol, glutamate, isoprene, adipic acid,                muconic acid, amino acid, glutathione, polypeptide,                phospholipid, polyamine, s-adenosyl methionine, and                fatty acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts carbon dioxide into useful                substances without supply of additional energy                sources (light and electric energy) from outside,                and reuses metal ions necessary for carbon dioxide                mineralization reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for converting carbon dioxide to useful                substance involves (a) performing carbon dioxide                mineralization process for producing                metal-carbonate product and sulfuric acid by                reacting carbon dioxide with metal sulfates, (b)                culturing sulfated microorganism in presence of the                metal-carbonate product and sulfur to produce a                mixture of useful substance produced by the                sulfated microorganism and the metal sulfate, and                (c) recovering the useful substance and the metal                sulfate produced in step (b), and recycling the                recovered metal sulfate to the carbon dioxide                mineralization step of step (a).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Microorganisms: The sulfated                  microorganism is chosen from Acidianus                  ambivalens, A.brierleyi, Aquifex pyrophilus,                  Hydrogenobacter acidophilus, H.thermophilus,                  Thiobacillus denitrificans, Thiomicrospira                  crunogena, Sulfurimonas, Halothiobacillus,                  Acidithiobacillus, and Thermithiobacillus                  tepidarius. The Sulfurimonas is chosen from                  S.autotrophica, S.denitrificans, S.gotlandica,                  and S.paralvinellae. The Halothiobacillus is                  chosen from H.halophilus, H.hydrothermalis,                  H.kellyi, and H.neapolitanus. The                  Acidithiobacillus is chosen from A.albertensis,                  A.caldus, A.cuprithermicus, A.ferridurans,                  A.ferrivorans, A.ferrooxidans, and                  A.thiooxidans.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (A)(a) involves                  converting carbon dioxide into a useful substance                  by (i) converting into carbonates/bicarbonates in                  a reactor comprising carbon dioxide, a base                  solution and a metal sulfate, where the process                  is carried out by a carbonic anhydrase, (ii)                  reacting the carbonate/bicarbonate with a base                  solution to produce a water-soluble carbonate,                  (iii) reacting the water-soluble carbonate and                  the metal sulfate to form a metal carbonate and a                  water-soluble sulfate, (iv) electrolyzing the                  water-soluble sulfate and separating it into a                  sulfuric acid and a base solution, and (v)                  recovering the base solution produced in step                  (iv) to recycle the step of producing the                  water-soluble carbonate of step (ii). The step                  (b) further involves adding at least component                  chosen from oxygen (O2), nitrate (NO3-) and                  nitrite (NO2-) as an electron acceptor. The step                  (c) involves recovering the metal sulfate by                  passing the mixture produced in the step (b)                  through a filter to filter out the metal sulfate.                  Preferred Component: The metal is an alkali metal                  chosen from calcium, magnesium, iron, silicon,                  and aluminum.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US11407667-B1
TI Separating and removing carbon dioxide from            seawater in extraction system comprising reactor and            membrane, by pumping filtered seawater into reactor,            exposing to membrane and allowing to flow back and            forth across membrane and evacuating air gap.
AU MOYLAN C R
AE MOYLAN C R (MOYL-Individual)
GA 202299827D
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and removing carbon dioxide from                seawater (112) in an extraction system (100)                comprising reactor and a membrane (120), comprises:                (a) pumping seawater into the extraction system                forming filtered seawater (116); (b) filtering the                seawater to remove solids; (c) pumping the filtered                seawater into the reactor; (d) exposing the                filtered seawater to the membrane; (e) allowing the                filtered seawater to flow back and forth across the                membrane until equilibrium is established, where                carbon dioxide and water vapor form in an air gap;                (f) evacuating the air gap above the filtered                seawater to remove the carbon dioxide and water                vapor; (g) separating the carbon dioxide and water                vapor in a cooler; and (h) sequestering the carbon                dioxide. The catalytic enzyme is carbonic                anhydrase. The membrane comprises a membrane                material having silicone and polydimethylsiloxane.                The catalytic enzyme converts bicarbonate ion into                dissolved carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                removing carbon dioxide from seawater in an                extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: separates and removes carbon                dioxide from seawater and thus indirectly from the                atmosphere; and utilizes the catalytic enzyme                capable of boosting carbon-capture kinetics of                bicarbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic system of                separating and removing carbon dioxide from                seawater.
   100System
   112Seawater
   114Filters
   116Filtered seawater
   120Membrane

PT P
PN CN214862357-U
TI Energy-saving drying device for processing carbon dioxide, has cooling box whose top end face is provided with purifying box, where inner side end of cooling box is provided respectively with absorbing groove and cooling groove.
AU LUO Y
AE JIANGXI JIANGLIN NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2304Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an energy-saving drying device for processing carbon dioxide, wherein the structure comprises a cooling box and a purifying box; the carbon dioxide is input into the rotating groove through the air inlet pipe; the bidirectional motor drives the rotating plate to rotate, so as to absorb the water on the surface through the absorbing layer; then the carbon dioxide enters into the drying groove through the exhaust hole; under the action of the heating plate, heating, and then inputting the air outlet pipe through the air suction port and the air guide groove under the action of the high pressure air pump; then cooling the carbon dioxide by cooling water, and discharging under the action of the exhaust fan; It solves the problem that the existing carbon dioxide drying device in the process of drying the gas, because it is directly heating and drying the carbon dioxide, causing the moisture in the carbon dioxide needs to be permanently before drying, so that the whole working efficiency is low; and also not effectively cooling the carbon dioxide after heating, causing the back carbon dioxide to be stable too high, may have the safety of hidden trouble.

PN WO2021228979-A1; EP3909735-A4; EP3909735-A1
TI Method for processing concrete granulate for subsequent recycling of granulate, involves determining whether predetermined carbon dioxide saturation of granulates is reached, and removing granulate enriched with carbon dioxide.
AU GUTKNECHT V
   MAZZOTTI M
   TIEFENTHALER J
AE ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   NEUSTARK AG (NEUS-Non-standard)
   NEUSTARK AG (NEUS-Non-standard)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
GA 2021D0659C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing concrete granulate (2), comprises filling a regionally gas-tight container (4) with concrete granulate. A volume flow of gas comprising carbon dioxide is supplied into the container depending on a certain amount of a sensor of a carbon dioxide absorption that has taken place through the concrete granulates in the container. A determination is made whether a predetermined carbon dioxide saturation of granulates has been reached. The granulate enriched with carbon dioxide are removed. The amount of carbon dioxide absorbed is determined by measuring a pressure and carbon dioxide concentration by the sensor. The gas fed into container comprises 95-100% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for processing concrete granulate for subsequent recycling of concrete granulates used as gravel substitute in production of concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables enriching the concrete granulate with carbon dioxide to lead to toxic metals being better bound and unintentional escape into the environment can be significantly reduced, thus expanding application possibilities of the loose concrete granulates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) plant for processing concrete granulate for subsequent recycling of concrete granulate;
   (2) system;
   (3) concrete granulate; and
   (4) concrete comprising concrete granulate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the plant.
   Concrete granulate (2)
   Storage tank (3)
   Container (4)
   Inlet (5)
   Supply line (6)
