PN CN109369950-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide foamed phenolic foam, prepared by e.g. mixing modified phenolic resin and foaming agent, converting high-pressure tanked liquid carbon dioxide into a supercritical state, mixing curing agent, foaming and curing.
AU ZHONG D
   SUN Z
   ZHANG W
AE SHANDONG TIANYANG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201920933V
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide foamed phenolic foam is claimed. The supercritical carbon dioxide foamed phenolic foam is prepared by mixing phenolic resin master batch with curing agent in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.6, where the phenolic resin master batch comprises modified phenolic resin and 3-10 pts. wt. (preferably 3-8 pts. wt.) foaming agent, the foaming agent is supercritical carbon dioxide, or a mixture of conventional foaming agent and supercritical carbon dioxide, and the mixing ratio of conventional foaming agent and supercritical carbon dioxide is arbitrary.
   USE - Used as supercritical carbon dioxide foamed phenolic foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing supercritical carbon dioxide foamed phenolic foam, comprising (i) mixing modified phenolic resin and foaming agent, (ii) converting high-pressure tanked liquid carbon dioxide into a supercritical state by means of vacuum extracting gasification and cooling and pressurization liquefaction, (iii) mixing step (ii) product with step (i) product to obtain phenolic resin master batch, (iv) placing curing agent and step (iii) product in a high pressure pump having a mixing nozzle, and mixing at a certain temperature and ambient temperature and foaming, and (v) curing for a certain time to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The conventional foaming agent is pentane and/or cyclopentane. The foaming agent is Tween (RTM: Polysorbate) series, silicone series and/or ionic series. The composition of foaming agent is silicone oil AK158, Tween-20 (RTM: Polyoxyethylenesorbitan monolaurate), Tween-40 (RTM: Polyoxyethylenesorbitan monopalmitate) and/or Tween-80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylenesorbitan monooleate). The curing agent is sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, phenolsulfonic acid, methanesulfonic acid and/or acetic acid. Preferred Method: The preparation of modified phenolic resin, comprises taking phenol, liquid formaldehyde or paraformaldehyde, and carrying out graft modifying with hydroxymethylurea to obtain modified phenolic resin. In step (iv), the temperature of the material is 55-65 degrees C and the ambient temperature is -10 degrees C to 35 degrees C. In step (v), the curing time is 4-8 minutes.

PN US2018104642-A1
TI Method for reducing aerosol emissions during            carbon dioxide capture from flue gas in amine based            solvent process, involves forming lean solvent, feeding            solvent to absorber column, and feeding steam to flue            gas containing carbon dioxide.
AU MABROUK R
   JOVANOVIC S
   NAUMOVITZ J
   KRISHNAMURTHY R
AE MABROUK R (MABR-Individual)
   JOVANOVIC S (JOVA-Individual)
   NAUMOVITZ J (NAUM-Individual)
   KRISHNAMURTHY R (KRIS-Individual)
GA 201829968Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding flue gas                containing carbon dioxide to an absorber column                (A), where the absorber column contains amine                solvent. The carbon dioxide is absorbed in the                amine solvent forming a rich solvent. The rich                solvent is fed to an inter-stage cooler (WC1-WC4).                The rich solvent and fed rich solvent are recovered                to a regeneration column (R). The carbon dioxide is                separated from the rich solvent. The carbon dioxide                is recovered. Lean solvent is formed. The lean                solvent is fed to the absorber column. Steam is fed                to the flue gas containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for reducing aerosol emissions during                carbon dioxide capture from flue gas in an amine                based solvent process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables positioning a flue gas                blower downstream from an absorber to reduce post                combustion capture parasitic load so as to                consequently increase net power generation                efficiency. The method enables utilizing an                absorber intercooler to control temperature rise of                the solvent to enhance carbon dioxide                absorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a facility for carbon dioxide capture from a                flue gas stream using amine based solvents.
   Absorber column (A)
   Steam ejector (B)
   Demister (DM)
   Regeneration column (R)
   Inter-stage coolers (WC1-WC4)

PN CN113336635-A
TI Synergistic conversion of kitchen waste and carbon dioxide source water by mixing kitchen waste and water into hydrothermal reactor, adding carbon dioxide source to hydrothermal reactor for hydrothermal reaction, reducing carbon dioxide source to formic acid and converting into formic acid.
AU JIN F
   YANG Y
   ZHONG H
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 2021A45096
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synergistic conversion of kitchen waste and carbon dioxide source water involves (i) mixing kitchen waste and water into the hydrothermal reactor, where the mixed kitchen waste and water account for 35%-50 vol.% of the hydrothermal reactor, adding carbon dioxide source to the hydrothermal reactor for hydrothermal reaction, where the carbon content of kitchen waste, and the molar ratio of carbon dioxide source is 1:(10-30), the pH of the hydrothermal reaction is 8-10, and the reaction temperature is 280-380 degrees C, after 2-4 hours of reaction, reducing the carbon dioxide source to formic acid by kitchen waste, and converting kitchen waste into formic acid, acetic acid, lactic acid and nitrogen-containing compounds.
   USE - Method for synergistic conversion of kitchen waste and carbon dioxide source water and heat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of food waste and water is (1-10):10. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 300-380 degrees C. Preferred Components: The kitchen waste comprises food waste generally refers to the garbage generated in residents' daily life and activities such as food processing, catering services, and unit meals, where the chemical composition of the kitchen waste comprises carbohydrates, proteins and oils. The carbon dioxide source comprises bicarbonate, pure carbon dioxide and/or waste gas containing carbon dioxide. The bicarbonate comprises bicarbonate compound of formula: M1(HCO3)x,M1 = lithium, sodium, potassium, calcium and magnesium.Preferred Method: The method involves when the carbon dioxide source is pure carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide-containing waste gas, after adding alkali to the hydrothermal reactor and leaving it still for 5-10 hours until the pH of the reaction solution is 8-10. The method involves adding alkali to the hydrothermal reactor, when the carbon dioxide source comprises pure carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide-containing waste gas, where the alkali is of formula: M2(OH)y,M2 = lithium, sodium, potassium, calcium and magnesium..

TI High power carbon dioxide laser system for creating extreme ultraviolet radiation from tin droplet in extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus, has laser beam produced by plasma ignition of gas discharge in carbon dioxide discharge region.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 202040838E
AB    NOVELTY - The high power carbon dioxide laser system for creating extreme ultraviolet radiation from a tin droplet in the extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus, comprises a laser beam that is produced by plasma ignition of a gas discharge in a carbon dioxide discharge region. Quartz tubes are used as a discharge region to contain the carbon dioxide gas that is used to produce the laser beam. The inner surface of the quartz tubes is coated with a layer of aluminum oxide.
   USE - High power carbon dioxide laser system for creating extreme ultraviolet radiation from a tin droplet in the extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Service life of the high power carbon dioxide laser system is enhanced. The use of the aluminum oxide coated quartz tubes stops carbon dioxide power degradation as a result of silicon dioxide.

TI Method for sequestration of carbon dioxide to reduce emission of carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust gas through catalytic convertor of vehicle, involves loading mono ethanol amine into vermiculite material, followed by recirculating.
AU PREETHI V
AE HINDUSTAN INST TECHNOLOGY & SCI (HIND-Non-standard)
   V P (VPPP-Individual)
GA 201878858E
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide sequestration method involves loading mono ethanol amine into vermiculite material, passing an amine reactor where the mono ethanol amine solution is sprinkled through a perforated pipe over the exhaust gas, and allowing the mono ethanol amine to absorb the carbon dioxide passing through the amine reactor, followed by utilizing a recirculation pump to recirculate the carbon dioxide absorbed mono ethanol amine to a top of the reactor again and sprinkling through a perforated pipe.
   USE - Method for sequestration of carbon dioxide to reduce emission of carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust gas through catalytic convertor of vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficient carbon dioxide removal and adsorption effect, decreases the health risk to individuals inhaling vehicular exhausts and provides sustainable environment for future generation, and reduces energy consumption, and is easy, eco-friendly and cost effective.

TI Fixing carbon dioxide to concrete by forming gas injection hole in concrete contained in concrete structure, introducing gas containing carbon dioxide into gas injection hole, and capping one end of gas injection hole to contain gas in gas injection hole.
AU YUSUKE F
   TOMOHIRO F
   SANJAY P
AE FUJITA CORP (FUKG-C)
   UNIV NIPPON (UYNI-C)
GA 202278052X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fixing carbon dioxide to concrete (102), involves forming a gas injection hole (104) in the concrete contained in the concrete structure (100), introducing a gas containing carbon dioxide into the gas injection hole, and after the introduction of the gas, capping one end of the gas injection hole to contain the gas in the gas injection hole.
   USE - The method is used for fixing carbon dioxide to concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently fixes carbon dioxide to concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a concrete structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a method of fixing carbon dioxide to concrete.
   Concrete structure (100)
   Concrete (102)
   Gas injection hole (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The method further involves: depressurizing the gas injection hole prior to the introduction of the gas. The gas injection hole is a through hole; capping the other end of the gas injection hole prior to introduction of the gas, covering a part of the side wall of the gas injection hole with a protective tube, the part reaches the one end of the gas injection hole, where the concrete structure comprises a reinforcing bar, and the length of the protective tube in the direction in which the gas injection hole extends is larger than the sum of the shortest distance from the outer surface of the concrete structure to the reinforcing bar and the diameter of the reinforcing bar; placing concrete in the gas injection hole after the introduction of the gas; to form the concrete structure, pouring the ready-mixed concrete so as to embed at least a part of the core material in the formwork in which the core material is arranged, and hardening the ready-mixed concrete, the formation of the gas injection holes is done by removing the core material. Preferred Components: The gas further comprises water. The gas injection hole is a bottomed hole. The gas injection hole extends perpendicularly to the outer surface of the concrete structure. The gas injection hole extends in a direction inclined with respect to the outer surface of the concrete structure. The gas injection hole is formed using a drill. PN CN114130350-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent used for            capturing carbon dioxide in industrial flue gas,            involves immersing and calcining multi-source solid            waste to obtain adsorbent, drying oxide, mixing dried            oxide with adsorbent, and calcining obtained            mixture.
AU LUO L
   GUO Y
   ZHU T
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2022390155
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent                involves: (1) immersing and calcining multi-source                solid waste to obtain an adsorbent; and (2) drying                oxide, mixing the dried oxide with the adsorbent,                and calcining the obtained mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide adsorbent used for capturing carbon dioxide                in industrial flue gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low in cost, simple in                operation, and environmentally-friendly, and                provides carbon dioxide adsorbent, which has high                efficiency of capturing carbon dioxide. The oxide                is used to modify the multi-source solid waste                adsorbent, which can improve adsorption                performance, high temperature anti-sintering                performance and cyclic adsorption performance of                the adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The immersing step is                  carried out in 0.5-5 mol/L salt solution                  comprising sodium carbonate, sodium chloride,                  potassium carbonate and/or potassium chloride for                  1-5 hours. The step (1) involves, after                  calcination, ball milling at 200-500 rpm for                  10-30 minutes. The step (2) involves, after                  drying, ball milling at 200-500 rpm for 10-30                  minutes.Preferred Conditions: The calcination step                  in step (1) is carried out by heating at a                  heating rate of 1-5degrees Celsius/minute to                  800-1000degrees Celsius and heat-preserving for                  1-3 hours. The drying step in step (2) is carried                  out at 100-150degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours. The                  calcination step in step (2) is carried out by                  heating at a heating rate of 1-5degrees                  Celsius/minute to 700-1000degrees Celsius and                  heat-preserving for 1-3 hours.Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  the oxide in step (2) is 1-20 %mass.Preferred Components: The multi-source solid                  waste comprises limestone, calcium carbide slag,                  steel slag, calcium silicate slag, papermaking                  sludge, desulfurized gypsum, coal ash and/or                  metallurgical slag tailings. The oxide comprises                  aluminum oxide, ferric oxide, magnesium oxide,                  zirconium dioxide, titanium dioxide, lanthanum                  oxide and/or yttrium oxide.

AU XIONG Y

GA 2021B16216
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide comprises subjecting the raw material containing carbon dioxide to catalytic conversion reaction under the action of plasmon material composite material catalyst and driving by the infrared ray to obtain the carbon- containing compound.
   USE - The method is useful for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can use clean and reserves and rich solar energy; is clean and economical; uses the energy in the sunlight to store the most infrared which greatly improves the conversion efficacy of the solar energy to the chemical energy; and synthesizes a series of necessary chemicals thus realize the fixing of carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere with high-efficacy and high selectivity of carbon dioxide directional catalytic conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The plasmon material composite material comprises plasmonic material comprises an plasmon material having a light-absorbing property in the infrared wavelength range and material with carbon dioxide catalytic conversion performance comprising metal material, alloy material, semiconductor and/or metal organic frame material. The plasmon material comprises plasmon metal materials and the shape structure of the composite material catalyst comprises a fully coated core-shell structure, porous core-shell structure, main-satellite structure, heterogeneous structure and/or layered stack, the metal material comprises palladium, copper, platinum, rhodium, nickel, iron, cobalt or zinc, the alloy material comprises palladium, copper, platinum, rhodium, nickel, iron, cobalt and/or zinc, the semiconductor material comprises indium hydroxide, indium oxide and/or titanium dioxide and the metal organic frame material comprises MIL-125 (Ti), UiO-66 (Zr), MIL-101 (Fe), MIL-125-NH2 and/or Porous coordination network (PCN)-222. The plasminogen material comprises gold, silver and/or copper, the shape of the plasmon material comprises nano ball, nano cube, nano sheet, nano cone and/or nano rod and the composite catalyst comprises a physically mixed composite catalyst. The wavelength of the infrared ray is 750-2500 nm, the infrared ray comprises near infrared ray and the infrared ray comprises two or more than two wavelengths of infrared ray. The plasma material composite material catalyst can be loaded on the catalyst carrier comprises carbon carrier, silicon dioxide carrier, alumina carrier, ceramic carrier, molecular sieve, silicon carbide, kaolin, fluorine polymer and/or quartz glass and the catalyst carrier comprises a gel carrier and the load amount of the load is 1-50 wt.%. The generating source of the infrared ray comprises xenon lamp, an light emitting diode lamp, a tungsten lamp and an infrared lamp and the intensity of the infrared ray is 5-10000 mW/cm2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material containing carbon dioxide comprises an aqueous solution of carbon dioxide and/or a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and the mixed gas further comprises water vapor, hydrogen, methane and/or ammonia and the condition of the catalytic conversion reaction further comprises heating, pressurizing and/or applying voltage. Preferred Method: The heating temperature is 0-800 degrees C, the pressure of the pressurizing is 0.1-10 Mpa and the bias voltage range of the applied voltage is 0.05-3 V. The catalytic conversion comprises catalytic reduction and the reaction vessel of the catalytic conversion reaction comprises a light-permeable fixed bed reactor with quartz or glass window, a light-permeable fluidized bed reaction vessel with quartz or glass window, a fixed bed reactor integrated with internal light source and/or a fluidized bed reaction vessel integrated with internal light source.

AU LI D
AE LI D (LIDD-Individual)
GA 2021097466
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonium bicarbonate, comprises heating gas raw materials ammonia and carbon dioxide at a temperature of 60 degrees C, evenly mixing the water vapor from the steam pipe network in the gas mixer at a mol ratio of 1:1:1, controlling the temperature to 60-90 degrees C, and then adding ammonium bicarbonate to cool and crystallizing in the crystallizer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing ammonium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the impurity in the solution when the liquid phase crystallization, corrosion, additive to the product quality of the negative influence; and is easy to obtain high purity ammonium bicarbonate.

The cooling pipe is a plastic pipe.

TI Ultra-light thermoplastic elastomer foam material preparation involves putting thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer into reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide for supercritical foaming, and then releasing pressure.
AU ZHANG X
AE FUJIAN XINGXUN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (FUJI-Non-standard)
   FUJIAN XINGXUN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202098955F
AB    NOVELTY - Ultra-light thermoplastic elastomer foam material preparation involves putting thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer into a reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide for supercritical foaming, and then releasing pressure to obtain foamed intermediate product. The obtained foamed intermediate product is put into another reactor under nitrogen gas for secondary supercritical foaming to obtain the foamed product with a density of 0.05-0.1 g/cm3.
   USE - Method for preparing ultra-light thermoplastic elastomer foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the ultra-light thermoplastic elastomer foam material in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with extremely low density, small shrinkage, good elasticity and stable performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is thermoplastic polyurethane, thermoplastic polyether ester elastomer, PEBAX (RTM: a linear chain of polyamide and polyether segment), thermoplastic polyurethane and PEBAX (RTM: a linear chain of polyamide and polyether segment) blend, thermoplastic polyurethane and thermoplastic polyether ester elastomer blend Or thermoplastic polyether ester elastomer and PEBAX (RTM: a linear chain of polyamide and polyether segment) blend extruded, molded or injection molded sheet.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by using cobalt-based catalyst comprises taking carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials, conjugated cobalt porphyrin microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as cocatalyst, then reacting in absence of solvent at room temperature and pressure for 12-48 hours to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by using cobalt-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclic carbonate has simple preparation method, low cost, high catalytic activity, stable structure performance and mild reaction condition; and is easy to recycle, and suitable for large scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated cobalt porphyrin microporous polymer material is prepared by performing Scholl coupling reaction of cobalt tetraphenylporphyrin as monomer and anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst i.e. 7,12,17,24-tetrakis(4-methylphenyl)-2- lambda (5),22,23- lambda (5),25-tetraaza-1-cobaltaoctacyclo(11.9.1.1(1,8).1(3,21).0(2,6).0(16,23).0(18,22).0(11,25))pentacosa-2,4,6,8,10,12,14,16(23),17,19,21(24)-undecaene-2,23-bis(ylium) of formula (I). Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, oxidized styrene, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The amount of active component heterogeneous catalyst is 0.2-0.5 mol.% of the amount of epoxy compound. The used amount of co-catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol.% of the epoxy compound.

PN CN107927696-A
TI Preparation of black pepper essential oil includes crushing black pepper, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, stirring, standing, and collecting yellowish transparent clear liquid and yellow semi-solid product.
AU TAN R
AE GUANGDONG YUJIN FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201832100L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of black pepper essential oil comprises crushing black pepper into powder, adding to extraction kettle, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain mixture of black pepper oil and resin, placing to reaction vessel, stirring, standing to divide into three layers, and collecting yellowish transparent clear liquid upper layer and yellow semi-solid product middle layer.
   USE - Method for preparation of black pepper essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption and high extraction rate of effective components. The product has full-bodied aroma of black pepper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The preparation of black pepper essential oil is carried out by crushing black pepper powder, sieving through 30-60 mesh sieve, adding to extraction kettle, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 44-51 degrees C and at 14-19 MPa for 0.5-2 hours, separating twice at temperatures of 50-52 degrees C and 38-43 degrees C and pressures of 6-8 MPa and 6-7 MPa, adding products to reaction kettle, stirring at 50-65 degrees C and at speed of 30-50 revolutions/minute for 30-60 minutes, and standing for 60-120 minutes to divide into 3 layers; and collecting yellowish transparent clear liquid upper layer and yellow solid product middle layer, suction-filtering yellow semi-solid product, filtering yellow transparent liquid, and combining filtrates.

AU LANG X
   GU Y
AE HENAN XIAOWEI ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018154543
AB    NOVELTY - A printing and dyeing waste water is treated by adjusting pH value by filling carbon dioxide to 6-8, anaerobic reacting through internal circulation (IC) anaerobic reaction tower, aerobic biochemical reacting, coagulating sedimenting, hydrolysis acidifying, membrane bioreactor (MBR) treating, and advanced processing by decoloring.
   USE - Method for treating printing and dyeing waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has water outlet suspended solid close to 0, reduces chemical oxygen demand concentration of drainage, and keeps anaerobic strain activity in IC anaerobic reaction tower to realize zero discharge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for printing and dyeing waste water treatment system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The MBR treatment is done by MBR membrane bioreactor. Adjusting pH value is further involves acidification processing and anaerobic reacting, and degrading polymer in waste water to small molecular substance including acetic acid. Coagulation sedimentation is done into sedimentation tank by adding coagulant comprising polyaluminum chloride and/or polyacrylamide. Aerobic biochemical reaction is done by adjusting and coagulating sedimenting. Deep processing further involves ultrafiltering comprising decoloring. The method further involves reverse osmosis treating and electrodialysis processing after ultrafiltering.

TI Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus comprises carbon dioxide supply device for supplying carbon dioxide, and electrochemical reaction device comprising gas diffusion electrode containing gas diffusion layer and catalyst layer.
AU KIM Y R
AE KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD (HHIH-C)
GA 202140230K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide treatment apparatus using a gas diffusion electrode comprises carbon dioxide supply device for supplying carbon dioxide to the gas diffusion electrode side of the electrochemical reaction device, and an electrochemical reaction device for converting carbon dioxide supplied to the gas diffusion electrode into sodium hydrogen carbonate or sodium carbonate through an electrochemical reaction. The electrochemical reaction device comprises gas diffusion electrode serving as a cathode, an anode, and an electrolyte provided between the gas diffusion electrode and anode. The gas diffusion electrode comprises gas diffusion layer for diffusing carbon dioxide delivered from the carbon dioxide supply device to the catalyst layer, and a catalyst layer provided on one surface of the gas diffusion layer to promote a reduction reaction in the gas diffusion electrode.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus effectively removes carbon dioxide using an electrochemical reaction device, easily removes carbon dioxide by supplying carbon dioxide in a gaseous state to the gas diffusion electrode and converting it into sodium carbonate or sodium hydrogen carbonate through an electrochemical reaction, and has improved carbon dioxide removal efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The oxidation reaction of sodium chloride occurs at the anode to give sodium(II) ions, chlorine and electrons, when power is applied to the gas diffusion electrode and anode. The carbon dioxide is supplied to the gas diffusion electrode and sodium ions and electrons generated at the anode are moved to the gas diffusion electrode. The carbon dioxide is converted into sodium hydrogen carbonate or sodium carbonate.

bringing (S2) the crude synthesis gas into contact                with a cold methanol substream and with at least                one methanol substream recirculated from downstream                process in the absorption apparatus to obtain a                product gas stream depleted in acidic gas                constituents, water and metal carbonyl which is                discharged from the process, and a partial methanol                stream loaded with acidic gas constituents, water                and metal carbonyl, multi-stage regenerating (S3)                the loaded partial methanol stream by pressure                reduction (flashing) and/or temperature increase                (hot regeneration) process to obtain at least one                regenerated methanol partial stream which is                recycled to process (S2), and carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide-containing gas streams discharged                from the process to obtain a methanol partial                stream depleted of acid gas constituents, and                comprising water, methanol and metal sulfides,                introducing (S4) the methanol partial stream into a                methanol-water separation column, separately                distilling into a methanol-containing overhead                which is partially recycled to process (S2) and/or                (S3), and into a hydrous bottom product which is                discharged from the process, and directly adding                (S5) water into a feed line of the methanol partial PN EP3323805-A1; WO2018091158-A1; CA3040324-A1; AU2017362465-A1; CN109890788-A; IN201947023230-A; EP3541780-A1; ID201904574-A; BR112019010088-A2; US2019359558-A1; US10550075-B2; RU2739768-C2; RU2019117895-A; EP3541780-B1; AU2017362465-B2; IN370657-B; CN109890788-B
TI Integrated process for producing urea and            urea-ammonium nitrate involves e.g. producing aqueous            solution of urea starting from ammonia and carbon            dioxide, and producing ammonium nitrate from ammonia            and nitric acid in ammonium nitrate stage.
AU RUGNONE L
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE CO LTD (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 201840123U
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process (P1) for producing urea and                urea-ammonium nitrate, comprises e.g.: (a)                producing aqueous solution of urea (15) starting                from ammonia and carbon dioxide, in urea synthesis                process; (b) producing ammonium nitrate (32) from                ammonia and nitric acid in ammonium nitrate stage;                (c) mixing at least part of aqueous solution of                urea with at least part of ammonium nitrate to                produce urea-ammonium nitrate (33) in urea-ammonium                nitrate stage; and (d) urea synthesis process                comprising reaction of ammonia and carbon dioxide                at synthesis pressure to form urea reaction mixture                (4).
   USE - The process is useful for producing urea and                urea-ammonium nitrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides ammonia-containing stream                from the first recovery stage having a purity of at                least 90 wt.% (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated process (P1) for producing urea and                urea-ammonium nitrate, comprises: (a) producing                aqueous solution of urea (15) starting from ammonia                and carbon dioxide, in a urea synthesis process;                (b) producing ammonium nitrate (32) from ammonia                and nitric acid in a ammonium nitrate stage; (c)                mixing at least part of the aqueous solution of                urea with at least part of the ammonium nitrate to                produce urea-ammonium nitrate (33) in a                urea-ammonium nitrate stage; (d) urea synthesis                process comprising the reaction of ammonia and                carbon dioxide at a synthesis pressure to form a                urea reaction mixture (4) containing urea and                unconverted materials, and also comprising the                recovery of unconverted materials in many recovery                stages including at least a first recovery stage                operating at a first recovery pressure lower than                the synthesis pressure, and a second recovery stage                operating at a second recovery pressure lower than                the first recovery pressure; (e) releasing offgas                (19) by a step of carbamate decomposition in the                second recovery stage, and containing unconverted                ammonia, are at least partially condensed at the                second recovery pressure, obtaining condensate                offgas; (f) recycling at least part of the                condensate offgas or a solution containing at least                part of the condensate offgas, to the first                recovery stage; and (g) withdrawing an                ammonia-containing stream from the first recovery                stage at the first recovery pressure, and feeding                the ammonia-containing stream to the ammonium                nitrate stage. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) urea- urea-ammonium nitrate integrated                plant for carrying the process (P1), comprising a                urea synthesis section for the production of                aqueous solution of urea starting from ammonia and                carbon dioxide, a urea-ammonium nitrate section,                where at least part of the aqueous solution of urea                is mixed with ammonium nitrate to produce                urea-ammonium nitrate, the urea synthesis section                comprising a synthesis loop for the reaction of                ammonia and carbon dioxide at synthesis pressure to                form reaction mixture containing urea and                unconverted materials, many recovery sections                including at least a first recovery section                operating at a first recovery pressure lower than                the synthesis pressure, and a second recovery                section operating at a second recovery pressure                lower than the first recovery pressure, second                recovery section comprising a carbamate decomposer                and a condenser for a partial condensation of                offgas released by the carbamate decomposer,                obtaining condensate offgas, at least one line                arranged to recycle at least part of the condensate                offgas or solution containing at least part of the                condensate offgas to the first recovery section,                and at least one line to feed an ammonia-containing                stream from the first recovery stage to the                ammonium nitrate stage; and
   (2) modifying (P2) urea-urea-ammonium nitrate                integrated plant, comprising installation of                additional condenser in the second recovery                section, for condensing a current of offgas                released by a carbamate decomposer of the second                recovery section, obtaining condensed offgas, the                provision of a line to recycle a solution                containing the condensate offgas to the first                recovery stage, the provision of a line to withdraw                a pressurized ammonia-containing stream from the                first recovery section and recycling the                pressurized ammonia-containing stream to the                ammonium nitrate section.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) is                  further integrated with a nitric acid stage for                  the production of solution of nitric acid, where                  at least a portion of the solution of nitric acid                  is used for the production of ammonium nitrate in                  the ammonium nitrate stage, the process comprises                  withdrawing a substantially pure ammonia gas from                  the first recovery stage and feeding the ammonia                  gas to the nitric acid stage, and the process                  further comprises withdrawing pure ammonia gas                  from the scrubbing process and feeding the                  ammonia gas to the nitric acid stage. In the                  process (P1): the ammonia-containing stream                  withdrawn from the first recovery stage, either                  in a gaseous state or in a liquid state, is                  contacted with a solution of nitric acid in a                  pressurized pipe reactor of the ammonium nitrate                  stage; and the pressurized pipe reactor operates                  at a pressure equal to the first recovery                  pressure. The step (f) includes: feeding at least                  part of the condensate offgas directly to the                  first recovery stage; mixing the condensate                  offgas with the gas phase before or during the                  condensation, obtaining a carbamate solution at                  the third pressure, and recycling the carbamate                  solution to the first recovery stage; and                  recycling a first portion of the solution                  containing condensate offgas to the step of                  partial condensation of the gas phase obtained                  after decomposition of the reaction mixture, the                  first portion of solution being mixed with the                  gas phase before or during condensation,                  recycling a second portion of the solution to the                  scrubbing process, for use as a scrubbing medium.                  The urea synthesis process comprises a third                  recovery stage operating at a third recovery                  pressure which is lower than the first recovery                  pressure and greater than the second recovery                  pressure, the third recovery stage comprises a                  decomposition of a urea solution obtaining a gas                  phase containing ammonia and carbon dioxide, and                  subsequent condensation of the gas phase. The                  first recovery stage comprises: scrubbing carbon                  dioxide and ammonia vapors contained in a                  carbamate mixture after partial condensation in a                  condenser of the with first recovery stage                  obtaining a carbamate solution, where the                  ammonia-containing stream directed to the                  ammonium nitrate stage includes the carbamate                  solution obtained after scrubbing or vapors                  obtained from a subsequent step of decomposition                  of the diluted carbamate solution; and                  decomposition of the urea reaction mixture,                  obtaining a urea solution and a gas phase                  containing ammonia and carbon dioxide, and                  partial condensation of the gas phase obtaining                  the carbamate solution and carbon dioxide and                  ammonia vapors which is subjected to the                  scrubbing. In the process (P1), the urea is                  produced with any of a total-recycle process, a                  partial-recycle process and a once-through                  process. The process (P2) further comprises                  installation of an additional low-pressure                  recovery section in the urea section, the                  additional recovery section operating at a third                  pressure lower than the first pressure but                  greater than the second pressure, the additional                  low-pressure recovery section comprising at least                  a decomposer and a low-pressure condenser for                  condensation of gas containing ammonia and carbon                  dioxide, sending the solution containing                  condensate off gas to the low-pressure condenser                  of the low-pressure recovery section, and feeding                  a recycle solution containing ammonia and                  obtained from the low-pressure condenser to the                  medium-pressure recovery section. The ammonium                  nitrate section originally comprises an                  atmospheric neutralizer, the process (P2)                  comprising the replacement of the neutralizer                  with a pipe reactor, the pipe reactor being fed                  with the pressurized ammonia-containing stream                  withdrawn from the medium-pressure recovery                  section. Preferred Components: In the process                  (P1), the first recovery pressure is 2-25                  (preferably 8-20) bar and/or the second recovery                  pressure is atmospheric or is a near-atmospheric                  pressure slightly above the atmospheric pressure,                  preferably not greater than 1 barg, preferably                  0.5 barg, and/or the third recovery pressure is                  2-6 (preferably 3) bar.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111003217-A
TI Phase transition based sustainable propulsion method of liquid carbon dioxide used in aircraft, involves injecting carbon dioxide gas after phase change with predetermined discharge amount to obtain sustainable thrust.
AU CHANG Z
   WANG C
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2020331356
AB    NOVELTY - The method involve obtaining and liquefying (S1) carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide is contained in a thermally insulated container in the form of a liquid phase. The liquid carbon dioxide is instantly heated (S2) so that the carbon dioxide is converted from the liquid phase to the gas phase. The carbon dioxide gas after the phase change is injected (S3) in a predetermined direction with a predetermined discharge amount to obtain a sustainable thrust.
   USE - Phase transition based sustainable propulsion method of liquid carbon dioxide used in aircraft (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The raw materials used for propelling liquefied carbon dioxide can be obtained, without polluting target celestial environment, short response time is set, and precise control is allowed. The propelled liquefied carbon dioxide does not generate abnormal heat in the aircraft engine, thus avoiding ablation damage. The propulsion method involves pure physical process, no chemical reaction, no toxic and harmful substance emission, thus is environment-friendly. The propellant uses only carbon dioxide, has cheap cost and is easy to obtain, based on the propulsion method, thus reducing use-cost in a vast scale. The propelled liquefied carbon dioxide has high reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for liquefied carbon dioxide propulsion device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the liquefied carbon dioxide propulsion method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining and liquefying carbon dioxide gas (S1)
   Step for heating liquid carbon dioxide instantly so that the carbon dioxide is converted from the liquid phase to the gas phase (S2)
   Step for injecting carbon dioxide gas after the phase change in a predetermined direction with a predetermined discharge amount to obtain a sustainable thrust (S3)

PN CN114772551-A; CN114772551-B
TI High efficient utilization of methane-rich gas            involves mixing pre-treated methane with water vapor,            entering mixed gas into methane steam reforming            reactor, preheating to generate carbon dioxide, carbon            monoxide and hydrogen and utilizing methane flameless            combustion for heat supply.
AU YU X
   ZHAO C
   YE X
   CHANG T
   ZHANG X
AE ZHEJIANG PYNEO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202298574W
AB    NOVELTY - High efficient utilization of methane-rich gas                involves pre-treating methane, mixing pre-treated                methane with water vapor to form a mixed gas,                entering the mixed gas into a methane steam                reforming reactor and preheating to react and to                generate carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and                hydrogen. The methane steam reforming reactor uses                methane flameless combustion for heat supply. A                hydrogen separation tube is arranged in the methane                steam reforming reactor and a hydrogen separation                membrane is arranged on the hydrogen separation                tube. The hydrogen separation membrane is utilized                to separate high-purity hydrogen in-situ during the                methane steam reforming reaction. The hydrogen is                entered into the hydrogen purification and storage                process to prepare hydrogen product. The methane is                pre-mixed with a part of carbon dioxide from the                carbon dioxide purification/liquefaction/storage                process and preheated by flameless combustion,                followed by condensing and recovering water.
   USE - Method for high efficient utilization of                methane-rich gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to liquefy ammonia products                into liquid ammonia that acts as a chemical carrier                for long-distance (&#8805; 200km)                transportation of hydrogen, thus solving the                problem of hydrogen long-distance                transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High efficient utilization of methane-rich gas                involves pre-treating methane, mixing with water                vapor to form a mixed gas, entering the mixed gas                into a methane steam reforming reactor, and                preheating to react and to generate carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The methane steam                reforming reactor uses methane flameless combustion                for heat supply. A hydrogen separation tube is                arranged in the methane steam reforming reactor and                a hydrogen separation membrane is arranged on the                hydrogen separation tube. The hydrogen separation                membrane is utilized to separate high-purity                hydrogen in-situ during the methane steam reforming                reaction. The hydrogen obtained by the separation                is entered into the hydrogen purification and                storage process to prepare hydrogen product. The                remaining carbon dioxide-rich tail gas after the                separation of hydrogen in the methane steam                reforming reactor first exchanges heat with liquid                water and then enters into the carbon dioxide                purification/liquefaction/storage process, where                the carbon dioxide is separated, purified and                liquefied to make into carbon dioxide products. The                methane is pre-mixed with a part of carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide                purification/liquefaction/storage process after                pre-treatment and the mixed gas is preheated by                heat exchange with the combustion exhaust gas                produced by the flameless combustion. The mixed gas                and oxygen are injected into the flameless                combustion chamber, and the flameless combustion of                methane produces combustion tail gas carbon dioxide                and water. The heat generated by the flameless                combustion of methane is provided to the methane                steam reforming reactor. After the mixed gas of                carbon dioxide and water produced by the flameless                combustion process of methane is cooled, water is                condensed and recovered. The carbon dioxide enters                the carbon dioxide                purification/liquefaction/storage process.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for efficient utilization of methane-rich gas,                which comprises a first mixed preheater for mixing                and preheating methane and water vapor, the methane                steam reforming reactor filled with a nickel-based                catalyst and much hydrogen separation tubes, a                hydrogen separation equipment for purifying                hydrogen, a liquid water heat exchanger to recover                the heat of the carbon dioxide-rich tail gas, a                carbon dioxide separation equipment for purifying                carbon dioxide to obtain carbon dioxide product, a                second mixing preheater for mixing and preheating                methane and carbon dioxide, a methane flameless                combustion chamber for flameless combustion on the                mixed gas from the second mixing preheater and the                separated tail gas from the carbon dioxide                separation equipment, and a cooler for separating                carbon dioxide and water produced by the methane                flameless combustion chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxygen required for                  the flameless combustion of the methane is                  provided by an air cryogenic separation process                  or an air pressure swing adsorption technology,                  where the air cryogenic separation technology or                  air pressure swing adsorption technology also                  produces high-purity nitrogen, oxygen and                  nitrogen with a molar ratio of 1:2-2.5.

AB    NOVELTY - A sodium bicarbonate production apparatus comprises a reactor (a) receiving an aqueous sodium carbonate solution and a carbon dioxide gas, a carbon dioxide microbubble generator receiving carbon dioxide gas and fluid and generating microbubbles in the received fluid, and a carbon dioxide macrobubble generator generating carbon dioxide macrobubbles and injecting carbon dioxide macrobubbles to the sodium carbonate aqueous solution.
   USE - Apparatus is used for producing sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus enables economical, efficient and rapid production of sodium bicarbonate with high yield of 99.99% and improved productivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The apparatus further comprises a reactor (b) receiving the fluid flowing out of the reactor (a), and a rotating flow generator that generates a rotating flow by receiving carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide microbubble generator generates microbubbles in the incoming fluid and injecting carbon dioxide macrobubbles to the reactor (b). The macrobubble generator comprises several macrobubble generating nozzles, and a carbon dioxide gas supply line providing a path for supplying carbon dioxide gas to the macrobubble generating nozzles. In the carbon dioxide gas supply line, macrobubble generating nozzles are separated from each other and coupled. The carbon dioxide gas flowing along the carbon dioxide gas supply line is combined to be sprayed through the macrobubble generating nozzles. The reactor (b) comprises a reaction vessel, and a stirring device installed inside the reaction vessel. The stirring device comprises a rotation shaft, and an impeller attached to the rotation shaft and arranged vertically and rotated by a drive motor. The reaction vessel comprises an inlet through which fluid flowing out of the reactor (a) is introduced, an inlet through which carbon dioxide microbubbles can be introduced, and an outlet for discharging gas to the outside, including a space for mixing the fluid received from the reactor (a) and the carbon dioxide microbubbles and preventing it from flowing out to the outside.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluid is a fluid that flows out of the reactor (b). The amount of carbon dioxide gas injected into the reactor (a) is more than the amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced into the reactor (b), preferably 4 times or more the amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced into the reactor (b). Preferred Properties: The average diameter of the carbon dioxide macrobubbles is 1-2 mm. The average diameter of the carbon dioxide microbubbles is 50 mu m or less.

AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from process stream                involves taking carbon dioxide and one or more                further components, providing the process stream at                a pressure such that a partial pressure of the                carbon dioxide in the process stream at 15 bar,                drying process stream sufficiently so that a dew                point of the process stream. The carbon dioxide is                reduced to a temperature of (-)20 degrees C or                less, cooling the process stream in one heat                exchanger to provide the process stream which                includes carbon dioxide as a two phase stream,                expanding the two phase stream so as to reduce the                temperature of the two phase stream to a                temperature that is within 15 degrees C of freezing                point of the two phase stream and separating the                two phase stream to provide a vapor stream enriched                with one of the one or more further components to                provide a liquid stream that is enriched with the                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for separating carbon dioxide from                process stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate carbon dioxide                from process stream which has capacity electric                storage batteries, eliminates fuel such as crabon                dioxide, nitic oxide, crabon mono-oxide and                significantly reduces air pollution particularly at                ground level in large urban conurbations,                implementation of a hydrogen-based world economy,                require a very large hydrogen production capacity,                and cost-effective also.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide (carbon dioxide )                separation system, which comprises a compressor                configured for compressing a process stream, where                process stream comprises carbon dioxide and one or                more further components, a drier is configured for                removing moisture from the process stream, one heat                exchanger is configured for cooling the process                stream against one or more cooling streams and                providing the process stream as a two-phase stream,                one expander is configured for cooling the                two-phase stream via expansion of the two-phase                stream; and a mass transfer column is configured to                receive the two phase stream and generate a vapor                phase stream and a liquid phase stream;
   (2) a hydrogen production system, which                comprises a reactor unit configured for receiving a                hydrocarbon feed stream and oxygen and forming a                product gas stream that includes sum of hydrogen                and carbon mono oxide, steam generating boiler is                configured for cooling the product gas stream that                includes sum of hydrogen and carbon mono oxide and                forming steam; and
   (3) a method for producing hydrogen, which                involves reacting a hydrocarbon feed stream and                oxygen into a reactor unit to form a product gas                stream by using sum of hydrogen and carbon mono                oxide, passing the product gas stream that includes                sum of hydrogen and carbon mono oxide through a                steam generating boiler, adding steam to the                product gas stream that includes sum of hydrogen                and carbon mono oxide, converting the product gas                stream that includes sum of hydrogen and carbon                mono oxide in one reactor to form a stream,                processing stream cthat includes sum of hydrogen                and carbon mono oxide in a pressure swing adsorber                to provide a product stream formed of substantially                pure hydrogen and also provide waste gas steam                comprising carbon dioxide, forming a liquid carbon                dioxide product stream in a cryogenic separation                unit operating with auto-refrigeration by passing                the waste gas stream having carbon dioxide such                that 50 mol.% carbon dioxide in the waste gas                stream tha includes carbon dioxide which is                separated into the liquid carbon dioxide product                stream and recycling a vapor phase stream from the                cryogenic separation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The drying includes                  passage of the process stream that inlcudes                  carbon dioxide through a desiccant-packed bed.                  The cooling includes cooling the process stream                  against a portion of the liquid stream that is                  enriched with the carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Process: The method further incolves expanding                  the first portion of the liquid stream that is                  enriched with the carbon dioxide and the second                  portion of the liquid stream that is enriched                  with the carbon dioxide so as to reduce the                  temperature of the first portion of the liquid                  stream that is enriched with the carbon dioxide                  and the second portion of the liquid stream that                  is enriched with the carbon dioxide prior to                  cooling the process stream in the first heat                  exchanger against the first portion of the liquid                  stream that is enriched with the carbon dioxide                  and cooling the process stream in the second heat                  exchanger against the second portion of the                  liquid stream that is enriched with the carbon                  dioxide, expanding the two phase stream so as to                  reduce the temperature of the two phase stream to                  a temperature of 10 degrees C freezing point of                  the two phase stream. Preferred Conditions: The                  two phase stream is separated by passing the two                  phase stream through a distillation column. The                  distillation column includes a stripping section                  below a feed point of the two phase stream into                  the distillation column and rectifying section                  above the feed point of the two phase stream into                  the distillation column. The 60 mol.% hydrogen is                  recover in present in the vapor stream enriched                  with one of the one or more further components.                  The process stream is an hydrogen carbon dioxide                  stream from a hydrogen production process.

TI Device for capturing and cleaning carbon dioxide            generated by alcohol fermentation, has collecting tower            comprising collecting tower main body, where carbon            dioxide flowing upwards flows out through recycling            port and is received by washing tower.
AU HUANG H
   WEI J
   NONG Y
AE GUANGXI HAIYING ALCOHOL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B46583
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for                capturing and cleaning carbon dioxide generated by                alcohol fermentation, comprising: a collecting                tower for receiving the carbon dioxide generated by                the alcohol fermentation and a washing tower                connected with the collecting tower and used for                washing the carbon dioxide; wherein the collecting                tower comprises a collecting tower main body and a                collecting channel set in the collecting tower main                body, the side wall of the collecting tower main                body is provided with a first air inlet, the first                air inlet is set on the middle upper part of the                collecting tower main body, the first air inlet and                the alcohol fermenting tank communicate for                receiving the carbon dioxide after alcohol                fermentation; the first end of the collecting                channel is connected to the first air inlet, the                second end extends to the bottom of the collecting                tower main body; the top part of the collecting                tower main body is provided with a first recycling                port, the carbon dioxide flowing upwards flows out                through the first recycling port and is received by                the washing tower. The device of the utility model                can separate the carbon dioxide generated after                alcohol fermentation and the foam and material                liquid carried in carbon dioxide, so as to improve                the purity of carbon dioxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU LI Y
   ZHU C
   TAN H
   SHI J
   NIE Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202181297K
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a carbon dioxide chamber (7) that is provided on outside of a superconducting energy pipeline (3). The outer side of the carbon dioxide chamber is sequentially provided with a stacking insulation layer (8) and an outer wall surface (9) of the insulation structure. A carbon dioxide adsorption plate (4) adopts a copper sheet with a surface roughness between 5-10 mu m which is radially fixed on the superconducting energy pipeline and a layer of carbon dioxide adsorption paper is pasted on the surface of the copper sheet. The stacking insulation layer adopts polyisocyanurate (PIR) foam or other flame-retardant thermal insulation materials. The radiation shielding layer adopts aluminum foil material.
   USE - Insulation structure applied to long-distance superconducting energy pipeline.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-temperature fuel channel is opened to pre-cool the pipeline so that the carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide chamber is sublimated and the carbon dioxide chamber becomes vacuum chamber and then the superconducting cable is operated. The structure greatly reduce the construction and operation costs of energy pipeline.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the insulation structure applied to long-distance superconducting energy pipeline.

   Carbon dioxide adsorption plate (4)

   Carbon dioxide chamber (7)
   Stacking insulation layer (8)
   Outer wall surface (9)

PN KR2020076093-A; KR2273078-B1
TI Separation of carbon dioxide from gas-to-liquid synthesis process, involves passing carbon monoxide-removed mixed gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide through separation membrane to permeate and separate carbon dioxide.
AU GI M
   KWON O
   LEE Y C
   YEOUL L J
   LEE J H
   NAM J
   SUNGTEAJEA
   LIMJOOYOUNG
   HASUNGYONG
   LEE C
   LIM J H
AE KOREA GAS CORP (KOAS-C)
GA 202060647F
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of carbon dioxide from gas-to-liquid synthesis process involves supplying a mixed gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide to a separation membrane (M1) to permeate hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and separate carbon monoxide, and supplying the mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon to a separation membrane (M2) to permeate and separate carbon dioxide.
   USE - Separation of carbon dioxide from gas-to-liquid synthesis process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates carbon dioxide from gas-to-liquid synthesis process using two different separation membranes which can selectively separate a specific gas from the mixed gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide-separation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed gas permeated through membrane (M1) comprises 60-80% hydrogen and 10-15% carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide-separated gas comprises 6.5% or less carbon dioxide. The mixed gas before passing through membranes (M1, M2) has a ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide of 2-2.4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Membrane (M1) is made of at least one polymer chosen from polysulfone, polyethersulfone, and polyimide. Membrane (M2) is made of at least one polymer chosen from polydimethylsiloxane and polytrimethylsilylpropyne. Preferred Properties: Membrane (M1) has a hydrogen gas permeability and a carbon dioxide permeability of 200 gas permeance unit (GPU) (1x 10-6 cm3/cm2.second.cm mercury) or more. Membrane (M2) has a carbon dioxide permeability of 330 GPU (1x 10-6 cm3/cm2.second.cm mercury) or more.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. A first heat                exchanger (50) that exchanges heat between the                regenerated absorption liquid delivered from the                regeneration tower (31) and the carbon                dioxide-containing absorbing liquid delivered from                the absorption tower.

   ADVANTAGE - The double-effect chemical absorption type                carbon dioxide recovery system has extremely high                thermal efficiency.

   Regeneration tower (31)
   First heat exchanger (50)
   Regeneration absorption liquid cooler                (56)

PT P
PN BR102020018617-A2
TI Performing adsorption of carbon dioxide in polymeric nanoparticle to increase gas fixation rate in cultivation of microalgae and cyanobacteria involves developing polymeric nanoparticle for adsorption of carbon dioxide.
AU GREQUE DE MORAIS M
   PEREIRA VARGAS B
   VIEIRA COSTA J A
AE UNIV FEDERAL DO RIO GRANDE FURG (URGR-C)
GA 2021956257
AB    NOVELTY - Performing adsorption of carbon dioxide in polymeric nanoparticle to increase gas fixation rate in cultivation of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria involves developing polymeric nanoparticle for adsorption of carbon dioxide in cultivation of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria.
   USE - Method for performing adsorption of carbon dioxide in polymeric nanoparticle to increase gas fixation rate in cultivation of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The polymeric nanoparticle is developed with polyacrylonitrile polymer and dimethylformamide solvent. The nanoparticle is developed by electrospraying technique for adsorption of carbon dioxide. The nanoparticle is developed by combination of different polymers and solvents, which contain metallic nanoparticle in their composition. The electrospraying technique is performed by using an equipment having positive displacement pump, capillary and power supply with direct current of high electric potential. The electrospraying technique is performed with polymer solution having concentration of 0.5-1%, electric potential of 18kV, feed rate of 500 mu L per hour, distance between capillary and collector is 150mm, capillary diameter of 0.45mm, relative humidity of 45-60% and ambient temperature between 20-23 degrees C. The microalgae and/or cyanobacteria are of Chlorella, Chlorella kessleri strain LEB 113, Chlorella pyrenoidosa, Chlorella mannophila, Auxenochlorella protothecoide, Chlorella homosphaera, Stigmatophora, Gymnopilus luteoviridis, Chlorella ellipsoidea, Chlorella variegata, Chlorella sorokiniana, Chlorella emersonii, Chlorella minutissima, Spirulina species strain LEB 18, Spirulina platensis, Spirulina platensis paracas, Scenedesmus species, Scenedesmus bijugatus, Scenedesmus incrassatulus, Dunaliella species, Oscillatoria species, such as Oscillatoria limnetica, Oscillatoria curviceps and Oscillatoria splendida, Ankistrodesmus species, such as Ankistrodesmus densus, Ankistrodesmus braunii, Ankistrodesmus falcatus, Ankistrodesmus fusiformis and Ankistrodesmus gracilis, Skeletonema species, Chaetoceros species, Trichodermium, Microcoleus, Emiliania, Rhodobacter, Cyanobium, Nostoc muscorum, Synechoccus nidulans LEB 115, Synechocystis salina LEB 118, Selenastrum capricornutum LEB 119 and Jaaguinema geminatum LEB 120. The process is characterized by using carbon dioxide from gaseous effluents from the pulp, energy, chemical, petrochemical, metals, non-metallic minerals, food, technology, cosmetics and other sector capable of releasing carbon dioxide to environment. The process is characterized by arrangement of nanoparticle in free form in culture media in bioreactor or photobioreactor for cultivation of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria. The process is characterized by use of free nanoparticle in culture medium in vertical tubular photobioreactor, Erlenmeyer or raceways for cultivation of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria. The process is characterized by use of natural, artificial light or by absence of light in the crops. The process controls culture parameter, such as temperature, pH, lighting, dissolved oxygen, alkalinity and photoperiod. The process is characterized by use of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria together with nanomaterial for fixation of carbon dioxide and/or other harmful gases. The process is characterized by use of carbon dioxide adsorbent nanoparticles with transfer of gas to nanoparticles and subsequently of nanoparticles to culture medium, which provides greater contact between carbon dioxide and microalgae and/or cyanobacteria. The nanoparticles with high surface area per unit volume, high porosity and presence of basic functional groups are used that increases attraction by acid gasses such as carbon dioxide. The process is characterized by increasing rate of carbon dioxide fixation in microalgae and/or cyanobacteria, by increasing contact time of gas with culture medium, and by providing a greater amount of carbon dioxide to Calvin Benson cycle, which promotes the organification of the inorganic carbon source.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC A04-D02; A12-W11D; A12-W11L; A12-W14; D05-A04A

TI Gaseous carbon monoxide assisted steam huff and            puff anti-channeling and increasing injection,            comprises e.g. simultaneously injecting carbon dioxide            gas and foam gel into formation, and reacting carbon            dioxide gas reacts with sodium silicate to generate            silicic acid gel plugging agent.
AU HUANG X
   LOU S
   LI X
   LIU J
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202235356G
AB    NOVELTY - Gaseous carbon monoxide assisted steam huff                and puff anti-channeling and increasing injection,                comprises (1) taking carbon dioxide gas as the                inner phase, and taking the foam gel as the outer                phase wrapping the carbon dioxide gas,                simultaneously injecting carbon dioxide gas and                foam gel into the formation, so as to use the                obtained carbon dioxide gas foam gel plugging agent                to control the profile of the formation, and (2)                injecting the front liquid slug sodium silicate,                the intermediate isolation liquid slug and the rear                liquid carbon dioxide gas slug into the formation                alternately in turn, and in the formation, reacting                carbon dioxide gas reacts with sodium silicate to                generate silicic acid gel plugging agent, so as to                further fill the steam channeling channel.
   USE - The method is useful for gaseous carbon                monoxide assisted steam huff and puff                anti-channeling and increasing injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is reasonably designed and based on                reliable, reduces gas injection pressure, increases                injection gas volume, solves the problems of gas                channeling and no intake air during gaseous carbon                dioxide assisted huff and puff.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foam gel comprises                  1-1.5 wt.% organic gel, 0.3-0.5 wt.% foaming                  agent and water (remaining amount). The volume                  ratio of the foam gel the gaseous carbon dioxide                  is 1: 500-1: 600. The frontend liquid comprises                  sodium silicate aqueous solution with mass                  concentration of 6-8%. The modulus of the sodium                  silicate aqueous solution is 3-3.3.Preferred Method: In step (1), the injection                  amount of gaseous carbon dioxide is calculated                  according to the following formula as given in                  specification. The extrusion pressure for                  synchronously injecting carbon dioxide gas and                  foam gel the stratum is 5-7 MPa. The injection                  amount of the sodium silicate is calculated                  according to the following formula as given in                  specification. The intermediate isolation liquid                  is injected according to 1.5-2 times of the                  internal volume of the oil pipe. In step (2), the                  theoretical injection amount of the post-liquid                  carbon dioxide gas is calculated according to the                  amount of sodium silicate contained in the                  pre-liquid slug sodium silicate, multiplying the                  theoretical injection amount of the post-liquid                  carbon dioxide gas by the injection coefficient                  to obtain the actual injection amount of the                  post-liquid carbon dioxide gas, where the                  injection coefficient is 1.2-1.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic gel                  comprises phenolic gel chromium gel, zirconium                  gel and/or titanium gel. The foaming agent                  comprises petroleum sulfonate, dodecyl sodium                  sulfate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, sodium                  dodecyl sulfate, and/or nonyl phenol                  polyoxyethylene ether. The middle isolating                  liquid is water.
