TI Carbon-dioxide decomposition apparatus has catalyst holding unit which contains alkali silicic acid compound, carbon peeling mechanism which peels carbon produced on surface of catalyst, and carbon-dioxide-containing gas introducing unit.
AU KONDO J
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 202144122K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide decomposition apparatus comprises catalyst holding unit (11) which has catalyst holding region for accommodating alkali silicic acid compound used as catalyst for decomposing carbon dioxide by heating catalyst holding unit; carbon peeling mechanism in which carbon produced on surface of alkali silicic acid compound is made to peel from the surface; chamber (100) which accommodates catalyst holding region and carbon peeling mechanism inside and by which produced carbon and oxygen are discharged from a discharge port (109) while catalyst holding region is heated to a predetermined temperature by a heating installation (50); carbon-dioxide-containing gas introducing unit which introduces carbon-dioxide-containing gas with respect to catalyst holding region in the chamber; and dust collector which collects carbon discharged from the discharge port.
   USE - A carbon-dioxide decomposition apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide can be continuously decomposed by heat processing using alkali silicic acid compound as a catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the chamber portion of the carbon-dioxide decomposition apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Catalyst holding unit (11)
   Heating installation (50)
   Chamber (100)
   Taper portion (101)
   Discharge port (109)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chamber has a taper portion in which interior area spreads as it goes to perpendicular direction upward direction, and the catalyst holding region is provided under the taper portion. Carbon peeling mechanism is located in the edge portion of the perpendicular direction upward side of the catalyst holding region, and the edge portion of the perpendicular direction downward side has a pair of porous component provided with the hole which has a hole diameter smaller than the average particle diameter of the particles of the alkali silicic acid compound. In the catalyst holding region, a fluidized-bed of the alkali silicic acid compound is formed by the carbon-dioxide-containing gas introduced. Volumes of the catalyst holding region are 2-100 times of the volume of the alkali silicic acid compound that the catalyst holding region accommodates. By the carbon-dioxide-containing gas, the particles of the alkali silicic acid compound collide with the porous component located in the perpendicular direction upward side. Particles of the alkali silicic acid compound that disperse from the catalyst holding region are made to settle to the catalyst holding region by the taper-shape of the taper portion. Catalyst holding region further includes crystalline fiber comprising alumina and silica as main component, amorphous substance comprising alumina and silica as main component, or silica wool. Catalyst holding region further contains silica wool which functions as the carbon peeling mechanism. At the catalyst holding region, the alkali silicic acid compound is maintained in the silica wool, each being located in the edge portion of the upstream side and the downstream side of the flow direction of the gas in the alkali silicic acid compound. At the catalyst holding region, the alkali silicic acid compound is maintained in the state with which the particles and the silica wool of the alkali silicic acid compound are mixed. The catalyst holding region is maintained at the chamber inner portion by a pair of porous component provided with the hole which has a hole diameter smaller than the average particle diameter of the particles of the alkali silicic acid compound. The catalyst holding region further includes crystalline fiber which has alumina and silica as main component or amorphous fiber which has alumina and silica as main component. Flow direction of the gas in the chamber is a direction which goes to upward direction from the perpendicular direction downward direction, and taper portion in which an interior area spreads is provided in the upstream side of the chamber as it goes to the perpendicular direction upward direction. The catalyst holding region is provided in the site applicable to the taper portion. The particles of the alkali silicic acid compound that disperse from the catalyst holding region are made to settle to the catalyst holding region by the taper-shape of the taper portion. Preferred Devices: Heating installation is a horizontal-type trough-type solar furnace which heats an object by condensing sunlight; and linear condensing portion in which trough-type solar furnace is maintained horizontally, is provided with the catalyst holding region. According to the heating capability of the heating installation, the flow volume of the carbon-dioxide-containing gas introduced from the carbon-dioxide-containing gas introducing unit is controlled. A water-removal device for removing moisture content from carbon-dioxide-containing gas is provided in the upstream side of the carbon-dioxide-containing gas introducing unit.

   YANG P

AE GUANGDONG XINGLIAN PRECISE MACHINERY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202137311J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves weighing a total weight of                a bottle cap, and injecting proper amount of carbon                dioxide into the bottle. AU KIM K
   KANG I O
   SEOK S Y
   YEUP L T
   KIM J
   HAN J
AE KOREA GAS CORP (KOAS-C)
GA 202120836H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves producing gas by performing pilot production process by a production well of a coal bed methane gas (CBM) reservoir. Content ratio of methane gas and carbon dioxide gas is measured. Langmuir experiments are performed on a core drilled in a reservoir. Langmuir pressure constants and Langmeyer volume constant are determined. Adsorption amount of carbon dioxide in the coal bed methane reservoir is analyzed by using the content ratio of the methane gas and the carbon dioxide gas and amount of methane gas or carbon dioxide adsorption of the core. Corrosiveness of the production well is evaluated by Dewaard process after analyzing the adsorption amount of carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for evaluating gas composition in a coal seam methane gas reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating total amount of the carbon dioxide in a storage layer so as to solve loss gas identification and free gas targeting problems, thus improving calculation precision of the amount of the carbon dioxide gas in the storage layer.

   USE - Catalyst is used for converting carbon dioxide (claimed) into synthetic fuel, such as carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has relatively high structural stability even in repeated oxidation-reduction cycles, and excellent long-term stability and carbon dioxide decomposition activity at relatively low temperature, and enables economical, efficient and large-scale conversion of carbon dioxide into synthetic fuel with high selectivity, and reduced electrical energy consumption, and without using additional equipment, such as membrane separation facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst comprises a strontium-iron-cobalt oxide of formula: SrFeCo1-xOy (I).
   x = 0 to less than 1;and
   y = 2-4.

Preferred Process: The conversion of carbon dioxide involves preparing two quartz tubes (t1,t2) each comprising an inlet and an outlet, selecting the catalyst, injecting the catalyst into the tubes (t1,t2), activating the catalyst by connecting a reducing gas supply pipe to the inlet of the tube (t1) and a reducing gas recovery pipe to the outlet of the tube (t2) and performing heat-treatment at 100-1000 degrees C, inducing carbon dioxide conversion reaction by connecting a gas supply pipe comprising carbon dioxide to the inlet of the tube (t2) and a gas recovery pipe comprising carbon dioxide conversion reactant to the outlet of the tube (t2) and performing heat-treatment at 300-800 degrees C, preferably 600-700 degrees C, respectively replacing the gas supply pipe and the gas recovery pipe connected to the tube (t1) with the gas supply pipe and the gas recovery pipe connected to the tube (t2), and periodically repeating the replacement of the gas supply pipes and the gas recovery pipes with each other. The replacement of the gas supply pipe and the gas recovery pipe involves exchanging the heat-treatment temperatures of the tubes (t1,t2).

AU INUI Y

AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 201951426C
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide gas generation agent                comprises the reaction product of carbon dioxide,                and a mixture containing an amine polymer                containing an amino group in a repeating unit, and                an alcohol compound.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas generation agent is used in                nonaqueous electrolyte storage battery (claimed)                e.g. lithium-ion secondary battery, sodium ion                secondary battery, lithium-sulfur secondary                battery, electrical double layer capacitor, and                lithium ion capacitor, used for notebook personal                computer and electric vehicle. Can also be used for                foaming agents, fire extinguishers, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas generation agent is                capable of suppressing thermal runaway of                nonaqueous electrolyte storage battery by quickly                generating high capacity carbon dioxide at the time                of overcharge, and improves safety of battery. PT P
PN US2018142365-A1; JP2018141227-A; JP6931769-B2
TI Reducing carbon dioxide electrochemically to generate ethylene selectively includes preparing electrolysis device having e.g. cathode and anode containers and cathode and anode electrodes and applying voltage to cathode and anode electrodes.
AU KUSAMA S
   YOTSUHASHI S
   SAKAI A
   HASHIBA H
   OKAMOTO S
   SATO H
   NAKAJIMA K
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (MATU-C)
GA 2018412052
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide electrochemically to generate ethylene selectively, comprises: (a) preparing an electrolysis device comprising a cathode container, an anode container, a cathode electrode, an anode electrode, and a solid electrolysis membrane; and (b) applying a voltage to the cathode electrode and the anode electrode in such a manner that an electric potential of the cathode electrode is negative with regard to an electric potential of the anode electrode to generate ethylene selectively due to electrochemical reduction of the carbon dioxide on the cathode electrode.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing carbon dioxide electrochemically to generate ethylene selectively.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the electrolysis device that reduces the carbon dioxide electrochemically to generate ethylene selectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing carbon dioxide electrochemically to generate ethylene selectively, comprises: (a) preparing an electrolysis device comprising a cathode container, an anode container, a cathode electrode, an anode electrode, and a solid electrolysis membrane, where a first electrolysis solution is stored in the cathode container, the first electrolysis solution comprises the carbon dioxide, a second electrolysis solution is stored in the anode container, the cathode electrode is in contact with the first electrolysis solution, the cathode electrode comprises a carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises a crystalline copper phthalocyanines, the anode electrode is in contact with the second electrolysis solution and the cathode container and the anode container are separated from each other with the solid electrolysis membrane; and (b) applying a voltage to the cathode electrode and the anode electrode in such a manner that an electric potential of the cathode electrode is negative with regard to an electric potential of the anode electrode to generate ethylene selectively due to electrochemical reduction of the carbon dioxide on the cathode electrode. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an electrolysis device (100) for generating ethylene selectively due to reduction of carbon dioxide electrochemically, comprising a cathode container (12), an anode container (15), a cathode electrode (13), an anode electrode (17), and a solid electrolysis membrane, where a first electrolysis solution is stored in the cathode container, the first electrolysis solution contains the carbon dioxide, a second electrolysis solution is stored in the anode container, the cathode electrode is in contact with the first electrolysis solution, the cathode electrode comprises a carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises a crystalline copper phthalocyanines, the anode electrode is in contact with the second electrolysis solution and the cathode container and the anode container are separated from each other with the solid electrolysis membrane;
   (2) a carbon dioxide reduction electrode capable of converting carbon dioxide into ethylene selectively by application of a voltage, comprising crystalline copper phthalocyanines; and
   (3) a carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprising the crystalline copper phthalocyanine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the electrolysis device.
   Cathode container (12)
   Cathode electrode (13)
   Anode container (15)
   Anode electrode (17)
   Electrolysis device (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode electrode is formed of a material comprising carbon, platinum, gold, silver, copper, titanium, iridium oxide or its alloy. The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst further comprises carbon black, where the crystalline copper phthalocyanine is mixed with the carbon black.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The electrolysis device further comprises a reference electrode disposed in the cathode container, where the reference electrode is in contact with the first electrolysis solution and the reference electrode has a region of silver/silver chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least a part of the crystalline copper phthalocyanine is: alpha -type crystalline or beta -type crystalline; 1,2,3,4,8,9,10,11,15,16,17,18,22,23,24,25-hexadecafluorophthalocyanine copper crystal; 2,3,9,10,16,17,23,24-octafluorophthalocyanine copper crystal; 2,9,16,23-tetra-tert-butyl phthalocyanine copper crystal; or 5,9,14,18,23,27,32,36-octabutoxy-2,3-naphthalocyanine copper crystal.
   EXAMPLE - 150 mg Carbon black and 66 mg of copper phthalocyanine beta -type crystalline powders were dispersed in a first solvent consisting of N,N-dimethylformamide. Then, an ultrasonic wave was applied to the dispersion liquid. N,N-dimethylformamide was removed using a rotary evaporator. In this way, a crystalline copper phthalocyanine-carbon black hybrid catalyst was provided. The copper phthalocyanine content contained in the crystalline copper phthalocyanine-carbon black hybrid catalyst was 44% by weight ratio. 0.5 mol/l Potassium chloride aqueous solution (first electrolyte solution) was bubbled for 60 minutes with a carbon dioxide gas supplied through a pipe at a carbon dioxide supply rate of 125 cm3/minute. The carbon dioxide gas was dissolved in the first electrolyte solution. Then, the cathode container was sealed. Voltage was applied between the anode electrode and the cathode electrode using a potentiostat in such a manner that the electric potential of the cathode electrode was negative with regard to the electric potential of the anode electrode. The value of the applied voltage was controlled with the potentiostat in such a manner that the electric potential of the cathode electrode with regard to the reference electrode was -1.6 volts. After the voltage was applied for 10000 seconds, the type and the amount of reaction products generated in the cathode container were measured with gas chromatography and liquid chromatography to obtain 41% generation ratio of ethylene by reducing carbon dioxide on the cathode electrode using the crystalline copper phthalocyanine-carbon black hybrid catalyst.

PN CN111717915-A; CN111717915-B
TI Method of increasing carbon dioxide capture rate in gas produced from carbon dioxide flooding in oil fields, involves feed gas pre-cooling, flash separating feed gas, low temperature stripping, and recovering tower overhead gas.
AU WANG C
   LIU T
   WU Q
   YAN G
   LIN M
   WANG S
   HAN J
   CHENG S
   DUAN S
   YU P
   CHEN H
   ZHANG G
   WANG J
   HAO M
   LUO Q
AE SINOPEC OILFIELD SERVICE CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC PETROLEUM ENG CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2020985052
AB    NOVELTY - Method of increasing carbon dioxide capture rate in produced gas from carbon dioxide flooding in oil fields involves feed gas pre-cooling, carbon dioxide cooling, flash separating feed gas, low temperature stripping, and recovering tower overhead gas.
   USE - Method of increasing carbon dioxide capture rate in gas produced from carbon dioxide flooding in oil fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed under reduced venting, has improved carbon dioxide recovery rate, and achieves cold energy recovery and utilization, and recycling of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for increasing carbon dioxide capture rate, which comprises feed gas pre-cooler, carbon dioxide cooler, flash tank, stripping tower, bottom reboiler, top cooler, top gas separator, top gas compressor, and compression outlet cooler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably involves passing raw material gas through bottom reboiler while reducing temperature of raw gas to 8 degrees C, passing to pre-cooler, pre-cooling to 3-4 degrees C, passing to carbon dioxide cooler, cooling to -18 degrees C to -20 degrees C, so that the raw material gas is partially condensed, passing partially condensed feed gas to flash tank, flash separating, passing flash vapor to overhead gas recovery process, passing liquid phase from flash tank to stripper, purifying to obtain liquid carbon dioxide product with carbon dioxide content of more than 99%, throttling and cooling separated liquid phase, passing to tower top cooler, cooling, passing to pre-cooler to exchange heat with raw gas, passing gas to top of tower after the temperature rises, pressurizing compressor to the inlet pressure of the raw gas, cooling by the outlet cooler, merging with the raw, throttling gas phase of the separator, reducing pressure and temperature, passing to cold box, exchanging heat with the outlet gas of the overhead gas compressor to increase temperature, and passing to fuel gas system as fuel. The recovery of tower top gas preferably involves merging top gas of the stripping tower with the gas phase of the flash tank, cooling to -45 degrees C to -42 degrees C by the top cooler, passing to top gas separator, throttling, and cooling separated liquid phase to -50 degrees C to -48 degrees C, passing to tower top cooler to provide cooling, exchanging heat to -35 degrees C to -32 degrees C, passing to raw material gas pre-cooler to exchange heat with the raw material gas to increase to 0 degrees C-2 degrees C, passing to overhead gas compressor to pressurize to the feed gas inlet pressure, cooling to 50 degrees C by the compression outlet cooler, merging with the raw material gas, where gas phase of the overhead gas separator comprises 32-35% carbon dioxide, and balance hydrocarbon gas, throttling and reducing pressure, reducing temperature to -69 degrees C to -65 degrees C, passing to top of the tower, heating to -25 degrees C to -22 degrees C, exchanging heat with the gas at the outlet of the tower top gas compressor to increase the temperature to 10-13 degrees C, and using as fuel gas for self-use in the station. Preferred Composition: The feed gas comprises more than 70% carbon dioxide, and less than 200 ppm water, and has pressure of more than 3 MPa. Preferred Component: The liquid phase separated from the flash tank comprises heavy hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide.

TI Processing a carbon dioxide used for converting            carbon dioxide into products e.g., acetic acid or            carbon monoxide by contacting carbon dioxide with            catalyst in presence of hydrogen in reactor to convert            carbon dioxide to e.g., acetic acid.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
   OLAYIWOLA B
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 2021356513
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide (106) is processed by                contacting carbon dioxide with catalyst (104) in                presence of hydrogen in a reactor (102) to convert                carbon dioxide to acetic acid and carbon monoxide;                discharging a product effluent from reactor to a                condenser heat exchanger, where the product                effluent comprises acetic acid, carbon monoxide,                and water, and where the condenser (114) comprises                a condenser heat exchanger or a quench tower; and                condensing the acetic acid and the water in the                condenser.
   USE - Processing a carbon dioxide used for                converting carbon dioxide into products (claimed)                e.g., acetic acid or carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process converts carbon dioxide emissions                from steam cracking systems or ODH reactor systems                into desirable products, such as acetic acid or                carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (100) to convert carbon dioxide (106) into                products, comprising a reactor (102) comprising                catalyst (104) to convert carbon dioxide in                presence of hydrogen into acetic acid and carbon                monoxide, and discharge a product effluent                comprising acetic acid, carbon monoxide, water, and                unreacted carbon dioxide; and a condenser (114) to                receive the product effluent and condense acetic                acid and water, where the condenser discharges a                liquid product stream comprising acetic acid and                water and a gas product stream comprising carbon                monoxide and unreacted carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                reactor system having a reactor with catalyst for                conversion of carbon dioxide into acetic acid or                carbon monoxide.
   System (100)
   Reactor (102)
   Catalyst (104)
   Carbon dioxide (106)
   Condenser (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method comprises                  providing a feed to reactor, where the feed                  comprises the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen.                  The method comprises heating the feed upstream of                  the reactor. Heating the feed comprises heating                  the feed in a heat exchanger upstream of the                  reactor. Contacting carbon dioxide with the                  catalyst in presence of hydrogen in the reactor                  also converts carbon dioxide to ethane and                  ethylene, and where the product effluent further                  comprises ethane and ethylene. The method                  comprises feeding water to the reactor, where                  selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide in                  the reactor favors acetic acid over carbon                  monoxide, ethane, and ethylene. The method                  comprises feeding water as steam to the reactor,                  where selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide                  in the reactor favors acetic acid over carbon                  monoxide, ethane, and ethylene. The method                  comprises not feeding water to the reactor, where                  selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide in                  the reactor favors carbon monoxide over acetic                  acid, ethane, and ethylene. The method comprises                  maintaining an operating temperature of the                  reactor at less than or equal to 400 degrees C,                  where the catalyst comprises oxidative                  dehydrogenation (ODH) catalyst. Preferred                  Components: The feed comprises water, and where                  selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide in                  the reactor favors acetic acid over carbon                  monoxide. The feed does not comprise water, and                  where selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide                  in the reactor favors carbon monoxide over acetic                  acid. Selectivity of conversion of carbon dioxide                  in the reactor favors carbon monoxide over acetic                  acid, ethane, and ethylene in absence of feeding                  water to the reactor. The catalyst comprises                  oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) catalyst, and                  where the product effluent further comprises                  unreacted carbon dioxide and unreacted hydrogen.                  The reactor comprises a fixed-bed reactor having                  the catalyst in a fixed bed. The catalyst                  comprises a mixed metal oxide having formula                  MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf.a=1;b,c,d=0.01-1;e=0-0.1; andf=number to satisfy the valence state of                  corresponding elements in the catalyst.Preferred Devices: The condenser comprises a                  shell-and-tube heat exchanger. The system                  comprises a recycle conduit to convey greater                  than or equal to 1 portion of gas product stream                  to the reactor. The system comprises a preheater                  to heat feed to the reactor, where the feed                  comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The                  preheater comprises a heat exchanger. The heat                  exchanger comprises a shell-and-tube heat                  exchanger.

GA 201930702K
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide separation                portion (41,61) which separates carbon dioxide from                the external air supplied to object space (5). A                gas feed route (135) includes the                highly-concentrated-gas feed route (136) for carbon                dioxide connected from the carbon dioxide                separation portion to the object space. A                controller (110) performs the carbon dioxide                concentration ascending movement which supplies the                high carbon dioxide gas whose carbon dioxide level                is higher than the external air before a process in                the carbon dioxide separation portion through the                highly-concentrated-gas feed route to the object                space.
   USE - Air composition adjusting device used as air                conditioner in warehouse, for adjusting composition                such as oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide                level of air in warehouse for accommodating                agricultural products such as plant, fruit such as                banana and avocado, vegetables, grain, bulb, and                fresh flower.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide levels of the air of object                space can be carried out using a separation                membrane in high concentration than the carbon                dioxide levels in air. The high                carbon-dioxide-levels gas or low                carbon-dioxide-levels gas can selectively be                supplied to the object space by switching the                switching device.

TI Absorbing carbon dioxide from gas stream            containing carbon dioxide by contacting gas stream with            absorbent comprising aqueous composition which contains            substituted heteroaromatic compound with nitrogen atoms            in heteroaromatic ring.
AU PUXTY G D
   CONWAY W
   YANG Q
   FERON P H M
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2018948569
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is absorbed from a gas stream                containing carbon dioxide by contacting gas stream                with absorbent comprising aqueous composition which                contains substituted heteroaromatic compound                comprising six-membered heteroaromatic ring having                1-3 nitrogen atoms in the heteroaromatic ring and                greater than or equal to 1 primary amine                substituents.
   USE - Absorption of carbon dioxide from a gas stream                containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The substituted heteroaromatic compound of the                composition provides a high cyclic capacity for                carbon dioxide, allowing efficient capture and                desorption of carbon dioxide while also providing a                low vapor pressure in aqueous solution and good                solubility of the substituted heteroaromatic                compound and species formed on absorption of carbon                dioxide. The aqueous composition may be used                without a requirement for specific co-solvents and                provides flexibility in formulating aqueous                compositions for carbon dioxide capture and                release. The long term stability of the substituted                heteroaromatic compound during carbon dioxide                capture is enhanced in the presence of a further                amine of higher basicity than the substituted                heteroaromatic compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Primary amine substituents are represented by                formula -R1NH2.
   R1=1-6C alkylene or ethers of formula                -R2-O-R3-; and
   R2, R3=1-3C alkylene.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) composition of adsorbed carbon dioxide                comprising aqueous solvent, greater than or equal                to 1 aqueous solution of absorbent compound for                carbon dioxide, and absorbed carbon dioxide which                is at a concentration above the equilibrium                concentration when the solution is exposed to air                at below the boiling point of the solvent;                and
   (2) use of aqueous solution of a substituted                heteroaromatic compound in capturing carbon dioxide                in a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Substituted                  heteroaromatic compound has structure of formula                  (I). Substituted heteroaromatic compound is                  selected from formulae (Ia)-(Id) or their                  mixtures, preferably formula (Ia). Substituted                  heteroaromatic compound is selected from formulae                  (IIa)-(IIc) or their mixtures. Substituted                  heteroaromatic compound is selected from                  2-(aminomethyl)pyridine, 3-(aminomethy)lpyridine,                  and 4-(aminomethyl)pyridine, preferably                  3-(aminomethyl)pyridine. Concentration of                  substituted heteroaromatic compound is 1-80 wt.%,                  preferably 10-80 wt.% of the aqueous solution.                  Substituted heteroaromatic compound is dissolved                  in the aqueous solution in an amount of greater                  than or equal to 20 wt.% of the water content of                  the aqueous solution. Aqueous composition                  comprises additional amine selected from                  tert.-amines and/or hindered amine, having a pKa                  greater than that of the substituted                  heteroaromatic compound, preferably a pKa of                  greater than or equal to 8.85 at 25 degrees C.                  Aqueous composition comprises carbon dioxide                  absorbent selected from imidazole and                  N-functionalised imidazole. Weight ratio of                  substituted heteroaromatic compound to further                  absorbent is 1:10-10:1. Water content of the                  aqueous solution is greater than or equal to 10                  wt.%, preferably greater than or equal to 20                  wt.%. Absorbent comprises 10-60 wt.%                  (aminomethyl)pyridine comprising                  2-(aminomethyl)pyridine, 3-(aminomethyl)pyridine,                  and/or 4-(aminomethyl)pyridine; 10-60 wt.% amine                  selected from tert.-amines and/or sterically                  hindered primary and secondary amines; and 20-80                  wt.% water. Amine selected from tert.-amines and                  primary and secondary sterically hindered amines                  is selected from 2-amino-1-propanol,                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, piperidine,                  2-piperidinylmethanol, 3-piperidinylmethanol,                  4-piperidinylmethanol, 2-piperidinylethanol,                  4-piperidinylethanol, and/or                  2-(dimethylamino)ethanol, preferably                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol.X=N or CR, provided that greater than or                  equal to 3 X groups are CR;R=1-4C alkyl, OH, OH(1-4C alkyl), 1-4C                  alkoxy, 1-4C alkoxy(1-4C alkyl), provided that                  greater than or equal to 1 R group is                  -R1'-NH2;R1'=R1 or 1-6C oxyalkylene;R4=optional C substituent selected from 1-4C                  alkyl, OH, OH(1-4C alkyl), 1-4C alkoxy, 1-4C                  alkoxy(1-4C alkyl);n=0, 1, or 2; andR1''=1-4C alkylene.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R1', R1''=methylene.

TI New amine ethyl piperazine used as carbon dioxide absorbent for trapping carbon dioxide in power plant flue gas, refinery tail gas, steelworks tail gas, cement plant tail gas, lithiation plant tail gas, water gas, marsh gas.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG Y
   LV X
   CHEN S
   LIU A
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021B89267
AB    NOVELTY - Amine ethylpiperazine compound (I) is new.
   USE - Amine ethyl piperazine of formula (I) used as carbon dioxide absorbent for trapping carbon dioxide in gas, where gas contains carbon dioxide comprises power plant flue gas, refinery tail gas, steelworks tail gas, cement plant tail gas, lithiation plant tail gas, water gas, marsh gas, natural gas or carbonate ore decomposition gas (all claimed), and in technical field of carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The amine ethyl piperazine has good thermal stability, and excellent trapping performance for trapping carbon dioxide recycling performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amine ethylpiperazine compound of formula (I) is new. R2 is C1 to C4 alkyl, amine derivative of formula (VI) or R1, R3 and R4 are independently C1 to C4 alkyl group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1)a method for preparing amine ethyl piperazine of formula (I);
   (2) a carbon dioxide absorbent, which comprises 10-50 wt.% amine ethyl piperazine of formula (I); solvent which comprises water, or organic solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The method for preparing amine ethyl piperazine of formula (I) involves providing R2 which is C1 to C4 alkyl then preparation method comprises mixing chloro piperazine derivative of formula (II), amine derivative of formula (III) and water; carrying out substitution reaction to obtain amine ethyl piperazine where R1 is C1 to C4 alkyl; when R2 is then preparation method comprises mixing compound 3 of formula (IV), compound 4 of formula (V) and water, then carrying out substitution reaction to obtain amine ethyl piperazine, where R1, R3 and R4 are independently C1 to C4 alkyl.Preferred Conditions: The R1 is methyl, R2 is methyl, amine derivative of formula (VI) then preparation method of amine ethyl piperazine involves mixing tri (2-chloro-ethyl) amine hydrochloride methylamine and water, carrying out substitution reaction to obtain amine ethyl piperazine. The mol ratio of chloro piperazine derivative of formula (II), amine derivative of formula (III) is 1:5 to 30; temperature of substitution reaction is 50-80degrees Celsius; time is 8-16 hours. The mol ratio of compound 3 of formula (IV), compound 4 of formula (V) is 1:1 to 1:5; the temperature of substitution reaction is 50-80degrees Celsius; time is 6-12 hours.
   Preferred Definitions: C1 to C4 alkyl is methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl or isobutyl.

PN CN109174017-A; CN109174017-B
TI Solid amine adsorbent used in carbon dioxide            adsorption component for adsorbing carbon dioxide,            comprises hollow fiber and organic amine loaded on            inner surface of hollow fiber.
AU TAN X
   ZHANG Y
   ZHU H
   WANG C
   SHENG J
   CHEN Z
   GU X
   XIE D
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   SHANGHAI SUISHAN IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 201909499A
AB    NOVELTY - Solid amine adsorbent comprises hollow fiber                and organic amine loaded on inner surface of the                hollow fiber.
   USE - The solid amine adsorbent is useful in a                carbon dioxide adsorption component for adsorbing                carbon dioxide, preferably carbon dioxide having                concentration of less than 1% (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves surface area of carbon                dioxide adsorption reaction and increases removal                rate of carbon dioxide significantly. The hollow                fiber has a convenient and adjustable pore                structure, a wider range of optional organic amines                and can accommodate introducing groups of different                structures and different molecular sizes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the solid amine                adsorbent involving loading an organic amine                solution on the inner surface of the hollow fiber,                preferably the inner surface of a finger-shaped                porous layer of the hollow fiber and removing                solvent to obtain the solid amine adsorbent;                and
   (2) a carbon dioxide adsorption component                comprising at least one solid amine adsorbent,                preferably 3-20 solid amine adsorbents.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is                  present in the form of a film. The hollow fiber                  inner layer is the finger-shaped porous layer and                  outer layer is a spongy micro-porous layer. The                  organic amine comprises one or a combination of                  at least two of triethylenetetramine,                  tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine,                  polyethyleneimine, gamma                  -aminopropyltriethoxysilane and                  diethylenetriamine. The solvent is one or a                  combination of at least two of methanol, ethanol                  or toluene. Preferred Composition: The organic                  amine solution has a mass percent concentration                  of 9-60 %mass, preferably 10-30 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hollow fiber inner                  layer is the finger-shaped porous layer and outer                  layer is a spongy micro-porous layer. Preferred                  Method: The method involves preparing the hollow                  fiber by a phase conversion method by (i)                  dissolving a preparation raw material in an                  organic solvent to obtain a homogeneous solution                  and (ii) degassing, spinning, solidifying and                  sintering the homogeneous solution to obtain the                  hollow fiber, where the preparation raw material                  package of the hollow fiber comprises any one of                  polysulfone, polyethersulfone, polyetherimide,                  aluminum oxide or yttria-stabilized zirconia. The                  method involves carrying out degassing in a                  vacuum drying oven at 0.01-0.1 MPa and spinning                  at a gas pressure of 0.01-4 MPa. The method                  involves removing the solvent by drying at 40-80                  degrees C for 1-3 hours. The carbon dioxide                  adsorption component is prepared by (i) taking at                  least one solid amine adsorbent and sealing one                  end of the adsorbent using a modified acrylate                  adhesive and (ii) bonding and fixing an unsealed                  end of the adsorbent using an adhesive to form a                  bundle and connecting the unsealed end of the                  bundle with a quartz tube to obtain the carbon                  dioxide adsorption component, where the quartz                  tube is used for introducing mixed gas of carbon                  dioxide to be removed.

   USE - Reduction of carbon dioxide used for manufacturing methane, methanol, formic acid, ethane, ethanol, ethylene glycol, acetic acid, glycolic acid, glyoxal, glyoxylic acid, oxalic acid, carbon monoxide and hydrogen used for fuel, electric power, and town gas used in transportation application.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reduction of carbon dioxide involves contacting carbon dioxide in solution containing reduced form, adding electroconductive substance which forms reaction zone of reduction reaction, alkali which adjusts the pH of solution, and electron donor which supplies carbon dioxide with electron to the solution and further involves generating the reduced form from oxidant of the metal ion by an electrochemical reduction reaction. PN CN111498873-A
TI Carbonate roasting method to extract lithium carbonate from lepidolite by mixing lepidolite and defluorinating additive, boil roasting defluorinating, mixing and sintering with carbonate and carbon powder, and solid and liquid separating.
AU YANG C
   XIONG X
   ZHONG W
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202077236C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonate roasting method to extract lithium carbonate from lepidolite involves mixing lepidolite and defluorinating additive for preparing granulation, introducing water vapor for boil roasting defluorinating to obtain defluorinated lepidolite, mixing and sintering defluorinated lepidolite, carbonate and carbon powder to obtain sintered clinker, generating carbon dioxide exhaust gas, leaching sintered clinker, separating solid and liquid to obtain lithium-enriched leaching mother liquor, purifying carbon dioxide exhaust gas by water counter-current spraying, introducing into leaching mother liquor, and performing carbonatation for depositing lithium to obtain battery grade lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for carbonate roasting for extracting lithium carbonate from lepidolite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is clean and environment-friendly, improves defluorinating efficiency of lepidolite, relieves device fouling problem in sulfate sintering process, uses escaped carbon dioxide for leaching lithium carbonate, reduces production cost, and destroys silicon dioxide net-like structure making silicon dioxide in free state and easier for fluorine to escape with water vapor through mixing and roasting lepidolite and defluorinating additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbonate roasting system to extract lithium carbonate from lepidolite comprising orderly connected boiling roasting furnace, rotary kiln, leaching groove and carbon precipitation lithium groove. The top portion of rotary kiln is connected with bottom of carbon precipitation lithium groove through gas pipeline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The defluorination additive comprises calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium hydroxide and/or magnesium hydroxide. The mass ratio of lepidolite and defluorinated additive is 1:0.05-0.2. The carbonate comprises magnesium carbonate, manganese carbonate, calcium carbonate and/or ferrous carbonate. The mass ratio of defluorinated lepidolite to carbonate is 1:0.2-0.6. The leaching reagent is water. The mass ratio of water to sintered clinker is 2-5:1. Preferred Method: The method involves mixing lepidolite and defluorinating additive for preparing granulation, introducing water vapor for boil roasting defluorinating at 550-900 degrees C for 30-90 minutes at water vapor flow rate of 0.003-0.02 m3/hour each ton of lepidolite to obtain defluorinated lepidolite, mixing and sintering defluorinated lepidolite, carbonate and carbon powder at 700-900 degrees C for 30-180 minutes to obtain sintered clinker, generating carbon dioxide exhaust gas, leaching sintered clinker at 40-90 degrees C for 60-180 minutes, separating solid and liquid to obtain lithium-enriched leaching mother liquor, purifying carbon dioxide exhaust gas by water counter-current spraying, introducing into leaching mother liquor, and performing carbonatation for depositing lithium at 60-95 degrees C for 2-8 hours.

A foaming kettle (21) has a carbon dioxide inlet, a second feed port and a second discharge port. The second feed port is provided in communication with the first discharge port of the prepreg kettle. The carbon dioxide enters the foaming kettle through the carbon dioxide inlet. A collector (3) is arranged at a distance from the second discharge port to form a gap between the foamed particles and the outside so that the foamed particles from the second discharge port are instantaneously foamed at the gap and then enter the collector.

TI Hard surface cleaning composition having specific            pH comprises carbon dioxide, ionic acrylic based            rheology modifier i.e. hydrophobically-modified            swellable emulsion polymer, pH adjuster, surfactant,            alkyl benzene sulfonic acid and water.
AU CUILLERET M
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 201896904T
AB    NOVELTY - Hard surface cleaning composition comprises:                carbon dioxide; an ionic acrylic based rheology                modifier comprising at least one of an alkali                swellable emulsion polymer and a                hydrophobically-modified alkali swellable emulsion                polymer; a pH adjuster; a surfactant; at least one                of an alkyl benzene sulfonic acid and an alkyl                benzene sulfonic acid salt; water; a cosolvent;                and, optionally, a pH color indicator, where the                composition has a pH of 3.5-6.
   USE - The composition is useful for cleaning hard                surface comprising toilet bowls, sinks, showers,                kitchen and toilet tiles, glass countertops and                splash surfaces, preferably vertical                surfaces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                cleaning a hard surface, comprising applying the                composition onto the hard surface forming a foam,                and letting the foam remain on the hard surface,                where the carbon dioxide evolves from the foam                resulting in a change in the pH of the foam over                time, and the change in the pH triggers a                transition of the foam from an initial color upon                application to the hard surface to a transitioned                color, where the initial color and the transitioned                color are different, and the transitioned color                provides a visual cue that cleaning is                occurring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition does                  not contain both monoethanolamine and diethylene                  glycol monobutyl ether. The composition further                  comprises an additive consisting of a salt, a                  builder, a fragrance, an enzyme, a corrosion                  inhibitor, a chelant, an acid and/or a bleaching                  agent. The composition contains: less than 0.5                  wt.% of sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate                  in aggregate; less than 0.005 wt.% of a chelating                  agent; less than 0.05 wt.% of n-butane; and less                  than 0.005 wt.% of a polysulfonic acid consisting                  of methanedisulfonic acid, ethanedisulfonic acid,                  propanedisulfonic acid or 1,3,6-naphthalene                  trisulfonic acid in combination with a foaming                  agent consisting of alkyl sulfates, alkyl                  sulfonates, amine oxides or alkanolamides. The                  composition is provided in a single component                  system. The ionic acrylic based rheology modifier                  comprises is of formula (I).R1, R2 = H or 1-6C alkyl; andR3, R4 = H or 1-24C alkyl or alkoxylated                  alkyl.
   EXAMPLE - Typical hard surface cleaning composition                comprised of: 5 wt.% Acusol 835 (RTM: Alkali                swellable emulsion polymer) as ionic acrylic based                rheology modifier; 1.04 wt.% Nansa SSA/F (RTM:                Alkyl benzene sulfonic acid/sulfonate) ; 1 wt.%                Ecosurf EH6 (RTM: Alcohol ethoxylate surfactant); 1                wt.% Dowanol (RTM: Dipropylene glycol n-butyl                ether); 1.25 wt.% sodium hydroxide; 90.71 wt.%                water; and red phenol as color indicator.

TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump regenerator, has connecting piece whose side is connected regenerator tee pipe that is connected with first connecting pipe, where left side of tee pipe is connected with second connecting pipe.
AU LIU R
   ZHANG B
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201878002P
AB    NOVELTY - The regenerator has a regenerator bracket (2) provided with two first fixing pieces (4) and heat return pipes (3). A lower part of the heat return pipes is provided with two second fixing pieces (5). A side of the second fixing pieces is fixedly connected with a connecting piece (8). A side of the connecting piece is fixedly connected with a regenerator tee pipe (9) that is fixedly connected with a first connecting pipe. A left side of the regenerator tee pipe is connected with a second connecting pipe (10), where the first and second connecting pipes are a copper pipe. The regenerator bracket is connected with first and second fixing blocks.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump regenerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The regenerator realizes heat regeneration in an effective manner, and the first and second fixing pieces are fixed with the heat return pipe through two three-way pipe so as to realize dismounting and exchanging of a pipe in a convenient manner, and is simple in structure and simple to operate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump regenerator.
   Regenerator tee pipes (1)
   Regenerator bracket (2)
   Heat return pipes (3)
   Fixing pieces (4,5)
   Connecting pipes (7,10)
   Connecting piece (8)
   Regenerator tee pipe (9)

PT P
PN US2018252683-A1; WO2018158728-A1
TI System of controlling pressure of mobile phase, has secondary pressure control element located downstream of primary pressure control element and placed to maintain pressure of mobile phase above threshold value.
AU FOGWILL M O
   SHREVE J A
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 2018693435
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pump (303) for pumping a mobile phase including carbon dioxide (CO2). The pump is located upstream of a chromatography column (315). A primary pressure control element (321) is located downstream of the column and placed to control pressure of the mobile phase within the column. A detector (323) is located downstream of the primary pressure control element. A secondary pressure control element (327) is located downstream of the primary pressure control element and placed to maintain a pressure of the mobile phase above a threshold value between an outlet of the primary pressure control element and the point of detection within the detector.
   USE - System of controlling pressure of mobile phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The secondary pressure control device ensures mobile phase density all the way into the detector, thereby preventing phase separation and analyte precipitation, which occurs without a secondary pressure control device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of controlling pressure of a mobile phase in a carbon dioxide-based chromatography system; and
   (2) a device for managing pressure within a carbon dioxide-based chromatography system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a CO2-based chromatography system having a secondary pressure control device.
   Pump (303)
   Chromatography column (315)
   Primary pressure control element (321)
   Detector (323)
   Secondary pressure control element (327)

PN DE102017201988-A1; WO2018145996-A1; AU2018217852-A1; CN110268099-A; EP3562973-A1; US2019382906-A1; AU2018217852-B2; US11242603-B2; CN110268099-B
TI Electrolysis process for recovering hydrocarbon            from carbon monoxide, involves controlling pulsed            voltage or current between anode and cathode such that            working potential of cathode in energized state has            predetermined profile.
AU ENGELBRECHT A
   FLEISCHER M
   HAEMMERLE M
   MOOS R
   WIESNER K
   HAMMERLE M
   WIESNER-FLEISCHER K
   HAMMERL M
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 2018615070
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves applying a pulsed voltage                or impressing a pulsed current between an anode and                a cathode. The measurement of respective current                open circuit potential (OCP) is repeated, which has                the cathode in a de-energized state relative to a                reference system or predetermining an OCP curve.                The pulsed voltage or the pulsed current between                the anode and the cathode is controlled such that a                working potential of the cathode in the energized                state relative to the reference system has a                predetermined profile (PF) relative to the                respective current OCP or the predetermined OCP                curve. The predetermined profile has portion (tk)                with cathodic level and a portion (ta) with anodic                level.
   USE - Electrolysis process for recovering                hydrocarbon from carbon monoxide or carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The Faraday efficiency can be maintained for                longer period of time. The hydrocarbon can thus be                produced with high Faraday efficiency. The                corresponding amount of energy can be saved. The                efficiency of the electrolysis process is                improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                electrolysis apparatus for recovering hydrocarbon                from carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic views of the                pulse operation with respect to the OCP potential,                pulse profile with voltages against silver/silver                chloride, and Faraday efficiency of gaseous                products in the electrolysis of carbon                dioxide.

PN WO2018143374-A1; JP2018122255-A; AU2018216442-A1; CA3032538-A1; EP3476463-A1; US2019160421-A1; EP3476463-A4; IN201917002946-A; RU2697449-C1; AU2018216442-B2; JP6770453-B2; CA3032538-C; EP3476463-B1; US11135543-B2
TI System for processing exhaust gas discharged during manufacture of iron, comprises device for reducing iron oxide into reduced iron, device for recovering carbon dioxide, recovery device collector and desorbed material return line.
AU HIRATA T
   TANAKA H
   KAMIJO T
   INUI M
   KAWASAKI S
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI JUKOGYO KK (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 201861398F
AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust gas processing system (10A) comprises a reduction device (14) for reducing iron oxide (11) into reduced iron (13) by adding a reducing agent (12), an acidic gas recovery device (21) for recovering carbon dioxide an acidic gas, using a carbon dioxide absorbing liquid (20), which is an acidic gas-absorbing liquid, from exhaust gas (16) containing acidic gas and at least powdery iron-based solid substance (15) exhausted from the reduction device, a recovery device collector (31A) for collecting iron-based solid substance with a filter, and a desorbed-material return line (L4) for desorbing collected iron-based solid substance and returning desorbed material (32A) including the released iron-based solid substance to the reduction device.
   USE - System for processing exhaust gas discharged during manufacture of iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of discarded waste materials can be reduced. The iron-based solid substance can be effectively reduced, and the recycle efficiency can be enhanced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the exhaust gas processing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Exhaust gas processing system (10A)
   Iron oxide (11)
   Reducing agent (12)
   Reduced iron (13)
   Reduction device (14)
   Iron-based solid substance (15)
   Exhaust gas (16)
   Absorption liquid (20)
   Recovery device collector (31A)
   Desorbed material (32A)
   Desorbed material return line (L4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The collector includes a withdrawal line for withdrawing the acidic gas absorbent from the acidic gas recovery device, a filtrate feed line for returning the filtrate after capturing the iron based solids to the acidic gas recovery device. The acidic gas recovery device has a cooling device for cooling exhaust gas containing at least iron-based solid material and acidic gas discharged from the reduction device by circulating cooling water, a trapping device for collecting iron-based solids contained in the circulating cooling water, a cooling tower desorbing device for desorbing iron-based solids collected by collecting device, a desorption return line for returning a desorption tower including a ferrous solid material desorbed by the cooling tower desorption device to the reduction facility side. The cooling tower desorption device has an extraction line, which extracts cooling water from the cooling device, and a filtrate supply line, which returns the filtrate after collecting iron-based solid substance to the cooling tower. The collector has a filter chosen from a cartridge filter, a precoat filter, a metal slit filter, a metal-mesh filter, a sintered metallic filter, and the magnetic filter, preferably magnetic filter. A filter material is provided on the surface of the filter. The particles of the iron-based compound coated with a resin are deposited on the surface of the filter material.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Air conditioning refrigerant includes            polyisobutylene succinimide, bromo-3,3-difluoropropene,            2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, propylene,            pentafluoropropane, trifluoroiodomethane,            1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, isobutane, propane and            lubricant.
AU BAI L
AE HEFEI JIASHICHENG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2018590445
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioning refrigerant comprises                0.01-0.02 pt. wt. polyisobutylene succinimide,                18-30 pts. wt. 3-bromo-3,3-difluoropropene, 25-47                pts. wt. 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, 15-25 pts. wt.                propylene, 14-26 pts. wt. pentafluoropropane, 3-7                pts. wt. trifluoroiodomethane, 9-14 pts. wt.                1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, 16-29 pts. wt.                isobutane, 15-25 pts. wt. propane, 0.02-0.06 pt.                wt. lubricant, 13-21 pts. wt. liquid carbon dioxide                and 1-2 pts. wt. ionic liquid.
   USE - Air conditioning refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerant does not destroy the ozone                layer, has low greenhouse effect potential, low                toxicity, good refrigeration performance and small                dosage, saves energy and is convenient to                use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the air conditioning refrigerant                comprising weighing polyisobutylene succinimide,                3-bromo-3,3-difluoropropene,                2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, propylene,                pentafluoropropane, trifluoroiodomethane,                1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, isobutane, propane,                lubricant, liquid carbon dioxide and ionic fluid,                placing ionic liquid, 3-bromo-3,3-difluoropropene,                lubricant, polyisobutylene succinimide and liquid                carbon dioxide in vacuum vessel for 5-8 hours to                obtain first mixture, adding                2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, propylene,                pentafluoropropane, trifluoroiodomethane,                1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, isobutane and propane to                first mixture, mixing and standing for 20-30                minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Air conditioning                  refrigerant comprises 0.013 pt. wt.                  polyisobutylene succinimide, 22 pts. wt.                  3-bromo-3,3-difluoropropene, 29 pts. wt.                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, 18 pts. wt.                  propylene, 15 pts. wt. pentafluoropropane, 4 pts.                  wt. trifluoroiodomethane, 10 pts. wt.                  1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, 19 pts. wt. isobutane,                  18 pts. wt. propane, 0.03 pt. wt. lubricant, 16                  pts. wt. liquid carbon dioxide and 1.1 pts. wt.                  ionic liquid. The lubricant is polyalkylene                  glycol or polyol grease. The ionic liquid is                  N-octylpyridine tetrafluoroborate ionic                  liquid.

   ZHANG S
   LUO S
   CHEN X
   WANG J
   ZHAO H
   ZHU J
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
GA 201857274H
AB    NOVELTY - A continuous slag discharge device of synthesizing nickel carbonyl, wherein said device comprises a slag bin connected in turn by pipeline, a slag bin, bin three, slag hopper is set on the top of the slag inlet, the bottom is provided with a slag discharging port; slag storehouse is set on the top of the slag inlet, the bottom is provided with a slag discharging opening, one side of the second of said slag storage hopper is provided with an air inlet, the other side is provided with an air outlet, the air inlet of the slag bin and low pressure carbon dioxide tank, high pressure CO tank are connected through a pipeline; slag storehouse is set on the top of the slag inlet, the bottom is provided with a slag discharging opening, one side of slag storage hopper is provided with an air inlet, the other side is provided with an exhaust port, the slag hopper air inlet and fan, and the nitrogen are respectively connected by a pipeline; the pipeline of the connection is provided with a control valve. The utility model can make the nickel carbonyl synthesis is carried out continuously, continuous synthesis time is long, the production efficiency is high.

PT P
PN CA2947365-A1
TI Pressurized combustion of asphaltenes for preparation of bitumen, involves combusting asphaltenes in presence of oxygen in to carbon dioxide, converting boiler feed water to high-pressure steam, and co-injecting flue gas with steam.
AU CHAN L
   HOOD R L
AE BRIGHTON ENG SOLUTIONS LTD (BRIG-Non-standard)
GA 2018358195
AB    NOVELTY - Pressurized combustion of asphaltenes involves pumping the asphaltenes into a pressurized vessel with oxygen (13), combusting the asphaltenes to carbon dioxide such that the primary combustion components are carbon dioxide, water, sulfur dioxide, and nitrogen dioxide, cooling the resulting flue gas (17) to 10-100 degrees C above the temperature of a high-pressure (HP) steam (22) in a heat exchanger to convert the boiler feed water (18) to the HP steam suitable for use in the steam-assisted gravity drainage (SAGD) injection wells, and co-injecting the flue gas with the HP steam sent to the SAGD injection wells for the production of bitumen or for sequestration of the flue gas within the reservoir. The asphaltenes are removed from bitumen or extra-heavy oil at the SAGD central processing facility.
   USE - Pressurized combustion of asphaltenes for preparation of bitumen and extra-heavy oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and environmentally-friendly combustion of asphaltenes at a pressure enough to send the flue gas in the injection wells without the need for recombustion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram explaining the pressurized combustion of asphaltenes.
   Oxygen (13)
   Flue gas (17)
   Boiler feed water (18)
   High-pressure steam (22)
   Excess high-pressure steam (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: An amine or inhibitor of acidic attack on carbon steel is also used to generate the HP steam.
DC H08 (Petroleum products, other than fuels and lubricants - this includes hydraulic fluids and electrical oils even when of non-petroleum origin (C10M).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21)); Q72 (Steam generation (F22))
MC H01-D03; H01-D06B; H01-D08; H01-D11; H08-B; Q49-A; Q49-V35; Q72-A02; Q72-U40
IP E21B-043/24; C10C-003/00

PT P
PN CN107892774-A
TI Supercritical foaming method of polypropylene, involves placing high melt strength polypropylene and nucleating agent in polypropylene foamed sheet extruder, injecting carbon dioxide gas, heating, extruding, shaping, cooling and rolling.
AU YIN P
AE CHENGDU RIZHIRONG PLASTIC PROD CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201831071D
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical foaming method of polypropylene, involves placing high melt strength polypropylene raw material and nucleating agent in polypropylene foamed sheet extruder, melting, injecting carbon dioxide gas, heating, mixing (I) to form gas-saturated homogeneous liquid polypropylene system with large number of gas cores, mixing (II) in extruder melt pump, extruding the polypropylene through T-mold of extruder, shaping, cooling and rolling to obtain final product. The nucleating agent is talc masterbatch made by blending talc powder on the base of homopolymer polypropylene.
   USE - Supercritical foaming method of polypropylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical method enables foaming of polypropylene with excellent reworkability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical foaming method of polypropylene, involves (1) placing high melt strength polypropylene raw material and nucleating agent in polypropylene foamed sheet extruder, melting, (2) injecting carbon dioxide gas into melt to form gas saturated liquid polypropylene system, (3) heating, mixing (I) to form gas-saturated homogeneous liquid polypropylene system with large number of gas cores, (4) mixing (II) in extruder melt pump, making the supersaturated gas in the system to diffuse into gas core, (5) extruding the polypropylene through T-mold of extruder, shaping, cooling and rolling to obtain polypropylene foamed sheet. The nucleating agent is talc masterbatch made by blending talc powder on the base of homopolymer polypropylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The size of talc powder is 1200 mesh. Preferred Composition: The talc powder is 4-5% of total mass of raw materials. Preferred Conditions: The injection pressure of carbon dioxide gas is 0.7 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The melting process is carried out at 175 degrees C. The mixing process (I) is carried out at 195 degrees C and mixing process (II) is carried out at 235-240 degrees C. The T-mold temperature of the extruder is set at 195 degrees C. The extruder is set with rotation speed of 30-60 rpm, melt pump rotation speed of 25-55 rpm, and die gap of 1.0-3.5 mm. Preferred Composition: The concentration of gas-saturated homogeneous liquid polypropylene system is 3-7%.

TI Promoting acylation of amine by carbon dioxide comprises mixing amine compounds, carboxylate or thiocarboxylate compounds with reaction solvent under the action of carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU LI Y
   WANG H
   BAI J
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202161729D
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting acylation of amine by carbon dioxide comprises mixing 0.2 mmol amine compounds, 1-3 equivalents of carboxylate or thiocarboxylate compounds with reaction solvent under the action of carbon dioxide at 0.5-50 atm, and reacting at 35 degrees C for 8 hours to obtain amide compound.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting acylation of amine by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions, high tolerance to substrate functional groups, strong applicability, and high yield, and is efficient, reliable and environmentally frindly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for promoting alcohol acylation by carbon dioxide, comprising mixing 0.2 mmol alcohol compounds, 1-3 equivalents of thiocarboxylate compounds and reaction solvent gamma -valerolactone under the action of carbon dioxide of 0.5 to 50 atm, and reacting at 100 degrees C for 16 hours to obtain ester compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction solvent is ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, ethyl acetate or dimethyl sulfoxide. The amine compound is N,2-dimethylaniline, N,3-dimethylaniline, N,4-dimethylaniline, indoline, N-butylaniline, N-benzylaniline, 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinoline, dibenzylamine, N-ethylaniline, 4-phenylbutan-1-amine, furan-2-ylmethanamine, 4-chloroaniline, naphthalen-2-amine, 3-fluoroaniline, 4-chloro-N-methylaniline, 1H-indol-5-amine, 3-aminophenol, aniline, 4-tert-butylaniline, 4-ethylaniline and/or N1-phenylbenzene-1,2-diamine. The carboxylate or thiocarboxylate compound is potassium acetate, potassium benzoate, potassium ethanethioate, potassium benzothioate, potassium pentanethioate, potassium cyclohexanecarbothioate or potassium 4-chlorobenzothioate. The alcohol compound is e.g. o-cresol, m-cresol, p-cresol, 2-bromophenol, 4-chlorophenol, 4-iodophenol, naphthalen-1-ol, naphthalen-2-ol, 4-(hydroxymethyl)benzonitrile, hydroquinone, 2-phenylethanol or 2-methoxyphenol. PT P
PN CN108979604-A
TI Controlling fluidity of carbon dioxide in non-miscible phase using oil-based foam involves using oil-soluble surfactant system to form oil-based foam under reservoir conditions, and then controlling fluidity of carbon dioxide.
AU LI S
   WANG Q
   LI Z
   WANG Z
   LI B
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
GA 2018A25435
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling the fluidity of carbon dioxide in non-miscible phase using oil-based foam involves using an oil-soluble surfactant system to form an oil-based foam under reservoir conditions, and controlling the fluidity of the non-miscible carbon dioxide based on the oil-based foam formed by the oil-soluble surfactant system.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling the fluidity of carbon dioxide in non-miscible phase using oil-based foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: effectively controls the fluidity of carbon dioxide in the non-miscible phase; expands the volume of the carbon dioxide; improves the oil displacement effect; effectively solves the problem of water blistering and oil defoaming; does not introduce water phase; and reduces the corrosion of oil production equipment and the separation of oil and water of the production liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves uniformly mixing the oil-soluble surfactant system, which forms oil-based foam under reservoir conditions with a thin oil at room temperature and pressure to prepare an oil-soluble surfactant solution, and then alternately injecting the oil-soluble surfactant solution into a reservoir with carbon dioxide. The method involves: (1) mixing the oil-soluble surfactant system with the thin oil to obtain an oil-soluble surfactant solution; (2) injecting the oil-soluble surfactant solution into an injection well of carbon dioxide gas to form a liquid slug and then injecting into carbon dioxide gas; (3) baking for 1-5 days; and (4) carrying out open well production, followed by continuously injecting carbon dioxide gas, where the injection rate of carbon dioxide gas is 20-100 tons/day, monitoring the production gas-to-oil ratio in the production stage, when the gas production is suddenly increased, considering that the gas is inside the reservoir and the production is completed, and repeating the above steps (1)-(3).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oil-soluble surfactant system consists of FS-50 fluorosurfactant and Span 20 (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate) in a volume ratio of 2:3. The thin oil is diesel. The mass ratio of the oil-soluble surfactant solution to the carbon dioxide gas in the step (2) is 1:(10-30).

PN CN110092710-A
TI Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide recycling comprises synthesizing methanol using carbon dioxide in high-pressure stainless steel fixed-bed reactor, passing gas through gas-liquid separator to separate and subjecting to rectification.
AU WANG Q
   WANG X
AE NINGBO HI-TECH ZONE QUANBAOSHOU NEW MATE (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201969858F
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide recycling comprises synthesizing methanol using carbon dioxide in high-pressure stainless steel fixed-bed reactor comprising high-pressure stainless steel fixed-bed reactor composed of raw material gas station, pressure regulating valve, flow controller, gas mixer, gas purifying tube, reaction tube and gas-liquid separator, where raw material gas station is divided into hydrogen gas station and carbon dioxide gas station and supplies stable pressure of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to gas mixer according to molar ratio of 3-7:1, gas mixer uniformly mixes carbon dioxide and hydrogen supplied by gas station and supplies gas to gas purification pipe; gas purifying tube purifies mixed gas and supplies to reactor and after reactor is completely reacted, passing gas through gas-liquid separator to separate methanol and water in liquid state and subjecting to rectification.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst used has higher catalytic activity and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide recycling comprises synthesizing methanol using carbon dioxide in high-pressure stainless steel fixed-bed reactor comprising high-pressure stainless steel fixed-bed reactor composed of raw material gas station, pressure regulating valve, flow controller, gas mixer, gas purifying tube, reaction tube and gas-liquid separator, where raw material gas station is divided into hydrogen gas station and carbon dioxide gas station and supplies stable pressure of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to gas mixer according to molar ratio of 3-7:1, gas mixer uniformly mixes carbon dioxide and hydrogen supplied by gas station and supplies gas to gas purification pipe; gas purifying tube purifies mixed gas and supplies to reactor and reaction temperature in reactor is 240-270 degrees C, gas pressure is 3-7 MPa and gas space velocity is 40000-80000 hour-1, and after reactor is completely reacted, passing gas through gas-liquid separator to separate methanol and water in liquid state, and subjecting to rectification to obtain product methanol, where yttrium-magnesium-zinc-copper doped catalyst is used in reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The yttrium-iridium-magnesium-zinc-copper doped catalyst is prepared by adding 15-20 pts. wt. copper nitrate, 7.5-12.5 pts. wt. zinc acetate, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. iridium acetylacetone, 3-8 pts. wt. urea, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. yttrium hexafluoroacetylacetonate, 0.1-0.9 pts. wt. magnesium hexafluoroacetylacetonate 1,2-dimethoxyethane complex, 0.01-0.06 pts. wt. (R)-1,1-spirobihydro-7,7-diphenol, 1-5 pts. wt. hydrazine dihydrochloride and 15-20 pts. wt. glycine into crusher and crushing at high speed for 3-10 minutes, placing mixed powder in oven at 110-150 degrees C for 5-10 hours, placing mixed powder in ball mill and ball-milling for 1-5 hours, calcining mixed powder in muffle furnace at 100-180 degrees C for 3-8 hours under nitrogen atmosphere, cooling and grinding. In the method, yttriu-magnesium-zinc-copper doped catalyst is reduced by hydrogen at 230-300 degrees C for 120-180 minutes at 0.1-1 MPa before reaction. Preferred Components: The gas purifying tube has gas desiccant and deoxidizing agent to remove moisture and oxygen in gas. The loading amount of yttrium magnesium zinc copper doping catalyst is 20-30 pts. wt.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
