GA 2018559579
AB    NOVELTY - Method for continuously removing carbon dioxide vapor from carrier gas involves directly contacting a carrier gas (102) with a liquid mixture (108) in a separation chamber (106), condensing the carrier gas at a lower temperature than a carbon dioxide vapor (104), condensing and/or complexing the carbon dioxide vapor with the liquid mixture, reconstituting the liquid mixture after passing through the separation chamber by a chemical separation process chosen to remove an equivalent amount of the carbon dioxide from the liquid mixture which is removed from the carrier gas, restoring the reconstituting the liquid mixture to temperature and pressure through heat exchange, compression, and expansion, in preparation for recycling back to the separation chamber, and returning the liquid mixture to the separation chamber such that the carrier gas leaving the exchanger has 1-100% carbon dioxide vapor removed.
   USE - Method for continuously removing carbon dioxide vapor from carrier gas e.g. combustion flue gas, syngas, producer gas, natural gas, steam reforming gas, and hydrocarbon (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient removal of carbon dioxide vapor from carrier gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The liquid mixture chosen from a combination of components which can be maintained in a liquid phase at a temperature below the carbon dioxide carbon dioxide vapors condensation point, such that the carbon dioxide vapor desublimates, a combination of component(s) forming a chemical complex with the carbon dioxide vapor and extracting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide vapor from the carrier gas, and/or a combination of component(s) absorbing the carbon dioxide vapor and extracting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide vapor from the carrier gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the removal of carbon dioxide vapor.
   Carrier gas (102)
   Carbon dioxide vapor (104)
   Separations chamber (106)
   Liquid mixture (108)
   Separation system (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The separation chamber is a counter-current and direct-contact exchanger. The liquid mixture is conveyed from the separation chamber to the chemical separation process by a cryogenic-style pump chosen from centrifugal, piston, pressure-recovery, propeller, circulator, slurry, positive-displacement, diaphragm, progressive-cavity, screw, and vane pumps. The cryogenic-style pump comprises internal components formed of materials resistant to acidic solutions or corrosive solutions. The internal components are ceramics, and stainless steel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid mixture is a mixture of water and a compound chosen from ionic compounds including potassium carbonate, potassium formate, potassium acetate, calcium magnesium acetate, magnesium chloride, sodium chloride, lithium chloride, and calcium chloride, and, soluble organic compounds including glycerol, ammonia, propylene glycol, ethylene glycol, ethanol, and methanol. Preferred Process: The chemical separation process is chosen from distillation, pressure-swing separation, liquid extraction, reverse osmosis, forward osmosis, filtration, and stripping.

PN KR2020129648-A; KR2232146-B1
TI Membrane-based process useful for separating and capturing carbon dioxide in gas mixture has e.g. carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture generated by combustion of fuel and oxygen-containing gas in gas turbine and discharged from gas turbine.
AU KIM J
   HOON L S
AE UNIV HANYANG IUCF-HYU (UHYG-C)
GA 2020B50159
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane-based process comprises a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is generated by combustion of a fuel and an oxygen-containing gas in a gas turbine (201) and discharged from a gas turbine. The carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is compressed, and the compressed gas mixture is cooled by transferring to a heat exchanger. The cooled gas mixture is transferred to a separation membrane module having selective permeability to carbon dioxide. At least a part of the first retention flow is transferred to a subsequent separation membrane module to have a retention flow of a rear separation membrane module having a lower carbon dioxide concentration than the first retention flow, and a carbon dioxide concentration higher than that of the rear separation membrane module, and a permeate flow of a rear-stage separator module is formed. At least a part of the permeate flow of the downstream membrane module is recirculated to the gas turbine.
   USE - The membrane-based process is useful for separating and capturing carbon dioxide in gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane-based process increases separation efficiency when capturing carbon dioxide based on separation membrane from exhaust gas generated from combustion process, provides cooling required to operate membrane process at low temperatures, controls temperature of gas mixture and design and control heat exchanger by utilizing low-cost cooling heat generated in regasification process of liquefied natural gas, has higher carbon dioxide concentration, thus overcomes limitations of existing membrane-based technology, and reduces electrical energy required for operation of entire process by configuring to increase concentration of carbon dioxide before being injected into distillation column through multi-stage separation membrane method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of membrane-based process for separating and capturing carbon dioxide in gas mixture (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Gas turbine (201)
   Heat recovery boiler (202)
   Compressor (203)
   Dryer (204)
   Heat exchanger (211)

PT P
PN KR2022042872-A; KR2410889-B1
TI Producing light hydrocarbon comprises e.g. performing direct hydrogenation of carbon-dioxide by feeding captured carbon-dioxide and renewable hydrogen to reactor containing iron-potassium/gamma-alumina catalyst with recycled carbon-dioxide.
AU KIM J Y
AE UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
GA 202248529X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing light hydrocarbon, comprises e.g. performing direct hydrogenation of carbon-dioxide by feeding captured carbon-dioxide and renewable hydrogen into a reactor containing iron-potassium/ gamma -alumina catalyst together with a recycled carbon-dioxide and hydrogen stream; providing reactor outlet stream containing light (2-4C) hydrocarbons, 5C or more hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, methane, carbon-dioxide and hydrogen, where the 5C or more hydrocarbons are removed, and supplying stream containing the remainder to a cellulose acetate membrane to separate carbon-dioxide and hydrogen; supplying carbon-dioxide and hydrogen separated to a hydrogen separation device using a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process to perform hydrogen separation; supplying residual stream passed through the cellulose acetate membrane to an absorber containing monoethanolamine to absorb and separating the introduced carbon-dioxide; and supplying stream passing through the absorber to a de-methanizer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing light hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has energy and economical efficiency; achieves low carbon-dioxide emission 2-4C hydrocarbon production, and a net carbon-dioxide emissions of 99.2%, carbon and energy efficiency of 42% (a reduction in carbon-dioxide emissions of 1.85 kg per kg of 2-4C hydrocarbons produced).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing light hydrocarbon, comprises performing direct hydrogenation of carbon-dioxide by feeding captured carbon-dioxide and renewable hydrogen into a reactor containing a iron-potassium/ gamma -alumina catalyst together with a recycled carbon-dioxide and hydrogen stream; providing reactor outlet stream containing light (2-4C) hydrocarbons, 5C or more hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, methane, carbon-dioxide and hydrogen, where the 5C or more hydrocarbons are removed, and supplying stream containing the remainder to a cellulose acetate membrane to separate carbon-dioxide and hydrogen; supplying carbon-dioxide and hydrogen separated to a hydrogen separation device using a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process to perform hydrogen separation; supplying residual stream passed through the cellulose acetate membrane to an absorber containing monoethanolamine to absorb and separating the introduced carbon-dioxide; supplying stream passing through the absorber to a de-methanizer, and separating the carbon monoxide and methane in the demethanizer to purify the light (2-4C) hydrocarbons; supplying carbon monoxide and methane separated in demethanizer and residual stream containing hydrogen and carbon-dioxide of the PSA process to a combined heat and power (CHP) to provide on-site electricity, where the recycled carbon-dioxide supplied to the reactor containing the iron-potassium/ gamma -alumina catalyst is the carbon-dioxide collected in the absorber containing the monoethanolamine (MEA) and carbon-dioxide captured in CHP is recycled, and the recycled hydrogen supplied to the reactor containing the iron-potassium/ gamma -alumina catalyst is supplied to a hydrogen separation device using a PSA process, and the separated hydrogen is recycled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The direct carbon-dioxide hydrogenation step is performed by reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction. The captured carbon-dioxide by the reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction is reduced to carbon monoxide, and carbon monoxide is a Fischer-Tropsch reaction to supply hydrogen to hydrocarbons. The process of removing hydrocarbons above 5C is performed by a flash tank and a condenser. The step of absorbing and separating the introduced carbon-dioxide by supplying it to the absorber is performed by capturing the carbon-dioxide in the MEA solution. The stream passing through the absorber contains 2 C or more hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, methane and hydrogen, and is dehydrated before being supplied to the demethanizer. The de-methanizer process is performed at -90 degrees C to -114 degrees C and a pressure of 40 bar. The cellulose acetate membrane is a hollow fiber cellulose acetate.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN114702992-A
TI System for separating and purifying produced gas            containing carbon dioxide in oil field, comprises            compressor, desulfurizer, dryer, precooler,            dealkylation tower, multi-stage alkane separating and            purifying tower, condenser, carbon dioxide purifying            tower, subcooler and pressure swing adsorber.
AU REN Z
   CHEN B
   GAN D
AE GAN D (GAND-Individual)
   XIAN BENQING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022910531
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a compressor (1), a                desulfurizer (2), a dryer (3), a precooler (4), a                dealkylation tower (5), a multi-stage alkane                separating and purifying tower, a condenser (15), a                multi-stage carbon dioxide purifying tower, a                subcooler (14) and a pressure swing adsorber. The                precooler (4) comprises a raw material gas inlet, a                raw material gas outlet, a carbon dioxide                purification exhaust gas inlet and a carbon dioxide                purification exhaust gas outlet. The outlet of the                carbon dioxide purification exhaust gas of                precooler is connected with the inlet of the                pressure swing absorber (10). The outlet of the                pressure swing adsorber rich in carbon dioxide                exhaust gas is connected with the inlet of the                compressor. The upper outlet of the multistage                alkane separation and purification tower is                connected with the condenser, and the lower outlet                of the multistage carbon dioxide purification tower                is connected with the subcooler.
   USE - System for separating and purifying produced                gas containing carbon dioxide in oil field.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide liquid obtained from the                gas from the dehydrocarbon tower is re-injected                into the oil field by multi-stage rectification,                and the gas removed by the multi-stage                rectification is then subjected to pressure swing                adsorption to remove carbon dioxide to obtain                high-purity natural gas. The CO2-rich gas removed                by pressure swing adsorption is refluxed to be                mixed with the produced gas before compression as a                raw material gas, and there is no exhaust gas or                mixed gas emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for separating and purifying produced gas                containing carbon dioxide in an oil field, which                involves:
   pressurizing produced gas from an oil field by                a compressor;
   removing sulfur from the pressurized produced                gas through a desulfurizer;
   removing moisture from the produced gas from                the desulfurizer through a dryer;
   cooling the produced gas from the dryer                through a precooler;
   introducing produced gas from the precooler                into the middle portion of a dealkylation                tower;
   feeding a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and                methane from an outlet of a condenser of a                dealkylation tower into a first-stage carbon                dioxide purification tower in a multi-stage carbon                dioxide purification tower;
   feeding the first non-condensable gas from an                outlet of a condenser of the first-stage carbon                dioxide purification tower into a second-stage                carbon dioxide purification tower;
   feeding the n-1 th non-condensable gas from                the outlet of the condenser of the n-1 stage carbon                dioxide purification tower into an n-stage carbon                dioxide purification tower;
   mixing carbon dioxide from the first-stage                carbon dioxide purification tower, carbon dioxide                from the second-stage carbon dioxide purification                tower and carbon dioxide from the n-stage carbon                dioxide purification tower, and allowing the                mixture to enter a subcooler, where the outlet of                the subcooler is a product subcooled liquid carbon                dioxide;
   enabling the nth non-condensable gas to enter                a precooler for recovering cold, and enabling the                nth+1 th non-condensable gas to be at an                outlet;
   the n +1 th non-condensable gas entered a                pressure swing absorber, an exhaust gas outlet of                the pressure swing absorber is carbon dioxide-rich                exhaust gas, the carbon dioxide-rich exhaust gas                and produced gas from an oil field after                gas-liquid-solid separation are mixed to serve as                raw material gas to enter a compressor, and a                product outlet of the pressure swing absorber is                product natural gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a system flow diagram of                system for separating and purifying produced gas                containing carbon dioxide in oil field.

PN CN110950737-A
TI Synthesizing methanol by-producing carbon dioxide            from nitric acid comprises neutralizing melamine tail            gas and nitric acid to obtain carbon dioxide and water            vapor, and synthesizing carbon dioxide and            hydrogen.
AU TANG Y
   QIN Y
   SONG G
   GONG Y
   KONG D
AE BEIJING EDGEIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020282673
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol by-producing carbon                dioxide from nitric acid comprises neutralizing                melamine tail gas and nitric acid to obtain carbon                dioxide and water vapor, and synthesizing carbon                dioxide and hydrogen to obtain methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol                by-producing carbon dioxide from nitric acid with a                device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, convenient, economical                and easy to implement, achieves zero carbon dioxide                emissions and reduces pressure of carbon dioxide                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for producing methanol by-producing carbon                dioxide from nitric acid comprising a                neutralization tower having a melamine tail gas                inlet, a nitric acid inlet, an ammonium nitrate                outlet, and a carbon dioxide outlet; a reactor                having a raw material inlet and a product outlet,                the raw material inlet is connected to the carbon                dioxide outlet, where hydrogen enters the reactor                from the raw material inlet, and the methanol flows                out from the product outlet, preferably, the                reactor comprises a fixed bed reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperatures of carbon                  dioxide and water vapor obtained after the                  neutralization reaction are each independently                  150-200 degrees C. The molar ratio of hydrogen to                  carbon dioxide is 2-5:1. The temperature of the                  synthesis reaction is 190-270 degrees C. The                  pressure of the synthesis reaction is 3-10MPa.                  The volumetric space velocity of the synthesis                  reaction is 3000-10000/h-1. The first gas-liquid                  separation is performed on carbon dioxide and                  water vapor, and the synthesis reaction is                  performed using the carbon dioxide obtained after                  the first gas-liquid separation. The first                  gas-liquid separation is performed after firstly                  condensing carbon dioxide and water vapor. The                  temperatures of carbon dioxide and water obtained                  after the first condensation are each                  independently 80-130 degrees C. The product                  obtained by the synthesis reaction of carbon                  dioxide and hydrogen is sequentially subjected to                  second condensation and second gas-liquid                  separation to obtain the methanol. The first                  condensation gas obtained after the second                  gas-liquid separation is used to perform the                  first condensation on the carbon dioxide and                  water vapor obtained after the neutralization                  reaction. The temperature of the first mixed gas                  is 30-60 degrees C. The first mixture is used to                  perform the first condensation to obtain a second                  mixture. The second mixed gas is mixed with the                  carbon dioxide obtained after the first                  gas-liquid separation, and then the synthesis                  reaction is performed with hydrogen. The liquid                  obtained by the second gas-liquid separation is                  rectified to obtain the methanol.

TI Subcooling controller for carbon dioxide cooling system, comprises processor for determining that measured temperature external to heat exchanger, measured temperature of carbon dioxide refrigerant, and measured pressure of refrigerant is above threshold.
AU USELTON R B
AE LENNOX IND INC (LENN-C)
GA 202216912Q
AB    NOVELTY - The subcooling controller comprises a sensor for measuring temperature external to a first heat exchanger to remove heat from carbon dioxide refrigerant. A processor determines whether the measured temperature is above a threshold. The processor activates a subcooling system (134) in response to the determination. The subcooling system has a condenser (135) to remove the heat from a second refrigerant, and a second heat exchanger coupled to an exterior surface of a flash tank (115). A compressor (130) is provided to compress the second refrigerant from the second heat exchanger and send the second refrigerant to the condenser. The second heat exchanger comprises a fin and a plate.
   USE - Subcooling controller for a carbon dioxide cooling system. Can also be used for an air conditioning system and a refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces pressure in a refrigerant line by removing additional heat from carbon dioxide refrigerant. The system provides separate subcooling for carbon dioxide refrigerant, thus reducing the refill rate on carbon dioxide refrigerant when the cooling system is idle and one or more of the measured temperature external to the first heat exchanger, measured temperature of the carbon dioxide refrigerant and the measured pressure of the carbon dioxide refrigerant are above a threshold, and in response to that determination, activating a subcooling system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) A method for operating the subcooling controller for a carbon dioxide cooling system.
   (2) A computer-readable non-transitory storage medium embodying software that is operable for operating the subcooling controller for a carbon dioxide cooling system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a cooling system with subcooling.
   Cooling system (100)
   Flash tank (115)
   Compressor (130)
   Subcooling system (134)
   Condenser (135)

GA 2021B2342M
AB    NOVELTY - System for welding titanium alloy using carbon dioxide as ambient atmosphere comprises an environment chamber provided with a top opening. A carbon dioxide air inlet pipeline is hermetically connected with the environment chamber and is connected with the inner part of the environment chamber, for introducing carbon dioxide gas into the environment chamber. A clapboard with hole is uniformly distributed with a through hole. The upper half part and the lower half part of the environment chamber are connected by the through hole. A dry ice layer is sprayed on the lower half part of the environment chamber, for continuously subliming to provide carbon dioxide to the upper half part of the environment chamber. A welding working table is set on the upper half part of the environment chamber.

TI Method for inverting concentration of carbon dioxide column for use in atmospheric monitoring field, involves determining carbon dioxide profile component in optimal analysis field, and determining carbon dioxide column concentration.
AU TIAN X
   JIN Z
   ZHANG H
AE INST ATMOSPHERIC PHYSICS CHINESE ACAD SC (ATMO-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2072W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining cost function of an inversion model after rewriting data inversion cost function based on a nonlinear least squares four-dimensional transformation assimilation method. A prior state vector, observation spectrum, a sample state vector and simulated spectrum as known information. The cost function is minimized based on the nonlinear least square four-dimensional transformation assimilation method. A carbon dioxide profile component in optimal analysis field is determined when cost function iteration reaches convergence standard. Carbon dioxide column concentration is determined according to the carbon dioxide profile component.
   USE - Method for inverting concentration of a carbon dioxide column for use in atmospheric monitoring field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rewriting traditional data inversion cost function into incremental cost function, minimizing the cost function in an effective manner by iterative solving based on the nonlinear least squares four-dimensional transformation assimilation method. Eliminating need to establish a tangent linear model and an accompany model so as to reduce calculation amount. Eliminating need to rebuild the tangent linear model and the accompanying model during model updating process and reducing maintenance and update cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a device for inverting concentration of a carbon dioxide column;
   (2) an electronic device including a memory and a processor which executes instructions stored in the memory for performing a method for inverting concentration of a carbon dioxide column; and

TI Method for detecting carbon dioxide in volume            involves determining current concentration and            determining whether current carbon dioxide level is not            decaying fast enough if current concentration is            greater than ambient carbon dioxide level.
AU PAYMENT B V
AE VI-ENTERPRISES LLC (VIVI-Non-standard)
   VENTURI LLC (VENT-Non-standard)
GA 2019421681
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining whether the                current concentration is greater than an ambient                carbon dioxide level, and if the current                concentration is greater than an ambient carbon                dioxide level, determining whether the current                carbon dioxide level is not decaying fast enough,                if the current carbon dioxide level is not decaying                fast enough,then incrementing a timer, and when the                timer is greater than a stabilization value,                determining whether the volume is currently moving,                or is static, and when the volume is static,                determining whether a temperature in the volume is                within an acceptable range, and if the temperature                in the volume is outside of the acceptable range,                determining whether this is a first instance of a                condition where the carbon dioxide level is not                decaying fast enough, and the temperature is                outside of the acceptable range, and if it is the                first instance of the condition, sending a first                alert.

PT P
PN CN108397171-A
TI Method for extracting dense oil by nitrogen assisted carbon dioxide stimulation, involves providing carbon dioxide and nitrogen to drive crude oil to wellbore and finally to ground during seepage process in formation.
AU LI Z
   WU S
   SU Y
   ZHANG C
   LV G
   WANG M
   XI L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
GA 201865238L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting carbon dioxide into the compact reservoir from an injection well (3). The volume of the injection is 0.1-0.2 PV. The soaking is performed. The well production is started after the completion of the soaking. The carbon dioxide flooding crude oil flows from the formation to the wellbore and finally is discharged to the ground during the seepage process of the formation. The nitrogen is injected into the compact reservoir from the injection well. The carbon dioxide is injected into a compact reservoir. The soaking is carried out. The well is produced after the completion of the soaking. The carbon dioxide and nitrogen are provided to drive the crude oil to the wellbore and finally to the ground during the seepage process in the formation. The above steps are repeated for 2 to 5 rounds of throughput after the nitrogen assisted carbon dioxide throughput of one round is completed.
   USE - Method for extracting dense oil by nitrogen assisted carbon dioxide stimulation in dense oil reservoir exploitation field.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen can effectively maintain formation pressure due to the low solubility of nitrogen in crude oil, so as to increase the elastic energy of the formation. The synergistic effect of nitrogen and carbon dioxide allows the well to maintain high oil recovery after a single throughput. The dense oil recovery is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the process for extracting dense oil by nitrogen assisted carbon dioxide stimulation.
   High pressure carbon dioxide tank (1)
   Ground injection device (2)
   Injection well (3)
   High pressure nitrogen tank (4)
   Horizontal well (5)

PT P
PN WO2021215737-A1; KR2021131537-A
TI Smart ventilation distributor using end-tidal carbon dioxide partial pressure, includes dividing membrane driven to rotate clockwise or counterclockwise depending on monitoring state through carbon dioxide potentiometer.
AU MIN T J
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2021C1014H
AB    NOVELTY - The smart ventilation distributor includes a membrane provided inside the junction of the ventilation distributor formed in a 3-way and a carbon dioxide potentiometer for monitoring the carbon dioxide partial pressure state of the ventilator distributor. The dividing membrane is driven to rotate clockwise or counterclockwise depending on the monitoring state through the carbon dioxide potentiometer, and only one side of the intake air is ventilated occurs, and the air is discharged to only one side of the exhalation at a time to prevent reflux. The carbon dioxide potentiometer operates the dividing membrane at the starting point of the end-tidal refractory period when the carbon dioxide partial pressure changes according to respiration.
   USE - Smart ventilation distributor using end-tidal carbon dioxide partial pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The dividing membrane is driven to rotate clockwise or counterclockwise depending on the monitoring status through the carbon dioxide potentiometer, allowing ventilation to occur only in one side of the intake air at a time, and conversely, it is possible to prevent backflow by allowing air to be discharged to only one side of the exhalation at a time. The 3D printing technology can be combined with the carbon dioxide voltage divider currently used in smart ventilation facilities to prepare for the spread of Corona at a minimal social cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the breathing state according to the position of the screen according to the smart ventilation distributor.

PN WO2021037700-A1; EP3986596-A1
TI Producing direct reduced iron involves treating raw material comprising iron oxide containing material and carbonaceous containing material in direct reduction unit for producing direct reduced iron and hot off-gas comprising carbon dioxide.
AU SCHULTES J W
   KARNER W
AE SCHULTES J W (SCHU-Individual)
   KARNER W (KARN-Individual)
   SCHULTES J W (SCHU-Individual)
   KARNER W (KARN-Individual)
GA 2021222205
AB    NOVELTY - Producing direct reduced iron involves treating raw material comprising iron oxide containing material and carbonaceous containing material in a direct reduction unit at an elevated reaction temperature between 1000-1200 degrees C for a given time period for producing the direct reduced iron and hot off-gas comprising 80-95% carbon dioxide. The direct reduction unit is heated to reaction temperature using electrically heating, and method involves purifying off-gas from polluting constituents others than carbon dioxide and obtaining carbon dioxide final gas from purified off-gas or using the purified off-gas as carbon dioxide final off-gas.
   USE - Method for producing direct reduced iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce direct reduced iron in efficient and economical manner with low carbon dioxide, and diminishes or reduces ever-increasing content of carbon dioxide in atmosphere which leads to a global warming of the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a direct reduction plant for producing direct reduced iron, which comprises a direct reduction unit in which raw material comprising iron oxide containing material and carbonaceous material is treated, a purification unit, an off-gas cleaning device for purifying the off-gas after from polluting constituents others than carbon dioxide, where carbon dioxide final gas is obtained in the purification unit from the purified off-gas or the purified off-gas is used as carbon dioxide final gas, and an electrical heating element is provided in the direct reduction unit for heating the direct reduction unit to the reaction temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method further involves using heat of hot off-gas for producing steam and using at least portion of produced steam to drive a steam turbine and an electrical generator for producing electrical energy. The method further involves obtaining carbon dioxide final gas from the purified off-gas in a carbon capture and storage process, and using residual heat of steam downstream or in low-pressure section of steam turbine as heat source for the carbon capture and storage process. The method further involves obtaining carbon dioxide final gas from purified off-gas by cooling purified off-gas for condensing water comprised in the off-gas and by removing the condensed water, purging the off-gas from the direct reduction unit with portion of the produced steam, using an oxy-fuel burner in direct reduction unit for purging the off-gas from direct reduction unit with the produced combustion products, and using at least portion of produced electrical energy for electrically heating the direct reduction unit. The plant comprises an oxy-fuel burner for burning fuel with pure oxygen, and is arranged in the direct reduction unit for producing combustion products that are used for purging the off-gas from the direct reduction unit.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

PN DE102018008914-A1
TI Method for operating an internal combustion engine of a motor vehicle, involves introducing water into the combustion chamber, where carbon dioxide is added to water into the combustion chamber and carbon dioxide is stored in water tank.
AU FREISINGER N
AE DAIMLER AG (DAIM-C)
GA 2020396952
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing water (12) into the combustion chamber (16). The carbon dioxide is added to the water into the combustion chamber. The carbon dioxide is stored in a water tank (22) of an injection device of the motor vehicle. A container (26), in which pressurized carbon dioxide is stored and is attached to a connection of the water tank. The carbon dioxide is obtained through line from a gas tank of the motor vehicle, in which carbon dioxide under pressure is stored. The gas volume is formed in the water tank, which is acted upon by the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for operating an internal combustion engine of a motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves introducing water into the combustion chamber, where carbon dioxide is added to the water into the combustion chamber and carbon dioxide is stored in a water tank of an injection device of the motor vehicle, and thus enables to reduce the tendency of the internal combustion engine to knock, achieves good mixture preparation and prevents freezing of water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an injection device for introducing water into combustion chamber of an internal combustion engine of a motor vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an injection device for introducing water into an internal combustion engine of a motor vehicle.
   Injector (10)
   Water (12)
   Combustion chamber (16)
   Water tank (22)
   Container (26)

PN KR2095897-B1; WO2020204280-A1; KR2020117841-A; KR2020117842-A
TI High pressure gas carbon dioxide production device has first water level meter that measures pressure difference between gaseous carbon dioxides discharged through first pressure measuring tube from upper end of inner tank.
AU BIN K J
   KYUM K H
   CHOI M S
   BEOM L J
   KWON J B
   KIM H K
   LEE J B
AE DAESUNG IND GASES CO LTD (DAES-Non-standard)
GA 202027699H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first level measuring unit that is provided to measure the water level of the liquid carbon dioxide stored in the inner tank. The portion of the region through the upper end of the inner tank and the outer tank is exposed to the outside air and the first fluidly connected to pressure measuring tube. A second pressure measuring tube penetrates the lower end portions of the inner tank and the outer tank. A first water level meter is provided between the first pressure measurement tube and the second pressure measurement tube to measure the level of liquid carbon dioxide in the inner tank. The first water level meter measures the pressure difference between the gaseous carbon dioxide discharged through the first pressure measuring tube from the upper end of the inner tank and the vaporized gaseous carbon dioxide flowing through the second pressure measuring tube from the lower end of the inner tank.
   USE - High pressure gas carbon dioxide production device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is capable of minimizing phase change in a storage tank in which liquid carbon dioxide at a high pressure of 55 bar or higher is stored, and stably measuring the level of the liquid carbon dioxide storage amount.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the high pressure gas carbon dioxide production device. (Drawing includes Non-English language text)
   Low pressure tank unit (100)
   Cooling unit (110)
   Liquid pump unit (200)
   High pressure tank unit (300)
   Vaporized unit (400)

TI Electric power generation system for supplying            fuel gas to engine, has running condition control            apparatus which is provided for controlling methane in            mixed gas, and concentration of carbon dioxide is            controlled.
AU OKADA O
   HANAI N
   MIYATA J
   MATSUO H
AE RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)

   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
GA 201924141E
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a fuel gas supply                apparatus (13) which is supplied to a gas engine                (11) as said fuel gas. A gas concentration sensor                (14) is configured for measuring the concentration                of one of carbon dioxide levels of the mixed gas                and methane concentration. The carbon dioxide in                the mixed gas is removed form carbon dioxide                scrubber compartment by fuel gas supply apparatus.                A running condition control apparatus (17) is                provided for controlling the running condition                influencing the increase/decrease in the                carbon-dioxide removal rate of the carbon dioxide                scrubber compartment. The gas concentration sensor                is positioned in any one of the front part and back                part of the carbon dioxide scrubber compartment.                The running condition control apparatus is provided                for controlling methane in the mixed gas. The                concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled by                controlling the running condition of the carbon                dioxide scrubber compartment based on measured                result of the gas concentration sensor.
   USE - Electric power generation system for supplying                fuel gas to engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The variation of the carbon dioxide levels of                fuel gas and methane concentration is suppressed.                The operation of the gas engine is maintained                stably, and the life time of the electric power                generation system is improved.

   Gas engine (11)
   Fuel gas supply apparatus (13)
   Gas concentration sensor (14)
   Running condition control apparatus                (17)

PN US2018206737-A1; WO2018134821-A1; US2019209021-A9
TI Device for monitoring respiratory status of patient, has processor configured to integrate measurements obtained from flow sensor and carbon dioxide sensor and to determine respiratory status of patient based on integration.
AU COLMAN J L
   KERTSER M
   EMTANIS ABU ALI I
AE ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201858004R
AB    NOVELTY - The device (200) has a flow sensor (210) configured to measure a respiration rate of a patient and to identify apnea events in breathing of the patient. A carbon dioxide sensor (220) is configured to operate at low power and to measure a carbon dioxide concentration in the breathing of the patient. A processor (230) is configured to integrate measurements obtained from the flow sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor and to determine a respiratory status of the patient based on integration. The carbon dioxide sensor is provided with a blackbody, a molecular correlation spectroscopy source or an infrared (IR) LED.
   USE - Device for monitoring a respiratory status of a patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces ON time of the sensor, thus reducing power consumption and provides sufficient information regarding the carbon dioxide concentration in the patient's breath during period that the sensor is ON. The device can enable monitoring changes in carbon dioxide concentrations based on color changes obtained by the calorimetric carbon dioxide sensor as well as changes in flow monitored by the flow sensor, thus providing simple, portable, power efficient and low cost ventilation monitor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for monitoring a respiratory status of a patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a dual-sensor breath monitoring device including a flow sensor and a low power carbon dioxide sensor.
   Device for monitoring respiratory status of patient (200)
   Flow sensor (210)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (220)
   Processor (230)
   Display (240)
   Single light detector (250)

   LAI J
   XING Y
   ZHANG X
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
   BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 201892438J
AB    NOVELTY - An ionic liquid-supercritical carbon dioxide mixed electrolyte comprises supercritical carbon dioxide, an ionic liquid and a lithium salt.
   USE - Ionic liquid-supercritical carbon dioxide mixed electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid-supercritical carbon dioxide mixed electrolyte has improves the reversibility of the secondary battery, simple and easy manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing ionic liquid-supercritical carbon dioxide mixed electrolyte, which involves, adding a lithium salt and an ionic liquid to the reaction vessel, adding supercritical carbon dioxide and stirring thoroughly to obtain the finished product, where the temperature of the reaction vessel is 10-80 degrees C and the pressure is 5-50 MPa
   (2) a lithium-supercritical carbon dioxide battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium salt is bis(trifluoromethane)sulfonimide lithium salt, lithium trifluoromethanesulfonate, lithium tris(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)methide and lithium perchlorate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt, 1-propyl-3-methylimidazolium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt, N-methyl, propyl piperidine fluoromethanesulfonimide salt, N-methyl, butyl piperidine bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt, N-methyl, propyl pyrrolidine bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salt and N-methyl butylpyrrolidine bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide salts.

   ZHU X
   OUYANG X
AE HUNAN HYBRID RICE RES CENT (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021803232
AB    NOVELTY - Adjustable isotope labeling device has plexiglass (1), sealing ring (2) constitute an external circulating blade chamber (3) connected with a carbon dioxide absorption pool through an organic hose. The external circulating blade chamber is connected with a circulating pump (4) through the organic hose. The external circulating blade chamber is fixed on a multi-angle fixing frame (6) through a metal supporting rod. A carbon dioxide supply pipe is connected with an organic hose (5) through a three-way electronic control valve. A carbon dioxide storage tank is connected with the three-way electronic control valve through the carbon dioxide supply pipe. Carbon dioxide concentration detection probe is placed in the external circulation leaf chamber. The carbon dioxide concentration detection probe is connected to the carbon dioxide detector through the optical fiber, the carbon dioxide absorption pool is connected to the circulating pump through an organic hose.
   USE - Adjustable isotope labeling device used for plant photosynthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has improved efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adjustable isotope labeling device has plexiglass (1), sealing ring (2) constitute an external circulating blade chamber (3) connected with a carbon dioxide absorption pool through an organic hose. The external circulating blade chamber is connected with a circulating pump (4) through the organic hose. The external circulating blade chamber is fixed on a multi-angle fixing frame (6) through a metal supporting rod. A carbon dioxide supply pipe is connected with an organic hose (5) through a three-way electronic control valve. A carbon dioxide storage tank is connected with the three-way electronic control valve through the carbon dioxide supply pipe. Carbon dioxide concentration detection probe is placed in the external circulation leaf chamber. The carbon dioxide concentration detection probe is connected to the carbon dioxide detector through the optical fiber, the carbon dioxide absorption pool is connected to the circulating pump through an organic hose, and the carbon dioxide supply pipe is connected to the organic hose through a three-way electronic control valve. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is controlled to ensure the supply of carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide storage tank is in communication with the three-way electronic control valve and the organic hose through the carbon dioxide supply pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an adjustable plant photosynthesis isotope labeling device.

AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing a pyroelectric field to improve                photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide into                methanol by a bismuth tungstate (Bi2WO60) catalyst                comprises catalytically converting carbon dioxide                to methanol under visible light by Bi2WO6.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pyroelectric field                  generated by the temperature difference between                  cold and hot can further improve the output of                  visible light catalytic conversion of carbon                  dioxide to methanol. The Bi2WO6 is an orthorhombic crystal with both                  semiconductor properties and pyroelectric effect,                  and Bi2WO6 is a lamellar structure.

TI Preparing carbon dioxide-based ionic flame            retardant polyurethane comprises e.g. carrying out            polymerization reaction of carbon dioxide-derived            pyridine cationic cyclic carbonate and diamine compound            as monomers under the action of a catalyst.
AU SUN Z
   ZHOU Y
   YAN F
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202259404F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-based ionic flame                retardant polyurethane comprises taking carbon                dioxide-derived pyridine cationic cyclic carbonate                and diamine compound as monomers, and carrying out                polymerization reaction under the action of a                catalyst to obtain carbon dioxide-based ionic                flame-retardant polyurethane.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-based ionic flame-retardant                polyurethane is useful in the field of functional                polymers (claimed), and in the field of                flame-retardant polymer materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environmentally                friendly; and does not only realizs the resource                utilization of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide-based ionic flame                retardant polyurethane comprises taking carbon                dioxide-derived pyridine cationic cyclic carbonate                and diamine compound as monomers, and carrying out                polymerization reaction under the action of a                catalyst to obtain carbon dioxide-based ionic                flame-retardant polyurethane.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide-based ionic flame retardant polyurethane                obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cation of the                  carbon dioxide-derived ionic cyclic carbonate is                  pyridine cation i.e.                  1-methyl-1'-((2-oxo-1,3-dioxolan-4-yl)methyl)-[4,4'-bipyridine]-1,1'-diium                  compound of formula (I). The anion of the carbon                  dioxide-derived pyridine ionic cyclic carbonate                  is chloride, bromide, iodide ion,                  tetrafluoroborate ion, hexafluorophosphate ion,                  trifluoromethanesulfonic acid ion and/or                  bis(trifluoromethane)sulfonimide ion. The diamine                  compounds are ethylenediamine, butanediamine,                  hexamethylenediamine, octanediamine,                  cyclohexanediamine,                  4,4-dicyclohexylmethanediamine, isophoronediamine                  and/or m-xylylenediamine. The catalyst is an                  organic catalyst or an inorganic catalyst, the                  organic catalyst is s 4-methylcholine oxide,                  triazabicycle, diazabicycle and/or triethylamine,                  the inorganic catalyst is potassium carbonate,                  cesium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, cesium                  hydroxide monohydrate and/or potassium                  tert-butoxide. The molar content of the catalyst                  is 0-20 mol.% of the carbon dioxide-derived ionic                  cyclic carbonate, but not 0.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  dissolving the carbon dioxide-derived pyridine                  cationic cyclic carbonate in an organic solvent                  to obtain carbon dioxide-derived ionic cyclic                  carbonate solution of 0.1-2 mol/l, adding diamine                  compound to the carbon dioxide-derived ionic                  cyclic carbonate solution, carrying out                  ultrasonic treatment to obtain uniformly mixed                  precursor solution, adding a catalyst to the                  precursor solution, and performing ultrasonic                  treatment to obtain uniformly mixed precursor                  solution, heating the precursor solution at                  25-100&#8451; in an atmosphere with nitrogen                  purging to remove air, and reacting for 2-48                  hours to obtain carbon dioxide-based ionic flame                  retardant polyurethane, the organic solvent is                  dimethyl sulfoxide, acetonitrile, acetone, and/or                  N,N-dimethylformamide, the molar ratio between                  carbon dioxide-derived pyridinium cationic cyclic                  carbonate and diamine compound is 3:1-1:3. The                  temperature of the polymerization reaction is                  25-150&#8451;, and the time of the                  polymerization reaction is 2-48 hours.n = 2-18.

GA 202189195J
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a radiating device of carbon dioxide laser marking machine, comprising a fixing shell; one side of the fixing shell is provided with a sealing cover; the other side of the fixing shell is provided with a radiating port; the fixing shell is fixedly installed with a carbon dioxide laser tube main body through a fixing frame; the front and back ends of the carbon dioxide laser tube main body are respectively with an exhaust port at the front and back ends of the fixed shell; the exhaust port is internally provided with a radiating fan; the right side of the carbon dioxide laser tube main body is provided with a reflecting lens main body; the carbon dioxide laser tube main body comprises a radiating mechanism; a carbon dioxide laser tube protecting shell; a carbon dioxide laser tube; the carbon dioxide laser tube protective shell is fixed in the fixed shell through a fixing frame; two sides of the carbon dioxide laser tube protective shell are provided with a radiating mechanism; the device improves the radiating effect of the carbon dioxide laser tube main body, and improves the service life of the device.

PT P
PN CN113797235-A
TI Preparing compound Ganoderma lucidum spore oil useful for protecting liver comprises mixing Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body particles and broken wall Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of mixture, and collecting extract.
AU SHEN J
   CHEN S
   ZHU M
   GUO Y
   ZHOU Y
AE ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
   ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP JIANGSU PHARM (ZHON-Non-standard)
   NANJING ZHONGKE PHARM CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022040303
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing compound Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises mixing Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body particles and broken wall Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles in a mixing ratio of 1:1-2, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of the mixture, where the extraction kettle temperature is 38-42degrees Celsius under 30-35 MPa for 3-4 hours, and collecting the extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing compound Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, which is useful for protecting liver (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oil: achieves better liver protection effect than a single product on the market.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The mixing ratio of Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body particles and broken wall Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles is 1:2. The Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body particle is prepared by crushing the Ganoderma lucidum, sieving through a 40-50 meshes sieve to obtain Ganoderma lucidum fine powder, adding Ganoderma lucidum fine powder with water of 20-30% of the mass of Ganoderma lucidum fine powder, granulating, and drying at 55-60degrees Celsius. The broken wall Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particle is prepared by adding the Ganoderma lucidum spore powder with a wall-breaking rate of more than 92% into 20-30% water of the mass of the Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, granulating, and drying at 55-60degreesC. The carbon dioxide supercritical extraction condition comprises the extraction kettle temperature is 38degreesC under 30 MPa for 4 hours and the extract was collected. Preferred Components: The Ganoderma lucidum is Ganoderma sinense.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC B04-A08D2; B04-A10; B14-N12; B11-B03C; D10-A05
IP C11B-009/02; A61P-001/16; A61K-036/074

PN CN112979198-A; CN112979198-B; US2022267162-A1
TI Preparation of modified montmorillonite            self-repairing agent for restoration of cement-based            material, involves mixing nano-grade montmorillonite            powder with deionized water, standing, removing            supernatant and reacting.
AU ZHANG L
   MEI K
   WANG Y
   GAN M
   LI X
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
   INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 202172337V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of modified montmorillonite                self-repairing agent comprises mixing nano-grade                montmorillonite powder with deionized water,                standing, removing supernatant to obtain                montmorillonite glue solution, reacting                montmorillonite glue solution in supercritical                carbon dioxide atmosphere at 50-60 degrees C and                8-12 MPa to obtain reaction glue solution,                performing solid-liquid separation and                drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing modified                montmorillonite self-repairing agent for                restoration of cement-based material                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified montmorillonite self-repairing                agent improves cement self-repairing efficiency by                interlayer load carbon dioxide molecule, increases                interlayer spacing to improve adsorption capacity,                and provides growth activity point position for                calcium carbonate precipitation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of                  nano-grade montmorillonite powder is 10-100 nm.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  nano-grade montmorillonite powder and deionized                  water is 1:(3-5). Preferred Method: The standing                  step is done for 12-36 hours. The pressure of                  supercritical carbon dioxide atmosphere is                  controlled to 9-11 MPa. The reaction is carried                  out under stirring conditions at stirring rate is                  100-300 revolutions/minute. The reaction is done                  for 4-8 hours. The drying step is carried out at                  30-50 degrees C for 12-36 hours.

PN CN112745495-A
TI Preparing stretch-resistant polycarbonate used for disposable tableware, involves performing copolymerization of aliphatic alkylene oxide, cyclic alkylene oxide, and carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst.
AU XIE J
   HE M
   LIU Z
   QIAN J
   CUI A
   LIANG S
   CHEN Q
   WANG W
   CHEN X
   LIU G
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2021506415
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stretch-resistant polycarbonate involves performing copolymerization of aliphatic alkylene oxide, cyclic alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst, where molar ratio of cyclic alkylene oxide and aliphatic alkylene oxide is 1:0.1-0.4.
   USE - Method for preparing stretch-resistant polycarbonate used for disposable tableware (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing stretch-resistant polycarbonate has good tensile properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a polycarbonate, which is prepared by method for preparing stretch-resistant polycarbonate, where content of carbonate structural units in polycarbonate, which is 85-95%, and tensile strength of polycarbonate, which is 20-40 MegaPascal, and elongation at break, which is 10-50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The R is selected from one or more of yttrium (Y), neodymium (Nd), lanthanum (La), samarium (Sm), gadolinium (Gd), dysprosium (Dy) and holmium (Ho).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of cyclic alkylene oxide to aliphatic alkylene oxide is 1:0.2-0.3. The molar ratio of RL3, ZnEt2 and glycerol is 1:15-25:5-14. The temperature of copolymerization reaction is 50-100 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C; and pressure of reaction is 2.0-5.0 MegaPascal, preferably 3.0-4.0 MegaPascal; reaction time is 6-15 hours, preferably 8-12 hours. Preerred Components: The aliphatic alkylene oxide is selected from one or more of ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, pentane oxide, hexane oxide and octane oxide. The cyclic alkylene oxide is selected from one or more of cyclopentene oxide, cyclohexene oxide and cyclooctene oxide. The catalyst includes RL3, ZnEt2 and glycerol, where R is rare earth metal and L is ligand, and L is selected from one or more of trifluoroacetic acid (CF3COOH), trichloro acetic acid (CCl3COOH), chloroacetic acid (CH2ClCOOH), dichloroacetic acid (CHCl2COOH) and benzoic acid (C6H5COOH).

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); ; P24 (Hand, travelling articles, brushes (A45, A46).); P27 (Shop, household, furnishings (A47F, G, H).); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))
MC A02-A; A02-A00A; A02-A07; A05-E06A; A10-D05; A12-D04; P24-B; P27-B03; Q34-X

PN CN112144076-A
TI Integrated membrane electrode useful as membrane            in electrolytic water hydrogen production, carbon            dioxide electric conversion field of electrolytic cell,            comprises porous compound membrane, and porous            catalytic layer comprising polysulfone.
AU KANG P
   WANG X
AE CARBON ENERGY TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202103184L
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated membrane electrode comprises porous                compound membrane and porous catalytic layer                compounded with the porous compound membrane. The                porous compound membrane comprises inorganic                powder, polysulfone and support body. The porous                catalytic layer comprises polysulfone and                catalyst.
   USE - The electrode is useful as membrane in                electrolytic water hydrogen production, carbon                dioxide electric conversion field of electrolytic                cell (claimed), and lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode: has high mechanical strength,                high product gas purity, high current density, high                catalytic activity and high product selectivity;                reduces electrolytic energy consumption; and                improves stability of the film electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the integrated membrane electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous catalytic                  layer comprises cathode catalytic layer or anode                  catalytic layer and ion exchange resin. The                  inorganic powder is oxides, carbides, nitrides or                  hydroxide powders of zirconium, antimony, cerium,                  and titanium. The polysulfone is polyarylsulfone,                  polyethersulfone, polyphenylsulfone or bisphenol                  A polysulfone. The catalyst is electrolytic water                  hydrogen production catalyst or electrocatalysis                  carbon dioxide production cobalt catalyst. The                  support comprises reinforcing fiber mesh cloth.                  The reinforcing fiber is polypropylene,                  polytetrafluoroethylene,                  tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoroalkyl vinyl ether                  copolymer, chlorotrifluoroethylene-ethylene                  copolymer, vinylidene fluoride polymer,                  ethylene-tetrafluoroethylene copolymer,                  polyphenylene sulfide or Polyether-ether-ketone                  (RTM: Colorless organic polymer thermoplastic)                  fiber. The first solvent and second solvent are                  N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide or dimethylsulfoxide. The                  catalyst active component is iridium, platinum,                  ruthenium, nickel, titanium, cobalt, tin, iron,                  antimony metal element and its oxide or                  two-component alloy or oxide solid solution when                  the porous catalytic layer on the membrane                  electrode is anode catalytic layer. The active                  component of the catalyst is nickel, titanium,                  cobalt, molybdenum or two-component alloy or                  phosphides or sulfides comprises nickel,                  titanium, cobalt, and molybdenum or alloy of two                  components and phosphorus and sulfur when the                  porous catalytic layer on the membrane electrode                  is cathode catalytic layer. The catalyst is                  simple substance and oxides of iridium, tantalum,                  ruthenium, platinum, titanium or alloys or oxide                  solid solutions of components when the porous                  catalytic layer on the membrane electrode is                  anode catalytic layer. The catalyst is simple                  substances and oxides of silver, gold, tin,                  bismuth, copper, cobalt, zinc, indium, nickel,                  bismuth, or alloy or oxide solid solution of                  components when the porous catalytic layer on the                  membrane electrode is cathode catalytic                  layer.

PN CN110255591-A
TI Co-production of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate involves adding nitric acid solution to phosphate slurry, adjusting pH value, reacting, performing solid-liquid separation, introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide and mixing.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG Z
   XIAO Y
   XU D
   WANG X
   YANG L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201992057W
AB    NOVELTY - Co-production of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate involves adding nitric acid solution to phosphate slurry in a liquid-solid ratio of 2-4:1, adjusting pH value to 2-4, reacting to obtain phosphate rock slurry, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain solid phase and magnesium-containing solution, washing the solid phase, drying to obtain low-magnesium concentrate, introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide into the magnesium-containing solution and mixing. Phosphate rock slurry is obtained by mixing phosphate rock powder and water.
   USE - The method is useful for co-production of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves precipitating at 10-60 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C, adjusting pH value to 7-8, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain concentrated slurry and magnesium-containing filtrate, adjusting pH value to 11-12, precipitating at 10-60 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C, carrying out solid-liquid separation, washing and drying. Preferred Conditions: The reacting step is carried out at 20-60 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours, preferably 2-4 hours. The adjusting pH value step is carried out to 2-3, preferably 2-2.5. Preferred Components: The liquid-solid ratio is 2-3:1, preferably 2.5-3:1. Preferred Property: Phosphate rock powder has fineness of greater than or equal to 40 mesh, preferably 40-150 mesh, and phosphorus pentoxide content of less than or equal to 25 wt.%. The solid phase has moisture content of 10-20 wt.%.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vine tea flavone extract comprises (i) drying vine tea leaves, (ii) pulverizing the dried vine tea leaves to obtain vine tea powder, (iii) subjecting vine tea powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain crude extract, and (iv) removing solvent in the crude extract.
   USE - The vine tea flavone extract and antialcoholic composition are useful for alleviating hangover and protecting liver (all claimed) and preventing liver fibrosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract reduces serum alanine aminotransferase/aspartate aminotransferase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) vine tea flavone extract obtained by the above method; and
   (2) antialcoholic composition, comprising vine tea flavone extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the vine tea leaves are dried to a moisture content of less than 5%. In the step (iii), the cosolvent for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 80% ethanol and 3% acetic acid and the extraction time is 3-4 hours. The antialcoholic composition further comprises 10-30 pts. wt. vine tea, 5-15 pts. wt. Hovenia dulcis, 5-10 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 15-30 pts. wt. kudzu root, 5-25 pts. wt. dried orange peel and 0.2-2 pts. wt. vine tea flavone extract. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 25 l/hour. In the step (iv), the solvent is removed in oxygen-free environment under protection of nitrogen.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic; Antialcoholic; Antiinflammatory.

PN KR2019032027-A
TI Preparing hydrophobic metal organic framework-carbon nitride complex used e.g. to selectively absorb oil from water and oil mixed solvent, comprises carbonizing amine compound to carbon nitride, and coating with metal organic framework.
AU COSKUN A
   KIM D
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201930315F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic metal organic framework (MOF)-carbon nitride (CN) complex, comprises (a) carbonizing amine compound to obtain CN, and (b) coating carbon nitride with MOF.
   USE - The hydrophobic MOF-CN complex is useful for selectively absorbing oil from water and oil mixed solvent, and converting carbon dioxide into chloropropene carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrophobic MOF-CN complex improves water stability of ZIF, has improved catalyst efficiency for carbon dioxide conversion, can selectively absorb oil from mixture of water and oil, acts as natural shield against hydrolysis of ZIF crystals, and can be widely applied to ZIF and MOF.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out at 400-1000 degrees C. Preferred Components: The amine compound includes melamine, monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, methyl diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, pentaethylenehexamine, ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, piperazine, polyethyleneimine, 3,5-diamino-1,2,4-triazol, adenine, 2-amino-1,4-benzenedicarboxylic acid, diisopropylamine, 3-amino-tetrazol and dodecylamine. The carbon nitride comprises 4-50 wt.% nitrogen. The MOF is zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF). The MOF-CN complex selectively absorbs oil in amount of 50-140 wt.% from water and oil mixed solvent. The solvent includes pentane, petroleum ether, hexane, isopropyl ether, decane, acetone, methanol, pump oil, propanol, toluene, benzene, tetrahydrofuran, methyl 3-methoxypropionate, ethylene glycol, 1,2-dichlorobenzene, dichloromethane and chloroform.
   EXAMPLE - Melamine was carbonized by annealing at 400 degrees C, 600 degrees C, 800 degrees C and 1000 degrees C under argon atmosphere in an electrical furnace into CN form, heating temperature was increased at heating rate of 10 degrees C/min maintained at desired temperature for 10 minutes, and naturally cooled to room temperature. The CN form was washed with methanol and dried in vacuum state. 0.025 M Zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 0.05 M 1-methylimidazole solution were prepared to grow ZIF-8 nanocrystals on the surface of CN form. 100 ml 0.025 M Zinc nitrate hexahydrate solution and 100 ml 0.05 M 1-methylimidazole solution were mixed, stirred for 1 minute, poured into flask containing CN form and kept at room temperature for 6 hours. The ZIF-8/CN form was thoroughly washed with methanol, dried at 80 degrees C to remove residual solvent, incubated at room temperature for 4 hours, and vacuum-treated at 150 degrees C for 6 hours to activate micropores of ZIF-8.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Cemented carbide formation involves ball milling, drying, press forming, sintering and cooling, where cooling comprises spraying liquid carbon dioxide on sintered cemented carbide surface.
AU ZENG G
AE UNIV HUNAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHL-C)
GA 201907587K
AB    NOVELTY - Cemented carbide formation involves ball milling, drying, press forming, sintering and cooling, where the cooling comprises spraying liquid carbon dioxide on a sintered cemented carbide surface. The temperature is greater than 1500 degrees C, where the spray speed per unit area is 500-800 ml per minute. The spraying is stopped and the cemented carbide is naturally cooled.
   USE - Method for forming cemented carbide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms the cemented carbide in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved strength, hardness, wear resistance and fatigue resistance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the method for forming cemented carbide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The liquid carbon dioxide is controlled to be cooled at different temperatures at -37 to -50 degrees C. The ball milling process for 1-3 hours. The sintering is performed under vacuum conditions. The sintering temperature is 300-310 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cemented carbide powder comprises 65-70 pts. wt. tungsten carbide powder, 10-15 pts. wt. cobalt powder, 3-3.5 pts. wt. tantalum carbide powder, 2-3 pts. wt. niobium carbide, 8-10 pts. wt. molybdenum carbide, 6-8 pts. wt. zirconium carbide, and 25-30 pts. wt. titanium carbide.
