PN EP3424967-A1; WO2019007771-A1; CN110809590-A; EP3649166-A1; US2021147623-A1; EP3649166-B1; ES2884027-T3
TI Producing polyoxyalkylene polyols useful for            producing polyurethane polymer, comprises reacting an            alkylene oxide with carbon dioxide in the presence of            hydrogen-functional initiator compound and a double            metal cyanide catalyst.
AU MUELLER T E
   GUERTLER C
   COZZULA D
   KOEHLER B
   SUBHANI M A
   LEITNER W
   MULLER T E
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 201912605F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyoxyalkylene polyols, comprises                reacting an alkylene oxide with carbon dioxide in                the presence of hydrogen-functional starter                compound and a double metal cyanide catalyst. The                reaction is further carried out in the presence of                alpha , beta -epoxy- gamma -haloalkane.
   USE - The polyoxyalkylene polyols are useful for                producing polyurethane polymer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyoxyalkylene polyols can be obtained                using lower amounts of catalyst and shorter                reaction times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) polyoxyalkylene polyol produced by the                above method; and
   (2) polyurethane polymer obtained byreacting a                polyol component comprising the polyoxyalkylene                polyol with at least one polyisocyanate                component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is carried                  out in a reactor. The method comprises (a)                  introducing a suspending agent which does not                  contain hydrogen-functional groups into the                  reactor and (b) continuously adding at least one                  hydrogen-functional starter substance into the                  reactor during the reaction. In the method, no                  hydrogen-functional starter substance is                  introduced into the reactor during the                  presentation of the suspending agent or a partial                  amount of the hydrogen-functional starter                  substance is introduced into the reactor. The                  suspending agent in the step (a) is introduced                  together with a double metal cyanide catalyst.                  The step (c) comprises adding a partial amount of                  alkylene oxide at 90-150 degrees C to the mixture                  of the step (a) and then interrupting addition of                  the alkylene oxide. The method comprises (c1)                  adding a first partial amount of alkylene oxide                  under an inert gas atmosphere in a first                  activation stage and (c2) adding a second partial                  amount of alkylene oxide under carbon dioxide                  atmosphere in a second activation stage. The                  dosage of the hydrogen-functional starter                  substance in the step (b) is terminated prior to                  the addition of the alkylene oxide. The dosage of                  the alpha , beta -epoxy- gamma -haloalkane and                  the carbon dioxide in the step (b) takes place                  simultaneously. Preferred Components: The alpha ,                  beta -epoxy- gamma -haloalkane comprises                  epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin and/or                  epiiodohydrin, preferably epichlorohydrin. The                  alkylene oxide comprises ethylene oxide and/or                  propylene oxide. The alkylene oxide and the alpha                  , beta -epoxy- gamma -haloalkane are used in a                  molar ratio of greater than or equal to 99:1 to                  less than or equal to 50:50, preferably greater                  than or equal to 95:5 to less than or equal to                  50:50.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The                  hydrogen-functional starter starter compound                  comprises a polyol. The polyoxyalkylenpolyol                  comprises a content of units derived from greater                  than or equal to 1 mol% to less than or equal to                  30 mol%, preferably less than or equal to 3 mol%                  to less than or equal to 15 mol% alpha , beta                  -epoxy- gamma -haloalkane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

AB    NOVELTY - A polyaniline/polyvinylamine multilayer composite film preparing method involves utilizing a plate microporous membrane as a supporter, growing the polyaniline to the surface of the microporous membrane by electrochemical deposition to obtain a membrane having a polyaniline separation layer and modifying the polyvinylamine to the surface of the polyaniline separation layer by a coating method to repair defects and surface modification.

PN CN108948113-A
TI Extracting isoquercitrin comprises crushing Homalomena medicinal material, adding into supercritical extraction kettle, introducing liquid carbon dioxide and extracting, adsorbing using macroporous resin, concentrating and re-crystallizing.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 2018A0523T
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting isoquercitrin comprises (i) crushing Homalomena medicinal material into particle size of 40-60 meshes, adding into a supercritical extraction kettle, introducing liquid carbon dioxide, extracting under pressure of 5-15 MPa at 60-80 degrees C for 3-5 hours and analyzing at 60-70 degrees C under pressure of 15-20 MPa to obtain extract, (ii) adding the extract into macroporous adsorption resin, eluting using 70% ethanol solution, collecting eluate and concentrating to obtain precipitate, and (iii) re-crystallizing the precipitate and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting isoquercitrin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environmentally friendly, and produces product with high yield and purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the macroporous resin is HZ816 macroporous resin or HPD100 macroporous resin. In the step (iii), the recrystallization solvent is 70% methanol.

TI Sheet-shaped body cosmetic useful for promoting            blood circulation, comprises carbon dioxide gas,            transient receptor potential cation channel subfamily M            member 8 agonist, water and ethanol.
AU KIMURA S
AE KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
   KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
   KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
   KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
GA 201878142K
AB    NOVELTY - Sheet-shaped body cosmetic comprises (A)                500-20000 ppm carbon dioxide gas, (B) 0.15-3 %mass                transient receptor potential cation channel                subfamily M member 8 (TRPM8) agonist, (C) water,                and (D) 5 %mass or less ethanol.
   USE - The sheet-shaped body cosmetic is useful for                promoting blood circulation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sheet-shaped body cosmetic has reduced                skin irritation, and is easily applied to the body                without being bound by further time and                place.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for promoting blood circulation, which involves                applying the body cosmetic to calf, ankle, foot                sole, shoulder, waist or arm for more than 15                minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The cosmetic comprises                  0.2-3 %mass component (B) and 60 %mass or more                  component (C). The cosmetic has a gel layer. The                  gel layer has a thickness of 0.7 mm or more. The                  component (B) is menthol having a content of                  0.2-1 %mass. The cosmetic further comprises (E)                  oil agent. The component (B) is menthol, menthyl                  lactate, trimethyl isopropyl butanamide,                  menthoxypropanediol, camphor and icilin. The gel                  layer is a hydrous gel layer containing a                  crosslinked structure formed from an anionic                  polymer, a crosslinking agent and water. The gel                  layer has a pH of 4.5-7. The gel layer is                  laminated on the sheet base material.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic. Test details are described but no                results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108559631-A
TI Extracting high efficiency pepper flavor            substances comprises e.g. selecting fresh pepper raw            materials, pretreating raw material and extracting            concentrate using supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction unit.
AU CHEN S
AE CHEN S (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201877962D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting high efficiency pepper flavor                substances comprises (i) selecting fresh pepper raw                materials, (ii) pretreating raw material, washing                the raw material, selecting, juicing, separating                and concentrated to obtain concentrate, and (iii)                extracting concentrate using supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction unit, passing concentrate                alternately through kettle of extraction vessels in                communication with back end separation vessel to                achieve continuous extraction of concentrate.
   USE - This method is useful for extracting pepper                flavor substances.
   ADVANTAGE - The pepper flavor substance has high hemp                degree, strong stability, no residual residue of                extractant, uniform dispersion, and can satisfy                food processing industry, flavor and needs of many                industries including flavor and fragrance industry                and catering industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (d), cosolvent                  in cosolvent pump is 70-90% ethanol. In step (d),                  total amount of concentrate entering extraction                  vessel is 80% capacity of extraction vessel, and                  flow rate of concentrate in extraction vessel is                  80% x extraction vessel capacity/hour. The                  sanshoamide essential oil comprises amide and                  enol substance, where amide content is 120-330                  mg/g, and enol content is 600-800 mg/g. Preferred                  Method: The step (ii) comprises (a) cleaning and                  selecting raw materials to remove residual                  pepper, (b) diluting raw material obtained in                  step (a) with water in a ratio of 1:1, and                  extruding juice by press or squeeze dryer, and                  (c) pumping juice into centrifuge to separate                  concentrate. In step (c), pumping juice into                  centrifuge, separating concentrate, water and                  slag based on different material density,                  controlling centrifuge speed at 4000-16000                  revolutions/minute, feed rate at 1.5-10 m3/hours.                  The method comprises recycling water separated in                  step (iii) as dilution water in step (ii),                  separating slag in step (iii) and returning to                  press or squeezer to be squeezed out and                  discarded. The step (iii) comprises (d) pumping                  concentrate obtained in step (ii) continuously                  into extraction vessel using cosolvent pump,                  turning on cold cycle of supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction unit and heating switch of                  extraction kettle and separation tanks at each                  stage until temperature of freezer drops to                  0&#176; C, temperature of extraction kettle                  reaches 35-55&#176; C, and temperature of                  separation kettle I reaches 35-50&#176; C,                  when temperature of separation kettle II reaches                  30&#176; C, opening plunger pump to drive off                  air in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                  device, and pressurizing extraction tank after                  air in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                  device is driven out, when extraction pressure                  reaches 15-35 MPa, controlling pressure of                  separation reactor I simultaneously to 6-12 MPa                  and controlling pressure of separation kettle II                  to 5 MPa, controlling supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid from entering bottom of extraction                  vessel and passing through raw material layer in                  extraction vessel to extract pepper concentrate,                  extraction time is 0.5-2.5 hours, (e) after                  reaching extraction time, supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid flows out from top of extraction                  vessel with dissolved solute and sequentially                  enters separation reactor I and separation                  reactor II for gas-liquid separation from                  separation kettle I, bottom sampling valve of                  separation kettle II collects separated extract                  to obtain essential oil with flavor of pepper,                  and gaseous carbon dioxide re-enters extraction                  system, and recycling (it is judged whether the                  production ends; if not, step (f) is performed,                  and otherwise, step (h) is performed, (f)                  transferring plunger pump feed and carbon dioxide                  gas into other extraction kettles through valve,                  and concentrate obtained in step (ii) is                  continuously pumped into extraction kettle using                  cosolvent pump, turning on cold cycle of                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device                  and heating switch of extraction kettle and                  separation kettles of stages, when temperature of                  freezer is lowered to 0&#176; C, temperature                  of extractor reaches 35-55&#176; C,                  temperature of separator I reaches                  35-50&#176; C, and temperature of separator                  II reaches 30&#176; C, opening plunger pump                  to drive off air in supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction device, and pressurizing extraction                  kettle after air in supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction device is driven out, when extraction                  pressure reaches 15-35 MPa, pressure of                  separation reactor I is controlled to 6-12 MPa,                  and pressure of separation reactor II is                  controlled to 5 MPa, controlling supercritical                  carbon dioxide fluid from bottom of extraction                  kettle and passing through raw material layer in                  extraction vessel to extract pepper concentrate,                  where extraction time is 0.5-2.5 hours, (g)                  dissolving supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in                  solute which flows out from top of extraction                  kettle and sequentially enters separation vessel                  I and separation vessel II for gas-liquid                  separation, (determining whether production ends;                  if not, step (f) is performed, otherwise step (h)                  is performed), and (h) end. The method comprises                  draining extraction kettle during gas-liquid                  separation, and removing waste residue from                  bottom valve of extraction kettle. In step (e),                  extraction temperature is preferably                  35-42&#176; C, in which extraction pressure                  is preferably 23-30 MPa, and separation vessel I                  pressure is preferably 8-10 MPa.

PN CN108498560-A; CN108498560-B
TI Preparation of sweet potato leaf flavonoid extract used for preparing pharmaceutical composition for preventing tuberculosis involves pulverizing sweet potato leaves with e.g. yeast, fermenting, and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting.
AU DAI K
   GUO H
   GE B
   ZUO Z
   LUO Z
AE YANGZHOU POLYTECHNIC INST (YZPI-C)
   YANGZHOU POLYTECHNIC INST (YZPI-C)
GA 201872493M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of sweet potato leaf flavonoid extract comprises pulverizing dried sweet potato leaves with yeast powder, acetyl coenzyme A, and glucose, adding water, adjusting pH to 4.0-5.0, fermenting at 30-40 degrees C for 48-72 hours, adding 95% ethanol solution, reflux-extracting for 8-10 hours, filtering, concentrating, adding to an extraction vessel, and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting.
   USE - Method for preparing sweet potato leaf flavonoid extract used for preparing pharmaceutical composition for preventing tuberculosis (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the preparation of a pharmaceutical composition comprising sweet potato leaf flavonoid extract as active ingredient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pulverization is done by pulverizing to 20-80 mesh with 5-10 g/g yeast powder, 1-2 mg acetyl coenzyme A, and 1-2 g glucose and adding 1.2-1.5 L water and 3.0-4.0 L 95% ethanol solution. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done at extraction pressure of 28-30 MPa, temperature of 45-48 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate of 50-55 L/hour for 2-3 hours. Preferred Components: The pharmaceutical composition further comprises excipient of diluent, excipient, and carrier. The composition is in the dosage form of solid or liquid.

PT P
PN CN107857726-A
TI Preparing nicotinamide by one-step synthesis method comprises adding 3-methylpyridine and manganese dioxide to high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia and filtering to remove manganese dioxide.
AU LIU S
   LIANG X
   SHI Y
   WANG H
   YANG H
AE ANHUI RUIBANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JSGX-C)
GA 201826419A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nicotinamide by one-step synthesis method comprises (i) adding 3-methylpyridine and manganese dioxide to high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia gas, reacting under conditions of high temperature and high pressure, cooling and filtering to remove manganese dioxide, (ii) washing manganese dioxide with ethanol, evaporating under reduced pressure to removing ethanol and then vacuum distillating to remove unreacted 3-methylpyridine, and (iii) washing the solid with diethyl ether to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nicotinamide by one-step synthesis method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short reaction time, high yield and simple post-treatment; and the nicotinamide has high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between manganese dioxide and 3-methylpyridine is 0.1-0.12; the molar ratio between ammonia gas and 3-methylpyridine is 1:1-1.1; and the molar ratio between carbon dioxide and 3-methylpyridine is 1:1-1.1. Preferred Method: In step (i), the reaction temperature is 150-160 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 3-4 MPa; the reaction time is 3-4 hours. In step (i), the temperature of ethanol vacuum distillation is 40-50 degrees C and the degree of vacuum is 0.07-0.09 MPa. In step (ii), the temperature of 3-methylpyridine vacuum distillation is 90-95 degrees C and the degree of vacuum is 0.07-0.09 MPa.

PN CN107739363-A; CN107739363-B
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate involves carrying out reaction on carbon dioxide and epoxide using conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyridine/conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHAI S
   CHEN L
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
   DI S
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201818332Y
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using cobalt-based catalyst involves carrying out reaction on carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials using conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyridine/conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material as a heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as a cocatalyst at room temperature under normal pressure and solvent-free condition for 12-48 hours to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate using cobalt-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate using cobalt-based catalyst under mild reaction condition with high catalytic activity and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyridine microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction on cobalt(II) tetraphenylporphyrin and 2,2-bipyridyl as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst, and is of formula (I). The conjugated cobalt porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction on cobalt(II) tetraphenylporphyrin and pyrrole as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst to obtain the product, and is of formula (II). Preferred Components: The epoxide is chosen from propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether and/or cyclohexeneoxide. The cocatalyst is used in an amount of 3.6-7.2 mole% of the epoxide. The heterogeneous catalyst is used in an amount of 0.2-0.5 mole% of the epoxide.;

A return air damper (6) is used for controlling an intake amount of return air to the air conditioner. PN CN114154759-A
TI Method for predicting total amount of urban carbon dioxide from bottom to top, involves re-setting mark value for each department of each type of land city if national carbon dioxide emission is not in initial preset national future.
AU ZHANG Z
   CAI B
   CAO L
AE MIN ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT ENVIRONMENTAL (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202244675Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing several prefecture-level cities across the country into 7 categories according to geographic location of city and industrial structure of a certain year. The total projected carbon dioxide emissions of a certain type of city in a future year are obtained. The total carbon dioxide emissions of each type of city are added to get the national carbon dioxide emissions. The determination is made whether the national carbon dioxide emissions are within initially pre-set national carbon dioxide emissions forecast range for a certain year. The forecast of carbon dioxide emissions for all cities in a certain year is completed in the future if the national carbon dioxide emissions are just within initial pre-set national carbon dioxide emissions forecast range for a certain year in the future. The projected total CO2 emissions are obtained for each type of city in a future year. A forecast of carbon dioxide emissions for all cities is completed in a future year;
   USE - Bottom-up approach to forecasting total amount of carbon dioxide in cities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately predicting the city carbon dioxide emission in the future, and correcting the prediction result at the same time, and better controlling and distributing city carbon-dioxide emission, which greatly improves the working efficiency and accuracy of the data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the bottom-up method for predicting the total amount of carbon dioxide in city. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

A carbon dioxide distribution pipe is arranged inside the second section, and used to evenly distribute CO2 gas inside the second section. The carbon dioxide distribution pipe is connected with a carbon dioxide gas inlet pipe, and the carbon dioxide gas inlet pipe is used to pass carbon dioxide gas into the carbon dioxide distribution pipe. AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting waveband On                waveform signal data and waveband Off signal data                by a differential absorption laser radar system. A                spectrum energy model is constructed. The waveband                on waveform data and the waveband off signal data                are taken as an input of the spectrum energy model.                A spectral energy model parameter group is obtained                after fitting a band on signal. The spectrum energy                models are input to a Levenberg-Marquardt                algorithm. An algorithm iteration optimization                model parameter set is performed. An integral                weight is calculated by an integral weight                function. An optimized difference absorption                optical thickness is calculated. A carbon dioxide                column concentration is calculated according to the                calculated integral weight.
   USE - Differential absorption laser radar carbon                dioxide concentration inversion method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables optimizing differential                absorption laser radar inversion carbon dioxide                concentration with high precision based on spectrum                energy model equation for multiple wave crest                reconstruction signal energy, thus reducing the                parameter needed by the inversion carbon dioxide                concentration by the effect of low error, so as to                finish inversion of carbon dioxide with high                accuracy and stability, and hence effectively                optimizing the error due to traditional carbon                dioxide integral weight function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                differential absorption laser radar carbon dioxide                concentration inversion method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN WO2020112107-A1
TI Method for decomposing carbon dioxide gas, involves directing mid-infrared laser beam including wavelength into reaction container, and recombining activated species to form solid carbon-oxygen structures.
AU DECRUZ A G
   CAMAITI M
AE MONALASER LLC (MONA-Non-standard)
GA 202049264F
AB    NOVELTY - The method (20) involves evacuating (21) an air from a reaction container. A carbon dioxide gas and water vapor gas molecules are combined (22) in the reaction container. A mid-infrared laser beam is directed (25) and includes a wavelength of 2.3 to 3.3 microns into the reaction container to photo-dissociate (26) the carbon dioxide gas into carbon, oxygen and carbon-oxygen activated species. Activated species are recombined (28) to form solid carbon-oxygen structures. The carbon dioxide gas is decomposed.
   USE - Method for decomposing carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The practical, low risk and environmentally safe way is provided to remove the carbon dioxide from exhausts or combustion gases of petroleum and coal based products. The carbon dioxide is inserted in the reaction chamber through the same 3-way stopcock inlet using the conventional techniques in order to have sufficiently high pressure to ensure the efficient decomposition by mid- infrared light.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a laser controlled decomposition method.
   Laser controlled decomposition method (20)
   Step for evacuating air from reaction container (21)
   Step for combining carbon dioxide gas and water vapor gas molecules (22)
   Step for directing mid-infrared laser beam (25)
   Step for photo-dissociating carbon dioxide gas (26)
   Step for recombining activated species (28)

PN CN111961165-A; CN111961165-B
TI Preparing heat/carbon dioxide dual-stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer, comprises e.g. mixing temperature-sensitive macromolecule aqueous solution with dispersant solution, stirring and deoxidizing in N2 atmosphere.
AU FENG Y
   LUO X
   ZHENG P
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020B82692
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heat/carbon dioxide dual-stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer, comprises (1) mixing the temperature-sensitive macromolecule aqueous solution with dispersant solution, stirring and deoxidizing for 3-5 hours at 10-20 degrees C in N2 atmosphere, adding the carbon dioxide response monomer, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours, adding the initiator solution, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours, heating to 60-80 degrees C for 15-20 hours to obtain paste reaction product; (2) dissolving the paste reaction product obtained in step (1) with deionized water, and freeze-drying to obtain a foam-type product; and (3) crushing the foam-type product obtained in step (2), washing with alkane, drying in vacuum at 30-60 degrees C to constant weight, obtaining heat/carbon dioxide dual stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer.
   USE - The viscosity-increasing polymer is useful for in preventing gas channeling during carbon dioxide flooding, preferably preventing gas channeling during polymer-improved water/carbon dioxide alternate flooding (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The viscosity-increasing polymer: can achieves significant thermal/CO2 viscosity increase at a low concentration, and achieves thermal gelation at a higher concentration; is beneficial to maintain the higher viscosity of the polymer slug and the plugging of the high permeability layer during the improved water/carbon dioxide alternate (WAG) flooding process of the polymer with the simultaneous presence of heat and carbon dioxide, and can realizes the control of carbon dioxide gas channeling and the further improvement of crude oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing heat/carbon dioxide dual-stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer, comprises (1) mixing the temperature-sensitive macromolecule aqueous solution with dispersant solution, stirring and deoxidizing for 3-5 hours at 10-20 degrees C in N2 atmosphere, adding the carbon dioxide response monomer, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours, adding the initiator solution, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours, heating to 60-80 degrees C for 15-20 hours to obtain paste reaction product; the temperature-sensitive macromolecule aqueous solution prepared by mixing the temperature-sensitive macromolecules and water in mass ratio of 2:(11-33); the mass ratio of temperature-sensitive macromolecule to CO2 responsive monomer is (0.05-5.00):1; the amount of dispersant is 10-15% of the total mass of temperature-sensitive macromolecules and carbon dioxide responsive monomer; the amount of initiator is 0.05-0.2% of the total mass of temperature-sensitive macromolecules and carbon dioxide responsive monomer; (2) dissolving the paste reaction product obtained in step (1) with deionized water, and freeze-drying to obtain a foam-type product; and (3) crushing the foam-type product obtained in step (2), washing with alkane, drying in vacuum at 30-60 degrees C to constant weight, obtaining heat/carbon dioxide dual stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) heat/carbon dioxide dual-stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer, where polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer is used as temperature-sensitive main chain, and poly N,N-dimethylamino ethyl methacrylate is branched heat/carbon dioxide dual stimulation responsive polymer; and
   (2) heat/carbon dioxide dual-stimulation viscosity-increasing polymer is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The temperature-sensitive macromolecule is triblock copolymer of PEO and PPO, the general molecular formula is PEOm-PPOn-PEOm, the structure and performance of temperature-sensitive macromolecules change with the change of m and n values. The CO2 responsive monomer is a tertiary amine methacrylate; the tertiary amine methacrylate is N,N-dimethylamino ethyl methacrylate, N,N-diethylamino ethyl methacrylate, and/or N,N-diisopropylamino ethyl methacrylate. The dispersant is alkylated polyvinylpyrrolidone; the solvent for dissolving the dispersant is C6-C12 alkanes, and the amount used is 20-30 times the mass of the dispersant; the initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptonitrile, dibenzoyl peroxide, and/or dodecanoyl peroxide; the solvent for dissolving the initiator is the C6-C12 alkane used to dissolve the dispersant, and the amount used is 200-1000 times the mass of the initiator. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the polymerization reaction process adopts water bath heating, and heating rate to 60-80 degrees C is 1-2 per minute; the whole reaction is carried out under N2 protection; in the step (3), crushing the foamed product and washing with at least one of C6-C12 alkanes. In the method, obtained heat/carbon dioxide dual stimulation thickening polymer product is further purified by dialysis: dissolving the product obtained in step (3) with deionized water, dialyzing it with dialysis bag with molecular weight cut-off rate of 50,000 for 2 weeks, freeze-drying, placing the freeze-dried product in oven at 60 degrees C and drying to constant weight to obtain polymer powder.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.);

PN EP3831867-A1; WO2021110589-A1; CN114729115-A; EP4069763-A1
TI Continuous production of polyether carbonate            polyol involves adding hydrogen-functional starter            substance and suspension medium to reactor, while            adding catalyst, and adding alkylene oxide to mixture            in presence of carbon dioxide.
AU VOR D E L
   BRAUN S
   HOFMANN J
   TRAVING M
   TRAVEN M
   HOFFMANN J
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202162743F
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production of polyether carbonate                polyol involves adding alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide                catalyst or a metal complex catalyst based on                cobalt and/or zinc metals with respect to a                hydrogen-functional starter substance (a), by                adding a partial amount of the substance (a) and a                suspension medium which does not contain                hydrogen-functional groups to a reactor, while                optionally adding the double metal cyanide catalyst                or the metal complex catalyst, activating the                double metal cyanide catalyst several times, adding                a partial amount of alkylene oxide to the obtained                mixture in the presence of carbon dioxide,                detecting the temperature peak and/or a pressure                drop in the reactor occurring due to the exothermic                chemical reaction, continuously adding the                substance (a), the alkylene oxide, and the catalyst                into the reactor, and continuously discharging the                obtained mixture from the reactor.
   USE - Continuous production of polyether carbonate                polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables continuous and stable                production of polyether carbonate polyol with high                productivity, by simple and                environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous production of polyether carbonate                polyol involves adding alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide                catalyst or a metal complex catalyst based on                cobalt and/or zinc metals with respect to a                hydrogen-functional starter substance (a), by                adding a partial amount of the substance (a) and a                suspension medium which does not contain                hydrogen-functional groups to a reactor, while                optionally adding the double metal cyanide catalyst                or the metal complex catalyst, activating the                double metal cyanide catalyst several times, adding                a partial amount of alkylene oxide to the obtained                mixture in the presence of carbon dioxide, with                respect to the total amount of alkylene oxide used                in the activation and copolymerization processes,                detecting the temperature peak and/or a pressure                drop in the reactor occurring due to the exothermic                chemical reaction, continuously adding the                substance (a), the alkylene oxide, and the catalyst                into the reactor, and continuously discharging the                obtained mixture from the reactor. The suspension                of catalyst in the suspension medium and/or the                substance (a) in the reactor is maintained at a                temperature (T1) of 100-150 degrees C, before the                continuous addition process of the substance (a),                the alkylene oxide, and the catalyst and optionally                after the addition process of the substance (a) and                the suspension medium, and/or the alkylene oxide                addition process. The temperature (T1) is                continuously reduced to a temperature (T2) of                50-135 degrees C, from the starting of the addition                of alkylene oxide. The temperature (T2) is reached                after 50 minutes at the earliest. The temperature                (T1) is 10% or more, with respect to the                temperature (T2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkylene oxide is                  preferably ethylene oxide or propylene oxide. The                  substance (a) contains 50 ppm or more component                  (K) chosen from a compound containing a                  phosphorus-oxygen bond(s) or a phosphorus                  compound capable of forming the phosphorus-oxygen                  bond(s) by reaction with hydroxyl-functional                  compounds. The substance (a) is ethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol,                  polyether carbonate polyol-3, or polyether polyol                  having a molecular weight of 150-8000 g/mol and a                  functionality of 2-3, preferably ethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, or pentaerythritol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The temperature (T2) is                  preferably maintained at 60-130 degrees C. The                  temperature (T1) is preferably maintained at                  110-150 degrees C. The temperature (T1) is                  preferably maintained at 15% or more, with                  respect to the temperature (T2). The temperature                  (T2) is preferably reached after 100 minutes at                  the earliest. The continuous process is                  restarted, after a standstill of 24 hours or                  less. Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  free alkylene oxide in the reactor during the                  addition of alkylene oxide is 1.5-5 wt.%, with                  respect to the reaction mixture in the                  reactor.

PN DE102019217121-A1
TI Electrolytic system for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide, comprises cathode compartment adjoining anode compartment, anode gas separation device for carrying anolyte, mixing container and electrolyte preparation device.
AU HANEBUTH M
   MELTZER K
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202151032J
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolytic system (1) comprises at least one carbon dioxide electrolyzer comprising at least one electrolytic cell (3). The cell comprises an anode (2) in an anode compartment (3A) and a cathode (4) in a cathode compartment. The cathode is designed as a gas diffusion electrode. The cathode compartment is divided by the gas diffusion electrode into a cathode compartment (3G) and a cathode compartment (3K). The compartment (3K) adjoins the anode compartment, an anode gas separation device (11), a mixing container (10) and an electrolyte preparation device (21). The compartment (3K) is connected to the mixing container through a line (101) for carrying a catholyte (K). The compartment (3A) is connected to the anode gas separation device through a line (102) for carrying an anolyte (A). The anode gas separation device is connected to the mixing container through a line (103) for guiding the anolyte.
   USE - Electrolytic system for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide for generating electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures recycling of unconverted carbon dioxide, uses electrolyzer and cell having improved efficiency, and uses electrode having improved service life. The method does not require complex preparation steps for the electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrolytic system (1) comprises at least one carbon dioxide electrolyzer comprising at least one electrolytic cell (3). The cell comprises an anode (2) in an anode compartment (3A) and a cathode (4) in a cathode compartment. The cathode is designed as a gas diffusion electrode. The cathode compartment is divided by the gas diffusion electrode into a cathode compartment (3G) and a cathode compartment (3K). The compartment (3K) adjoins the anode compartment, an anode gas separation device (11), a mixing container (10) and an electrolyte preparation device (21). The compartment (3K) is connected to the mixing container through a line (101) for carrying a catholyte (K). The compartment (3A) is connected to the anode gas separation device through a line (102) for carrying an anolyte (A). The anode gas separation device is connected to the mixing container through a line (103) for guiding the anolyte. The mixing container is connected to the electrolyte preparation device through a line (104) for carrying an electrolyte comprising the catholyte and the anolyte. The electrolyte preparation device is connected to the compartment (3A) through a line (105) for carrying the regenerated anolyte. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation of the electrolytic system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   Electrolytic system (1)
   Anode (2)
   Electrolytic cell (3)
   Anode compartment (3A)
   Cathode compartments (3G,3K)
   Cathode (4)
   Mixing container (10)
   Anode gas separation device (11)
   Electrolyte preparation device (21)
   Lines (101-105)
   Anolyte (A)
   Catholyte (K)

PN CN112500001-A; WO2022126410-A1; CN112500001-B
TI Low energy consumption carbon enrichment cement            production system, has cooling zone whose inlet is            connected with pure oxygen and high-concentration            carbon dioxide flue gas, and another cooling zone whose            inlet filled with air.
AU PENG X
   CHEN C
   DAI Z
AE TIANJIN CEMENT IND DESIGN & RES INST (CHNB-C)
   TIANJIN CEMENT IND DESIGN & RES INST (CHNB-C)
GA 2021285957
AB    NOVELTY - A low energy consumption carbon enrichment                cement production system comprises raw material                preheating and pre-decomposition system, smoke                chamber, rotary kiln and cooling machine. The                rotary kiln is provided with first burner. The                smoke chamber, rotary kiln and cooling machine are                connected in turn. The raw material preheating                pre-decomposition system comprises decomposing                furnace and pre-heater. The decomposing furnace is                provided with second burner and raw material inlet.                The bottom air inlet of pre-heater is connected                with air outlet pipe of decomposing furnace. The                inlet of a cooling zone is connected with mixed gas                of pure oxygen and high-concentration carbon                dioxide flue gas, and another cooling zone whose                inlet is filled with air.
   USE - The low energy consumption carbon enrichment                cement production system is useful for producing                cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The system solves the problem that the                existing cement kiln carbon dioxide enrichment                technology is easy to blow wind between the first                cooling zone and the second cooling zone. It also                solves the problem of high energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing cement clinker.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the carbon enrichment cement production                system.
   Smoke chamber (1)
   Rotary kiln (2)
   Cooling machine (3)
   Decomposing furnace (4)
   Pre-heater (5)
   First cooling area (301)
EA    (WO2022126410-A1)   NOVELTY - Carbon enrichment cement production system                comprises a raw material preheating                pre-decomposition system, a smoke chamber, a rotary                kiln, and a cooling machine. The kiln is provided                with a first burner. The chamber, the kiln and the                machine sequentially communicate the system. The                system has a decomposing furnace and a pre-heater.                The furnace has a second burner and raw material                inlet. The bottom air inlet of the preheater is                connected with the air outlet pipe of the furnace.                The top end air outlet of preheaters discharges                low-temperature smoke.
   USE - The cement production system is useful in                producing cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement production system consumes low                energy, can realize the self-enrichment of carbon                dioxide, which greatly facilitates the subsequent                capture and purification of carbon dioxide, makes                the temperature of the cooled cement clinker which                meets the subsequent production requirements, and                does not affect the cooling effect of the                material.

PT P
PN CN109628498-A
TI Processing anaerobic-pyrolysis coupling of organic waste to produce electric charcoal comprises e.g. adding an organic waste and anaerobic activated sludge to the anaerobic fermentation tank for the first anaerobic fermentation.
AU GAO Y
   QIN X
   ZHAO Z
   ZHANG Y
AE JIANGSU JIULI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019385699
AB    NOVELTY - Processing anaerobic-pyrolysis coupling of organic waste to produce electric charcoal comprises adding an organic waste having volume ratio of 1:1 and anaerobic activated sludge to the anaerobic fermentation tank for the first anaerobic fermentation, conveying the biogas containing carbon dioxide and methane obtained by the first anaerobic fermentation to the pressure swing adsorption device and adsorbs carbon dioxide by pressure swing to obtain an energy gas, separating the anaerobic digestion reaction obtained by the first anaerobic fermentation by solid-liquid separation, refluxing the biogas slurry into the anaerobic fermentation tank for recycling, adding organic waste for second anaerobic fermentation, drying the solid residue obtained by the second anaerobic fermentation and passing to the pyrolysis reactor for pyrolysis, conveying the pyrolysis gas in pyrolysis reactor obtained by pyrolysis reaction to the pressure swing adsorption device.
   USE - The method is useful for processing anaerobic-pyrolysis coupling of organic waste to produce electric charcoal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces by-product biochar, which is suitable for soil remediation; improves the energy yield, improves heat utilization; and can achieve energy self-sufficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing anaerobic-pyrolysis coupling of organic waste to produce electric charcoal comprises adding an organic waste having volume ratio of 1:1 and anaerobic activated sludge to the anaerobic fermentation tank for the first anaerobic fermentation, conveying the biogas containing carbon dioxide and methane obtained by the first anaerobic fermentation to the pressure swing adsorption device and adsorbs carbon dioxide by pressure swing to obtain an energy gas, separating the anaerobic digestion reaction obtained by the first anaerobic fermentation by solid-liquid separation, refluxing the biogas slurry into the anaerobic fermentation tank for recycling, adding organic waste for second anaerobic fermentation, drying the solid residue obtained by the second anaerobic fermentation and passing to the pyrolysis reactor for pyrolysis, conveying the pyrolysis gas in pyrolysis reactor obtained by pyrolysis reaction to the pressure swing adsorption device by pressure swing adsorption to remove carbon dioxide to obtain energy gas, partially converting the energy gas obtained by the pressure swing adsorption device into electricity is the local power grid, where the other part directly supplies gas to the local gas network through the gas transmission pipeline, conveying pyrolysis residue obtained by pyrolysis reaction to the mechanical embedding system as biochar, adding manganese dioxide to be loaded in biochar as a soil remediation agent, and applying as a soil remediation agent to the soil remediation area, recovering pyrolysis reactor residual heat obtained by pyrolysis reaction, residual heat obtained by internal combustion engine power generation system, the residual heat obtained by the pressure swing adsorption device and the residual heat obtained by the mechanical embedding system, and conveying to the thermodynamic circulation system for the local heating network for heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic waste includes kitchen waste, vinegar waste, and/or agricultural and forestry waste in any ratio. The organic waste is slurry having solid content of 30-50%. The water content of anaerobic activated sludge is 95%. The mass ratio of the biogas slurry used in the reflux cycle and the added organic waste is 1:1-1:3. The mass of manganese dioxide supported in the biochar is 3% by mass of the biochar. The pyrolysis reactor comprises a fixed bed, a fluidized bed and an ebullated bed. Preferred Method: The time of the first anaerobic fermentation is 30-40 days, and the first anaerobic fermentation control reaction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, the second anaerobic fermentation time is 7-10 days, and the second anaerobic fermentation control reaction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, controlling the temperature of the pyrolysis reactor of pyrolysis reaction at 600-800 degrees C.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines);

PT P
PN CN109499348-A
TI Medical carbon dioxide absorbent calcium lime, is            prepared by utilizing raw materials of main absorbent,            humectant, lithium hydroxide, pH indicating dyeing            agent, disintegrating agent and remaining water and            pulverizing main absorbent.
AU DAI J
AE JIANGSU LIFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019287524
AB    NOVELTY - A medical carbon dioxide absorbent calcium                lime preparing method is prepared by utilizing the                raw materials of 50-55 wt.% main absorbent, 3-5                wt.% humectant, 3-5 wt.% lithium hydroxide, 3-5                wt.% pH indicating dyeing agent, 3-5 wt.%                disintegrating agent and remaining water,                pulverizing the main absorbent and passing through                the 800 mesh sieve to obtain the main absorbent                powder, preparing the main absorbent, humectant and                reinforcing agent and stirring to prepare a                polyvinyl alcohol solution by a water bath method                to prepare a pH indicator dye solution, weighing                binder dissolved in water and adding with alkaline                agent solution and stirring to obtain solution,                adding the disintegrating agent to the solution and                stirring to become a uniform slurry and cylindrical                strip is oscillated by a porous cylindrical                extruder, heating the cylindrical strip by an                infrared heating tunnel furnace, cooling the                cylindrical strip, atomizing with water and shaking                to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Medical carbon dioxide absorbent calcium                lime.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical carbon dioxide absorbent calcium                lime has better hardness and moisture, in simple,                safe and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A medical carbon dioxide absorbent calcium                lime preparing method is prepared by utilizing the                raw materials of 50-55 wt.% main absorbent, 3-5                wt.% humectant, 3-5 wt.% lithium hydroxide, 3-5                wt.% triethanolamine, 3-5 wt.% reinforcing agent,                3-5 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol, 3-5 wt.% pH indicating                dyeing agent, 3-5 wt.% disintegrating agent and                remaining water, pulverizing the main absorbent and                passing through the 800 mesh sieve to obtain the                main absorbent powder, preparing the main                absorbent, humectant and reinforcing agent                according to the mass percentage of 77:4:3 and                stirring for 1 hour to prepare a polyvinyl alcohol                solution with a mass fraction of 8 wt.% by a water                bath method to prepare a pH of 15 g/L pH indicator                dye solution, weighing 0.5 g/L binder dissolved in                water and adding with 0.75 g/L alkaline agent                solution to stir to substantially uniform to obtain                solution, adding the disintegrating agent to the                solution and stirring for 15 minutes to become a                uniform slurry and a cylindrical strip has a                diameter of 3-4 mm is oscillated by a porous                cylindrical extruder, uniformly heating the                cylindrical strip by an infrared heating tunnel                furnace at 100-120&#176; C for 5-10 minutes,                cooling the cylindrical strip, atomizing with water                to a water content of about 14 wt.% and shaking by                a shock sieve to filter the dust to obtain the                finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main absorbent is                  calcium hydroxide. The humectant is calcium                  chloride and the enhancer is sodium                  aluminosilicate. The disintegrant is sodium                  carboxymethyl starch. The alkaline agent is                  lithium hydroxide and triethanolamine. The pH                  indicating coloring agent is sudan yellow, mrthyl                  violet or ethyl violet. The adhesive is polyvinyl                  alcohol and magnesium chloride.

PT P
PN US2018257032-A1
TI System used to reduce pollutants from gas stream, comprises first set of mixing equipment adapted to mix hydroxide with gas stream, and second set of mixing equipment adapted to mix e.g. hypochlorite and bicarbonate product with gas stream.
AU YABLONSKY A E
   STOLA A
   GERMAIN A
   JONES J
AE CARBONFREE CHEM HOLDINGS LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2018709192
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: a first set of mixing equipment adapted to mix a hydroxide with a gas stream to create a first mixture in which carbon dioxide in the gas stream can react with a hydroxide to produce a bicarbonate product or a combination of bicarbonate and carbonate products in a first liquid outflow; and a second set of mixing equipment adapted to mix a hypochlorite and the bicarbonate product or the combination of bicarbonate and carbonate products with the gas stream to create a second mixture in which nitrogen-based or sulfur-based acid gases can react with the hypochlorite and the bicarbonate product or the combination of bicarbonate and carbonate products to produce nitrate or sulfate products in a second liquid outflow, where a gas stream flows through the second set of mixing equipment before at least a portion of the gas stream flows through the first set of mixing equipment.
   USE - The system is useful for effectively reducing pollutants from a gas stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: cleans natural gas stream containing acid gas pollutants via the installation of scrubbing system at a natural gas refinery, power plant or other process plant; removes carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen-based and/or sulfur-based acid gases in an absorption column and converted into a carbonate, a bicarbonate, a nitrate and/or a sulfate; provides ecologic and cost efficiencies that are superior to conventional systems, while absorbing most or all of the acid gases, including at least one carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen-based and/or sulfur-based acid gases; and easily disposes sulfate and nitrate byproducts and further processed to generate fertilizer for economic gain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) effectively reducing a quantity of pollutants from a gas stream, comprising (a) obtaining a hydroxide in an aqueous mixture, (b) obtaining a hypochlorite in an aqueous mixture, (c) mixing the hydroxide with carbon dioxide in the gas stream to produce bicarbonate products or a combination of carbonate and bicarbonate products in a first mixture, thus sequestering the carbon dioxide in a mineral product form, and (d) mixing the hypochlorite and at least a portion of the bicarbonate products or a combination of carbonate and bicarbonate products from the first mixture with a sulfur-based or nitrogen-based acid gas in the gas stream to produce nitrate or sulfate product in a second mixture, thus sequestering the acid gas; and
   (2) scrubbing natural gas stream, where the scrubbing phase comprises mixing hypochlorite and bicarbonate with a sulfur-based or nitrogen-based acid gas in the natural gas stream to produce nitrate or sulfate product in a mixture, thus sequestering the acid gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid gas is sulfur oxides (SOx), hydrogen sulfide or nitric oxides (NOx) (where x is undefined). The second mixture comprises 0.5-5 wt.% of hypochlorite and 0.5-5 wt.% of bicarbonate. The molar ratio of hypochlorite to bicarbonate is 1:1-5:1.The hypochlorite is in the form of sodium hypochlorite and the hydroxide is in the form of sodium hydroxide. The protonated salt solution comprises at least a portion of the hydrochloric acid. The hypochlorite comprises 0.5-5 wt.% and the bicarbonate comprises 0.5-5 wt.%. Preferred Method: The step (c) comprises altering the reaction equilibrium to favor the production of bicarbonate products. Obtaining the hydroxide comprise obtaining a group-1 or group-2 salt, mixing the salt with acid and water, acid and steam, or acid, water, and steam to produce a protonated salt solution, and electrolyzing the protonated salt solution to produce the hydroxide, a hydrogen gas, and a chlorine gas. Obtaining the hypochlorite comprises mixing the hydroxide with the chlorine gas to produce the hypochlorite. The method further comprises: obtaining the hydroxide in a second aqueous mixture and spraying the aqueous mixture to mix with the gas stream before the gas stream is mixed in step (c) and after the gas stream is mixed in step (d); obtaining the hydroxide in a second aqueous mixture and spraying the aqueous mixture to mix with the gas stream before the gas stream is mixed in step (c) and step (d); reacting the hydrogen gas and the chlorine gas to produce hydrochloric acid; reacting the sulfate or nitrate products with ammonia and carbon dioxide to produce a bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate or ammonium nitrate; separating the sulfate products from the second liquid outflow and converting the sulfate products to calcium sulfate; and a fifth set of mixing equipment adapted to convert the nitrate products to ammonium nitrate. Mixing the hypochlorite and at least a portion of the bicarbonate products or a combination of carbonate and bicarbonate products comprises mixing a solution or the combination of solutions comprising 0.5-5 wt.% of hypochlorite and 0.5-5 wt.% of bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises: a conduit adapted to transfer the liquid outflow to the second set of mixing equipment to create the second mixture; a chlor-alkali cell adapted to electrochemically produce the hydroxide with a protonated brine solution, where the chlor-alkali cell is in fluid communication with the first set of mixing equipment; a third set of mixing equipment adapted to mix hydroxide from the chlor-alkali cell with chlorine gas from the chlor-alkali cell, where the chlor-alkali cell is in fluid communication with the third set of mixing equipment and the third set of mixing equipment is in fluid communication with the second set of mixing equipment; a fourth set of mixing equipment adapted to mix hydroxide from the chlor-alkali cell with the gas stream before at least a portion of the gas stream flows through the first set of mixing equipment; a burner adapted to combust hydrogen gas and chlorine gas from the chlor-alkali cell to produce hydrochloric acid; a fifth set of mixing equipment adapted to convert the sulfate or nitrate products to ammonium sulfate or ammonium nitrate; a fifth set of mixing equipment adapted to separate the sulfate products from the second liquid outflow and form calcium sulfate in solid form; and a fifth set of mixing equipment adapted to convert the nitrate products to ammonium nitrate. The first set of mixing equipment comprises two phases a first phase adapted to mix the hydroxide with a portion of the gas stream exiting the second set of mixing equipment to produce carbonate products in a first phase liquid outflow and a second phase adapted to mix the first phase liquid outflow with another portion of the gas stream exiting the second set of mixing equipment to produce bicarbonate products in the first liquid outflow.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least a portion of the hydrochloric acid is in communication with the chlor-alkali cell to protonate a brine solution. The gas stream is a natural gas stream or a flue-gas stream.

The evaporator is connected with the compressor that is fixed with an inlet. A mixing tank (6) is connected with a heating returning water inlet for refrigerating process of trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump cycle by using natural working fluid carbon dioxide. A thermoelectric cooling device (1) is provided with multi-pairs of a thermocouple.

PT P
PN CN110317598-A; CN110317598-B
TI Aqueous solution useful for improving carbon            dioxide flooding effect of a tight reservoir, comprises            wettability modifier, penetrant, surfactant for            reducing the oil-water interfacial tension and            water.
AU JI B
   MA T
   XU G
   HE Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 201987561S
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous solution comprises 0.01-0.2 wt.%                wettability modifier, 0.01-0.1 wt.% penetrant, and                0.05-0.5 wt.% surfactant for reducing the oil-water                interfacial tension and water.
   USE - The aqueous solution is useful for improving                carbon dioxide flooding effect of a tight reservoir                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous solution: reduces carbon dioxide                interfacial tension of the oil-water interface and                interfacial tension between the oil and water                penetrate into the micropores of the matrix under                the promotion of the penetrant; increases carbon                dioxide flooding effect; changes the wettability of                the surface of the microporous matrix; drives crude                oil out of the micropores by the action of seepage,                reaches the crack and achieves the effect of                improving oil recovery under the displacement of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing aqueous solution, comprising                mixing water, the wettability modifier, penetrant                and the surfactant; and
   (2) improving the carbon dioxide flooding                effect of tight reservoir, comprising injecting                aqueous solution that enhances carbon dioxide                flooding effect of dense reservoirs and carbon                dioxide gas alternately into the reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous solution                  preferably comprises 0.02-0.1 wt.% wettability                  modifier, 0.02-0.08 wt.% penetrant, and 0.1-0.4                  wt.% surfactant for reducing the oil-water                  interfacial tension and water. Preferred                  Components: The water is reclaimed water of                  formation water, tap water, surface water or oil                  reservoir, preferably the reservoir is refilled                  with water. Preferred Method: The method of                  injecting an aqueous solution that improves the                  effect of carbon dioxide flooding in tight                  reservoirs, shutting off the well, and injecting                  carbon dioxide gas after 10-72 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The wettability                  modifier comprises fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene                  ether sulfate, sodium alpha -alkenylsulfonate,                  lauryl sulfosuccinate monosodium disodium and/or                  coconut oil diethanolamide, preferably lauryl                  sulfosuccinate monosodium disodium. The penetrant                  comprises polyoxyethylene isooctyl ether                  phosphate and/or polyoxyethylene aliphatic                  alcohol phosphate (sic). The surfactant for                  reducing the oil-water interfacial tension                  comprises cocamidopropyl hydroxysulfobetaine                  and/or laurolamide propyl                  hydroxysulfobetaine.

A control signal is transmitted to a gas compression device and a gas extracting device when the carbon dioxide concentration is higher than or equal to a preset concentration. The gas compression device and the gas extracting device are controlled based on the control signal for controlling a gas compressing and transporting process. Outside gas of a gas separation component in the container is extracted by the gas extracting device.

PT P
PN CN107937126-A
TI Super critical carbon dioxide extraction of sea-buckthorn seed oil involves dividing seabuckthorn seed into two portions which are respectively marked as A and B, crushing A portion of seed, putting into extraction kettle after drying.
AU YAN G
AE LANZHOU NEW AREA FUZE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 201834004T
AB    NOVELTY - Super critical carbon dioxide extraction sea-buckthorn seed oil involves dividing seabuckthorn seed into two portions which are respectively marked as A and B, crushing A portion of seed, putting into extraction kettle after drying, extracting carbon dioxide fluid in supercritical state into extraction kettle. The solution with sea plant extract of the carbon dioxide fluid is extracted into the separation kettle and separated to obtain seed extracting solution A and carbon dioxide gas. The B portion seed is dried, crushed and placed into extraction kettle. The seed extraction liquid A is added into extracting kettle, stirred and mixed with portion B seed. The carbon dioxide fluid in a supercritical state is added into extraction kettle, and extraction is carried out. The seed plant is added with carbon dioxide fluid into separation kettle for separation, and separated to obtain seabuckthorm seed oil and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is used for extracting sea-buckthorn seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of sea-buckthorn seed oil in a high yield and high beneficial component content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of A portion and B portion is 1.5-2:1. The extracting temperature is 50-60 degrees C. The pressure is 1.0-1.8 megapascal. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 0.09-0.14 L/minute. The extracting time is 3-4 hours. The separating temperature of A portion is 45-55 degrees C, and pressure is 1.5-2.5 megapascal. Preferred Process: The seabuckthorn seeds are placed in an oven at 60-80 degrees C, and dried for 2 hours. The water content of seabuckthorn seeds is 6-10%. The number of seabuckthorn seeds is 40-60 mesh. The carbon dioxide gas is separated into extracting kettle and used again after compression. The B portion of sea-buckthorn seed is dried in oven at 60-80 degrees C for 2 hours. The sea water content is 3-5%. The seabuckthorn seed number is 70-90 mesh. The extracting temperature is 70-80 degrees C. The pressure is 1.5-2.2 megapascal. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 0.15-0.18 L/minute. The extracting time is 4-5 hours. The separating temperature is 60-80 degrees C, and pressure is 1.9-2.8 meagapascal.

PN CN112933958-A
TI Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, by using            carbon dioxide as raw material, and titanium            dioxide/titanium carbide photocatalytic film as            catalyst, and performing gas-solid phase photocatalytic            carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
AU SU T
   CHEN L
   SUN M
   QIN Z
   JI H
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2021710097
AB    NOVELTY - Method for photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction, involves using carbon dioxide as a raw                material, and a titanium dioxide/titanium carbide                photocatalytic film as a catalyst, and performing                gas-solid phase photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction reaction under light conditions to obtain                products such as carbon monoxide, methane, ethylene                and ethane.
   USE - The method is used for photocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves efficiency of                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, and                promotes carbon-carbon coupling reaction to obtain                2C products such as ethane and ethylene                products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i)                  preparing titanium dioxide/titanium carbide                  photocatalytic film by adding lithium fluoride to                  a hydrochloric acid solution, mixing and                  stirring, dripping titanium aluminum carbide to a                  mixed solution of hydrochloric acid and lithium                  fluoride, etching at 40 degrees C, washing with                  deionized water by centrifugation to pH of                  greater than or equal to 6, performing ultrasonic                  treatment to obtain a dispersion of titanium                  carbide nanosheets, vacuum freeze-drying part of                  the titanium carbide nanosheet dispersion to                  obtain titanium carbide nanosheet powder, placing                  the titanium carbide nanosheet powder in a muffle                  furnace, baking to obtain titanium dioxide                  nanosheet, placing the titanium dioxide nanosheet                  in deionized water, performing ultrasonic                  treatment to obtain a dispersion of titanium                  dioxide nanosheet, stirring and mixing the                  titanium dioxide nanosheet dispersion and the                  remaining titanium carbide nanosheet dispersion                  according to the titanium dioxide/titanium                  carbide mass ratio of 1:1-20:1, performing                  suction filtration to obtain a wet titanium                  dioxide/titanium carbide photocatalytic film, and                  vacuum-drying at 60-90 degrees C to obtain                  titanium dioxide/titanium carbide photocatalytic                  film, and (ii) performing photocatalytic                  reduction of carbon dioxide by placing the above                  titanium dioxide/titanium carbide photocatalytic                  film in a top-illuminated quartz photocatalytic                  reactor, using a 300 W xenon lamp as light                  source, where the light source current is 10-20                  A, and the top of the photocatalytic film is 10                  cm away from the light source, and bubbling                  carbon dioxide and water vapor at a flow rate of                  1-10 ml/minute for photocatalytic gas solid phase                  reaction for 2-4 hours to obtain carbon dioxide                  reduction products such as carbon monoxide,                  methane, ethylene and ethane.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN KR1935885-B1
TI Removing astringency of persimmon by discharging carbon dioxide from container to raise supply temperature of carbon dioxide, supplying into interior of de-scooping compartment, performing warm-tempering process and removing carbon dioxide.
AU JUN B S
AE JUN B S (JUNB-Individual)
GA 201930739N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing astringency of persimmon involves (a) discharging carbon dioxide from several carbon dioxide containers to raise supply temperature of carbon dioxide supplied to the take-up chamber and lower the pH, (b) supplying carbon dioxide passed through step (a) into interior of a de-scooping compartment containing a squeezed feeling so that the internal pressure of the de-scooping compartment is maintained at 5-10 kg/mm2, (c) performing warm-tempering process in which the inner temperature of the de-sowing chamber is rapidly heated to 35-45 degrees C for 5-6 hours, and (d) removing carbon dioxide from the interior of the de-scooping chamber after the warm-tempering process and then maintaining the internal temperature of the de-scooping chamber at 25-35 degrees C for 10-12 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for removing astringency of persimmon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for removing astringency of persimmon involves (a) discharging from several carbon dioxide containers to raise supply temperature of carbon dioxide supplied to the take-up chamber and lower the pH, (b) supplying carbon dioxide passed through step (a) into interior of a de-scooping compartment containing a squeezed feeling so that the internal pressure of the de-scooping compartment is maintained at 5-10 kg/mm2, (c) performing warm-tempering process in which the inner temperature of the de-sowing chamber is rapidly heated to 35-45 degrees C for 5-6 hours, and (d) removing carbon dioxide from the interior of the de-scooping chamber after the warm-tempering process and then maintaining the internal temperature of the de-scooping chamber at 25-35 degrees C for 10-12 hours. The preliminary process is performed by using a carbon dioxide capture flow tube in several carbon dioxide containers are connected through a connection channel, discharging the temperature of the carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide container, firstly raising the temperature of carbon dioxide discharged into the carbon dioxide container, heating in a heating unit formed of the heating coil installed in the outer circumference of the carbon dioxide capture flow tube in the longitudinal direction, secondarily raising the temperature of the carbon dioxide flowing from the carbon dioxide capture flow tube using an electric heat exchanger connected to the carbon dioxide capture flow tube through a branch pipe communicated with the carbon dioxide capture flow tube, and an electric heat exchanger comprising a coil pipe installed along the inner periphery of the inner side of the electric heat exchanger while being connected to the branch pipe, performing hydrothermal exchange step by elevating the temperature of the carbon dioxide through the heat exchange step to the third degree using an electric water heating unit comprising a coil pipe installed along the inner periphery of the inside of the receiving body while being connected to the connecting pipe and receiving body connected to the coin tube through a connection pipe communicating with the coin tube, and lowering pH of the carbon dioxide passing through the hydrothermal exchange step through gas scrubber comprising guide pipe for guiding the water contained in main portion connected to the coil pipe through a flow pipe communicating with the coil pipe and containing water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The temperature of the water contained in the gas scrubber is 45-50 degrees C. Preferred Device: The air chamber is provided for discharging air inside de-scooping chamber when the inner pressure of the de-scooping chamber is increased in the warm tempering process.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
