PT P
PN WO2022182296-A1
TI Material used for adsorbing carbon dioxide from            environment, e.g., gas separation equipment, carbon            dioxide recovery and storage, inter conversion of            hydrocarbons, natural gas purification, fuel synthesis            and in carbon dioxide sensors, comprises anion-doped            alkaline earth metal oxide.
AU WU P
   SENEVIRATHNA A J M H
AE UNIV SINGAPORE TECHNOLOGY & DESIGN (UYSI-Non-standard)
GA 2022B2429W
AB    NOVELTY - Material comprises an anion-doped alkaline                earth metal oxide.
   USE - The material is used for adsorbing carbon                dioxide from an environment (all claimed), gas                separation equipment, post- and pre-combustion                carbon dioxide capture, carbon dioxide recovery and                storage, catalysis, inter conversion of                hydrocarbons, natural gas purification, biogas                upgrading, building materials including cement                materials, fuel synthesis and in carbon dioxide                sensors. The material is used in coastal protection                engineering, to adapt to rising sea levels and                acidification of sea water due to carbon dioxide                adsorption, and adsorbing carbon dioxide from                wastewater. The material is used to desorb carbon                dioxide, which may be suitable for use in                regulating carbon dioxide concentrations both                indoor and outdoors, as well as facilitating                organism growth, for example in a marine                environment. The carbon captured by the material is                used in the manufacture of carbon products such as                carbon nanotubes and carbon powders, thus making                the material as valuable for sourcing carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The material effectively and efficiently                adsorbs carbon dioxide from air at room                temperature, improves carbon dioxide adsorption                properties of oxide of alkaline earth metal,                prevents formation of side-products, such as                carbonates on surface of material, has high                porosity and high surface area, increased number of                carbon capture sites to improve carbon dioxide                adsorption, exhibits higher carbon dioxide                adsorption capacities at room temperature compared                to conventional sorbents, is stable over multiple                adsorption-desorption cycles, is prepared in                cost-efficient manner, and avoids carbon dioxide                adsorption process to be performed under highly                energy consuming high-temperature conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) (1) a method for preparing the material,                involving contacting a hydroxide salt of an                alkaline earth metal with a second salt of the                alkaline earth metal; and
   (2) (2) a method for adsorbing carbon dioxide                from an environment, involving contacting the                material with the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material further                  comprises water. The material is in the form of                  nanofibers, flakes, rods and/or sheets. The                  alkaline earth metal is chosen from magnesium,                  calcium, strontium and/or barium. The anion is                  chosen from halide, sulfate, nitrate and/or                  phosphate. The second salt of the alkaline earth                  metal is not the same as the hydroxide salt of                  the alkaline earth metal. The solvent comprises                  acetic acid and/or water. Preferred Composition:                  The weight ratio of the hydroxide salt of the                  alkaline earth metal and the second salt of the                  alkaline earth metal is 99.9:0.1-80:20. The                  weight ratio of the hydroxide salt of the                  alkaline earth metal and the polymer is 1:5-1:20.                  Preferred Method: The contacting step is                  performed in a solvent. The method further                  involves forming the material into fibers. The                  forming step is performed by electro spinning.                  The method further involves removing the solvent.                  The method further involves calcining the                  material. The method further involves grinding                  the material into a powder. The method further                  comprises aging the material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The contacting step                  further involves using a polymer. Preferred                  Components: The polymer is polyvinyl acetate or                  polyacrylonitrile.
   EXAMPLE - Chloride solution (1%) was prepared by                dissolving 0.0025 g magnesium chelatase and 0.2475                g magnesium hydroxide in 5 ml acetic acid via                sonication in a water bath at 40&#176; C for 1                hour. Then, the solution was mixed with the 5%                aqueous polyvinyl acetate as (prepared by                preparaing aqueous polyvinyl acetate (PVA) (5 wt.%)                solution by dissolving PVA powder in deionized                water, at 90&#176; C for 2 hours, cooling the                solution to room temperature (RT) and stirring                continuously for another 12 hours) of volume ratio                of 15:100 (0.750-5 ml), with further sonication in                a water bath at 40&#176; C for 20 minutes to                eliminate precipitation. The 5% chloride solution                was prepared by using 0.0125g magnesium chelatase                and 0.2375 g magnesium hydroxide, and the 10%                chloride solution was prepared by using 0.025 g                magnesium chelatase and 0.225 g magnesium                hydroxide, followed by a similar procedure for the                1% chloride sample. Electro spinning was carried                out by using a needle-collector setup in top-down                configuration with aluminum foil spread across the                collector plate. All the samples were electrospun                with an applied voltage of 20.5 kV and 21Gx l/2"                needle with the sharp end ground flat. The                needle-collector distance was 12 cm, with a flow                rate of 0.3 ml/hour. After electro spinning, the                wet nanofibre layer deposited on the aluminum foil                was oven-dried at 60&#176; C for 48 hours. This                gave the solidified layer having a brittle                consistency, which was then collected as flakes for                calcination in a box furnace. During calcination,                the furnace temperature was increased from                30-300&#176; C with a heating rate of                2&#176; C/minute. The samples were heated at                300&#176; C for 2 hours, followed by air                cooling to room temperature. The prepared doped                chloride sorbent samples were then subjected to                mechanical grinding using a mortar and pestle to                obtain a fine powder.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. AU TIAN X
   ZHANG Z
   LIU S
AE SHANGHAI MEGA VISION MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202299551J
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for fixing                carbon and removing hardness, comprising a water                tank and a carbon dioxide gas tank, the carbon                dioxide gas tank is located above the water tank                and communicate with the water tank, the carbon                dioxide gas tank is provided with a Venturi                ejector, the carbon dioxide gas tank is connected                with the carbon dioxide inlet channel. The utility                model through a certain pressure to the gas phase                CO2 dissolved in water to form carbonate or                bicarbonate ion, calcium and magnesium ion in the                water to form precipitate can be recycled, on the                one hand, it can realize the waste water recycling,                saving resource, on the other hand, reducing carbon                dioxide concentration in the atmosphere, at the                same time, realizing double function of solid                carbon and removing hardness, The invention does                not need adding hardness, chemical cost, no side                product, simple device structure, convenient                operation, environment-friendly and safe.

TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide from effluent gas involves providing rice-derived product chosen from puffed rice cake and puffed rice grains, and contacting rice-derived product with effluent gas.
AU AL-MARZOUQI A H
   KHOUKHI M
   SALEH A F O D
   MOHAMMAD A F R
AE UNIV UNITED ARAB EMIRATES (UYUN-Non-standard)
GA 2022555796
AB    NOVELTY - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from effluent gas involves providing rice-derived product chosen from puffed rice cake and puffed rice grains, and contacting rice-derived product with effluent gas for capturing carbon dioxide from effluent gas.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from effluent gas
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to capture carbon dioxide gas from effluent gas using rice-derived product having smooth surface, acid-balanced factor, high porosity, and high interfacial areas. The method enables capturing of carbon dioxide by scrubbing reactive solvent due to its cost-effectiveness and capability of treating great quantities of exhaust gas. After preparing the rice-derived product, the rice-derived product is functionalized with hydroxyl groups which allow the puffed rice structure to separate carbon dioxide from the effluent gas stream in a very efficient way.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart explaining the process for preparing rice-derived product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The method further involves adjusting pH of the rice-derived product to 10 or more using sodium hydroxide solution, before contacting with effluent gas. The effluent gas is contacted with rice-derived product by loading rice-derived product into gas contact device. Puffed rice cakes are stacked together within the gas contact device, or spaced within the gas contact device.

PN CN112683837-A
TI Carbon dioxide concentration detection method based on infrared technology, involves determining magnitude and output voltage value under each node, and obtaining estimated concentration value of carbon dioxide.
AU LV J
   MAO K
   QIU J
   CHI K
AE HANGZHOU MULTIIR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202145805V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating the temperature values at the three temperature nodes for a carbon dioxide concentration detection sensor node based on infrared technology according to the applied ambient temperature range. The carbon dioxide concentration value of each node in any range is calculated according to the carbon dioxide concentration measurement range. The output voltage value of the node at temperature and carbon oxide concentration is measured. The output voltage value is obtained under the node for the current temperature value and the output voltage value. The magnitude and the output voltage value are determined under each node, and the estimated concentration value of carbon dioxide is obtained.
   USE - Carbon dioxide concentration detection method based on infrared technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables flexibly adjusting calibration stage collection data and concentration estimation accuracy by adjusting a parameter value to easily adjust corresponding collection data amount so as to satisfy detection requirement of the concentration estimation accuracy.

TI Preparation of CHA zeolite membrane for separating            carbon dioxide, involves adding support including CHA            particle seed layer into zeolite solution containing            silicon and aluminum in preset molar ratio, and            performing hydrothermal synthesis.
AU CHOI J
   LEE K Y
   HONG S
   LEE K
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2020590290
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of CHA zeolite membrane involves                adding support deposited with CHA particle seed                layer into a synthetic solution for growing zeolite                containing an organic structure-directing agent and                silicon and aluminum sources, and performing                hydrothermal synthesis. The molar ratio of silicon                to aluminum in the synthetic solution is 5 or                more.
   USE - Preparation of CHA zeolite membrane used for                separating carbon dioxide from mixture containing                carbon dioxide and molecule chosen from methane,                nitrogen, oxygen, ethylene, ethane, propene and                propane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high performance CHA                membranes having high capacity to separate carbon                dioxide/nitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane even                under wet conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) CHA zeolite membrane; and
   (2) separation of carbon dioxide from mixture                containing carbon dioxide and molecule chosen from                methane, nitrogen, oxygen, ethylene, ethane,                propene and propane using CHA zeolite                membrane.

Preferred                  Component: The support is chosen from alumina,                  polypropylene, polyethylene, PTFE, polysulfone,                  polyimide, silica, glass gamma -alumina, mullite,                  zirconia, titania, yttria, ceria, vanadia,                  silicon, stainless steel and carbon.

The carbon dioxide absorbent as a main                  component is kept with water at 59-60 degrees C                  temperature.

TI Manufacture of cellulose fiber having anti-inflammatory function, involves soaking cellulose fibers in carbon dioxide fluid, entering anti-inflammatory drug to pre-swollen cellulose fiber and forming drug-loaded cellulose fibers.
AU ZHU W
   WANG H
   LONG J
   XIAO H
   SHI M
AE BINZHOU LOFTEX IND CO LTD (BINZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   NANTONG CELLULOSE FIBERS CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   ZHEJIANG JINCAI NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019008410
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of cellulose fiber having anti-inflammatory function, involves pre-swelling or pretreating cellulose fibers, adding additive to increase solubility of anti-inflammatory drugs in supercritical carbon dioxide fluids, indirectly dissolving hydrophilic anti-inflammatory drugs that are not easily soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid by supercritical microemulsion/anti-micelle method, adding anti-inflammatory drugs to tank of high-pressure equipment to remove air, passing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure inside the vessel to 8-30 MPa at 32-120 degrees C, soaking cellulose fibers in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, entering anti-inflammatory drug to pre-swollen cellulose fiber, continuously performing relative movement between drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and cellulose fiber, releasing pressure, and forming drug-loaded cellulose fibers capable of sustained release.
   USE - Manufacture of cellulose fiber having anti-inflammatory function.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides cellulose fiber having anti-inflammatory function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive is methanol, ethanol or acetone. The anti-inflammatory drug is chosen from tea polyphenols, indoleacetic acid, perillyl alcohol, cysteine, gallic acid, methionine, lichenic acid, ubiquinol, licorice flavonoids, cinchona, arginine, lactulose, melatonin or isoleucine. The addition amount of anti-inflammatory drug is 1-15 %mass with respect to content of cellulose fiber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: Cellulosic fibers include natural cellulose fibers and regenerated cellulose fibers. The natural cellulose fibers include cotton fibers and hemp fibers. The regenerated cellulose fiber comprises viscose fiber, high wet modulus viscose fiber and solvent spinning regenerated cellulose fiber. The supercritical microemulsion is formed using sodium bis(2-ethylhexyl)sulfonate succinate, ethanol, water and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.

PN CN108821315-A; CN108821315-B
TI Thermo-chemically decomposing circulating carbon dioxide and simultaneously decomposing water to dihydrogen comprises e.g. introducing water, iodine and sulfur dioxide to the Bunsen reaction device, stirring the reaction solution and mixing.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG Z
   ZHOU Z
   LIU J
   ZHU J
   HUANG Z
   YANG W
   CHENG J
   NI M
   CEN K
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201891925U
AB    NOVELTY - Thermo-chemically decomposing circulating carbon dioxide and simultaneously decomposing water to dihydrogen (H2) comprises e.g. (1) introducing water, iodine (I2) and sulfur dioxide to the Bunsen reaction device, and stirring the reaction solution at a constant rate to ensure uniform mixing, at 20-120 degrees C performing self-distribution thermal reaction; the chemical reaction is: iodine reacting with sulfur dioxide and water to form hydrogen iodide and sulfuric acid, adding excess iodine in the reaction ensures liquid-liquid phase separation of the mixed solution formed by the Bunsen reaction, forming an upper and lower layered hydrogen iodide phase and sulfuric acid phase solution; (2) performing multi-stage sulfuric acid concentration treatment on the separated sulfuric acid phase solution at 120-260 degrees C, 0.08-1.3 atm under adiabatic conditions; (3) heating the concentrated sulfuric acid to 800-900 degrees C to catalytically decompose to form sulfur dioxide, water and the final product oxygen.
   USE - The method is useful for thermo-chemically decomposing circulating carbon dioxide and simultaneously decomposing water to dihydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: innovatively combines the carbon dioxide mineralization technology with the thermo-chemical sulfur iodine open circuit to decompose water to produce hydrogen, mineralizes and fixes carbon dioxide under relatively mild reaction conditions, and simultaneously produces high value-added hydrogen; avoids the traditional hydrogen iodide and iodine rectification separation process in the hydrogen production cycle has higher thermal efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermo-chemically decomposing circulating carbon dioxide and simultaneously decomposing water to dihydrogen (H2) comprises (1) introducing water, iodine (I2) and sulfur dioxide to the Bunsen reaction device according to the ratio of the amount of the substance: 14-16:1.5-9:1, and stirring the reaction solution at a constant rate to ensure uniform mixing, at 20-120 degrees C performing self-distribution thermal reaction occurs at 1-2 atm to form an aqueous hydrogen iodide (HI) phase and sulfuric acid phase solution; the chemical reaction is: iodine reacting with sulfur dioxide and water to form hydrogen iodide and sulfuric acid, adding excess iodine in the reaction ensures liquid-liquid phase separation of the mixed solution formed by the Bunsen reaction, forming an upper and lower layered hydrogen iodide phase and sulfuric acid phase solution; (2) performing multi-stage sulfuric acid concentration treatment on the separated sulfuric acid phase solution at 120-260 degrees C, 0.08-1.3 atm under adiabatic conditions; (3) heating the concentrated sulfuric acid to 800-900 degrees C to catalytically decompose to form sulfur dioxide, water and the final product oxygen, the chemical reaction is: catalytically decomposing sulfuric acid with to form sulfur dioxide, water and oxygen, then returning sulfur dioxide, water and oxygen to the Bunsen reaction unit, where recycling sulfur dioxide, and water and discharging oxygen as a final product; (4) electrolyzing the hydrogen iodide phase solution obtained in the step (1), obtaining a concentrated hydrogen iodide solution on the cathode side of the electrodialysis cell, and returning the iodine and the diluted hydrogen iodide solution obtained on the anode side to the Bunsen reaction device; (5) spontaneously reacting the concentrated hydrogen iodide solution and the magnesium silicate natural mineral, controlling the reaction temperature at 20-90 degrees C, and the reaction forms magnesium iodide, water and silicon dioxide, and the hydrogen iodide solution is concentrated in step (4) is iodine entrained in the mixture constitutes a mixed liquid; (6) washing the mixed liquid by filtration to obtain a by-product silicon dioxide, and distilling the filtrate to obtain magnesium iodide n-hydrate (MgI2.nH2O) crystals and separated iodine , where returning iodine to the Bunsen reaction device in the step (1), (7) placing magnesium iodide n-hydrate (MgI2.nH2O) in a fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor, and then introducing any ratio of carbon dioxide, nitrogen gas and water vapor to carry out hydrolysis and carbonation reaction, and controlling the reaction temperature at 180-350 degrees C, finally obtaining hydrogen iodide vapor and product magnesite; the chemical reaction is: reacting magnesium iodide with water and carbon dioxide to produce magnesite and hydrogen iodide; and (8) introducing the generated hydrogen iodide steam into the hydrogen iodide catalytic decomposition reactor for thermal decomposition, and controlling the reaction temperature is at 300-500 degrees C to finally obtain iodine and product dihydrogen, and the chemical reaction is: catalytically decomposing hydrogen iodide to produce iodine and dihydrogen, and where iodine returns to the reuse of the Bunsen reaction device in step (1). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for thermo-chemically decomposing circulating carbon dioxide and simultaneously decomposing water to dihydrogen comprising a Bunsen reaction, where the device further comprises a liquid phase separating device, hydrogen iodide(HIx) concentrating device sulfuric acid enrichment facility, concentrated sulfuric acid catalytic decomposition device, a magnesium iodide generation reactor, a magnesium iodide distillation device, magnesium iodide hydrolysis and hydrogen iodide carbonation reactor catalytic decomposition reactor, the Bunsen reaction device connecting liquid phase separating device; liquid phase separation device respectively connecting HIx concentrating device and concentrating device sulfuric acid, sulfuric acid enrichment facility, concentrated sulfuric acid catalytic decomposition device, Bunsen reaction device are connected, HIx concentrating device is connecting Bunsen reaction device and a magnesium iodide generating reactor magnesium iodide generating reactor magnesium iodide distillation device connected, respectively connecting Bunsen reaction device and the magnesium iodide hydrolyzing carbonation reactor magnesium iodide distillation device, magnesium iodide hydrolysis carbonation reactor connecting to hydrogen iodide catalytic decomposition catalytic decomposition reactor, reactor and then connected with the Bunsen reaction device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium silicate natural mineral is serpentine or olivine.

PN CN114005557-A
TI Efficient double-circulating sodium cooled fast reactor generating system of supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, has low pressure precooler that is connected with cold side inlet of low pressure carbon dioxide heat exchanger.
AU BAI W
   YANG Y
   LI K
   YAO M
   LI H
   ZHANG Y
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202226062H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high pressure and high temperature regenerator (5) whose outlet of the cold side is communicated with the inlet of the cold side of the high pressure sodium-supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger (2). The outlet of the cold side of the low-pressure sodium-supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger (11) is communicated with the inlet of the low-pressure turbine (12) in the low-pressure supercritical carbon dioxide cycle. The outlet of the low pressure turbine is communicated with the inlet of the low pressure compressor (16) through the hot side of the low pressure regenerator (14) and the hot side of the low pressure pre-cooler (15) in turn. The outlet of the low pressure compressor is communicated with the cold side inlet of the low pressure sodium-supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger through the cold side of the waste heat exchanger (7) and the cold side of the low pressure regenerator in turn.
   USE - High-efficiency double-circulation sodium-cooled fast reactor generating system for supercritical carbon dioxide reactor used in nuclear energy power generation technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides a supercritical carbon dioxide into the stack of efficient double-circulating sodium-cooled fast reactor generating system, thus improving the main gas temperature so as to improve the generating efficiency. The system cancels the middle loop between the traditional reactor and power island, and improves the power generation efficiency, and realizes the good matching of the endothermic temperature range of super critical carbon dioxide power cycle and the exothermic window of the liquid metal sodium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working method of high-efficiency double-circulating sodium-cooled fast reactor generating system of supercritical carbon dioxide reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an integral system diagram of an efficient double-circulating sodium cooled fast reactor generating system of supercritical carbon dioxide reactor.
   Reactor (1)
   Low pressure regenerator (14)
   High pressure turbine (3)
   Low pressure regenerator (4)
   High pressure and high temperature regenerator (5)
   Waste heat exchanger (7)
   High pressure sodium-supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger (2)
   Low-pressure sodium-supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger (11)
   Low pressure turbine (12)
   Low pressure precooler (15)
   Low pressure compressor (16)
   High pressure re-compressor (10)

PN CN108341397-A; CN108341397-B
TI Flue gas recycling system comprises reaction furnace, flue gas carbon dioxide absorption device and absorbing liquid regenerating device and a nitrogen heating device, where reaction furnace main body has cylindrical structure.
AU ZHU X
   JIANG S
   LIU Y
   ZHANG N
   LIU J
AE ZHU X (ZHUX-Individual)
GA 2018618925
AB    NOVELTY - Flue gas recycling system comprises reaction furnace, flue gas carbon dioxide absorption device, carbon dioxide collecting device and absorbing liquid regenerating device and a nitrogen heating device, where reaction furnace main body has cylindrical structure, where both upper and lower has round table-shaped structure, coal pyrolysis unit (2) and synthesis unit (3), where smoke heat exchange unit (1) is provided with smoke inlet and top is provided with smoke gas outlet, where coal pyrolysis unit is provided with coal, coal pyrolysis units are provided with nitrogen inlet tube, where nitrogen introducing pipe is provided with exhaust hole and coal pyrolysis unit and synthesis gas unit are separated by stainless steel which is provided with uniform exhaust hole. The synthesis gas unit is provided with carbon dioxide inlet (301), where middle parts is provided with synthesis gas reaction area, and upper parts is provided with synthesis gas reaction zone (302) is filled with catalyst.
   USE - Flue gas recycling system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recycling flue gas, which involves:
   (A) performing flue gas through flue gas inlet into heat exchange unit at 800-900 degrees C and flowing out from the flue gas outlet;
   (B) entering flue gas into carbon dioxide absorbing device and contacting with spray carbon dioxide absorbent solution to form carbon dioxide rich liquid;
   (C) preheating double-layer pipeline, carbon dioxide rich synthesis gas and heating automatic temperature-controlling heater to desorb carbon dioxide from absorption liquid to form barren and barren carbon dioxide and recycling through the nitrogen introducing tube;
   (D) introducing nitrogen to coal pyrolysis unit at 400-800 degrees C, generating pyrolysis reaction;
   (E) transferring pyrolysis gas and nitrogen mixed gas through holes in stainless steel walls on synthesis gas unit to form carbon dioxide through carbon dioxide inlet of synthesis gas unit through lower parts to syngas reaction zone;
   (F) mixing with pyrolysis gas under catalysis of hydrogen and carbon monoxide reaction to form synthesis gas; and
   (G) generating synthesis gas by synthesis gas outlet and controlling the temperature of reaction area to 648-850 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of flue gas recycling system.
   Smoke heat exchange unit (1)
   Coal pyrolysis unit (2)
   Synthesis unit (3)
   Carbon dioxide inlet (301)
   Synthesis gas reaction zone (302)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The diameter ratio of flue gas heat exchange unit, coal pyrolysis unit and synthesis gas unit is 1:1:1. Preferred Conditions: The synthesis gas reaction zone is filled with catalyst is lignite semi-coke. The synthesis unit pressure is 0.1 megaPascal and coal pyrolysis unit pressure is 0.3-0.5 megaPascal.

PT P
PN CN112295548-A
TI Preparing polyamino porous composite material comprises preparing porous material loaded with carbon dioxide molecules grafted modified polyamine, and removing carbon dioxide molecules grafted on the polyamine.
AU XIAO A
   LI Y
   ZHANG H
   YAN K
   LIN Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC QINGDAO SAFETY ENG INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202116417C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyamino porous composite material, comprises (i) preparing porous material loaded with carbon dioxide molecules grafted modified polyamine, and (ii) removing carbon dioxide molecules grafted on the polyamine in the porous material loaded with carbon dioxide molecules grafted and modified polyamine.
   USE - The polyamino porous composite material is useful as a hydrogen sulfide adsorption material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyamino porous composite material has excellent absorption capacity for hydrogen sulfide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polyamino porous composite material obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) includes first contacting the polyamine with carbon dioxide so that carbon dioxide molecules are grafted on the polyamine to obtain carbon dioxide molecule grafted modified polyamine, and then loading carbon dioxide molecule grafted modified polyamine on the porous material by impregnation method. The step (i) includes secondary contacting polyamine with porous material under the carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain polyamine-based porous composite material. The method comprises removing carbon dioxide molecules grafted on the polyamine from the porous material loaded with carbon dioxide molecules grafted to the modified polyamine by vacuum treatment or inert gas purging activation treatment. The vacuum treatment conditions include: a vacuum degree of -0.08 to -0.1 MPa, a vacuum treatment temperature of 60-150 degrees C, and vacuum treatment time of 2-24 hours. The inert gas purging activation treatment is carried out at 50-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: The weight average molecular weight of polyamine is 50-200,000, preferably 100-10,000, and the polyamine is polyethyleneimine. The porous material is metal organic framework material, covalent organic framework material, porous molecular sieve and/or porous silica, the pore size of the porous material is 0.4-20 nm, and the specific surface area of the porous material is greater than or equal to 300 m2/g, preferably 300-7000 m2/g. In step (i): the loading amount of the polyamine component is 30-300 wt.% of the porous material.

PT P
PN JP2022067482-A
TI Structure for recovering carbon dioxide for use in carbon-dioxide collection apparatus has reaction layer comprising substrate, carrier, and adsorbent for adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide carried on the carrier, and heating layer.
AU KUNIEDA M
   KAWAGUCHI A
   HIGUCHI T
AE IBIDEN CO LTD (IBIG-C)
GA 202265854B
AB    NOVELTY - A structure for recovering carbon dioxide comprises reaction layer comprising substrate, carrier, and adsorbent for adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide carried on the carrier, and heating layer for heating the reaction layer. Width of the reaction layer between the two heating layers is 20-100 cm, and volume ratio of the reaction layer with respect to total volume of the reaction layer and the heating layer is 0.6-0.9.
   USE - A structure for recovering carbon dioxide for use in carbon-dioxide collection apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The structure is capable of efficiently adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide and suppressing the cost of desorbing and releasing carbon dioxide to a low level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Substrate constituting the reaction layer is made of a honeycomb structure in which a set of through holes serving as a flow passage of a gas containing carbon dioxide are arranged in the extending direction of the through-hole through a partition wall. Honeycomb structure is provided with a substrate and a structure which is a carrier. Base body is selected from carbon, metal, and/or highly heat-conductive ceramic. Heating layer is made of a plate-like heat-transfer body, is made of a heat transfer body having a set of through-holes serving as a flow passage for fluid for heating, and is adhered to the reaction layer. Honeycomb structure and heat-transfer body are laminated so that the flow passage of the honeycomb structure constituting the reaction layer and the flow passage of the heat-transfer body constituting the heating layer cross each other. Preferred Properties: Thermal conductivity of the honeycomb structure is 15-300 W/m.K.

TI Catalyst useful for preparing low-carbon olefin by            carbon dioxide hydrogenation comprises carbon dioxide            adsorbing metal oxides and molecular sieves.
AU SU J
   WANG Y
   LIU S
   ZHOU H
   LIU C
   JIAO W
   ZHANG L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 202041507C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carbon dioxide adsorbing                metal oxides and molecular sieves, where the carbon                dioxide adsorbing metal oxide is indium-based                oxides and/or gallium-based oxides.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing                low-carbon olefin by carbon dioxide hydrogenation                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) manufacturing a catalyst comprising                combining carbon dioxide adsorbing metal oxide and                molecular sieve with each other (e.g. individually                packaged and/or mechanically mixed with each other,                preferably with each other), where the carbon                dioxide adsorbing metal oxide is indium-based oxide                and/or gallium-based oxide; and
   (2) producing low-carbon olefin by carbon                dioxide hydrogenation comprising reacting raw                material containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen to                obtain a lower-carbon olefin through a bed                containing the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide                  adsorbing metal oxide and molecular sieve exist                  in a form independent of each other e.g. a                  separate package and/or a mechanical mixture of                  each other. The molecular sieve is a                  carbon-containing molecular sieve obtained by                  treatment. The reaction temperature is at 340-460                  degrees C, the reaction pressure of 0.5-8 MPa,                  and/or the volumetric space velocity of 800-8000                  h1. The indium-based oxide includes the following                  molecular formula In XaOe, where X is a                  transition metal, a lanthanide metal, an element                  and/or element in Group IIIA, the value range of                  a is 0-4 (preferably, the value range of a is                  greater than or equal to 0 and not less than 4,                  more preferably 0.3-3), and e is the total number                  of oxygen atoms required to satisfy the valence                  of each element in the catalyst. The transition                  metal is zirconium, vanadium, chromium, manganese                  and /or the lanthanide metal is cerium and/or the                  group IIIA element is gallium and aluminum. The                  molecular sieve is silico-phosphoaluminum                  molecular sieve and/or phosphoro aluminum                  molecular sieve, preferably a phosphorus aluminum                  molecular sieve.The molecular sieve is AlPO                  and/or SAPO molecular sieve, preferably AlPO-18,                  AlPO-17, AlPO-34, AlPO-14 , AlPO-11, AlPO-5,                  SAPO-18, SAPO-17, SAPO-11 and/or SAPO-5. The                  weight ratio of the carbon dioxide adsorptive                  metal oxide and molecular sieve is (1: 6)-(6: 1),                  preferably (1: 4)-(4: 1). In the reaction gas,                  the volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen                  is 1: (0.5-5), preferably 1: (0.5-3).

PN CN110921622-A; CN211998812-U
TI Integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system of methanol water vapor and hydrogen mixed gas comprises three-phase heat exchange device, reforming device includes heating chamber provided outside reaction chamber.
AU YUE X
   LI J
   LV S
   HAN D
   ZHAO J
   YUE Y
   CHEN F
AE CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECH (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 2020259213
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system of methanol water vapor and hydrogen mixed gas comprises a three-phase heat exchange device, a reforming device, a hydrogen separation device, a steam trap, a water-cooled heat exchanger, a refrigerator and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device. The reforming device includes a reaction chamber, and a heating chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber. The reaction chamber includes an upper reaction chamber suitable for reforming the methanol water vapor and a lower reaction chamber suitable for reforming the hydrogen mixed residual gas. The upper reaction chamber is in communication with the lower reaction chamber. The upper reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler. The lower reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler. The upper reaction chamber opens a first inlet and a first outlet. A second inlet is opened in the lower reaction chamber.
   USE - Used as integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system of methanol water vapor and hydrogen mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves hydrogen production efficiency; has optimized and simplified structure; and can be used as a small-scale hydrogen production equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system of methanol water vapor and hydrogen mixed gas comprises a three-phase heat exchange device, a reforming device, a hydrogen separation device, a steam trap, a water-cooled heat exchanger, a refrigerator and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device. The reforming device includes a reaction chamber, and a heating chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber. The reaction chamber includes an upper reaction chamber suitable for reforming the methanol water vapor and a lower reaction chamber suitable for reforming the hydrogen mixed residual gas. The upper reaction chamber is in communication with the lower reaction chamber. The upper reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler. The lower reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler. The upper reaction chamber opens a first inlet and a first outlet. A second inlet is opened in the lower reaction chamber. The first inlet is connected to the methanol water vapor inlet pipe. The first outlet is connected to the inlet of the hydrogen separation device. The hydrogen separation device is connected with a pure hydrogen outlet pipe and a carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet pipe. The carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet pipe is sequentially connected to a three-phase heat exchange device, a steam trap, a water-cooled heat exchanger and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device. The carbon dioxide liquefaction device is connected to the carbon dioxide outlet pipe and the hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe. The hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe is connected to the second inlet. An air pump for raising the delivery pressure of the hydrogen mixed residual gas in the tube is provided on the hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe. The methanol water vapor enters the tube. Pure hydrogen outlet pipes are connected to three-phase heat exchange devices. The methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a liquid pump. The pump pressure of the liquid pump is 18-50 MPa. The water cooling heat exchanger is connected to the water cooling tower. The operating temperature of the water-cooled heat exchanger is 18-30.8 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing high-pressure hydrogen using high-pressure hydrogen production system comprising (i) liquid pump sends methanol water into the methanol steam pipe inlet pipe, where the pump pressure is 18-50 MPa, heating methanol water and vaporizing into methanol steam into the reaction chamber on the reformer, reforming methanol steam in the reaction chamber on the reformer to produce hydrogen, sending the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to the hydrogen separation device, where the gas phase components of the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and 65-75% carbon monoxide are hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, (ii) hydrogen absorption tube in the hydrogen separation device separates the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and collecting separated pure hydrogen from the output of the pure hydrogen output tube, the remaining carbon dioxide mixture residual gas is output from the carbon dioxide mixture residual gas outlet pipe, controlling the carbon dioxide mixture residual gas by a liquid pump to control the pressure, and the water-cooled heat exchanger controls the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixture residual gas, carbon dioxide liquefaction separation, where the gas phase components of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are 25-45% hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0-3% water, and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, where the pressure controlled by the liquid pump is 18-50 MPa, and the temperature controlled by the water-cooled heat exchanger is 18-30.8 degrees C, (iii) carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is made into liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in the carbon dioxide separator, and the liquid carbon dioxide is output and collected, the components of the hydrogen mixed residual gas are 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, and 3-9% carbon monoxide, (iv) sending the residual hydrogen gas mixture into the lower reaction chamber of the reformer, dispensing water to make a reformed mixture gas, and distributing water according to the content of carbon monoxide, reforming the mixed hydrogen remaining gas into water in the lower reaction chamber to form a reformed gas mixture, where the gas phase components of the reformed gas mixture are 62-77% hydrogen, 22-27% carbon dioxide, and 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide, so that the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the reformed gas mixture is close to the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide, and (v) reformed mixed gas enters the upper reaction chamber and mixing with the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and the mixed gas of the reformed mixed gas and hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide is mixed in the upper reaction chamber and sending to hydrogen separation, hydrogen separation operation is performed again in the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the integrated high-pressure hydrogen production system of methanol water vapor and hydrogen mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pure hydrogen outlet pipe is connected to a hydrogen storage tank, and pure hydrogen is sent into the hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of a liquid pump, the hydrogen storage tank is connected to a hydrogenation machine, and the hydrogen storage tank is connected to a hydrogenation machine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The output pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are output through the three-phase heat exchange device after heat exchange and cooling. The methanol water is heat-exchanged and vaporized into methanol water vapor through a three-phase heat exchange device. The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.

PT P
PN EP3906981-A1; WO2021224502-A1; EP3906981-A4
TI Extracting extract compounds from material for use in e.g., food, by contacting carbon dioxide with material, contacting carbon dioxide comprising dissolved extract compound with sorbent, and recirculating regenerated carbon dioxide.
AU DE VRIES T J
   HOFLAND G W
   WOERLEE G F
   VALE LOPES P D
   VALE L P D
   DE V T J
AE FEYECON DEV & IMPLEMENTATION BV (FEYE-Non-standard)
   FEYECON DEV & IMPLEMENTATION BV (FEYE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4816X
AB    NOVELTY - At least one extract compound is extracted from a material by (a) contacting carbon dioxide with material to dissolve an amount of greater than or equal to 1 extract compound from the material into the carbon dioxide; (b) contacting carbon dioxide comprising greater than or equal to 1 dissolved extract compound with a sorbent to sorb greater than or equal to 1 extract compound onto the sorbent and for regenerating carbon dioxide; (c) recirculating the regenerated carbon dioxide; and (d) repeating recirculating at a constant density of carbon dioxide of 100-1000 kg/m3, and repeating recirculating for greater than or equal to 10 cycles.
   USE - Extraction of at least one extract compound from a material (claimed) for use in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows easy extraction of extract compounds from material to valorize these in an economical manner, without large solvent streams. The extracted compound is easily retrieved as a concentrate from the process, for the case that its retrieval is important to minimize waste or that it in itself is a valuable product, e.g. a pharmaceutically acceptable product. The method provides a cost effective way of using carbon dioxide extraction, and provides low amounts of harmful (extracted) compounds in the treated material, typically less than ppm, and at times, much lower. The extraction can be done with minimally affecting nutritional, flavor, and mouthfeel aspects and retrieving such compounds as valuable concentrates for pharmaceutical or other applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) assembly for extraction of at least one extract compound from a material, comprising pressure vessel which encloses pump in the pressure vessel for circulating carbon dioxide through the pressure vessel, sorbent container in the pressure vessel for holding a sorbent, and material container in the pressure vessel for holding the material comprising greater than or equal to 1 extract compound, where pump is fluidly coupled to material container and to sorbent container for circulating carbon dioxide through material and through sorbent at a density of 100-1000 kg/m3; and
   (2) extract plant material comprising less than or equal to 1 ppm, preferably less than or equal to 10 ppb pesticide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: During recirculating, carbon dioxide has pressure drop for one cycle of less than or equal to 1 MPa, preferably less than or equal to 0.1 MPa. During recirculating, carbon dioxide is maintained at a substantially constant temperature, preferably within a constant range of less than plus minus 1 degree. Constant temperature is 10-140 degrees C, preferably 40-70 degrees C. Preferred Compositions: Sorbent is selected from activated carbon, silicates, natural and synthetic mineral clays, aluminosilicates, phyllosilicates, aluminum phyllosilicates, zeolite material, maltodextrins, and/or super absorbing polymers. Weight ratio of carbon dioxide to material is 10:1-1:1. Carbon dioxide comprises 10-100% of maximum solubility of water in carbon dioxide at constant density and temperature, preferably 50-95% of maximum solubility of water in carbon dioxide at constant density and temperature.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Material is plant material, preferably where the plant material before extraction comprises less than or equal to 1% extract compound, and/or ratio of material to sorbent is 5:1-1000:1, preferably 20:1-200:1. Material is a plant material selected from tree bark, root, needles, beans, pods, seeds, hard fruit, flowers, and/or woods, preferably beans selected from coffee beans, cocoa beans, soy beans, and peas, and/or preferably plant material comprises pre-processed plant material. At least one extract compound is selected from polycyclic aromatic compound, halogenated compound, terpenoid, cannabinoid, triterpenoids, pesticide, and/or converted pesticide, preferably converted pesticide is selected from microbially, enzymatically, (photo) chemically, and incompletely combusted pesticide. Preferred Device: At least one of the sorbent container and the material container each comprises a permeable wall, allowing the carbon dioxide to respectively entering the respective sorbent container, entering the material container, exiting the sorbent container, and/or exiting the material container. Sorbent container and material container are fluidly coupled in series to one another, in particular where actuator, sorbent container, and material container are fluidly coupled in series to one another. The pump is adapted to overcome a pressure drop over the assembly of maximum 5 bar, preferably less than 1 bar.

PN CN109315454-A
TI Controlling dough fermentation by detecting weight information of dough in fermentation tank, calculating rate threshold and cumulative amount threshold and obtaining actual rate and actual accumulation of carbon dioxide.
AU XIONG M
   WANG D
   ZUO T
   LIANG S
   LIU M
AE ZHUHAI GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 2019174313
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling dough fermentation involves (a) detecting the weight information of dough in fermentation tank, calculating rate threshold and cumulative amount threshold for generating carbon dioxide by dough weight information, obtaining actual rate and actual accumulation of carbon dioxide in fermentation tank, decreasing the fermentation temperature when the actual rate of carbon dioxide production exceeds the rate threshold, and vice versa, and controlling the end of fermentation when the actual accumulation of carbon dioxide exceeds the cumulative threshold.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling dough fermentation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) fermentation device comprises fermentation tank body and heating device, where the heating device is connected to fermentation tank for heating the heating device, further comprises dough fermentation control system, arranging weight detecting device and carbon dioxide detecting device on the fermentation tank, and connecting dough fermentation control system to heating device; and
   (2) method of controlling fermentation device involves using control device to sends control command to heating device to control the dough fermentation process, decreasing the fermentation temperature in fermentation tank when the actual rate of carbon dioxide production exceeds the rate threshold, increasing the fermentation temperature in fermentation tank when the actual rate of carbon dioxide production is below the rate threshold, and controlling the end of fermentation when the actual accumulated amount of carbon dioxide exceeds the cumulative amount threshold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The actual production rate and accumulation amount of carbon dioxide in fermentation tank are calculated by periodically detecting the carbon dioxide content in fermentation tank. Preferred Device: The system comprises weight detecting device, carbon dioxide detecting device and control device, where the weight detecting device for detecting the quality of dough in fermenting tank, carbon dioxide detecting device for detecting the content of carbon dioxide in fermentation tank, connecting control device to weight detecting device, calculating a rate threshold and cumulative amount threshold for generating carbon dioxide, connecting signal to carbon dioxide detecting device, calculating actual rate and actual accumulated amount of carbon dioxide in fermentation tank, using control signal for adjusting the fermentation temperature and ending the fermentation. The fermentation device further comprises (a) temperature detecting device for detecting the temperature of space in fermentation tank in real time, and connecting the temperature detecting device and dough fermentation control system signal, (b) humidity detecting device for detecting the temperature of space in fermentation tank in real time, and connecting the humidity detecting device and dough fermentation control system signal connection. The fermentation device is chosen from oven, bread machine, electric rice cooker and microwave oven. Preferred Method: The method involves detecting space temperature value and space humidity value in fermentation tank in real time, and controlling the dough fermentation process in accordance with detected space temperature value and space humidity value, reducing the fermentation temperature in fermentation tank by reducing the duty cycle of heating device and increasing the working duty ratio in fermentation tank to increase the fermentation temperature in fermentation tank.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and environmentally                friendly; has convenient and controllable nitrogen                doping content of the product, and good                repeatability. The catalyst is environmentally                friendly, and has a high yield, and potential                economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, weighing                  the catalyst precursor based on the molar ratio                  of strontium, iron, and cobalt is                  0.2-2:1:0.5-1.5, weighing citric acid at 0.8-2                  times the molar amount of iron, dissolving in                  water, placing in a water bath at 80 degrees C,                  drying at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, and                  performing calcination at 600-1200 degrees C for                  2-10 hours to obtain catalyst precursor. The                  introduction of nitrogen atoms in the catalyst                  enters the gap in-situ to promote the formation                  of cobalt carbide in the reaction atmosphere. The                  catalyst is modified by introducing copper oxide,                  zinc oxide, and aluminum oxide to increase the                  alcohol yield.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of electrode catalyst layer for                a carbon dioxide electrolysis cell, which involves                applying and drying, on a substrate, a composition                comprising metallic catalyst supported on carbon                material, ion-conductive material, and hydrophilic                polymer, and further including reducing the content                of hydrophilic polymer contained in the composition                by extraction with an organic solvent, or reducing                the content of hydrophilic polymer after                application of composition by firing the formed                composition layer at a high temperature;
   (2) electrode for a carbon dioxide                electrolysis cell, where catalyst layer is formed                on an electrode substrate;
   (3) carbon dioxide electrolysis cell                comprising anode unit having an anode which                oxidizes water or hydroxy ions into oxygen and                anode solution-flow path through which an anode                solution is supplied to anode, cathode unit having                a cathode which is equipped with the electrode and                which reduces carbon dioxide into a carbon                compound, and a gas-flow path through which carbon                dioxide is supplied to cathode, and separator                separating anode unit and cathode unit from each                other; and
   (4) carbon dioxide electrolysis apparatus                comprising the electrolysis cell and an electric                power unit supplying electric currents between                anode and cathode in the electrolysis cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Carbon material are                  selected from carbon particles, carbon nanotubes,                  and/or graphenes. Metallic catalyst is at least                  one metal or oxide selected from gold (Au),                  silver (Ag), copper (Cu), platinum (Pt),                  palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), cobalt (Co), iron                  (Fe), manganese (Mn), titanium (Ti), cadmium                  (Cd), zinc (Zn), indium (In), gallium (Ga), lead                  (Pb), and/or tin (Sn), and metallic catalyst also                  has at least one structure selected from                  nanoparticles, nanostructures, and/or nanowires.                  The anode has a 1st face keeping in contact with                  the separator and a 2nd face fronting on the                  anode solution flow path so that anode solution                  can keep in contact with the anode. The cathode                  has a 1st face fronting on the cathode solution                  flow path and a 2nd face fronting on the gas-flow                  path, and the cathode solution-flow path is                  placed between the separator and the cathode that                  the cathode solution can keep in contact with                  both the separator and the cathode. The anode                  comprises a substrate of mesh material, punching                  material, porous material, and/or metal fiber                  sintered material, and further the anode                  comprises a substrate which contains anode                  catalyst or comprises a catalyst layer which                  contains anode catalyst and which is formed on                  the surface of the substrate. Substrate comprises                  a metal material containing Ti, Ni, and/or Fe,                  and the anode catalyst comprises a metal material                  containing Ni, Fe, Co, Mn, La, Ru, Li, Ir, In,                  Sn, and/or Ti, or comprises a metal oxide                  material containing the metal. Anode and cathode                  solutions comprise hydroxy ion, hydrogen ion,                  potassium ion, sodium ion, lithium ion, chloride                  ion, bromide ion, iodide ion, nitrate ion,                  sulfate ion, phosphate ion, borate ion, and/or                  hydrogen carbonate ion. Separator is selected                  from cation exchange membrane, anion exchange                  membrane, and/or porous membrane. Preferred                  Properties: The catalyst layer has thickness of                  5-200 mu m. Pore-diameter distribution measured                  by a mercury injection method shows the highest                  peak of frequency distribution in the diameter                  range of 5-200 mu m, provided that the diameter                  and the logarithmic differential pore volume are                  plotted on the horizontal and vertical axes,                  respectively. Nanoparticles have a mean diameter                  of 1-15 nm. Metallic catalyst has a weight per                  unit area of catalyst layer in a range of 0.01-5                  mg/cm2. Preferred Condition: Firing step includes                  heating the composition layer at less than or                  equal to 400 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Organic solvent is                  selected from acetone, ethanol, methanol,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylacetamide, and/or                  N,N-dimethylformamide. Carbon compound produced                  by reduction of carbon dioxide contains methane,                  ethane, ethylene, methanol, ethanol, and/or                  ethylene glycol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Hydrophilic polymer is                  selected from polyvinyl alcohol,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, methoxypolyethylene oxide                  methacrylate, polyacrylic acid,                  polyethyleneimine, polyvinylamine, cyclodextrin,                  and methylcellulose. Ion-conductive material is a                  cation-exchange resin or an anion-exchange                  resin.

PT P
PN CN113292984-A
TI Preparing pickering emulsion with carbon            dioxide/double-response surfactant includes mixing            carboxy-modified nano silicon dioxide particles with            carbon dioxide/redox double response surfactant, adding            in oil-aqueous phases and emulsifying.
AU REN H
   SUN C
   SUN Y
   SUN S
   HAN Q
   ZHAO Y
   HU S
AE DONGYING SPRING PETROLEUM ENG TECHNOLOGY (DONG-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2021A0561G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pickering emulsion with carbon                dioxide/double-response surfactant, comprises (a)                preparing carboxy modified nano silicon dioxide                particles, (b) preparing carbon dioxide/ redox                double response surfactant by performing                Friedel-Crafts reaction of ferrocene and amino acid                in an organic solvent, where the concentration of                aqueous phase used as reference is 0.1-2.0x 10-3                mol/l, and (c) mixing carboxy-modified nano silicon                dioxide particles with carbon dioxide/ redox double                response surfactant, adding into oil-aqueous                phases, and emulsifying at specific rotating speed                by a vortex oscillator to obtain the product.
   USE - The pickering emulsion is useful in the field                of chemical agent of oil-gas field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion: has double stimulation response                performance and good stability; and does not                require to maintain stable state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   the pickering emulsion with carbon                dioxide/double-response surfactant prepared by the                above mentioned method; and
   use method of pickering emulsion with carbon                dioxide/double-response surfactant, comprising                introducing carbon dioxide into the emulsion at                room temperature to 25&#176; C to cause carbon                dioxide/double-response surfactant lose activity,                detach from the surface of the carboxy-modified                nano silica particles and change the hydrophobicity                and hydrophilicity of the surface of the                carboxy-modified nano-silica particles, initiating                demulsification of the emulsion, introducing                nitrogen into the emulsion at room temperature to                restore the activity of the carbon                dioxide/double-response surfactant, re-absorbing on                the surface of the carboxy-modified nano silicon                dioxide, homogenizing the system to obtain the                stable pickering emulsion, adding an oxidizing                agent to the emulsion to oxidize the ferrocene                group of the carbon dioxide/double-response                surfactant, make carboxy modified nano silica to be                more hydrophilic and initiate demulsification of                the emulsion, adding reducing agent to the emulsion                to reduce the ferrocene group and recover it's                original properties, and homogenizing the system to                obtain stable pickering emulsion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  using amino propyl triethoxy silane and silicon                  dioxide nano particles in toluene as organic                  solvent to reflux and react, washing with water                  and ethanol for multiple times to obtain an                  amino-modified nano particles, adding the                  amino-modified nanoparticles, succinic anhydride                  and triethylamine in N, N-dimethyl formamide as                  organic solvent to react, and washing with water                  and ethanol for multiple times to obtain the                  carboxy-modified nano silicon dioxide particles.                  The step (b) comprises refluxing and reacting                  6-amino caproic acid with thionyl chloride in                  methylene dichloride as organic solvent to obtain                  acyl chloride, reacting acyl chloride with                  ferrocene under a nitrogen atmosphere, taking                  anhydrous aluminum chloride as catalyst, and                  purifying to obtain carbon dioxide/ redox double                  response surfactant. The step (c) comprises                  emulsifying at rotating speed of 8000-10000                  revolutions/minute by the vortex oscillator to                  obtain the product. referred Components: in the                  step (c), the primary particle diameter of the                  carboxy-modified nano silica particle is 20-200                  nm, and the concentration of aqueous phase used                  as reference is 0.051-2%. in the step (c), the                  oil phase is toluene, dichloromethane, isooctane,                  dodecane or paraffin oil, and the volume fraction                  of the oil phase in the pickering emulsion is                  5-90%. The amount of carboxy-modified nano silica                  particles are 0.05-2 wt.%, and the amount of                  carbon dioxide/ redox double response surfactant                  is 0.1-2 mM based on water.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

   USE - (I) is useful for trapping gas including acid                gas and carbon dioxide; in carbon dioxide                cyclization catalyst; and for capturing carbon                dioxide or for catalyzing the cyclization of carbon                dioxide and epoxy compounds (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multistage hole polymer i.e. substituted                N2,N4-bis((4-(bis(4-ethylphenyl)amino)phenyl)(4,6-diethyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-ylamino)methyl)-N6-(1-(4-(bis(4-ethylphenyl)amino)phenyl)-2-(4,6-diethyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)ethyl)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triamine                of formula (I) is new.
   R1-R3 = -H, -NO2, amino group, primary amino                group, -C(=O)H, -CH3, -OH, -C(=O)OH, 1-6C                hydrocarbon group, -F, -Br, -Cl or -I.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) use of (I) for trapping gas including acid                gas and carbon dioxide comprising coordinating                metal ions in multistage pore polymer to prepare                polymer based catalyst, or coordinating zinc ions                in multistage pore polymer to produce polymer-based                catalyst that catalyzes the integration of carbon                dioxide capture and conversion, where temperature                of capture process is 273-373 k, and partial                pressure of gas is not greater than 1 bar;                and
   (3) carbon dioxide cyclization catalyst                comprising multistage pore polymer and coordinated                metal ion, where metal ion is zinc ion including                zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc sulfate, zinc                nitrate or zinc acetylacetonate, and loaded metal                ion is loaded in an amount of not less than 0.1                wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises dispersing monomer                  1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triamine and monomer i.e.                  substituted 4,4',4''-nitrilotribenzaldehyde of                  formula (III) in polymerization solvent, carrying                  out polymerization under protective atmosphere,                  separating reaction structure by solid-liquid                  separation, washing and drying to obtain product.                  Preferred Components: The multistage pore polymer                  has balanced microporous structure and mesoporous                  structure, where content of microporous structure                  is 35-45%; and balance is mesoporous structure.                  The molar ratio between                  1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triamine and monomer (III)                  is 0.6:1-1.2-1:1. The cyclization temperature is                  80-120 degrees C; partial pressure of carbon                  dioxide is 1-4 MPa; and epoxy compound i.e.                  substituted oxirane of formula (IV). Preferred                  Method: The polymerization temperature is 150-200                  degrees C. The method comprises washing product                  with acetone, tetrahydrofuran and                  dichloromethane, and then freeze drying.R4-R7 = 1-7C hydrocarbon group, 6-24C                  aromatic hydrocarbon group, 1-7C alkoxy group,                  6-24C aromatic oxy group, halogenated 1-7C                  hydrocarbon group or cyclic group.

PN US2021171345-A1
TI Carbon dioxide-steam reforming process for producing hydrocarbon fuel, involves contacting gaseous mixture comprising hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water with catalyst, where rate of carbon formation is less than baseline rate.
AU ORTIZ-TORAL P
   WANGEROW J
   LINCK M B
   MARKER T
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 202162989Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-steam reforming process (10) involves contacting a gaseous mixture comprising hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water with a catalyst (6) to produce a stream of a synthesis gas product having a molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide of (1.5:1)-(2.3:1), where the process includes a rate of carbon formation that is less than a baseline rate of carbon formation of a baseline process, in which the water of the gaseous mixture is replaced with an equimolar amount of oxygen as carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-steam reforming process for producing hydrocarbon fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables carbon dioxide-steam reforming process with excellent overall thermodynamics, catalyst stability, and process efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show schematic views illustrating dry reforming and carbon dioxide steam reforming processes.
   Hydrocarbon-containing feedstock (1)
   Carbon dioxide-containing oxidant (2)
   Water-containing antioxidant (3)
   Catalyst (6)
   Carbon dioxide steam reforming process (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting is performed by flowing the gaseous mixture through a reactor vessel containing the catalyst, at a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) from (0.1-2.5) hour-1, to provide the synthesis gas product as an effluent from the reactor.Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a solid support comprising cerium oxide and including platinum and rhodium deposited thereon. The gaseous mixture comprises natural gas or methane from a renewable methane source. The catalyst comprises a noble metal on a solid support comprising cerium oxide. The noble metal is chosen from platinum, rhodium, rubidium, palladium, silver, osmium, iridium, and gold, preferably platinum and rhodium.Preferred Composition: The content of the rhodium is 0.05-5 wt.% of the catalyst. The content of the platinum is 0.05-5 wt.% of the catalyst. The gaseous mixture comprises at least 100 mol-ppm of total sulfur. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and a conversion of the hydrocarbons of at least 85%, are maintained for at least 500 hours of operation. The gaseous mixture comprises aromatic and olefinic hydrocarbons in a combined amount of 1-25 mol%. The content of the cerium oxide is at least 95 wt.% of the solid support.Preferred Conditions: The contacting is performed at 649-816degrees Celsius, and a gauge pressure of 0-517 kPa. The contacting of the flowing stream of the gaseous mixture and catalyst is carried out at 649-760degrees Celsius.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbons include methane.

GA 2019131515
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining surface temperature data and carbon dioxide concentration data in preset time. The surface temperature data and the carbon dioxide concentration data are associated with a detection time point. The preset time is divided into multiple cycles. The obtained surface temperature data processing value and carbon dioxide concentration data processing value are determined by using a singular value decomposition process. Related coefficient of the surface temperature data and carbon dioxide concentration data is determined. Surface temperature and carbon dioxide concentration correlation is obtained according to the related coefficient.
   USE - Method for obtaining correlation between surface temperature and carbon dioxide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables analyzing surface temperature data and the carbon dioxide concentration data by singular value decomposition process, which requires less expert experience to obtain results with practical reference values.
