PN DE102019107440-A1; WO2020193410-A1; CN113811379-A; EP3941611-A1; US2022193607-A1
TI Solid reactor for storing and releasing carbon dioxide, has inlet that is provided for feeding in fluids and outlet for discharging fluids, interior of housing having two different solids provided for storing thermal energy.
AU SPECHT E
AE UNIV MAGDEBURG VON GUERICKE OTTO (MAGD-C)
   UNIV MAGDEBURG VON GUERICKE OTTO (MAGD-C)
   UNIV MAGDEBURG VON GUERICKE OTTO (MAGD-C)
GA 2020941524
AB    NOVELTY - The solid reactor has a fluid-tight housing which including an interior space, an inlet for feeding in fluids and an outlet for feeding out fluids. The interior of the housing is filled with two different solids. The solid is provided for storing thermal energy and the other solid is provided for reversible storage and release of carbon dioxide. The housing comprises components which are designed for the introduction or discharge of the solids. The area of the inlet and the area of the outlet of fluids shut-off devices, which are designed to change the pressure in the interior of the solids reactor and to introduce fluids into the solids reactor and to discharge fluids emerging from the solids reactor. The solids for storing and releasing carbon dioxide are selected from calcium oxide and calcium oxide-containing substances selected from limestones and dolomites.
   USE - Solid reactor for storing and releasing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy-efficient separation of carbon dioxide is enabled from combustion products. The annoying use of waste heat is eliminated from carbonization and the time-consuming energy generation of calcination. The energetic coupling of the fluid flows achieves a high level of energy efficiency, so that only a small amount of external energy has to be introduced into the system to carry out the corresponding reaction processes of carbonization and calcination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The solids for storing thermal energy are selected from inert mineral materials such as quartz, granite, silicon dioxide, igneous rocks, silicon carbides, zirconium oxides, metallic phase change materials, cast iron, gray cast iron or mixtures of the solids. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for storing and releasing carbon dioxide, has solid reactor and a compressor for compressing the carbon dioxide-containing fluid, which is introduced through the inlet of the solids reactor. The compressor are designed such that it removes the carbon dioxide-depleted fluid from the reactor discharged by the outlet of the solids reactor, adiabatically expanded, and a countercurrent recuperator which is designed for the heat exchange of the compressed fluid, which contains carbon dioxide, and the fluid depleted of carbon dioxide. A region of the inlet and feed device for heated fluids is arranged to heat the carbon dioxide-containing fluid after the heating in the countercurrent recuperator and before entering the inlet of the solids reactor. The compressors compress and burn air and natural gas and heating oil and blow in the combustion gas through the feed device. The compressors compress and burn air and natural gas and heating oil and blow in the combustion gas via the feed device; and
   (2) a method for storing and releasing carbon dioxide, involves providing solid reactor for the storage and release of carbon dioxide and introducing a fluid containing carbon dioxide into the solids reactor. The fluid is compressed such that the partial pressure of the carbon dioxide is 1 to 2 bar. The introduction of the carbon dioxide-containing fluid is ended when the temperature inside the solids reactor is 910 to 960. The pressure inside the solids reactor is less than 0.4 bar.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the graphical view of the equilibrium curve peg for calcination and carbonization with reaction area at ambient pressure. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN114436275-A
TI Preparing nano-silicon dioxide, comprises            introducing carbon dioxide gas into silicate solution,            generating orthosilicic acid precipitate, using organic            alcohol solvent to wash orthosilicic acid precipitate            for pore expansion, drying and calcining.
AU CHEN H
   LI C
   ZHANG Z
   SHEN X
   YAN F
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 202266328W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-silicon dioxide, comprises                introducing carbon dioxide gas into silicate                solution, generating orthosilicic acid precipitate,                using an organic alcohol solvent to wash                orthosilicic acid precipitate for pore expansion,                or washing the orthosilicic acid precipitate with                water and adding organic alcohol solvent to perform                azeotropic distillation to realize pore expansion,                drying orthosilicic acid precipitate, and                calcining.
   USE - The nano-silicon dioxide is useful for carbon                dioxide adsorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can greatly improve the pore volume                of the nano-silicon dioxide, reduce the silicon                dioxide material preparation, is environmentally                friendly, safe, and has no health risk. The                adsorbent has good adsorption performance, ad has                good application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) nano-silicon dioxide, prepared as                mentioned above;
   (2) carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises a base                body and an active substance loaded on the base                body. The base body is nano-silicon dioxide. The                active substance is organic amine substance;                and
   (3) use of carbon dioxide adsorbent for                absorbing carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide gas flow                of 15-100 vol.% under the condition of                30-110degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step of                  precipitation, the concentration of the silicate                  solution is 10-100 g/l, the concentration of the                  carbon dioxide gas is 5-40 vol.%, the flow rate                  of the carbon dioxide gas is 400-2000 ml/minute                  of carbon dioxide gas per liter of the silicate                  solution. The silicate solution comprises sodium                  silicate solution and/or potassium silicate                  solution. The organic alcohol solvent comprises                  ethanol, propanol, and/or butanol.Preferred Method: In the step of                  precipitation, the carbon dioxide gas is                  introduced into the silicate solution at                  25-80degrees Celsius until the pH of the reaction                  system is 9.8-10.3, the introduction of the                  carbon dioxide gas is stopped, and the                  orthosilicic acid precipitate is generated. In                  the step of pore expansion, the orthosilicic acid                  precipitate is washed with organic alcohol                  solvent until the conductivity of the filtrate is                  less than 0.1 mus/cm, and the washing is stopped.                  The step of pore expansion comprises performing                  azeotropic distillation at azeotropic boiling                  point temperature of the water and organic                  alcohol solvent for 0.5-2 hours, and distilling                  at the boiling point temperature of the alcohol                  solvent for 0.5-2 hours. In the calcining step,                  the drying temperature is 60-100degrees Celsius,                  and the calcination temperature is 100-600degrees                  Celsius.

PN CN111939954-A; CN111939954-B
TI Basic lead carbonate nanosheet used for            electrocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation, where            target product is formic acid, has hexagonal            structure.
AU ZHANG B
   DU W
   JI Y
   SHI Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020B7197Y
AB    NOVELTY - Basic lead carbonate nanosheet has a hexagonal                structure with a side length of 300-500 nm and a                thickness of 8-12 nm.
   USE - The basic lead carbonate nanosheet is used for                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation,                where target product is formic acid                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The basic lead carbonate nanosheet has more                active sites, stable structure, simple synthesis                method, low cost and obvious hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the basic lead                carbonate nanosheet, which involves                electrochemically treating lead carbonate                nanoparticles in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to                obtain the basic lead carbonate nanosheet by taking                the lead carbonate nanoparticles loaded on carbon                paper as cathode, carrying out a potentiostatic                reaction in a closed three-electrode system, and                carrying out continuous introduction of carbon                dioxide in a closed environment, so that the                atmosphere in the electrolyte and above the                electrolyte are carbon dioxide, reaching a carbon                dioxide atmosphere, maintaining constant potential                at -1.2 to -2 V, and reacting for at least 10                minutes, where the electrolyte is 0.5-1 M sodium                bicarbonate aqueous solution; and
   (2) the use of the basic lead carbonate                nanosheet for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation, where the target product is formic                acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The counter electrode                  is a carbon rod or a platinum sheet. The                  reference electrode is a saturated calomel                  electrode. The application uses saturated calomel                  electrode as a reference electrode, and a carbon                  rod or platinum sheet as a counter electrode.                  Preferred Method: The application involves                  adopting a three-electrode system, using basic                  lead carbonate nanosheet as the cathode, using                  0.5 M sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution as                  electrolyte, connecting CHI electrochemical                  analyzer/workstation, and adding carbon dioxide                  bubbled by water to the electrolytic cell.                  Preferred Conditions: The constant potential is                  maintained at -1.2 to -1.6 V, and the reaction is                  carried out for 10-30 minutes. When the potential                  is -1.6 V, the current reaches 2 mAx cm-2, and                  the Faraday efficiency reaches 95% (Ie Faraday                  efficiency for formic acid products). Preferred                  Properties: The hexagonal structure is an                  equilateral hexagon with a side length of 360-400                  nm and a thickness of 8-10 nm.

   RAVAGNI A
   BUCHELI O
   ALYOUSEF Y M
AE EZ-ENERGIES GMBH (EZEN-Non-standard)
   EZ-ENERGIES GMBH (EZEN-Non-standard)
   EZ-ENERGIES GMBH (EZEN-Non-standard)
   EZ-ENERGIES GMBH (EZEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020097939
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide involves feeding a                gaseous hydrocarbon feed (100) into a solid oxide                fuel cell (SOFC) system (2), converting the feed in                the system into an anode exhaust stream (101)                comprising carbon dioxide, injecting the anode                exhaust stream as an injection gas (105) into an                underground coal bed (74) for desorbing coal bed                methane from the coal and adsorbing carbon dioxide                onto the coal, extracting the coal bed methane from                the underground coal bed, and discharging a                production gas (108) comprising the coal bed                methane from the underground coal bed. The system                (2) generates electricity.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gaseous                hydrocarbon feed in production of coal bed methane                from underground coal bed e.g. non-minable coal                bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables energy-efficient removal of                carbon dioxide from gaseous hydrocarbon feed, and                versatile and economical production of coal bed                methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system.
   Solid oxide fuel cell system (2,2a)
   Underground coal bed (74)
   Gaseous hydrocarbon feed (100)
   Anode exhaust stream (101)
   Injection gas (105)
   Production gas (108)
   Public gas grid (113)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The feed comprises at                  least one biogas. The injection gas comprises 20%                  nitrogen and 80% carbon dioxide, or 45% nitrogen                  and 55% carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The                  process further involves sequestering atmospheric                  carbon dioxide using biomass, converting the                  biomass to biogas, and feeding the biogas as the                  gaseous hydrocarbon feed into the system (2). The                  process involves consuming the anode exhaust                  stream with a controlled amount of air to control                  the amount of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in a                  consumed anode exhaust stream, and injecting the                  consumed exhaust stream (a) as the injection gas                  into the underground coal bed, or consuming the                  anode exhaust stream with a controlled amount of                  a cathode off gas of the system to control the                  amount of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the                  stream (a), and injecting the stream (a) as the                  injection gas into the underground coal bed, and                  feeding the production gas as the gaseous                  hydrocarbon feed into the system (2), or feeding                  the production gas to a public gas grid (113).                  The process involves feeding the anode exhaust                  stream of the system (2) into a SOFC system (2a),                  converting the anode exhaust stream in the system                  (2a) into a carbon dioxide enriched anode exhaust                  stream, and injecting the carbon dioxide enriched                  anode exhaust stream as the injection gas into                  the underground coal bed, and adapting the ratio                  of nitrogen with respect to carbon dioxide in the                  anode exhaust stream depending on a coal quality                  of the underground coal bed.

AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims an edible fungi planting greenhouse environment control system, belonging to the field of edible fungi, for edible fungi, comprising several sub-system in parallel, the subsystem comprising a spiral ascending of the placing table, the central position of the placing table is provided with an upright post vertical to the horizontal plane, is detecting rod are radially distributed with its central axis as the starting point on the upright post, above the placing table detecting rod extends to the corresponding position of the detecting rod is equipped with a carbon dioxide concentration sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration adjustor outlet; carbon dioxide concentration adjustor outlet through pipeline is connected with the carbon dioxide concentration adjustor in the upright post; the carbon dioxide concentration sensor and the carbon dioxide concentration adjustor are connected through A/D conversion circuit is connected with the microprocessor. planting in view of the above technical solution, the invention can realize the edible fungi and more precisely of the growth environment monitoring, creative planting idea and monitoring system, which helps to improve the growth quality of edible fungi and improve quality of the edible fungi.

PT P
PN WO2019006352-A1; US2020180964-A1; US11230473-B2; US2022212935-A1
TI Carbon dioxide sequestration using effluent water            comprises combining effluent water with carbon dioxide            from carbon dioxide-containing emission stream to form            reaction solution and controlling pH to induce            precipitation of carbonate salt.
AU SANT G N
   KWEON H
   CALLAGON LA PLANTE E
   CALLAGON L P E
   SIMONETTI D A
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 201901720V
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises combining effluent water with                carbon dioxide sourced from a carbon                dioxide-containing emission stream to produce a                reaction solution and controlling the pH of the                reaction solution to induce precipitation of a                carbonate salt from the reaction solution.
   USE - The method is used for direct aqueous                carbonation for carbon dioxide sequestration using                effluent water derived from oil and gas extraction                operations, industrial mineral-containing effluent                water, desalination brine, sea water, and other                effluent water. The method is useful for treating                effluent water that is re-used for irrigation,                rangeland restoration, animal consumption and oil                recovery operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective and                environmentally sustainable, avoids stripping                carbon dioxide from flue gas using a scrubbing                media, avoids compression of carbon dioxide for                long-distance transport by pipeline, and avoids the                use of large amounts of acid and base for allowing                mineral dissolution and carbonation. The direct                carbonation route imposes no criterion for steam                generated by the power plant thus eliminating any                parasitic energy losses. The waste heat derived                from flue gases is re-used to improve carbonation                process. The heterogeneous buffer agents used in                the method are less hazardous, solvent-free and                environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The pH controlling step                  comprises introducing a pH buffer agent to                  increase or adjust the pH of the reaction                  solution to greater than or equal to 6,                  preferably greater than or equal to 6.5. The                  method further comprises recovering heterogeneous                  buffer agent, subsequent to the precipitation of                  the carbonate salt by filtration. Preferred                  Components: The pH buffer agent is a                  heterogeneous buffer agent. The carbon                  dioxide-containing emission stream is a flue gas                  stream. The heterogeneous buffer agent is a                  polymer-supported buffer agent. The heterogeneous                  buffer agent is clay to support ion exchange                  reaction.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

PN CN109031468-A; CN109031468-B
TI Method for measuring net exchange flux of nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide based on single gas analyzer and eddy correlation principle, involves calculating net exchange flux of nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide.
AU WANG K
   ZHENG X
   WANG D
AE INST ATMOSPHERIC PHYSICS CHINESE ACAD SC (ATMO-Non-standard)
   INST ATMOSPHERIC PHYSICS CHINESE ACAD SC (ATMO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4914V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves comprising an ultrasonic anemometer for measuring three-dimensional wind speed and Cartesian coordinate speed. An air sample is collected and transferred to a closed gas analyzer by using a gas sampling system. Nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide concentrations contained in the air sample are detected by the gas sampling system. Concentration data of the nitrous oxide, the methane and the carbon dioxide is received and stored by a data collecting and processor. Net exchange flux of the nitrous oxide, the methane and the carbon dioxide is calculated by adopting eddy correlation method.
   USE - Method for measuring net exchange flux of nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide based on single gas analyzer and eddy correlation principle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing net exchange flux synchronization of the nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide synchronous in an effective manner, and exchange flux improving measuring efficiency and accuracy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for measuring net exchange flux of nitrous oxide, methane and carbon dioxide based on single gas analyzer and eddy correlation principle. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN JP2022160029-A
TI System for culturing algae, has algae incubator            which is capable of receiving supply of carbon dioxide            rich absorption liquid as nutrient source from carbon            dioxide recovery unit and is provided for culturing            algae.
AU TAKAOKA H
   TAKAHASHI H
AE COSMO ENG KK (MAZN-C)
GA 2022D4211D
AB    NOVELTY - The algae-culturing system (10) has carbon                dioxide feeder which is provided for supplying                carbon dioxide-containing gas. A carbon dioxide                recovery unit is provided for obtaining a carbon                dioxide-rich absorption liquid by bringing a carbon                dioxide lean absorption liquid into contact with                the carbon dioxide-containing gas. An algae                incubator is capable of receiving the supply of the                carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid as a nutrient                source from the carbon dioxide recovery unit and is                provided for culturing the algae. The carbon                dioxide-lean absorption liquid and the carbon                dioxide-rich absorption liquid are absorption                liquid containing an absorbent. The absorbent is                ammonia or an amine-based compound. The absorbent                is ammonia or an organic amine compound. The algae                is photosynthesizable algae.
   USE - System for culturing algae used as fuel in                thermal power plant, iron mill, cement factory,                refinery, blast furnace in chemical plant, lime                furnace, heating furnace, reactor, and                boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy loss at the time of carbon dioxide                separation is not caused. The toxic property to the                algae is reduced by the effect of dilution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an algae                culture method.

The carbon                dioxideadsorbent has strong adsorbability to carbon                dioxide, the air inlet temperature can be improved                to overcome partial adsorbption force.

AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202061084P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a topological homeostatic cycle that is provided with a mechanically assisted supercooling cycle state and a superheat recovery cascade cycle state during operation. The carbon dioxide sub-cycle is provided with an evaporator (1), a carbon dioxide compressor (2), a carbon dioxide-water heat exchanger (3), a carbon dioxide-refrigerant heat exchanger (4), and a carbon dioxide throttle valve (5).
   USE - Carbon dioxide heat pump heating system with topological homeostatic cycle.

The unit cost is reduced and the practicality is enhanced by common heat pump compressor. The exhaust heat loss of carbon dioxide is reduced and the energy efficiency of the system is improved by addition of a carbon dioxide-water heat exchanger as a superheater. The built topological homeomorphic cycle can switch between mechanically assisted supercooling cycle and superheat recovery cascade cycle according to the change of ambient temperature, always running at a higher energy efficiency, the continuous and efficient heating throughout the heating season is ensured.

AU DAI X

GA 201913338P
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises carbon dioxide removing device (2), ammonia removal device and a decarburization tower. The carbon dioxide removing device and ammonia removal device are connected by decarbonization tower. The carbon dioxide removing device is horizontal cylindrical structure, and multiple circular turntables (4) are uniformly distributed inside. The top is provided with first biogas liquid inlet (3) and carbon dioxide gas outlet (6) connected to first vacuum pump (5). The bottom is provided with first air inlet pipe, gas channel uniformly distributed with plurality of air holes and first biogas outlet. The ammonia removing device comprises second liquid inlet located above the side, ammonia discharge port at the top and connected to the second vacuum pump, and second air inlet pipe and second liquid outlet at the bottom. The first biogas outlet of the carbon dioxide removal device and the second liquid inlet of the ammonia removing device are in communication with each other.
   USE - The device is useful for removing carbon dioxide deamination from sludge digestion biogas slurry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: removes the green and harmless carbon dioxide and ammonia nitrogen in the biogas slurry; and realizes the resource utilization of carbon dioxide and ammonia nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing carbon dioxide deamination from sludge digestion biogas slurry using the device by subjecting the anaerobic digestion biogas slurry to solid-liquid separation to obtain sludge digestion biogas slurry and biogas residue, thermally hydrolyzing the biogas residue to generate hot water to interpret the pressure gas, and then subjecting the sludge digestion biogas slurry to removing deamination treatment which is specifically comprises (i) primarily pumping the sludge digestion biogas slurry into the carbon dioxide removing device for removing carbon dioxide in a high temperature vacuum environment to obtain the carbon dioxide biogas slurry, removing the carbon dioxide biogas into the ammonia removal device to remove ammonia, where the degree of vacuum in the carbon dioxide removing device is -0.08 MPa, the sludge digestion biogas slurry temperature is 50 degrees C, the degree of vacuum in the ammonia removal device is 0.07-0.08 MPa, the temperature of the carbon dioxide removed biogas slurry is 70-80 degrees C, and (ii) withdrawing the removed carbon dioxide and ammonia through the first vacuum pump and the second vacuum pump and then reacting into the decarbonization column to form ammonium hydrogencarbonate.

PN WO2020254051-A1; EP3986835-A1; US2022212163-A1
TI Chabazite-type zeolite used as a sorbent for            carbon dioxide in a process for separation of carbon            dioxide from methane, includes at least two cages            composed of four and eight membered rings connected by            one six-membered double ring.
AU BARRIER N
   BOULLAY P
   GILSON J
   GRAND J
   MINOUX D
   MINTOVA S
   NESTERENKO N
   DEBOST M
AE TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 2020C9604V
AB    NOVELTY - Chabazite-type zeolite includes at least two                cages composed of 4 and 8 membered rings connected                by one 6-membered double ring, and having a                silicon/aluminum (Si/AI) molar ratio comprised                between 1 and 15. The chabazite-type zeolite                comprises caesium and potassium with a                cesium/potassium (Cs/K) molar ratio of at most 5.0                as determined by Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical                Emission Spectrometry. The chabazite-type zeolite                forming nanoparticles with an average crystal size                comprised between 5 nanometer and 250 nanometer as                determined by the Schemer equation and with a                specific surface area comprised between 50 m2 g-1                and 200 m2 g-1, as determined by dinitrogen (N2)                sorption measurements.
   USE - Chabazite-type zeolite used as a sorbent for                carbon dioxide with preference in a process for                separation of carbon dioxide from methane or in a                process for separation of carbon dioxide from an                inert gas such as dinitrogen (N2), helium (He)                and/or argon (Ar) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chabazite-type zeolite is template-free,                seeds-free and fluoride-free, and allows good                adsorption of carbon dioxide together with a good                selectively over nitrogen and/or methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an amorphous precursor of chabazite-type                zeolite for the preparation of a chabazite- type                zeolite, which includes amorphous precursor of                chabazite-type zeolite has a molar composition                comprising a silicon oxide (SiO2): b aluminum oxide                (Al2O3): c M12O: d potassium oxide (K2O): e cesium                oxide (Cs2O): f water (H2O), where a, b, c, d, e,                and f are coefficients, where the coefficient a is                ranging from at least 10.0 and at most 20.0, the                coefficient b is ranging from at least 0.3 and at                most 2.5, the coefficient c is ranging from at                least 5.0 and at most 11.0, the coefficient d is                ranging from at least 0.7 and at most 1.6, the                coefficient e is ranging from at least 0.05 and at                most 0.60 and the coefficient f is ranging from at                least 60 and at most 200 where M12O is selected                from sodium oxide (Na2O) and/or lithium oxide                (Li2O);
   (2) a method for the preparation of an                amorphous precursor of a chabazite-type zeolite,                which involves providing an aluminate precursors                aqueous suspension; providing a silicate precursors                aqueous suspension; adding at least three metallic                precursors in the aluminate precursors aqueous                suspension to form a first aqueous suspension,                where the at least three metallic precursors                comprises cesium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and                at least one selected from sodium hydroxide and/or                lithium hydroxide; and forming an amorphous                precursor of zeolite by adding dropwise the                silicate precursors aqueous suspension into first                aqueous suspension with preference, the at least                three metallic precursors comprise cesium                hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and sodium                hydroxide; and
   (3) a method for the preparation of a                chabazite-type zeolite, which involves preparing an                amorphous precursor of a chabazite-type zeolite,                and further comprising mixing the amorphous                precursor under stirring and/or orbital shaking;                adding an additional silicate precursors aqueous                suspension and mixing amorphous precursor under                stirring and/or orbital shaking; heating amorphous                precursor at a temperature comprised between 90                degrees C and 160 degrees C such as to form one or                more crystals of chabazite-type zeolite; cooling                down one or more crystals of chabazite-type zeolite                at a temperature comprised between 20 degrees C and                25 degrees C; and dispersing one or more crystals                of chabazite-type zeolite in water, and recovering                one or more crystals of chabazite-type                zeolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The chabazite-type                  zeolite forms monodispersed nanoparticles, or                  forms aggregates, with preference, aggregates                  have a size between 400 nanometer and 2000                  nanometer, as determined by Scanning Electron                  Microscopy. The chabazite-type zeolite has an                  M1/Al molar ratio ranging from 0.02-0.20, as                  determined by Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical                  Emission Spectrometry, where M1 is selected from                  sodium (Na) and/or lithium (Li). The Cs/K molar                  ratio is at most 3.0 as determined by Inductively                  Coupled Plasma Optical Emission Spectrometry. The                  chabazite-type zeolite has a cesium/aluminum                  (Cs/Al) molar ratio comprised between 0.15 and                  0.45 as determined by Inductively Coupled Plasma                  Optical Emission Spectrometry, preferably between                  0.20 and 0.40. The nanoparticles have an average                  crystal size of at most 220 nanometer as                  determined by the Schemer equation. The ratio                  M1zO/ aluminum oxide (Al2O3) is superior or equal                  to 7.0. Preferred Components: The one or more                  aluminate precursors of aluminate precursors                  aqueous suspension are selected among sodium                  aluminate (Na2Al2O4) , aluminum sulfate                  (Al2(SO4)3) hydrated alumina, aluminum powder,                  kaolin clays and a mixture. The one or more                  silicate precursors of silicate precursors                  aqueous suspension are selected among colloidal                  silica, silica oxyhydroxide species, silica                  hydrogel, silicic acid, fumed silica, tetraalkyl                  orthosilicates, silica hydroxides, precipitated                  silica, clays and a mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111363132-A; CN111363132-B
TI Improving quality of polycarbonate-polyether polyol prepared by liquid phase comprises e.g. pumping raw materials in from inlet of straight tube reactor, straight tube reactor includes heating section group and cooling section group.
AU MAO H
   WANG H
   WANG Z
AE HANGZHOU PULI MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020636392
AB    NOVELTY - Improving quality of polycarbonate-polyether polyol prepared by liquid phase comprises e.g. (1) pumping raw materials in from inlet of the straight tube reactor, the straight tube reactor includes a heating section group and a cooling section group, placing heating section group in straight tube reactor, at the inlet end, placing cooling section group at outlet end of the straight tube reactor, the heating section group includes a first heating section group and a second heating section group, the first heating section group and second heating section group is connecting in series, and the raw materials include chain transfer agent, epoxy compound, catalyst and carbon dioxide, so that in the presence of the catalyst, the chain transfer agent, epoxy compound and carbon dioxide are in contact in the first heating section group, carrying out polymerization reaction to obtain a reaction liquid stream containing polycarbonate-polyether polyol and cyclic carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for improving quality of polycarbonate-polyether polyol prepared by liquid phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving quality of polycarbonate-polyether polyol prepared by liquid phase comprises (1) pumping raw materials in from inlet of the straight tube reactor, the straight tube reactor includes a heating section group and a cooling section group, placing heating section group in straight tube reactor, at the inlet end, placing cooling section group at outlet end of the straight tube reactor, the heating section group includes a first heating section group and a second heating section group, the first heating section group and second heating section group is connecting in series, and the raw materials include chain transfer agent, epoxy compound, catalyst and carbon dioxide, so that in the presence of the catalyst, the chain transfer agent, epoxy compound and carbon dioxide are in contact in the first heating section group, carrying out polymerization reaction to obtain a reaction liquid stream containing polycarbonate-polyether polyol and cyclic carbonate, (2) flowing reaction liquid stream to second heating section group, and the chain transfer agent, epoxy compound and carbon dioxide in the reaction liquid stream are in contacting with carbon dioxide in the second heating section group to carrying out polymerization reaction, the outlet end of the second heating section group has a pipe connecting to the inlet end of the second heating section group, so that the reaction liquid stream circulating in second heating section group, and so on until the reaction liquid, polycarbonate, the content of polyether polyol is /(70%, so as to obtain polymerization reaction product stream, and (3) part or all of the polymerization reaction product stream flows through the cooling section group, thus separating part of the polycarbonate-polyether polyol to form a polycarbonate-polyether polyol product stream, and the remaining polymerization reaction product stream is recycling back to step (1), where, the catalyst is a zinc-cobalt double metal cyanide complex catalyst obtained by reacting a water-soluble metal salt of zinc and cobalt in a water-soluble solvent, the catalyst is modifying by a mixed acid during synthesis, and the mixed acid contains at least one organic acid and at least one water-soluble inorganic acid, where the water-soluble inorganic acid is dilute sulfuric acid and dilute hydrochloric acid, and has a pH of 0-5, preferably 0-4, more preferably 1-3, the dilute sulfuric acid refers to an aqueous solution of sulfuric acid, which can be diluting by adding de-ionized water with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain a pH 0-5, the dilute hydrochloric acid refers to an aqueous solution of hydrochloric acid, which can be diluting by adding de-ionized water with concentrated hydrochloric acid to obtain a pH 0-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The outlet end of the first heating section group has a pipe connected to the inlet end of the first heating section group, so that the reaction liquid stream circulates in the first heating section group while maintaining the reaction liquid stream, the content of cyclic carbonate is /)30%. In the step (3), after the polymerization reaction product stream flows through the cooling section group, pumping from the outlet of the straight tube reactor to a gas-liquid separator, and the reaction liquid stream passing through the gas-liquid separator, pre-separating into a gas phase material and a liquid phase material, the gas phase material includes carbon dioxide, the liquid phase material then flows through the catalyst filter, separating or recovering catalyst nano particles, passing through a rectification device to further separating small molecule by-product cyclic carbonate to obtain polycarbonate-polyether polyol product stream, pumping polycarbonate polyether polyol product stream to a polycarbonate-polyether polyol storage tank for filling. The rectification device includes a falling film tower and a scraped evaporator, and the liquid phase material passing through falling film tower and the scraped evaporator in sequence to perform a separation procedure, separating small molecule by-product cyclic carbonate, thus obtaining polycarbonate-polyether polyol product stream. The heat exchanger is arranging between the first heating section group and the cooling section group, so that the polymerization reaction product stream heating raw materials pumped in from the inlet end of the first heating section group. The reaction liquid flows through the first heating section group and heating to 70-120 degrees C, and then the reaction liquid flows through the second heating section group and heating to 80-130 degrees C. The heating section group includes a first heating section group, a second heating section group, and a third heating section group, the reaction liquid flows through the first heating section group, the second heating section group and the third heating section group in sequence, carrying out polymerization reaction, the reaction liquid flows through the first heating section group and heating to 70-120 degrees C, and the reaction liquid flows through the second heating section group and heating to 80-130 degrees C, the reaction liquid flows through the third heating section group and heating to 90-150 degrees C, the first gas supplementary pressure feeding device is providing between the first heating section group and the second heating stage group, and the first gas supplementary pressure feeding device is connecting to a carbon dioxide pipeline through a pipeline, and the pressure in the straight tube reactor is maintaining at 2-6 MPa. Preferred Components: The chain transfer agent is ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, neopentyl glycol, glycerol, trimethylolpropane, trimethylolethane, 1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol, pentaerythritol, dipentaerythritol, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, dodecanedioic acid, terephthalic acid, isophthalic acid, orthophthalic acid, phthalic acid, trimesic acid, pyromellitic acid, catechol, resorcinol, and/or hydroquinone, and the epoxide is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide and/or epichlorohydrin.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110684711-A
TI Constructing in vitro recombinant epidermal model of atopic dermatitis phenotype by recovering primary keratinocytes, digesting, preparing single cell suspension, culturing cells, changing medium and incubating under carbon dioxide.
AU WU D
   LU Y
   DENG Z
   LIU W
   JIN Y
AE GUANGDONG BIOCELL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202007402A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing in vitro recombinant epidermal model of atopic dermatitis phenotype involves recovering and expanding primary keratinocytes to P3-P7 generation cells, digesting with trypsin, preparing single cell suspension with MCDB 153 medium, inoculating cells in a culture flask, culturing, formulating epidermal cells in the logarithmic growth phase into epidermal cell suspensions, inoculating epidermal cell suspension into a cell culture chamber containing a polycarbonate filter, culturing, then placing the cell culture chamber in the epidermal model dish, adding cell culture medium (I) to each culture dish, continuing culturing, changing the medium, discarding the culture medium, replacing the culture medium in the epidermal model culture dish with cell culture medium (II), performing the gas-liquid surface culture, replacing with cell culture medium (III) and incubating under carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing in vitro recombinant epidermal model of atopic dermatitis phenotype.
   ADVANTAGE - The in vitro recombined epidermal model has changes in tissue section structure and biochemical indicators similar to human skin under atopic dermatitis pathological condition, realistically simulate the pathological state of human atopic dermatitis skin, reduces species differences and individual differences and has improved true validity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing in vitro recombinant epidermal model of atopic dermatitis phenotype involves (a) recovering and expanding primary keratinocytes to P3-P7 generation cells, digesting with trypsin, preparing single cell suspension with MCDB 153 medium, inoculating 1-5x 105 cells/ml in a culture flask and culturing at 37 degrees C under and 5% carbon dioxide, (b) formulating epidermal cells in the logarithmic growth phase into epidermal cell suspensions with a cell density of 1.25-2.5x 106 cells/ml using cell culture medium (I), inoculating 200 mu l epidermal cell suspension into a cell culture chamber containing a polycarbonate filter, culturing at 37 degrees C under and 5% carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours, then placing the cell culture chamber in the epidermal model dish, adding 60-80 ml cell culture medium (I) to each culture dish, continuing culturing for 1-3 days at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, and changing the medium once in every day to achieve subcutaneous culture of epidermal cells and (c) discarding the culture medium in the cell culture chamber, replacing the culture medium in the epidermal model culture dish with cell culture medium (II), adding 50-70 ml cell culture medium (II) per culture dish, such that the liquid surface and the upper surface of the cells in the cell culture chamber are at the same horizontal line, performing the gas-liquid surface culture, continuing culturing for 12-14 days, changing the medium once in every day, replacing the culture medium in the epidermal model culture dish with cell culture medium (III) and incubating for 2 days under 5% carbon dioxide. The cell culture medium (I) is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with insulin, isoprenaline, hydrocortisone, epidermal growth factor, adenine, transferrin, L-carnitine, and calcium chloride. The cell culture medium (II) is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with insulin, isoprenaline, epidermal growth factor, adenine, transferrin, L-carnitine, L-serine, palmitic acid, oleic acid, arginine, succinic acid, calcium chloride, keratinocyte growth factor and granulocyte colony-stimulating factor. The cell culture medium (III) is prepared is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with insulin, isoprenaline, epidermal growth factor, adenine, transferrin, L-carnitine, L-serine, palmitic acid, oleic acid, argininosuccinic acid, calcium chloride, keratinocyte growth factor, granulocyte colony-stimulating factor, interleukin-4, interleukin-13, and TNF.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The cell culture medium (I) is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with 0.5-5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.1-1 mu M isoprenaline, 0.02-0.2 mu g/ml hydrocortisone, 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 10-50 mu g/ml adenine, 5-12 mu g/ml transferrin, 0.001-0.1 mM L-carnitine and 0.03-0.2 mM calcium chloride. The cell culture medium (II) is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with 0.5-5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.1-1 mu M isoprenaline, 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 10-50 mu g/ml adenine, 5-12 mu g/ml transferrin, 0.001-0.1 mM L-carnitine, 0.01-0.1 mM L-serine, 0.01-0.05 mM palmitic acid, 0.01-0.05 mM oleic acid, 0.05-0.1 mM argininosuccinic acid, 1.0-1.5 mM calcium chloride, 1-10 ng/ml keratinocyte growth factor and 10-20 ng/ml granulocyte colony-stimulating factor. The cell culture medium (II) is prepared using MCDB 153 as a basic medium supplemented with 0.5-5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.1-1 mu M isoproterenol, 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 10-50 mu g/ml adenine, 5-12 mu g/ml transferrin, 0.001-0.1 mM L-carnitine, 0.01-0.1 mM L-serine, 0.01-0.05 mM palmitic acid, 0.01-0.05 mM oleic acid, 0.05-0.1 mM argininosuccinic acid, 1.0-1.5 mM calcium chloride, 1-10 ng/ml keratinocyte growth factor, 10-20 ng/ml granulocyte colony stimulating factor, 10-50 ng/ml interleukin-4, 10-50 ng/ml interleukin-13 and 10-20 ng/ml TNF. Preferred Method: In step (a), the trypsin is 2.5% trypsin.

PT P
PN WO2019003213-A1; EP3645666-A1; US2020222874-A1
TI Plant for converting biogas into high added value            chemical product, comprises reforming section for            converting methane and carbon dioxide in biogas into            syngas, section for synthesizing chemical products, and            automation/control section.
AU MANENTI F
AE POLITECNICO DI MILANO (MIPT-C)
   POLITECNICO DI MILANO (MIPT-C)
   POLITECNICO DI MILANO (MIPT-C)
GA 201902158F
AB    NOVELTY - A plant comprises a compact module comprising                a reforming section (A) for converting methane and                carbon dioxide contained in a biogas into a syngas,                a section (B) for synthesizing methanol by using                the syngas and for synthesizing chemical products,                such as dimethyl ether, formaldehyde and acetic                acid, by using the methanol, an optional section                (C) for purifying and separating the chemical                products, at least one section (D) chosen from                compression, lamination, heat-exchange, water gas                shift, pressure swing absorption, solid oxide fuel                cell, burner, membrane and medium pressure steam                units, and an automation/control section (E). The                section (D) is arranged upstream or downstream of                at least one of the sections (A)-(C). At least the                whole section (B) or its portion can be substituted                with a further and different section (B) or its                portion to obtain the chemical products different                from the one obtained before replacement of the                whole section (B) or its portion.
   USE - Plant used for converting biogas into high                added value chemical product e.g. methanol,                dimethyl ether, formaldehyde and acetic acid (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant is energetically self-sustainable,                and can efficiently provide easily-transportable                liquid chemicals with high added value, high purity                and definitive carbon dioxide sequestration                (negative impact), through a non-invasive                installation of extremely flexible module which can                transform the biogas into the desired final                products. The synthesis of chemical products by                using the plant or system is economical and                sustainable. It is possible to reduce/eliminate                purification section of the module. It is possible                to efficiently abate carbon dioxide i.e. 2/3 of the                total carbon fixed in carbon dioxide are not                reintroduced into the atmosphere, but are fixed in                the liquid chemical molecule. The plant module is                easy to install. It is inserted downstream of the                biogas plant and does not require any modification                with respect to the existing plant module in its                non-energetically integrated forms. The plant                module offers high-production flexibility, and the                final product can be changed at any time with                minimal investment. Without any incentive, the                plant module offers revenues at least equal to an                incentivized biogas, more than twice higher than                those of an un-incentivized biogas, about three                times higher than those of an incentivized                bio-methane, and ten times higher than those of an                un-incentivized biomethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A plant comprises a compact module comprising                a reforming section (A) for converting methane and                carbon dioxide contained in a biogas into a syngas,                a section (B) for synthesizing methanol by using                the syngas and for synthesizing chemical products,                such as dimethyl ether, formaldehyde and acetic                acid, by using the methanol, an optional section                (C) for purifying and separating the chemical                products, at least one section (D) chosen from                compression unit, lamination unit, heat-exchange                unit, water gas shift unit, pressure swing                absorption unit, solid oxide fuel cell unit,                burner, membrane unit and medium pressure steam                unit, and an automation/control section (E). The                section (D) is arranged upstream or downstream of                at least one of the sections (A)-(C). At least the                whole section (B) or its portion can be substituted                with a further and different section (B) or its                portion to obtain the chemical products different                from the one obtained before replacement of the                whole section (B) or its portion. At least one                section (B) or its portion is connected to at least                one adjacent sections chosen from sections (A), (C)                and (D), by hydraulic connection device. The plant                is located downstream of a biogas production                plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The                  hydraulic/mechanical connection device is                  valve/flange/valve-type connection device. The                  reforming section (A) is integrated with an                  engine currently installed in the existing biogas                  plant. The energy required to carry out the                  reformation is provided by the thermal waste                  produced by the engine and/or by the reforming                  section (A) through feed-effluent technology or                  by the use of the effluents of the plant as                  engine supply. The section (B) is a stage                  reactor. The heaters section (D) is replaced by                  the engine installed in the biogas plant.                  Preferred Property: The plant has volumetric                  dimensions of 4x 2x 2 m, preferably 4x 1.5x 1.5                  m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reformation is                  carried out by reacting methane and carbon                  dioxide to form 2 moles of hydrogen and 2 moles                  of carbon monoxide and/or reacting methane and                  water to form 3 moles hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide, and reacting carbon monoxide and water                  to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen by water gas                  shift reaction, at a pressure between 5 and 35                  bar, preferably between 10 and 20 bar, a                  temperature between 650 and 900 degrees C,                  preferably between 700 and 800 degrees C in the                  presence of platinum and rhodium catalysts,                  cerium catalysts, or cerium and lanthanum                  catalysts. The feeding is partialized. The                  reforming section (A) is energetically integrated                  with the synthesis section (B) by partializing                  the hot gas stream leaving the reformer (A) to                  heat in situ the reactant stream to be fed to the                  various reactions occurring in the section (B).                  The biogas from the washing section of the biogas                  production plant and partially compressed in the                  compression section (D), is fed to the first                  section of the reforming module (A), then passed                  into the synthesis section (B), and finally                  passed into the section (C), where methanol is                  separated, unreacted syngas is recycled and sent                  upstream of the section (A), and water is removed                  by flash separation and/or membrane system. The                  biogas is divided into two streams (S1) and (S2),                  where the stream (S1) is supplied to the section                  (A), and the stream (S2) is used as a fuel to                  generate power and thermally sustain the                  reformation in the heat-exchange section (D) by                  gas-gas or firebox exchanger. After the heat                  exchange for energy recovery, the bio-syngas is                  sent to another section (D) comprising a pressure                  swing absorption battery for hydrogen recovery,                  and the hydrogen is then sent to another section                  (D) comprising solid oxide fuel cell battery for                  generating power, where the poor hydrogen stream                  is sent to the section (B) for producing acetic                  acid. The water gas shift unit in the section (D)                  is used for obtaining the optimal ratio required                  for acetic acid synthesis. The excess of hydrogen                  released from the solid oxide fuel cell battery                  is supplied to a catalytic hydrogenation system                  of carbon dioxide to reform methane. The biogas                  is supplied to the section (A) along with a                  medium pressure steam stream from the medium                  pressure steam unit in the section (D). After the                  heat exchange in the energy recovery section (D),                  the bio-syngas is sent to the acetic acid                  synthesis section (B). When the chemical product                  is methanol, the synthesis is carried out by                  reacting 2 moles of hydrogen and carbon monoxide                  to form methanol, reacting 3 moles of hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide to form methanol and water,                  and/or reacting carbon monoxide and water to form                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen, in the presence of                  iron and copper oxide catalysts. When the                  chemical product is dimethyl ether, the synthesis                  is carried out by reacting methane to form                  dimethyl ether and water, in the section (B)                  comprising a reactor which is a double-step                  reactor with intermediate separation. When the                  product is acetic acid, the synthesis is carried                  out by reacting methane and carbon monoxide to                  form acetic acid in the presence of copper-zinc                  oxide-aluminum oxide or copper-zinc-zirconium                  oxide catalysts in the presence of gallium oxide                  at a pressure between 10 and 40 bar, preferably                  between 10 and 20 bar. When the chemical product                  is formaldehyde, the synthesis is carried out by                  reacting methanol to form formaldehyde and                  hydrogen.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. TI Method for diagnosing indoor air environmental characteristics using multi-tracking mechanism, involves calculating required air cleaning and ventilation capacities, and presenting management plan of air integrated management system.
AU LEE J
   YOO K W
   PARK D K
   HO J
AE EH R & C CO LTD (EHRC-Non-standard)
GA 202221827E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves defining (S10) a first modeling for predicting changes in indoor and outdoor fine dust concentration and indoor carbon dioxide concentration. The changes in the indoor and outdoor fine dust concentration are installed (S20). An appropriate amount of carbon dioxide to a room is supplied (S30). The change in the indoor and outdoor fine dust concentration is measured (S40). An air purifier inflow flow rate and infiltration amount are estimated (S50) through an optimization algorithm. Air flow characteristics comprising direction among air flow characteristics through the estimated air purifier inflow flow rate and infiltration amount are estimated (S60). The change in indoor fine dust concentration for outdoor air concentration condition is predicted (S70) through a second modeling. Required air cleaning capacity and required ventilation capacity are calculated (S80). A management plan of an indoor air integrated management system is presented (S90).
   USE - Method for diagnosing indoor air environmental characteristics using multi-tracking mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The indoor air quality is managed by estimating air flow characteristics having a direction as well as a ventilation rate when measuring the ventilation rate using the multi-tracker technique.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for diagnosing indoor air environmental characteristics using multi-tracking mechanism.

PT P
PN CN113952963-A; CN113952963-B
TI Preparing copper indium sulfide photocatalyst            based on co-modification useful for photocatalysis of            carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. adding cuprous chloride,            indium chloride, sublimation sulfur powder into            ethylene glycol, centrifuging, washing, drying, and            adding cobalt nitrate hexahydrate.
AU JI H
   YANG J
   YANG Z
   XU H
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202219252B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper indium sulfide photocatalyst                based on co-modification comprises (i) taking                cuprous chloride, indium chloride, sublimation                sulfur powder, adding into ethylene glycol,                ultrasonically processing and stirring to form an                opaque yellow-green dispersion; (ii) adopting                hydrothermal method, transferring the solution in                autoclave, placing in an oven to react, cooling,                centrifuging, washing, drying in vacuum drying                oven, and obtaining bimetallic sulfide CuInS2 was                obtained; (iii) adding specific amount of CuInS2                into the absolute ethanol solution, ultrasonically                processing, stirring, adding a specific amount of                cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, mixing the two                uniformly by ultrasonication, and placing in an oil                bath to react; (iv) naturally cooling to room                temperature, collecting by centrifugation, washing                for many times with absolute ethanol and deionized                water, respectively, and drying in a vacuum drying                oven; and (v) placing sample in an ark, calcining,                and cooling.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalysis                of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst serves as active sites for                carbon dioxide reduction and accelerate the                catalytic reaction kinetics, and improves the                photocatalytic carbon dioxide conversion                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper indium sulfide photocatalyst                based on co-modification comprises (i) taking                cuprous chloride, indium chloride, sublimation                sulfur powder, adding into specific amount of                ethylene glycol, ultrasonically processing and                stirring for specific time to form an opaque                yellow-green dispersion; (ii) adopting hydrothermal                method, transferring the solution in autoclave,                placing in an oven to react for specific time,                naturally cooling to room temperature,                centrifuging, washing for many times with absolute                ethanol and deionized water, drying in a vacuum                drying oven for specific time, and obtaining the                bimetallic sulfide CuInS2 was obtained; (iii)                adding specific amount of CuInS2 into the absolute                ethanol solution, ultrasonically processing,                stirring, adding a specific amount of cobalt                nitrate hexahydrate, mixing the two uniformly by                ultrasonication, and placing in an oil bath to                react for specific time; (iv) naturally cooling to                room temperature, collecting by centrifugation,                washing for many times with absolute ethanol and                deionized water, respectively, and drying in a                vacuum drying oven for specific time; and (v)                placing the sample in an ark, calcining at specific                temperature, and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the                  mass ratio of copper indium sulfide and cobalt                  nitrate hexahydrate is 95-105 mg: 5-15 mg.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molar                  mass ratio of cuprous chloride, indium chloride                  and sublimated sulfur is 1:1:5, the ultrasonic                  stirring is carried out for 10-15 minutes, and                  the stirring is carried out at 1000                  revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes.In the step                  (ii), the reaction is carried out in oven at                  180-200degrees Celsius for 48 hours, the                  centrifugal speed is 12000 revolutions/minute,                  the temperature of the vacuum drying oven is                  60degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 12                  hours. The step (iii) comprises adding specific                  amount of CuInS2 into the absolute ethanol                  solution, ultrasonically processing for 5                  minutes, stirring at 350 revolutions/minute for 5                  minutes, adding a specific amount of cobalt                  nitrate hexahydrate, mixing the two uniformly by                  ultrasonication for 10 minutes, and placing in an                  oil bath to react at 70degrees Celsius at speed                  of 300-400 revolutions/minute for 2 hours. In the                  step (iv), the centrifugal speed is 10,000                  revolutions/minute, the temperature of the vacuum                  drying oven is 60degrees Celsius, and the drying                  time is 12 hours. In the step (v), the                  calcination is carried out in air at 300degrees                  Celsius at a rate of 5.2degrees Celsius/minute 1                  hour.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing organic waste for recycling hydrocarbons involves coarsely crushing, drying and pyrolyzing organic waste to generate pyrolysis product e.g. carbon powder and pyrolysis gas, gasifying and reforming generated pyrolysis product, captured carbon dioxide and steam by drying to generate synthesis gas, using the synthesis gas as a raw material to produce methanol or ammonia, using methanol and ammonia as raw materials to synthesize organic products, and recycling organic products and its derivatives after being used and discarded. The synthesis gas is composed of carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
   USE - Method for utilizing organic waste for recycling hydrocarbons used in energy, chemical industry and circular economy applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize carbon dioxide emission reduction, reduce fossil resource consumption, and absorb organic waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system which utilizes organic waste to realize the recycling of hydrocarbons, which includes organic waste pretreatment system, pyrolysis system, flue gas treatment system, carbon dioxide storage device, gasification reforming system, synthesis gas purification system, gas separation system, air separation system, methanol synthesis system, ammonia synthesis system and organic waste recycling system, where the material outlet of the organic waste pretreatment system is connected with the material inlet of the pyrolysis system, the pyrolysis product outlet of the pyrolysis system is connected with the air inlet of the gasification reforming system, the air inlet of the gasification reforming system is also connected with the water vapor outlet of the organic waste pretreatment system, the air inlet of the gasification reforming system is also connected with the air outlet of the carbon dioxide storage device, the gas outlet of the gasification reforming system is connected to the gas inlet of the synthesis gas purification system or gas separator, the gas outlet of the synthesis gas purification system is connected to the methanol synthesis system, the hydrogen outlet of the gas separation system is connected to the hydrogen inlet of the ammonia synthesis system, and the material inlet of the organic waste pretreatment system is connected with the material outlet of the organic waste recycling system.

PN CN111408372-A; CN111408372-B
TI Preparing copper-based carbon dioxide            electroreduction catalyst with nano-hollow spherical            appearance, by dissolving 5-hydroxyisophthalic acid            ligand and copper acetate metal salt in methanol,            adding N,N-dimethylacetamide, and carbonizing.
AU LAN Y
   YANG R
   CHEN Y
   WANG Y
AE UNIV NANJING NORMAL CHANGZHOU INNOVATION (UNNO-C)
GA 202067966B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing copper-based carbon                dioxide electroreduction catalyst with nano-hollow                spherical appearance, involves (a) dissolving                5-hydroxyisophthalic acid ligand in methanol,                dissolving copper acetate metal salt in methanol,                mixing two dissolved products in glass bottle,                uniformly stirring, adding N,N-dimethylacetamide to                glass bottle, transferring to high temperature and                high pressure reactor, reacting, after cooling to                room temperature, repeatedly washing unreacted                material using dimethylacetamide and methylene                chloride, and vacuum drying to obtain synthesized                copper-muriate of potash, and (b) dissolving                synthesized copper-muriate of potash and polymer in                ethanol in beaker, uniformly stirring obtained                mixed solution at room temperature for 30 minutes,                completely dissolving mixed solution to obtain blue                gel, leaving still the gel for 3 days, and                carbonizing at 400 degrees C for 5 hours, after the                gel is gradually hardened, and naturally cooling to                room temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing copper-based carbon                dioxide electroreduction catalyst with nano-hollow                spherical appearance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the morphology of obtained                catalyst, and is simple and feasible in synthesis                process, convenient to operate and suitable for                large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing copper-based carbon                dioxide electroreduction catalyst with nano-hollow                spherical appearance, involves (a) dissolving 365                mg 5-hydroxyisophthalic acid ligand in 10 ml                methanol, dissolving 400 mg copper acetate metal                salt in 30 ml methanol, mixing two dissolved                products in glass bottle, uniformly stirring for 30                minutes, adding 10 ml N,N-dimethylacetamide to                glass bottle, transferring to high temperature and                high pressure reactor, reacting at 85 degrees C for                24 hours, after cooling to room temperature,                repeatedly washing unreacted material using                dimethylacetamide and methylene chloride, and                vacuum drying to obtain synthesized copper-muriate                of potash, and (b) dissolving synthesized                copper-muriate of potash and polymer in ethanol in                beaker in a mass ratio of 10:1 to obtain mixed                solution, uniformly stirring obtained mixed                solution at room temperature for 30 minutes,                completely dissolving mixed solution to obtain blue                gel, leaving still the gel for 3 days, and                carbonizing at 400 degrees C for 5 hours, after the                gel is gradually hardened, and naturally cooling to                room temperature to obtain the solid product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  carbonizing by increasing the temperature to                  5-400 degrees C/minute from room temperature,                  maintaining the temperature for 5 hours,                  naturally cooling to room temperature, and                  collecting the product after completely cooling.                  The load of carbon dioxide electroreduction                  catalyst is 1 mg.cm-2.

PN CN110586064-A
TI Lithium-doped zirconium oxide- loaded indium oxide catalyst useful for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, prepared by mixing mixed solution of zirconium oxide octahydrate and lithium nitrate and indium(III) oxide.
AU GONG J
   YANG C
   ZHAO Z
   PEI C
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019A7700E
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium-doped zirconium oxide- loaded indium oxide catalyst, is claimed. The lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide is prepared by bulk doping monoclinic zirconium oxide with lithium atoms by co-precipitation method, where molar doping amount of lithium ions (x) is 5-10%, uniformly loading lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide with indium oxide particles, where the amount of the indium oxide is 6-10% of the lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has simple structure, convenient preparation and low operating pressure and maintains long-term stability and achieves more than 10% carbon dioxide conversion and 90% methanol selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving zirconium oxide octahydrate and lithium nitrate in deionized water according to the value of x to obtain 0.2-1 M/l mixed solution and heating the mixed solution to 50-90 degrees C, (ii) continuously stirring the mixed solution, adding concentrated aqueous ammonia dropwise into the mixed solution until the pH of the mixed solution is 8-10, (iii) aging the mixed solution at 50-90 degrees C for 1-3 hours, centrifuging obtained suspension, washing, drying under vacuum, calcining at 300-500 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain (x) lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide, where x is the molar doping amount of lithium ions in the lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide, and (iv) dissolving hydrated indium nitrate in deionized water to obtain 0.4-1 M/l solution, adding the solution dropwise into the (x) lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide by ensuring mass fraction of indium oxide is 6-10%, processing ultrasonically, drying, calcining at 300-500 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain indium(III) oxide/(x) lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide catalyst; and
   (2) used method the catalyst in hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, comprising (1) tableting and granulating the catalyst, and (2) subjecting granular to high-pressure continuous reaction in reaction gas at 250-350 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide is a carrier and the indium oxide is catalytically active component. The particle diameter of the lithium-doped monoclinic zirconium oxide is 50-100 nm; and the indium oxide particles is 8-15 nm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the speed of adding concentrated ammonia water is 20-50 drops/minute. In the steps (iii) and (iv), the vacuum-drying is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 8-12 hours. In the step (iv), the ultrasonic treatment time is 1-3 hours.
