TI Flux-cored wire used for gas shielded arc welding,            is filled with flux in outer layer containing            composition of slag component containing titanium            dioxide, silica, zirconium dioxide, alumina and            magnesium oxide, and alloy composition.
AU NAOKI M
   MUKAI N
   GEI N
AE KOBE SEIKO SHO KK (KOBM-C)
   KOBE STEEL LTD (KOBM-C)
   KOBE SEIKO SHO KK (KOBM-C)
   KOBE SEIKO SHO KK (KOBM-C)
   KOBE STEEL LTD (KOBM-C)
GA 2019484082
AB    NOVELTY - A flux-cored wire is filled with flux in an                outer layer. The flux-cored wire enables                gas-shielded arc welding using shielding gas                including 0-5 vol.% oxygen, 0-15 vol.% carbon                dioxide and remainder of gas including argon and                excluding arsenic, antimony, lead and bismuth. The                contents of oxygen and carbon dioxide gas satisfy                relation (i). The outer layer and flux comprise                composition of slag component containing 4-9 %mass                titanium dioxide, 0.3-2 %mass silica, 1.5-3 %mass                zirconium dioxide, 0.3 %mass or less alumina and                0.5 %mass or less magnesium oxide, and alloy                composition containing 10-35 %mass chromium and 4.5                %mass or less niobium, and satisfies relation                (ii).
   USE - Flux-cored wire is used for gas shielded arc                welding (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The flux-cored wire has excellent in slag                releasability, welding workability, defect                resistance and bead geometry, and high sputter                generation amount, and is capable of                high-efficiency operation without risk of reheat                cracking, and provides welding bead having                excellent corrosion resistance even when used in                equipment operating at high temperature for long                period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flux-cored wire is filled with flux in an                outer layer. The flux-cored wire enables                gas-shielded arc welding using shielding gas                including 0-5 vol.% oxygen, 0-15 vol.% carbon                dioxide and remainder of gas including argon and                excluding arsenic, antimony, lead and bismuth. The                contents of oxygen and carbon dioxide gas satisfy                relation: ((CO2)+(3x (O2))) less than or equal to                15 (i). The outer layer and flux comprise                composition of slag component containing 4-9 %mass                titanium dioxide, 0.3-2 %mass silica, 1.5-3 %mass                zirconium dioxide, 0.3 %mass or less alumina and                0.5 %mass or less magnesium oxide, and alloy                composition containing 10-35 %mass chromium and 4.5                %mass or less niobium, and satisfies relation: ((3x                (O2))+(CO2)+(0.0085x A2)-(0.19x A)) less than or                equal to 20, where A is ((Cr)+(4.3x (Nb))) (ii). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for welding                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The composition of                  slag component further contains 0.25-1.5 %mass                  alkali metal component including sodium compound,                  potassium compound and lithium compound, 0.05-0.8                  %mass metal fluoride, 0.5 %mass or less ferric                  oxide and 0.2 %mass or less unavoidable metal                  oxide. The wire contains 8-30 %mass flux and 7-15                  %mass slag component. PN WO2019099928-A2; WO2019099928-A3; US2020338639-A1; IN202017025479-A
TI Apparatus for manufacturing carbon dioxide turbine            of e.g. automotive passenger car, by utilizing metal            powder, has cartridge provided with thermal            communication channeling medium and sealing and            pressurizing fabrication system.
AU FRIESTH K
AE FRIESTH K (FRIE-Individual)
   FRIESTH K (FRIE-Individual)
GA 201945820L
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a dispensing unit for                dispensing a set of layers of powder within a                boundary to a target surface. A beam management                unit performs beam management utilizing mirrors on                X-axis and Y-axis. A focusing lens system controls                beam diameter of Z-axis. A transparent thermal                barrier unit (1016) is arranged between beams and a                build area. A computer control system monitors and                analysis three-dimensional (3D) object and                two-dimensional sliced layers by utilizing a                machine learning process. An actuator is provided                with a lift mechanism. A cartridge (1020) is                provided with a thermal communication channeling                medium and a sealing and pressurizing fabrication                system.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing a thermal and                mechanical component i.e. ultra critical,                transcritical and subcritical carbon dioxide                turbine such as liquid fuel rocket engine, of a                vehicle e.g. automotive passenger car and                commercial vehicle, by utilizing metal powder and a                wire in a fabrication unit for various                applications. Uses include but are not limited to                aerospace applications, automotive applications,                medical devices and agriculture communication                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus reduces pinch points and                unnecessary cavitation within channels, and                increases potential flow characteristics and                thermal effectiveness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a fabrication unit
   (2) a modular sealing and bearing turbine                cartridge
   (3) a method for fabricating a ultracritical,                transcritical and subcritical carbon dioxide                turbine
   (4) a method for generating electricity,                heating and cooling with a ultracritical,                transcritical, subcritical carbon dioxide                turbine
   (5) a thermal energy management system for                solid oxide fuel cells
   (6) a method for managing thermal energy                flowing into and out of an SOFC system
   (7) a monolithic advanced gas cooled nuclear                reactor
   (8) a liquid fuel rocket engine
   (9) an annular thrust cell arrangement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of an apparatus for manufacturing a thermal and                mechanical component of a vehicle.
   Power supply system (1002)
   Laser power control system (1004)
   Laser optics control system (1006)
   Transparent thermal barrier unit                (1016)
   Cartridge (1020)

PN WO2019084324-A1; WO2019084324-A8; CA3084716-A1; BR112020008227-A2
TI Carbon dioxide sequestration from atmosphere comprises applying microbial agent comprises e.g. Bradyrhizobium spp and Trichoderma spp to plant root cortex, enhancing photosynthetic processes and increasing root biomass.
AU HARMAN G
   MARINO A S
   CADLE-DAVIDSON M
AE ADVANCED BIOLOGICAL MARKETING INC (ADBI-Non-standard)
GA 201937593J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide sequestration from the atmosphere comprises (a) applying at least one microbial agent to at least one plant, the microbial agent comprises Bradyrhizobium spp, Trichoderma spp, Bacillus spp, Pseudomonas spp and/or Clonostachys spp, where the microbial agents are applied to the root cortex of the plant, (b) enhancing photosynthetic processes of the plants and (c) increasing biomass of the root and the carbon dioxide is sequestered due to the increased biomass of the root.
   USE - The method is useful for sequestering carbon dioxide from the atmosphere. The composition is useful for enhancing growth of plants, where the plant growth enhancement is in the form of greater root mass, greater depth of rooting, greater shoot mass, greater length of shoots, increased leaf greenness, increased yields, improved stand plant vigor, increased nitrogen use efficacy, or resistance to abiotic stress.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances nitrogen utilization and increases crop yield. The composition and the system are capable of reducing nitrous oxide emissions in air and leaching of nitrates into soil and water and enhancing sequestration of carbon from air. The system is capable of enhancing resistance of plants to abiotic stresses, enhancing nitrogen use efficacy in plants (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) a composition for enhancing growth of plants comprising a Trichoderma strain capable of contacting a plant or a plant seed under conditions effective for the Trichoderma strain to colonize the roots of the plant or a plant grown from the plant seed, thus creating a plant Trichoderrna system and growing the plant or plant seed under conditions effective to sustain the system in a planting medium and to enhance plant growth, where the Trichoderma strain is Trichoderma viride strain NRRL 50520; and (2) a plant Trichoderma system comprising a plant or a plant seed and a Trichoderma strain which is Trichoderma virdie strain NRRL B50520, where the strain is contacted with the plant or plant seed under conditions effective for the strain to colonize the roots of the plant or a plant grown from the plant seed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The application comprises broadcast application, aerosol application, spray-dried application, liquid, dry, powder, mist, atomized, semisolid, gel coating, lotion, linked or linker material, material, material in furrow application, spray application, irrigation, injection, dusting, pelleting, or coating of the plant or the plant seed or the planting medium with the microbial agent. The step of contacting is carried out by broadcast application, liquid or dry in furrow application, spray application, irrigation injection, dusting pelleting or coating of the plant or the seed or the planting medium with Trichodermastrain. The Bacillus strain is mixed further with at least strain of Trichoderma spp. Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises at least one metabolite comprising 6-pentyl pyrone, harzianic acid, hydtra, harzinolide and/or 1-octene-3-ol. Preferred Components: The microbial agent comprises e.g. Trichoderma harzianum (T22), Trichoderma harzianum strain K2 (PTA ATCC 97Th), Trichoderma arroviride strain K4 (PTA ATCC 9707), Trichoderma viride strain K5, Trichoderma viride strain NRRL W50520 and Bacillus amyloliquefaciens As1, As2, and/or As3. The plant comprises e.g. wheat, barley, oats, rye, cotton, sorghum, sunflower, peanut, potato, sweet potato, bean, pea, turnips, cauliflower, onion, garlic, eggplant, pepper, carrot, squash, pumpkin, apple, pear, melon, strawberry, grape, raspberry, pineapple, soybean, maize, clover sugarcane, roses, snapdragon, tulip, daffodil, herbs, ornamental shrubs, ornamental grasses and turfgrass. The microbial agent a physical agent comprises a biological agent, an adjuvant, an acid, a base, a palette, a genetically modified organism free agent a herbicidal agent, a pesticidal agent, a growth promoting agent, a microbe, bacterial species, viral species, fungal species yeast species plant species, spores, cellular components, metabolites compounds, surfactants, emulsifiers, metals, elements, nutrients derivatives, media, medium, K1, K2, K3, K4, KS, Mi As2 and/or As3. The Trichoderma strains are provided as a granule, dust, powder, slurry, film, and/or liquid suspension. The metabolites are derived from Trichodenna spp. The metabolites are derived from Bacillus spp comprising sufactin, iturin, fengycin and other bacterial metabotites including polyketides. The metabolites are derived from eukaryotic and prokaryotic sources. The additional Bacillus strain is Bacillus amloliqofaciens As3. The Trichodermastrain is Trichoderma viride K5 strain.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109168810-A
TI Controlling pests of Citrus, by extracting e.g. Camellia oleifera fruit, marigold root and medlar using ethanol, adding slurry to finished paper, forming finished paper to bag, and spraying medicinal extract on Citrus fruit tree.
AU LIU Q
AE GUANGXI LONGLIN JINQIU ECOLOGICAL AGRIC (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019085017
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling pests of Citrus, involves obtaining 8 pts. wt. Camellia oleifera fruit, 12 pts. wt. marigold root, 5 pts. wt. medlar, 3 pts. wt. black radish root, 2 pts. wt. elaeagnus, 3 pts. wt. black sapwood, 10 pts. wt. coriander leaves and 12 pts. wt. black cedar, adding 20 wt.% water to the mixture, uniformly mixing the materials, treating the mixture, filtering the mixture to obtain slurry and medicinal juice, subjecting the juice to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, performing the supercritical extraction of carbon dioxide, adding anhydrous ethanol as solvent to the mixture, extracting the mixture, concentrating and recovering ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain Chinese medicinal extract, adding the slurry into finished paper, forming the finished paper into a bag that is sealed, and spraying the traditional Chinese medicinal extract on the Citrus fruit tree at time of fruit-breaking process.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling pests of Citrus (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for controlling pests of Citrus, involves obtaining 8 pts. wt. Camellia oleifera fruit, 12 pts. wt. marigold root, 5 pts. wt. medlar, 3 pts. wt. black radish root, 2 pts. wt. elaeagnus, 3 pts. wt. black sapwood, 10 pts. wt. coriander leaves and 12 pts. wt. black cedar, adding 20 wt.% water to the mixture, uniformly mixing the materials, treating the mixture with continuous high-speed disc washer for 30-50 minutes, filtering the mixture to obtain slurry and medicinal juice, subjecting the juice to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, performing the supercritical extraction of carbon dioxide at extraction temperature of 45-50 degrees C and extraction pressure of 10-15 MPa for 15 minutes, adding anhydrous ethanol as solvent to the mixture, extracting the mixture, concentrating and recovering ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain Chinese medicinal extract, adding the slurry into finished paper, forming the finished paper into a bag that is sealed, and spraying the traditional Chinese medicinal extract on the Citrus fruit tree at time of fruit-breaking process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method involves adding the extract slurry to disintegrator to disperse slurry at 4000-5000 revolutions per minute (rpm), adopting standard paper forming device, adding auxiliary agent to the paper, performing quantitative control at 120 g/M2, and drying the wet paper sheet by hot pressing at 1.5 MPa and 90 degrees C for 20 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Pesticide.
DC C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-A08; C04-A10; C04-A98; C14-B01; C14-S18; P13-A04; P13-E01; P13-E02; P13-E06
IP A01G-013/00; A01G-013/02; A01N-065/32; A01P-001/00; A01P-003/00; A01P-007/04

PT P
PN CN109078349-A; CN109078349-B
TI Low temperature methanol washing energy saving device comprises medium pressure flashing tower is connected to a reabsorption tower and semi-lean liquid stripper comprising an upper column, middle column and lower column.
AU LI G
   ZHANG S
   YAN X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC HUBEI CHEM FERTILIZER CO (SNPC-C)
GA 201901715F
AB    NOVELTY - Low temperature methanol washing energy saving device comprises medium pressure flashing tower (1) is connected to a reabsorption tower (2) and semi-lean liquid stripper (3) comprising an upper column, a middle column and a lower column and medium pressure flashing tower is also connected to the upper column of the semi-lean liquid stripper. The top of the upper tower is connected to the first heat exchanger (4), the bottom is connected to the middle tower, the top of the middle tower is connected to the second heat exchanger (5) and the bottom of the middle tower is connected to the lower tower through the third heat exchanger and bottom of the lower tower is connected to the carbon dioxide absorption tower.
   USE - Used as low temperature methanol washing energy saving device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: saves regenerative energy consumption; ensures the operation load; has a simple structure and reasonable process flow; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for washing low temperature methanol, comprising (i) passing part of the carbon dioxide-rich methanol liquid in the medium pressure flashing tower into reabsorption tower for flashing, and another part upper tower of the semi-lean liquid stripping tower for flashing; (ii) flashing carbon dioxide out in the upper tower of the semi-lean liquid stripper is sent into the first heat exchanger for heat exchange, and passing flashed semi-lean liquid into middle tower for flashing; (iii) sending tail gas flashed out of the middle tower into the second heat exchanger for heat exchange, and passing low temperature semi-lean liquid after flashing into third heat exchanger for heat exchange and lower tower for stripping; and (iv) passing semi-lean liquid of the lower tower into carbon dioxide absorption tower for reusuing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of low temperature methanol washing energy saving device.
   Medium pressure flashing tower (1)
   Reabsorption tower (2)
   Semi-lean liquid stripper (3)
   First heat exchanger (4)
   Second heat exchanger (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises maintaining carbon dioxide-rich methanol liquid in the medium pressure flash column at-30 to-36 degrees C at 0.8-1.3MPa. The step (ii) comprises flashing carbon dioxide in the upper column at -50 to-65 degrees C at 0.1-0.2 MPa, and the semi-lean solution after flashing is -50 to-65 degrees C at 0.1-0.2 MPa, flashing tail gas in the middle tower at -70 to-55 degrees C at 0.1-0.2 MPa, flashing low-temperature semi-lean solution at -70 to-55 degrees C at 0.1-0.2MPa, the semi-lean liquid after heat exchange in the third heat exchanger is -45 to -55 degrees C at 0.1-0.2 MPa and semi-lean liquid of the tower is -70 to-55 degrees C at 0.1-0.2 MPa. The method comprises flashing carbon dioxide out in the upper tower cools the shift gas from the conversion unit in the first heat exchanger, flashing exhaust gas out in the middle tower cools the shift gas from the conversion unit in the second heat exchanger, flashing low temperature semi-lean liquid cools and depleting the lean methanol from the methanol-rich/lean methanol heat exchanger in the third heat exchanger.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The bottom of the upper column of the semi-lean liquid stripper is connected to the middle column by a siphon. The bottom of the lower column of the semi-lean liquid stripper is connected to the carbon dioxide absorption tower by a pressure pump. The upper tower of the intermediate pressure flash column is connected to the upper tower of the reabsorption tower and the semi-lean liquid stripper. The first heat exchanger is a converter/carbon dioxide product heat exchanger, second heat exchanger is a shift gas/purification gas heat exchanger and third heat exchanger is a reabsorption tower methanol/lean methanol heat exchanger. The semi-lean liquid of the lower tower is pressurized to -61.04 degrees C at 3.38 MPa, mixed with the carbon dioxide-rich methanol liquid from the unshifted gas absorption tower and enters the upper part of the carbon dioxide absorption tower for carbon dioxide absorption.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E10-E04E1; E11-Q01B; J01-A02; J01-E02A
IP B01D-003/06; B01D-003/14; B01D-053/18; C01B-032/50

PN CN108371316-A; CN108371316-B
TI Preparing edible fungus nano seasoning powder by crushing, seasoning edible fungus, performing enzymatic hydrolysis, concentrating, granulating edible fungus, processing granules in tank by controlling pressure and removing granule.
AU BI J
   YI J
   ZHAO Y
   ZHOU M
   WU X
   LV J
AE AGRO FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINESE (CAGS-C)
   CAAS FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (CAGS-C)
GA 201866117N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing edible fungus nano seasoning powder involves (a) crushing and seasoning the edible fungus, (b) subjecting the seasoned edible fungus to enzymatic hydrolysis, concentrating the enzymatic hydrolyzate and granulating the edible fungus and (c) adding the granules in processing tank which is connected to vacuum tank through pipeline, introducing carbon dioxide into processing tank at 10-35 MPa, maintaining for 5-30 minutes, reducing the pressure in the processing tank to 0.8-1.5 MPa, turning on vacuum pump to maintain absolute pressure in vacuum tank to 3-5kPa, opening the exhaust valve to reduce the pressure in processing tank to 3-10 kPa within 0.2 seconds and maintaining for 1-2 minutes and closing the exhaust valve, introducing carbon dioxide into processing tank to maintain the pressure in processing tank at 10-35 MPa, maintaining for 5-30 minutes, adjusting the pressure in the treatment tank to normal pressure and removing edible fungus granules.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing edible fungus nano seasoning powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing edible fungus nano seasoning powder, involves (a) crushing the edible fungus and seasoning the edible fungus, (b) subjecting the seasoned edible fungus to enzymatic hydrolysis, concentrating the enzymatic hydrolyzate and granulating the edible fungus and (c) adding the granules in a processing tank which is connected to vacuum tank through pipeline, where the vacuum tank is connected to vacuum pump, and an exhaust valve is arranged on pipeline, introducing carbon dioxide into processing tank at 10-35 MPa, maintaining for 5-30 minutes, reducing the pressure in the processing tank to 0.8-1.5 MPa, turning on vacuum pump to maintain absolute pressure in vacuum tank to 3-5kPa, opening the exhaust valve to reduce the pressure in processing tank to 3-10 kPa within 0.2 seconds, and maintaining for 1-2 minutes and closing the exhaust valve, introducing carbon dioxide into processing tank to maintain the pressure in processing tank at 10-35 MPa, maintaining for 5-30 minutes, adjusting the pressure in the treatment tank to normal pressure and removing edible fungus granules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The edible fungus comprises 30-60 %mass shiitake mushroom or its by-product, 15-40 %mass Pleurotus eryngii or its by-product and 15-40 %mass Flammulina velutipes or its by-product. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding 2-4% seasoning to sterilized edible fungus and boiling at 150-200 kPa for 30-60 minutes, where the seasoning comprises 1-2 pts. wt. licorice, 1-2 pts. wt. aniseed, 1-2 pts. wt. cinnamon and 2-3 pts. wt. Chinese prickly ash. The step (b) involves adding the seasoned edible fungus to 25 %mass water, crushing at 9000-15000 revolutions/minute for 1-3 times to a particle size of less than 400 mu m, adding 0.5-1.5% enzyme to seasoned edible fungus, adjusting the pH to 6-6.5, hydrolyzing at 50-55 degrees C for 90-120 minutes, where the enzyme is a complex enzyme which comprises protease and cellulase in a mass ratio of 1-2:1-2, adding accessories to edible fungus hydrolyzate, adjusting the pH to 5-6, reacting at 110-125 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours, where the accessories comprise 0.3-0.5 %mass glucose, 0.6-1.2 %mass xylose, 0.1-0.15 %mass arginine, 0.25-0.75 %mass soy hydrolyzate and 0.4-0.95 %mass fish protein hydrolyzate, adding the reacted material into high-pressure micro-jet nano-homogenizer at 120-350 MPa for 1-5 times to a particle size of less than 200 nm, concentrating the homogenized material at 50-60 degrees C and 0.08-0.10 MPa for 10-20 minutes, mixing the concentrated material with 2-10 %mass seasoning accessories and granulating the material. The seasoning accessories are prepared by using 5-10 pts. wt. maltodextrin, 2-4 pts. wt. chicken extract, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium glutamate, 1-2 pts. wt. spices, 2-4 pts. wt. flavoring agent, 0.5-2 pts. wt. edible salt, mixing, crushing and passing through 80-100 mesh sieve. The flavoring agent is prepared by mixing 5-8 pts. wt. anise, 2-5 pts. wt. sweet basil, 3-6 pts. wt. mustard, 2-5 pts. wt. caraway, 3-5 pts. wt. cardamom, 2-4 pts. wt. clove, 3-5 pts. wt. cinnamon, 6-8 pts. wt. coriander, 3-5 pts. wt. dill, 1-3 pts. wt. nutmeg, 2-5 pts. wt. Perilla leaves, 2-5 pts. wt. garlic, 1-3 pts. wt. bay laurel, 1-3 pts. wt. Coleus, 1-3 pts. wt. rose and 2-5 pts. wt. musk, crushing, extracting with 100-120 pts. wt. water at 90 degrees C for 20 hours, filtering the extract, (b) mixing 2-4 pts. wt. long pepper, 2-4 pts. wt. agilawood, 1-3 pts. wt. honey, 2-5 pts. wt. apple peel, 2-4 pts. wt. hawthorn, 2-3 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 80-100 pts. wt. water with residue, beating the pulp, adding 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. amylase and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. protease, digesting at 18-20 degrees C for 2 hours, inactivating the enzyme and filtrating the filtrate and (c) mixing both the extract.

A power supply is electrically connected with the first electrode and the second electrode, where the hydrogen of the hydrogen discharging module enters the first compartment from the hydrogen inlet and is converted into hydrogen ion by the first electrode and passes through the proton exchange membrane, and the carbon dioxide enters the second compartment from the carbon dioxide inlet and is converted into carbon monoxide and water from the second electrode with the hydrogen ion, the carbon monoxide and water are sent out by the outlet.

AU HU Y
   CHEN H
AE DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 202250032C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a ventilation device (10) provided with two operation modes with different air quantity levels. Multiple carbon dioxide sensors (20) are fixed at different positions in an object space adjusted by the ventilation device to obtain a detection value of carbon dioxide concentration at the different positions. A control device (30) is connected with the ventilation devices and the carbon dioxide sensors. The control device determines a mode conversion threshold value according to change trend carbon dioxide content. The ventilation device controls an operation mode of the ventilation device according to a mode conversion reference value.
   USE - Ventilation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system automatically and efficiently adjusts the concentration of carbon dioxide in the room by controlling the ventilation device according to the detection value of the carbon dioxide sensor. The carbon dioxide sensors are set at different positions in the object space adjusted by the device so as to obtain the detection values of the concentration at the different positions, thus avoiding the uneven distribution of concentration caused by personnel flow situation and effectively controlling the operation of the device to reduce the concentration. The control device determines the mode conversion threshold value and the preset calculation logic to obtain mode conversion reference value, so that the device can reduce the operation mode of ventilation device due to carbon dioxide concentration near the specific sensor near the reference value and frequently switching condition in an effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ventilation system linkage control method .

The electrolyte, the alkali metal electrode, and the carbon-based catalyst electrode are located in the outer casing. The outer casing is separated into two sides by a diaphragm. The alkali metal electrode and the carbon-based catalyst electrode are located on both sides of the diaphragm and are immersed in the electrolyte. PT P
PN CN112979499-A; CN112979499-B
TI Separating mixed gas of            heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide involves            placing mixed gas of heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and            carbon dioxide into adsorption tower in presence of            adsorbent.
AU QING F
   DONG L
   GUO Q
   ZHANG N
   QUAN H
AE BEIJING YUJI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING YUJI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   CHEM & CHEM ENG GUANGDONG LAB (CHCH-Non-standard)
GA 202172251C
AB    NOVELTY - Separating mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide                involves placing mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide into                adsorption tower in presence of adsorbent. The                adsorbent is a modified porous carrier. The                adsorbent is obtained by mixing porous carrier with                metal soluble salt aqueous solution, immersing at                room temperature, filtering, and drying. The metal                soluble salt is potassium, sodium, magnesium,                aluminum, chromium, iron, cobalt, nickel, zinc                chloride or different nitrates. The mixed gas after                the adsorption treatment enters the separation                tower, where the carbon dioxide flows out from the                top of the tower. The liquid phase materials in the                bottom of the tower enter the drying tower.
   USE - Method for separating mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates the mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide in a                simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                high efficiency and utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide                involves placing mixed gas of                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile and carbon dioxide into                adsorption tower in presence of adsorbent. The                adsorbent is a modified porous carrier. The                adsorbent is obtained by mixing porous carrier with                metal soluble salt aqueous solution, immersing at                room temperature, filtering, and drying. The metal                soluble salt is potassium, sodium, magnesium,                aluminum, chromium, iron, cobalt, nickel, zinc                chloride or different nitrates. The mixed gas after                the adsorption treatment enters the separation                tower, where the carbon dioxide flows out from the                top of the tower. The liquid phase materials in the                bottom of the tower enter the drying tower. The                liquid phase material in the tower kettle is                treated with a desiccant in the drying tower to                remove inorganic impurities, where the inorganic                impurities include water and acid. The dried                material enters the rectification tower, where                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile is extracted from the                top of the rectification tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The porous carrier is                  activated alumina, activated carbon, molecular                  sieve, and silica gel. The packing of the                  separation tower is a metal Pall ring, a metal                  Raschig ring, a metal ring, or a metal structured                  packing. The packing of the rectification tower                  is a metal Pall ring, a metal step ring or a                  metal ring.

PN WO2022114794-A1; KR2022071558-A
TI Preparing calcium formate by preparing formic            acid-amine adduct by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide,            preparing calcium formate to produce calcium formate            and amine by reacting formic acid-amine addition            compound with calcium compound, and separating prepared            calcium formate.
AU JUNG H W
   KIM C H
   CHOI H
   JIN J H
   YOON S H
   YOON H Y
   CHEONG H
   YUNSUNGHO
   YOUNG Y
AE UNIV CHUNG ANG IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYCU-C)
GA 202274656V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium formate, involves                preparing a formic acid-amine adduct by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, preparing calcium                formate to produce calcium formate and amine by                reacting the formic acid-amine addition compound                with the calcium compound, and separating the                prepared calcium formate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                formate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures and separates calcium                formate in simple process by excluding the                complicated separation process and auxiliary base                required in the existing formic acid production                process, and does not need for catalyst separation                procedure, and ensures stable operation for                prolonged period of time, and is economical, and                improves efficiency of the process, when using the                waste desulfurized gypsum as a calcium compound,                since calcium sulfate separated from waste                desulfurized gypsum can be used as a separate                resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing formic acid,                involving recycling and using the unreacted carbon                dioxide discharged from the hydrogenation step and                amine or water discharged from the carbonate step,                the calcium formate production step and separation                step; and
   (2) an apparatus for producing calcium                formate, comprising a calcium formate production                unit that reacts a formate-amine addition compound                with a calcium compound to produce calcium formate                and amine, and a separation unit that separates the                prepared calcium formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of preparing the                  formic acid-amine addition compound involves                  performing a carbonation step of reacting carbon                  dioxide, amine, and water to prepare ammonium                  bicarbonate, and performing hydrogenation step by                  reacting the ammonium bicarbonate with hydrogen                  in the presence of a catalyst to form a formic                  acid-amine adduct. Preferred Components: The                  calcium compound comprises calcium oxide, calcium                  sulfate, and their combinations, where the                  calcium compound is obtained from waste lime,                  portland cement, seashells, incinerator ash,                  desulfurized gypsum, and their combinations. The                  calcium compound comprises calcium oxide and                  calcium sulfate, and in the calcium formate                  production step, together with the calcium                  formate and the amine compound, producing calcium                  sulfate. The catalyst is a heterogeneous catalyst                  in which an active metal or an active metal salt                  is supported in a porous carrier. The active                  metal comprises iridium, ruthenium or their                  mixture. The porous carrier comprises                  nitrogen-containing or nitrogen-doped porous                  carbon body, nitrogen-doped metal oxide,                  nitrogen-doped metal nitride, or covalent organic                  framework having a nitrogen-containing frame                  ligand. Preferred Conditions: In the carbonation                  step, carbon dioxide is contained in the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas and supplied, and the                  amine selectively absorbs carbon dioxide in the                  carbon dioxide-containing gas. Preferred Device:                  The apparatus comprises a carbonation reaction                  unit for preparing ammonium bicarbonate by                  reacting carbon dioxide, amine and water, and a                  hydrogenation reaction unit for preparing a                  formic acid-amine adduct by reacting the ammonium                  bicarbonate with hydrogen in the presence of a                  catalyst. The hydrogenation reaction unit is a                  trickle bed reactor in which a heterogeneous                  catalyst is fixed. The apparatus comprises an                  amine storage container configured to form an                  aqueous amine solution by mixing water and an                  amine in front of the carbonate reaction unit                  which is an absorption tower in which a carbon                  dioxide-containing gas is supplied from a lower                  portion, and an amine aqueous solution containing                  water and an amine is supplied from an upper                  portion. The carbonate reaction unit is a                  homogenizer for forming ammonium bicarbonate by                  reacting high-purity carbon dioxide with an                  aqueous amine solution. The apparatus comprises a                  separator for separating the amine discharged                  from the carbonate reaction unit and recycling it                  to the carbonate reaction unit, and a separator                  for separating unreacted carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen discharged from the hydrogenation                  reaction unit and recycling them to the                  hydrogenation reaction unit, where the amine and                  water separated in the separation unit are                  recycled by moving to the carbonate reaction                  unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is a                  tertiary amine of NR1R2R3. The tertiary amine                  comprises triethylamine, tripropylamine,                  tributylamine, N,N-dimethylbutylamine,                  N-methylpiperidine, 1-ethylpiperidine,                  N,N-dimethylpiperazine, dimethylcyclohexylamine,                  dimethylhexylamine, N-methylpyrrolidine,                  N-ethylpyrrolidine dimethylphenethylamine, and/or                  dimethyloctylamine.R1, R2 and R3 = alkyl group having 1-6C                  atoms, and the sum of carbon atoms contained in                  the alkyl groups is less than 12.

PN CN114057565-A
TI Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid comprises e.g. dissolving carbon dioxide in polar solvent, adding into prepared Grignard reagent (2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)magnesium chloride mixed solution, and reacting to obtain magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride, and then hydrolyzing.
AU LI P
   DING S
   YAN D
   WANG R
   ZOU R
   DUAN Q
AE SHANDONG HUAAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022317857
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid comprises (i) dissolving carbon dioxide in a polar solvent, adding dropwise to the prepared Grignard reagent (2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)magnesium chloride mixed solution, stirring and refluxing at -20 to 70degrees Celsius and under pressure of 0-0.6 MPa for 0.5-5 hours, and reacting to obtain magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride, and (ii) reacting magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride, and (ii) hydrolyzing magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride under acidic conditions to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cheap and easily available raw materials; is safe, environmentally friendly and easy to operate; has high conversion rate and short reaction process; produces few by-products; and utilizes the solvent can be recycled; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid comprises (i) dissolving carbon dioxide in a polar solvent, adding dropwise to the prepared Grignard reagent (2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)magnesium chloride mixed solution, stirring and refluxing at -20 to 70degrees Celsius and under pressure of 0-0.6 MPa for 0.5-5 hours, and reacting to obtain magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride, and (ii) hydrolyzing magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride under acidic conditions to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): the polar solvent is diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, methyltetrahydrofuran, toluene, N-methylmorpholine, N,N-xylidine and/or pyridine, preferably mixed solution of diethyl ether and tetrahydrofuran in a volume ratio of 1-2:1-2, the amount of polar solvent is 0.5-5 times, preferably 1.5-2.5 times the amount of carbon dioxide. In step (ii): the acid in the acidic condition is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or nitric acid, preferably hydrochloric acid.Preferred Method: In step (i): the reaction conditions are the reaction temperature of 20-50degrees Celsius, the reaction pressure of 0-0.1 MPa, and the reflux and reaction time is 1-2 hours. The method comprises (i) dissolving carbon dioxide in a polar solvent, adding into the prepared Grignard reagent (2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)magnesium chloride mixed solution with a constant pressure dropping funnel, stirring and refluxing reaction at 20-50degrees Celsius under a pressure of 0-0.1 MPa for 1-2 hours to obtain magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride solution, (ii) adding hydrochloric acid solution to magnesium 3,3,3-trifluoropropanoate chloride solution, stirring and reacting for a certain period of time to obtain 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid, and (iii) extracting and separating product with dichloromethane after the reaction is completed to obtain 3,3,3-trifluoropropionic acid, which is weighed, detected and the yield is calculated.

TI Carbon dioxide responsive interpenetrating network gel particle comprises acrylamide monomer, temperature and salt resistance monomer, carbon dioxide responsive monomer, crosslinking agent, initiator one and initiator two.
AU DU D
   PU W
   CHEN B
   FAN H
   LIU R
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202082348G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) responsive interpenetrating network gel particle comprises 15-25 wt.% acrylamide monomer, 1-5 wt.% temperature and salt resistance monomer, 3-6% CO2 responsive monomer, 0.3-0.8 wt.% crosslinking agent, 0.01-0.05 wt.% initiator one and 0.01-0.05 wt.% initiator two.
   USE - CO2 responsive interpenetrating network gel particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 responsive interpenetrating network gel particle has smaller particle size, increases crosslink density per unit volume, strength, size and control intensity during CO2 displacement process, and shows CO2 responsiveness and good reversibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing CO2 responsive interpenetrating network gel particles, which involves:
   (A) dissolving acrylamide, 20-28% temperature and salt resistance monomer and 50% crosslinking agent in distilled water completely;
   (B) adding initiator 1 to system, passing in N230 minutes to remove oxygen in system, increasing temperature to 45-60 degrees C, and reacting for 2-4 hours;
   (C) granulating product, drying, and crushing to obtain pre-crosslinked gel particles;
   (D) dissolving CO2-responsive monomer, initiator 2 and remaining crosslinking agent in water, controlling mass of added monomer to be 7-15 wt.% of solution, adding pre-crosslinked gel particles to aforementioned solution under stirring conditions, allowing mixture to absorb water, swelling for 24 hours, raising temperature to 60-70 degrees C, and reacting for 4 hours; and
   (E) granulating product, drying, and crushing to obtain CO2 responsive interpenetrating network gel particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The temperature-resistant and salt-resistant monomers are N,N-dimethyl octadecyl allyl ammonium chloride, N,N-dimethyl hexadecyl allyl ammonium chloride, methacryloyl chloride, polyoxyethylene alkyl phenol ether or polyoxyethylene methacryloyl chloride modified alkyl alcohol ether. The CO2 responsive monomer is dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate or dimethylaminopropyl methacrylamide. The crosslinking agent is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide. The first initiator is persulfate and azobisisobutylamidine hydrochloride. The second initiator is 4,4'-azobis(4-cyanovaleric acid). Preferred Conditions: The degree of polymerization of in methacryloyl chloride modified alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether or methacryloyl chloride modified alkyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether is 7-30.

TI Air composition adjusting device used as air            conditioner in warehouse, has controllerto perform            oxygen concentration ascending movement which supplies            oxygen concentration gas whose concentration is higher            than external air to object space.
AU NORITAKA K
   NAOHIRO T
   HIDENORI M
   KAMEI N
   TANAKA N
   MATSUI H
AE DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)

   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 201930702H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a deoxygenation portion (41,61)                which separates oxygen from the external air                supplied to object space (5). A gas feed route                (135) includes the highly-concentrated-gas feed                route (136) for oxygen connected from the                deoxygenation portion to the object space. A                controller (110) performs the oxygen concentration                ascending movement which supplies the high oxygen                concentration gas whose oxygen concentration is                higher than the external air before a process in                the deoxygenation portion through the                highly-concentrated-gas feed route for oxygen to                the object space.
   USE - Air composition adjusting device used as air                conditioner in warehouse, for adjusting composition                such as oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide                level of air in warehouse for accommodating                agricultural products such as plant, fruit such as                banana and avocado, vegetables, grain, bulb, and                fresh flower.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen concentration of the air of object                space can be carried out using a separation                membrane in high concentration than the oxygen                concentration in air. The high                carbon-dioxide-levels gas or low                carbon-dioxide-levels gas can selectively be                supplied to the object space by switching the                switching device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air                conditioner in the warehouse. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Object space (5)
   Deoxygenation portion (41,61)
   Controller (110)
   Gas feed route (135)
   Highly-concentrated-gas feed route                (136)

PT P
PN CN109529871-A; CN109529871-B
TI Urchin-like copper-based catalyst, useful for catalyzing complete combustion of carbon monoxide with oxygen as oxidant, has spherical sea urchin structure, and outwardly diverging columnar bodies are formed on spherical sea urchin.
AU ZHANG H
   CHEN J
   XIONG K
   SHEN Y
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYSS-C)
GA 2019311630
AB    NOVELTY - Urchin-like copper-based catalyst has a spherical sea urchin structure, where outwardly diverging columnar bodies are formed on the spherical sea urchin.
   USE - The copper-based catalyst is useful for catalyzing the complete combustion of carbon monoxide with oxygen as an oxidant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based catalyst is simple to prepare and unique in shape.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the copper-based catalyst, comprising (i) mixing a copper precursor solution with a manganese precursor solution to obtain a copper-manganese mixed solution, (ii) adding water, hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and absolute ethanol to the copper-manganese mixed solution, and stirring at room temperature and at 80-100 rpm, (iii) slowly adding aqueous ammonia to the reaction mixture obtained in step (ii), and stirring at room temperature and at 250-350 rpm, (iv) performing hydrothermal treatment on the mixed solution obtained in step (iii), and (v) filtering the reaction mixture obtained in step (iv), washing, drying and calcining the obtained filtrate; and
   (2) the use of the copper-based catalyst, where oxygen is used as a catalyst to oxidize carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide at 30-40 degrees C, 50% carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide at 100-110 degrees C and all carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide at 170-180 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The catalyst is used to catalyze the complete combustion of carbon monoxide with oxygen as an oxidant under oxygen-poor conditions, including 0.5-5% carbon monoxide, 0.5-5% oxygen and argon. Preferred composition: The molar ratio of the copper precursor solution to the manganese precursor solution is (0.5-1.65):1. Preferred Component: The copper precursor is copper nitrate and the manganese precursor is manganese nitrate. Preferred Properties: The columnar body has a rhombic cross section and the length of the columnar body is not greater than 1 mu m.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-E01; E31-N05C; E35-A; E35-S; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N02-D01; N03-E; N06-E01; N07-C01
IP B01J-023/889; B01J-035/08; B01J-037/10; C01B-032/50

PN CN108793885-A; CN108793885-B
TI High-strength self-healing concrete comprises e.g. Portland cement, water, carbon dioxide foaming agent, dispersant, aggregate, modified fiber composite, phenolic antioxidant, stearic acid and modified m-pentadiene petroleum resin.
AU WANG J
AE BENGBU KERUIDA MACHINERY DESIGN CO LTD (BENG-Non-standard)
   KUNMING SHUNHONG NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 201892738U
AB    NOVELTY - High-strength self-healing concrete comprises 100-120 pts. wt. Portland cement, 30-50 pts. wt. water, 10-20 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent, 20-30 pts. wt. dispersant, 55-65 pts. wt. aggregate, 20-30 pts. wt. microbial composite, 20-30 pts. wt. modified fiber composite, 1-3 pts. wt. phenolic antioxidant, 1-3 pts. wt. stearic acid and 4-8 pts. wt. modified m-pentadiene petroleum resin.
   USE - Used as high-strength self-healing concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases flexural and compressive strength of the concrete; improves the self-repairing ability of concrete; extends the repair time of concrete; and increases durability and compactness of the concrete; and the concrete: has high strength recovery rate after repair, and synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing concrete comprising (i) mixing Portland cement, dispersant, aggregate, modified m-pentadiene petroleum resin and 0.5 times water uniformly, then heating at 300 degrees C under the pressure of 100 Mpa, and passing carbon dioxide foaming agent to prepare slurry A, (ii) mixing microbial composite, modified fiber composite, phenolic antioxidant, stearic acid and 0.5 times water uniformly at a temperature of 180 degrees C under a pressure of 50 MPa to obtain slurry B, and (iii) cooling the slurry A and the slurry B to room temperature, mixing uniformly, stirring, and curing to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concrete comprises 110 pts. wt. Portland cement, 40 pts. wt. water, 15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent, 25 pts. wt. dispersant, 60 pts. wt. aggregate, 20-30 pts. wt. microbial composite, 20-30 pts. wt. modified fiber composite, 1-3 pts. wt. phenolic antioxidant, 1-3 pts. wt. stearic acid and 4-8 pts. wt. modified m-pentadiene petroleum resin. Preferred Method: The dispersing agent is guar hydroxypropyltrimonium chloride, colloidal silica, diatomaceous earth, silicon carbide powder, fly ash and phenolic resin composition in a weight ratio of 2:2:8:5:3:10; and the dispersing agent is prepared by (a) dispersing 2 pts. wt. guar hydroxypropyltrimonium chloride in water uniformly, and stirring evenly to obtain solution A having water content of 80%, (b) uniformly dispersing colloidal silica in ethanol, and stirring uniformly to obtain solution B, (c) adding solution A into solution B, where the weight ratio between solution A and solution B is 2:1, stirring uniformly, distilling off the ethanol under reduced pressure, and spray-drying to water content of 30% to obtain mixed slurry A, (d) dispersing 8 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 5 pts. wt. silicon carbide powder, 3 pts. wt. fly ash and 10 pts. wt. phenolic resin in water to obtain mixed slurry B having water content of 30%, (e) placing the mixed slurry B in a ball mill for grinding, adding mixed slurry A dropwise while grinding, continuing grinding for 24 hours, spray drying, and sieving. The modified fiber composite is prepared by (i) mixing 40 kg polypropylene fiber and 20 kg cellulose fiber in the reaction kettle, adding 35 kg 75% isopropyl alcohol, then adding 35 kg 35% aqueous sodium hydroxide solution and 15 kg polyethylene glycol 400, stirring, warming, and controlling the temperature at 75-80 degrees C, the stirring rate is 2000 revolutions per minute, and the reaction is 40 minutes, (ii) maintaining a temperature of 75-80 degrees C, continuing adding 35 kg 95% isopropyl alcohol solution, then adding 35 kg 50% aqueous sodium hydroxide solution and 25 kg 3-chloro-2-hydroxypropyltrimethylammonium chloride, and then adding 1 kg 50% potassium iodide aqueous solution, and stirring at 3000 revolutions per minute for 40 minutes, and (iii) purifying and drying the product obtained in step (ii). The microbial composite is prepared by (a) placing 1 kg hydroxyethyl methylcellulose, 1 kg polyacrylamide, 0.1 kg phosphite antioxidant, 2 kg anaerobic bacteria, 3 kg medium and 7 kg deionized water in a high shear emulsifier for emulsification to obtain emulsion, (b) placing 8 kg hydroxyethyl- beta -cyclodextrin and 25 kg deionized water in a grinder for grinding to obtain paste, and (iii) adding the emulsion obtained in the step (a) into paste obtained in the step (b), and stirring until the mixture is uniformly mixed, and freeze-drying. The modified m-pentadiene petroleum resin is prepared by placing 98 kg m-pentadiene petroleum resin in a molten kettle, heating and stirring until molten, then adding 0.5 kg phosphite antioxidant and 1.5 kg polyisobutylene succinic anhydride, stirring until the mixture is even, cooling and granulating by a granulator. Preferred Components: The aggregate is perlite.

The hydrocarbon comprises methane.Rf1, Rf2, Rf3, Rf4, Rf5 = linear or branched 1-6C alkyl or a linear or branched 1-6C fluorinated alkyl group, preferably 2,2,2-trifluoroethyl groups, where at least one of Rf2 and Rf3 is a fluorinated alkyl group and at least one of Rf4 and Rf5 is a fluorinated alkyl group;andR1, R2 = 1-6C linear or branched alkyl.Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide and methane absorption selective ratio of the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid is greater than or equal to 10. Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide absorbing liquid has a carbon dioxide absorbing capacity of greater than or equal to 0.4 at carbon dioxide partial pressure of 2 MPa (40 degrees C), greater than or equal to 0.6 at carbon dioxide partial pressure of 4 MPa (40 degrees C) and greater than or equal to 0.7 at carbon dioxide partial pressure of 6 MPa(40 degrees C).

TI Capturing and storing carbon dioxide, involves            carburization or partial carburization of a transition            metal oxide with a heated gaseous fuel stream, and            carbonation of an alkaline earth metal oxide with the            carbon dioxide.
AU MATERIC V
AE HOT LIME LABS LTD (HOTL-Non-standard)
   HOT LIME LABS (HOTL-Non-standard)
   HOT LIME LABS (HOTL-Non-standard)
   HOT LIME LABS LTD (HOTL-Non-standard)
   HOT LIME LABS LTD (HOTL-Non-standard)
GA 2018715239
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and storing carbon dioxide, involves                carburization or partial carburization of a                transition metal oxide with a heated gaseous fuel                stream to produce carbon dioxide, and carbonation                of an alkaline earth metal oxide with the carbon                dioxide to form a carbonate.
   USE - The method, apparatus, and sorbent material                are useful for capturing, storing, and/or releasing                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Provided is method for recovering clean carbon                dioxide from a fuel stream, storing and releasing                the carbon dioxide for use as required. It is                particularly useful on a small to medium scale                where clean carbon dioxide is required for                horticultural uses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an apparatus (400) for capturing and                storing carbon dioxide, comprising: (a) a unit for                receiving a heated gaseous fuel stream; (b) a unit                for contacting the heated gaseous fuel stream with                a transition metal oxide to form a transition                metal, metal carbide or mixture of metal carbide                and metal or a lower oxidation level metal oxide,                and carbon dioxide; and (c) a unit for contacting                the carbon dioxide with an alkaline metal oxide or                alkaline earth metal oxide to form an alkaline                metal carbonate or alkaline earth metal carbonate;                or an apparatus for capturing and storing carbon                dioxide, comprising: (a) a unit for receiving a                heated gaseous fuel stream; (b) a unit for                contacting the heated gaseous fuel stream with a                transition metal oxide to facilitate reduction of                the transition metal oxide to form a transition                metal ion, and carburization of the transition                metal ion to form a transition metal carbide and                carbon dioxide; and (c) a unit for contacting the                carbon dioxide with an alkaline metal oxide or                alkaline earth metal oxide to form an alkaline                metal carbonate or alkaline earth metal carbonate;                and
   (2) a sorbent material (415) suitable for                carbon dioxide capture and storage apparatus,                comprising a composite of an alkaline earth oxide                and a transitional metal oxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                apparatus for capture and storage carbon                dioxide.
   Apparatus (400)
   Inlet gases (401, 402)
   Single vessel (410)
   Main functional zones (411, 412, 413)
   Sorbent material (415)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Alternatively,                  capturing and storing carbon dioxide, involves:                  (a) contacting a heated gaseous fuel stream with                  a transition metal oxide; (b) reducing the                  transition metal oxide to form a transition metal                  and carbon dioxide; (c) carburizing or partially                  carburizing the transition metal to form an                  amount of metal carbide; and (d) contacting the                  carbon dioxide with an alkaline metal oxide or                  alkaline earth metal oxide to form an alkaline                  metal carbonate or alkaline earth metal                  carbonate. The method involves the preliminary                  step of creating the heated gaseous fuel stream                  by mixing the fuel stream with air. It involves a                  further step of carbon dioxide release, the                  carbon dioxide release step involving: (e)                  contacting the metal carbide or mixture of                  transition metal and metal carbide formed in step                  (c) with air to produce a transition metal oxide                  and carbon dioxide; and (f) calcining the                  alkaline metal carbonate or alkaline earth metal                  carbonate of step (d) to form carbon dioxide and                  an alkaline metal oxide or alkaline earth metal                  oxide. The rate of release of carbon dioxide at                  steps (e) and (f) is controlled by the rate of                  contact between the transition metal carbide and                  air. The fuel stream is contacted with a                  transition metal oxide and alkaline metal oxide                  or alkaline metal earth metal oxide substantially                  simultaneously. The transition metal oxide and                  alkaline metal oxide or alkaline earth metal                  oxide are combined in a composite sorbent                  material. Forming a metal carbide is completed or                  partially completed such that the maximum amount                  of carbon deposition is below a metal/carbon                  ratio of 0.9. Carburization step is only                  partially completed and the metal oxide is                  reduced to a mixture of metal carbide and metal                  or a lower oxidation level metal oxide. The                  sorbent material is a pelletized composition of                  calcium oxide (CaO), iron ore (Fe2O3) and an                  inorganic binder. Preferred Components: The                  gaseous fuel stream is selected from a                  carbonaceous gas or a product of partial                  oxidation of carbonaceous fuels. The transition                  metal and/or compounds are selected from iron,                  nickel, manganese, copper or cobalt and its                  mixture. The alkaline metal or alkaline earth                  metal and/or carbonates or oxides are selected                  from calcium, magnesium or lithium and its                  mixture. The inorganic binder is selected from                  alumina silicate, Portland cement, calcium                  aluminate cements and/or calcium hydroxide. The                  sorbent material includes a catalyst selected                  from nickel, cobalt, platinum and rhodium. It is                  in a pelletized form having substantially 20%                  porosity. Preferred Conditions: The gaseous fuel                  stream is heated at 500-900 degrees C, e.g. 700                  degrees C. The method is conducted within 600-800                  degrees C. Preferred Compositions: The maximum                  amount of carbon deposition is below a                  metal/carbon ratio of 0.6. The sorbent material                  includes CaO and Fe2O3 in a ratio of greater than                  or equal to 3:1. The sorbent material is formed                  from 50-75 wt.% CaO, 15-40 wt.% Fe2O3 and 5-15                  wt.% inorganic binder. It is formed from 60 wt.%                  CaO, 30 wt.% Fe2O3 and 10 wt.% inorganic binder                  cement.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The sorbent material                  includes an organic binder, where the organic                  binder is selected from polyvinyl alcohols,                  starches, dextrin, cellulose polymers,                  polyethylene glycols, lignosulfonates, bitumen,                  paraffins, wax emulsions and polyacrilates.                  Preferred Composition: The proportion of organic                  binder is 0.1- 20% of the pellet mass before                  firing.

TI Light decomposing carbon dioxide combustion power system comprises compressor, combustion chamber, gas turbine gas tank, generator, regenerator and membrane separator.
AU XIE Y
   CHEN T
   ZHONG K
   HOU Y
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 201807124K
AB    NOVELTY - A light decomposing carbon dioxide combustion power system comprises compressor, combustion chamber, gas turbine gas tank (A), generator, regenerator (A), regenerator (B) and membrane separator (A). The decomposition process is carried out by compressing air and oxygen, performing combustion and expansion, dividing flue gas generated by combustion into two portions, adding one flue gas into regenerator (A) for heating carbon dioxide, separating carbon dioxide, and residual gases, decomposing carbon dioxide, sending back oxygen to compressor, and collecting nitrogen as product.
   USE - Light decomposing carbon dioxide combustion power system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The light decomposing carbon dioxide combustion power system has high energy saving effect, zero emission of carbon dioxide, and strong expansion effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A light decomposing carbon dioxide combustion power system comprises compressor, combustion chamber, gas turbine gas tank (A), generator, regenerator (A), regenerator (B) and membrane separator (A). The decomposition process is carried out by compressing air and oxygen in compressor, entering into combustion chamber for combustion and expansion in the gas turbine, driving generator to generate electricity, dividing flue gas generated by combustion into two portions, adding one flue gas into the regenerator (A) for heating carbon dioxide, and entering other flue gas into regenerator (B) to heat water to steam, separating flue gas in the membrane separation device (A), separating carbon dioxide, and residual gases such as nitrogen, sending carbon dioxide to optical decomposition carbon dioxide system for decomposition, storing remaining portion in the gas tank (A), sending back oxygen to compressor to participate in secondary combustion, and collecting nitrogen as a product, decomposing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and water vapor into oxygen and hydrocarbon under action of catalyst in vacuum tube, separating carbon dioxide, oxygen, and hydrocarbon by membrane separator (B), returning carbon dioxide to trough solar energy device to participate in the reaction, returning back oxygen to the compressor for performing compression and combustion again, storing hydrocarbons in gas tank (B) and sending to the combustion chamber for replenishment. The optical decomposition carbon dioxide system comprises trough solar energy device, membrane separator (B) and gas tank (B). The inner wall of the vacuum tube is attached to the catalyst containing nickel-copper-zinc-iron oxide of formula: Ni0.49Cu0.24Zn0.24Fe2O4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide separated by membrane separator (A) is heated by regenerator to control temperature and demineralized water is heated by heater to form steam and control temperature.

TI Improving carbon dioxide corrosion resistance of            martensitic heat-resistant steel involves forming oxide            coating on the surface of heat-resistant steel by            plasma sintering, main components of oxide coating            comprises silicon dioxide.
AU WEI F
   SHI Q
   ZHAO Y
   LIU X
   TANG M
AE HANGZHOU LINGYANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020A6717D
AB    NOVELTY - Improving carbon dioxide corrosion resistance                of martensitic heat-resistant steel involves                forming oxide coating on the surface of                heat-resistant steel by plasma sintering.
   USE - Method for improving carbon dioxide corrosion                resistance of martensitic heat-resistant                steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve carbon dioxide                corrosion resistance of martensitic heat-resistant                steel that improves the carbon dioxide corrosion                resistance of the martensite heat-resistant steel                and prolongs the service life of the martensite                heat-resistant steel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The main components of                  the oxide coating comprises 60-75 wt.% silicon                  dioxide and 10-15 wt.% aluminum oxide. The oxide                  coating ingredients also includes 4-8 wt.%                  zirconium dioxide, 2-6 wt.% titanium dioxide, 1-4                  wt.% sodium oxide, 1-3 wt.% potassium nitrate.                  The oxide coating is melted at high temperature                  of 1350 plus minus 50 degrees C, water cooled                  after reaction, and powder is prepared by ball                  milling. The heat-resistant steel includes 9-12                  wt.% chromium martensitic heat-resistant steel.                  The chemical composition of heat-resistant steel                  includes 0.05-0.45 wt.% carbon, 0-4 wt.% silicon,                  8-12 wt.% chromium, 0-2 wt.% tungsten, 0-1.0 wt.%                  manganese, 0-0.3 wt.% sum of tantalum and                  niobium, 0-0.2 wt.% vanadium, balance is iron.                  Preferred Process: The method involves polishing                  sample and chamfering with No. 150, No. 240, No.                  400 and No. 600 metallographic sandpaper. The                  surface is sandblasted and finally dried by                  alcohol and acetone. The oxide coating                  ingredients are melted at high temperature of                  1350 plus minus 50?OC, and then cooled in water.                  The oxide coating powder is obtained by ball                  milling. The powder size is 50-100 nanometer. The                  oxide coating powder is mixed with absolute                  ethanol in weight ratio (1.5-2):1, sprayed on the                  surface of the martensitic heat-resistant steel.                  The oxide coating is prepared by curing reaction                  and sintering at 700 plus minus 50 degrees C for                  5-15 minutes, and thickness of the oxide coating                  is 20-50 micrometer.

PN CN114130363-A
TI Preparing mesoporous silicon dioxide foam-based            solid amine adsorbent by extracting aluminium slag from            coal ash used for collecting carbon dioxide, involves            taking coal ash extracted aluminium slag as raw            material.
AU WANG H

   HUANG J
   LIU Z
   HAO Z
   DONG L
   LI X
   WANG Z
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202238958J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesoporous silicon dioxide                foam-based solid amine adsorbent by extracting                aluminium slag from coal ash involves taking coal                ash extracted aluminium slag as raw material,                dissolving it in sodium hydroxide solution,                synthesizing mesoporous silicon dioxide foam                material by hydrothermal synthesis method as                silicon source under the action of template agent,                dipping in amine polymer to obtain solid amine                adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing mesoporous silicon                dioxide foam-based solid amine adsorbent by                extracting aluminium slag from coal ash used for                collecting carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare mesoporous                silicon dioxide foam-based solid amine adsorbent by                extracting aluminium slag from coal ash in a                simple, eco-friendly and cost-effective                manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The specific steps are                  mixing the aluminium slag with sodium hydroxide                  solution, heating and stirring, filtering,                  collecting the filtrate to obtain the solution                  containing sodium silicate, mixing acetic acid,                  ammonium fluoride and P123 template agent in                  distilled water, heating and stirring, then                  adding pore-expanding agent, after mixing                  uniformly, gradually adding sodium silicate                  solution containing silicon dioxide under                  vigorous stirring, where silicon element in                  silicon dioxide is from the solution containing                  sodium silicate obtained, standing, taking out                  and washing to neutral and drying, calcining to                  obtain mesoporous silicon dioxide foam material,                  amine polymer is dissolved in methanol, stirring                  at room temperature to mix uniformly, then adding                  mesoporous silicon dioxide foam material                  obtained, stirring at room temperature until the                  methanol is completely evaporated, vacuum drying                  to obtain solid amine adsorbent. Preferred                  Conditions: The concentration of the sodium                  hydroxide solution is 1 mol/l, the volume of the                  sodium hydroxide solution is 20-40 ml, the                  quality of the aluminium-extracting slag is 1-3g;                  the heating temperature is 50-90degrees Celsius,                  the stirring time is 0.5-2h. The volume of the                  sodium hydroxide solution is 34 nl, 1.7g, quality                  of the aluminium slag is the heating temperature                  is 90degrees Celsius, the stirring time is 2h.                  The mass ratio of the acetic acid, ammonium                  fluoride, P123 template agent and distilled water                  is 4.47: 0.06: 3: 50. The mass ratio of silicon                  dioxide to P123 is 1: 2; the pore-expanding agent                  is trimethylbenzene, the mass of the                  trimethylbenzene is 0.5-6 g.

PN US11253548-B1
TI Producing constituents of therapeutic product from platelets and alpha-granules, involves isolating platelets from mammalian source, exposing platelets and their alpha-granules to supercritical carbon dioxide to produce therapeutic product.
AU URIBE Y
   BODOR M
   DREGALLA R
AE NAPA MEDICAL RES FOUND (NAPA-Non-standard)
GA 202227741Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing the constituents of a therapeutic product from platelets and alpha-granules, involves isolating platelets from a mammalian source. The platelets and their alpha-granules are exposed to supercritical carbon dioxide to release intramembrane platelet components and intramembrane alpha-granule components to produce constituents of the therapeutic product, where the intramembrane alpha-granule constituents are soluble in the final therapeutic product.
   USE - Method for producing constituents of therapeutic product from platelets and alpha-granules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables releasing and capturing therapeutic intramembrane components from platelets and alpha-granules, and maximizing the amount of intramembrane components for therapeutic use while maintaining the integrity, conformation, therapeutic efficacy, and bioactivity of the intramembrane components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The platelets and alpha-granules are exposed for 1-30 minutes. The isolated platelets are suspended in a fluid to form its mixture, where the mixture is exposed to the supercritical carbon dioxide. The intramembrane platelet components and intramembrane alpha-granule components are re-suspended in at least one of a biological fluid, saline, or water for use as the final therapeutic product.Preferred Method: The step of producing constituents of therapeutic product further involves off-gassing the supercritical carbon dioxide after an exposure time, where the supercritical carbon dioxide is maintained above freezing temperatures during the off-gassing; pelleting the isolated platelets into a concentrated mass and removing at least 90% of the supernatant, where the concentrated mass with the removed supernatant is exposed to the supercritical carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of therapeutic product preparations and its biological data that do not support the claims.
