PN IN202111016514-A
TI Production of bio-compressed natural gas from biogas by pressurizing biogas to specific pressure and cooling down to specific temperature, where impurities get separated in liquid form and purified gas can be further pressurized.
AU PRASAD B
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY ROORKEE (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B1105U
AB    NOVELTY - Production of bio-compressed natural gas (CNG) from biogas involves pressurizing the biogas to a specific pressure and cooling down to a specific temperature. Impurities other than methane get separated in liquid form and the purified gas can be further pressurized for injection into the natural gas pipeline networks or filling in cascade of cylinders.
   USE - The process is useful for production of bio-CNG from biogas (claimed); and for producing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in liquid forms. The liquid carbon dioxide can be used in fire extinguishers, precision sand casting, homogenous cloud effect and breweries.
   ADVANTAGE - The process for production of bio-CNG from biogas, is cost-effective, simple, and has minimal methane loss.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: Production of bio-CNG involves: (a) scrubbing the biogas with water to remove the suspended particulates; (b) removal of moisture with the help of dehydrating agents; (c) compression of the biogas at 80-120 kg/cm2; (d) cooling the compressed biogas at 35-5 degrees C in a condenser cum separator for the removal of hydrogen sulfide and other condensable, if any, in liquid form; (e) subsequent cooling of the compressed biogas at less than or equal to 30 degrees C in a second condenser cum separator for the removal of carbon dioxide in liquid form; (f) adding the odorizer in bio-CNG for the injection into city gas distribution (CGD) pipeline network if available; (g) injection into CNG pipeline network, if available, and subsequent compression of the resultant bio-CNG to a pressure of 200-250 kg/cm2 for the filling in cascade of cylinders. The hydrogen sulfide is recovered in liquid form and converted to elemental sulfur. The carbon dioxide gas is recovered in liquid form. The bio-CNG is injected into CGD or CNG pipeline networks or used for filling of cascade of cylinders.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PT P
PN CN111100957-A
TI Granulating high-temperature liquid slag and recovering waste heat comprises using low-temperature air, water and low-temperature carbon dioxide as heat exchange medium in turn, and using three-stage three-medium method.
AU WANG X
   CHEN W
   WANG H
   LIU L
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 2020403570
AB    NOVELTY - Granulating high-temperature liquid slag and recovering waste heat comprises using low-temperature air, water and low-temperature carbon dioxide as heat exchange medium in turn, using three-stage three-medium method, and exchanging heat with high-temperature liquid slag to obtain solid slag at room temperature and heat exchange medium after heat absorption.
   USE - The method is useful for granulating high-temperature liquid slag and recovering waste heat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not directly contact the water with the slag; takes full account of the energy and resource properties of the hot slag; uses the multi-step cooling to fully utilize the heat in it; and has great significance to practice and promote.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method is specifically comprises (i) using high-speed low-temperature air to cool the small droplets of slag formed after the crushing of high-temperature liquid slag to form high-temperature solid slag and high-temperature air, (ii) using the high-temperature solid slag obtained in the step (i) by water cooling to obtain a medium-temperature solid slag and high-temperature water vapor, and (iii) cooling counter-current heat exchange between the low-temperature carbon dioxide and the intermediate-temperature solid slag obtained in the step (ii), and stabilizing the intermediate-temperature solid slag at room temperature to obtain solid slag and high temperature carbon dioxide. The method further comprises recovering the waste heat of high-temperature air, high-temperature water vapor and high-temperature carbon dioxide. The high-temperature air obtained in the step (i) and the high-temperature carbon dioxide obtained in the step (iii) are heat-exchanged with a water-cooled heat exchanger, and the high-temperature water vapor obtained by heat exchange and the high-temperature water vapor obtained in the step (ii) are used for power generation. In the step (i), the high-temperature liquid slag is broken into small droplets of slag by centrifugal granulation. The step (i) specifically comprises using the centrifugal granulation method to crush the high-temperature liquid slag at high temperature to form small droplets of slag, using high-speed low-temperature air to quickly cool the small slag droplets, and solidifying the surface of the small slag droplets to obtain a spherical high-temperature solid slag solid surface. The temperature of the small droplets of the slag is 1150-1250 degrees C. The high-temperature liquid slag is any one of blast furnace slag, converter steel-making slag, electric furnace steel-making slag, and coal-fired furnace slag, and the temperature is greater than 1700 degrees C. The normal temperature solid slag is recycled as a raw material for new building boards.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A07B; M24-B01A
IP C21B-003/06; C21B-003/08

TI Preparing medical tube comprises e.g. adding medical tube and isocyanate solution into reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, adjusting temperature and pressure, reacting and placing processed medical tube in the modified material.

   QUE Y

GA 2020164324
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medical tube comprises (i) adding the medical tube and isocyanate solution into a reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure in the kettle, and reacting in the supercritical state to obtain the medical tube with isocyanate group, and (ii) placing processed the medical tube in the modified material and reacting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing medical tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces an active functional group on the surface of a medical tube, realizes the surface modification of medical tube and improves the biological performance of medical tube.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for medical tube prepared according to above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the reaction time is 30-90 minutes. In the step (ii), the temperature in the reaction kettle is adjusted to 90-120 degrees C, and the pressure in the reaction kettle is adjusted to 8-14 MPa. In the step (ii), the method further comprises depressurizing the reaction kettle, washing and drying the medical tube with acetone and deionized water. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature between the medical tube material and the modified material is 25-30 degrees C and the reaction time is 8-24 hours. Preferred Components: The isocyanate is hexamethylene diisocyanate or toluene diisocyanate. In the step (iii), the modified material is heparin, polyethylene glycol, phosphorylcholine, glycidyl methacrylate, hydroxyethyl methacrylate, phosphorylcholine, or albumin. The modified material is 20-40 mg/ml heparin. The medical tube material is polyether block amide, polyethylene, polypropylene, polyamide, thermoplastic polyurethane or polytetrafluoroethylene.

   Electrode for oxidative reaction (104)

Preferred Properties: The chemical reactor has flow velocity (Q) of electrolyte solution, when formic acid present in the reactor is calculated using the relation: (jx Wx L)/(Fx (Cf-Ci)) less than or equal to Q, where L is length, W is orthogonal to direction of reduction catalyst, j is current density, Cf is density of formic acid of electrolyte solution in container exit, Ci is formic acid of electrolyte solution in container packed opening and F is Faraday. The reactor has flow velocity (Q) of electrolyte solution, when carbon dioxide is present is calculated using the relation: (jx Wx Lx MCO2)/(px Fx (Cin/100-0.80)) less than or equal to Q, where MCO2 is molecular weight of carbon dioxide, p is saturated solubility of carbon dioxide in electrolyte solution, Cin is carbon dioxide in container packed opening.

   m, n, p = 1.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt forming agent is potassium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrating agent is toluene. The solvent is N, N-dimethylformamide, N, N-dimethylacetamide, sulfolane, N-methylpyrrolidone or dimethyl sulfoxide. Preferred Method: The method further involves adding a mol of 4-aminophenylhydroquinone, b mol of bisphenol A monomer, c mol of bisphenol hydrazone, and d mol of 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone under nitrogen protection, after mixing, adding salt-forming agent, dehydrating agent and solvent with water at 120-130 degrees C, and refluxing for 3-5 hours, removing hydrating agent, raising the temperature to 170-185 degrees C, distilling off remaining dehydrating agent, and reacting for 6-20 hours to obtain an amino-containing polyarylene ether ketone.

Preferred                  Method: In the use method, applying medium and                  high temperature heat pump in cold environment of                  -35 degrees C to -5 degrees C, preferably -30                  degrees C to -15 degrees C, more preferably -15                  degrees C to -5 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN109111590-A
TI Preparing polyethylene terephthalate foam            comprises using polyethylene terephthalate particles as            base material, adding triglycidyl isocyanurate or            hexamethylene diisocyanate, mixing, adding mixture into            high pressure kettle, and injecting.
AU YUAN C
AE WEIHAI WEISAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (WEIH-Non-standard)
GA 201903950N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyethylene terephthalate foam                comprises (i) using polyethylene terephthalate                (PET) particles as base material, adding                triglycidyl isocyanurate or hexamethylene                diisocyanate to base material, and mixing, (ii)                adding PET mixture obtained in the step (i) high                pressure kettle, injecting with carbon dioxide                pressurized to 24-26 MPa, reacting for 24-26 hours                at constant temperature, (iii) depressurizing the                high pressure kettle, then subjecting material                obtained in step (ii) to glycerin bath for foaming,                and (iv) extrusion and molding the material                obtained in the step (iii).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                polyethylene terephthalate foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the plasticizing effect                of carbon dioxide and improves the tensile strain                behavior ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  amount of the triglycidyl isocyanurate or the                  hexamethylene diisocyanate is 0.9-1.36% of the                  total weight. Preferred Method: In the step (ii)                  primarily heating the high pressure kettle, and                  when the temperature is raised to 60-65&#176;                  C, using the carbon dioxide for blowing treatment                  for 4-6 minutes. In the step (iii) foaming by                  using a glycerin bath at 150-160&#176; C for                  1-1.5 minutes, and quenching the material by cold                  water. In the step (iv), washing the material is                  washed with alcohol or distilled water, extrusion                  and molding.

GA 2018A35646

PN CN108977234-A; CN108977234-B
TI Decarburization of blast furnace gas and/or coke converter oven gas involves filtering blast to-be-decarburized gas, absorbing carbon dioxide in resultant with methyldiethanolamine solution, cooling, filtering, dispersing and processing.
AU YANG Y
   DAI L
   WANG W
   HOU J
AE DAI L (DAIL-Individual)
   YANG Y (YANG-Individual)
   WANG W (WANG-Individual)
   HOU J (HOUJ-Individual)
GA 2018A2598X
AB    NOVELTY - Decarburization of blast furnace gas and/or converter coke oven gas involves filtering blast furnace gas and/or converter coke oven gas, removing impurities, introducing methyldiethanolamine lean solution, absorbing carbon dioxide, obtaining methyldiethanolamine rich solution, cooling resultant gas, subjecting to gas-liquid separation, filtering, separating mechanical impurities, mixing with methyldiethanolamine rich solution, flashing resultant mixture, transferring resultant to a dispersing system for dissipation, filtering, subjecting to heat exchange, stripping, separating methyldiethanolamine rich solution, analyzing acid gas of methyldiethanolamine rich solution, forming methyldiethanolamine lean solution, subjecting remaining mixture to liquid-gas separation, discharging resultant gas, and cooling separated methyldiethanolamine lean solution.
   USE - Decarburization of blast furnace gas and/or converter coke oven gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The furnace gas comprises 55-65 vol.% carbon monoxide, 5.8-6.2 vol.% carbon dioxide, 28-32 vol.% nitrogen, 1-4 vol.% hydrogen, 0.001-0.005 vol.% oxygen, 1-5 ppm phosphine, 0.1 mg/Nm3 or less tar and dust, in total, and 0.1 mg/Nm3 or less sulfur. Preferred Process: The decarburization of blast furnace gas and/or coke oven gas further involves subjecting to-be-decarburized gas to filtering, compressing at 0.95-1 MPa, reducing impurities content to 1 mg N/m3 or less and desulfurizing such that total sulfur content is 0.1 mg/Nm3 or less, before processing with methyldiethanolamine solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The coke oven gas comprises 20-25 vol.% methane, 55-60 vol.% hydrogen, 8-12 vol.% carbon monoxide, 0.0005-0.0007 vol.% oxygen, 2-4 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.1 mg/Nm3 or less tar and dust, in total, and 0.1 mg/Nm3 or less sulfur.

PT P
PN CN108862380-A
TI Preparation of zirconium tetrachloride involves subjecting zircon sand to carbonation and chlorination reaction to absorb released heat to form zirconium tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide.
AU WU Z
   YIN B
   FAN X
   HUANG B
   LIU X
   SUN Y
   HE L
AE XINTE ENERGY CO LTD (XINT-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG JINGSHUO ADVANCED MATERIALS CO (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018955288
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zirconium tetrachloride, involves (1) mixing aluminum ash, zircon sand and carbon reducing agent, introducing chlorine gas, heating, subjecting alumina in the aluminum ash to chlorination and exotherm reaction to form aluminum chloride, subjecting zircon sand to carbonation and chlorination reaction to absorb the released heat to form zirconium tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation of zirconium tetrachloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The balance of heat is achieved to compensate for the heat required by the system in which the material is located. The dependence on the external heating system or cooling system is reduced, thus achieving energy saving. The production cost of zirconium tetrachloride is reduced, the recycling of aluminum ash is realized, environmental pollution is eliminated, and economic benefits are improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature in the step (1) is 300-500?OC. The mass ratio of aluminum ash, zircon sand and carbon reducing agent is (20-40):(40-70):(10-20). The temperature of cooling synthesis gas (A) is 150-180 degrees C. The temperature of the cooling synthesis gas (B) is 40-120?OC. The temperature of the cooling synthesis gas (B) is not higher than 15 degrees C. Preferred Process: Zirconium tetrachloride is prepared by adding aluminum ash to water, dissolving soluble matter in the aluminum ash in water to obtain a pretreated ash, preprocessing aluminum ash in water, dissolving soluble substance in aluminum ash in water to obtain the treated aluminum ash, obtaining synthesis gas (A), carrying out specific cooling by liquid silicon tetrachloride, precipitating zirconium tetrachloride by cooling, obtaining synthesis gas (B), cooling synthesis gas (B), cooling the precipitated aluminum chloride product, obtaining synthesis gas (C), precipitating silicon tetrachloride by cooling, dissolving the aluminum chloride product in water, hydrolyzing silicon tetrachloride in the aluminum chloride product to form silica, removing water-insoluble impurities and silica from the aluminum chloride product, obtaining an aluminum chloride solution, adding a base to the aluminum chloride solution to form aluminum hydroxide, and calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain alumina. Preferred Components: The aluminum ash is electrolytic aluminum ash and/or melt cast aluminum ash.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

GA 201883632Q

PN CN108516556-A; CN108516556-B
TI Preparing high-purity silicon dioxide, comprises e.g. adding sodium hydroxide solution into silicon slurry for dissolution reaction, introducing carbon-dioxide gas for carbonation decomposition and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU YANG S
   ZHOU X
AE CHENGDU SILIKANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201873511D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity silicon dioxide, comprises (i) adding sodium hydroxide solution into silicon slurry for dissolution reaction and filtering to obtain sodium metasilicate solution; (ii) adding sodium metasilicate solution to sodium carbonate solution, introducing carbon-dioxide gas for carbonation decomposition, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain metasilicate precipitate and sodium carbonate solution; and (iii) subjecting metasilicate precipitate to acid cleaning, washing with water and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity silicon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully utilizes the waste organosilicone slurry in the production process of the organosilicone as a raw material, thus achieves waste utilization; and is economical and environmental-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organosilicone slag is waste material of a silicone production process, where 50-58% elemental silicon and 30- 35% silica. Preferred Method: The dissolution reaction operation in the step (i) is using an open container in contact with air, sodium hydroxide solution is added dropwise to the silicone slurry under constant stirring, where the sodium hydroxide concentration is 35-50%, reaction liquid to solid ratio is 3:1-10:1, after the completion of the addition, reaction time is 2-5 hours and reaction temperature is 50-80 degrees C, after completion of the reaction, filtration was carried out to obtain sodium metasilicate solution having a silica concentration of 50-60 g/l. In the step (ii), purification and decontamination treatment is carried out before adding the sodium metasilicate solution prepared in the step (i) to the sodium carbonate solution by adding calcium oxide to the sodium metasilicate solution obtained in the step (i) to purify and remove impurities, amount of calcium oxide added is 8-10 g/l sodium metasilicate solution, reaction time is 0.5-2 hours, reaction temperature is 80-90 degrees C, after the completion of the reaction, filtration was carried out to obtain a purified sodium metasilicate solution. In the step (ii), the carbonation decomposition reaction is carried out under ultrasonic conditions, pH of the carbonation decomposition solution is controlled at 11-12, the decomposition temperature is 50-55 degrees C and the decomposition time is 5-6 hours. In the step (ii), carbon-dioxide content of the carbon-dioxide gas used exceeds 99%. In the step (iii), the acid pickling operation is adding the metasilicate precipitate prepared in the step (ii) to the hydrochloric acid solution, turning on ultrasonic wave for acid pickling, filtering, continuously adding filter residue with deionized water for washing and washing with water until the filtrate is neutral. The hydrochloric acid concentration is 60-65 g/l, reaction temperature is 80 degrees C and reaction time is 3-5 hours. In the step (iii), the drying temperature is 90-120 degrees C and the drying time is 2-10 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E11-W; E31-P01

PT P
PN WO2018004468-A1
TI Returning aromatics to fermentation broth, by regulating temperature of fermentation broth, collecting mixture of carbon dioxide and aromatics, guiding mixture into condenser, cooling mixture, and returning condensed aromatics to broth.
AU BEROVIC M
   BEROVIC R
   KAVCIC M
   PECAVER U
AE VINTECH PROCESNE TEHNOLOGIJE DOO (VINT-Non-standard)
GA 2018013039
AB    NOVELTY - Process for returning aromatics to a fermentation broth, involves obtaining a fermentation broth comprising yeast for processing into wine, regulating temperature of the fermentation broth to a temperature of 16-30 degrees C, collecting a mixture of carbon dioxide and aromatics, guiding the mixture into a condenser, cooling the mixture in the condenser to condense the aromatics, and returning the condensed aromatics to the fermentation broth.
   USE - The process is useful for returning aromatics to a fermentation broth (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces the loss of aromatics during fermentation of fermentation broth during wine production, and prepares wine with excellent smell and taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for returning aromatics to a fermentation broth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The process further involves preparing of a suspension of wine yeast, preparing wine must, adding suspension of wine yeast to ready-made wine must, and further fermenting the resulting mixture. The step of preparing the wine must further involves preparing the must in a fermentor at 15-30 degrees C, preferably 18 degrees C. The wine must is mixed with a stirrer at 20-80 revolutions per minute (rpm), preferably 50 rpm while simultaneously blowing over the surface of the liquid with gaseous nitrogen flow at 0.3-1.2 l/minute, preferably 0.5 l/minute for 10-30 minutes, preferably 20 minutes. The step of adding the suspension of the yeast to the prepared wine must further involves adding 0.1-0.5 g/l, preferably 0.3 g/l yeast to the wine must, and maintaining the temperature of the mixture at 15-30 degrees C, preferably at 18 degrees C for 45-75 minutes, preferably 60 minutes. The step of fermentation of the obtained mixture further involves fermenting the mixture of the wine must and the suspension of wine yeasts for a time up to 360 hours, preferably up to 240 hours, and mixing the mixture with a stirrer at 20-80 rpm, preferably 50 rpm while simultaneously blowing of the liquid gaseous surface area at 0.3-1.2 l/minute.

TI METHOD FOR REMOVING CARBON DIOXIDE FROM NATURAL            GAS.
AU SERGEEVA M S
   PETUKHOV A N
   VOROTYNTSEV I V
   VOROTYNTSEV V M
AE UNIV NIZHNY NOVGOROD STATE TECH (UYNI-Non-standard)
GA 202200030Y
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of inorganic                chemistry, namely to the separation of natural gas                components by gas hydrate crystallization, and can                be used to remove carbon dioxide from natural gas.                The method for removing carbon dioxide from natural                gas includes the formation of carbon dioxide gas                hydrates at a pressure of 2.0 to 8.0 MPa and a                temperature of 273 to 278 K and their subsequent                decomposition to form a carbon dioxide concentrate.                A natural gas stream with a 6-10-fold molar excess                of water relative to the molar concentration of                hydrate-forming gases in natural gas is fed into                the gas hydrate crystallizer. The components of                natural gas that have not passed into the gas                hydrate phase are removed from the gas hydrate                crystallizer. The formed gas hydrates are taken by                a screw into the separation module for destruction                into water and carbon dioxide concentrate at an                increase in temperature from 293 to 323 K. The                remaining components of natural gas are taken out                for processing.
   USE - Inorganic chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increase in the degree of removal of carbon                dioxide from natural gas.

PN CN113265282-A
TI Separating carbon dioxide from natural gas comprises e.g. determining raw material methane and carbon dioxide, ionic liquids as absorbent, making absorbent to enter absorption tower discharges mixed ionic liquid absorbent into flash tank.
AU HAN J
   YANG Y
   YANG X
   WU S
   TIAN J
   MA Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU LIGHT IND (UZHL-C)
   UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2021A0210M
AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from natural gas comprises determining raw material F, using a mixture of methane and carbon dioxide as raw material F, using a mixture of two ionic liquids as absorbent S, making the raw material F to enter the absorption tower at 20 degrees C, making the absorbent S to enter the absorption tower B1 at 10-30 degrees C, where absorption tower discharges the mixed ionic liquid absorbent and the methane gas product D1, and the carbon dioxide content in the methane gas product is less than 0.1%, the absorption tower B1 discharges the mixed ionic liquid absorbent W1 into the flash tank B2, and the flash tank B2 discharges the regenerated absorbent W2.
   USE - The method is useful for separating carbon dioxide from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; and realizes the efficient separation of carbon dioxide; obtains the absorbent capable of recycling; is economical; and consumes less energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cations of the ionic liquid include imidazoles, pyridines, quaternary ammonium salts, and quaternary phosphonium salts. The anion of the ionic liquid includes hexafluorophosphate, dicyanamide, bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide, tetrafluoroborate, and diethyl sulfate. In the process of determining the raw material, the molar content of carbon dioxide is 0.01-5% and includes the end value 0.01 -5%. The mass ratio of the two ionic liquids in the absorbent is 1:9. Preferred Method: In the processing of the absorption tower B1, the temperature of the absorption tower B1 is 10-30 degrees C and the pressure is 1-3 atm. The molar flow ratio of the absorbent S to the raw material F is 17:36. In the processing step of the flash tank B2, the temperature of the flash tank B2 is 80-160 degrees C, and the pressure is 0.01-0.1 atm. In the treatment step of the flash tank, the regenerated absorbent W2 as the absorbent S enters to the absorption tower again. In the processing of the flash tank B2, the carbon dioxide gas product is discharged from the upper part of the flash tank B2, and the regenerated absorbent W2 is discharged from the bottom of the flash tank B2.

AU ZHENG J
   SHI T
   MA X
   HAN G
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202127815Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting upper and lower clouds below when a laser radar transit. Differential absorption optical thickness between an aircraft and a high cloud is set. The differential absorption optical thickness between a high cloud and a low cloud is set. An initial value to be corrected is obtained by directly measuring the differential absorption optical thickness. An indirect difference of an error equation is established. The obtained indirect difference is resolved after more accurate differential absorption optical thickness and obtaining the corresponding carbon dioxide concentration.

PN CN113388148-A; CN113388148-B
TI Preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide-assisted heat conducting composite material, involves mixing thermoplastic polymer material, compatilizer and heat conducting filler, soaking in heat conducting filler, vaporizing and dispersing.
AU LU X
   QU J
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021B24882
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide-assisted heat conducting composite material, involves (S1) mixing thermoplastic polymer material, compatilizer and heat conducting filler to obtain polymer melt, (S2) setting pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide exceeding pressure of polymer melt, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide in polymer melt and soaking in heat conducting filler, (S3) vaporizing supercritical carbon dioxide to form carbon dioxide gas by releasing pressure, and uniformly dispersing heat conducting filler in polymer melt through blasting action of carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide-assisted heat conducting composite material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide promotes peeling, intercalation and dispersion of heat conducting filler, enhances interface interaction between the polymer molecular chain and heat conducting filler, to construct three-dimensional heat conducting path and reduce interface thermal resistance, which improves thermal conductivity of composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heat conductive filler comprises expanded graphite or boron nitride. Preferred Process: The process involves mixing in supercritical carbon dioxide-assisted melting blending extruding device, which comprises twin-screw extruder, supercritical carbon dioxide injection device and vacuum exhaust pump. The twin-screw extruder is orderly provided with feeding section along moving direction of thermoplastic polymer material, injection section and exhaust section. The feeding section is provided with feeding port. The injection section is connected with supercritical carbon dioxide injection device through supercritical carbon dioxide injection port. The exhaust section is connected with vacuum exhaust pump through vacuum exhaust port. The multiple injection sections and multiple exhaust sections are repeatedly arranged. The injection section is provided with metering pump. The pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide exceeds pressure of polymer melt of injection section by adjusting feeding amount. The rotating rate of screw and temperature of machine barrel of extruder. The steps (S2) and (S3) are repeated for several times, preferably more than 3 times. Preferred Composition: The ratio of total amount of supercritical carbon dioxide to added amount of polymer melt is (2-40): 100, and ratio of heat conducting filler to thermoplastic polymer material is (5-40):100. The screw length-diameter ratio of twin-screw extruder is (35-48):1, and screw rotation speed of extruder is 200-500 rpm, and cylinder temperature is 150-300 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polymer material comprises polyolefin or polyester, and compatibilizer comprises maleic anhydride graft copolymer or glycidyl methacrylate graft copolymer.

PN CN109809407-A
TI Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in biomass            pyrolysis tail gas, involves using biochar produced by            biomass pyrolysis process as reducing agent to reduce            carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU ZHANG Z
   DU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201948821Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in biomass                pyrolysis tail gas involves using biochar produced                by biomass pyrolysis process as reducing agent to                reduce carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   USE - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in biomass                pyrolysis tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increased utilization of                carbon dioxide in biomass pyrolysis tail gas and                reduced greenhouse gas emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The biomass is crop                  straw, trees, agricultural and forestry waste,                  agricultural product processing waste, bagasse,                  furfural residue, corn cob, rice husk or                  reed.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal                  catalyst is added to reaction system to                  accelerate the reduction rate of carbon dioxide.                  The amount of alkali metal added to the reaction                  system is 0.1-20 %mass of the amount of biochar.                  Preferred Process: The biomass pyrolysis process                  comprises rapid pyrolysis of biomass, slow                  pyrolysis of biomass or flash pyrolysis of                  biomass. Preferred Components: The alkali metal                  catalyst is alkali metal, alkaline earth metal or                  rare earth metal. The alkali metal is lithium,                  sodium, potassium, rubidium or cesium. The                  alkaline earth metal is magnesium, calcium,                  strontium or barium. The rare earth metal is                  lanthanum, cerium or samarium. The basic metal                  catalyst is added by directly adding alkaline                  metal precursor to biomass raw material during                  biomass pyrolysis preparation. The alkaline metal                  precursor is added to pyrolysis product biochar                  by impregnation. The basic metal oxide or the                  catalyst loaded with basic metal oxide is                  physically mixed with the biochar. The alkaline                  metal precursor is nitrate, acetate, carbonate,                  hydroxide or oxide. Preferred Conditions: The                  biochar reduction carbon dioxide reaction is                  carried out at 773-1473 K under reaction pressure                  of 0.01-8.0 MPa, and the pyrolysis gas space                  velocity is controlled at 0.1-20 l/g-anthrax, and                  the pyrolysis carbon space velocity is controlled                  at 0.1-100 g/hour. The biochar reduction carbon                  dioxide reaction is carried out in fixed bed                  reactor or fluidized bed reactor.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

The peak-to-peak                value of the second harmonic signal of the first                stage is averaged by the computer. The average                value is converted into the concentration according                to the linear relationship to obtain the                concentration value of carbon dioxide in the first                stage. The peak-to-peak signal value of the second                harmonic signal of the second stage is averaged by                the computer. The average value is converted into                the concentration according to the linear                relationship to obtain the concentration value of                carbon dioxide in the second stage. The                concentration value of carbon dioxide in the second                stage is subtracted from the concentration value of                carbon dioxide in the first stage, and the                difference obtained is the concentration of carbon                dioxide discharged from the skin.
   USE - Carbon dioxide concentration detecting system                discharged from a skin of a patient in a medical                and cosmetic industry for treating trauma skin,                burn and other factors.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has strong stability, high                detection sensitivity, is capable of detecting in                real time, and does not need sample pre-treatment,                and has small volume of the whole detection skin                the carbon dioxide concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                carbon dioxide concentration detecting system.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

   2Function generator

PN WO2022131925-A1; NO202001387-A
TI Production of hydrogen from natural gas, by            reacting natural gas in reformer or reacting solid            fossil fuels or biomass in gasifier to form syngas,            reacting syngas, separating shifted gas mixture,            separating hydrogen-depleted tail gas mixture and            recycling carbon dioxide-depleted tail gas            mixture.
AU BERSTAD D
   STRAUS J
AE SINTEF TTO AS (SNTF-C)
GA 202281647A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing hydrogen from natural                gas, solid fossil fuels or biomass, involves                reacting natural gas in a reformer or reacting                solid fossil fuels or biomass in a gasifier to form                syngas, reacting the syngas to form a shifted gas                mixture, comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, in                a water-gas-shift section (WGS), separating the                shifted gas mixture into a hydrogen gas and a                hydrogen depleted tail gas mixture or retentate gas                mixture in a hydrogen separation unit, separating                the hydrogen-depleted tail gas mixture or retentate                gas mixture into a carbon dioxide liquid and a                carbon dioxide depleted tail gas mixture in a                carbon dioxide capture and liquefaction unit, and                recycling the carbon dioxide-depleted tail gas                mixture from the carbon dioxide capture and                liquefaction unit without recompression to the WGS                section and to the reformer or the gasifier, where                the carbon dioxide-depleted tail gas mixture being                at a pressure is 25-120 bar when recycled to the                WGS section.
   USE - The method is used for producing hydrogen from                natural gas, solid fossil fuels or biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high carbon capture ratio                and efficiency of the hygrogen production process,                enables free-flow recycling of the tail gas,                increases conversion of both carbon monoxide and                methane to finally carbon dioxide, is cost                efficient and increases the total hydrogen                recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises compressing the hydrogen-depleted tail                  gas mixture or retentate gas mixture in                  compressor, cooling the hydrogen-depleted tail                  gas mixture or retentate in heat exchanger,                  separating a carbon dioxide-rich liquid product                  from the hydrogen-depleted tail gas mixture or                  retentate gas mixture in a bulk separator at a                  bulk separation pressure of 35-120 bar, and                  purifying the carbon dioxide-rich liquid product                  in flash separation stage at a pressure level                  lower than that of the bulk separator. The                  hydrogen separation unit is a pressure swing                  adsorption (PSA) unit, and the hygrogen-depleted                  tail gas mixture is subjected to multistage                  compression with interstage cooling to a pressure                  35-120 bar, before entering heat exchanger in the                  carbon dioxide capture and liquefaction unit. The                  remaining water of the hydrogen-depleted tail gas                  mixture is removed during the multistage                  compression. The hydrogen separation unit is a                  membrane module, where the shifted gas mixture is                  fed to a palladium or palladium-alloy or other                  hygrogen-conducting membrane where the hydrogen                  permeates through the membrane, and the                  hydrogen-depleted retentate gas mixture is                  compressed to 35-120 bar, and dehydrated before                  entering heat exchanger in the carbon dioxide                  capture and liquefaction unit. The carbon                  dioxide-rich liquid product is purified in flash                  separation unit, and a gas stream from flash                  separation unit is recycled and mixed with the                  hydrogen-depleted gas mixture or retentate gas                  mixture originating from the hydrogen separation                  unit. The WGS section contains more than one WGS                  reactor in series, and the recycle flow is sent                  to the last WGS reactor. The fraction of 60-90 %                  of the carbon dioxide-depleted tail gas mixture                  is recycled to the WGS section, a fraction from                  10-40% of the carbon dioxide-depleted tail gas                  mixture is recycled to the reformer or the                  gasifier, and a remaining fraction from 0-30% is                  sent to a furnace for combustion. Preferred                  Components: The WGS section comprises at least                  one WGS reactor. The reformer is selected from                  autothermal reformers or a combination of                  autothermal reformers and gas heated reformers,                  where the required heat of reaction is provided                  inside the autothermal reformers through a                  combustion reaction. The reformer is a                  hydrogen-fuelled steam methane reformer.                  Preferred Composition: The carbon                  dioxide-depleted tail gas mixture from the carbon                  dioxide capture and liquefaction unit comprises                  the following gas composition, 5-25 mol% carbon                  monoxide, 3-25 mol% methane, 30-80 mol% hydrogen,                  10-25 mol% carbon dioxide and a balance of                  nitrogen and residuals.
   EXAMPLE - The example section were described the general                preparation of hydrogen it does not support the                claims.

TI Metal-carbon dioxide battery system used in e.g. rover, contains positive carbon black porous cathode made of carbon nanoparticles derived from candle soot or carbon-based materials, and catalyst containing metal oxide/sulfide composite.
AU SHARMA C S
   PATHAK A D
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY HYDERABAD (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C7773U
AB    NOVELTY - A metal-carbon dioxide battery system comprises a metal anode, a candle soot carbon porous cathode (1) utilizing Martian atmosphere gases, an electrolyte (2), a catalyst (3), and a separator between the anode and cathode. The positive carbon black porous cathode is made of carbon nanoparticles derived from candle soot or other carbon-based materials. The catalyst contains candle soot carbon-transition metal oxide/sulfide composite.
   USE - Metal-carbon dioxide battery system used in lander, rover, orbiter and penetrator for Mars exploration. Uses include but are not limited to sodium-carbon dioxide batteries, lithium-carbon dioxide batteries, aluminum-carbon dioxide batteries and potassium-carbon dioxide batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is robust, has high energy density, high rate performance, long cycle stability, efficiently operates in the Martian atmosphere by utilizing local resources such as carbon dioxide, and reduces effective weight of overall mission spacecraft. The use of catalyst lowers the overpotential during charging and discharging of the battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for fabrication of the metal-carbon dioxide battery, which involves preparing porous cathode using nickel foam and carbon based material, preparing electrolyte, synthesizing catalyst, cutting metal foil into circular disc for anode, assembling the battery in mesh coin cell case in an argon glove box, using separator between carbon electrode and electrolyte, using gasket with a spring, and crimpling the coin cell assembly inside the glove box.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the battery system.
   Porous cathode (1)
   Electrolyte (2)
   Catalyst (3)
   Mars atmosphere (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode is selected from lithium, magnesium, manganese, aluminum, zinc, sodium, potassium, iron, silicon and/or their compounds. The transition metal oxide/sulfide composite is selected from porous manganese oxide or molybdenum sulfide. The cathode is preferably made of carbon-based materials selected from candle soot carbon and graphitic pencil composite with carbon black nanoparticles or carbon-based compounds selected from hard carbon, soft carbon, synthetic graphite, activated carbon (AC), graphene, biomass-derived carbon, carbon nanotube, fullerene and reduced graphene oxide. The positive porous carbon electrode further comprises a catalyst layer comprising candle soot carbon, metal oxide composite, metal and/or metal oxide, preferably a metal-containing catalyst layer or a metal oxide-containing catalyst layer. The metal in the catalyst layer is selected from platinum, gold, ruthenium, palladium, cobalt and chromium. The metal oxide in the catalyst layer is selected from manganese oxide, molybdenum sulfide and titanium dioxide. The cathode is provided to capture carbon dioxide from Martian atmosphere. The electrolyte is a liquid comprising a solvent and a salt compound, or an organic and inorganic solid electrolyte (SSE) material comprising metal-ion conductor. The salt in the electrolyte is selected from lithium hexafluorophosphate, sodium perchlorate, potassium perchlorate, lithium borofluoride, sodium hexafluorophosphate, potassium hexafluorophosphate, sodium borofluoride, potassium borofluoride, lithium bis(oxalato)borate, lithium trifluoromethanesulfonimide (LTFSI), sodium hexafluoroarsenide, potassium hexafluoroarsenide, sodium trifluoromethane sulfonate, potassium trifluoromethane sulfonate, sodium bistrifluoromethyl sulfonyl imide, lithium nitrate, sodium trifluoromethane sulfonimide, potassium bistrifluoromethyl sulfonyl imide and/or lithium bisperfluoro-ethysulfonylimide.

The electrolyte is preferably a ternary equiproportion solvent mixture of ethylene carbonate (EC), dimethyl carbonate (DMC) and diethyl carbonate (DEC) with lithium hexafluorophosphate salt.

PT P
PN EP3868905-A1; DE102020104490-A1
TI Method of implementing lime-carbon dioxide cycle in iron and steel production involves contacting slag with process water, supplying purified carbon dioxide-containing production waste gas into reaction tank, stirring, and conveying calcium carbonate and process water into settling basin.
AU SCHULTZ D
   DANIEL S
AE TCG CLEANLINESS GMBH (TCGC-Non-standard)
   TCG CLEANLINESS GMBH (TCGC-Non-standard)
GA 202198900X
AB    NOVELTY - Method of implementing lime-carbon dioxide cycle in iron and steel production involves (a) providing calcium oxide-containing slag from the iron and steel production process in at least one dissolving tank, (b) contacting the slag with process water, dissolving the calcium oxide by forming calcium hydroxide, (c) conveying the calcium hydroxide and process water into a reaction tank, (d) supplying purified carbon dioxide-containing production waste gas into reaction tank, stirring to form calcium carbonate, and (e) conveying the calcium carbonate and process water into at least one settling basin, in which the calcium carbonate settles in at least one settling basin.
   USE - Method of implementing lime-carbon dioxide cycle in iron and steel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows the carbon dioxide formed in the production process to-be-removed from the production waste gas, and reduces the environmental pollution caused by carbon dioxide from steel production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system (1) for implementation of lime-carbon dioxide cycle in iron and steel production, which consists of at least one dissolving container (2), reaction tank with stirrer which is connected downstream of the at least one dissolving tank, and at least one settling tank connected downstream of the reaction tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for implementing lime-carbon dioxide cycle in iron and steel production.
   System (1)
   Dissolving container (2)
   Filter container (3)
   Valve (4)
   Pump (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method involves heating reaction container with purified production exhaust gas through a jacket heater. Preferred Components: The system preferably comprises at least one dissolving container, at least one filter container with a filter medium and is connected to at least one dissolving container through a fluid circuit, reaction vessel with jacket heater and is connected downstream of at least one filter vessel, and tank with a bed of lime granulate which is connected downstream of the reaction vessel. The system further comprises three or more dissolving containers and filter containers which are connected to one another by a fluid circuit, and intermediate container which is connected to downstream of the three or more filter containers and upstream of the reaction container. The system further comprises at least one pH value measuring device and at least one pressure sensor which are connected in the fluid circuit between the at least one filter container and at least one dissolving container. The reaction vessel is designed as flotation. At least one filter container comprises sand filter as the filter medium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably involves providing calcium oxide-containing slag from the iron and steel production process in at least one dissolving container, contacting the slag with process water until the pH value of process water is 10-12, dissolving the calcium oxide by forming calcium hydroxide, filtering the calcium hydroxide from the process water by filter medium in at least one filter container (3), rinsing the filter medium with process water, conveying calcium hydroxide and process water into a heated reaction vessel, supplying purified carbon dioxide-containing production waste gas into the reaction vessel to form calcium carbonate, and conveying the calcium carbonate and process water through a granular lime bed into a tank, in which the calcium carbonate is deposited on the granular lime and limestone is formed. The method preferably involves heating the reaction vessel to 60 degrees C or more. The process (d) involves rinsing the filter medium with process water, in which measured differential pressure at the inlet and outlet of the at least one filter container exceeds predetermined threshold pressure, and collecting the calcium hydroxide and the process water in an intermediate container after rinsing the filter media and before conveying into the heated reaction container. The process (a) involves milling the slag with an average grain diameter of 0.5-1.5 mm. The method further involves (g) feeding the limestone into the iron and steel production process. Preferred Properties: The mass ratio of calcium hydroxide to carbon dioxide in the reaction vessel is 1:0.6. The mass ratio of process water to slag is 1:3. Preferred Condition: The process (a)-(c) are carried out in parallel in three or more dissolving containers and three or more filter containers.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A07B; M24-B01A; M25-E02; M25-G05; M25-J
IP C22B-001/00; C01F-011/18; C22B-007/04; C22B-026/20; C21B-003/04; B01D-053/62; C21B-003/00; C04B-002/00

PN CN213040304-U
TI Carbon dioxide blasting tube filling machine, has threaded shaft sleeve provided with threaded rod, where end of threaded rod is provided with mounting wheel that is sleeved with fixing plate and side of fixing plate is provided with clamping mechanism.
AU DENG M
AE DENG M (DENG-Individual)
GA 202148030M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide blasting tube filling machine, comprising a mounting shell and a mounting bottom plate; the mounting shell is provided with a carbon dioxide high pressure pump; the carbon dioxide high pressure pump is provided with a carbon dioxide raw liquid tank; one end of the output pipe is provided with a one-way valve; one end of the one-way valve is provided with a pouring needle; one side of the mounting shell is provided with a mounting plate; the mounting plate is provided with a threaded shaft sleeve; the bottom of the threaded shaft sleeve is provided with a first transmission gear; the mounting bottom plate is provided with a transmission motor; one side of the transmission motor is provided with a second transmission gear engaged with the first transmission gear; the threaded shaft sleeve is provided with a threaded rod; one end threaded rod is connected with the clamping mechanism. The filling machine has simple structure; when using, the carbon dioxide blasting tank is fixed by the clamping mechanism; the control transmission motor is electrified; the clamping mechanism drives the carbon dioxide blasting pipe to move upwards to make the filling needle connected with the carbon dioxide blasting pipe; it can carry out pouring work; a one-way valve is set to prevent carbon dioxide reflux; It avoids the perfusion of the hand-hold instrument.
DC Q69 (Storing/distributing gas/liquid (F16T, F17)); Q79 (Weapons, ammunition, blasting (F41, 2))
MC Q69-B01; Q69-C; Q69-T; Q79-A09; Q79-T02A

PN CN111577448-A; CN212716887-U
TI Four-stroke or two-stroke internal combustion engine using liquid carbon dioxide, has one end of infusion pipe is connected with liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, air nozzle set in cylinder works synchronously with oil nozzle.
AU WEN D
AE WEN D (WEND-Individual)
   WEN D (WEND-Individual)
GA 2020847138
AB    NOVELTY - The engine has an one end of an infusion pipe (5) is connected with a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1). An other end is connected with a pressurizing air tank (20) through a first one-way valve (19) to form a carbon dioxide gas supply pipeline. An infusion tube (5) is provided with a radiating tube (6) and an infusion pump (7). An infusion pump is located on an upper unit of the radiating tube. An air return pipe (42) is connected with the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1) to form a carbon dioxide return air pipeline. An air nozzle (26) set in a cylinder works synchronously with an oil nozzle or a spark plug ignition.
   USE - Four-stroke or two-stroke internal combustion engine using liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The fan-free heat dissipation saves an air inlet grille of an automobile. A headstock layout is reduced. An eliminating a high temperature of an internal combustion engine compartment and exhaust gas release is helpful to avoid infrared detection and tracking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of four-stroke or two-stroke internal combustion engine using liquid carbon dioxide.
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Infusion pipe (5)
   Radiating tube (6)
   Infusion pump (7)
   First one-way valve (19)
   Pressurizing air tank (20)
   Air nozzle (26)
   Air return pipe (42)
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).); Q14 (Vehicle accessories (B60H, B60N, B60Q, B60R, B62H-J)); Q24 (Ships; waterborne vessels; related equipment (B63)); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))
MC P35-C01; P35-C03; P35-C05; P35-U03; Q14-M; Q24-B03; Q51-A01J; Q51-A03A; Q51-G02; Q51-J02A; Q51-J02F

PT P
PN CN211311935-U
TI Carbon dioxide heat pump clothes dryer, has drying box whose top plate is provided with air inlet and lower side plate is provided with air outlet, and condenser connected in series with pipeline that is filled with carbon dioxide gas.
AU MA J
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU LIGHT IND (UZHL-C)
GA 2020832756
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide heat pump clothes dryer, comprising a clothes drying box, wherein the top plate of the clothes drying box is provided with an air inlet; the lower side plate of the clothes drying box is provided with an air outlet; the air inlet and the air outlet are connected through the circulating air channel; the circulating air channel is provided with a fan at the air outlet; The carbon dioxide heat pump clothes dryer further comprises a carbon dioxide heat pump system; the carbon dioxide heat pump system comprises a compressor, an evaporator set in the circulating air channel and located downstream of the fan and at least one condenser located downstream of the evaporator; the pipeline is filled with carbon dioxide gas. the wet air passes through the evaporator and the condenser in turn; the water passes through the evaporator to integrate water, the condenser makes the air heating again into the clothes drying box; using the carbon dioxide heat pump to exchange heat with the air through the carbon dioxide; compared with the electric heating mode of the existing technology, it is more safe to use. and the circulating air channel makes the air cycle use, avoids the wet pollution and heat pollution to the external environment, improves the conversion rate of the energy.
DC X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC X27-D02
IP D06F-058/20
